boardgame: Dawn of Heroes: The Anna's Roundtable Expansion
description:
The first Anna's Roundtable expansion adds 200 new unit cards, 22 new shop cards, and 5 new solo/co-op bosses!

This doubles the total unit pool for the base game and vastly expands the variations of Anna's Roundtable.

In Big Bull Junior, it's all about your stocks portfolio/collection. You try to collect companies' stocks and try to increase the price of the companies (or trying to decrease the price of companies held by other players) in the central market. But since you don't know what stocks are held by other players, you might inadvertently help them increase the value of their collection!

During one's turn, a player can collect a card, increase/decrease the value of one of the stocks in the central market. You can bluff, strategize or collude to spike/crash stock price.

At the end of 10 rounds, the wealthiest/richest player wins the game.

Its time to pull your socks oops....stocks! Can you become a big bull.

—description from the designer

The Agents' Agency is looking for talented agents to recruit for their secret missions. In order to qualify, you receive 15 letters from all over Europe. Can you solve all the puzzle and decode the secret messages?

The game contains 15 letters in envelopes. Solving the puzzles in the first one will tell you the next envelope. Note the order in which you opened the envelopes - this will be crucial for your final score. In the end you will find an evaluation of your success, so you know whether you qualified for being a secret agent.

Twerk to win! This super fun game puts your twerking skills to the test. Fill your box up with the ping pong bills and race to see who can empty their box the quickest! Twerk Pong is perfect for parties, gatherings and family fun and has three different game play modes. Be prepared for some laughs!

—description from the publisher

Census is a survey based card game that allows players to answer 500 carefully designed questions anonymously, generating hours of fun and laughter.

—description from the publisher

Take on your opponent and spin the wheel! Don't get splattered!

Who is going to get whipped cream in their face?! You can also use the jelly, slime or whatever you want on the hand!

Spin the spinner and turn the lever on the side of the base the number of times that you landed on when the spinner was spun (e.g if you land on a two you turn the lever twice). The ajm is to avoid being pied in the face by the hand. Whoever is pied the least wins!

It's Bananas is the hilarious new party game for kids, teens, and tipsy adults. Split into two teams, and compete in a series of monkey challenges. The team with the most bananas wins! Tail tricks, find the fleas, monkey talk, coconut races more than 18 challenges to keep your friends, family, and kids laughing! Gameplay lasts 30 minutes, is for 6+ years, and takes 4+ players, making Its Bananas! the ideal game for large groups, dinner parties, ice breakers, games night, thanksgiving fun, kids birthday parties, family gatherings, and Xmas.

—description from publisher

Welcome to Belly Battle!

Build your own restaurant empire! Buy restaurants from Mr.Munchwalla catering to one of the six different cuisines and collect Munch Fees from visiting players or choose to upgrade your restaurants by hiring more chefs or getting a Star rating. Exchange, swap or take over other restaurants. Get more visitors during Munch time!

When any player goes bankrupt, the game ends. Every other player then calculates their wealth. 5. The player who has the maximum wealth (Sum of restaurants + chefs+ Stars + Money), wins the game.

—description from the designer

Behind the appearance and hidden among the details of an illustration of a densely populated underwater landscape with organisms, there are four adventures concealed, narrated in a booklet. As the stories unfold, players must carefully observe the illustration to find the solutions to the various questions and puzzles posed by the different characters.

—description from the designer

Players enter a proving ground to build army of various beings, equip them with weapons and abilities and cast a diverse array of spells - all to survive in the arena as long as possible.

Fatal Arena is a quick battle game, where the fate of all players depend on a single deck of cards. Players look for ways how to build effective offense AND defense from the small number of cards they can gather each game.

Plays similar to Hearthstone or Magic: the Gathering, with simpler rules and a unique yet straight-forward resource system: your "resource" is simply the number of cards in your hand.

—description from the designer

Zombies, vampires, skeletons, ghosts and magic so black noone unleashed it for 3000 years.
RAW is a quick, rough battle game for 2-6 necromancers. It's full of undead monsters, terrifying magic and vicious traps. The goal is to bite through opponent's defenses and turn the enemy necromancer into dust.

Featuring 4 undead races - zombies, skeletons, vampires and ghosts, each with unique way resilience. Playes build and army to defend their lair and to attack and loot the lairs of other players, all from a single common deck of cards.

Cards are (un)balanced in a way that brings hilarious twists in each game and their combinations give way to unexpected stories.

—description from the designer

Galo da Madrugada is the biggest carnival block on the planet.
In this simple mancala game based on the carnival block, players try to collect "Foliões" (the people who participate in the block during the carnival).
Each round, players seed markers (as usual in a mancala), and where the last marker ends, a card is collected, representing a person, instrument, dance, or other form of expression of Galo da Madrugada.
Each card shows "foliões" in different colors and, at the end of the game, each color of "folião" has brings different amounts of points and groups of "foliões" of different colors bring additional points.

Yo Sabo The Game is the new card game for your next game night! It started off as a way to remember some tricky Spanish words but it has evolved into a way to reconnect with your childhood and share some funny memories with your friends. Do not worry, you can still win the game by answering Latino Trivia or by winning the challenges on the Chancla cards!

Tales of Myth & Legend is a paragraph book game set in the time of ancient Greece. Players each control a fledgling hero or heroine that has their sights set on power and glory. The players compete not only with each other, but also against the Alexander the Great himself.

In each round of the game, players simultaneously play a movement card that determines how far they will travel. Based on the results, the turn order will be adjusted, with players traveling farther distances going last, and those traveling shorter going first. In turn order, each player visits a city or location and has an encounter there, choosing a type — e.g., political, religion, or strength — based on the types available. The encounter is read aloud to the player, then they have to choose how to proceed.

Tales of Myth & Legend features many surprises and hidden components that are unlocked only based on certain events! With thousands of possible stories and encounters, this is a game you'll be playing for years to come!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Zombicide: 2nd Edition – Monty Python's Flying Circus: A Rather Silly Expansion
description:
And now for something completely different.

The zombie invasion is about to get very silly indeed with the introduction of the Monty Python’s Flying Circus expansion for Zombicide 2nd Edition! Some of the most nonsensical creations from the classic TV show by the revered English comedy troupe are being brought to the tabletop through amazing miniatures and irreverent gameplay.

This set adapts some of the funniest sketches to ever grace the small screen into Survivors and hordes of enemies, as well as new Equipment and even a new Mission. Yes, most of these enemies are not really dead (they’re pining), but why should Monty Python start making sense now?

Grab a herring, a raw chicken, or a good pair of mice-playing mallets, and get ready to face hordes of silly walkers, vicious grannies, fish-slapping dancers, and many other foes nobody expects!

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Zombicide: 2nd Edition – Monty Python's Flying Circus: Gumby Pack
description:
An exclusive set for those that pre-order the Monty Python Flying Circus: A Rather Silly Expansion through CMON's website (included at no additional charge).

The Gumbys can be used in several different ways in your game:

Abomination: The mob of Gumbys work as a single Abomination, moving as a loud and clueless mob that can be taken out one by one. Their strength is in numbers, as they can overwhelm Survivors with their sheer stupidity (or a lucky brick to the head).

Companion: As ghastly as that may sound, the Gumbys can follow Survivors around as Companions, helping pummel zombies with their angry little clenched fists (it’s like throwing lemmings against a blender).

Survivor: If you gather enough Gumbys you can kinda make one half-decent Survivor. All 6 Gumbys together act as a single obnoxiously loud Survivor. The more there are, the more damage they deal hurling bricks, though each wound takes one Gumby out. Be careful though, as these guys are very prone to getting in the way of friendly fire.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
After getting deeply embarrassed in front of a classroom in 9th grade, a 5-year period of isolation, loneliness, and social anxiety was triggered. Lacking any real tools to support myself (other than a hoodie), the following years involved doing everything in my power to hide from further shame or embarrassment.

After forcing myself into out into the world and slowly regaining confidence to take the hoodie off, a curiosity grew to better understand myself and this internal world I seemingly had very little control of.

I stumbled into meditation, soaked up any talk on psychology, explored therapy, art, men's groups, and above all - found friendships with others who shared this desire to look inward and share more openly about not only the good stuff, but also the less than ideal - our fears, pains and insecurities.

Over time I noticed that it was in these open conversations that our relationship grew. Not to mention they helped us feel a bit less alone - like we were in this together. And within the men's groups, we were paving a new way for what had been told to us as men - to do it alone, to stay strong and not show weakness.

Strangely enough, after being forced to hide myself when I was younger, it has been these moments of seeing others and being seen more deeply that have given me a sense of meaning, connection and purpose.

Ultimately, this game is a tool to create those moments - to explore, uncover, and share what makes us who we are. While scary at times (or damn near impossible), I believe that it's in this process that ourselves and our relationships can transform.

90 QUESTION CARDS.
3 LEVELS OF REALNESS.

—description from the publisher

Detective Bullshit is a game for 3-5 players you win by making shit up.

Think of Clue meets Cards Against Humanity, sort of. It's a competitive storytelling game centered around a fictional criminal case. Unlike Clue, there's no right answer, but like Cards Against Humanity, the fun is in getting your friends to pick your ridiculous story.

For each case, one player is Chief Bullshit and other players are detectives.

The Chief draws a victim card and decides the location of the crime. It's then up to the other detectives to play suspect cards from their hands and convince the Chief that their suspect is guilty. But things get complicated in the 3 rounds of each case as additional evidence, complications, and bribery keep even the best bullshitters on their toes.

This expansion adds 90 new cards including 6 blanks where you can add your own phrases and people.

boardgame: Cretaceous Rails: Variable Player Powers Mini Expansion
description: The Cretaceous Rails Mini Expansion Promo Cards consist of four cards depicting time tourism company employees and their pet baby dinosaurs. The mini expansion introduces variable player powers to the game. The promo cards were originally distributed as a free bonus for For Glory: Champions Kickstarter backers and given away at Origins 2023.

We decided to release 12 new cards of Underwater Cities. This Mini-expansion will be issued on occasion of 5th Anniversary of Delicious Games and its 1st title Underwater Cities.

—description from the publisher

Hamburg, as one of the largest sea and trading cities, is already worthy of our attention, how much more interesting is it not to learn about the national life and freedoms of this famous city?
Each player has starts an equal number counters. Each player puts an equal number of counters into the kitty. The 'court' (figurine) cards are dealt to each player in equal quantities. The smaller cards are put into a small bag and drawn out. Each small card has a cry in 3 languages as well as a number. The first player to match one of his Court Cards to a number or cry, takes the small card. The first person to match 5 numbers  receives half of the kitty.
With the other half of the kitty, another type of game begins.   The cards are shuffled and evenly distributed. The player who can put together the figure and lettering cards with a number, so that the whole appears complete, hands them over to the Banker and receives 1 or 2 counters for them. If there are more cards, someone starts to give away a card of his choice to his neighbour. In this way, it is possible to find the cards that match and to exhaust the kitty, which ends the game.
Regrettably, the game is all luck-oriented but it look great.

Far from here, through the towns and valleys of the land, something important lies in wait. You cannot resist it, even if many troubles lie between here and there. With good friends at your side, set off on a fantastical questto discover what lies at Journey's End.

In Picaresque, you will choose one player to be the Game Master (GM); everyone else is a player. The GM will describe the scenes the players journey through and introduce the trials they face. The players will devise plans to overcome the trials in order to get to their Journey's End.

The game takes place over a series of stages, each representing a stage of the journey. Each stage has four parts which take place in turn:

1. Create a new scene: The GM places a new scene between the current scene and Journey's End. This is the next place characters are trying to get to on their journey, which will eventually lead to the Journey's End.

2. Explore the scene: Players roleplay freely, exploring the current scene and looking for ways to get to the new scene.

3. Introduce the Trial: The GM selects a Trial card as an obstacle in their way. The players devise a plan to overcome the Trial.

4. Face the Trial: At a climatic moment in the plan, the GM pauses the roleplay to resolve the Trial.To succeed, the players require a total greater than or equal to 7; otherwise the players fail. Reveal the top 2 cards of the deck and add their Values, then applying bonuses.

If players passed the trial, they earn a Boon. After overcoming two trials, the next scene is Journey's End. Players must overcome one last Trial to reach their final destination and succeed in their quest.

Join the famous actors from the TV series "Mot i Brøstet" through various crazy situations. The first of three games based on the show.
The players get a piece each connected with one of the characters from the show; Nils, Trine, Målfrid, Karl or Henry. The players roll dice and travel through the game board representing a trip from the characters home during breakfast, and further on through the town of Oslo, then Las Palmas, and finally back home. Some of the spaces have specific events that happens to whoever lands on them, ranging from moving to other spaces, losing or gaining a turn or being sent to alternative paths through the board. These spaces all have small stories including the characters from the show explaining the result. First player to the last space wins.

boardgame: Mot i Brøstet Det Orginale Brettspillet
description:
The second board game based on the Norwegian sitcom "Mot i Brøstet". This one is very reminiscent of Ludo, with elements from Monopoly and Quiz-games.
Each player start with 5 pieces connected to one of the characters from the show; Karl, Henry, Nils, Målfrid, Trine or Edna. They start on their own separate tile beside the game board. Each turn the players roll 2 dice, and send their pieces circling around the abstract gameboard. On their way they might land on specific spaces which might give out trivia questions from the show, random events inspired from episodes, spaces which gives you benefit if the right character land on them or the Mot i Brøstet-space giving you a Mot i Brøstet-token. These spaces might give different benefits, and the Mot i Brøstet-token can be used in a variety of ways. Once a player have gotten all their pieces one round around the board and then into the center up each characters own path to the goal, they win.

Join the crazy gang from the TV series "Mot i Brøstet" in a wild game in familiar surroundings. From the start in the corner of the sofa, it's all about solving the task you are assigned as quickly as possible, but beware, the other players can actively destroy you. This is the third board game based on the Norwegian sitcom Mot i Brøstet.

The board is designed after the set from the show, with both the Kitchen, Living room, Entrance and Upstairs. Each player starts on the sofa in the living room with a piece reperecenting one character from the show; Karl, Nils, Henry, Målfrid, Trine or Edna. Taking turns, a player need to roll a 6 on a die before they can leave the sofa and enter the start space. Then they have to choose a task, either Kitchen, Entrance, or Upstairs. There are only 2 or each, so only 2 can choose the same. Then the players need to manuver the house moving spaces to the question space on their chosen room, where another player can freely chose a trivia question from the trivia manual. If the player chose correctly, they can move back to the sofa to win. If not, they need to move back to the sofa and pick a new task.

If player chose to take certant detours to their task, they can aquire a detroy chip. Each destroy chip is catogarized with the different task, and once used, get placed on a space in the middle of the living room named "Ø". Any player with that task must go back to that space to remove that chip before they can answer any trivia questions.

There are also banana peel-spaces, forcing a player to lose a turn if landed on.

—user summary

Potions is a roll and write fantasy game in which you will have to create a Magic potion to save the city before the curse catches you.

You take the game sheet, a pen and 2 dice and optionally a character card that influences certain aspects of the game. The dice rolls to mark certain areas on the game sheet on order to generate the resources needed to create the potions and win the game. But certain rolls activate the Crypt Track that will ultimately make you lose the game when completed.

So, welcome, welcome to this epic fantasy game in which you will take on the role of a young magic apprentice who seeks to create a magic potion to save your city from an ancient curse.

The city of Mystical has been cursed for centuries and the only magician who knew the recipe to create the potion, Zoltar the Renegade, has been exiled by the King for refusing to make the potion.

Now you, like Magnus Novice or Aria Novice, must delve into the magic lake, investigate mystical ingredients and overcome epic challenges to find a way to break the curse by creating the King's potion. Will you be able to complete this dangerous mission and become the hero of Mystical?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Tales from the Red Dragon Inn: Kickstarter Kit
description:
We are well aware that Kickstarter Backers are always excited about bonuses! As a thank you for backing the campaign on Kickstarter, all Adventurer Pledge Level backers will also receive the Kickstarter Kit, for free! The Kickstarter Kit includes the Beer Goat, a summonable buddy you can take into the dungeon, complete with a miniature and item card. Throughout the campaign Stretch Goals will add more content to the Kickstarter Kit.

Stretch Goals have unlocked more content for the kit, including Solo Mode with the Beer Goat as your companion, and a mysterious new mini. These mysterious minis are important foes you'll face in the campaign, and revealing who they are would spoil the narrative. The silhouette for the mystery mini is a stand-in and does not represent the newly unlocked content.

Finally, content in the Kickstarter Kit will not be available in the retail version of the game.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Tales from the Red Dragon Inn: Demo Kit – Kickstarter Edition
description:
This scenario pack is our convention and retail demo scenario, a taste of what the game has to offer. The scenario, Just Stopping By for a Pint is labeled as 2.5 and intended to be played between scenarios 2 and 3 in the campaign.

The Demo Kit will only be available to demo teams at brick and mortar stores and conventions, and won’t even be for sale to the general public!

—description from the publisher

Contained in the Kickstarter Edition of Tales from the Red Dragon Inn.

In Stan Lee's Genesis, players must harness their creative energy to give birth to a unique universe. Genesis is intended to put the power of creation into the hands of the players and honor the legacy of Stan’s brilliance as one of the preeminent creative forces of the 20th century. Every game is different as players both compete and collaborate as they construct a comic book universe full of heroes, villains, super team, evil syndicates, galactic threats, and all-encompassing cross over events!

—description from the publisher

A game master is chosen who shuffles all the cards well. Then he distributes 6 cards to each player, which are placed face up upon receipt. The rest of the cards are given to the game master, who piles them face down in front of him.
The first player takes one of his blue- or red-printed tiles, calls out the name, and exchanges it with the game master for a blue pin for a blue-printed card, or a red marker for a red-printed card. The needle is placed at the beginning of the river identified by the map, while the red marker is placed at the location indicated by the map.
In turn, the players continue with one card each. It should be noted that the blue-printed cards with rivers and the red-printed ones with cities that are on the Rhine can be used without further ado, while for the black-printed cards with cities that are on tributaries of the Rhine, you only then get a black marker, which is then placed on the relevant location if the river on which the city is located is already pinned by a blue pin.
Whoever gets rid of their cards first wins.

—user summary

boardgame: Radiant: Roster Expansion #2 – The Haunting of Cruach
description:
This roster expansion contains three brand new heroes for Radiant: Offline Battle Arena. Enhance your game with a new teams, new strategies and a deeper, more challenging draft. Una, the Spectral Tyrant, is a Tank hero for whom death is merely a setback. Alyn, the Knight of Blood, is a Warrior hero who comes closer to his monstrous aspect the more blood is spilled. Aurelia, the Living Saint, is a Support hero who channels the power of the divine to heal and enhance her allies.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Radiant: Roster Expansion #3 – Wardens of the White Forest
description:
This roster expansion contains three brand new heroes for Radiant: Offline Battle Arena. Enhance your game with a new teams, new strategies and a deeper, more challenging draft. Grandfather the Eldest, is a Tank hero who shares the blessing of the forest with his allies. Maka the Foundling is a Warrior hero who hunts enemies with stealth and guile. Brighid the Fae Enchantress is a Support hero who weaves enchantments and illusions to confound her foes and protect her friends.

—description from the publisher

Highland Whist is a casual trick-taker for four (4) players in fixed partnerships.

Teams score based on the number of tricks captured. The scoring has several peaks (that score more than 0 points) and valleys (that score 0 points).

The scoring valleys are at 0, 3-4, 7-8, and 11-12 tricks. Try not to fall in…

—description from the designer

(The rules now include a trick & draw variant for two players.)
family: Organizations: The Game Artisans of Canada

description:
Why should kids have all the fun? Bring family and friends together to play The Scrabble Bingo board game reimagined for intergenerational connections. This family game is actually three different games in one. Scrabble and Bingo, two iconic games, are combined into a fun game of strategy and luck. Players can also play using classic Scrabble gameplay, or quick rounds of the Scrabble Pass game, an easy word game that has everyone playing against the timer. All three games are designed with older adults in mind, and created with easy-to-grasp, easy-to-read components. Get the conversations started when grandkids use words unfamiliar to older generations and vice versa. You never know what you’ll discover, but you know you’ll have fun and be engaged!

—description from publisher

Greatest Generation, Baby Boomers, Gen X, Millennials, and Gen Z all think their generation is better than the rest, but who really is the greatest? Bring family and friends together to play The Game of Life board game reimagined for intergenerational connections. How you handle major events in the game depends on how you choose to play. Older adults can interact with younger generations and share the laughs and stories that come with connecting through the power of play. Make family game nights memorable as you experience the unexpected twists and turns of life’s adventures from start to retirement as one of five generations.

—description from publisher

Bet on the present or bet on the future. The card game to see the timing to challenge.

Players get encounter cards in exchange for the dialogue cards in their hand, and compete for points.

The active player draws an encounter card into play. At this time, the encounter card for the next turn will also be revealed, so please check it carefully.

The active player play the Dialogue Card with the same time zone as the Encounter Card and acquires the Encounter Card. However, at this time, other players can say "Wait a minute!" and participate in the Encounter Card acquisition. In the event of a battle for the Encounter Card, the winner is the player who puts into play the Dialogue Card of the Type written on the back of the Encounter Card.

By considering the number of cards in opponents' hands and the upcoming encounter cards, strategically determine when to preserve cards in your hand and when to move aggressively to increase your chances of winning.

The game ends when a confession card appears or when all players have no more cards in hand.

—description from the designer

boardgame: World of Tanks Miniatures Game: American – M4A3E2 Sherman Jumbo Expansion
description:
The American M4A3E2 Sherman Jumbo is the big-boned brother of the classic Sherman, with 2 Survivability making this a tough target to damage.

Turbocharger and the Ford GAA Engine help the Jumbo position, while the 76mm Gun M1A1 makes it pack a little more punch.

Contains a tank miniature and tank card. Your tank can be further personalized to match your requirements using the five crew, module, and upgrade cards included.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: World of Tanks Miniatures Game: British – Achilles
description:
The British Achilles takes the flexibility of the American M10 Wolverine and throws a bigger gun into the mix to increase the lethality! Marcus Quince with Situational Awareness and Improved Aiming quickly improve the Achilles' Initiative to make a truly scary Tank Destroyer.

Includes a tank miniature and tank card. Your tank can be further personalized to match your requirements using the five crew, module, and upgrade cards included.

boardgame: World of Tanks Miniatures Game: German – Jagdpanther

The German Jagdpanther takes the mobility of the legendary Panther and mounts a bigger gun in a stealthy Tank Destroyer package.

Use Markus Theissen's Intuition perk or the 10.5cm PaK L/52 Gun to boost Initiative, and take Sixth Sense to ensure this Assault Gun isn't flanked.

Includes a tank miniature and tank card. Your tank can be further personalized to match your requirements using the five crew, module, and upgrade cards included.

Loza's M4-A2 Sherman is the Soviet take on the classic American Sherman, and has unique differences including Mobility 3 and 6 Hit Points, giving this Medium Tank the edge above the rest. Pair this Smooth Ride and Ivan Revinskiy to make any hit deadly.

Includes a tank miniature and tank card. Your tank can be further personalized to match your requirements using the five crew, module, and upgrade cards included.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Humbug: das zweifelhafte Kartenspiel – Original Edition 2
description:
This "dubious" card game is a crazy-cheeky card game ideal for cheating, guessing, bluffing and betting!
It's perfect for fun, casual game nights with friends and family!

Guess if the player whose turn it is is either describing the picture on the card or making up a scenario. Unmask the swindler and collect points in the form of betting chips.

It's perfect for fun, casual game nights with friends and family!

Guess if the player whose turn it is is either describing the picture on the card or making up a scenario. Unmask the swindler and collect points in the form of betting chips.

boardgame: Humbug: das zweifelhafte Kartenspiel – Original Edition 4
description:
This "dubious" card game is a crazy-cheeky card game ideal for cheating, guessing, bluffing and betting!
It's perfect for fun, casual game nights with friends and family!

Guess if the player whose turn it is is either describing the picture on the card or making up a scenario. Unmask the swindler and collect points in the form of betting chips.

In this single player PnP you sold your soul to the devil and now want it back. The devil's deal is that you help his demons recover lost sins and then you get your soul back. But the longer you stay in Hell, the harder it is to come back...

In this PnP you need one of the game boards (Circles of Hell), 4 dice, a pen and a meeple to mark your movement through the Circle of Hell. First you roll to set one objective out of six that you have to fulfill in order to please the demons. 
Then you roll 4 dice each round with which you can do actions such as moving, collecting or cleansing or you can craft items or transform sins in order to fulfill your objectives. 
But be aware the time also goes forward and if you take too long you will not get out of Hell.

Holy Poop is the game where players take turns taking a dump! 
Cap a poo pile to make it yours. 
The player with the biggest poo pile wins.

Test your brains and your bowels New age strategy game!

boardgame: Tank Leader: Eastern Front – Designer Signature Edition
description:
Tank Leader: Eastern Front, Designer Signature Edition is a tactical level, two-player game covering armored warfare in the Second World War where formations are activated by playing command cards. This edition is dedicated to the memory of the original game series designer, John Hill, and includes numerous system updates as introduced in later entries of the game series. We have assembled a top-notch group of contributors, including veterans of the game series along with the original designers of Western Front Tank Leader and Desert Steel to deliver numerous enhancements to the game series.

This edition of Tank Leader: Eastern Front is another Classic Reborn! by Compass.

Tank Leader: Eastern Front simulates a variety of tactical situations facing a battalion commander of armored forces on the Eastern Front. It is not restricted to any single battle, area of Russia, or time period, but presents the players with a host of differing situations. Command, control, and communications (C3) is the pivotal factor in the game and is simulated through the use of formation cards which rate units’ C3 abilities. The card play portion of the game is critical, for it determines tactical initiative — who will move first, and how quickly a formation responds to changing situations. Numerous basic and advanced game scenarios are provided.

The game system is reach in detail to recreate tactical armored warfare, and features formation activations and operations, movement and fire operations, morale, tactical doctrine, non-vehicle units, anti-infantry fire, indirect fire, transport, entrenching, overruns, close assault, forward observers, area of operations, German and Soviet tactical doctrine, artillery, random reinforcements, and much more.

This Designer Signature edition represents a culmination of the forward strides made with subsequent games published in the series; Western Front Tank Leader and Desert Steel. Both designers of those subsequent game releases, Leonard Quam and Peter Corless, are collaborating on this new edition of the game along with other veterans of the game series to incorporate advances and refinements to the game system. Expect to see this and future games in the series to be published in the future!

Product Information:

Complexity: 4/6 out of 10 (basic/advanced game)
Solitaire Suitability: 3 out of 10
Time Scale: 60 minutes per scenario
Map Scale: 150 meters per hex
Unit Scale: platoons – 20-30 men, 2-5 tanks, trucks, halftracks, and 3-5 tubes of artillery
Players: two
Playing Time: 3-8 hours depending upon scenario

description:
Enter Ion Core, a thematic sandbox game set in a post-apocalyptic dystopian world. Humanity has been driven to piracy to secure a brighter future and it's up to you to guide your faction to victory!

Procedurally generated maps by placing tiles using specific actions ensure a diverse and different seafaring and treacherous landscape each time you play. How will you navigate in order to brave the perils of Ion Core’s world? Plan your course well or enemy factions will take all the glory!

Enjoy an adventure with minimal luck factor – actions like Scavenge or Plunder can be enhanced using a deck or upgrades to allow players to either enhance or mitigate chances, shifting the outcome to your favor.

Pillage or visit cities to upgrade your ship, gather spoils and forgotten relics, hunt dangerous beasts, enlist new crewmembers or entertain them to increase their morale, trade your way to riches or plunder your way to victory!

With a variety of ways to play and different aspects to focus on, there is never a dull moment - reading through your opponent’s intent is just as important as planning your own. No matter how you choose to advance, make sure to set your sights on controlling the energy source of this cruel world, and gather the most renown at the end! The choice is yours!

Beautifully hand-drawn artwork and components will suck you into Ion Core’s world and its unparalleled aesthetic! Each character comes to life through backstory and art to immerse players and make bonds, not just progress.

But beware, you are not alone in sailing the seas... Enemy ships and a mysterious, relentless adversary haunt your every move so plan well, take risks and brave your way to total control!

—description from the publisher

This date centers around the importance of unity in your relationship and working together in all things. You'll get the chance to test your teamwork skills. in an escape room game designed by the Happily team.

You'll be working against the clock to solve the clues given in the game before the time limit expires. In your box, you'll also find some great snacks to make your date night that much sweeter.

—description from publisher

Sail: Seafarers introduces a variety of brand new content to the base game.  Included are four new scenarios that challenge players with new hazards they’ll need to overcome, such as whirlpools and pirate ships.  Seafarers also introduces four new pirate characters with new pirate powers that add even more ways to strategize and work together.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
In the year 2071, humanity stands on the brink of a new age, the age of Martian colonization. Mars, the barren crimson jewel of our solar system, teems with untapped potential. Earth's global powers and private corporations have launched an ambitious project : to transform Mars into a second home for humanity.

Equipped with advanced technology, in Pioneers on Mars two factions compete to claim and colonize different regions of Mars. Their mission is not only about survival, but about harnessing the planet's unique resources and establishing a self-sustaining settlement.

The duel for Mars begins with the selection of specialized tools, each represented by unique icons: dust devils to simulate the Martian environment, rovers for exploration, solar panels to harvest the sunlight, greenhouses for cultivating food, satellites for communication, settlers as the brave pioneers, and volcanoes as potential energy sources.

The Mars colonization project is an incredible journey, a testament to human tenacity and ingenuity. Each turn, each decision brings them one step closer to creating a new world, a new home, a new beginning. The players are no longer merely participants in a game; they become architects of the future. The survival of humanity is now in their hands. The countdown to colonize Mars starts now.

—description from the designer

Perilous Pond is a game about rolling dice and trying to make the best out of the final result. Doing this is a bit of a spatial puzzle, moving your frog into a position with lots of options for future turns while still keeping safe from the pike. The game is suitable for everyone, a simple and fun mechanic kids easily master and a depth of strategy that gives the game another dimension.

The pond is not a static playing field. As you and your opponents move your frogs around, chasing fireflies, the pond will change. As a frog hops across the pond the lily pads, they will first be submerged and if pushed off of once more sink completely. This makes the pond harder and harder to navigate as the game progresses.

boardgame: Free at Last: Leaders and Coalitions
description: Contains redesigned leader counters, additional leaders, corrected replacement cards and a new set of rules for solitaire play.

description:
Disney sjovt at lære klokken is a memory-based, educational board game published by Disney. Designed to teach children about time, the game challenges 2 to 4 players to match clock pieces to corresponding time-slot pieces on their board.

During gameplay, players take turns flipping over Disney character pieces to reveal a background color, which indicates the color of the clock piece they should look for. Players then flip a clock piece; if the color and time match one of their time-slot pieces, they cover it with the clock piece. Otherwise, both the clock and Disney pieces are flipped back over.

The game continues with players trying to remember the placement and colors of the pieces on the table. The winner is the first player to cover all of their time-slot pieces with matching clock pieces. A delightful way to learn about time, this game is suitable for children aged 5 and up.

Each player places his 3 pawns in the starting space of the inner circle of his color. If a player rolls a 6, he places one pawn on the red-bordered starting space and then has a second roll. He now advances in the direction of the line. After every throw of a 6, another figure is placed on the starting point. If the player's pawn reaches a space on the track that is already occupied by another player, he throws his pawn back into the starting space. 
If a player has his 3 pieces on the track, he is entitled to another throw after every 6 thrown.
Each player strives to break the ring with his pawn and thus reach the next ring. For this purpose he must occupy the red space with his pawn. Immediately afterwards, he has a second roll, through which he continues his pawn in the next ring. If he exceeds the red space with the number of points rolled, he reaches the pendulum spaces marked with black arrows. With the following throws he has to shuttle back and forth over the red space until he occupies it and then leads his pawn into the next ring with another throw.
If the player reaches the red space of the outer ring with a pawn, he places it in the target space of his color.
If a player has occupied the target space with his 3 pawns, he is the winner of the game.

You are in the Gobi Desert, in Mongolia. Take the stones from the Ovoo, these stone embankments erected in the Mongolian shamanic culture in order to communicate with the deities of the mountains.

Use strategy to place them wisely. Build the tallest cairn to claim victory!

—description from the publisher

In Muraÿa, your aim will be to try and predict your opponent's moves in order to block his Soldier counter or move your counter to the other side of the board, into the opponent's territory formed by the 1st row of squares on the opposite side.

Players take turns. On his turn, a player may perform 1 or 2 of the following actions:

     move his soldier 1 hex,
     move a wall of his color,


The same type of action can be repeated, with the same piece or two different pieces (if it's a wall). If the player performs 2 actions of different types, they can be played in any order.

A wall can only be moved on the spaces between the squares, while a soldier can only move on the squares:

Soldiers can only move vertically or horizontally, while Walls can rotate 90° or move in a straight line. Walls cannot be placed on the edge of the board.

The game ends if the opponent's piece is blocked in any way, or if one of the 2 players' pieces reaches the edge of the board on the opponent's side.

Asli is a territory game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the intersections (points) of an initially empty square grid (board). There is also an off-board location called prison.

This game is based on Lifeline.

				
				
					DefinitionsA group is a stone along with all stones one can reach from it through a series of steps onto orthogonally adjacent stones of its color.

A group is alive if there is a free path along the lines of the board between that group and another group of its color. The path does not need to be a straight line. A group is also alive if there has never been more than one group of its color on the board. A group that is not alive is dead.

A territory is an empty point along with all points one can reach from it through a series of steps onto orthogonally adjacent empty points. You own a territory if all stones orthogonally adjacent to it are of your color.

An incursion is a placement by one player in a territory owned by the other player. An incursion is minimal if it turns exactly one live enemy group into a dead group.

				
				
					PlayBlack plays first, then turns alternate. On your turn, perform exactly one of these actions:


     Hold: Remove an enemy stone from the prison.
     Play: Place a stone of your color on an empty point, then move all dead enemy groups to the prison. If the prison now contains stones of both colors, remove pairs of opposite-colored stones from it until at most one color remains.


At the end of your turn, there must be no dead friendly groups on the board. You cannot make a minimal incursion if your opponent just made a minimal incursion anywhere on the board.

The last player to perform an action wins. If a play recreates any previous end-of-turn board position with the same player to move, the game ends in a draw. This is possible in theory, but probably requires colluding players.

Before the game begins, to make it fair, the first player places a number of black stones in the prison, and then the second player chooses sides.

—description from the designer

In this light strategy game, aspiring pastry chefs compete to become the world's Master Chocolatier by crafting delicious Chocolate Landscapes and enlisting the help of talented mice.

Build your Chocolate Landscapes by collecting sets of Task cards and assigning Mice to them. Use your Power cards to stop your opponent's progress and to boost your chances of success.

You need 3 different Task Cards from the same landmark and 5 mice assigned to them in order to gain a Quest Token. Collect Quest Tokens for completed landscapes to win!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Warhammer Underworlds: Wyrdhollow – The Headsmen's Curse
description:
The Headsmen’s Curse has come. Nothing shall stay the bite of the dread blade Terminus, for it was fashioned long ago by Nagash himself, and has a vile sentience of its own. Damned souls surround it, bound to serve as judge, jury and executioner. Their former identities are subsumed into the greatsword’s necromantic magic; always there is one to wield the blade, one to sharpen its edge, one to bear the headsman’s block, and a scriptor to sentence the condemned. No matter the crime against the Supreme Necromancer, the sentence is always the same – Death!

The Headsmen’s Curse is an aggressive warband, seeking to bring the justice of Nagash to the Wyrdhollow. They'll have to work together to write out the sentence, sharpen the blade, supply the block, and make the killing strike. This set includes a full Rivals deck to use straight out of the box, and the models are all push-fit – so you can get playing in no time.

This set includes:

4x push-fit The Headsmen’s Curse miniatures – no glue required to assemble
4x double-sided fighter cards, one for each fighter in the warband – Wielder of the Blade, Bearer of the Block, Scriptor of the Sentence, and Sharpener of the Blade
1x The Headsmen’s Curse Background/Plot card
1x The Headsmen’s Curse Cover/Rivals deck card
A 32-card Rivals deck consisting of (12x The Headsmen’s Curse objective cards, 10x The Headsmen’s Curse gambit cards, 10x The Headsmen’s Curse upgrade cards)

—description from the publisher

"Pöbbkviss" is an engaging Icelandic trivia game, a phonetic play on the English term "Pub Quiz". The game comes with 1,000 trivia cards, featuring questions from various categories including general knowledge, famous quotes, fivefold (a popular segment in the trivia show Kviss), and three hints. Suitable for gatherings, travel, and parties, it can accommodate an unlimited number of contestants, typically played in teams with one person serving as the quizmaster.

Nine spacefaring crusader states will engage in a zero-sum (nearly) multidimensional tug-of-war for ownership over nineteen keystone worlds, sending ships from their fleets out into the cosmos up to ten times per game.

Any or all of these nine states can be controlled by an algorithm.  As a human player, you must be able to visualize how algorithm-based opponents will behave throughout each nineteen-planet sequence of action resolution and also to visualize how you and your human opponents (if any) can affect the algorithm and send it spinning in a different direction.

(You could also set the nine algorithms against each other to see what happens.)

You will need to balance a healthy supply of ships in your reserves (i.e., NOT on the map) with a strong presence of control flags among the 19 worlds to avoid elimination.

Win by planting your 11th flag, by having 5 fleets in play at the start of a round, or by surviving all 10 rounds and having the highest score.

—description from the designer

description:
Promotional cards from Spiel Essen Fair.
Goal cards to earn extra points.

Krakkakviss is the junior counterpart of the Icelandic trivia game Pöbbkviss. It's designed for players aged 6 and up, and includes 100 cards featuring 300 age-appropriate questions. This engaging trivia game encourages team play and cooperative learning, making it ideal for family game nights and children's gatherings. With Krakkakviss, trivia fun is within reach for young players, fostering their curiosity and knowledge.
family: Versions & Editions: Junior Versions of Grown-Up Games

description:
Become parent of the year!

Rodzice Roku is a game about motherhood and fatherhood, but taken with a pinch of salt. If you don’t have kids, this game will prepare you for the challenges of parenthood. And if you are already a parent, this game will bring back old memories. Those who decided not to have kids can check if their devotion is right. 
The game will take you for a trip over 3 trimesters of pregnancy and you will be collecting baby’s layette. 
Check if you are able to do multiple tasks at once and if you have all the knowledge parents must have. Can you make quick decisions and finally can you draw and sculpt in … poo?
If so, there’s is a high chance that you will become the parent of the year.

boardgame: La Pétoche: une pétanque de poche
description:
It is a pocket version of Pétanque.

Pétanque is a dexterity game that originated in France, but is now played all over the world. You win points by getting your boules close to the jack. Playing Pétanque is a great way to spend time with friends.

—description from the designer

Mysterious disappearances have tensions running high in Deadwood, South Dakota. In the badlands, Ursula’s Worm Cult prepares a macabre ritual. Sheriff Seth Bullock prepares to lead a combined posse of townsfolk and First Peoples to stop them before it’s too late.  Meanwhile, Marshal Bass Reeves is hot on the trail of Jack McCall who is hiding from the ghost of “Wild” Bill Hickok, and Hao T’e Zui’s Righteous Bandits are set to clash with E.B. Farnum and his allies in the Deadwood Miner’s Alliance.  Which of these feuds will be paid off in a debt of blood?

This new Doomtown expansion features 4 copies of 56 new cards, including historical people and places of Deadwood! New rules feature Doomtown’s first dual faction dude as well as Headlines that occur during High Noon.

Following the recent Welcome to Deadwood expansion and Doomtown: Weird West Edition, the Doomtown story continues in Deadwood, South Dakota. Debt of Blood is Pine Box Entertainment’s sixth expansion for Doomtown, (previously Doomtown: Reloaded expandable card game) and the first following Weird West Edition. The expansion is compatible with all previous cards.

—description from the publisher

In the jungle, the animals are always looking for trouble. So, get ready to win this fight, where anything goes! Welcome to Rumble Jungle!
Place matching cards to continue the rumble, if you can't, you will loose it and receive some heavy blows!

EVANGELION:2015 Angel Attack is a trick-taking game in which the player who advances the scenario by making full use of Seele and the Angels and finally carries out the Human Instrumentality Project.

It's basically an orthodox trick-taking game, but the rules are reversed only for the final order.

Making murder mystery games cool again.

The story: It’s December 31, 1999, and the threat of the Y2K bug divided the population into two types of people: those who are panicking and those who are partying like it’s 1999. Jo Jive, the owner of the hottest record label of the decade, just wants to party. Jo invites the most VIP of VIPs to the hip new nightclub, Encore Lemieux, for a potential end-of-the-world celebration. What was intended to be a bougie, totally exclusive affair takes a turn when some uninvited guests arrive, invitation mysteriously in hand.

As midnight approaches and the threat of the Y2K bug draws near, not everyone plans to ring in the New Year in typical fashion. A killer is afoot.

Key suspects: Jo Jive, London Ritz, Nicki Simpson, The Rainmaker, and Reggie P. 11 extra guests are included.

—description from the publisher

Euro-style roll and write game where you'll build your own kingdom, and defend it from fearsome Kaijus.

In this new adventure, players will spend their turns building their kingdom according to their style of play: build villages to gain more points, recruit leaders to create an engine of abilities and extra score, or build shields to improve your defenses.

Each time you play a game of Kaikoro Kingdoms, you can choose a different Kaiju to be the current threat for all players. Each Kaiju will attack and punish players differently, so take advantage of that to create your strategy!

Up to 100 players can play Kaikoro Kingdoms at the same time, but if that's not your thing, each Kaiju comes with its own unique rules if you decide to play solo.

—description from the designer

A 2-4 player tactical hand-building game that gives you loads of opportunities to be clever and outwit your opponents.

You are a Colossus, battling for control of beautiful Environments. You will build hands on 3 different Environments at once because you don't yet know where the next Skirmish will happen. Each player starts with an identical deck, but you can steal / sacrifice cards to create a better deck. Each Environment and Item has special rules that create interesting and unique problems and opportunities. Colossi rewards clever tactics and thinking on your feet.

The first player to win 3 Skirmishes wins the game.

Welcome to Cryptid Cafe: The Chef's Apprentice! This game is set in the same universe as the popular Cryptid Cafe. This time around, instead of serving customers in the front-of-house, we’ll be venturing into the back-of-house, where players will take command of their cryptid line cook with aspirations of becoming a chef someday. However, before putting on that glorious white chef’s hat, they must first become…The Chef’s Apprentice.

Each turn, you and your fellow players will choose monstrous food dishes from the main chef's table board and plate them in your own tableau format. Your placement, preparation, and garnishing skills will determine how well you satisfy the customers' requests and earn their praise. Your cryptid line cook's expertise will also come in handy as you prepare dishes. However, be warned of challenges from the Kraken Chef, who will test your skills and separate you from the pretenders.

The player who earns the most praise from the customers and our resident Chef wins the game.

Are you ready to become The Chef's Apprentice?

—description from the designer

A collection of new adventures for use with The Fantasy Trip Legacy Edition!

This package includes:

One 64-page hardcover book packed with five adventures.
    The Chaos Triads, by Steven Marsh
    The Curse of Katiki-Mu, by Robert Saint John
    Fire in the Temple, by David L. Pulver
    The Clockwork Tower, by Greg Poehlein
    Tomb of the Wizard-King, by Christopher R. Rice

Also inside:

An afterword, with further game notes, by Steve Jackson.
5 sheets of die-cut megahexes & counters, for the adventures.
5 dry-erase treasure cards.
5 dry-erase fighter cards.
2 different 9" x 12" pocket folders you can use to carry your campaign notes, character sheets, and other loose sheets.

boardgame: Sync or Swim: Promo Routine Cards
description:
Available: https://beziergames.com/collections/all-games/products/sync-or-swim-kickstarter-promo-pack
Cards 1-5 for each routine. (25 cards)

5 additional routines originally included in the kickstarter version of the game:


     Splash Showdown
     H2 Open
     Fishy Finals
     Tidal Tournament
     Synchrolympics

Alternate Victory Rules

A player must complete two Victory Conditions in order to win the game.

When a player achieves their first Victory Condition, they place one of their Control Markers on the matching Victory Condition on the back of this card.

The first player to achieve their second Victory Condition immediately wins the game.

Once a player achieves a Victory Condition, they do not loose it if they no longer meet the Victory Condition requirements later in the game.

This version of Mish Mash is the Celebrity Edition.

Unscramble the words to identify a celebrity. The first player to answer correctly wins the card. The first player to collect 5 cards wins the game

boardgame: 九頭竜館の殺人(The Murder at Cthulhu Manor)
description: The day after a seance held in an old mansion. In the back of the basement, the body of a psychic who claimed to have "witch's blood" was found. The suspects are a reporter, a university professor, a painter, a chauffeur, and the owner of the mansion, all of whom attended the seance and have different backgrounds. What shadow will the legend of the "monster that ruled the people in ancient times" that remains in this area cast over the case?
family: Series: Mystery Party in the Box

description: Italy, 1960s. The boss is murdered in the mansion of a mafia family, where a struggle for the heir to the throne is still smoldering. Next to the boss's body, a man is left tied to a chair with a rope. The situation becomes increasingly chaotic as the actions of the boss's brother, wife, and other members of the family, as well as the family fortune teller and prostitutes, become intertwined.
family: Series: Mystery Party in the Box

boardgame: 人狼村の祝祭 (The Tale of Twilight Wulves)
description:
A small village where werewolf lore remains. The morning after an ancient festival, a traveling merchant was found dead. The body was horribly beaten, hair plucked, and throat gutted - as if he had been attacked by a werewolf. The blacksmith's wife had been found dead in the same manner two months earlier in the village. Was this really the work of a werewolf?　Where did the knight sent from King's Landing disappear to?　Why are there two fortune tellers..?

In this genre, each player plays a different character, and each character is given a separate winning condition. Other players' winning conditions are unknown, and after the end of the game, all players who achieve the conditions become winners.
family: Series: Mystery Party in the Box

boardgame: ダークユールに贖いを(Redemption on Dark Yule)
description:
Once every ten years, vampires gather in a town in the far north.
On the night of the great gathering to decide how to treat the humans, an incident was discovered just before the opening of the gathering.
What was found was a charred, blackened corpse. The number two vampire of one of the two opposing factions had been destroyed. Which was the victim?　The higher ranking vampires glaring at each other. The newcomers are trying to solve the case for a hidden purpose. Vampire hunters' traps are hidden in plain sight. And the shadow of an old vampire from the distant past.
Who has achieved redemption...?
family: Series: Mystery Party in the Box

boardgame: 河辺の夜の夢 (A Riverside Night's Dream)
description:
It was the end of the 16th century, just as the reign of Queen Elizabeth (I) was ending. The incident took place in a riverside town in central England. A man who had been staying in an inn was found dead after plunging his head into the river not far from the back door.
The travelers who were also staying at the inn, the local white witch who is well-liked by the locals, and the inn's servants all look at each other with suspicion. Each of them looks at the other with suspicion. What is the truth behind the incident that took place in the hometown where the playwright, who has gained fame, returned to after a long absence?　And what will become of the story woven by those with secrets?

In this genre, each player plays a different character, and each character is given a separate winning condition. Other players' winning conditions are unknown, and after the end of the game, all players who achieve the conditions become winners.
family: Series: Mystery Party in the Box

boardgame: Boss Monster: 10th Anniversary New Cards pack
description: This card pack includes the 18 all new cards added to the Boss Monster 10th Anniversary edition of Boss Monster: Master of the Dungeon (the first Boss Monster game). These cards are NOT needed if you've already purchase or received the 10th Anniversary ed.

A dragon that has appeared out of nowhere roars. The terrified king also roars. The people laugh at the king's cowardice. The king shouts that this is nothing to laugh about. The people just shrug.

"It's my fault for expecting loyalty in the first place! Yes, I'll give you what you want: honor and power to whoever brings me that fat lizard!"

The laughter dies down and tension settles in the room. Some are already gathering their gear. We must hurry! There's only one dragon!

Open one region card. Region cards must be opened from lower-level regions first. If the card you open is a monster, fight it; if it's a place, perform the actions for that place, then open a new region card.

Battles are fought by drafting dice. You use the dice you earn to equip equipment, use skills, and defeat monsters. As you progress through the game, you can use the gold and experience you earn to get higher-grade equipment or learn more powerful skills.

At the end of the battle, pass the line marker to the player who struck the last blow, and proceed with the town phase if any players are exhausted; otherwise, open the next region card and continue the adventure.

Repeat the Village and Adventure stages to grow and prepare for the Dragon battle. Dragons can be found in level 3 locations. For more information, please refer to the rulebook at the link below. Please note that this document is not finalized and the contents are subject to change until production.

boardgame: 神さま日本酒おつぎします (Kamisama nihonshu otsugishmasu)
description:
You are a waiter and you are entertaining drunken gods. However, the situation changes when you enter "Hebereke Mode," in which the cards are flipped over! The gods' pitch goes up and a big surprise happens. Can you entertain the drunk gods well enough to succeed in your mission?

The "Drunken Summit" mode is a cooperative type in which players communicate with each other and line up numbered cards, while the "Drunken After Party" mode is a competitive type in which players play cards that match each other's conditions.
The cards are double-sided, and the "Drunken Mode," in which the cards are reversed, is the key feature. The cards are double-sided, and the "Drunken Mode" is the key to the game, which can cause a major upheaval if used properly.

Six men and women participated in a certain hot spring tour.
Just an unusual tour held in a slightly unusual place,
It seemed that all the unusual people gathered together.
But the hot springs were great and the view from there was exceptional.
The next morning, one of the tourists, "Kirifuri" is found dead.
He died from a fall from a cliff.
Dead men have no mouths to feed. Accidental death or murder? The truth was supposed to be a mystery...
However, one of the tourists happened to be an shaman, a person who can speak the voice of the dead.
And not just one, but all of them seem to be itako.
How is this possible? The investigation begins in a bewilderment.

On board the Blue Swan, a space freighter traveling to a distant planet. Five men and women are forcibly awakened from cryo-sleep. Sirens blaring and warning lights flashing red. What is happening on board this ship?

A space survival for survival begins.

In this genre, each player plays a different character, and each character is given a separate winning condition. Other players' winning conditions are unknown, and after the end of the game, all players who achieve the conditions become winners.

Well shuffled, backs up, the 40 cards are placed in the middle of the table. 19 "male ideals", 20 "role models of real femininity" - and the maiden room for the beauty who loses in the race for the right man. 
In turn, each player takes a card. A gentleman's card and a lady's card always make a pair, one suitable or one unsuitable! This can be argued about and there will also be disputes, which, if necessary, will ultimately be decided by the selected game master.
Whoever has a matching pair reads out the two texts and asks for recognition so that they can discard the pair.
When all cards have been taken from the table, you draw from your right neighbor. At the end, a lady card and the maiden room remain in one hand. That costs a deposit.
The player to arrange the most auspicious marriages receives the award and is the winner.

The game is from about 1925.

—user summary

Space-themed game dedicated to kids. Moving along the way, players will have to collect "astrogiocando tokens" and answer questions about the solar system. Each correct answer will donate a token that can be used to solve one of your objectives and whoever manages to conclude two of them first will be the winner.

The Last Journey is the third episode of the story-driven escape game trilogy, Sunshine Island.

Disguised as a soldier, you sail away from Bornholm, where a deadly fungal disease has spread. A test tube and a memory card with your sister’s potential cure for the disease must be handed over to the researcher MALM. He can complete the job and perhaps stop the extremely dangerous infection from spreading. Unfortunately, not everyone wants you to succeed. Gaia agents are also looking for the material and MALM. Take on a challenging mission in the role of Sigrid.

50 Clues is an award-winning immersive puzzle game that offers the experience of an escape room, but in a format that can be played at home. boardgame: Star Trek: Away Missions – Chancellor Gowron: Klingon Expansion
description:
Klingons are warriors trained from birth to fight. They are deadly and tough, and their weapons are brutal. Few others can stand up to a Klingon in a one-on-one fight.

As a Klingon player, you can’t go wrong by simply getting up close and personal with the enemy. Seek out honor and the death of your foes!

The goal of the game is to collect an army of Noble Animals while Avoiding the Corrupted Animals. Simple, right?

In your quest to become the savior of the forest, you will venture through the realms of Day, Night, and Dream! During your adventure, you will team up with a powerful hero, use magical abilities, steal, and do everything in your power to overcome the evil forces that have corrupted the Spheres of Life!

But remember, although everyone wants to rid of the evil forces, only one can become the savior!

—description from the publisher

Tintas y Mentiras ("Inks and Lies") is a game of bluffing and color creation.

Based on the CMYK color mode, we bluff other players with the three cards on hand (cian, magenta and yellow) to get ink on the used card. If successful we get white counters, if not, we get black stains.

Then we clean the stains and blend colors with the primary inks to get green, blue and red.

All this for the objective of buying points cards on the market, using primary and blended colors. The game ends when a player buys the third card and the current round is completed.

The winner is the player with the most points.

—description from the publisher''

Rock Fall is a strategic word game for one to five players, aged 7+, where players deflect letters into words.

Miners are digging a mine shaft. Rocks are falling. Rocks are encoded with letters.

As the rocks fall and are deflected by planks, they land and sometimes form words from adjacent letters.

Quickly spot words before your opponent to score points.

Game Play:

After randomly inserting deflector planks into the mine shaft, place the rock tiles into the bag.

Players simultaneously drop rocks into the mine shaft. The rocks will tumble and deflect.

Be quick to spot landed words of the same colour.

Call out and score your words.

Play several rounds.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Star Battles: Solar Powered Ships – Solex
description:
Starbattles (Solar Powered Ships) is a compilation of Ship Control Sheet Diagrams and rules for Solar-Power-Ships (or, Sailing Ships of the Galaxies)

It includes 24 CSD's and a 4 Page rules supplement for use with Starbattles (Mainline)
Cursed Manor is a print-and-play escape room-style game in which you help a team of ghost hunters in unraveling the mysteries of a haunted mansion.

Work your way through missions together, with one goal: to play rounds flawlessly. Only when you create a harmonious symphony of colours and numbers will you find that perfect synergy.

Synergy is a cooperative campaign-style game where you play through 30 different missions. The goal is simple: make sure your team wins a certain number of rounds to complete the mission. Action cards might make your job easier, whereas challenges and super and hyper cards will make it more difficult for you.

A round consists of playing two number cards and one goal card. Usually in the order of number-goal-number. Cards are played step-by-step. Cards are always played face down and only revealed once all players have played a card, after which the next card can be played face down. Players have to strategically pick their goals and deduce which numbers other players might play. Once you all have played your final card you will find out if you are in perfect harmony with each other or whether your teamwork needs a little tuning.

—description from the publisher

In this rummy variant, players are astronaut recruiters trying to gather groups of similar astronauts for NASA.  There are 10 sets of 12 astronauts represented by a unique color and symbol, each with a different theme.  The cards are individually illustrated with the astronauts represented in a whimsical fashion (as the astronauts are also arts and crafts enthusiasts).  There is also a set of 10 wild cards and a set of 10 cards with 2 symbols each.

Each player starts with a hand of three cards.  On your turn, you draw a card from the deck or discard pile and either play a card to a group or discard a card.  The first card of a group cannot be a wild or dual symbol card. Afterwards, card symbols must match.  Players may not have more than three active groups, and a group may have no more than five astronauts.  Five card groups are immediately set aside for later scoring.  A player may otherwise forgo their turn in order to set aside an incomplete group.  Once the deck is empty, players get one last turn and the game ends.

The winner is the person with the highest score.  All groups are scored, both in play and out of play.  Single card groups score nothing.  2-4 card groups score 2-4 points respectively.  A five card "astronaut group" scores 10 points.

Chennis is a Kyoto Shogi variant where pieces promote or demote on every move, creating a highly dynamic rally over a tennis court-themed board. Captured pieces may be dropped or "served" back onto the board, as in Shogi, and Kings are restricted to their own court or the net, similar to Xiangqi. Chennis also includes pieces not found in International Chess such as the Cannon and Soldier from Xiangqi, and the Ferz from Shatranj. Drops, alternating piece promotion, and restricted King mobility mean that there are surprising diversity of ways to mate your opponent and win despite only beginning the game with four pieces plus the King.

Tholian Go is a symmetrical two player abstract game where the goal is to surround and remove your opponent's tokens.

The board is comprised of separate dark nodes (connected as triangles) and light nodes (connected in hexagons) overlaid in a geometric pattern.

Triversi is a three-player version of Reversi (Tri-riversi). Similar to the traditional two-player Reversi, you flip the opponent's pieces by sandwiching them with your own color pieces. However, unlike the traditional Reversi with black and white pieces, this version uses red, blue, and yellow pieces. This introduces the thrill of not always gaining points when flipping pieces. Players have the option to place a piece face-down. It is a strategic abstract game.

—description from the publisher

Smart10 is a quiz game. The Österreich edition features questions about Austria.

There are ten possible answers to each question, and every player gets to answer every question. 
If the answer is correct, the player gets an answer marker. 
But should you answer or pass?
If you don't score your answer markers on time (and thereby pass on this round), you can end up losing them.
So if you are not 100% certain of an answer - do you gamble or do you pass?
Plays great in teams.

A group of (male) friends on vacation have to face the usual question: how many girls will they be able to hook up with? Who will be the Califfo, the one who manages to seduce the most and most beautiful women?

Everyone takes his best car, prepares to pay for dinners and bouquets of flowers, makes the most of his qualities of beauty, poetry, sympathy, and even imagines a few lies. And, given that it is a challenge, even putting a spanner in the works of others is legitimate…

Il Califfo is a game in which the protagonists compete for all the free girls who are around for each evening of their holiday, making the most of the qualities of their characters and the resources represented by the cards in their hands. Approaching the most beautiful can be a temptation, but be careful: a girl is not always as charming as she seems at first sight, and sometimes she can hide real surprises, positive or negative!

DISCLAIMER

Il Califfo is a diseducative and politically incorrect game, but any criticism in this sense is rejected as idle by the authors. Most games aim to variously kill and/or massacre your opponent, when it's not about crushing the world under the heel of your own armies. Acting as a Don Juan and collecting women like trophies is not very edifying, but trying to make love with consenting girls is still considered by the authors to be less unseemly than breaking people's heads.

—description from the rulebook (translated)
A limited bonus terrain set (comprising tiles, monster, crystal), offered with preorder of the game directly from the publisher.

Oh! Hey! Crosswalk is an abstract game inspired by pedestrian crossings. The goal is to transfer all five of your pieces from your territory to the opponent's territory.

Each turn, you can take three actions, which can be used for the following:

     Move an Adult/Dog/Senior/Child piece forward by one space.


The pieces have the following characteristics:

     Dog pieces can pass through other pieces.
     the Piece in front of a Senior piece must retreat on the next turn.A Senior piece can only take one action per turn.
     Child pieces can only take one action per turn but can move up to two spaces.


Utilize the characteristics of the pieces to be the first to cross the pedestrian crossing and achieve victory.

—description from the designer

boardgame: The Fabulous Adventure: Who'll save the Princess?

But be careful, you'll need to be smart and stop in time so you don't get too many dragons or other traps. You could lose your points or worse...skip you turn !

Curse Fear is an aggressive version of memory game inspired by Japanese supernatural -SHINREI- themes. Similar to traditional memory games, players flip over two cards from a face-down arrangement and collect pairs with matching numbers and suits. However, unlike traditional memory games, players use the collected cards to inflict curses upon each other on the central game board. The player who accumulates the most curses either by obtaining four curses or at the end of the game loses.

—description from the publisher

Achieve shape objectives and score as many points as possible.

At their turn, the active player must draw and place a Quadri card according to the following rule :
Cover at least 1 and maximum 3 squares of one or more already placed cards. At any time, even if
they are not the active player, a player can achieve one of their objectives.

Quadri offers 3 playing modes :

- Competitive : The player with the most points wins
- Cooperative : All the players must achieve 10 common objectives before the deck of only 20 Quadri cards is exhausted.
- Ball trap : The last player with objectives cards in hand wins.

—description from the publisher

This is a story-telling game that follows the structure of classic dramatic formulas. As a result, the stories created by players will have a complete structure and include exciting conflicts and twists.

The basic game consists of 6 rounds, each representing a chapter of the story, in order:

     Purpose
     Obstacle
     Breakthrough
     Defeated
     Turning Point
     Ending


Each round consists of four steps:

1. Ideation and Card Play
All players brainstorm and think about the plot for the current round's chapter. For example, in the first round, players must state the story's purpose and include one or two cards in their hands.
This step is not round-specific; any player can play his/her cards face down and place his/her marker at any time once he or she made the story in the head.
As soon as three players have played their cards, this step ends.

2. Story-telling
According to the order of play cards, players reveal their cards and describe the content of their story.

3. Vote for the Plot
All players take one of their Admire tokens and hold them in hand, then simultaneously place them on the story they like the most, excluding their own. 
The story with the highest number of votes becomes the main plot.

4. Scoring and clean-up
The highest-voted cards are placed on the Chapter board for this round, reminding everyone of the main plot's content.
The players who told stories received Admire tokens, earning 1 point each.
The players who voted for the main plot receive a Scroll token, earning 1 point each.
Each player can adjust their hand of cards and then discard other cards played this round.

These four steps are repeated for the next round, continuing the storyline. For example, in the second round, players must describe the obstacles the protagonist encounters while trying to achieve their purpose.

After 6 rounds, the player with the highest score wins!
At the same time, you will have created a structured, complete, and interesting story!"

YouBet! is a fast-paced and strategic card game for two to four players, in which short and alternating alliances arise between the players each round. In the end, only one can be the winner. Who knows how to best play the game and their fellow players?

In YouBet!, players form a pile of a total of 12 cards each round. In advance, each player indicates the colour and the number they will play for that round. The goal of the game is to collect enough, but not too many, of your colour and number. However, you only have limited influence on the pile, so you will have to work together to earn the most points. Who you work with varies every round, and sometimes you are completely on your own.
