Recruit, counter, retire, and assassinate to dominate in Deck of Spies, a solo deckbuilding game played with a standard deck of cards.

The top card on the Spy Deck represents a spy that you must outwit. Each turn you must either counter or recruit the spy that is face up on top of the Spy Deck. Optionally, you may be able to retire spies from your hand in addition to your counter or recruit action. If things aren’t going well, you may be forced to assassinate your target.

To win Deck of Spies, at the end of the game you must have more than half of the Key Spies (J,Q,K's) in your deck.

Every player gets a pawn. Two dice are needed for the game. Anyone who has reached space 91 may only use one die.
The players take turns to roll and move.
If you come to stand on a space that borders the lower part of the nine, you can move to the upper part of the nine.
However, if you land on the upper part of the nine when rolling the dice, you have to go back to the lower part of the nine.
If you come to a red number, you have to go back to the next red number. If you stand on OGA, you have a free throw.
Whoever reaches space 100 first wins the game.

Take on the role of a queen bee, guiding your dedicated bees towards victory in a thrilling quest for nectar. Strategize to collect nectar from the blooming field of flowers to turn into the most Honey Points, ensuring your hive's survival as winter approaches. ??

Honey Hop is designed for players who love engaging and strategic gameplay. With its unique bumping mechanic, you'll navigate the colorful game board, outmaneuver opponents, and cleverly solve challenges to create honey. Diversity in nectar color leads to creating more Honey Points (e.g. Victory Points). This captivating board game offers a perfect blend of simplicity and depth, appealing to both casual players and seasoned strategists.

Gather your friends and family, for Honey Hop accommodates 2 to 4 players, allowing you to enjoy the game with your loved ones. Inside the captivating box of Honey Hop, you'll find meticulously crafted components. From 20 beautifully illustrated hexagonal tiles representing the lush flower field and your hive, to the 8 adorable bee tokens, 4 player boards, 6 frame pieces, 36 nectar pieces, 40 honey pieces, 9 month tokens, 1 Calendar board, 1 month marker, 30 cards, 1 rain marker, 1 crown piece, and 1 rule booklet. Every detail has been carefully designed to enhance your gaming experience. Immerse yourself in the world of Honey Hop, where every move counts and every decision can lead you closer to victory.

Will you strategically navigate the field, collect the most nectar, and claim victory? Discover the joys of this unique and captivating board game, and let the adventure begin!

boardgame: G.I. JOE Mission Critical: Vehicle Pack #3
description:
Four new Vehicle cards bring more firepower in the fight against Cobra.

Includes: Conquest X-30, Hammer, Mauler MBT, and Rolling Thunder.

Ahoy traveler! Welcome aboard the Shadow of Endurance, a replica ship based off Ernest Shackleton's pioneering vessel. We're setting sail for the Antarctic waters - hope you brought a camera and attentive gaze. This adventure will be full of animals that breach the ocean surface.

As seafaring guides, we like to up the stakes from time to time. Every trip includes a contest that challenges passengers to spot animals along the way and log them in a ship journal. Creatures earn different points based on rarity, from common species like Krill and Crabs to uncommon species like the mighty Blue Whale. The passenger who logs the highest scoring animals will have contributed the most sightseeing value to the trip, and thus earns the title of Breach Master.

Obtaining this title will not be easy! Animals don't surface predictably and your shipmates are looking for sneaky ways to claim your discoveries for themselves. It's all light-hearted fun until we enter the depths of the deck, where sea monsters dwell and environmental degradation is seen.

Think you have what it takes to win the battle of the breach? It's time to play Splash Down!

—description from the designer

Scars of Baladorne is an immersive and strategic board game that takes you on a journey to the world of Baladorne. In this game, players assume the role of commanders, leading their armies into intense battles for control over the realm. With a focus on tactical movement, players strategically position their units, utilizing their unique attributes and abilities to gain the upper hand.

The game revolves around turn-based gameplay, where players command up to four units per turn. Each unit can perform primary and secondary actions such as moving across the board, launching attacks on surrounding enemy units, or unleashing special abilities. The goal is to eliminate the opponent's captain, while carefully managing your units' health and damage points.

Scars of Baladorne is suitable for both experienced board game enthusiasts and newcomers alike. The combination of unit attributes, action choices, and the strategic use of utility cards adds depth and variety to each game. Prepare for battle as you make critical decisions that will determine the outcome of the war for Baladorne.
Players command armies, strategically position units, and unleash unique abilities in intense battles to eliminate the opponent's captain and claim victory in the war for Baladorne.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Heroes of Normandie: War Stories #01 – Blue and Grey at Moon-sur-Elle
description:
Five days after D-Day, on June 11th, 1944, the men of the 29th Cavalry Reconnaissance Troop were ordered to maintain the liaison between the 101st U.S. Airborne and the 175th Infantry Regiment (29th Infantry Division) near Sainte-Marguerite-d’Elle.

They were then sent to scout the roads leading to Moon-sur-Elle. Strong opposition awaited them and the fighting was fierce. It was critical to current operations to establish an observation post in the village, so it had to be taken, regardless of the cost!

This War Story requires the Heroes of Normandie: Big Red One Core Box + Battle Pack #01: Bloody Omaha.

boardgame: Heroes of Normandie: War Stories #04 – The Black Baron
description:
“Medals do not make soldiers“. At only 23 years of age, Kurt Knispel, a Sudeten German, became the most successful tanker of the whole Second World War. He was working in the automobile industry before being drafted and taught to handle a Tiger I. He was assigned to the then 13. Panzer-Division and sent to Russia. Later he commanded a Königstiger in the now 12. Panzer-Division in Normandy.

In October 1944, he left for Hungary to take part in Operation Debrecen. On April 28, 1945, one day after his promotion to Feldwebel, Knispel was seriously wounded in the head. He died two hours later in a military hospital near Vrbovec.

Kurt managed to destroy 168 tanks! Nicknamed “The Black Baron“, he truly became the ace of aces of the Wehrmacht tankers. But then, why didn’t he have the recognition of a Wittman, or an Otto Carius? Because Kurt didn’t give a damn about Nazism, nor its honors or medals. That, combined with his unkempt appearance and modified uniform brought about the tale that he never received the Eisernes Kreuz and was thus dubbed “knight without a cross“.

This is pure myth, as he received the Eiserne Kreuz I. Klasse in 1943 and the Deutsche Kreuz in Gold in May 1944.

This War Story requires the Heroes of Normandie: Big Red One Core Box and Battle Pack #02 – Battle for Caen: Epsom, Charnwood, Goodwood.

—description from the publisher
family: Series: Heroes System Tactical Scale World War 2

description:
Castaways is a party game that is inspired by the popular reality TV show 'Survivor' and combines social deduction, card drafting mechanics and superior social skills as players attempt to take their secret castaway character to the end and win the title of The Ultimate Castaway.

The arrival of Daily Mail and Camp Life cards causes uncertainty and chaos in camp (and around the table) as teams deliberate how to best play the hand dealt to them.

And then off to the Challenges we go! The challenges are totally random, and the choice of line-up (and who to sit out) can determine the final outcome.

The losing team will make their way to the Outcast Tribunal where one Castaway will be voted out of the game.

Not so fast! Tribunal Twist cards and personal advantages are real, and when played will induce a crazy tribunal experience.

Once down to 10 castaways the merge is on the horizon, bringing with it a shift in the game. At this stage, players now need to look out for themselves. And be confronted with the big question - stay true to your alliance or backstab and blindside?

boardgame: DC Deck-Building Game: Legion of Doom Expansion Pack
description:
Banded together from remote galaxies are thirteen of the most sinister Super-Villains of all time: the Legion of Doom! This expansion pack adds 13 Legion of Doom Super-Villains based on the classic Challenge of the Superfriends TV series to any DC Deck-Building Game base set and comes inside a collectible box with foil cover.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Legendary: A Marvel Deck Building Game – 2099
description: This new set expansion allows players to recruit the futuristic heroes of 2099 to battle against the high-tech enemies of the future. The set adds 5 Heroes, 2 Masterminds, 2 Villain groups and 4 Schemes. The Heroes in this expansion include Spider-Man 2099, Ghost Rider 2099, Doom 2099, and more, and all cards included in this set feature original art based on the Marvel Comics. The game box comes with 100 playable cards and a rulesheet.

'Sandbox style card game with a high level of complexity and "take-that" elements.'

Building upon the core elements of "Shields up!", this card game puts you and your opponent in the unenviable shoes of Chief Engineers - pushing their starships to the absolute limit, in a tense space battle to the death.

Players will have to manage their respective ships' four key systems (Reactor, Life Support, Shields, and Weapons) and implement cunning (and warranty-voiding) modifications to these systems, hoping to push the envelope just enough to destroy their opponents ship without wrecking their own.

A simple pen and paper game for two players. Beginners should use a 3x3 grid at first, so just draw a tic-tac-toe board and you're ready to go!

The game starts on an empty board. Before the match begins the players must choose a set of numbers equivalent to the number of spaces on the board. Since a 3x3 grid is 9 spaces you could use 1-9, 2-10, or 3-11 for example. The most popular sequence so far has been 2-10. To play you take turns writing a number from the set in an empty space. Each number can only be used once. The match is over when the board is full.

One player's score is the product (remember, product means multiply) of each row added up and the other player's score is the product of each column added up, so make sure you decide which player is which before you start. Highest score wins. Draws go to whichever player DOESN'T move last.

Use the pie rule for balance. Some opening moves are strong, and some are weak, try to figure out the most balanced options. The pattern of winning and losing choices is interesting.

For a co-op game try to create draws! Draws are extremely rare and difficult to create.

—description from the designer

In this thrilling board game, you step into the paws of mischievous chef raccoons on a culinary quest to open the most sought-after eateries in the forest.

Each round consists of a gathering phase and a feeding phase. In the gathering phase, chefs may dig through 2 trash cans for ingredients by flipping over food chits. They have 30 seconds to gather as many ingredients as can fit in the bowls at their food stand. In the feeding phase, chefs draw patron cards and attempt to satisfy the unique food requests at the bottom of each patron's card. At the end of 5 rounds, the chef who has served the patrons with the highest total points claims victory for their restaurant!

With its whimsical theme, unique gameplay mechanics, and strategic decision-making, Raccoon Restaurant is a thrilling culinary adventure that will keep you on your toes!

—description from the designer
Drawing card game.

Blue is the colour, football’s the game. Take charge at Stamford Bridge and lead Chelsea to the promised land. The faithful are holding out for someone to make them compete for the title again, someone to show them there’s a Blue Day on the horizon. Is it you?

—description from the publisher

Schnapp die Möpse Memo+ is a memory game for the whole family.
Become a pug snatcher and collect as many matching pugs as possible. The player with the most collected pairs and the most pug points wins the game.

You can play the game in two different game modes: Normal and Plus.
The optional Plus mode offers additional abilities when collecting pairs.

—description from the publisher

Each round in Get the Ick the active player is presented with three distasteful situations that other people might do, such as:


     Seeing them write "hehe" in a text.
     Holding their nose while jumping into a swimming pool.
     Walking really slowly.


The active player secretly chooses one of these situations as the thing that most icks them out, then everyone else tries to guess which situation this is. While guessing, however, the other players can secretly mark a situation as something they actually enjoy, scoring bonus points if others don't guess their "secret attraction" ahead of the tiles being revealed.

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: 2023 Roll to Move Goody Box
description:
A limited edition goody box available on the web store during Roll to Move 2023.

This Shadows of Brimstone goody box includes:


     Raijin Storm Lord Roll-To-Move2023 Miniature with 40 mm black plastic base
     Storm Hat (P-1022)
     Fashionable Top Hat (P-1024)
     Skull of Artesh (P-1023)
     Crumbling Stone Encounters Set (5 cards, P-1017 to P-1021)
     Broken T-Junction Map Cards (Spanish Fort/Jargono, P-1015 and P-1016)
     Broken T-Junction Map Tile
     Crumbling Stone Counters

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Haunted by Darkness Game Supplement
description:
Exploring the dark mines and stepping through portals to terrifying OtherWorlds can haunt a Hero that just set out looking for fortune and adventure. As the Darkness grows, and the dread of unspeakable horrors that lurk in the Void reveal themselves, it is difficult not to bear the scars of reaching too deep into the dark.

This 15 card Game Supplement for Shadows of Brimstone features all new Darkness and Growing Dread cards, themed for the Old West! With effects that often haunt (remain in play on) a particular Hero for the rest of the Adventure, these new Darkness and Growing Dread cards add fresh danger to your missions.

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Helmet of Shinjaro
description:
A promo Forbidden Fortress Artifact card first given out to Dicefest 2022 attendees, but later available as part of the 2023 Roll to Move webstore sale for orders over $100.

Forbidden Fortress Artifact - Clothing - Hat

One Darkstone

Once per turn you may reduce the damage you would take from a single hit by 2. Requires Strength 3 or higher to use.

1 Weight, 1 upgrade slot, $550.

Promo 455

A promo Forbidden Fortress Artifact card first given out to Dicefest 2022 attendees, but later available as part of the 2023 Roll to Move webstore sale for orders over $100.

Forbidden Fortress Artifact - Clothing - Hat

Once per fight, prevent up to D6 wounds you are about to take. For each use, roll a D6 - Discard on the roll of a  1.

1 Weight, 1 upgrade slot, $350.

Promo 456

boardgame: A Touch of Evil: Dark Gothic – Terror in the Night Game Supplement
description:
When the cold sun sets and the shadow of night falls across the land, terrors comes out to stalk the forests and streets of Shadowbrook. As townsfolk go missing and rumors spread, it quickly becomes clear that this brooding evil is stealing folk for a purpose, and no one is safe in the dark!

This 15-card Game Supplement for the ''Dark Gothic'' Deck Building Game is themed for the Terrors that stalk the night. It inlcudes a set of new Terror in the Night Minions, as well as new Events, Gear, Locations, and Allies.  This supplement utilizes the new Roaming mechanic first introduced in the ''Colonial Horror'' Expansion.
family: Game: A Touch of Evil: Dark Gothic

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Swarm Pack #2 – Slashers
description:
An Enemy Swarm Pack is a new themed set of cards focusing on fighting swarms of a particular Enemy Type, as well as a new set of Spawners for that Enemy Type. In this case, the pack focuses on fighting rampaging swarms of hungry Slashers and has a Feeding Burrow that spawns new Slashers during a Fight.

This pack also includes Enemy Record Sheets for the OtherWorld variants of Slashers (Swamp Slashers and Gorged Slashers, as well as two new OtherWorld variants... Larval Slashers and Slashers of the Deep), with the stats and modifiers worked into the Enemy Record Sheet itself, as well as new themed Elite Charts and Brutal sides for these variants.

A new Mission, Feeding Frenzy, allows the Heroes to go on an adventure to hunt down the burrow of these beastly creatures that are rampaging through the area.  This set assumes you have access to the Slasher Enemies (miniatures and cards) from the ''Swamps of Death'' Core Set and does include an additional set of 6 Slasher miniatures, giving you a swarm of up to 9 of these savage beasts.

This set contains miniatures for 1 XL Feeding Burrow as well as 6 large Slashers.

The Feeding Burrow is a new Enemy introduced in this set, that acts as a Spawner for Slashers. A large rocky outcropping of Dark Stone, it is the ideal burrow for a nest of Slashers that bury themselves in the ground, feeding on the black rock. At the end of every Turn, more Dark Stone markers are added to the Feeding Burrow, until there is enough to spawn a new Slasher, consuming the Dark Stone as it erupts from the ground! This creates a new goal within the Fight for the Heroes to battle their way to the Feeding Burrow and destroy it before you are overwhelmed by a flood of Slashers. This can be dangerous though as the Dark Stone Radiation of the black rock can add up fast. The Burrow itself though can be a good source of some extra Dark Stone for the Heroes.

As the nuclear winter spreads through all the continents, obliterating the planet’s ecology, you realize that it’s not safe to be by yourself, raising a toddler.

You pack what little food you have left, a few clothes, and open the hidden entrance of the complex for the first time in... has it been 5 years already?

Road Behind is a survival, risk management solitaire game, in which you and your child travel south along a winding road, avoiding dangerous people and inhospitable places, rummaging for food and shelter among disaster, looking for a latitude where the disaster hasn’t reached, on which you, and especially the kid, can start a new life.

Managing the risk of events along the road is a critical part of the game. The other is choosing wisely between comfort and progress, when to hide, when to rest, and when to push forward towards the end of the road... and safety.

[GAMEPLAY]

Each turn, you will:

     Perform actions.
     Draw new action cards.
     Consume food.
     Update weather.
     Affect morale.
     Reset shelter.
     Review inventory.


Additionally, whenever an Event is triggered, it must be resolved immediately, before continuing the turn.

You use actions to move on the road, use an item, rest, or perform special actions like rummaging, taking shelter or hiding.

You win the game if you reach the last card in the road deck, and after applying all its icons you have at least 1 KID action card left on your action deck, hand or spent pile.

—description from the designer

Dragon in the Hut is an action-packed, portable, and easy-to-learn card game with a pinch of strategy, a splash of rivalry, and two drops of bluff. And dragons, obviously.

Players take turns drawing cards, trying to find dragon eggs in the forest and build an hut as a safe and warm place for their scaled companions to be born and raised.

Take an egg, put it in a hut, and keep it safe until it hatches to get a shiny new dragon.

The first player to hatch three or more dragons wins.

—description from the designer

Oh no! Your doggie has lost all his bones. Help him find them and bring them back to his dog house. Be the first to help your doggie collect all his bones and you win! Get them nose down to a bone and watch as they magically pick it up with their magnetic noses!

This law of attraction will help your doggie win the game. Learning to play this game will be way easier than teaching your dog to fetch. Place all the bones color-side facedown in each dugout space around the board, place the doggies in their respective kennels, and roll the die. Choose a direction, and hop as many spaces as the die indicates.

Use your doggie’s magical nose to pick up the bone where your doggie landed. If the color of the bone matches the color of your doggie, put it in his/her kennel, if not, return it to the dugout space, and let the next player test their luck. Once players fetch all three bones, their pup is very close the victory bark. Just make sure he/she runs back to the doghouse before the others catch up. Diggin’ Doggies is a great indoor game to play with the family or a few friends.

It teaches counting, color recognition, hand-and-eye coordination, following game directions and taking turns, all while your family is having fun. Updated version of International Playthings Award Winning Diggity Dogs.

—description from the publisher

Krakkaleikar is an engaging and lively charades-style game designed for groups of friends or families. The game consists of 500 cards with various prompts that players must act out, with actions ranging from impersonating a vampire to walking like a sleepwalker. The aim is to perform the prompt on the card in such a way that your team can correctly guess what you're enacting. Its easy-to-understand mechanics and diverse prompts make "Krakkaleikar" a fun game suitable for all occasions. The call to action is simple: "One, two, action!

Stalin’s First Victory (SFV): Covers the five-day campaign that determined the outcome of the 1929 Sino-Soviet War.
A low complexity two-player grand tactical game at 1.25 miles/2 km/hex, and one day turns. Units are battalions, companies and regiments.

22 x 34-inch game map with both battles; Stalin’s First Victory 48 5/8-inch counters; Taierzhuang 128 5/8-inch counters.

Taierzhuang / Stalin's First Victory were both published in World at War #91, June 2023

(description from the publisher)
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
It is 1918. The Republic of Poland regains its long-awaited independence. Unfortunately, the country is in ruins. After the tragic death of your uncle, you inherit an old vodka factory. Her condition is deplorable. Luckily, the market is in good shape. Poles want to have fun, and above all drink! Your goal is to make your business successful. Attention, other industrialists want to take your place. The one who produces the best vodka wins. 
Pół litra is a medium-advanced and fast (about 30 m.) "worker placement" game in a print and play format. The aim of the game is to produce the best vodka by running your vodka factory.

—description from the designer

Rada nad zlato ("Priceless Advice") is a semi-cooperative trivia party game in which it is important to demonstrate not only knowledge, but also good judgment of opponents, tactical skills and the art of bluffing.

900 questions await the players, divided into 20 topics - from the classic (history) to the unusual (mysteries). Players are secretly divided into two teams. Only some players will reveal their team affiliation.

Throughout the game, players will select topics and questions for their opponents (or teammates), advice them in with their own answers, and also use special effects to turn the game in their team's favor. The aim of the game is to score more points than the opponents.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
You are among one of the leaders of the Nabataean people. Guide them as they build Petra during its era of prosperity. Build great monuments, grow frankincense and myrrh, build prosperous trade routes, and journey across the lands of ancient Jordan. Players will do all of this on a shared map in this civilization building roll & write game.

Players will build Petra by drawing on an expanding shared map, as opposed to traditional Roll & Write games with individual player sheets.

This game uses push your luck mechanics, asymmetric player abilities, dice drafting, and variable scoring to produce an engaging, fun, strategic and highly replayable game.

—description from the designer
family: Components: Dry Erase Markers / Dry Erase Surfaces

description:
You are the most powerful of ancient magicians and shapeshifters, but for a thousand years you have waited, sealed inside a meagre brass lantern, lost under the sands of time. A group of ardent archeologists have now uncovered your magical prison, and want to use your powers to grant them power and riches beyond belief. Polishing your lantern they have awakened not only your slumbering spirit, but your powerful cleverness and guile. Will you be able to grant these foolish mortals their hearts desires while outplaying them at their own game? Success for you means freedom from endless years of servitude and a return to earthly pleasures. Or is your fate sealed, like the burnished brass lantern which has been your solitary cell for centuries?

Djinn Rummy is a card game based on classic rummy depicting the battle of wits between a Genie and mortals. Mortals attempt to lay down runs and sets to score points and empty their hands of cards. The Genie plays differently, by playing a run from 1 to 10 (symbolizing the thousand years they have spent imprisoned), trying to empty their hand first. However, the Genie may take cards played by mortals by granting them "wishes" that possibly increase their chances to score and to win. If the Genie wins, they score alone; if any mortal wins, they score alone; but if a Genie grants three wishes to any mortal, that mortal and the Genie score, excluding all other mortals. At the end of each round, the Genie appoints a mortal as the new Genie to hinder their chances of winning.

—description from the publisher

Mozkovna: Společenská hra (Brainworks: The Board Game) is a fast-paced trivia game that exercises all parts of the brain in 5 categories.

The challenges that must be completed within a given time limit are divided into logic, graphic, text, knowledge, as well as those for perception and memory. In the game, it is possible to bet on your opponents - on their success or failure - to gain additional valuable points.
 The goal of the game is simple - to score as many points as possible by correctly solving the puzzles and properly betting on your opponents.

Space Aces: Landing Party is a fast-playing casual card game for 2-3 players that is quick to pick up, challenging to master, and plays in just 10-30 minutes. Combine two decks to play with up to 4 players.

Premise: It is the 23rd century and the golden age of galactic exploration has begun. As proud members of the Stellar Alliance, your crew has been sent out on a mission of discovery. But you are just one of many crews, and everybody wants the prestigious honor of discovering new planets. Set your phasers to “stunning” as you deploy a daring cast of Crew Members & Special Guests for a fun-filled episode as you outwit your friends to boldly go and get there first!

Cards: 18 standard crew members & 3 cadets make up the core game, and 8 included special guests can be mixed in to open up new avenues of strategy and tactics. The fast-paced play and infinite replayability will keep you and your friends coming back for "just one more" to settle the score.

Landing Party is a small game of deep strategy. Each player starts with 7 unique crew member cards on their starship and 1 crew member hidden in their ship's transporter buffer. Each player takes turns beaming down a crew member of equal or higher rank to take command on a newfound world. The last player able to beam down a crew member wins the mission for their crew.

Designed to minimize luck and richly reward skillful play. Since all the cards in play (except for 2) are known by all players, the game becomes one of evolving tactics and strategy. The highest ranked crew members are not necessarily the best. Each crew member and special guest has a unique special ability. Mastering when & how to use these abilities is the real secret to success.

There's always an interesting choice to be made! Will you try to crush your opponents with high ranking Officers? Or will you try to draw out their low cards and trap them with a clever Secret Agent? Will you use your Tactician to force an opponent to play that key crew member too soon? Or will you sacrifice your Red Shirt to revive a stunned character in a shocking turn of events? Do you keep your crew member's rank or do you stun it to activate its special ability? The possible strategies and tactics are endless!

Stuck in a bind? Activate your Starship and fire phasers to stun whatever character is in command on the planet. But each player can only do this once, so use this ability wisely!

The beauty of the game comes from the deep interactions between the crew members and their special abilities as well as reading your opponents and guessing their strategy. The more you play, the more tricks & tactics you will discover. And since each round only takes about 10 minutes, there's always time for one more!

—description from the designer

Grand Prix Re-Run provides a quick and clean way of replaying seasons of Formula One, producing accurate and realistic results and offering the excitement of Qualifying for each race, it takes about an hour to play each race.

Driver and car combinations have different potential and varying degrees of reliability both in terms of breakdown and crash propensity. The driver/car combinations are each given a Seasonal Rating reflecting their performance over the season, together with Crash and Reliability Ratings.

The meteor strikes have caused great damage. We’ve gathered as much wreckage as possible so please use the salvage carefully. Complete the primary objective and build the planet that will preserve humanity. Expand your developments onto the Supermoon in case the meteorite hazards get out of hand. Planet Unknown is a polyomino tile-laying game that mixes tile-placement, engine-building, simultaneous play, and resource and rover management for a unique and competitive gameplay experience. Supermoon is the first expansion.

SUPERMOON SUPERPUZZLE
A new decision point has been created. Place your tile on your moon or onto your planet. The moon comes equipped with a 3d satellite mini to help clear meteorites in a unique way. You could even choose to deploy a rover on your moon. Tile selection, tile placement, and meteorite cleanup will be the key to mastering this new puzzle.

EXTENDED GAMEPLAY
The net effect of the Supermoon expansion will be games that take roughly 5 simultaneous turns longer, extending your gameplay from ~65 minutes to ~80 minutes. This extension allows new strategies to emerge and players must challenge existing assumptions about Planet Unknown retain to their winning edge.

SALVAGE TILES
A supply of salvage tiles will be available to each player. Each salvage tile is a split polyomino, salvaged from the recent meteor showers, and always has one resource and one meteorite. Instead of drafting from the S.U.S.A.N., you may choose to place any two of your salvage tiles placing them together or apart.

NEW PLANET AND CORPORATION
With the recent meteor shower, a new corporation has assembled from the wreckage. Mix and match the new planet and corporation content to create even more asymmetric pairings in Planet Unknown.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
A strategic, competitive, 2-player game to print and play. Win the war of espionage using both luck and strategic skills. Two world powers on the brink of war send out their spies and armies to obtain secret information and strike in strategic locations.

Each player has a map with a 5x7 grid, a character token, 3 army tokens and one die, also a coin is needed. Each players secretly notes the location of their spies and secret documents. Be the first to retrieve the secret documents and uncover the enemy spies.

Roll your die to move around the map, find the documents, uncover the spies. The game rounds are divided into three phases, and each phase is played simultaneously by both players. Players must start and end each turn at the same
time. You can active secret documents to influence your abilities, move around the grid and place armies and fight with them by flipping a coin. The first player to uncover the 3rd enemy spy wins!
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Deep Shelf: United Reclamation – Faction Expansion
description:
The United Reclamation: Faction Expansion adds an entirely new fifth faction to the game with its own unique play style.

United Reclamation is a highly mobile faction that utilizes a second submarine, has superior scouting capabilities, and the ability to use slag and raw ore to create cheap refurbished facilities.

—description from the publisher

Keyflower: Talton Lodge is a new village tile for the game Keyflower.

Talton Lodge is being published to celebrate co-designer Richard Breese’s marriage to Penny Gray in September 2023 at Talton Lodge and will be available at Spiel in October 2023.

Talton Lodge is located in the middle of the Cotswolds countryside in England, amongst 34 acres of parkland, woodland, orchard, and garden.  The Festival Marquee – the giant tipis shown on the tile – are situated in the grounds.

When using the Talton Lodge tile, score 1 point (or 2 points if the tile is upgraded) for each active keyple and waving keyple icon on the player’s other tiles in their village. These active keyples are the keyple icons located in the top info panels on the un-upgraded side of the village tiles and in the (only) info panel in the upgraded side of the village tiles, the summer boat tiles (including boats 4a and 4b), and the relevant turn order tile.

For example, if the Fair tile was in the player’s village and the player also owned the Talton Lodge tile, then the Talton Lodge tile would score 2 points for the Fair, or 4 points if the Talton Lodge tile was upgraded.  If the Fair tile was upgraded, then the Talton Lodge tile would score 3 points for the Fair, or 6 points if the Fair was also upgraded.

—description from the publisher
Build combinations of jumps to eliminate your opponents, but be careful because those same combinations can be used against you.

You're about to become a magician with a magic hat and a magic wand, you're a magician who can make pictures disappear! But only the first person to grab the wand token can perform the trick! Will you be able to guess the words from the erased pictures?

Catch Sketch: Magic Hat is the expansion of Catch Sketch with new variant rule!

Idols Clash is a strategic card game that combines building and deduction elements with the pleasure of collecting fanart illustrations inspired by the K-Pop imaginary. 
The game is designed for 2-4 players for a duration of 30 minutes and is suitable for an audience over 14 as it tackles the severe and extremely important issues of mental health, burn out and exploitation of competition among young people in the K-Pop Industry. The game comes with trigger warnings about these topics. 
It ranks among statistical games as the element of luck affects some actions (such as direct clashes between players that occur through the roll of dice and random distribution of cards). It is rebalanced through the adaptation of game strategies based on the placement of players on the board game and the goals to reach.

—description from the designer

The harvest is over, leaves fell and the sun, bathing our lavra in gold, is waning. Soon, cold wind will come from over the Dnieper and blanket the plains of Kyiv with snow. There are only a few things left to do – to finish the central fresco on the ceiling and to cover the main iconostasis of our monastery with holy icons.

Players will take the roles of gifted monks which carry on their shoulders the weight of a near-impossible deadline, as well as their own physical degradation, exhaustion, and laziness.

—description from the designer

Taco Football Woman World Cup ! Keep this crazy string of words in mind. As soon as there is a match between a card and a spoken word, race to slap your hand on the central pile of cards. The last person to do so must take them all! Be quick to get rid of all your cards, but watch out! Your mind will play tricks on you. This addictive game is as unique as its name!

—description from the publisher
family: Series: Taco Cat Goat Cheese Pizza

boardgame: あわてんぼうのサンタクロース (Awatenbou no Santa Claus)
description:
The player is a frazzled Santa Claus who has arrived there on the 24th.
He left his watch at home, so he measures 24 hours by feel and places presents.
Who will be the first to place a present on the 25th?

Each player decides his or her own color and holds a Santa card of each color and a card for 2, 4, or 8 hours. Put one of the Santa cards in front of you so that you can recognize your color.
After all players take out their cards, the game starts at midnight on the 24th and performs the following processing in order from the first card issued.
If it is on the 24th, it will be advanced by one hour.If it is the first present after midnight on the 25th, the player who placed the present will win.

In Wake players move their sailing boats upwind two cards at a time. As the biggest gains and losses in yacht racing are usually on the up-wind legs, this is what Wake models.

Cards are played face down simultaneously. Then flipped to determine player order. But a player does not have to use the card they played, for a card to be added to a boats wake (always two cards long) it must not be less than the sum of the two cards already in the wake. If a player is unable to add a card to their boats wake, they may "bank" it face down for additional value in a following turn.

The first boat to round the windward mark is the winner.

Wake is a free to print n play game.

—description from the designer

In Fields of Arle, created by Uwe Rosenberg, one to two players live as farmers in the small and peaceful town of Arle in East Frisia. The flax grown in the land surrounding the village makes it a profitable place to work and live. Fields of Arle takes players through four and a half years of this era of prosperity, with different opportunities available as the seasons change. Farm the land to capitalize on the demand for flax, or find other ways to make the most of the small town’s prosperity.

This Big Box version includes the "Tea & Trade" expansion.

description:
This special edition of Krakkakviss takes players on a global journey, making it a vibrant trivia game for kids interested in the world around them. It covers a wide range of topics such as countries, cities, food, languages, travel, and much more.

The game encourages competitive play where players can hold their own trivia competition or challenge friends to see who can answer the most questions. Sample questions include: "What important document do you always need to bring when traveling abroad?", "What is the most common name in the world?", and "What does the Spanish word 'gracias' mean?" This edition of Krakkakviss provides a fun and interactive way for children to learn about the world.

—description from the publisher (translated)
family: Versions & Editions: Junior Versions of Grown-Up Games

description:
This special Christmas edition of Krakkakviss is an entertaining trivia game that delves into all things related to Christmas and various interesting facts that captivate children. The game includes 100 cards with 300 questions designed to get everyone into the holiday spirit. Sample questions include: "Which Yule Lad has a peg-leg?", "What are Grýla candles made of?", and "What two words can be found on most package labels?". Players can engage in their own trivia competition or challenge friends to see who can answer the most questions, making "Jóla-Krakkakviss" a fun and festive way to enhance knowledge about Christmas traditions and facts.

—description from the publisher (translated)
family: Misc: Advent / Christmas / Jule calendar game
Versions & Editions: Junior Versions of Grown-Up Games

description:
This version of Krakkakviss is an interactive trivia game designed for children with a keen interest in football. The game encompasses a wide range of football-related topics, including renowned football clubs like Liverpool and Manchester United, notable players, championship records, and tournaments like the World Cup and the European Championship. It also features content about the Icelandic national team and the Champions League. The game allows for individual play or can be enjoyed in a competitive format where players or teams challenge each other to answer the most questions, making it a fun and engaging way to expand knowledge about football.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Continuing the success of the popular Icelandic trivia game Krakkakviss, comes Krakkakviss 2, a trivia game for children that spans topics from all over the world. Designed for players aged 6 and up, the game includes 100 cards featuring 300 diverse questions. Sample questions include: "Which animal travels at night and sleeps upside down?", "From which country do pizza and pasta originate?", and "Who says 'Hakuna Matata'?" The game promotes team play and cooperative learning, making it a perfect choice for any occasion, from family game nights to children's gatherings. With Krakkakviss 2, trivia fun continues to engage young players, further nurturing their curiosity and broadening their knowledge.
family: Versions & Editions: Junior Versions of Grown-Up Games

description: Krakkakviss 3, the latest addition to the popular Icelandic trivia game series, is tailored specifically for younger audiences. This engaging trivia game comes with 100 cards containing 300 questions that cover a broad spectrum of topics suitable for any occasion. Inquisitive prompts such as "What is a super glue?", "Which girl has superpowers in the animated film Encanto?", and "What sport is Ping Pong?" inspire friendly competition and team play. Players can host their own trivia competition or challenge friends to see who can answer the most questions. Perfect for family game nights or children's gatherings, Krakkakviss 3 continues the tradition of making trivia fun and accessible for young players.
family: Versions & Editions: Junior Versions of Grown-Up Games

description: Pöbbkviss 2, the sequel to the highly successful Icelandic trivia game, is an entertaining choice for friend gatherings, family events, road trips, and parties. After the resounding success of the first edition in 2020, Pöbbkviss 2 brings 1,000 brand-new questions from various categories, including the addition of a new segment called "fivefold," which has gained popularity in the trivia show Kviss. The game accommodates an unlimited number of players and can be played in various ways. In the traditional version, players form teams, with one person taking on the role of the quizmaster. Teams write their answers on paper, and the team with the most correct answers at the end wins.

Ti Lo Chi is an abstract area control game with perfect balance, open information and a dash of luck.  Claim victory by being the first to control 15 tiles!

Each player has identical deck of 15 glyph tiles and 15 matching crystals.  You play with your hand of 3 tiles face up.

There are 3 phases to each turn; reveal, play, discard.
On their turn, players reveal by flipping over 2 additional tiles in to their hand so that they have five face up tiles.  The player then selects a tile to play adjacent to one or more tiles potentially gaining control of them.  The player places their crystal on the newly played tile and additionally replaces any opponent’s crystal with one of their own on any captured tile(s).  Their turn ends by selecting one of their 4 remaining face up tiles to be placed face up in to a discard pile.  Play continues clockwise with the next player repeating these steps.

—description from the designer

boardgame: B.R.A.V.E.: 勇士候補生寮対抗不統一試験
description:
A competition among 13 students, with each dormitory having its own special characteristics!
The first round is for first-year students, the second round for second-year students, and the third round for third-year students!
The first round is for first-year students, the second round is for second-year students, and the third round is for third-year students!

This is a single player card game in which the story is told by flipping cards.
It is a prequel to Hunter, an adventurer included in the Dorasure Basic Set.

Players read the text on the cards, decide the path to take, and sometimes make decisions as they read the adventure. Different endings are reached depending on the player's decisions and luck.
How will the record of your adventure end up!

boardgame: アリスと謎とくらやみの物語 (Alice to nazo to kurayami no monogatari)
description:
This is the story of Alice, somewhat after she has finished her adventures in Wonderland.
The main character is Alice, of course, and the setting is probably Wonderland.
The reason why I say "probably" is that everything in front of you is pitch-black, and you cannot even see your feet.
Alice, who was supposed to be sleeping in her bed just a moment ago, has wandered into the land of darkness.

She begins to walk fearfully in the darkness, but soon she has a means of communicating with "someone".
Encouraged by his or her words, Alice regains her smile and sets out to escape from the land of darkness.

You already know the story, don't you?
Yes. That "someone" is you.

You are the only one who can save her from being left in the darkness.
Now, let's solve the many mysteries together with Alice and reach the truth of the Land of Darkness!

To play this game, you will need a mobile device (smartphone or tablet) that can use LINE Messenger.
You will have to communicate directly with Alice and guide her.
By giving her the right instructions and letting her explore the land of darkness, you will discover many things.

The latest in the popular Icelandic series, Pöbbkviss 3 is a thrilling trivia game designed to provide entertainment for a broad range of social gatherings. Riding on the significant success of its predecessors in 2020 and 2021, this edition presents players with 1,000 completely new questions across diverse categories, including general knowledge, famous quotes, "fivefold," and the intriguing 'three hints' category.

Suitable for an unlimited number of participants, Pöbbkviss 3 maintains the tradition of its series, typically being played in teams with one person acting as the quizmaster. The game is flexible, supports various gameplay styles, and is guaranteed to challenge and engage trivia lovers in equal measure. Whether you're on a road trip or at a party, Pöbbkviss 3 promises a compelling blend of fun and knowledge-sharing.

You and your friends just finished robbing some weirdo, with a green cap, and a bag full of rupees.  Now you've got to figure out how to split the loot.  Being the upstanding bandits that you are, you decide to play a little game for keeps.

Rupee is a bag stealing game where you can be mean, nice, and always get your cut.  Players choose to spend rupees to complete objectives for points or use abilities to nab even better rupees for more points.

The game ends when a player draws the single, precious golden rupee in the entire game.  However, being upstanding bandits, it goes to the player who completed the most objectives.  Will you score high or wide?  Be kind or cruel?  Sabotage the bag or practice some alchemy?  Whatever you do, make sure you get the best share of the loot!

—description from the designer

Designed for the participants of the 2022 West Coast Rumble, these six scenarios are designed for minimum set up time, resulting in maximum playing time.

Q26 – Encounter at Le Valtru, 16 June 1944: The 2.SS-Panzerdivision Das Reich was forced to delay its counterattack on the British penetration west of Caen in order to deal with the British 49th Division’s advance south from Rauray. This delay allowed the 9th Cameronians to occupy Grainville and the 7th Seaforth Highlanders to advance to the hamlet of le Valtru in effort to secure the right flank of the now named “Scottish Corridor”. Before the Seaforths could reach le Valtru, they were hit by Kampfgrüppe Weidinger of 2.SS-Panzerdivision Das Reich.

Game Length: 5½ Turns, Board: p, Estimated Playing Time: 3 hours 21 minutes

Q26 – Holzthum Hold Out, 16 December 1944: For the opening phase of Unternehmen Herbstnebel (Operation Autumn Mist), Oberst Heinz Kokott’s 26.Volksgrenadierdivision had one of the toughest tasks in that it had to clear the roads west of Our River for the panzers of the 2. and the 116.Panzer Divisionen. Crossing the Our in rubber boats at 03:00 hours and infiltrating their way past the American outposts, Oberstleutnant Kaufmann’s Füsilier Regiment 39 had reached Holzthum at 06:00 hours before anyone in the US 28th Infantry Division sector was even aware an attack was in progress.

Game Length: 5½ Turns, Board: 12, Estimated Playing Time: 2 hours 28 minutes

Q27 – Stand at Sterlin's Castle, 8 December 1943: By 0600 hours, the 1st Canadian Infantry Brigade had crossed the Moro River and established a bridgehead; supported by engineers and armour, the Canadians then launched attacks on San Leonardo. The Germans retreated and San Leonardo was at last secured, but counterattacks directed at the fragile bridgehead held by the hard-pressed Canadians were the order of the day.

Game Length: 5½ Turns, Board: 9, Estimated Playing Time: 2 hours 4 minutes

Q28 – Going in Light, 9 June 1944: The immediate objective for the newly landed US 2nd Infantry Division was the village of Trévières, located just south of the Aure River. The plan of attack was for the 38th Infantry Regiment to strike directly at Trévières from the north and west, while the 9th Infantry Regiment would outflank Trévières by seizing Rubercy to the southeast - thus isolating the German position. It was no accident the Germans had chosen the tiny farm of Haute Hameau to make a stand against the approaching 9th Infantry. The old Norman farm with its stone wall courtyard formed an ideal defensive position, commanding the neighbouring country lanes running south in the direction of Rubercy.

Game Length: 5½ Turns, Board: 43, Estimated Playing Time: 2 hours 48 minutes

Q29 – Como Ridge, 22 July 1945: In late 1944, the Australian Army had assumed responsibility for Allied operations on Bougainville, replacing US troops who were subsequently redeployed to the Philippines. Following the failed landing by the Australians at Porton Plantation, the commander of the Australian II Corps, Lieutenant-General Stanley Savige, decided to focus the main effort of the Australian campaign upon driving south towards the Japanese garrison at Buin, where the bulk of the Japanese forces were holding out.

Game Length: 5½, Board: 36, Estimated Playing Time: 3 hours 7 minutes

Q30 – The Tombe Diversion, 1 July 1943: The initial plan for the capture of Viru Harbour called for a diversionary attack by Lieutenant Brown’s platoon on the small village of Tombe located on the east side of the harbour while the main body, under Lieutenant-Colonel Currin, would then attack the village of Tetemara on the west side of the harbour.

Game Length: 5½ Turns, Board: o, Estimated Playing Time: 2 hours 24 minutes
Lastuf Futbol is a card game for 2-4 players where you will have to score goals against your rivals to eliminate them. Do not let others score against you and be the last one standing!

The player takes on the role of a novice engineer and drives a single-car locomotive around the planet.
The locomotive is unfamiliar with driving and doesn't follow the expected path.
You arrive at other planets unexpectedly.

If you arrive on a planet other than the Moon, you can either board passengers or build a train.
Trains must be built in order to have many passengers on board,
The more passengers you have on board, the more points you will receive later.

In addition, you can also get bonuses written on trains during other players' turns.
After a certain amount of trains start to accumulate, there will be friction between conductors.
If conductors meet in the same area, they can threaten each other with a red passenger,
players will gain and lose passengers in that situation.

As you travel around the planets, you may encounter space pirates.
Space pirates can steal trains, so be careful!

The first player to drop off enough passengers on the moon to reach 30 points is the winner.
the first player to reach 30 points is the winner.

Matarnia is an epic board game about a group of adventurers trying to strike the final blow in a boss raid. While there is a common goal of defeating a single raid boss, each player's real goal is to land the "final blow" and claim all the rewards.

On each player's turn, they draw 2 cards from the character deck. After checking both cards, he or she places one card underneath the boss card without revealing it, and the other card to one side with the boss card revealed. The area under the boss card is called the party recruitment zone, and the area where the remaining card is placed is called the party waiting zone.

Character cards are divided into normal and special classes, with special classes being treated as two normal classes and should be left uncovered when placed in the Party Recruit Zone. However, depending on the boss card's abilities, you may need to keep special class cards covered as well.

On the other hand, instead of drawing cards when it's their turn, players can also keep the cards in the Party Recruit Zone to themselves and decide whether to keep them in the Party Recruit Zone or send them to the Party Waiting Zone. This can be used to confirm that your own calculations are correct, but it can also be used to disrupt your opponent's calculations.

If you think you can defeat the boss with the character cards in the Party Recruitment Zone on your turn, declare "Defeat". At this point, all cards in the Party Recruitment Zone are revealed. The boss card describes the boss's power, how much power each class brings to the party, and the boss's effects.

The game ends when one player captures the boss, when all other players are eliminated and only one player remains, or when the character deck is exhausted. If a player successfully captures the boss, award that player 2 victory tokens, and if only one player remains, award that player 1 victory token. If the deck is exhausted, no one gets any victory tokens.

The round is then repeated, and the player with the most victory tokens (3 or more) wins.

Players must select their heroes and use their skills to defeat their opponents each turn, based on a set master of a given skill. It's also important to figure out which master they are and defeat them.

There are four faction cards in Valentine. Players of three to six are randomly assigned an affiliation card and must fulfill their role throughout the game.

Once you're aligned, you'll pick a hero to recruit each turn. You start by picking your own hero, and then pass the rest to the player next to you. This is psychologically strategic, as you have to make your picks while trying to anticipate what your opponent will pick.

Once all players have chosen their heroes, they are called out in turn, starting with number one, and the battle begins. You'll need to use the compatibility between your characters to make predictions and attack the enemy.

As each player progresses through the game as a Heroic character, they take hits to their HP and gain access to unique abilities through their affiliation.

Crosscut is a two-player abstract strategy game where the goal is to be the first player to make a horizontal or vertical line spanning the board. The game is played on a square board. The pieces never stay on the perimeter squares of the board, so on a 10x10 board, the goal is to make a line that is 8 pieces long. This also means that it is possible to play the 10x10 game on a Chess board.

This expansion is a Kickstarter exclusive.
Additional Tactic, Scheme/Tactic, Gladiator, Champion and Arena cards

5 Champion cards
15 supply cards
11 Arena cards

Requires For Glory Base Game and For Glory Champions Expansion for play.

Brewery is a competitive eurogame board game.

The game takes place in 6 rounds, each round is divided into two main phases: the placement phase and the resolution phase plus a maintenance sub-phase where employee salaries and restoration are paid.

During the placement phase, the players take turns placing their workers, while during the resolution phase, starting from the first player, he chooses which action to resolve and a benefit is obtained in order of majority. Each action can only contain 4 workers who, identified by the placement order, will be able to resolve the action. During the Placing other workers can be added in the same areas to increase a player's influence. Players with more influence will get more and better effect from the action they are resolving. Resolving some actions will give resources to the player. There are 4 resources available: water, hops (aromas), malt & barley (cereals) and yeast + a special resource for fermentation.

The dominant player, i.e. the one with the most influence or, in case of a tie, the one who placed first will get all the resources of one type (resources are distributed unevenly), and so on everyone will choose what to take from every action where it is present.

The resources obtained from the resolution of the places as well as for the production of the beers are also used to buy more workers and to pay their salaries as $ currency. Buildings function much like buildings in other placement games. Some sponsors give permanent benefits while breweries can be used every turn by placing one of your workers on them to brew beer. As for stocks, having more influence will be beneficial and dominant players will get cheaper workers or get the best resources sooner. The game contains no money, so players will have to be careful when combining resources because if they overpay, no change will be returned. Players accumulate points when brewing beer from factories and hiring new workers. At the end of the 6th round, the points are subtracted for each empty space on the board, then the player with the most points is declared the winner with the most value in resources left in the warehouses!

—description from the designer

Örlagaspil is an engaging card-based party game, ideally suited as a drinking game for players aged 20 and older. Players take turns drawing cards and performing the actions stated on them. The first player to accumulate a sufficient number of "likes" is declared the winner. The dynamics of the game and the destiny laid out by the cards determine the number of sips a player needs to drink throughout the game.

The game features five types of cards:


     “Who is most likely to...?" - Players decide who among them is most likely to do the action described on the card.
     "Never Have I Ever" - Players admit whether they have done the action described on the card.
     "Pressure" - The player must answer a potentially challenging or revealing question.
     "Who am I?" - Players must guess the identity or concept described on the card.
     "Fate" - These cards likely include random, unpredictable actions or events.


Designed and promoted by popular Icelandic influencers and podcast hosts “Spjallið”, Örlagaspil comes in a neat box and is divided into five categories. Players can choose to include all categories or select their favorites for gameplay. This game is designed to enhance any party experience and can accommodate 2 to 10 players, or even more. As the game's tagline suggests, Örlagaspil makes a good party better​.

boardgame: The Dragon Prince: Mystery of Aaravos Booster Pack
description: This expansion adds new cards to the deck of each character in the base game. Two new cards are added to each character deck, adding powers and abilities from the fourth season of the show.

“It’s like a Saturday morning cartoon for Reservoir Dogs." —Eloquent Playtester

The Queen's Game begins! Who will be the last one alive?

Doom Room is a casual, chaotic, battle royale party/strategy game where everybody’s trying to kill somebody, but nobody knows who!

You and the other Prisoners of the Royal Jail must survive as long as possible while everybody tries to eliminate each other.

Improvise to survive as you face constant change, backstabbing opponents, and players coming back from the dead to get their revenge. And if you take too long, the Queen will join the game and kill you all herself!


    Quick-turn, best-of-three games that never play the same way twice
    All the fun of a party game in an easy-peasy strategy format
    Simple actions and difficult decisions that ages 10+ can learn in minutes
    Constantly changing conditions create crazy card combinations!


—description from the designer

Monsters Deck is a collection of 24 tarot-sized cards, each with a monster for Four Against Darkness.
You may use these whenever you would roll on a table to determine which Vermin, Minion, Boss or Weird Monster you encounter. Instead of rolling, draw a card from the appropriate deck, play it and then reshuffle it into the deck. You can combine monsters decks together, expanding the variation of encounters with every new set released.

Paladini Paladini is played on a hexagonal board (4, 5, or 6 spaces per side). Depending on the board size chosen, each player has 3 to 5 paladins. The first to be played is the "captain" paladin. The others can be played later from the supply.

On their turn, each player must place a new hexagonal tile on the board, paying attention to its orientation, and then place a paladin on it, either by moving it from another tile (if possible) or by playing one from the supply.

There are four types of hexagonal tiles. Each paladin placed on a knight, archer, or crossbowman tile can move in three directions (knight) or two directions (archer and crossbowman) in a straight line, for a number of hexes of their choice (but without moving beyond other tiles). The movement lines depend on the type of tile and the orientation chosen when placing it.
On the other hand, a paladin placed on a squire tile can move in all six directions, but only one hex.

To move a paladin already on the board, the new tile must be on a hex within reach of the paladin. If the played tile is unreachable, a new paladin must be played from the supply. Under no circumstances may the new paladin be on a hex reachble by opposing paladins.

A paladin can attack one or more enemy paladins. An attacked paladin must move or it will be eliminated.
When a player runs out of moves or loses their captain, the opponent wins the game.

—description from the designer

The object is to become a “Cultural Expert”.  The first player to collect one card from each category wins the game. Second place goes to the player with the most cards.

There are 5 card categories: Do’s & Taboos, Superstitions, Food & Drink, Holidays & Festivals and Customs.  Each category has it’s own color. The cards are removed from the box and placed in stacks according to category within easy reach of all players. Each player takes two cards from each category and replenishes their hand throughout the game.

The youngest player (player #1) starts the game by rolling the die. The die has 5 colors which match the cards and one wild side where the player can pick the category. The color on the die indicates the card category to be read. 
Light Green = Superstitions
Dark Green = Holidays & Festivals
Orange = Food & Drink
Dark Blue = Do’s & Taboos
Light Blue = Customs

Players do not read their own card, rather, the person to the right of the player looks at the two category cards in their hand that match the die. They strategically select the more difficult card to read to player #1.  A cultural fact on the card is read along with 3 country answer choices. Player #1 gets one chance to select the correct country that the card applies to (the answer is on the back).  If the payer #1 selects the correct country they win the card.  If they guess incorrectly, the card goes to the bottom of the deck. End of Turn. Play continues clockwise.

Example: 
Player #1 rolls and the die lands on dark blue. The player on the right of player #1 reads a Do’s and Taboos card from their hand. 
The card reads:

“Importing or selling chewing gum is illegal. There are high fines for improper disposal of gum.

Switzerland
Afganistan
Singapore  X

Player #1 gets one chance to guess Singapore as the correct country.

The game has an optional “Challenge Round” in which the player calls out “Challenge Round” before rolling the die and must guess the country without being read the 3 answer choices.  If the player is incorrect the turn ends.  If they are correct, they get to keep the card and roll again, but they must again answer the correct country with out hearing the answer choices. If they are wrong, they loose the card they previously won and the player to the right can try to guess the country.  Whoever gets it right gets the card.

The far future. The First Space Republic gathers an expedition to colonize the Andromeda galaxy. With representatives of all races, the intergalactic ship Ark sets out to explore new planets.
In the game Fly Further players will have to colonize different planets. Each has its conditions for victory points.
Discovering a new planet, players will secretly decide - to build a colony or a mine on it or to fly further to restore their energy. All 18 races of colonists have their special abilities, and the more effectively you use them, the more chances you have to win!

In this party game for 3-6 people, ages 16+, you choose a hero and give your character a name.

Each round, one of the players becomes the leader of the round and reads out a quest to be filled with the name of your hero and a character or item from the hero forest!

The gap in the text must be filled in as amusingly as possible so that the round leader determines your card as the winner of the round!

The winner is whoever has collected the most quest cards at the end or the hero who has reached a certain point limit that you have previously agreed on.

boardgame: Mindreader: Der telepathische Partyspaß mit den WIRKLICH heißen Fragen
description:
In this part game for 3-12 players, ages 16 and up, the players have to guess the answers to questions from the categories "Sexy Time", "Criminal Funny" and "Fun and Facts".

Another player draws a card, reads one of the two questions out loud and writes down their assessment. The other players assess what the questioner answered. Whoever is closest gets a point.

On the way to the market, the little hedgehogs learn where their food comes from. Can 1 to 3 children find the right ingredients for their favorite dish? Pay quickly and pack everything in the backpack. The player that acquires the ingredients first wins.

The game includes two difficulty settings.

This Dune: House Secrets bonus pack contains component upgrades and bonuses.


     3 new chapters - envelopes 1 to 3
     5 wooden consequence tokens
     black cloth bag for consequence tokens
     4 character cards: Arya Khoras, Daniel Arias, Javed Dhar, Gora Mallick
     5 large-format postcards in B5 format (176 × 250 mm): Tel Gezer, Tel Gezer: Owl Market, Ter Gezer: Ornitopter Airport, Tel Gezer: Morgan's Tavern, Tel Gezer: Sorc's Mansion
     large-format map of the northern polar region of Arrakis, A3 format (297 × 420 mm)


Pre-order English edition originally limited to 1500 copies.
family: Game: Detective – A Modern Crime Board Game (Portal Games)

description:
The Battle of Waterloo is perhaps the best known battle in history. The time is 18 June 1815. The Duke of Wellington's armies are drawn to defend the road to Brussels. Napoleon's superior French forces begin their assault. Can the British thin red line hold until the Prussian army arrives to fall upon Napoleon's flanks?

A simple introductory wargame.


This edition contains both the base game and the Advanced Game Expansion Kit
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
CAN YOU ESCAPE FROM THIS ADVENTURE?

You are Vincent Lenoir, Agent Alpha, one of the time travelers recruited by the Escape Time agency. Over the course of your missions, you have discovered that Escape Time's indentions are malicious and you intend to expose it to the light of day. But the agency is aware of your betrayal... Quick, it's time to flee before they silence you! Discover a breathtaking adventure in which you will have to observe, manipulate and solve puzzles (encrypted alphabet, labyrinth, deduction, logic, etc.), just like in a real escape room.

All the equipment to play like in an escape room!

In So Many Clues you will have to cooperate to find the answers. During the game you will have to work out how the 20 clues are connected. Be careful, it’s easy to go down the wrong track. As you figure out the clues you will discover which five answers the clues are pointing to - hopefully before your opponents.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Hvem gennemskuer først alle ledetrådene og finder svarene?

Det er nemt at se forbindelsen mellem ledetrådene Rogn, Skæg og Nytår, når I ved, at I skal finde en fisk. Men kan I finde svarene i fem forskellige kategorier, når de mange ledetråde er blandet sammen?

I So Many Clues kræver det overblik, viden og samarbejde at finde frem til svarene. I løbet af spillet skal I finde ud af, hvordan de 20 ledetråde hænger sammen, men pas på, for det er nemt at komme på vildspor. Efterhånden som I får styr på ledetrådene, vil I gennemskue, hvilke fem svar ledetrådene peger på – forhåbentligt før modstanderholdet.
Sådan har I aldrig quizzet før!

—description from the publisher

The War Chest tournament kit comes with a set of rules for running different styles of tournaments, as well as as series of new royal coins, control markers, seals, and an initiative marker as prizes for players to customize their game. These depict the Bear and Stag factions and can be used as an alternative to the base game components. Gold and silver colored versions of the coins allow players to secure bragging rights based on their finishing position!

—description from publisher

The mountain border of the kingdom has been conquered, on the other side an unoccupied new world! You have been granted a royal charter to settle this new world, expanding your duchy in the name of the king. Unfortunately so have your rivals, and only the most successful among you will keep his new lands. Explore, build and settle to become the strongest duke.

Duke’s Domain is a worker placement, resource management, exploration game. The goal is to have the most victory points after 10 turns. Victory points are earned through constructing building and exploring the map.

Players start with a village and 3 workers on the edge of an ever expanding map. Earn resources each turn by placing workers on resource tiles. Develop resources to increase the yield of a hex. Manage your resources well to continue developing your lands. Explore the adjacent lands to uncover more resources and earn victory points. Hire more workers to develop and explore more. Fund your efforts through building villages and towns.

—description from the designer

A Solo Roll and Write WWII Wargame, which is easy-to-learn and simple-to-play solitaire gaming experience where you play as a B-29 Bomber heading out on bombing rais in Japan.  All you need to play this warbook are 2 six-sided dice, a twenty-sided dice, a copy of the main game sheet from this book, and a pen or pencil.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
A promo card for Biomos available to Ulule backers and during some events. 
Add the card to the Giant Biomes deck. This card is harder to fulfill but is worth 12 points instead of the 10 points of every other Giant Biome.

Monsters Deck is a collection of 25 tarot-sized cards, each with a monster for Four Against Darkness. Monsters Deck - 2 contains all the classic monsters from the core rulebook adapted for all levels with the Highest Character Level mechanic (HCL). Use the highest Level in your party plus the bonus shown for the monster’s Level. Use these cards whenever you would roll on a table to determine which Vermin, Minion, Boss or Weird Monster you encounter. Instead of rolling, draw a card from the appropriate deck, play it and then reshuffle it into the deck. You can combine monster decks together, expanding the variation of encounters with every new set released.

Monsters Deck is a collection of 24 tarot-sized cards, each with a monster for Four Against Darkness.
You may use these whenever you would roll on a table to determine which Vermin, Minion, Boss or Weird Monster you encounter. Instead of rolling, draw a card from the appropriate deck, play it and then reshuffle it into the deck. You can combine monsters decks together, expanding the variation of encounters with every new set released.

Meet our friends: Coco the Monkey, Loco the Giraffe and Tuko the Toucan. They all live on a sunny island. They love to play together and pick coconuts. Today, they want you to play with them. But what is that? Oh no, a huge wave is coming! Can you help Coco, Loco and Tuko move all coconuts to the mainland before all of the islands get flooded? Players roll dice and move into islands to collect coconuts. You need to complete 8 coconuts to win. Do it before all islands are gone!

Micro Titans: Wrestling is a card game like no other, capturing the excitement, surprises, high spots, twists, turns and emotion of a pro-wrestling match. Season 1, MicroMania, is heavily influenced by the 1987—1992 golden era of professional wrestling. It will deliver the base game, a ten character strong roster and four additional game modes spread across multiple, fully loaded, game changing expansions.

Bezzerwizzer Sport is a trivia game in which you will have to use your knowledge and tactical skills.
The game contains 1200 questions that will challenge the knowledge of your friends, family and yourself on everything from athletics to soccer and racing to the olympics.

—description from the publisher (translated)

BEZZERWIZZER Sport er et quizspil, hvor du skal bruge din paratviden, dine taktiske evner og naturligvis din bedreviden. Spillet indeholder 1.200 spørgsmål, der vil udfordre dine venners, din families og din egen viden om alt fra atletik til landsfodbold og fra motorsport til OL.

BEZZERWIZZER Sport Kategorier: Atletik, Boksning & Motor, Cykling, Dansk Fodbold, Holdsport, Håndbold, International Fodbold, Landsholdsfodbold, OL, Singlesport, Vandsport og Vintersport.

—description from the publisher

Trilaterum is a fast-paced tabletop miniatures game, set in a unique and expanding universe where emerging factions battle for survival on the edge of the universe!
Highlights include

– integrated turns

– D8 system

– Company Level Army Combat

– a unique firing sequence that rewards intestinal fortitude

– Stands take damage markers that will accumulate over time, causing decreased readiness and eventual loss of stands

– Full army creation system to allow any figures to be used with the system

To play, just check the recipe, and spin for what you'll need to bake up perfect cupcakes, satisfaction guaranteed.
Even though it's make believe, it's splendid what you'll learn. Try not to spin the rotten egg, or you will lose a turn!
Be first to gather what you need and you will be a winner, and an expert cupcake maker, without spoiling your dinner!

—description from the box

Monsters Deck is a collection of 24 tarot-sized cards, each with a monster for Four Against Darkness.

You may use these whenever you would roll on a table to determine which Vermin, Minion, Boss or Weird Monster you encounter. Instead of rolling, draw a card from the appropriate deck, play it and then reshuffle it into the deck. You can combine monsters decks together, expanding the variation of encounters with every new set released.

Thermodynamics Workers is a roll-and-move game which takes place on a 2D graph of volume x pressure. Each cube a player has on the graph represents a different system, and the goal is to be the first person to collect 80 Joules of work when your systems increase in volume.

House of Stolen Goods in a push your luck card game in which players are trying to acquire goods in three different colors.  Each color has some cards worth points, as well as a thief, and a counterfeit work.  There are also some colorless cards, such as a safe and an appraiser.

On a player's turn, they reveal 1 to 3 cards from the deck and can choose to take them or discard them.  Under some circumstances, the player is obliged to discard them.  A player will only have 3 piles to sort cards to in front of them.  Alternatively, a player could also take the entire discard pile to occupy one of their piles.

The game ends when all but one player has acquired 3 piles.  The player with the most points wins.

boardgame: Stars, Galaxies and the Big Bang
description:
In Stars, Galaxies and the Big Bang, the players are creating galaxies, adding stars face down to a series of galaxies. Once a black hole card is drawn or one galaxy has nine stars, the round ends.  Players take turns drafting galaxies, revealing the cards and hoping for the lowest valued sum. Pairs of the same number stars are removed prior to calculating the sum.

The first player to 6 points wins.
publishers: 東京なかよしデザイン (Tokyo Nakayoshi Design)

description:
Beware the balance of shifting power in this fast-paced card battler. Will you select the right heroes and villains to defeat your opponent and take control of the Heroverse?

Heroverse: Battleforce is a fact paced 2 player card game in which you will face off in a series of battles with the Controlling Power Source changing from battle to battle. The game has alternative rules for 3-4 players. Each game takes about 5 minutes to play.

This deck consists of 48 character cards in 4 Power Source categories:


     Blue/Combat-based characters
