     Yellow/Technology-based characters
     Green/Energy-based characters
     Red/Magic-based characters


Each game consists of 2 rounds. Each round consists of 4 battles, played 3 cards at a time. The player with the most cards at the end of both rounds wins.

The Heroverse: Battleforce card game was created in 2003 and premiered at the inaugural New York Comic Con in 2006 at the Heroverse booth.

The 20th Anniversary Edition features new character art on 20 cards including 3 brand new illustrations by Eisner nominated artist Ryan Benjamin, created especially for this edition. Other changes include new special case power mechanics and balancing from the original game, and 6 bonus cards that can be swapped into the game.

—description from the publisher

Do you have what it takes to become Best Gardener? Liven up your game night with Garden Wars, a light-hearted, quick play, "take that!" tabletop card game of strategy and defense.

Your goal is to grow the most bountiful garden in town, while undermining the progress on your rivals. To win you must:

     Plant a diverse set of Crop cards with stacking bonuses.
     Defend your land from invading predators, disease, weeds and nosey neighbors with Expert and Block cards.
     Become a force of nature to take down your opponents with Attack cards. 
     Avoid disaster from flood, locust and drought with your trusty Almanac.


Standard gameplay is for 2-4 players, with expanded "4 Season" rules for 4-6 players. Play time is 30-60min.

Garden Wars is perfect for family game night or a quick round at the bar. The whimsical artwork and boisterous gameplay is sure to bring a smile to your face. Plant, grow and win!

—description from the publisher

In Critical Cut, the players take turns cutting a structured deck of cards and collecting the card they cut to. Points are awarded for finding sets of the same number, sequences of numbers, and flushes.  Bonus points are awarded for ranks which match a players' birth month or kings.

In Animal Duet, players aim to collect pairs of cards. The game uses a mechanic where players choose to either reveal a card from the deck into a row and then take another turn, or collect all revealed cards.  If a revealed card matches a card already in the row, a player busts and takes only the newest revealed card, ending their turn.

In Animal Duet, all face cards and jokers are treated as jokers and when taking cards from the row, if there is a joker present, the player takes all cards on either side of the joker and the joker itself (rather than the entire row.)

This is a game in which you invite cats by giving them fish.
Cats will often come to the cat who gives them fish, but you can't always give fish to the cat you want to give.
You may attract cats with their favorite food such as bonito flakes and silver vine.

The fish card is given to the cat sitting on the cushion with the number that comes out from the dice roll. The one whose total of the two fish cards is larger than the other's is the one that beckons the cat.
The player can invite the cat, but only half of the cards are known how many fish cards the other player has played.

This is a game where you can enjoy not only bluffing in conversation and psychological warfare, but also logical thinking by calculating backwards from the disclosed information!

boardgame: ラドラボ -Dr.ウーノの放射線研究所- (Radiation Laboratory)
description:
This game is an educational card game created with Dr. Tsunoyama of the Radioisotope Research Center, Kyoto University.

While having fun playing the game, you can learn about the basic knowledge you need to know now: the relationship between types of radiation and shielding.

This game is a radiation-themed card game for two players.
Select the appropriate card for each type of mysterious creature "radiation" and "block" it!
Sharp tactics and speedy development of whether you should play or draw a card are hot!

boardgame: 손맛 전자 빠른 수리 (Handmade Electronics Quick Repair)
description:
Handmade Electronics Quick Repair is a game about a part-time employee of "Handmade Electronics" who fixes televisions by beating them with his hands.

Prove that you have a 'touch sence' by arranging the parts in order faster than anyone else!

A card game that combines push-your-luck and action recall mechanics, inspired by Sorting in programming.

FOR THE FIRST TIME EVER simulate the intricate social webs of Pigeon East London (East Pigeon London). Every game another pigeon day in the eternal struggle for the next pigeon day, in this satirical and true-to-life card game.

50 REAL PIGEONS, each vital to their community and cause, are fighting the ideological battle of their little bitty pigeon century. Ranked on essentialising categories from Audacity to Cunning to Punctuality in Trump Card style, the game of Pigeon skewers modern tropes and archetypes

SPECIAL PIGEONS CAN CHANGE THE GAME IN A CLUCK, So don’t forget your reading glasses. They could determine the outcome of the intellectual struggle for the future of pigeon kind.

THE GAME OF PIGEON could be your "worst nightmare", or the "perfect present" for *that* friend. One or two Pigeons are somewhat foul-beaked with their language, and may not be suitable for little squabs!

FUN FOR GROUPS from 2-6, Pigeon speaks to anybody with an interest in card games, design, or anybody struck with an urban fascination for our most ubiquitous yet invisible bird.

FOLLOW THE RULES or CREATE YOUR OWN

THE REVOLUTION DOESN'T END

—description from the publisher

SquAlma is an abstract strategy game for 2 players of at least 8 years of age, with an average playing time of 20 minutes, aiming to create a path connecting both players’ back rows. It is played on a square board, consisting of 64 squares, 8 on each side. Each player is assigned with discs of either black or white colour and alternate turns, moving a disc of their colour following a set of rules, until victory condition is met.

—description from the designer

HexAlma is an abstract strategy game for 2 players of at least 8 years of age, with an average playing
time of 30 minutes, aiming to reach the opponent’s back row. It is played on a hexagonal board,
consisting of 61 hexagons, 5 on each side. Each player is assigned with discs of either black or white
colour and alternate turns, moving a disc of their colour following a set of rules, until victory
condition is met.

—description from the designer

HexCore 6 is an abstract strategy game for 2 players of at least 8 years of age, with an average playing time of 35 minutes, where players try to create the longest path/biggest group connected to the ‘’Core’’. It is played on a hexagonal board, consisting of 91 spots, 6 on each side. Each player is assigned with discs of either black or white colour and they alternate turns, following a set of rules until victory condition is met.

—description from the designer

Simon Scarrow's Eagle book series takes us back to the times when Claudius was emperor in Rome, beginning with the invasion of Britain, through the adventures of his characters Macro and Cato.

With this expansion of ONUS! you will experience first hand, the battles of the books, commanding Roman troops or enemies of the Empire, from skirmishes of a few men to large armies, even playing alone!

—description from the publisher

The B63 is a construction division of the Wiener Linien, the Vienniese public transport systems, responsible for maintaining and repairing the infrastructure of Viennas public transport. A worker had the idea tomcreate a board game to honor the often overlooked but vitally important work of this division.

Management really liked this idea, but the game she created was not yet a polished product. So they gave the job to Arno Steinwender and Ronald Hofstätter, who were already experienced as a design Team and they created a quick and fun game with tactical and memory elements under the White Castle games umbrella.

The game was given to workers in the B63 division as a christmas gift, and could be bought at ticket counters in Vienna for a limited time.

In Shards of Infinity, players must strategically tailor their decks, acquire new cards, and utilize allies and champions to reduce their opponents' health to zero. Only through meticulous planning and aggressive gameplay can one player emerge as the ultimate victor.

Shards of Infinity: Saga Collection is an entry point into the ever-expanding universe of Shards of Infinity as it contains the base game, all expansions, and all promo cards from the original edition, along with new and upgraded components. In addition to including the PvP, solo, and co-op modes, the Saga Collection introduces a new legacy mode that allows players to master gameplay from every set while delving into the game's lore. Additionally, the inclusion of a new Shard Master enables epic showdowns with up to six players.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Does your humour know no bounds? Imagine a pole-dancing dictator or keelhauled metalhead. Does a sneaky smile appear on your face? Then expand the StatYou base game with these Rude Deck cards and bring out the laughing sadist in you!

—description from the publisher
family: Mechanism: Give a Clue / Get a Clue

boardgame: Waste Knights: Second Edition – Beyond the Horizon: Castaway
description:
A new player knight, the Castaway.


     Castaway Miniature
     Knight Sheet
     4 Upgrade Cards
     5 Luck Tokens

The concept is simple: Fill the launcher with banana-scented poo! Fling poo! Collect bananas! Climb!

Start by choosing your monkey (P.U. Stink, Gassy George, Harry Dingle, or Tootsie) then spin the spinner to see how many flings you get. Lift the Monkey Flinger’s tail and load the Dough into the opening on his back. Push down on the tail until all the dough is in the Monkey’s hands. Pinch and shape poo before launching! Place one hand on the Monkey Flinger’s base to keep it steady and your other hand over the launcher. Aim at the vertical tree that is the game board and let the poop fly! After flinging a poo, collect a Banana Token for each Banana Bunch you knocked over. Continue flinging poo the number of times shown on the spinner. When a player collects 3 Banana Tokens, it’s time to trade them in and climb to the next level on the Tree! If you knock over the entire Tree, it’s a “BANANA BLOWOUT!” Collect 1 Banana Token and your turn ends. It’s now the next player’s turn. Trade in enough Bananas Tokens to climb to the top and be TOP BANANA!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Indiana Jones and the Hidden Treasure of Pyramid
description:
Indiana Jones and his female partner team up to explore a dangerous pyramid deep in the jungle rumored to contain a powerful, magic jewel from an ancient civilization. They will need to use their wits, gear, and a bit of luck to find the jewel and escape with their lives!

This game is either for 2-4 players competing against each other or 4-8 players competing as 2-player teams (with one player as Indy and the other as the Heroine). Each team moves, fights, and explores as one, with the Indy and Heroine players taking turns playing Explore cards to move their player pawn through the pyramid's corridors and rooms on the game map. Each Heroine provides either a bonus or a penalty to movement or combat (presumably, some are adept and helpful partners, like Marion Ravenwood, and some are actively working against Indy, like Elsa Schneider). Indy can land on another team's pawn to fight that other team and force them to trade Heroines. In a team game, that means that the Heroine players have to trade positions and now have different partners.

Each team's ability to move and fight is based on the Indy character's power level, a value between 3 and 6 representing Indy's health. Power may be lost due to damage from a trap or losing a fight and may be gained by finding medicine throughout the pyramid. If Indy's power level falls below 3, the team loses all their equipment and reset their pawn back to the start of the current floor they are on.

The players explore rooms in the pyramid, discovering traps and monsters to fight. Combat is handled by rolling one six-sided die and adding the result to Indy's current power level plus any other bonus or penalty. If the total exceeds the monster's power, the player wins and may take the equipment listed on the monster card (medicine, key to open a locked door, whip or pistol to aid in combat, or a mine car to move faster). The treasure room that is every player's goal is found on the 3rd floor of the pyramid. The first player team to reach this room and win a fight against a monster with a power level of 11 takes the jewel and escapes the pyramid as victor.

In 2025, aliens make first contact with Earth. They've arrived hoping to find a rich new source of resources or advanced technological trading partners. After all, they've detected deep space probes and evidence of space travel. Needless to say they're disappointed

Now 100 years later, humanity has made a name for itself as tenacious and resourceful mercenaries. Unable to face most aliens hand to claw, they've developed powered combat armor known as CASPers, Combat Assault Systems Personal. These 8 foot tall, half ton combat suits have given humanity a serious edge, and the aliens are tired of it.

In a carefully coordinated plot, Earth has been invaded, and the human merc armies fled in order to survive. With Earth occupied, the Omega War is underway. The future of Earth, her few tiny colonies and all of our people's lives are at the stake. This is the Omega War

The Four Horsemen: Omega War is a tactical, strategic and operational competitive card game for two to four players set in the Four Horsemen literary universe created by Chris Kennedy and Mark Wandrey. It is a brutal struggle for power that rewards both careful planning and bold execution as you assume the role of a Commander for one of the various factions.

—user summary

boardgame: Chess on a really big board
description:
Chess on a really big board is a large chess variant invented by Ralph Betza in 1996. The rules are the same as in regular chess except for these changes:

     Instead of a basic chessboard, in this the board is 16×16 squares. Therefore, starting position is changed.
     Added 8 fairy pieces and 8 extra pawns for each player. 
     Extended pawn movement: an unmoved pawn can move one step, up to the middle of the board (in the case of the 16×16 board, the eighth rank), or anything in between. Once it has moved for the first time, it can only make one step at a time. En passant can be done on any of the squares pawn has passed through.
     In castling, the rook moves the same distance as it does in chess. Thus, the king moves further (to the space right behind the rook)
     The 50-move rule becomes a 100-move rule.

Prison - can you escape? is a strategy board game, which aims to organize an escape plan to break out of prison. It can be played for 2-6 players and during the game you may have setbacks in your plans! The game has 6 characters that have various attributes and traits that can lead to different strategies. The cards and action spaces on the board give it a different character as they can condition the strategies at any time during the game.

—description from the designer

F2F is a face-to-face sci-fi ship battle game for 2 players. Each player draws from their own deck and lay a face-down line of cards, which could be ships, traps, or other effects. Players take turns revealing and attacking with one ship. The last player with one or more ships remaining is the winner.

You're a space junker in the YCG System, where a strange phenomenon is taking over every corner of the galaxy: planets are disappearing only to be replaced by asteroids! Hop between planets, collide with other ships, and avoid solar flares to make it out of the asteroid fields. Collect the most space junk to win!

Use your Space Hop cards to travel across the planets, collect tools and upgrades as you go and collect the most loot before asteroids fill the board!

Avoid solar flares and collide with your opponents to push them away from the best loot. Claim the Upgrade Expert card for bonus points by collecting the most ship upgrades.

A light strategy game with a random generated board to keep games fresh every time you play. Portable and easy to teach for all ages!

—description from the publisher

The party game where the whackiest tale wins!

Entertain fellow players to win points and become the Ultimate Crazy Travel Storyteller. Combine cards into a whacky travel story, vote with your leftover cards and use more flip flops to score higher!

Journey cards set the scene for everyone's Crazy Travel Story. Use 5 of the 7 Word cards in your hand to come up with a middle and an end. Word cards can be anything from a character to a place, an object or an activity.

Cards are ranked in order of difficulty with the harder cards being more difficult to work into a story but scoring more points. Harder cards are green with 3 flip flops, easy cards are pink with 1 flip flop and the blue cards are in the middle with 2 flip flops.

—description from the publisher

In Zombie: Survival Infection game you control of a camp of survivors attempting to outlast the undead. How you approach your survival is your choice. You can make allies by helping others, try to stay neutral, break a truce when it benefits you the most or go on the offensive. Just remember no matter what you choose your fellow camps are going to do everything they can ensure their survival.

Unique mechanic of Passing the Die can be used to build alliances or weaken enemies. Players knocked out before the game ends play as the zombies. If all survivors are knocked out at the same time the zombies win!

—description from the designer

«Quantum»? Did you say «quantum»? With |Hop>, come and explore the world of atoms. Move your electrons, use physical properties and discover the exciting world of physics!

|Hop> is a competitive card game, where players clash to move electrons from their “electrodes” to those of the adversary. By doing this, they will learn crucial cutting-edge concepts in quantum physics in a simple and fun way. No math required!

You are physics researchers. An accident occurred at the university: a cutting-edge experiment machine was fried because the energy was not controlled. Each person is trying to prove that it was the others who made the mistake.
Grants are at stake! So, roll up your sleeves and heat up the electrodes!

—description from the designer

In turns, players will move their color electrons across the cards that form the board in an attempt to reach another player’s source. They will have 2 actions to choose from among several possible types of movements. The player whose source is reached has lost; the others are safe… for this game!

—user summary

Buy low, sell high, use cards to your advantage, and don't get caught by a crash! Have the most points at the end of the game to win.

On your turn, Buy, Sell or use an action on your cards to manipulate the market to your advantage. Then flip over a Market card and move the market (one coin up and one coin down).

Build the best portfolio before the market crashes!

Includes 3 expansions:
Headline cards give each round an unexpected twist.
Booms and Busts cause multiple coins to rise or fall in value.
Colouful Pupcoins add a homage to DogeCrypto to your gameplay.

Crypto Millionaire have lots of scope for different strategies and high replayability with multiple varying Value Tracks to control the market during the game.

—description from the publisher

In Pun of Your Business, you and your business partners have just acquired an entire strip mall of empty stores — but what's a strip mall without its selection of niche shops? Using random combinations of business ideas and helpful market research, come up with clever puns to score points and name these stores before the grand opening!

Combine cards from the grid to make punny business names, focus on the combinations required by the market research card in the center, and complete the objectives to win.

Thirty market research cards and randomized grids from the 300+ business cards make every game different. Simultaneous play means there's no downtime waiting for turns, and you can play with groups big and small by teaming up for twice the fun.

—description from the publisher

Race to collect the most cards in this fast-reaction card game!

We've snapped photos of the crocodile Olympics for you to collect. Be the first person to slap matching cards and claim them as your own. Watch out for instant snap and change-direction cards that mix up game play. Be the last croc standing with cards to win the game.

—description from the publisher

Bust all your moves out to your followers and be the first player to get rid of all your cards to complete your video! The deck consists of 4 dance crazes, and featured numbered steps to do the dance. You can only play cards from your hand that match the dance craze in the center or show a higher numbered step as the card in the center. Look out for action cards that could cut your Wi-Fi or send your pet in to sabotage your video!

—description from the publisher

Everyone has a price. Can anyone guess yours in this wagering party game?

Guess "how much money" it would take your friends to do some crazy stuff, then score points based on how close you get. Each round, one person is the Daredevil who reads a stunt from a card aloud and then writes down how much money it would take to get them to do the dare.

Players quiz the Daredevil on the specifics of the activity, but you can't ask how much they'd do it for, duh! Then Everyone else writes down their guess, then places bets.

Reveal the Daredevil's price, the closest bet, AND the closest guess win points!

A great party game or icebreaker with low downtime and lots of player interaction. Multiple ways to score points, even with wrong answers and a different game every time with unexpected and funny results.

NSFW edition coming soon!
 —description from the publisher

Ferret Flush is a laugh-a-lot, easy-to-play card game for creatures who like to dig a little deeper. In some bizarrely funny situations, players explore the interplay between thoughts, feelings and actions. It’s a fine day to be a ferret.

The Goal: Earn the most points by the end of the adventure!

The Set Up: Shuffle the Situation Cards. Place the cards face down. Shuffle the Play Cards. Deal 5 cards to each creature. Place remaining Play Cards face down next to the Situation Cards

The Play: Dealer is the first Ferret.

Ferret turns up the top Situation Card, reads it out loud, & places it face up for all to see.

For every Situation Card, you’ll play 3 sets: Thought, Feeling, & Action.

Thought Set: All creatures (minus the Ferret) pick a Play Card from your hand with the best* thought for the situation. Pass Play Cards face down to the Ferret & pick up a new Play Card.

Ferret reads each thought out loud, picks a favorite, and places it face up below the Situation Card.

Feeling Set: Rinse & repeat. This time, pick the best* feeling to fit both the Situation and Thought.

Action Set: Again! Pick the best* action for the Situation, Thought, & Feeling.

If the Ferret picks your card for at least two sets, you win this round and earn a point. Keep the Situation Card to track your points. (Keep it face-up in front of you, not in your hand)

If you win three sets, that’s a Ferret Flush™! Keep the Situation Card & also draw an extra Play Card to add to your hand.

When there’s a three-way tie, those creatures must each act out the Feeling from the round. Ferret picks the best* actor to win the round.

Creature to the left of the last Ferret becomes the new Ferret.

Adventure ends when every creature has been the Ferret twice (3-4 creatures) or once (5-6 creatures). Creature with the most points wins.

—description from the publisher

In Dueling Cupids, create new love connections for classic characters while you explore their traditional roles in Shakespeare’s plays!
 You don’t need to be a Shakespeare expert to play, but you might learn more about his works while you do. Gain hearts for making matches, score points for collecting couples, and earn bonuses for hitting your cupid’s ideal targets.

Courage draws the bow… love guides the arrow!

—Justin Waggle, Owner and Game Designer, Gray Wolf Games

TAO is an abstract and strategic card game for 8+ / 2 players. It is inspired by oriental culture and Taoist philosophical thought. Although there are 4 factions facing each other (tigers and dragons, red and blue) the player stands above the parties and uses them as tools to achieve the goal: to open the portals and recover the greatest number of resources.

—description from the designer

Our first tabletop game, Consensus, requires strategy, pattern recognition, and communication. Made for 2-10 players, this game serves as a creative reaction to the polarized dialogue and toxic partisanship in our communities.

Consensus uses game theory and a variety of custom layers to stimulate a turn based debate between people with different agendas. You can control the setup to either be competitive or collaborative. Play to outsmart and outscore your opponents or add real topics and your opinions to find common ground.

At Game Genius, our goal was to build a fun game that doubles as a conversation tool. Within our services, we facilitate introductory play sessions with Consensus because it's an effective way to learn more about a group.

—description from the publisher

Ready for a bite-sized battle of words and wits? In Tidbit, Players (or teams) claim cards by identifying words connected to themes in a row (horizontal/ vertical/ diagonal). They will need to be clever and pay attention as others may be vying for the same cards!

After a series of 4 rounds, players count up their cards to determine the winner. But that's not all. There's another title up for grabs! With their claimed cards, players create a final word. Whoever uses the most cards earns the prestigious title, "Word of the Day".

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Judge Dredd: The Game of Crime-Fighting in Mega-City One – Judge Fish promo
description:
Promo playable character.

Judge Fish has 5 strength and the ability

"Aquatic:
You may cross the river where there is no bridge present. You may ignore bridge closures.

You can move from the Grand Hall of Justice to the Power Tower this way, but cannot enter the open water on the left of the map."
First promo pack, distributed by board game stores. It contains 4 cards (2 Dragonflies and 2 Government) to add to the game.
Second promo pack, distributed by the publisher at conventions. It contains 4 cards (2 Dragonflies and 2 Government) to add to the game.

In this game each player takes control over their own realm, represented by standard playing cards.

Realms & Royals is a simple racing/deck-building game where players try to expand cards on their hand in order to obtain the most valuable royal cards. Each suit represents distinct layer of the medieval society, and requires different combination of the cards in order to get it.

When it's your turn, you may do one of three actions:


     buy a card - receive the social strata card from the common market
     get a random card - play some cards with value at least 5 points to receive the card from the deck
     get a royal card - play the card of matching suit and required value to receive the royal card
     reset your deck - take all the played cards back to your hand


The game lasts until the deck empties or one of the players reaches 40 points by getting the right royal cards. Then the final scoring oncomes: each player gets points for their royals, but only the player with the highest total score of each suit in their deck gets the points.

There are multiple paths to victory. Will you focus on your people and build an everlasting empire, or you rather focus on your dynasty and introduce the most memorable leaders in history?

—description from the designer

The Ephipparius deck expansion contains 168 new cards for Agricola comprising 84 minor improvements and 84 occupations.

A very special expansion with more cards than former expansions, hence in a new box size: These cards have been thoroughly tested by the best Agricola players in the world. A must for every collector.

The Ephipparius-deck (E-Deck) can be played on its own, with the base game, or shuffled with any other Agricola expansion decks. 
This deck works best with Agricola Revised Edition.

In this fast-playing riff on Talisman, you play valkyries fighting your way from Midgard through Niflheim to Hvergelmir to hold back the dragon Nidhogg from starting Ragnarok.

Defeat enemies by beating their strength with a roll of the dice, but score more points by using fewer dice to do so. Enlist the souls of dead warriors from Valhalla to gain dice manipulation abilities, but too many warriors in your band will penalize your score.

Defeated cards in the board's grid give you bonus dice against other cards in their row and column, so plan ahead and choose your path to the center carefully.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Ahoy there, mateys! Welcome to 7 Seas: Pirate King, the exciting and immersive game that will have you setting sail on the high seas to become the most feared pirate of them all. With a unique pirate map board, ship and crew leveling up, and strategic battles against the Navy and Traders, 7 Seas offers a thrilling and engaging gameplay experience that will keep you hooked from start to finish.

In 7 Seas, you'll start with a small ship and crew, but as you navigate the treacherous waters of the 7 Seas, you'll have the opportunity to level up and improve your ship and crew. With each victory against the Navy and Traders, you'll earn treasure and resources that can be used to upgrade your ship's cannons and sails, your own leadership and cunning as captain, as well as your crew's skills and abilities.

But becoming the Pirate King won't be easy. You'll need to outmaneuver and outwit your opponents, making strategic decisions about when and where to attack, and how to best use your ship's strengths and weaknesses in battle.

And with a unique pirate map board, and different ways to level up your crew, every game of 7 Seas is different, offering a fresh and exciting gameplay experience every time you play. You'll explore new ports and encounter new challenges as you sail across the 7 Seas, battling against the Navy and Traders to claim your place as the Pirate King.

So gather your crew, hoist the Jolly Roger, and set sail for adventure in 7 Seas: Pirate King. With a rich and immersive game world, deep gameplay mechanics, and endless replayability, this game is sure to become a favorite among pirate lovers and board game enthusiasts alike.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Dead Inside: Zombie Survival Card Game
description:
Have you ever wanted to survive the zombie apocalypse from the safety of your kitchen table. Well now you can. Dead Inside is a unique zombie survival experience played with 2 to 4 players.

Each round you can scavenge for supplies. You may find all sorts of useful items including food, water, weapons and medical supplies. Use these items to keep yourself alive. Without food or water, you will die. Players have three ways they can die. Hunger, thirst or health. If either of these stats reach zero, you're dead.

While you are out scavenging you will no doubt come across some undead folks. With eight unique zombie types, some with special abilities, you better be prepare to defend yourself. You may choose to run or fight. But don't let that horde get too big or you will get overwhelmed.

Being the strongest survivor sometimes means doing a few underhanded actions. Dead Inside gives you the chance to sabotage other players. Break their legs so they can't run from their horde and are forced to fight. Cut them so they slowly bleed out, without the right medical supplies they are sure to experience a slow death.

—description from the designer

Components: (from the Kickstarter)

Each deck will come with 126 cards, 2 dice and an instruction booklet. 
Contained in the deck is
8 Unique Zombie Cards (41 Cards)
5 Unique Status Cards (16 Cards)
6 Unique Action Cards (14 Cards)
4 Unique Equipment Cards (12 Cards)
13 Unique Item Cards (43 cards)

There is also a mobile app on the Play Store to help track your health, water and food.
The app is not required in order to play the game, however.

Ancestral Spirits increases the level of player interaction and tactics by introducing two new types of cards: Mystic Vision and Ancestral Wisdom.

You start the game with one Mystic Vision card and get one more whenever you send a tribe to the Temple. They grant you special one-time bonuses and an ability to interfere with your opponents’ actions. You also get two Ancestral Wisdom cards at the start, and one more per each visit at the Shaman’s Hut. They are secret objectives that get you extra Happiness Points whenever fulfilled during the game, not at the endgame scoring.

Wrath of the Gods makes the game more unpredictable by making use of new decks of cards: Blessing and Wrath, all related to each of the six gods from the core game. They are drawn and resolved at the beginning of each season, depending on whether a god’s Monument was built in the previous season or not. Blessings can increase Harvest and provide you with extra resources, while Wrath cards make it harder to feed tribers, hunt and gather resources.

Stacks & Stacks is a quick and easy theme-based card game.
Claim cards from Stacks on the table.
Try to Claim the most points each Round.
Just 2 Rounds and a final Winner is declared.
Shuffle up and Play again!

Each theme has its own unique flair, goofy goblins or cute critters. 
All the familiar fun of classic playing card games.
Luck has its role, but strategic thinking and foresight pay off.

Hordes & Hoardes: Stacks & Stacks Deck
Once upon a time, there was a DRAGON who wanted more GOLD for his HOARD where he sleeps.
Instead of getting it himself, he thought he could threaten GOBLINS to take GOLD from other DRAGONS' HOARDS to feed his greedy need.
It would seem this was not the only DRAGON with such an idea. Who will get the most?

Critters & Crops; Stacks & Stacks Deck
Once upon a time, there was a BEAR who wanted more FOOD for the WINTER when he sleeps.
Instead of getting it himself, he thought he could threaten CRITTERS to take CROPS from FARMERS' FIELDS to feed his greedy need.
It would seem this was not the only BEAR with such an idea. Who will get the most?

Each Turn is 1 of 4 Easy Choices:
DRAW ◊ MOVE ◊ PLAY ◊ PLACE

3 Ways to Enjoy Stacks & Stacks
Standard Stacks (the standard way to play)
Solo Stacks (more fun than Solitaire)
Versus Stacks (Theme vs. Theme with added twists)

—description from the designer

As a member of The Assembly, you'll use your special powers and abilities to fight against the nefarious forces of Blacklace. You'll need to manage your energy carefully, making strategic decisions about when and how to use your powers to defeat your opponents. With a modular hex board, every game of The Assembly is different, offering a fresh and exciting gameplay experience every time you play.

​But if you choose to play as a member of Blacklace, you'll have your own unique abilities and powers to unleash on The Assembly. You'll need to work together with your fellow villains to outwit and outmaneuver the heroes, using your wits and cunning to gain the upper hand in battle.

As you battle against the other team, you'll earn Victory Points that will determine the ultimate winner of the game. With a fast-paced, action-packed gameplay experience, The Assembly is a game that will keep you engaged and entertained for hours on end.

​So gather your friends, choose your side, and get ready for an epic battle of good versus evil in The Assembly: Board Game. Whether you're a fan of superhero stories or just love a good game of strategy and tactics, this game has something for everyone.

—description from the designer

Introducing Lord of the Labyrinth, a cooperative-multi-progression board game that will take you on an unforgettable journey through perilous labyrinths filled with danger and mystery. In this game, you and your friends will go on a quest to defeat enemies, solve puzzles, and eventually make your way to the Minotaur—the Lord of the Labyrinth—as you embark on this roguelike adventure.

Using a combination of cards, a shared dice pool between allies and enemies, and highly replayable gameplay, Lord of the Labyrinth offers a unique and challenging experience that will keep you engaged and entertained throughout multiple games. You'll have the opportunity to level-up your characters as you progress, gaining new abilities, loot, and powers that will aid you on your journey.

But be warned: the Minotaur is not the only danger lurking in the labyrinths. You'll encounter treacherous traps, deadly monsters, and mysterious artifacts that could spell your doom if you're not careful.

Luckily, you won't be alone. As a cooperative game, Lord of the Labyrinth encourages players to work together to overcome the challenges that await them. By combining your skills, abilities, and dice puzzles, you'll be able to overcome even the most difficult obstacles and emerge victorious...eventually. As a roguelike, expect to fail sometimes, but use the knowledge, experience, and new loot you gained to help win the next time!

So gather your friends, prep your characters, and get ready to take on the Lord of the Labyrinth. It's time to begin an epic adventure that you won't soon forget.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Spitfire Pilot 1940: Dynamo to the Blitz
description:
Sat inside the cockpit with the engine rattling through your bones, you turn to engage. Managing your energy and fuel, can you gain position to get a shot off? Or will the bandit get the better of you? Sometimes it is better to disengage than push a losing fight.

Spitfire Driver is a solitaire game of aerial combat in the Second World War. Using a game card that acts as your Cockpit and also as a mission generator. The player and Bandit take alternating actions to try and get a shot on the opposites aircraft.

Each sortie lasts between 5-10 minutes, and the player has a logbook to advance their career. As the player completes a campaign, they have the option to keep the logbook and continue their career in different aircraft and theatres.

All is needed is at least four D6, a small marker for the advantage track, and a pen or pencil for the logbook. Everything else is included in the print and play PDF.

Do you have what it takes to complete a campaign in Supermarines iconic fighter?

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
CardRunners is a two-player co-operative 55-card game. Players are cyberpunks, trying to find the right combination of access codes to break into five buildings before the system crashes or the police arrive. CardRunners is a 30-minute game of hand management, recipe fulfillment, and sticking it to the Man.

Players navigate through a 3x3 grid of buildings with specific access codes. At the same time, players are playing, tucking, and swapping cards from a center row in hopes of collecting the needed access codes to break into the buildings. Once a player has the correct combination, they immediately take down the building. Once five buildings have been infiltrated, players win. However, if the police arrive, they lose.

—description from the publisher

Welcome to Blossom, the enchanting board game that will transport you to a vibrant world of colorful flowers and lush gardens. In this game, you'll compete with other players to create the most beautiful and bountiful flower garden by strategically drafting cards and managing your resources.

Blossom is an engine-building style game that challenges you to create the most efficient garden possible. You'll need to carefully select the flowers you plant, manage your water and fertilizer resources, and build your garden up over time to create a stunning and diverse landscape.

As you progress through the game, you'll acquire new abilities and powers that will help you build your garden even more effectively. You'll also encounter special events and challenges that will test your skills and force you to adapt your strategy on the fly.

But Blossom isn't just a game about mechanics - it's also a feast for the eyes. With stunning artwork and a vibrant color palette, Blossom is a joy to look at and play. You'll feel like you're strolling through a beautiful garden with every turn.

Whether you're a seasoned board game veteran or a newcomer to the hobby, Blossom offers something for everyone. It's easy to learn but difficult to master, and every game is different thanks to the card drafting mechanics.

So put on your gardening gloves, grab your watering can, and get ready to plant the most beautiful garden of them all in Blossom!

—description from the designer

Step into the world of Simpatico, the exciting civilization board game that will take you on a journey through time and space as you build your civilization from the ground up. With simple mechanics and countless options, Simpatico offers a deep and immersive gameplay experience that will keep you engaged and entertained for hours on end.

In Simpatico, you'll start with a small group of settlers and work to build a thriving civilization. You'll need to manage your resources carefully, making strategic decisions about where to focus your efforts and how to best grow your society. With a modular hex board, every game of Simpatico is different, offering a unique and challenging experience every time you play.

One of the things that sets Simpatico apart is the multiple ways to score. Whether you focus on faith, culture, military, or science, there are many paths to victory in this game. This gives you the freedom to pursue your own unique strategy and playstyle, making each game of Simpatico feel fresh and exciting.

As you progress through the game, you'll encounter challenges and opportunities that will test your skills and force you to adapt your strategy on the fly. With so many options and possibilities, Simpatico is a game that offers endless replayability and depth.

So gather your friends, prepare to build your civilization, and get ready for an unforgettable gaming experience with Simpatico. Whether you're a seasoned board game veteran or a newcomer to the hobby, this game has something for everyone.

—description from the designer

Welcome to Empyres: The Board Game, the strategic and immersive game that will take you on a journey through time as you build and expand your own empire. With worker placement, multiple paths to score, and tableau engine building, Empire offers a deep and engaging gameplay experience that will keep you coming back for more.

In Empyres, you'll start with a small kingdom and work to build a vast and powerful empire. You'll need to manage your workers carefully, making strategic decisions about where to place them and how to best utilize their skills and abilities. With multiple paths to score, every game of Empire is different, offering a wide range of strategies and playstyles to explore.

As you expand your empyre, you'll discover new cities, recruit great people, and build a wide range of buildings and wonders. With over 140 cities, 70 people, and 70 buildings to discover and explore, Empire offers a wealth of content and possibilities.

But to truly succeed in Empyres, you'll need to master the art of tableau engine building. By carefully constructing your own personal tableau of buildings, wonders, and people, you'll create a powerful engine that will fuel your empire's growth and success.

With a rich and detailed game world, deep gameplay mechanics, and endless replayability, Empyres: The Board Game is the perfect choice for fans of strategy and empire-building games. Whether you're a seasoned board gamer or a newcomer to the hobby, Empyres is a game that offers something for everyone. So gather your friends, prepare to build your empire, and get ready for an unforgettable gaming experience with Empyres: The Board Game.

—description from the designer

Disturblings is a push-your-luck, tower-defense game for one player that’s full of bullets and colorful oddities.

You play as three-armed anti-hero, Bobby Butcher, as he’s handcuffed to an injured sheriff and struggling to fight off the disturbingly cute, lab-altered creatures scampering towards you.

As you scramble to reload the sheriff’s service weapon, you’ll need to strategically reallocate resources to build up your defenses and ensure you’re not overwhelmed.

—description from the designer

Change the course of history in your very own 15 Minute Cold War.

Choose from the mighty Soviet Union, the United States of America, the People's Republic of China or the United Kingdom. Build up your unique technology and culture, your military defences and special abilities while balancing the need for protection with the urge for aggression.

Form new alliances with your fellow countries and use all of your skill to remain the last leader standing and the next great superpower!

—description from the designer

Treacherous Tides is an exciting board game that combines strategy, luck, and treachery on the high seas. In this thrilling adventure, players take on the roles of ambitious pirates, each with their own unique captain's abilities, as they aim to amass the most treasure. Using their captain's special skills, players must navigate the treacherous waters, outsmart opponents, and avoid dangerous obstacles like the legendary Kraken!

The game begins with players rolling dice to assemble their ship, acquire a flag, and recruit a crew. Armed with these resources and their captain's unique abilities, they set sail on a perilous journey to uncover hidden treasures scattered across the game board. The objective is to collect as much treasure as possible while anticipating the mutinous intentions of other pirates.

However, the true heart of Treacherous Tides lies in its devious nature. At any moment, another player can betray alliances, seize control of your ship, and claim your hard-earned loot for themselves. This creates an atmosphere of suspense and unpredictability, where careful planning and quick thinking are essential for success.

Treacherous Tides offers a quick but action-packed gaming session. The game's mechanics ensure that players must utilize their unique captain's abilities to navigate the waters, outsmart opponents, and overcome dangerous obstacles like the menacing Kraken.

—description from the publisher

In Chytré kostky - Dinosauři one player rolls four different die (size, weight, natural habitat, etc.), then everyone tries to find animal matching most rolled symbols. After every player has chosen one animal every picked animal is turned to other side and each match equals one point.

Once each player rolled once (or twice in two player game) it is end of the game and player with most points wins.

Can you believe it? Quests Over Coffee came out one year ago! I released it as a little thing for my birthday and it has found such a wonderful player base that it has exceeded all expectations. To celebrate the one-year Anniversary, I decided to release an expansion of 9 new cards! Thank you so much for playing.

What is included in this expansion?

2 Quests, 5 Items, 1 Environment, and 1 Companion!

Any Quests Over Coffee game requires 10 Quest cards and at least 6 Item cards, so it's best to start with the base game if you don't have enough! If you want to play with Environments, make sure to read the most up to date rule set (1.2 or above)!

What is Quests Over Coffee?

Quests Over Coffee is a single player dice throwing game where you try to claim fame and fortune. With a some strategy, some items, and a little bit of luck, you can find yourself on the other side of the glory or in the pit of a grave.

The game is also a system that allows for you to create your own Quests and Items to experience. Let your imagination and experience run free!

This mini-expansion includes 11 Ship cards. These helpful ships will give players an benefit during the game.

Included inside Darwin's Journey: Collector's Edition and was available as an add-on during the Kickstarter campaign for those backing at the base game only pledge level.

boardgame: Quests Over Coffee: Patreon Goodies – Promos and Things
description:
In a land not so far away, in a town you probably don't remember, there is a coffee shop that specializes in quests and, well, coffee. They like to tout that they're the only place where you can get a cup of decent coffee and complete a set of quests (with included fame and fortune) before you finish the last drop. Will you partake in this marketing ploy thinly veiled as a call for adventure?

Patreon goodies now available!

Note that these goodies are Quests Over Coffee card game specific, so things like Companions, Items, Quests, and so on. Spin-offs aren't included here.

This mini-expansion includes scenarios card that provides the player with a crafted game experience. Instead of randomly choosing components during setup, players will use specifically designed set of components which will lead the experience in a particular direction.

Included inside Darwin's Journey: Collector's Edition and was available as an add-on during the Kickstarter campaign for those backing at the base game only pledge level.

This mini-expansion introduces a treasure to be discovered, a pirate ship moving around with pirates that can perform raids on players' ships.

Include inside Darwin's Journey: Collector's Edition and was available as an add-on during the Kickstarter campaign for those backing at the base game only pledge level.

What is included in this game?

Single sheet to print and play on!

What will I need to play?

You will need 3d6 and 3 different tokens/markers (one for the player, one for time, and one for score).

What kind of game is this?

You will roll 3d6 and assign that to different pieces in the game. You travel around the board to try to score on squares that give you points during that turn. You have a limited amount of time to do it though!

Tipping Point is a contentious 2 player Negotiation game of Governance.  Players represent political factions that strive to cement their legacy by accumulating enough Victory Points (VPs) before the accumulation of Carbon Emissions or Government Debt result in a Critical Tipping Point in which all players lose the game. Players deploy their Political Capital to score VPs through actions of governance like elections, legislation, shifting policy, appointing justices, and more but not all actions can be done alone in a divided Government so players must occasionally work with and against their opponent at the same time.

—description from the designer

Experience Brexit Negotiations with Our Engaging, Role-Playing, Strategic Political Board Game!

You've certainly heard of Brexit, but now you have the chance to immerse yourself in the roles and strategies of Europe's most complex negotiations of the 21st century.

How to Win Brexit? is a game for EVERYONE with a passion for politics, negotiations, leadership, and making challenging decisions. All you need is to like strategic board games!

Embark on a journey back to the historic Brexit negotiations period of 2017-2019, with just a maximum of 90 minutes to strike a Deal. Navigate the highly polarized political climate, reconcile conflicting objectives, and tackle the challenging task of finding consensus.

Step into the shoes of a key political figure from either the United Kingdom or the European Union, and immerse yourself in the captivating world of politics and diplomacy!

In this role-playing game, you'll take on the role of a real-life character, such as the UK Prime Minister or the German Chancellor, amongst others. You'll need to navigate complex political interests, seeking common ground between teams, all while prioritizing your own team's needs.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Players move around the board first on a coach going to Dracula's Castle. they have to watch out for the wolves. When they get there, they must try to sneak away while the gypsies are unloading the coach, if they are unfortunate and the gypsies force them to enter the castle, they won’t be able to get out of the main entrance. they must Try to make your way to the back gate where they can escape by carefully jumping down. Making their way to the Village where they will find items to aid them. they must must first kill 3 vampires then they can find Dracula and kill him. they will find him in the crypt.

Players movement is done by the amount of actions they have, which depends on their health. player will have encounters with vampires in the hours of the night. Encounters are a die roll. Players can attack vampires and Dracula by finding items in buildings. Going to the castle and killing Dracula.

Object of the game is to must find weapons to kill three vampires then kill Dracula in the castle.

—description from the designer

The philosophy behind the rules is the primacy of human actions as opposed to the cumulative material effects of ballistic combat. In the chaos of battle, humans begin to ignore the increasing material flotsam around them and fall back on their training. Small Deadly Space portrays this reality by reducing the pages of material modifiers, which we think, erroneously, are the main factors in determining human behavior, and increases the role of psychology in battle as influenced by training, drill and unit elan.

—description from the rulebook

Crystallo: Untold Fortune turns up the tension and adds new push-your-luck decisions to the first phase of the game. Freeing a creature now carries the risk of collapsing a section of the cavern, but might also reveal a useful tool. Collect gems to trade for treasure, buy a shovel to dig up cards from your cavern, or peek at the future with a crystal ball. Play on Timid, Adventurous, or Valiant mode— if you dare!

—description from the publisher

Crystallo: Den of Dragons adds a new layer of puzzle-solving adventure to the second (dragon) phase of Crystallo. In addition to capturing the Black Dragon, players can choose an additional dragon to do battle with (Mystic, Armored, or Golden.) Polyomino treasure tiles must be placed on top of and within the confines of the dragon puzzle, adding another dimension to the challenge. Defeat both dragons and earn impressive bonuses to your score; fail at either one, and both escape!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Gdzie ktyje się magia? Bitwa, gra karciana: My Little Pony
description: It is played with cards depicting characters from the series My Little Pony: Friendship Is Magic. Each character has numerical values for traits such as: height, magic, friendliness, mischief and beauty. All cards are dealt. The player who starts takes the first blind card from his pile and chooses which Trait will be resolved. Then all players reveal their cards from the top of the pile. The player with the highest stat wins, takes all the cards, and starts the next turn. The game ends when one player gets all the cards.

One day while patrolling the marketplace, you witness a child miraculously healed by an old man. The man speaks gibberish about horde and lava-water, then darts into the alleys. Next thing you know, you're being accused of murder by a Suit.  Since Horde-infested alleys are just a fairy tale, and you are apparently being framed for a crime you didn’t commit by a corrupt mercenary, you take off after the man in search of the unknown.

In The Calling: A Roguelike Game, you are a Market Man, running through alleys and building your character, in hopes of finding a light in the darkness. Build your initial character, choosing stats and abilities. Run the alleys, fend off horde, and avoid traps to gain XP. Purchase better stats and abilities to amp up your survival before the game sucks you into the alleys forever.

The Calling is a dungeon escape, rogue-like game that plays in 15 minute round. Stats and abilities carry over between rounds, but the permadeath mechanic can be frustratingly brutal.

This game is made for solo gamers at its core. There is an add-on expansion that shifts gameplay from a solo roguelike to a 2-4 player roguelite, where the respawns are welcomed and points by goal attainment are key.

—description from the designer

In a world of darkness and uncertainty, Luminaries light the way. True light comes in many forms and colors but must always flow from the River.

In Luminary, you are in fact a Luminary yourself, bringing light in a time of darkness.

In this quick 24 card-tile game, you draft colored-light tiles, lay out a variety of light groupings, and modify your rotation-based, card-tile, tech tree as needed.

Gather light, establish light, and advance your tech tree as you seek to provide Light to attain victory.

—description from the designer

Ultimate Aquarium is a competitive tile placement game where players compete for the highest score by strategically choosing where and what to place in their aquarium.

You're trying to build a beautiful aquarium. Various fish, coral, decorations and other critters and objects can be drafted from the aquarium store into your tank. Each one provides points based on the type and position of other cards in your aquarium. Try to get a synergistic selection over 8 or 9 turns and build the Ultimate Aquarium.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: NOVA: Race from the Sun Promo Pack
description:
Six Technology Cards with blank fronts allow you to create your own Techs to mix in the game. The standard pack contains Tech Cards for Mars, the Belt, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, and Neptune.

—description from the designer

A deadly snowfall is killing everyone it touches, but not everyone has fallen victim to this mysterious threat. Four people have survived in a workshop, and now they're venturing into the world at the start of a survival adventure in which they must face menaces far more dangerous than the deadly snowflakes.

The Eternaut is a story-driven co-operative survival board game for 1-5 players set in Buenos Aires that uses simple game mechanisms to create a survival experience with strong narrative elements. You must build a team, gather items, fight enemies, resolve objectives, and decide how to proceed with your unique story.

Most of the entities in the game are marked with an Identification Number (called ID). When you want to instruct one of your Characters to interact with another entity (with another ID), you must play the correct Action card and read the matching paragraph, numbered with the combination of the 2 IDs. example: The player wants Simon to scavenge a metal cabinet, so they assign him a Rummage card. This action lets Simon (904) interact with the cabinet (124):  the player must read the Paragraph 124-904 in the Storybook to discover the result of the interaction. Each Scenario has a dedicated Storybook, so the same interaction between two entities is completely different in distinct scenarios.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Quidditch - Le Jeu de Plateau is a game in which you will take part in a Quidditch match, wizards and witches favourite sport game from "Harry Potter" books series.

Move your chasers, avoid the bludgers, protect your hoops and score goals. Audacity and strategy will lead you to victory ... but don't forget to catch the Golden Snitch!

Each player manage their own quidditch team. They moves their meeples (who represent the Chasers) whose role is to play with the Quaffle and to score goals. In parallel they try to catch the Golden Snitch by drawing and playing game cards.

Victory is set by the number of points won by scoring goals and/or grabbing the golden snitch.

Thanks to the game cards, players can send Bludgers to the opposing team to destabilize it. Spells cards also allow you to spice up the game and carry out special actions.

Quidditch: Le Jeu de Plateau is a dynamic boardgame, action-oriented, with a share of strategy and chance, while remaining as faithful as possible to the spirit J. K. Rowling’s imagination.

—description from the designer (translated)

*********************************************************************************************************************************************

Quidditch - Le Jeu de Plateau vous permet de prendre part à un match de Quidditch, le sport préféré des sorciers du monde de Harry Potter.

Déplacez vos poursuiveurs, évitez les cognards, protégez vos anneaux et marquez des buts. Audace et stratégie vous mèneront vers la victoire… mais n’oubliez pas d’attraper le vif d’or !

Chaque joueur incarne une équipe de Quidditch dans un match typique des romans de la série Harry Potter.

Il fait évoluer sur le plateau ses pions (qui représentent les Poursuiveurs) dont le rôle est de jouer avec le Souafle et de marquer des buts. En parallèle il tente d’attraper le Vif d’or en piochant et jouant des cartes de jeu.

La victoire est déterminée par le nombre de points gagnés en marquant des buts et/ou en attrapant le Vif d’or.

Grâce aux cartes de jeu les joueurs peuvent envoyer des Cognards sur l’équipe adverse pour la déstabiliser. Des cartes "sortilèges" permettent également de pimenter la partie et de réaliser des actions spéciales.

Quidditch - Le Jeu de Plateau est un jeu dynamique, orienté action, avec une part de stratégie et de hasard, tout en restant le plus fidèle possible à l’esprit du sport et de l’univers créé par J. K. Rowling.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Arkham Horror: The Card Game – Out of the Void Investigator Pack
description:
Out of the Void features the Arkham Horror: The Card Game investigator cards that first appeared in these acclaimed Arkham Horror novellas:


     Norman Withers featured in the Ire of the Void
     Silas Marsh, featured in The Deep Gate
     Carolyn Fern, featured in To Fight The Black Wind
     Dexter Drake, featured in Blood of Baalshandor
     Gloria Goldberg, featured in Dark Revelations


Also included are an alternate art version of Jenny Barnes and her two optional new signature cards from the Hour of the Huntress novella, as well as an alternate art version of Roland Banks, and his signature cards from the novella The Dirge of Reason.

—description from the publisher
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: Arkham Horror – The Card Game
Series: Arkham Horror Files (Fantasy Flight Games)
Versions & Editions: Card Versions of Non-Card Games

boardgame: A Fistful of Meeples: A Few Meeples More
description:
"A few Meeples more" brings a collection of modular mini-expansions to "A Fistful of Meeples". They can be added individually or all together at once, kitchen sink style.

Mavericks: These wild meeples can shift between builder, miner, robber, or deputy - all at a drop of a hat.

Skip, the Dog: This pooch guards buildings, keeping meeples from entering. They have to skip over Skip. Woof!

The ghostly Madame: The Madame is back - from the grave... Now she haunts the town, scaring meeples out of their wits - and out of their buildings!

The Biggins: Robber Baron, Sheriff, Gold Tycoon, and Freemason are bigger versions of their smaller counterparts. Each one has a unique special ability, adding in some extra spice.

Building Facades: These first come, first served tiles can be built on top of Marquees, providing new end-game scoring opportunities.

-description from crowdfunding campaign

3 to 4 players play cards on the field to score high values of points. Pairs or matches of cards are to be avoided, though.

If pairs happen on the field, these cards leave the play.

The players decide beforehand how many points they want to play or they decide for an amount of time that is played. If any of these conditions are met in the game, the game ends.

In a 3 player match, players take turns to guide "AI" to be the 4th player by playing 2 cards at random.

—description from the publisher

Bonsai: Vigorous Growth and Specialists includes two optional modules that can be added to Bonsai and used either individually or together.

The 'Vigorous Growth' module consists of three triple tiles with symbols "3", "4", or "5" printed on the back. With this module, players can accelerate the development of their bonsai tree by acquiring new special tiles.

At the beginning of your turn, you may discard as many cards as indicated on one of the triple tiles still available in order to obtain that tile. Turn the tile over and place it in your bonsai immediately, making sure that at least one of the four wooden sides of the tile touches a bonsai wood tile, then take the rest of your turn as usual.

The cards you discard must all be of different types. The types of cards are Growth, Tool, Helper, Master, and Parchment. You may discard cards from your play area and your deck. Discarded cards are removed from play and no longer provide their effects or any victory points at game end. Each player may obtain only one triple tile per game. Once obtained, a triple tile is no longer available.

The 'Specialists' module consists of a game board and four declaration tokens. With this module, players can proclaim themselves cultivation specialists...but will they actually be able to prove it?

At the beginning of your turn and only once per game, you can declare that you will be the player with the most tiles of a certain type in your bonsai at game's end.

To do this, choose a type of tile (wood, leaf, flower, or fruit) and a space not occupied by any tokens on the specialists' board. Stack a number of tiles of your chosen type from your personal supply on that space equal to the number shown on that space, then place your declaration token on top of the stack so that the type of the tiles beneath it remains visible. Once placed, a declaration token (and the tiles under it) cannot be moved or used. You may choose a type of tile that has already been previously chosen by another player.

Note: This module is not compatible with solo mode.

This mini-expansion for Quadropolis introduces 5 new Park tiles: the Playgrounds. The Playgrounds replace some Park tiles. Playgrounds are considered as Parks in all regards. However, their effect
is different:

     they come with 1 Inhabitant;
     they do NOT absorb pollution at the end of the game.

People come to camp, and the Patagonian species that inhabit the place want to hide!

In this game we must play and pass the cards from our hand to the other players, forming a personal grid on the table. In turn, each player will give a location where each player must place their next card on the grid, which can be above, below, left or right of another adjacent card. The objective will be to make the best score by choosing the hiding places common to all the players, which is not going to be easy, and whoever gets the best score will be able to claim victory!

boardgame: Books of Time: Pele Promo Card
description: One additional Chronicle page for Books of Time. Add to the other Chronicle pages before selecting pages for your game.

boardgame: Books of Time: Trade Book Promo
description: Three Trade book pages for Books of Time. Add these three pages to the stacks of pages for the offering before the game begins.

Epic Battles is a deck-building and dice-rolling game. Designed for quick learning and easy playability, Epic Battles is fun for people of all ages. Enough strategy for adults, simple to understand for kids. Epic Battles are sure to be enjoyed by everyone in your family!

Pick a castle, build up a defense, or charge your foe in the Epic tale based on medieval fantasy play.

Power-up cards and special attack cards give the game an epic twist. Just when you think your castle is a well-guarded fortress disaster hits! Plan your attacks carefully, your enemy is waiting to take advantage of your misfortune.

—description from the designer

Office War is a fast-paced card game that lets you take out your aggressions without losing your day job.  Players attempt to get each other's employees "fired" in a war of staplers, spitballs and wet mops.  Stay low, and whatever you do... Don't Get Caught!  The last player to retain control over any employees is the winner.

You will need to manage the amount of attention your employees gather in the office war.  Attention counters are small paperclips, and the game comes with a package of colorful paperclips.

To win, arm your employees with office weapons and play action cards that influence the office war. Office weapons include items like rulers, tacks, coffee creamers, and even "The Bark of Authority" (usable only by Executives).  Action cards are modifiers.  They include events like "Lunch with the Boss," "Reorganization," "Promotion" and "No Toilet Paper."

Using an office weapon comes with its own risk and reward.  For example, if a Maintenance employee uses their wet mop, they will dish out three attention counters to their target, but take three counters themselves.  Whenever an employee receives attention counters, the employee's controller must roll two dice.  If the dice roll is equal to or less than their number of attention counters, the employee is fired and escorted to the discard pile.

Each turn a player draws a card, may play an action card, equip a weapon, engage in employee v. employee office warfare, then redraws cards to the hand size.  Play passes until only one player remains, because as everyone knows, office wars are ruthless.

—user summary

The version set across the Italian peninsula of the famous Scotland Yard! Mister X has been hiding in Italian cities and has new means of transport and techniques to escape the detectives. An exciting hunt for Mister X throughout Italy, but only the most cunning will win. Will the detectives be distracted by the charm of the Italian landscapes or will Mister X be blocked and captured? The famous strategy game unites the whole family once again!

—description from the publisher (translated)

boardgame: Vampire: The Masquerade – Rivals: Royalty Pack 1
description: Vampire: The Masquerade – Rivals: Royalty Pack 1 features 23 cards that immortalize each winner from a "Prince of the City" tournament.

The Lovecraft's Tales is a Lovecraftian Horror Roll & Write with different maps based on the different short stories.

In this game you are an investigator into the Lovecraftian Mythos. Each playsheet recreates a famous Lovecraft story and therefore each playsheet will have its own objective.

You have a to choose one of four characters, one of the game maps, a pen plus 4 dice and token to move around the map.

Roll the dice and assign the numbers to various fields on the page to face Challenges and Encounters and collect clues to forward your clue track to victory! But some rolls damage you, so beware getting too much physical and/or mental damage and lose.

In turn, each story will have some small added rules that will only vary a little depending on the objectives and the narrative need of each story. Each game sheet will specify the specific needs, new objectives or added rules to overcome it.

—description from the publisher

There’s an old folk game played on car trips, where you come up with a word using the letters on a license plate, but you must use them in order – BRT can be answered with barrette, for example, but not antidisestablishmentarianism. That often-maddening restriction is at the heart of this persnickety bauble.

Given the same three letters in the same order, players will each conjure up a word. You’ll score points for its length, for how many times each of the three letters appears, and the use of difficult letters – your Qs, your Xs, your Js, and your so-forths. Each round will specify a type of word – for example, a noun or a verb – and your score will be doubled if it falls into that category… but only if no one else at the table thought of the same word! The best score of each round wins one of the three cards. This not only removes it from future rounds but might form a set to score you extra points at the end of the game.

This is a word game for sesquipedalianists, show-offs, and pedants – where the longer and more unusual the word is, the better it’s going to score. It’s the sort of game where it’s valuable to know that “rebutter” is not a verb describing what you do when your toast fell on the floor but no one else saw it, but rather a noun for a defendant’s pleading in response to a plaintiff’s surrejoinder – and therefore it IS TOO worth double points, Susan, SO THERE.

Five-Dollar Words is the 2023 Hollandays Sale Freebie game – given out for free with orders during Hollandspiele’s big end-of-year sale. That’s the only way to get your hands on this tiny wee little baby deck of cards containing a grotesque menagerie of alphabetic monsters and delights.

—description from the publisher

A comet speeds toward the earth. When it collides, you and every other dinosaur you know will die. But talking about it is “a bummer” and “we don’t want to think about that right now” and “why are you like this”, and after all it is game night.

In Velocirapture, you play as dinos playing Human Games while waiting for The End. Each dino’s unique Coping Mechanism prevents them from thinking too hard about their mortality. Xoe Allred brings a characteristic playfulness and charm to this experimental and delightfully meta party game made up of three dozen small games that are by turns surprising, compelling, irreverent, and perplexing.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game – The Two Towers: Saga Expansion
description:
The Fellowship has broken. During a fateful confrontation at Amon Hen, large Orcs bearing the White Hand of Saruman attacked the company, and in the chaos some members were captured, others killed. Now, Aragorn and the remainder of the Fellowship must let Frodo travel to Mordor while they pursue the Orcs that captured their friends.

Experience the next chapter of the journey to destroy the One Ring in this epic expansion. This expansion contains six scenarios that recreate the adventures featured in the second part of the legendary The Lord of the Rings saga. In this box, you’ll also find over 60 hero and player cards, which can be used to build or enhance your decks for any The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game scenario or campaign.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: The Lord of the Rings – The Card Game (LCG)

description:
This is a quiz game - fine the correct answer for each question and put the LÜK tile on that space. When you're done, turn it over and the pattern on the back will tell you if you were right or wrong.
A game for the whole family - everyone can share their expertise!

Seize: Guilded Treasures is a new, interactive, arena battle trading card game developed by King's Games. It takes elements of familiar card games mechanics and very new ones along with board interactions on a grid. Instead of having your characters sit in position they instead have specialized movement on the grid to give them more character and lively interactions. You play as a Guild Leader, and your deck of 60 cards is a representation of your resources of your Warchest along with the Mercenaries you have hired to complete the task of defeating the opposing Guild Leader. Your goal in Seize is to reduce the opposing Guild Leader's Warchest Loot to zero. How is that done? By defeating opposing mercenaries with a Bounty Cost, or having an allied mercenary get to the other side of the battlefield and Plunder their Warchest. Each Mercenary, Treasure, Ability, Movement, and Attack matter so it's up to you as a player to manage your resources and develop a strategy wisely to defeat your opponent!

—description from the designer

GLORP! is a multiplayer, card-collection style game where the goal is to collect 7 of one type of card. There are currently 4 types of cards each with its own ability. Of the four there are:

Blorp: This card lets you draw a card
Yoink: This card lets you steal a random card from your target
Skraggle: This card forces your target to discard a card
Gwarg: This card is an instant play card, allowing you to guard agaisnt oncoming Yoinks or Skraggles.

The thing that makes this game interesting is the fact that the more cards you spend the greater the effect. For example: Playing 3 Blorps allows you to draw 4 cards. This allows for more strategy and chaos!

Set Up:

     To set-up "GLORP!" take all cards out of the box and shuffle the deck, placing it in the center of the playing area.
     Deal out 5 cards to every player
     When are card or cards are used place them face up in a discard pile, This will shuffled and used as the main deck if you run out of cards.


HOW TO PLAY:
1. On your turn, you have two actions. 
    a. You may choose to play a card (cards) or not to. 
    b. Than at the end of your turn you draw a card from the 
       deck.
2. Once a player collects 7 of the same type of card the game is over!

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Keep the Heroes Out!: Boss Battles
description:
Keep the Heroes Out: Boss Battles! will bring more Scenarios, more Monsters, new ways to handle difficulty and, of course, Boss Battles! These big baddies will have their own special deck of abilities to spice things up!

—description from the publisher

Argonauts is a cooperative card game that requires careful strategic planning and just a bit of luck, as even the best-laid plans, can fall victim to the wrath of the Gods. The game’s objectives are to acquire the Golden Fleece and return safely to Iolkos without losing Argo or too many members of her crew. Each player controls heroes that participated in the Argonaut campaign, each with his / her own set of skills and special abilities. Following the journey from the myth, the Argonauts must face mythical monsters like Scylla and Charybdis, Harpies, Sirens, the construct Talos, and the dragon guarding the Fleece, not to mention the perilous journey full of lethal encounters with merciless pirates or having to weather destructive thunderstorms all the while making sure their cargo hold is well stocked with the necessary supplies for the journey to and from Kolchis and their ship sea-worthy.

Each turn, the players must decide which of their characters will participate in the encounter they face, dealing with threats with either might or cunning and keeping in mind that these characters will be unavailable to face the next encounter due to exhaustion. But the Argonauts are not alone in their quest.
