The Gods occasionally take interest in their actions, either helping or hindering the progress of our heroes, laying careful plans to waste… The players win the game if they successfully resolve all the threat cards of the adventure deck!

—description from the designer

In Troy, two players are divided into Greeks and Trojans where they fight to be crowned the winners of the game. In the game players use legendary heroes such as Achilles, Hector, military units such as Myrmidons, Dardanes, figures such as Helen of Troy, Cassandra, structures such as the Walls of Troy, the Trojan Horse, all under the watchful eye and the moods of the Gods of Olympus!

The game lasts 4 rounds, in each round players fight each other through duels between legendary heroes and earn victory points. Winner is the player with the most victory points at the end of the game!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Deus Ex Machina: The Card Game
description:
Deus ex Machina is competitive card game for 2 to 4 players with an innovative hero placement mechanism in which each player takes the role of an legendary Hero such us Hercules Perseus etc trying to achieve supremacy over the other legendary heroes. Their ultimate goal is to accumulate the most Glory Points by having heroes complete quests. The winner of the game is the player who has completed his secret objective!

—description from the designer

What does it look and feel like to be in the saddle, with a bag full of hot food on your back, as your traverse city high-rises looking for the address of customer? What is the financial impact of waiting around all day for work that never comes? Is it worth pushing your luck and taking a risk if it makes the difference in wages?

Meal Deal is a game that puts players in the saddles of cycle couriers working in the gig economy. With a finite amount of stamina on tap each day, the cards allow players to weave their own gig economy story based on academic research with couriers in Manchester, York and Edinburgh in the UK.

Solo card game (54 cards) exploring an old village while searching for lost postcards to add to your collection.

Cards are drawn and placed in slots according to a layout plan. Find and keep POSTCARDS when your SELF card is on a TERRAIN card whose attributes match that POSTCARD. CAR cards allow you to travel to any TERRAIN, growing your chances to collect POSTCARDS.

SIDEKICK cards and TRUNK slots allow you to store extra items like REPAIR KITS and WEAPONS to help out if you encounter NEMESIS or CAR DAMAGE cards, and FLEA MARKETS can give you an end game bonus for extra cards.
 When there are no more draw cards, the game is over. If an attribute appears in all 7 POSTCARD slots, you win. Postcards have attributes and values which add up to different final accomplishment designations.

—description from the designer

A "roll & score" baseball game based on the rules of craps.

This version requires neither game pieces nor a graphical representation of the “diamond”: The score sheet is the key feature of BCL™.
All necessary adjustments have been made to make the game smooth and with easy-to-remember rules.
The ultimate edition features updated and better-organized rules, new tables and statistics, and more actions for runners!

—description from the designer

Lucky Beach is a fast and easy game where players have to reveal beach-themed platelets and to collect as many beach treasures as possible. But hidden beneath all the treasures are crabs and sharks. Crabs immediately steal your treasure away - and with that valuable points - while the fourth drawn shark immediately stops the game. 
After three rounds, players count their treasures and the player with the most varied treasures wins!

Hill Hill is a first of its kind card-based billiards strategy game. Whether you’re an amateur pool player or a pro, this game provides unique challenges, helpful tools, and a pathway to improving your game in a fun, competitive new way.

Players take turns drawing a card and attempt to complete challenges/runs on the table. If you’re successful, you keep the card and accrue points. Players can also use certain cards to make their path to the “Hill” easier, or more difficult for opponents.

Three hundred ocean creatures populate the board of this carefully engineered game. Some are familiar like the blue whale, sockeye salmon and the clownfish, others less so, like the water bear, barreleye and horse-eye jack.

Pick a counter from the box and be the first to spot that animal on the board!

Each counter features the animal's commonly used name, so you can learn about different species as you go.
The hexagonal game board is composed of several double-sided triangular pieces that you can rearrange to keep the search for sea critters a challenge!
Featuring 300 charming bespoke illustrations by Caroline Selmes.
It's simple to understand and play, but addictively competitive!
A fun and challenging game to delight adults and children alike.

There are rules for 3 games included:

1. Leisure travel
Before the start of the game, place all the round buttons - printed side down - on any city ring! Then place the information boards face down anywhere across the railway lines! Distribute the tokens evenly among all players and leave 20 in the till! The first player rolls the die and may use a colored button to cover as many sections from a drawn location as he rolls (6 points = 12 sections). You choose your journey in such a way that you reach buttons that you can collect as quickly as possible. If you come across a notice board, you have to follow the given instructions as soon as you have traveled the route you rolled. If you can catch up with a fellow player, he has to return to Bern and pay the snatcher 5 points. If there are only 10 buttons left on the playing field, you can fly, i. H. the sections between two city circles are now considered sections. Whoever has the most points at the end of the game wins. Not only the tokens count as points, but also the buttons and information boards that you have scooped up.

2. Lay routes
Distribute the buttons evenly among the players! Each player places them face up in front of them! The first player places a port location. If he owns subsequent locations, he may move on. Once he has occupied a node, he can set the new direction of travel, provided he has an adjoining location. If he cannot continue, it is the next player's turn. If you reach an end point or a line that is already occupied, you can start a new one following an already occupied line. The first person to lay out their buttons wins.

3. Zigzag
Place all buttons, except fives, face down on any location! The first player draws a button and goes to the location printed on it. If this is occupied, he keeps the first button and continues in the same way with the new one. But if he comes to an uncovered place, the following player may continue. In the first round you can take a maximum of 5 buttons! Whoever buys the most buttons wins.

—user summary

boardgame: Ports: A Game of Battles with Boats
description:
Ports is a game about abstracted trade between two city-states along a life-giving river. The genesis of the game was as a chess mini-game in an RPG, but it quickly became more interesting than its container. It plays similar to traditional race games such as Backgammon, Sennet, and the Royal Game of Ur, with its canonical theme being trade and privateers competing on a Nile-type river.

On a fish-shaped board, there are two ports on one side and a winding river that overlaps itself that connects the two. Each player starts with three merchant ships in their port and there are two reinforcements for each player on the island in the middle of the river. Each player rolls four binary dice in order to move their ships along the river, ultimately to the other player's port. If one player's boat lands the other's boat, then they roll to see who wins the battle and whose boat needs to retreat to their original port. Whoever gets three boats to their opponent's port first wins!

—description from the designer

Participation in the dice game “Left and Right” is unlimited.
The amount of the bet must first be determined and placed, e.g. B. 20 coins per participant.

Dice case "Nimm"
Nimm 1: the player takes 5 coins. from the cash register
Nimm 2: the player takes 10 coins from the pool
R Nimm 1: the neighbor to the right of the dice player takes 5 coins from the cash register.
L Nimm 1: the neighbor to the left of the dice player takes 5 coins from the cash register.
Alles Nimm 2, so each player takes 10 coins from the cash register.

Dice case "Gib"
Exactly the same regulations apply as for "Take" with the only difference that the player himself or his neighbor "Left and Right" has to pay into the till.

Nimm deins (take yours): With this roll of the dice, the dice thrower takes his full bet from the till.

Setz doppelt (pay double): In this case, the thrower has to place double the stake in the cash register.

Nimm die Hälfte (take half): the dice player takes half the content of the cash register.

✝: Here the player is dead for one turn (i.e. the next following one) and has to sit out once.

If the cash register is empty due to a payout, the game begins again. 
Play to empty the cash register: this can be done according to the following rules:

     Each player is paid an equal part of the pool.
     You roll the till "dead" by counting each roll as "take". So it's just take.


—user summary

On lâche rien! is an observation and reflexes game for all ages.

Players take it in turns to turn over a card to form three piles. These will represent the procession.

Depending on the layout of the cards, you may have to tap certain piles. If you succeed to be the first, you win the cards in the pile. The player who collects the most cards before the end of the game wins.

The game is accompanied by a booklet that provides keys to understanding police practices in France. It was written by two associations fighting for the freedom to demonstrate and denouncing police violence: ACAT-France (Action by Christians for the Abolition of Torture) and LDH (Ligue des droits de l'Homme).

The Battle of Stalingrad is a low to moderate complexity tactical game set during 1942 of World War II. It pits the Russian forces against the German army. The stage is set...

1942, Southern Russia. The German Army, under General Friedrich Paulus, approaches the outskirts of Stalingrad. The city is named after the Russian legend, Joseph Stalin. The Germans are set on conquering the heavily fortified Russian metropolis. The Russians are determined to put up a stiff resistance, fighting from the rubble in what some would term the "War of the Rats."

Winter is approaching. It is imperative the Germans conquer the city quickly as demanded by German High Commander Adolf Hitler. The Russians intend to make Stalingrad the death knell of the German Sixth Army. The stage is set...

Battle of Stalingrad is the first game in the Battles series to focus on World War II.  It adds tanks and planes while keeping ease of play.

The game takes about 90 minutes to play. One player commands the German forces and the other player commands the Russian forces. Cards vary the game play.

—description form the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Series: Battle Series of World Battles (Historical Game Company)

boardgame: Kosmogonia 2086: Oracle of the Dead – The Card Game!
description:
Oracle of the Dead is an expansion of Kosmogonia 2086 - Kronos Epilogue for 3-5 players.
This expansion introduces as a new player the King of the Underworld, who claims victory asymmetrically from his opponents. Hades tries to outpower Kronos and take his place as the game's ultimate villain in the final showdown.

Also added to the game is Acheron, a new area with powerful treasures, a horde of undead spearmen, as well as many necromantic spells that contain the hidden knowledge of the oracle of the Dead.
Choose when to unleash the power of Persephone, summon the mighty Cerberus, and recruit the souls of your defeated opponents. Take the power of the grim lord into your hands and rule from the depths of the earth to the top of Olympus!

Electric horse racing to simulate the Kentucky Derby.

Race your Horse against your opponents' Horses and collect points for your finish in multiple races. The first Horse to reach 21 points wins the Day at the Derby.

Points:
1st: 4
2nd: 3
3rd: 2
4th: 1

Players pick fighters to represent them in the mother of all show-downs - a slap fight!

With customized dice to represent your fighters life, and cards that give you an arsenal of items, defenses, and attacks, roll your way to victory!

Plays similar to Exploding Kittens or Unstable Unicorns, with a strong dice rolling element to it.

—description from the designer

Only one of you will escape the Island alive. Who will that be? Apocalypso is a fast-paced dice game where players aim to be the last one standing. The game starts with the youngest player and moves clockwise from there. Each player gets to roll the dice up to three times to achieve their desired roll.

This is a fun and exciting party game that requires strategy and luck. With its easy-to-follow rules and quick gameplay, it's a great choice for game night with friends and family.

—description from the publisher

A Monster Pack (Level 3) available during Kickstarter campaigns.

Includes:
- 2x separators (one in cardboard and one in plastic)
- 15x monster cards

Nuts is the strategic memory game which will drive you nuts. Players take up the role of a Squirrel trying to hide their nuts and then trying to find them again. The winner is the player who finds the most nuts and avoids finding the trash.

The game is played in two stages - groundwork and discovery … or rediscovery more like.   During the first stage you will take turns placing tiles until all tiles in play are placed. Each player can see their own tiles, but not the opponents.

Note: Every new tile must touch a previous one to form one picture.  Keep in mind that you are trying not only to remember where you place your nuts, but also to confuse your opponents and make sure they don’t find all of theirs.

When all the tiles are laid out and you have a nice picture of a forest floor, it’s time to go Nuts!

Second stage: All players take turns picking a tile and flipping it until every player has flipped exactly 10
tiles and placed them in front of them. The aim is of course to pick the tiles that have your nuts underneath. There are 10 nuts in every set and as you get exactly 10 tries, there is
no room for mistakes.

When all players have picked their 10 tiles, it’s time to score.   The player who scored highest, wins the game.

Le Fléau de Martina comes with two scenarios and one campaign from the battle of Yarmouk in 636 to the assassination of the third calife in 656.

After several defeats against the armies of the Arab Caliphate, the old Byzantine emperor Heraclius presides over an empire practically cut in two. Far from lamenting, the emperor is determined to continue the fight at any price. Tensions are at their height because in addition to the military struggle, Heraclius' health and war efforts make the question of his succession more important than ever. Empress Martina is his second wife and the mother of nine of his eleven children. Two siblings will only complicate the succession and thereby compromise the resistance against the Arabs.
But despite early Arab victories, Syria is not yet subdued and casualties from fighting and epidemics are high on both sides. Each side stands to lose if they are not careful. While Byzantine armies rally in Asia Minor and Egypt, Arab troops are bogged down in sieges of cities and ports across Syria. Will the Byzantines be able to regroup and reverse the situation with a military coup. Will the Arabs be able to parry the attack and complete the conquest of the Levant?

This game sees two armies confronting each other as they seek to reorganize their forces. The Byzantine player must retake Syria and, at the same time, avoid the loss of Egypt and Asia Minor while managing a succession crisis.
The Arab player must secure the Syrian terminus of the trade route to Mecca but also seize the Egyptian breadbasket and perhaps even Constantinople.

Units: 1 counter= from a few hundred to 2000 men
Fleets: 1 counter= circa 100 ships
Map: around 50km per hex
Turn: One year
Difficulty level: 6/9

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Can you make it to the mansion without waking the ghost?

Try your luck and roll the dice, collecting Candy along the way and trying to beat other players' scores without going over the ghosts!

Roll dice for each round and take turns placing them on your path.

Use Super Trick cards to boost your score and collect your Candy rewards!

—description from the publisher

Let out your inner child to create the best problem-solving hybrids!

Flip over a Need card with a problem for all players to solve.

Choose a Thing players have to use to solve it.

Players scramble to find a crazy combo using the communal Thing card and a card from their hand and doodle the best solution!

—description from the publisher

Will you be the master of spice?

You and your friends are testing your luck to see who can eat the spiciest peppers.

Take turns eating small amounts of peppers to build up your Spicy Tolerance Level.

If you feel you can handle the heat, compete in a Spicy Pepper Eating Challenge. The first player to successfully complete 3 challenges wins the game!

—description from the publisher

Fill your bento box with tasty food tiles, complete goals and score points. May the best bento win!

Take turns to pick a food tile, a card or a partition from the resource grid.

Try to group food tiles of the same type together on your bento board and create compartments for them with partitions.

Use your Ability cards to help your game and complete Goal cards to boost your score.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Magnificent Flying Machines: The Great Air Races Expansion
description:
Between the years 1903 when the Wright brothers took to the skies at Kitty Hawk and 1909 aviation advanced gradually, but nothing encourages progress so much as competition, preferably for a prize. It was in the Spring of 1909 that the first aviation meetings and races were held in locations in France which paved the way for the First International Air Race at Reims in August.

Virtually everyone in the aviation business turned up and the event fired off a series of meetings mostly held around Europe and in the USA where pilots would compete on laps of the airfields- each attempting to fly further, higher, and faster than before. Finally, by 1911, Flying Machines had the range for races of hundreds of miles between cities, across countries and crossing the sea. The era of the Great Air Races had arrived.

The Great Air Races is an expansion to the game Magnificent Flying Machines which adds in varied terrain, new weather conditions, pilots and equipment and a series of race cards that recreate the courses and races in early Aviation history.

—description from the publisher

The Sequence Variant adds another layer of strategy to the standard Yakatabune rules by requiring players to play Fireworks cards from their hand in order from left to right. After drawing their starting hands, players can only change the order of their cards by making use of red Yakatabune cards. Forethought and careful planning will be the keys to victory in this clash of master pyrotechnicians!

—description from the designer

boardgame: 三つ星動物園 (Three Star Zoo)
description:
In Three Star Zoo, you place animals in cages from 1 to 12, roll the dice, and if your animal is in the cage with the correct number of stars, you can get coins or take coins from other players depending on the effect. If you get coins, you can expand your zoo by placing new animals, and finally, the person who collects three "stars," a sign of excellence, will win the "three-star" zoo.

—user summary

Pizza Time is a real-time dexterity game. Two players are racing to build pizzas based on their order cards. The conveyor belt is battery-operated, and players try to stack ingredients like pepperoni, tomatoes, and peppers on their pizzas using tweezers. At the end of the conveyor line, the pizza is placed on a plate. The conveyor belt gradually accelerates during play, so later pizzas become more difficult to assemble.

—description from the publisher

Should teenagers be given the vote? Should dangerous science be banned? Should fun jobs get lower salaries? You are the President. You decide in this social board game that will have you debating hot topics with your friends and family.

The President has been assassinated and you, the Senators, must vote on the issues facing your fictional state - while keeping one eye on the presidency, of course! Wrestle with political dilemmas of the past, present, and future and navigate the election without damaging your image. As you get closer to the top spot, find out how well you really know your friends and discover who has what it takes to lead the country.

The goal is to become the next popular president by gaining 125 popularity points. You move on the game board by rolling a die, and can land on different events that affects popularity. You draw different question cards which all players have to take a stance and vote on one of three answers, or come up with a better idea that all players may also vote on. When there's a tie, you'll have to debate and convince other players to change their vote.

Chaos Chess is a genre-mashing game that turns a common chessboard into a dynamic and exciting board game. The core rules of the game remain, but humorous cards allow players to break the rules of chess to do fantastic stuff like


     Make a piece move like a different piece
     Move an opponents piece for them
     Freeze a piece so it can't move
     Send a piece to or from "Limbo"


And much more. There are counters to track permanent and temporary effects. Eight new pieces included with this version highlight how flexible the game of chess really is - all while punching the game up to the next level. Pieces include


     Anti-Pawn
     Dragon
     Flame Snake
     Frankenpawn
     Last Pawn Standing
     Many Colored Animals
     Pinball Wizard
     Superpawn


Players can play cards on their turn by spending cards from their hand. Their opponent can "counter" any card played by discarding three cards. Players must balance card play with chess to achieve checkmate.

—description from the designer

You will need at least 3 players for a battle. One person will pull a card. Then both competitors have to pick a song based on that same card and perform. The 3rd player will act as the judge and decide who won that round (based on song selection and overall performance).      

If you have more than 3 players that's okay, you just need someone to act as the judge each round. Keep going until each player has battled at least 4 times, and then declare the winner!

If playing with groups, first pick group names just to add to the fun. There should be 2 or more groups battling at a time, and 1 group serving as the judges. One person from the first group will pull a card, and all of the groups have to pick a song based on that same card and perform.

The judges will decide which group won that round (based on song selection and overall performance). Keep going until each group has battled at least 4 times, and then declare a winner!

Tricks of the Trade adds a unique twist to the classic concept of a Trick taking game through negotiation and trading. True to its name, players negotiate and exchange cards with each other to try to find an advantage before the Tricks Phase of the game. The unique Tech Deck game effects on cards give players different asymmetric 1-time use abilities through the game making every game fresh and exciting.

OBJECTIVE – The objective of the Tricks of the Trade is to score points. Players score points by bidding accurately, winning tricks, and winning tricks containing cards matching the domain (suit) of their goal card. Skillful negotiation and trading helps players score by improving their odds to win tricks or take tricks with cards matching their goal cards.
GAME PLAY – In each round, players get to trade with each other. Then, they bid on how many tricks they think they will win. Players get to use a Game Effect from a card in their hand. They play 6 rounds of trick-taking and then determine scores based on tricks won.
GAME END AND WINNING – Tricks of the Trade ends after three rounds of play.
THEME – Trick Taking Variant Game

—description from the designer

boardgame: Star Trek: Away Missions – Commander Sela: Romulan Expansion
description:
The Romulans are always after more Intel on their foes, learning their strengths and weaknesses and how to defeat them.
While doing so they will be running plots within plots, planning assassinations and devious stratagems to derail their opponent’s plans.

Romulans avoid conflict until they have a decisive advantage, then make a well-directed surgical strike.

—description from the publisher

The Summer Fair is a quest-based game where players assume the identity of one of multiple animals. Said animal has a series of preparations they need to complete before the aforementioned Summer fair arrives.

The game is played without a die, with players being able to move anywhere from one to five squares every turn. As they move, players draw item cards(representing items they found on the forest floor) and encounter obstacles.

Obstacles are cards that are drawn once a player's pawn steps on designated squares. These cards will have requirements on them. The most common way to overcome an obstacle is by discarding a certain amount of common items (as specified on the card). Players are encouraged to make up explanations for how they would use the items to overcome the obstacle (i.e: I use the pen as a pole vault to jump over the fallen tree).

Each character has special abilities that act as boosts for movement, storage space... or as hindrances to the other characters. First one to finish their quests and return home wins. The Summer fair has arrived.

—description from the designer

This expansion contains 3 new modules that can be played independently or together:


    The King's Whims


At the beginning of each day, randomly draw a special card that describes the King's Whims. It explains what the King expects from his subjects for this turn and temporarily changes the rules, creating mini objectives for the day to earn the King's favors, which will help you for the rest of the game.


    The Throne Room


The Throne Room is a new place that gives access to new powers. However the places are very limited, even more so than in the other quarters of the city.


    Alliance or Betrayal


1 Tarot card used to signal alliance or betrayal.

boardgame: The Greatest Adventure: Stories from the Bible – Noahs' Ark
description:
"Journey with Noah and the Ark through the great flood."

"This fun and easy game is intended to help understand the stories, events, and people in the Bible with questions and answers."

"Each player in turn reads a question card. The player turns over the question card to see if the question was answered correctly. If correct, the player moves forward on the game path the same number of spaces indicated on the card."

No spinners or dice used.

Players race to collect the 5 virtues of responsibility, courage, honesty, generosity and friendship on their playing board.

Each board has spots with a character from "Adventure from the Book of Virtues" and a virtue. Players spin to create a match and cover each match with a chip.  If the player fills five boxes in a row, they shout Bingo! and win the game.

Make It To Monday is a board game made by gays, for gays and the people who love them. The game blends chance, strategy and "a little bit of shade" for gameplay that feels familiar and fresh at the same time, with distinctive gay themes.

Players must work their way from Friday night to Monday morning. At each turn, players draw a Weekend card to earn Fun points. Weekend cards come in 4 categories: Sex, Party Favors, Travel and Breeders (straight people, for the uninitiated). Players are dealt Choices cards - and more can be picked up along the way - these are used at the end of a players turn to increase their own Fun points or cut down someone else's Fun.

The game ends when the first player Makes It To Monday, but the winner is determined by tallying up the Fun points, including any bonuses or deductions from the Choices cards. The player with the most Fun points wins, because after all, the weekend isn't about who survives, but who has the most fun!

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Captain's log. Stardate 77214.5
... We have stranded on an unknown, dark planet
... The electromagnetic storm has pulled all power from our gear
... Only our laser blasters are still working
... Ahead we hear a sinister laughter
... Someone is up to something evil
... We activate our blasters, and go into battle in the dark!

In Zapp'em, 4 astronauts team up to take on 1 sinister alien villain. Players can also choose to play battle royale mode: every man for himself.

On a fictitious 8x9 grid, players take turns announcing where they stand by playing a grid card from their hand. From that location you can either:

     pick up an item: special guns, shields, nightvision or hearts
     use objects, or
     inflict a hitpoint at your enemy if you are within shooting range.


The astronauts can team up and share items. The villain can apply all three actions in one turn, if possible.

Whoever takes out the other, wins! You have to rely on tactics and cooperation: since the game plays in darkness, you never know where your opponent is. You don't see the hit points coming, which makes the game tense and exciting!

—description from the publisher

Finally vacation! But don't count on any relaxation. Here, maximum speed applies from start to finish. You have 18 days to visit as many destinations as possible. You travel by car, plane or boat.

Rules.

And so before you is a gorgeous map of the Swedish Kingdom.
At the beginning of the game, 4 destination cards are revealed. To have time to collect as many cards as possible, 18 days are given.
Players move their chips, from city to city, following the directions given after the scroll of the arrow.
A spinning arrow can indicate the number of steps from 3 to 5, a question mark, a ticket, a day.
Steps can be used both backwards and forwards within a single move.
The question mark means that you will have to answer 1 out of 3 difficult questions (for adults) or 1 out of 2 easy ones (for children). Both categories of questions concern Sweden. The prize for the correct answer will be any of the opened 4 destination cards.
A ticket means the player receives an additional ticket (one is already available at the start), which can be used when flying or sailing on a ship.
1 day means that the player advances the chip on the board with 18 days of vacation one day ahead, thereby bringing its end closer.
Of course, the lucky one who visits the largest number of cities wins.

Fair winds, guys!

—user summary

boardgame: Blood Bowl: Second Season Edition – Gutter Bowl
description:
Gutter Bowl is an exciting expansion to Blood Bowl that takes the action off the professional pitch – and into the streets and sewers of the Old World! You'll coach a team of enthusiastic – if inexperienced – players as they settle their differences in brutal street sports. What Gutter Bowl lacks in showmanship, sponsorships, and sporting competence compared to its big brother, Blood Bowl, it more than makes up for in violence and near-complete disregard for the rules!

Join the fast-paced tactical fantasy football game in a new urban setting, as your amateur players weave between obstacles, beat up opponents, and score those all-important touchdowns. This expansion includes a double-sided Gutter Bowl pitch – one side depicting a crowded marketplace packed with stalls, the other depicting a foul sewer – as well as a pair of matching double-sided dugouts. There's also rulebook featuring the gritty backstreets history of Gutter Bowl, plus new rules for playing games and leagues on (and underneath) the mean streets of the Old World.

This set contains:

     1x 36-page Blood Bowl: Gutter Bowl rulebook
     1x Double-sided fold-out Gutter Bowl pitch
     2x Matching double-sided dugouts


—description from the publisher

GNOMES - 3 HAPPY GAMES

COLLECTIONS AND COLORS
A great set of three fun games for the whole family.
Join the three gnome brothers, move to their magical world and play with them and learn.

The set consists of 3 games:

1. CATCH THE GNOME
In this game, we spread all the gnomes on the table or floor so that they form a circle.
Cards with sentences are placed in a pile, face down.
The player who starts, takes the first instruction tablet, reads it aloud, then puts it back to form a new pile.
Other participants do the same.
The players perform the presented task, and the collected gnomes are arranged in front of them.
The game continues until all the gnomes are collected or the task cards are used.
The winner is the one with the most gnomes.

2. PATTERNS AND COLORS
This game has a similar beginning to the previous one.
We spread the gnomes on the table or the floor, and the tiles with the patterns are placed in a pile, reverse side up.
One player distributes one tile to all participants.
Players keep their tiles face down in front of them.
At the start signal given by the youngest participant, everyone turns over their tiles.
Players choose gnomes matching their pattern and color for their tiles.
If one of the game participants decides that he already has all the matching elves, he draws another tile.
During the game, players can draw tiles any number of times until they run out.
The game ends when the gnomes or tiles with patterns run out.
The player who collects the most gnomes wins.

3. WHERE ARE YOU BROTHER?
The rules change here.
The gnomes land on the table or floor face down.
All players receive 4 tiles to start and keep them in front of them.
Each participant takes turns flipping one elf.
If the pattern of his clothes matches one of the tiles of a given player, he takes the elf to himself.
Otherwise, he puts it back face down.
The game continues until one player collects three brothers who will have clothes containing the same pattern from one of their tiles.
However, the fun with gnomes does not end here, as it is limited only by children's imagination.
You can invent new rules of the game at will.

The Power Generation is a skirmish game where music is power. Play as one of four WarBands of different music genres from the titular Power Generation, a generation of musicians who gained the ability to fight with the power of their music. Your goal is to either perform at venues on the customizable hex-grid map, or defeat the opposing WarBands in combat. The first WarBand to control 3 venues, or the last WarBand standing, is the winner.

—description from the designer

Xander has always loved the circus, now that he’s seen a dog circus he plans to train his own pup to learn how to stand on its hind legs, walk a ramp, even jump through a hoop!

Players will be creating the best dog circus they can to impress Xander, but in order to do so, they will need to win tricks. In this trick-taking game the trump suit changes each hand, with 4 total hands being played. Players must follow suit, play a card from the matching suit of the original card that led the trick if possible. After all players have played a card the trick ends.

Winning the trick: 
The highest ranked card matching the original suit played wins the trick and that player takes the cards. The player fills in a value of card onto a dog trick on their sheet. Tricks are worth points.

Losing the trick:
The player(s) who lost the trick will count the number of cards from one suit they have in their hand, they will write down the total number (or less as players can bluff) in the left most box of the matching suit on the player sheet (left side). When the boxes for the suit are full, the player can no longer count that suit.

Scoring:
Players add up the numbers for each dog trick on their player sheet, X counts as a 0, and write the total value of the numbers in the larger boxes. Players also add up the numbers they have written for each suit on the left side of the player sheet.

The player who has the most total points is the winner.

Play your cards as cleverly as possible to maximize your points!

—description from the designer

Monsters Deck is a collection of 24 tarot-sized cards, each with a monster for Four Against Darkness.
You may use these whenever you would roll on a table to determine which Vermin, Minion, Boss or Weird Monster you encounter. Instead of rolling, draw a card from the appropriate deck, play it and then reshuffle it into the deck. You can combine monsters decks together, expanding the variation of encounters with every new set released.

boardgame: Word Travellers: The Lost Luggage Card Game
description:
Lost Luggage is a game about reuniting lost luggage with their destination airports. It is a word game for two to five players, aged 7+. Using a combination of airport codes and single A-Z letters, call out words and drop off your lost luggage.

COMPONENTS

     five sets of 26 A-Z baggage cards 
     96 airport code cards
     one die.


SETUP 
Shuffle the airport code cards and place face down between the players. Give each player a complete set of 26 A-Z baggage cards to keep in-hand.
 GAME PLAY
Play in rounds until someone depletes all of their baggage cards or six consecutive rounds fail to make words. On each round:

Someone flips over an airport card and rolls the die, whose number indicates the maximum number of baggage cards to drop.

All players:

     Think of a word to make.
     Secretly select one or more letter in-hand to combine with one airport code (if possible).
     When all are ready, each player calls out their word.
     Drop the letters to verify their word.
     Discard their letter cards.
     Discard the airport card.


Play in rounds until someone has used all of the letter cards.
If you cannot make a word, try next time.

—description from the publisher

The aim of Arráncate Cuco is to teach children how to face different things that scares them.

Players will take a card with a fear and use a card with a strategy to face that fear (usually they will have to talk about the strategy)

The first player to face all fears in their board will win.

This is the second seasonal expansion for Small City Deluxe Edition.

It contains several new concepts never seen in Small City:

     Road tiles and Place tiles to connect your Residences to your Factories
     Specialized Citizens (policemen, traders...)
     Riots can happen depending on 2 New Action characters
     8 New real cities in Europe with 8 new twists
     2 New real buildings: Neuschwanstein Schloss and the Coloseum
     New Terrain: Forests with more planks.
     Build new Palaces and Statues


and of course more promise cards!

—description from the designer

This is the third seasonal expansion for the base game Small City Deluxe Edition.
It contains new concepts never seen in Small City:

     Supply Water to your buildings with Water Pumps
     2 New Action Characters Cards
     New Specialized Citizens (The teachers, the Workers...)
     New Real cities in North America with 8 new twists
     New Terrain: The beach
     New Real Buildings : the Golden Gate, the Statue of Liberty
     A new Citizen variant with the 11 men and 11 women provided in the base game
     3 New Leisure Buildings to make the game more complex


and of course, more promise cards

—description from the publisher

INTRODUCTION Cataclysm is a two-player game played on a square board of any size, initially empty. The two players, Red and Blue, take turns placing their own checkers on the board, one checker per turn, starting with Red. Passing is not allowed. Mark Steere designed Cataclysm in July 2023.

OBJECT Red must form a path of red checkers (interconnected via horizontal or vertical adjacencies, or both) connecting the two red sides of the board. Diagonal adjacencies are irrelevant in Cataclysm. Blue must form a path of blue checkers connecting the two blue sides of the board.

PLACEMENT You can place a checker on any unoccupied square.

CROSSCUT A crosscut is a group of four adjacent checkers arranged in a square. Two of the checkers in a crosscut are red and two are blue. Like-colored checkers are diagonally opposed.

CATACLYSM Whenever you form a crosscut, you form a bicolored group containing two or more red checkers and two or more blue checkers. Immediately replace all of the enemy checkers in the group with friendly checkers, concluding your turn.

—description from the designer

boardgame: 探偵無宿2 椚の杜に潜む悪魔 (Unregistered Detective 2: The Devil Lurks in Kunugi Forest)
description:
Yohei Kunugi, respected business leader and head of the Kunugi family, was found dead in his room, leaving his family with only a vague will and a haunting enigma. When Yohei's younger brother also turns up dead, it's obviously a murder plot. But who's behind it? Could it be Yohei's widowed daughter? Or maybe it's his illegitimate daughter, kept hidden in the mansion? There's also the gardener he hired to do his dirty work. The murderer could be his grandson, secretly in love with a maid, or maybe it's another maid jealous of their affair? And just who is the mysterious child wearing the fox mask, known only as Forget-Me-Not?

The card game 探偵無宿2 椚の杜に潜む悪魔 (Unregistered Detective 2: The Devil Lurks in Kunugi Forest) is a remake, not a sequel, to Unregistered Detective. It's inspired by classic Japanese murder mysteries, especially those written by Edogawa Ranpo, but this is not a deduction game. It's an atmospheric game where players take on the roles of detectives, each with their own special ability, who try to influence the story to gain the most victory points.

Each round begins with 3 Character Cards, drawn from the Investigation Deck and placed in an Investigation Lineup. As the round progresses, players draw Character Cards and add them to the Lineup. When a character is drawn, they trigger different actions. The cards in the Lineup also represent resources, which players use to play Event Cards, representing significant events in the case. Some Event Cards require a certain number of character deaths or even the deaths of specific characters. Event Cards grant victory points, resources only available to that player, and helpful actions. When a player draws the Night of Tragedy card, the round ends. If a character has more of their Character Cards in the Lineup than anyone else, they are murdered and no longer active in the game. In the event of a tie, no one dies unless 5 Curse Tokens have built up.

The game begins with only 1 of each character in the deck and 1 Curse Token in the play area, so it's unlikely someone will die in the first round. But additional cards and Curse Tokens are added each round, so further deaths are inevitable. Players play increasingly significant Event Cards, crafting a different version of the story each game. Once a player accumulates 10 victory points or five characters have died, the game ends and the winning detective is decided.

Eight additional cards for Catacombs Conquest, originally offered as part of the Beyond the Event Horizon expansion during the Catacombs Conquest Kickstarter campaign, and later sold on their own.

Sword team:
Weeping Matriarch, Carnelian Knight, Jade Knight, Battle Robot

Skull team:
Necromancer, Specter, Gobin Boar Rider, Keystone Keeper

—user summary

A social deduction tabletop game for people who love food more than people. There are two sides: the APPLESTATES (APOSTATES) aiming to end the Human Gods' tyranny. The DISSAPPLES (DISCIPLES) believe paradise still exists within the Human Gods' mouths.

Players end up on one of these two sides. There are destruction cards that have to be added by the APPLESTATES to kill the Human God, while the DISSAPPLES have defuse cards that prevent those destruction cards from going off. Whether they kill the Human God or not, players will have to turn on each other to save themselves from death using the cards they have.

—description from the designer

An exciting deckbuilding card game, where you will play the role of a cunning fortune teller who manipulates fate to shape his own fortune. He summons powerful commanders, masters ancient spells, and unleashes masterful tactics as you compete against other divination masters in your quest for absolute dominance.

In a game of Wise Wars, players must use their arcana cards to obtain one of the two possible victory conditions:


     Reduce the opponent's health points to zero.
     If the arcane recruitment deck runs out, the player with more Power points will be victorious.


—description from the designer

boardgame: Star Wars: Shatterpoint – You Cannot Run Duel Pack
description:
There is no more embittered rivalry than the one between Jedi Master Obi-Wan Kenobi and Darth Vader, the emperor's most terrifying enforcer and apprentice. Their duels have shaped the very course of history. Clashing with unmatched intensity, each knows every strength and weakness of the other.

With Star Wars: Shatterpoint – You Cannot Run Duel Pack, you can recreate the pair's first meeting from the Obi-Wan series. This set includes two brand new characters for Shatterpoint — Darth Vader & Obi-Wan, Out of Hiding — along with an immersive terrain display that players can use to showcase the moment from the show when not using them in game.

In Tomes of Arkham each player is a Rare Tomes book collector in H. P. Lovecraft's town of Arkham. Each round players utilise different events in order to acquire the most Renowned Tomes collection. When a player acquires their 8th Tome then everyone calculates their Renown Points, the player with the highest Renown is the winner.

Players have a number of Resources that they can use to purchase Tomes from the common sale area. Each Tome is part of a larger collection which adds to the Renown that the owner gains at the end of the game. At the start of each round new Tomes are placed up for sale and the First Player chooses an Event card to play later in the round secretly, then passes them to the next player who chooses their event card for the round and so on.

The Events determine the player turn order and each includes a special ability that the player can use when it is their turn; Henry Armitage can prevent other Events playing, Creative Banking lets players steal resources from other players, Viewing lets players gain resources from the Lost Tomes Collection, Guard Dogs prevent a players Tomes being stolen, Wise Investments allow players to gain Resources, Collector allows players to acquire more than 1 Tome per Round, Wilbur Whateley allows players to steal Tomes and the Stars are Right makes acquiring Tomes cheaper. But players must beware, owning certain Tomes can lead to players becoming Unstable and unlocking bizarre game twisting actions such as swapping Tomes, moving the First Player and losing resources.

—description from the designer

boardgame: DC Deck-Building Game: Crossover Pack 10 – Flashpoint
description:
Add this Crossover Pack, based on the classic Flashpoint comic book series, to any DC Deck-Building Game base game to play as time-shifted Super Heroes as a war between the Atlanteans and Amazons threatens the world's safety. Energize your game with the overpowered Alternate Timeline-Up of cards to propel you to victory!

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Team up to save the DC Universe! Crisis Collection 2 brings together the second two Crisis Expansion Packs for the DC Deck-Building Game series and offers a wide variety of new cards to add to any base game. Get ready to face some of the most epic challenges in comic book history with the introduction of Crisis events and Impossible Mode Super-Villains. But this time you won’t be going it alone, thanks to cooperative gameplay that lets you team up to win… and survive!

Crisis Expansion Pack 3 and 4 reimagine the eight Super-Villains from Forever Evil and the eight Super Heroes from Teen Titans with Crisis versions for cooperative play. In addition, the Villains from Forever Evil and Super-Villains from Teen Titans—and some new ones—have been upgraded to Impossible Mode, making them much more difficult to defeat. There are even six new oversized Characters in each expansion to go along with all the new main deck cards!

This is not a standalone product. Requires any DC Deck-Building Game base game to play.

Key Features

Includes Crisis Expansion Pack 3 and 4
Adds cooperative gameplay mode with Crisis cards that require players to work together to overcome
FOIL oversized Character cards
Impossible Mode Super-Villains that add new challenges to cooperative and competitive modes
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: Funkoverse Strategy Game: Tim Burton's The Nightmare Before Christmas 101
description:
In the Funkoverse Strategy Game, you combine your favorite characters and go head-to-head in exciting game scenarios. Each turn, you select one of your characters and perform two actions. Each character has access to basic actions like moving and challenges as well as several unique abilities that may be performed only by spending ability tokens. Funkoverse uses an innovative “cooldown” system — the more powerful the ability, the longer it will take for the ability token to become available again — so players have to spend their ability tokens wisely. Each character in Funkoverse is unique, so players are encouraged to try out different combinations of characters and items in order to discover their favorite synergies and powerful strategies for the different game scenario.

Funkoverse Strategy Game: Tim Burton's The Nightmare Before Christmas 101 is an expansion for any standalone Funkoverse title, consisting of the trick-or-treaters Lock, Shock, and Barrel from the film The Nightmare Before Christmas. You can stack all three figures together to treat them as a single character, or separate them and add them to your teams individually as companions.

boardgame: Star Blazers Fleet Battle System Game: Module II – The Dark Nebula Empire
description:
The latest edition to the Star Blazers Fleet Battle System contains updated and expanded rules along with the ship and weapons statistics to recreate man of the battles between the warring space powers from the acclaimed science fiction animated series.

Book 0103 introduces the Dark Nebula Fleet, as well as additional fleet units for the expanded EDF, Gamilon, and White Comet Empires.

The information in this rule book is based on the movies Yamato: The New Voyage and Be Forever Yamato.

The Dark Nebula rule book comprises 76 pages of rules, plus 20 additional pages of optional rules which players can utilize, or use to modify existing scenarios. The game has three levels of play to allow players to acquire the skills to master the complete game system. Also included are 16 scenarios plus the “Assault on Gatlantis” mini-game.

Statistics for 43 new space warships have been added to the original 42 warships which were introduced in the first game book. A total of 85 redesigned warship display forms plus an additional eight pages of statistical data for the Gatlantis mini-game, as well as five pages of ship counters are included. The game can be played with these counters or with the lead-free miniatures especially designed for this game system. A complete catalog can be found in the rules book.

A Technical Manual with 143 illustrations and line drawings of the new ships, battlecraft, new weapon systems, and space stations are also included in this publication.

—description from the publisher

Masters of Myth is a multiplayer card game featuring gods from ancient mythologies, including from the Norse, Greek, Egyptian, and Aztec pantheons. In this competitive 2-4 player game you play as either Odin, Zeus, Ra or Quetzalcoatl wielding their godly powers to defeat your opponents. Minions, rituals, spells, and relics are all summoned using Devotion, which also acts as your health pool. Unlike other card games, when your devotion is reduced to 0 instead of losing you get a second chance, flipping your God card over getting a refreshed devotion pool and new abilities allowing you to mount a comeback. The goal is to be the last god standing.

Each deck contains 40 cards with 20+ unique cards in each deck. Each card features art from our team of multicultural artists, depicting a mythological figure or event. With simple rules, this game is accessible for all ages and experience levels. Game pace is quick allowing you to get through small games in under an hour.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Expansion to The Barons' War rules. Outremer (French: outre-mer, lit. 'overseas'), also known as The Crusader States, is a political hotbed granting ambitious Frankish Barons the opportunity to make a reputation for themselves. This new battleground dominated by a scorching sun, inhospitable terrain and lethal new foes provides fresh challenges for any Commander.

New rules, named characters, and a linked scenario-driven campaign, Peril on the Pilgrim Road, encourage you to recreate warfare in the crusader states. Choose to create a retinue from the Armies of Outremer: settled Franks, crusaders, or the military orders. Alternatively, select from the myriad of warriors that comprise the cultured Forces of Islam, led by a sultan or emir of a particular Askar.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Say What You See: Star Wars
description:
The Star Wars edition of Say What You See is a super version of the classic quick-fire catchphrase guessing game that includes 76 catchphrase cards and asks players to simply say what they see. Show off how well you know the ins and outs of the popular films as you work to sold themed pictogram puzzles related to various characters and items from the franchise.

—description from the publisher

In this game you will be presented with a series of Star Wars themed puzzling pictograms. The first player to correctly solve the pictogram wins the card. The first player to collect 5 cards wins the game and the title of Say What you See: Star Wars champion!

—description from the back of the box
This expansion provides 2 more sets of player components to play with up to 6 players.

Breya stands at the precipice of change. The power of the Primordials who once ruled the land has faded, and the fledgling mortals have begun to grow in power. Each of the four prominent peoples of Breya have positioned themselves to succeed, but only one can ascend to complete hegemony over Breya...

Dawn of Breya is an area control game set in an original high-fantasy world, where players vie for hegemony over the land of Breya. Players will control one of four unique factions, each with their own history and strategy.

Players will expand their territory and bolster their military, carefully managing their usage and harvesting of resources, utilizing them in numerous ways in a turn.

Expansion and exploitation of the land comes at a cost, however, as the abundant heartland territories at the center of Breya not only risk conflict with other players, but with the ancient beings that lurk in the heartland of Breya. The combat between the players and fearsome Primordials is the climax of the game, with the players setting aside their differences in order to stand a chance against the powers of the Primordial they awakened.

Each game will have branching paths in map construction, secret objectives, unique leaders, and the Primordials that are encountered. Each playthrough presents new challenges and features a tenuous balance between competition in territory expansion and collaboration in defeating mighty Primordials.

—description from the publisher

Time travel in the proper paws is profitable! You are a felonious feline who can traverse the space-time continuum. You will steal valuable art, religious, and science artifacts from famous locations and characters of the past, present, and future. However, if you leave too many paw prints behind when you pilfer, then the time patrol will imprison you. Anything and everything is yours for the taking! But only the most cunning cat can avoid getting caught, claim victory, and become the wealthiest of all time.

Purloin is a hand management heist game. As a time-traveling cat burglar, you will purloin valuables from the past, present, and future. The player(s) with the most paws is caught by the Time Patrol dogs and cannot gain Endgame bonuses. Then after bonuses, the player with the most wealth wins!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Masters of the Universe: Battleground – Wave 6: Evil Horde Faction
description:
Of Leeches and Scorpions

Expand your battles with an exciting add-on featuring two new heroes from the Evil Horde: 
Leech and Scorpia! The menacing Leech possesses the power to drain mana from his enemies,
making him an awe-inspiring adversary for all mages. Meanwhile, with her deadly stinger and
ability to control lesser minions, Scorpia is a fearsome melee fighter and a tough nut to crack. 
With these new characters, you can unleash even more mayhem on the battlefield and tip the
scales of victory in your favor!

-description from publisher

Box contains:

- 1x Additional rules booklet
- 1x Manual
- 2x Character minis
- 1x Terrain accessory
- 17x Cards

boardgame: Masters of the Universe: Battleground – Wave 6: Fighting Foe Men Faction
description:
Heavy Weaponry

Take your game to the next level with the Battle Ram, an unstoppable armored vehicle that
can be used by any faction! With its rugged construction and powerful weaponry, the Battle 
Ram can smash through enemylines and strike fear into their hearts. It's missile launchers
and mounted laser make it a versatile and deadly addition to any army, while its sturdy design
allows it to withstand even the most punishing attacks. Whether you are assaulting an enemy
stronghold or defending your own territory, the Battle Ram is an indispensable tool for
achieving victory on the battleground.

-description from publisher

Box contains:

- 1x Additional rules booklet
- 1x Token
- 1x Manual
- 1x Character mini
- 1x Hexagonal base
- 11x Cards

boardgame: Second Great War at Sea: Sword of the Sea
description:
The Second Great War at Sea alternative-history story arc looks at naval campaigns in a world where the First Great War ended in late 1916, allowing the great empires to survive for another generation and clash again in 1940.
 Sword of the Sea brings the naval action to the Red Sea, Gulf and Aden and Persian Gulf as the Ottoman Turkish fleet and its German allies face the Imperial Persian Fleet with Indian (!) and Italian support.

You get 180 new silky smooth, die-cut playing pieces: 100 of them double-sized “long” pieces depicting major warships and the remaining 80 normal-sized square ones representing small warships, transports, aircraft and some markers needed for play. Ships and aircraft bring the Ottoman Turkish and Imperial Persian navies that never existed to your game table.

Sword of the Sea includes 33 new scenarios telling the story of this alternative Second Great War in Middle Eastern waters. Sword of the Sea is not a complete game and is not playable by itself; you’ll need Horn of Africa (and only Horn of Africa).

The Second Great War at Sea is an exercise in world-building never seen in wargame publishing; our Second Great War background book tells the full story of this history that never happened.

—description from the publisher
family: Series: Second World War at Sea (Avalanche Press)

boardgame: Voice of the Arabs: A 1967 – Sword of Israel Story
description:
In June 1967, Israel needed just four days to crush the armies of Egypt and Jordan, and then two more to finish off Syria. It was an overwhelming defeat for the Arab powers, and it has shaped the power politics of the Middle East and the world at large ever since.

But that’s not the story the Egyptian public heard. The powerful radio station Voice of the Arabs, located in Cairo, pumped out an absurd set of “alternative facts” regarding the course of the war. In this tale, the Egyptians and their allies were beating the hell out of the Israelis and on their way to an overwhelming victory.

Voice of the Arabs is a Campaign Study (a short book of scenarios and history) based on the story of the 1967 Six-Day War as the radio station told it: with the Egyptians and their allies overcoming the well-trained and well-equipped Israelis. It is and it isn’t alternative history: the battles never happened, but the Egyptian-sponsored fake news said they did.

Voice of the Arabs adds 11 new scenarios to 1967: Sword of Israel, split into two chapters, each with a battle game to link the scenarios together. This being an Avalanche Press product, there’s also the background story of the battles that never happened. You’ll need 1967: Sword of Israel (and only 1967: Sword of Israel) to play all 11 scenarios.

—description from the publisher

Storyburg is a co-op adventure story deckbuilding game.

A mysterious force is ripping apart Storyburg. Explore the perils solo or as a team by playing as Dorothy Gale, Peter Pan, Alice Liddell and Little Nemo. Players will travel to Oz, fly to Neverland, fall down the rabbit hole to Wonderland, and dream of Slumberland. Solve the mystery of the Pages and save Storyburg before it’s too late!

Players will equip cards, play actions, and create ultimate dice rolls in a thrilling adventure for the ages. Elements of dungeon-crawlers, deck-building, and pick-your-own-stories create a unique gameplay each time. With five action-packed Chapters, Storyburg will entertain your gaming group through multiple sessions.

—description from the designer
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Peter Pan

description:
Welcome to  Meerkat Kingdoms, a board game set in the diverse and treacherous Kalahari desert. In this untamed wilderness that spans three different countries and endures extreme temperatures, survival is a fierce challenge for its inhabitants. Among them, Meerkats embody resilience as they bravely navigate a world filled with perils, including predators like eagles, snakes, and hyenas. But the dangers don't end there – rival Meerkat families also vie for dominance.

In Meerkat Kingdoms, you step into the role of a Meerkat queen, tasked with securing your family's supremacy in this harsh environment. It's time to prove the strength of your matriarchy.  Meerkat Kingdoms is an area control game for with a very light Meerkat placement element. By strategically pairing your dice rolls, you must decide which crucial locations to deploy your Meerkat meeples to. Carefully consider which locations to fight over because if both players have an equal number of Meerkats there, neither will gain control.

The gameplay continues until players exhaust their supply of Meerkat meeples. However, once only one player remains with Meerkat meeples, the game reaches its final round, do your best to position your Meerkats wisely and faster than your opponents!

To secure victory, players must employ their best strategies to claim the most valuable locations and complete secret objective cards. It is through these endeavors that one can attain the most points and emerge triumphant as the ultimate Meerkat family in the Kalahari.

Push your luck and color your way into the highest scoring canine!

In Collar by Number, players roll a die and select a color to add to their pup’s picture. Each color scores points in a unique way, and may be improved by the colors around it - but be careful! Not all color combinations play nicely with each other!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Die Abenteuer des Robin Hood: Die Solo-Variante
description:
You're alone! Your friends left days ago. They went in search of Blackgarden, the infamous prison tower of the Sheriff. Perhaps they will succeed in freeing the prisoners there. Meanwhile, you are left behind in Sherwood Forest. Will you succeed in giving new courage to the people of the county?

German language Solo variant published during Covid lockdown.  To win, you must reach 18 Hope before the last hourglass is removed from the evil end?  Or, if you succeed in completing the 3 extra tasks, you win immediately!
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Robin Hood

boardgame: Roll N' Pin: The Brazilian Jiu Jitsu Game for the Whole Family
description:
Roll 'n' Pin' is an engaging 2-player tabletop game that simulates Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu competitions. Players assume the roles of skilled BJJ competitors, aiming to defeat their opponent either by achieving a "submission" or accumulating more points during the 10-minute match.

The game incorporates a combination of cards and dice, employing a straightforward war-style mechanic with a rock-paper-scissors system. This design choice ensures easy accessibility for players while allowing for strategic depth. To secure victory, players must defeat their opponent's cards, claim them, and add them to their own "submission" collection. The player who successfully gathers all the required cards for their submission emerges as the winner. Alternatively, players can opt to accumulate points cards to pursue victory through points.

Adding to the excitement, Roll 'n' Pin introduces "VAI!" cards, which serve as action cards. These cards offer players opportunities to bolster their strategies or make it more challenging for their opponent to achieve success.

The game thoughtfully incorporates various design details that create a strong connection to the theme of Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu while providing an avenue for players to learn about the martial art. Notably, the point system utilized in the game closely mirrors the scoring systems employed in actual BJJ tournaments.

Overall, Roll 'n' Pin offers an immersive and cohesive gaming experience, merging the world of BJJ with dynamic gameplay mechanics and thematic elements that educate and entertain players.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Mini Rogue: Sentinel & Crypt Promo Cards
description:
Sentinel & Crypt are two promo cards for Mini Rogue, packaged together.

     The mysterious Sentinel protects a secret door using their large shield and nasty spear.
     Built centuries ago, the Crypt contains danger, items, and maybe even a passageway to the unknown.


Because they require Lore cards, The Council or the Depths of Damnation expansion is required to play these. They were originally released at Essen 2023.

Falklands-Malvinas 1982 Operacional is an Operational level board-wargame for 2+ players (by teams) that simulates the air-naval campaign during the war for the Falklands-Malvinas islands in 1982 between Argentina and United Kingdom.

The game runs within a hexagonal board (50 kms/hex), showing from an operational perspective the combat actions between ships and aircraft of both sides.

Its mechanics are realistic and very close to the tactical, trying to represent all decision levels. You must determine what type of units to attack, with what, and what weapons to do so or to defend yourself. The mechanics used make it possible to recreate all types of combat and operations (air-to-air, air-to-surface, anti-submarine warfare, detection, bombing...), even giving the British player the chance to land on the Islands.

The use of random dice is relegated to the last phase of the player's decision, each threat in the game has its counterpart for its neutralization, that is where the player must decide who, how and with what... Detection in this game, it is the main mechanic, without knowing where your enemy is, you will hardly be able to damage him.

A replayable game where either side can win through different levels of victory, which does not leave the Argentine player as a mere spectator.

The game offers several scenario options. 
1. Combat scenarios (very few units, for learning the different mechanics). 
2. Historical scenarios (divided by actual days of combat, where historical actions such as the attack on HMS Sheffield, Operation Black Buck, and attacks on San Carlos Bay can be simulated). 
3. Campaign (the turns represent the entire campaign, where players can plan their own strategies based on historical victory conditions).

Game will be published in english/spanish language.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
The Liangzhu Civilization, located in Hangzhou, China, dates back to around 5300-4000 years ago, is one of the oldest civilization of mankind. In the game of Liangzhu, each player takes on the role of a tribe leader aiming to develop their agriculture by selecting cards and completing mission cards across three eras to increase taxes, bolster their military strength to defend against external threats, and utilize craftsmen to produce artifacts. Simultaneously, they must attend to the construction of canals to prevent flooding. After the three eras, the player leader of the most developed tribe wins the game.

在良渚这款桌面游戏之中，每位玩家扮演一名古时良渚的部落领袖，通过在三个时代选取卡牌并尽量完成任务卡，来发展自己的农业以增加赋税，发展自己的战力以抵御外敌，使用匠人制作工艺品，同时也需要注意修建水渠来阻挡泛滥的洪水。三个时代过后，发展最好的部落的玩家领袖获得游戏的胜利。

Alicorns is "The Niece's Game," designed for my 5-year-old niece who loved it from the beginning. It has been showcased as a prototype at various fairs and has been adored by Moms with Daughters, Grandmothers with Granddaughters, Kids, and Families of all ages who played it with me. Now it's ready and printed :)

Alicorns are happy creatures when they all fly together, like the colors on a rainbow :)

If you're playing with 2 players, choose 4 Alicorns each. If you're playing with 3 or 4 players, choose 2 Alicorns each. If you have 5-8 players, choose 1 Alicorn each. Each card moves all the Alicorns illustrated on the card forward by one space. Each turn, starting with the youngest player and going clockwise, draw a card from the scattered and turned deck and move ALL the Unicorns with that characteristic or r don't use that card. The first Alicorn to cross the finish line... Is the winner!

For example, the "Yellow" card moves Sun and Star forward by one space.

On the website, you can see many examples and learn how to create new imaginative winged steeds invented by you!

—description from the designer

This is a 2-4 player light strategy game that includes mechanics such as hand management, worker placement, and dice manipulation.​

You are one of the adventurers worldwide, who flock to Etariya in search of hidden ruins. Survey your surroundings and map out a unique exploration route to make the most of your discovery! ​

​—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
DOS is the follow up to the classic card game UNO. Like UNO, the object of DOS is to be the first player each round to get rid of all your cards and earn points for the cards left in your opponents' hands.

DOS Express offers all the fun of DOS but with a faster gameplay, allowing players to finish rounds more quickly! The smaller deck makes this version of DOS portable and easy to bring along while on-the-go.

On your turn you can play cards from your hand by matching their numbers to any of the face up cards in the center row. You can play one match for each card in the center row. You can also combine two cards from your hand that add up to the number you wish to match. If the card(s) you played match both number and color, you earn the right to place extra cards from your hand in the center row and even force other players to draw cards. Don't forget to yell "Dos!" whenever you have two cards in your hand or you could end up drawing more cards!

The round ends when one player gets rid of all his cards. That player earns points based on the values of the cards left in the other players' hands. Continue playing in rounds until someone reaches 200 points. That player is the winner.

In Word Drop, there are two teams: the “secret word team” and the “guessing team.” The secret word team is assigned a word that their main player must then use in a short conversation timed by a 3 minute sand timer. The other member(s) of the secret word team will need to help guide the flow of the conversation such that the main player can use their assigned word as naturally as possible.

The guessing team, who has to start the conversation, tries to figure out which words used by the secret word team feel a little off or unnatural in an effort to deduce what the assigned word might have been.

After the sand timer runs out, if the main player on the secret word team was able to “drop” the word successfully, the guessing team will state three words that they think could have been the secret word - if one is correct, the guessing team wins! If not, the secret word team wins by concealing their assigned word.

- Description from the publisher

By the close of 1805 the Third Coalition, which had been orchestrated by William Pitt, was in tatters following their catastrophic defeat at Austerlitz. However, in late 1805 a tiny Anglo-Russian army, sent by Pitt and paid for by the British government, disembarks in the Bay of Naples to support Ferdinand IV, King of the Two Sicilies. This army, commanded by the Russian General Lacy with the support of the British General Craig, approximately 15,000 men strong, is now the only Coalition force on mainland Europe.

Will they be able to defend Naples, or will they evacuate leaving the wretched Neapolitan army to its fate? Will Marshal Massena secure the Two Sicilies for his Emperor, or will the Coalition be able to save Ferdinand’s last stronghold, the island of Sicily itself?

The Limits of Glory series examines the influence of 'skill and luck' on the commanders present in a campaign. A Glory rating is attributed to all the significant participants in the campaign, made up from the skill they exhibited and the luck from which they benefited. Players must make difficult decisions as to when to use their commanders Glory to mitigate the situation on the board to their advantage by attempting to manage favourable and  unfavourable events thrown up by the Event Clock as the campaign unfolds.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players
Series: Limits of Glory (Form Square Games)

description: My Parents Might Be Martians plays like a junior version of Poetry for Neanderthals as adults must describe a hidden word to the kids' team using only one-syllable clues. Adults end up sounding like they're from another planet, while kids try to interpret the adults' clues and guess the correct words.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Race for the Galaxy: Xeno Counterstrike
description:
Xeno Counterstrike is the sequel to Xeno Invasion, together forming the 3rd arc of RFTG expansions.

It expands Race for the Galaxy into the starry rift frontier and border zone that lies between the galactic empires and the Xeno hive worlds, with more than 40 frontier worlds (in separate decks) and a different risk/reward method of settling them.

The resulting greater empire sizes extends the game end conditions to 15+ tableau cards and 15 VPs/player in the common pool. This makes Xeno Counterstrike the version for those players who have wanted RFTG to be just a bit longer (typically, 1-2 rounds more), as the lead now frequently changes near the end as those final large frontier worlds are settled, the larger economic engines hit their stride, and the last 6-devs hit the table.

Xeno Counterstrike can be played by itself with just the base game, though its fifth player and optional counterstrike game require Xeno Invasion.

The counterstrike game begins as a Xeno invasion which, once the Xenos are repulsed, turns the tables as the galactic empires then strike back at the Xeno home systems across the starry rift frontier and border zone.

In this version, tableau size no longer ends the game: the counterstrike game continues until either an economic victory (VP chips in the pool of 30 VPs/player run out) or a military victory over the Xenos occurs. This is the epic conquest version of Race for the Galaxy; final tableau sizes of 25 cards are not uncommon.

—description from the designer

Set out to chart the Stonespine Mountains! You may encounter an ambush by a slime monster, but thankfully a new hero Hortgully is there to protect you!

Enjoy four new cards that add more variety to your games of Cartographers and Cartographers Heroes. All the cards have thematic tie-ins to Stonespine Architects.


     1 ambush card (Slime)
     1 hero card (the minotaur Hortgully)
     1 scoring card (earn points for different terrain types next to a mountain)
     1 explore card (draw a mountain anywhere)


—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Set in the chaotic city of Orcwood you play the part of a prominent family. Grow your wealth and power by arranging marriages with the other families. The only problem is that this is a fantasy land; Witches, Goblins, Gelatinous Cubes, Vampires, Ogres and many more crazy creatures. Only the best matches will secure your victory, so who will you choose?

Taking in turns each player presents a bride and then chooses a suitor from one of the other players. They then roll the dice to see how successful the marriage was. The winner is the player who accumulates the most power.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Texas Chainsaw Massacre: The Escape Game
description:
Leatherface and his infamous killing machine are back in this escape game that immerses you in the grimy countryside of 1970s Texas. Dive into the unforgettable horror of the cult film The Texas Chainsaw Massacre with this immersive Escape Game. This game stays true to the terrifying atmosphere of the film and will test your skills. Are you ready to experience live horror? Who among the players will make it out in one piece? It's hard to say, but one thing is for sure: getting sliced and diced has never been this much fun!

In Texas Chainsaw Massacre: The Escape Game, you play as the iconic characters from the film – Pam, Kirk, Sally and company – trying to escape Leatherface and his family of psychos.
