Armed only with your player cards, a flashlight, and a clue guide, you must solve puzzles to escape the Sawyer family. Your team must work together to solve the puzzles and stay alive! The objective is to save as many characters as possible. The game consists of 8 sequences, each comprising 3 scenes.

Players can adjust the difficulty of the game by choosing the number of hearts (lives) for each character.

Up the ante in Pop!, a trick-taking card game for two.  Players will have cards in hand as well as display cards that are visible to their opponent.  Manage wild jokers, jacks that jump rank, and a slew of unknown cards.  Bluff and bully your way to victory much faster with strategic use of the pop cube.

Each round, players will try to win the majority of tricks.  Whoever captures four or more tricks wins the top prize.  The total value of the top prize can go up as players pop the scoring cube.  Each pop raises the top prize one point.  Don't feel like you can win the round?  Manage to capture three tricks and still win the consolation prize.  Does even three tricks seem impossible?  Fold before the score escalates too much and end the round immediately.

—description from the publisher

After the merging of Britain and the Fae realm and the vanishing of King Arthur, various factions — both mortal and Fae — now fight for power.

In Mordred, each player controls one of these asymmetrical factions and strives to earn the favor of the three great leaders: Mordred, Morgana, and Merlin. They must use the rounds available wisely as each action costs precious time. In the end, chaos will be inevitable, and players must prepare for it as the chaos will bring great peril, but even greater rewards.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Theme: King Arthur / The Knights of the Round Table / Camelot

description:
Cookierun Memory Battle is a memory game that uses the Cookierun IP. Players find two Cookie tiles that can defeat each monster and save the kingdom!

Place the 16 tiles face down on the game board and reveal 3 monster cards. Two Cookies are shown on each monster card. Try to find a team of two Cookies on one of three monster cards. If you succeed, you win that card and the two Cookie pawns and bring them in front of you.

If a Cookie pawn is owned by another player, take it from that player. The game ends when someone has four cards, and each card and Cookie pawn counts as one point.

—description from the publisher

Cookierun Rolling Pop is a dice rolling game in which you roll unique shaped Cookie dice and score points based on the side they land on. You can aim for a high score with more challenging postures!

On your turn, you roll 4 Cookie dice and may reroll once for free. Any additional rerolls require you to spend a Bear Jelly token. Once you fix your roll, you choose one of the 8 spaces on the score sheet and record your score based on the results of the roll.

After everyone plays 8 rounds, players sum up their scores and determine the winner.

—description from the publisher

This is an update kit for owners of Bossa editions released in 2022 and earlier (STANDARD, CLASSIC, THE ART "mizu" and "hana").
A set of expansion modifier chips and instruction sheets for the 2023 revised rules and optional extended rules.
designers: Banana Moon Studio Sapporo (バナナムーン･ステュディオ)
artists: Banana Moon Studio Sapporo (バナナムーン･ステュディオ)

description:
Wizard Buddies is a 3-5 player card game centered around spell casting, using magical items, and gathering wizards along the way.  You must keep your wits about you and your lexicon handy if you are going to overcome your opponents to become the wisest wizard of them all... or at least the one with the most Wizard Buddies!

In Wizard Buddies, each player plays as one of five Schools of Magic: Abjuration, Divination, Evocation, Necromancy, or Transmutation. On a turn, a player has three Action Points to spend. They can draw cards or play Spell Cards, Item Cards, or Wizard Cards, each at varying costs of Action Points. For example, Spell Cards and Item Cards cost one Action Point, but playing a Spell Card that aligns with your School of Magic won't cost you any. Similarly, playing a Wizard Card costs two Action Points. This is a hefty price to pay, but having the right combination of Wizard Cards in your play area is the key to winning. The game ends when a player has all five Schools of Magic accounted for in their play area OR three Wizard Cards from the same School of Magic in their play area that matches their own School. Magic can quickly spiral out of control, so be strategic in your card management and effective in your spell-casting!

—description from the designer

Majestic 13 is miniatures-agnostic tabletop wargame where you command an elite team of alien hunters in a secret war to protect the Earth. Designed to play solo, co-op, or as an adversarial skirmish game, Majestic 13 is created by Adam Loper and Vincent Venturella for 1-2 players.

From the book: "To the public, the modern era of alien encounters started in Roswell, New Mexico, on July 8th, 1947, with reports of a downed alien craft, which were quickly retracted and replaced by the story of a weather balloon. The public remains suspicious but accepts the story and moves on. Conspiracy theorists claim the government absconded with alien bodies and technology and hid everything from the people. Both stories are, of course, wrong."

Use any modern soldier miniatures you'd like for your team, and any kinds of weird creature models for the alien monsters you'll fight. Enjoy a deep campaign system with random mission generation and planned special missions, too. Keep upgrading your team members that survive each mission, and bring out new clones for the ones that don't.

—description from the publisher

In Alpino, you draft and place domino-style tiles, competing to make the most spacious habitat for each of the alpine animals. They will inhabit only the largest habitat made for them among all the players, and if you manage to make that habitat, then you score all of the accumulated points for that animal type at game's end.

In more detail, in each round players draft and place a tile, and while you want to group animals together, you must match the tiles by color/territory when placing them to link your growing habitats together. The tile that remains unchosen each round increases the value of one of the depicted animals, with the round's last player deciding which creature gets the bump.

At the start of play, you receive a secret tile that lets you know two of the animal types you might want to focus on while building habitats. In the last round of the game, you place your secret tile, ideally surprising everyone by boosting your habitats ahead of everyone else's.

Hero Fight is a board game based on empowering one's hero to defeat opponents in epic battles.

The game consists of:
100 cards
3 dice
30 gold crystals
36 vital crystals

type of cards:
-heroes
-weapons
-armours
-potions
-catastrophes
-environments
-power

How to play:


     Each player takes one hero card.
     Each turn reveals an environment that defines various effects.
     Players upgrade their heroes by purchasing weapons, armour and potions with gold crystals, and attack opposing heroes by reducing their vital crystals.
     The hero who remains standing wins.


Card position:


     The terrain consists of the hero card in the centre, the assigned weapon card on the right, the assigned armour card at the top and the assigned potion on the left.
     Each player will have two slots in his hand where he can keep cards not assigned to his hero.


Dice:


     attack die; is rolled by the attacking hero.
     defence die; is rolled by the defending hero.
      Fortune die; is rolled by the effects of an environment or a weapon or armour.


-PDF of the game rules in the files section (only in Italian, coming soon in English).

-If you have any advice or questions about the rules or the game feel free to leave a comment; we are neophytes in this field so any advice is welcome.

-instagram: hero.fight.blk

—description from the designer

Two great factions, Loyalists and Rebels, are fighting a fierce civil war.
The treacherous Usurper tries to gain total power for themselves.
In addition to their secret identity, each player has a secret rank. A high rank allows you to issue orders to other players but also makes you a target for assassination if discovered. If your rank is low you can try to bluff.
With limited ammunition and information, you will have to use wits and trickery to identify your allies and outsmart your opponents in this suspenseful social deduction game for 5-12 players.

Gameplay:

Each player starts with two secret cards: an Identity and a Rank, as well as 3 Health and 1 Ammo tokens. 
Play proceeds in turns, with each player having 2 Action Points to spend. There are several Actions players can take in their turn to increase their Ammo, give it away, or spend it to shoot other players. A special action called "Issuing an Order" or simply "Order" is also available, where a player can attempt to force another player to either carry out one of the aforementioned Actions, or privately show which side they are on.

However, the recipient of an Order can refuse, in which case the two players secretly compare Ranks, and the higher Rank wins out.

A player is dead when they lose all of their Health Tokens. A dead player does not reveal their identity, but can make a final statement. Their Rank and Ammo are given to the player that killed them.

Play ends when either the Usurper has gained the highest Rank in the game, or all players have passed. The Loyalists win by outnumbering Rebels at the end, and vice-versa. In case of a tie, the highest Rank breaks the tie.

—description from the publisher

description:
Legend has it Velkhana can control the cold itself, and use its freezing breath to conjure massive ice spires to stand in your way. Find it, hunters, and end its frozen reign!

The Velkhana expansion adds this magnificent elder dragon and all its adrenaline-pumping quests, rare weapons, and powerful equipment crafting options to your pledge. That means:

It also introduces a whole new way to win your campaign, because it includes a 5-star quest! Will you be the one to dethrone this crystalline monster, with its regal posture and crown of horns?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Monster Hunter World: Iceborne – The Board Game: Namielle Expansion
description:
Legend has it Namielle commands water and channels lightning to produce explosive bursts of steam, and will paralyse those who get too close with its electrified water pools.

The Kickstarter exclusive Namielle expansion adds this mysterious XXL elder dragon (120mm base) and all its electrifying quests, rare weapons, and powerful equipment to your Iceborne board game! That means:

It also introduces a whole new way to win your campaign, because it includes a 5-star quest! Will you be the one to pin down this mysterious, Kickstarter exclusive elder dragon?

boardgame: Catan: Das Duell – Die Big Box
description: CATAN: Das Duell – Die Big Box contains the CATAN: Das Duell base game, the Finstere & Goldene Zeiten ("Dark & Golden Times") expansion, and the Zusatzmaterial für Das Duell – Bonus Box extra material.

description:
Pollution is an interactive strategy game about building & powering cities, and lobbying for political influence. Players strategically leverage their cities and power plants to amass wealth in each of the 6 rounds. However, they need to be careful about the pollution they produce: Without proper management, they'll be faced with a hefty fine at the end of the game. Political influence is used to gather permission for the construction of new power plants.

Different kinds of power plants — nuclear, hydro, solar, wind, and coal — each come with their distinct pros and cons, thereby influencing gameplay strategy. Building a power plant automatically unlocks the next development level, offering you an array of upgrades including more advanced versions and special abilities.

Players compete with each other in various ways but every competition brings lots of opportunities for synergies. On the lobbying side players inadvertently help each other in their attempt to influence politicians. On the building side player interaction is made possible by a shared map where each player can attempt to be the new energy provider for an existing city or use idle power plants for their new building project. Since a city and its corresponding power plant do not have to be built by the same player complex decisions quickly emerge.

Finally, there are a number of different methods to deal with pollution, from natural carbon sinks to decommissioning old power plants. These actions have to be carefully planned though as they require special circumstances to be most effective.

Changing setups and goals provide a high replayability factor and long-term planning.

Gameplay:
The game is played over 6 rounds. 
In each round, players use their workers to perform one or two of four possible actions:

"Lobby": Gain political influence
"Build": Spend resources to build a city or power plant. 
"Offset": Implement a carbon offset project
"Decommission": Shut down one of your power plants.

Each action requires one worker. The round ends when all players have run out of workers. Now players take back their workers and gather income from the cities they have built, their lobbying efforts and the energy their power plants produced.

After the final round, a special income round is triggered which involves fines for all the pollution that is produced by each player. The player with the most money wins.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Monster Hunter World: Iceborne – The Board Game: Seething Anger
description:
Seething Hunger is an expansion containing new monsters, new weapons, new armors, new quests.

Nightshade Paolumu: A Paolumu subspecies able to put prey to sleep. It produces a potent sedative gas, stored within its throat sac, that it can expel to knock out unwary foes. Proficient in aerial combat.
Glavenus: A scaled brute wyvern that wields its heavy blue tail like a blade. It grows hotter and more keen with each strike until it glows red.
Acidic Glavenus: This acidic subspecies has corrosive fluid crystallised on its tail, spraying acid with each swing.
Silver Rathalos: A rare and dangerous Rathalos species with a
Shining silver carapace and enlarged spikes, more prone to rage than other Rathalos. When enraged, its breath grows more lethal and it gains a new set of attacks.
Seething Bazelgeuse: A Bazelgeuse variant that exists in a heated state. Its explosive scales burn even hotter than those
of its counterpart, increasing in number and blast radius as combat continues.

Where is the little WIR hiding now? It always hides when people fight. To help it emerge from its hiding spot, players in Das kleine Wir must puzzle and solve tasks together. Find certain shapes and colors, then position their game pieces accordingly on the game board — or elsewhere. Can you balance the colorful cardboard pieces on all three figures?

The weapons you craft, and the monsters you hunt to craft them, will change your experience in every campaign. Different hunters need different materials, and more hunters means more options and more hunting party combinations!

Four new Capital cards which you can shuffle into the deck. Compatible with all expansions.

These cards were available as promos in the 51st State Ultimate Edition campaign on Gamefound.

18Norway is an 18XX game based on Francis Tresham’s 1829, and follows the development of the Norwegian railroad from 1854 onward. You will be dealing with harsh weather and difficult terrain as you build your track across hills and mountains and around the fjords along the jagged North Sea coast line. All the while, companies have to take the inevitable nationalization into account.

—description from the designer

description:
To deliver deadly wounds and bring down your enemy, you must pursue and out-speed their escape. But can the paltry might of your attack penetrate their defenses? Become more powerful by changing your trait cards, and use your cunning to collect advantageous combinations—doubles, triples, and even the game-ending quadruple!

Players begin with four cards in front of them which represent their traits—speed, attack, defense, and health. Think of these traits as the player’s stats: the higher the number, the stronger, unless you seek to debilitate your opponent with the lower ones using a pair.

Each player’s turn consists of a variety of actions that they can perform depending on the cards currently in front of them. Players can choose to “focus” one of their traits, changing its value (whether higher or lower), by flipping over a card from a deck in the center of the playing field. Or they can choose to “attack” an opponent if they have sufficient speed and attack over an opponent’s own speed and defense, dealing damage to them. Finally, they can choose to perform a “gambit” on an opponent, in which they sacrifice a pair in front of them to replace an opponent’s trait (perhaps with something worse).

Additionally, players have a hand of trait cards that they draw under special circumstances and keep secret from others. When performing an action, they may use the cards in their hand to directly change their traits, offering themselves a secretive edge.

In short, Gladiators players compete to have the best stat values and try to inhibit those of their opponents. They may rely on the luck of the draw, or they may use their wits and cunning: to fight to become the last Gladiator standing.

—description from the designer

This is the first expansion for Rocket Ranchers Herding Cats in Space. Severe Weather adds various new cards to add to the wilderness deck. When added to the wilderness grid, these cards will change the way you play!

Gravity Wells prevent you from taking adjacent cards. Meteor Showers force you to leave behind a card from your beam. Power Storms prevent you from taking space cats. More effects are included in the box along with dogship tokens in your player color that function as a timer for the weather effect.

Will the weather ruin your plans or will you take advantage of these new effects to build the best ranch possible?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: My Little Pony: Adventures in Equestria Deck-Building Game – Collision Course Expansion
description:
Due to some magical mayhem, the Transformers find themselves in Equestria, and the Decepticons have teamed up with nefarious Pony Villains, which can't lead to anything good.

In My Little Pony: Adventures in Equestria Deck-Building Game – Collision Course Expansion, the Mane Six (and Spike!) team up with the Autobots as two-in-one character cards. Use the Autobots abilities by generating Energon on cards — or getting an energy boost by eating a Sugar Cube instead.

Three Dice Cricket is a solo sports replay game of test cricket. It uses real-life batting and bowling averages to rate players with a match playing out as batting partnerships until ten wickets have been taken, ending the innings. Each team has two innings with the higher aggregate score over both innings winning or, after five-days' worth of play has occurred without a result, the match being declared a draw.

Each batsman rolls the dice once and references a scoring chart to determine how many runs they score, with higher-rated batsmen likely to reach higher run totals. The game also includes ways of incorporating weather and pitch conditions as well as the important aspect of determining how each batsman was out and the bowler/fielder involved in the dismissal.

A full match can be played to completion in 90-120 minutes.

There is also a refinement of the charts to utilise percentage dice (2 x D10) which the designer refers to as "Percentage Cricket." The gameplay is the same, the charts just give slightly different results.

boardgame: Classic Napoleonics: Wargame Rules in the Age of Scruby
description: Classic Napoleonics: Wargame Rules in the Age of Scruby allows you to take command of the forces of the Napoleonic Period whether you are trying to recreate the famous battles of that era or are trying your hand at battle situations of your own making.

Something Wild is a line of card games featuring beloved characters and collectible Pop! pocket figures. Play numbered and colored character cards to make sets and runs. The first player to score three powers wins the game.

With Something Wild: SpongeBob SquarePants, you can score a set to snag the special power of your favorite animated sponge to help you win!

Combine Character Cards in sets and runs to score points, just like in Rummy. Use Power Cards to add a fun twist to the classic card gameplay.

Combine multiple Something Wild! games to add more character cards, more Pop! figures, and more powers to your game!

Something Wild is a line of card games featuring beloved characters and collectible Pop! pocket figures. Play numbered and colored character cards to make sets and runs. The first player to score three powers wins the game.

With Something Wild: Jurassic Park, you can score a set to snag the special power of a tiny T. Rex. Combine Character Cards in sets and runs to score points, just like in Rummy. Use Power Cards to add a fun twist to the classic card gameplay.

Combine multiple Something Wild! games to add more character cards, more Pop! figures, and more powers to your game!

boardgame: Home Team Heroes: The Football Card Game
description:
2024: "Soft-launch" featured first version of the game in Finnish language, for Finnish market.
2025: "Home Team Heroes: Season 1" Launched internationally with further developed game, in English language.

Finally, a football card game that actually plays like modern football.
Forget everything you know about sports card games. Home Team Heroes isn't another luck-driven simulator where higher numbers win. This is a tactical knife fight where every card placement matters, where tactical genius or good luck feel deserved, and where that perfect counter-attack you've been setting up actually mirrors the beautiful game's flow.

Decisions That Bite Back
Your hand management creates genuinely painful choices. That player could score now, but holding them back might prevent your opponent's counter. Event Cards are tactical tools: deploy a tactical foul to neutralise their star player, or save it knowing they're building towards something bigger? The Referee Card system adds Offsides and VAR-checks, just enough controlled chaos without destroying strategic planning.

Why Veterans Keep Coming Back
Every match tells a different story. High-octane shootouts or defensive masterclasses where 0-0 feels like victory. Those moments where your perfectly timed Referee Card dismantles their strategy. The game respects your intelligence whilst delivering genuine surprises.

The Gegenpress Isn't Just Flavour Text
Here's what's really addictive: when you're attacking and your Total Defence Power exceeds your opponent's, you trigger Gegenpress (it's a famous football strategy "after losing possession, winning the ball straight away for your team"), allowing consecutive attacks. Suddenly you're not just playing cards; you're implementing Klopp-style high pressing tactics.

Feels Like Real Football
Home Team Heroes makes you think like a manager, react like a player, and celebrate like a fan. For groups seeking competitive depth without three-hour sessions, for players who want meaningful decisions without analysis paralysis, this delivers. It's the tactical football game we didn't know we needed, wrapped in 30-minute matches that demand "just one more match". Attack, defend, counterattack, gegenpress, take advantage of tactical substitutions that would make Guardiola proud, you're not just playing a card game. You're playing football.

More Than a Game. Give Back by Playing.
Home Team Heroes actively supports football clubs of all levels from grassroots to leagues. Local clubs do invaluable community work, often with limited resources. Home Team Heroes creates free-of-charge fundraising opportunities by creating customised Team Decks for clubs, helping local clubs generate revenue whilst bringing communities together through gaming. Your purchase doesn't just expand your collection; it helps sustain the very clubs that inspire the next generation of players. With this business model, everyone can make a difference, sleeving up their brand new Team Deck for "one more match".

description:
Kuka on pelin HAKA consist of 400 cards, which have a number in the backside. The front side has a declaration of something, for which the right answer is the number in the backside.

There are two game modes in this game.
In easy mode player in turn tries to put three randomly chosen declarations in the descending order by the number in the backside. After that other players can contest the order. If no-one contests, then player in turn gets one point. If someone contests and the order is right, then the player in turn gets one point and the contester gets minus one point. If  the order was not correct, then contester gets one point and player in turn gets minus one point.

In the more challenging game mode you have a grid of nine cards (3*3), where you place your cards. On your turn you put your card in any place in the grid, so that descending order is maintained in the corresponding line and column. At the start there are no cards in the grid. You can play your card in the open space or on top of previously played card. Points are calculated in the same way as the easy mode.

There are five categories of numbers:
Number of people, number of something, amount in Finnish marks, percentages and other.

This game is a promotive game published by the Finnish construction company HAKA. From the cards in the game you can conclude that game was probably published 1990.

—user summary

Éboulis is a throwing card game combining skill and strategy.

The goal of Éboulis is to get to the mine's treasure. To fulfill this, players need to throw "tunnel" cards between their own mine entrances and the mine's treasure (which is placed in the middle of the table). Be aware of your opponents: they can throw "scree" cards in your tunnel to prevent you to win the treasure (and the game)!

The Arena of Baradum is an easy-to-play tactical fantasy arena brawler, for 2-6 players, 30-60 min. play time. The goal is to win Victory Points, which can be either collected by winning in Battle or gathering the mysterious Orbs. The game features 8 fantastic characters by 5 amazing artists, all completely in their own art style. Each character has their own unique deck and playstyle, which makes for a different experience each time you play the game. This makes it amazingly fun for both hardcore and casual gamers.

—description from the publisher

description:
Set in the world of Dinotopia (books by James Gurney), match 2 of the 3 elements (number, color, and symbol) on your 3 faceup cards to win.

The card game was included with the 1996 PC game.

Murder or accident? You have multiple deaths at sea to solve, and every detail counts!

In Unsolved: Tod auf der Jacht ("Death on the Yacht"), players look at picture cards to try to solve a case, with the game including three cases. To set up, shuffle the 30 base cards with the six case-specific cards. In each round, the investigators are given a picture card, which everyone examines carefully. Then the cards are passed on to their neighbors in turn. When everyone has looked at the cards, they discuss together which cards should be placed face up in the middle to help solve the case. A total of twelve cards may be revealed to answer these questions: Who is the victim? Is there a murder weapon? And who may have killed the victim and for what reason? ...Or was it an accident?

When the draw pile is empty, the group tries to reconstruct what happened and answer the open questions. Each correct answer is worth 2 points; each wrong answer is worth -1 point.

boardgame: Silent Night, Deadly Night: The Game
description:
Based on the classic 1984 horror film Silent Night, Deadly Night.

The game takes place on a board of 6*8 squares. The killer follows a path on the board, moving every turn depending on a dice roll. Up to four players must prevent the killer from reaching the last square of the board.

The players also move depending on a dice roll, but they don't have to follow a path. Their goal is to go to specific locations in order to gear up and try to slow down the killer, until they are ready to face him.

boardgame: Summoner Wars (Second Edition): Crimson Order Faction Deck
description:
Embrace entropy with the ever-hungry Crimson Order. Baron Daemar and his children know that death and decay will lay claim to aught. They feast upon those who oppose them, then grant unlife with their vampiric blood. Frustrate your foe with an enemy that grinds their forces down, all while healing their own!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Summoner Wars (Second Edition): Mountain Vargath Faction Deck
description:
Bring the uncaring harshness of the mountain to the battlefield! General Sunderved and his army of goat-folk apply brute force to every problem, smashing, ramming, and shoving any who get in their way. Reshape the battlefield as you see fit, then dominate it with the Mountain Vargath!

—description from the publisher

Jumping Jackalopes is a card game for two to five players who each turn play cards either to start a new set or add to an existing set of mythical creatures. Each creature has its own unique ability that activates when it is played that will help the player in some way. Additionally, players may also play a Jackalope on to one of the mythical creature sets in front of them. Playing a Jackalope causes a different Jackalope to jump from its current location to a different one. At the end of each round players score for the mythical creatures in front of them as well as a number of points for Jackalopes they control. Jackalopes are worth one point for each different mythical creature that a jackalope is on amongst all players. .

—description from the publisher

A new promo for both On The Underground games.

Setup: Before setting up the game, randomly draw three destination cards and place an obstruction tile in each of the stations they show. Shuffle those cards back into the deck.

Gameplay: A player must pay 2 branch tiles to connect a line to a station with an obstruction tile. That player then scores three points and removes the obstruction tile from the game.

boardgame: On the Underground: The Underground Challenge – Paris / New York
description:
NOTE: This mini-expansion requires a copy of On the Underground: Paris / New York.

In The Underground Challenge, you will compete against real Underground lines. The map starts with multiple lines already built, and you must try to beat the Underground's score.

Much like the engineering crews of the Underground, you must be quite resourceful at building track, and at the same time divert passengers to use your own lines, rather than the official ones.

If you can beat the Underground at its own game before the Destination cards run out, you win The Underground Challenge!

Each turn:
Take four actions - with each being placing Track tokens or taking Branch tiles, as in normal gameplay.
Perform Passenger movement.
Perform Passenger movement again.
The Passenger will thus visit one or two stations, and the Destination cards matching those stations will be removed and replaced as in normal gameplay.

The Passenger then will visit another one or two stations, with the destination cards matching those stations also being removed and replaced.

Passenger movement rules are the same as in the game for multiple players, with the following exception:
If a Passenger movement is up to the player’s choice (due to an equal number of empty spaces and lines involved in each potential route), the player must choose the route that gives the Underground the most points.

If all potential routes would give the Underground and the player equal points, the player may choose any one of those lines.

If your Score tile reaches or overtakes the Underground’s tile, the game is over, and you have successfully completed The Underground Challenge!

—description from the publisher

Horse and Musket Annual Number 5 is the fifth fan expansion to Horse & Musket. It will add new scenarios for owners of Volumes I-VI and Horse & Matchlock.

The scenarios include...

Hambledon Hill - August 4, 1645

Travel by Trolley is a 200-card expansion pack for Trial by Trolley. This expansion introduces new vacation-themed cards to the game about the highs, lows, and petty annoyances of modern travel.

In Trial by Trolley, one player is the conductor of an out-of-control trolley speeding toward a fork in the tracks. Everyone else splits into two teams and must use their wits and their cards to convince the conductor to spare their lives!

Players accomplish this goal by adding "innocent" cards to their track, "guilty" cards to their opponent's track, and modifier cards to anything the want. Once the tracks are down and the modifiers are in play, players must verbally argue to convince the judge which track to go down.

When the conductor makes their decision, everyone on the losing track receives a "death" token. At the end of a round after everyone has had the opportunity to be the conductor, the player(s) with the fewest death tokens win!

The foxes are busy gathering the resources they need to raise their kits, while the curious little ones explore the tunnels of their den in search of fabulous discoveries.

Can you accumulate the necessary resources to ensure the survival of your pack?

Over four rounds, you must collect the necessary resources in vital territories, fulfill objectives, and raise as many kits as possible while developing their instincts. The player who accumulates the most victory points (VP) wins the game.

—description from the designer

Welcome to Tribus, the card game! Tribus is a multiplayer deck-battler for 2 or 3 people. Be the last person standing to win!

In Tribus, players awaken in an afterlife called "Nimbus," ruled by the Great Dragon. For a chance at rebirth, players must fight through the "Carnival," an all-out tournament where only the most attuned to their colors can survive.

Using a "Tribus," a three chambered heart, players can choose and play with three of the seven colors of cards, each with their own unique playstyle. Players take turns playing one card at a time, one color at a time.

Summon "dudes," play epic moves, and knock your opponent's guard down to be reborn as an amazing warrior in the game of Tribus!

—description from the designer

Justice is a competitive, tableau-building game set in the same Dwarven society of Odrixia where Factory 42 is set. Players, as members of the Council of Law and Order, determine the guilt of accused criminals.

It's a trick-taking game where players utilize won cards strategically to influence the fate of suspects. With hidden scoring objectives, Justice presents a blend of cooperation and rivalry.

Immerse yourself in this captivating universe and experience the intricate balance between trick-taking mechanics and strategic decision-making.

Six location cards, which you can shuffle into the main deck regardless of the expansion you are using.

This set of cards was available as a promo in the 51st State: Ultimate Edition campaign on Gamefound and it's actually 51st State: Promo Set 1 (5 location cards) and 51st State: FLGS Promo (single Friendly Local Gun Store card).

Capitol Deal puts you in the shoes of an ambitious politician. Climb the ladder of power in DC and aim high: becoming President! How? Collect three full sets of Power Cards. But here's the twist! Unleash Action Cards to swipe cash, swap cards, and even squeeze campaign donations. Keep your rivals on their toes with Reaction Cards to foil their plans. Oh, and don't forget to build up those Campaign Funds! Why? Because just like you, those sneaky players are politicians too, always ready to deal, steal, and mess with your plans. Let the political games begin!

One of the most loved games of the '80s reworked in a pocket version of only 9 cards.

A highly competitive 'naughty' game in which the iconic red marbles become very dangerous red dice!

Explore the island moving at will, collecting hibiscus flowers and finds, but being careful of the many pitfalls,
between wild beasts hurled at you by your opponents, seismic tremors and continuous lava flows...

and more!

Recently, the village of Kalisburgh has been plagues by werewolves! If they want to live to tell the tale, the villagers will have to unite and eradicate the grisly infestation! Will you discover the werewolves?

Each player is secretly assigned a role - Werewolf, Villager, Seer or one of the other unique roles. There is also a Moderator who controls the flow of the game. This is a very social deduction game that requires no equipment to play, and can accommodate almost any large group of players.

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Crimson Hand Enclave Team
description:
Crimson Hand Enclave Expansion – The Crimson Hand Enclave are a secretive sect, even within the shadowy cult of the Crimson Hand. They specialize in tracking down the most legendary ancient relics and forbidden lore. This Expansion includes the new Crimson Hand Heroes, with 4 unique types of Crimson Hand to play as—the Inquisitor, leading the sect with fierce determination, the Cultist, a veteran member of the Order that has seen wonders and horrors beyond human comprehension, the Acolyte, well-versed in ancient knowledge and arcane magik, and the Cloaked Assassin, a silent hunter that melds into the shadows. This allows players to take on the role of a team of nefarious Crimson Hand cultist Anti-Heroes for the first time.

This Expansion includes all of the Heroes, Miniatures, and cards needed to play as a Crimson Hand Enclave, including Crimson Hand Personal Items, Starting Gear, Upgrades and Skill Tree,  Team Ability cards, Arcane Items and Ancient Orbs, and an 8 Mission narrative story campaign, allowing your Enclave to venture into other worlds on raids for relics, to infiltrate and spread the influence of the Crimson Hand, and even performing summoning rituals to bind creatures of the Void to your will (at least until they break loose).

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Brimstone Ranger Hero Pack

These cursed lands are filled with all manner of demons, creatures, and the living dead, but the Brimstone Rangers take it in stride. Only the toughest, saltiest, and most cunning riders survive in these parts...but it's here that the Brimstone Rangers thrive.

Splatters is a multiplayer competitive puzzle matching game. Two to ten players compete to quickly fill their “splat” with “splatters”. A game for all ages 5+.

LASERCUT COMPONENTS

     Ten wooden “splats” to hold splatters.
     120 wooden splatters.


SETUP
Spread all the puzzle pieces and splats faceup on your table. Each player grabs an equal number of splats. Discard any remaining splats.
 
GAME PLAY
Players all look for splatter pieces to fill their splat(s) at the same time. Game play is fast and frantic near the end as players compete to be first to fill their splats. Play in rounds.

WINNING
The first player to fill all of their splats is the winner of the round. Optionally, repeat until someone has won two rounds.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Egyptian Desert and Snowy Mountains Railroad Board Pack
description: A new folding double-sided railroad board with new missions.

2-player game of action and speed where the goal is to pass all of your pucks into the opposing player's side of the sectioned board. But the action must be done using one hand only and using the elastic band to propel your pucks through the opening to the other side.

Funny memory game for families: Did you know that wombats poop in cubes? Whose poop is bigger—a hippo or a moose?

—description from the publisher

Unicorns is a fast-paced card game with the art of modern animal unicorns, but not only the well-known horses.

Everyone plays simultaneously. The aim of this game is to find the unicorn animal that is shown exactly as many times as it is shown on the back of the next card (top of the draw pile).

Fastest player to identify the animal correctly will get the card. If he is incorrect, card gets discarded to the bottom of the deck as well as one of the cards the player won before (in one of the previous turns).

Game ends when there is a last card in the deck. The player who collects the most cards wins the game.

A legendary animal that allows all players to swap up to two animals from one of their locked realms with animals from unlocked realms, alliance, or hand. Or it allows all players to pull one realm type from their discard pill and add them to their hand.

Tunnel Rats is a tactical-level, narrative-style solitaire wargame using a tables-based game engine and randomized mapping system.

As the leader of a special assignment US Army Combat Engineers Tunnel Rat team operating in the Vietnam Mekong Delta region, your ultimate goal is to survive a 6-month Vietnam tour of duty during the year 1968. Your mission assignment is to enter underground Viet Cong tunnel complexes in the Cu Chi province and seek out and destroy any VC you may encounter. You will also attempt locate VC work shops and store rooms for the Combat Engineers to later destroy with C-4 explosives. And finally, the discovery of any VC documents in command post rooms is always an added bonus.

With most of the rules integrated into the tables, there is practically no learning curve or rules memorization. And with numerous Tunnel Rat skills, weapons, mission types, possible tactics, random events, a multitude of table modifiers, and a randomized mapping system, Tunnel Rats offers high replay value. Are you up to the challenge?

—description from the publisher

The game is over when there is no maki left, neither in the bag, nor on the ground. The team with the most makis wins.

Sci-fi scavenge on a derelict spacecraft while an alien presence stalks you.

Reveal cards for the rooms in the craft, oriented by dice rolls, manager resources, hunt for what you need.

boardgame: Davy Jones' Locker: Curse of the Ghost Ships
description:
A new island has appeared from the fog out o' nowhere 'n wit' it, a curse! A loot chest filled t' the brim wit' gold was found on this wretched island, but it wasn't 'til 'twas nigh' spent that the curse was discovered.  The first expansion to Davy Jones' Locker: The Kraken Wakes brings all new enemies, equipment, crew members, & all new ways to defeat your enemies!

Any ship at sea wit' even jus' a single piece o' gold from the chest on board be haunted by The Banshee & 'er fleet 'o Ghost Ships. She appears from nowhere, using 'er lantern to Siphon the souls of the crew! Unable t' fight back, ye must explore this Phantom Island t' find a way t' defend against these ghosts 'n break the curse that has been plaguin' the seas!

In Act 1, find the cursed gold & bring it back to the chest to break the curse! Brave the haunted Phantom Island in search of Spectral equipment to help in the battle against the Ghost Ships. But don't take too long, as every round The Banshee's curse grows stronger!

Once the last piece of cursed gold is placed into the chest, The Banshee is summoned & Act 2 begins! Carefully navigate the waters in battle to avoid Sea Stacks & Reefs, dare to return to Phantom Island in search of Grimoires to turn the tides in your favor, & use your Spectral equipment to siphon the souls back from The Banshee & send her back down to Davy Jones' Locker!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Dungeons & Dragons: Onslaught – Nightmare of the Frogmire Coven: Maps & Monsters Expansion
description:
While deep in your cups at the Distant Lands Inn, you are ambushed by a hunched and hooded stranger whose very presence chills you to the bone. Before you can react, fell sorcery transports you and your companions to the dark heart of the Frogmire swamp. Ensnared by a sinister coven and beset on all sides, the nightmare is just beginning…

This expansion set is a companion to the Dungeons & Dragons: Onslaught Harpers vs. Zhentarim core set, featuring stunning monster miniatures, a new double-sided game board, and eight sinister scenarios designed to freeze your blood and test your tactical acumen. Fight your way to the frightening Froghemoth, with removable tentacles and eyestalks that let you switch between underwater and above water versions! It also features a new deck of item cards to swap with the core set item cards, providing new abilities, and adding the danger of cursed items!

The scenarios in Nightmare of the Frogmire Coven take place on the included all-new double-sided map featuring a swamp on one side, and overgrown ruins on the other, both of which use bridge tokens of different sizes to create unique layouts.  There are six main interlinked scenarios, a solo scenario and a 3-4 player free-for-all scenario for players looking for new ways to play!

Can you defeat your rivals and escape the nightmare? Or will you be lost forever in the brackish depths?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: That Others May Live: Combat Search and Rescue – Vietnam
description:
That Others May Live is a solitaire card-driven wargame about Combat Search and Rescue during the Vietnam War. The player takes on the role of a CSAR commander tasked with the rescue of downed American aircrew in North Vietnam and Laos.

To conduct a successful mission, you'll need to harness the unique abilities of the rescue-dedicated aircraft under your command. These include A-1 Skyraiders, HH-53 Jolly Green Giant rescue helos, and an O-2 Skymaster Forward Air Controller.

Action cards are used to direct aircraft to troll for enemy fire and deploy ordnance such as smoke, CS gas, cluster munitions, and napalm. Command cards can be used to call in fast moving aircraft and exfiltration teams. Event cards may complicate or ease a rescue. Pilot & Parajumper cards can be assigned to aircraft to reflect unique abilities.

Hidden enemies are placed on the map and may be revealed by player actions. A variety of NVA anti-aircraft guns attempt to destroy your incoming rescue aircraft and must be effectively suppressed before sending in your rescue helicopters.

The game includes a base standard scenario, four historical scenarios, and a mini-campaign. Optional rules are provided to vary the difficulty level and provide added realism.

Midnight Guardians Thanks to guardians like you there's still hope! Choose 1 of 4 Game Modes to experience this puzzly, set collection game of "cute horror."

As a Midnight Guardian, you alone stand between the innocent critters of Shady Valley and Zumoffa, Lord of Night. Zumoffa has manifested a great Wall of Pain, but a curse holds sway over the Lord of Night. Zumoffa is incapable of harming anyone found asleep.

Each Day Phase, you must act quickly to meet the critters' needs so they can get to sleep when night comes. You do this by collecting the food and comfort items they seek, and locating potent treasures to help speed up your work and slow down your enemies. If you don't find what you need in the field, sometimes a small roadside stand has just the thing. Its prices fluctuate frequently, but sometimes that can work in your favor.

Each Night Phase, the Wall of Pain slithers down over the valley like a forest fire, consuming one-quarter of the playing field, including resources and unsaved critters, which bolsters the wall's power. Dark Seeds grow here and there, empowering the wall further. House-sized metal monstrosities called "Little Horrors" herald Zumoffa's approach by capturing critters in cages. The tension ramps up as the resources dwindle.

Additional options and challenges are available using the Event deck, which temporarily alters certain rules or introduces new ones while hinting at the larger story and the wonders of the technomagical world of Toonaria.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Small Railroad Empires: Achievement Cards Promo Pack
description:
Pack of five promo cards available as add-on during the Freight on Board Kickstarter.

Included are:

Unlocked Potential: I have used both of my locked tracks.
Forest Route: I have constructed tracks on 5 forest spaces anywhere on the board.
Idyllic Route: I have constructed tracks on 6 farmland spaces anywhere on the board. 
Through the Desert: I have constructed tracks on 6 desert spaces anywhere on the board.
Connection Master: I have constructed tracks or trains on 6 city spaces anywhere on the board. (Does not have to be deliveries)

Cards come in a small paper box with an explanation sheet.

Take the start at the alpine world ski Championships, Downhill, Super G, Giant, or Slalom.
Draw cards that will move you down the ski trail. But be careful not to get to the gates too hastily, our you'll have to stop to go through them. The goal is to be the first to cross the finish line!

boardgame: Small Railroad Empires: Loyal Partner Promo
description:
Promo Achievement Card added by the publisher as token of appreciation to backers of the Freight on Board Kickstarter campaign.

Loyal Partner: I have constructed a route that connects three factories from the Calias Company.

Blobs is a limited edition card game with 70 cards, each with its own unique effect and illustration. It is a short card game with a lot of interaction.

In Blobs the players are scientists working at the lab. Each scientist tries to create the best blobs and score the most victory points this way. However this is not without danger. Things can go wrong. Some blobs may be aggressive and may score negative victory points. Sometimes blobs do not survive and end up as chemical waste. But who knows? Perhaps even then any scientist can bring them back to life.

Players are taking turns in clockwise order until the end of the game is reached. During a turn a player performs actions from blobs in his/her room and plays a blob from his/her hand. Other players may optionally play a blob in response. A player executes the following four phases in his/her turn:

Phase 1: Start actions
Phase 2: Play a blob
Phase 3: End actions
Phase 4: Replenish hand

The game ends after the turn in which the active player has eight or more blobs in his/her room at end of turn. The player with the most victory points in his or her room is the winner.

—description from the designer

2023 Promo Card 1

Play: The opponent takes control of this.
Defeated: You lose 2 life.

Additional 2023 promo cards are in Mindbug: Beyond – Promo Pack

Epic Shogun is a fun, strategic card game that thrusts players into the world of feudal Japan, where emperors vie for the Shogun. Each player starts with a Castle and a hand of cards, aiming for one of three paths to victory: building a powerful Kingdom, forging strategic Alliances, or reaching a Kingdom value of 300.

The game revolves around the Flop, where three cards are revealed from the Draw deck, forming the initial battlefield. Players take turns drawing, burning unwanted cards, and buying from the Flop to strengthen their armies.

Constructing a Kingdom involves placing Kingdom cards around the Castle, creating Towers that must be conquered in descending order. The player who completes the final Princess Tower wins. Building a 300-value Kingdom also secures victory.

Strategic alliances play a vital role, allowing players to attack and subjugate rival Castles, creating Allied Kingdoms. Allies provide blind cards, bolstering the ruler's power.

Battles ensue as players form Troop Hands with a sum of up to 21. Epic 21 combinations, achieved through Hero cards, offer a potent advantage. The Final Showdown determines the winner, as players battle relentlessly, seeking to conquer rival Towers.

Epic Shogun's blend of strategy, diplomacy, and tactical decision-making makes it a thrilling and engaging card game. Embrace alliances, plot your path to victory, and rise as the supreme ruler of feudal Japan in this epic saga of conquest and triumph.

—description from the designer

Everyone in this surreal apartment building loves rumors and especially loves gossiping about them with their neighbors.
Your goal is to come up with the best background story for a given rumor that is spreading around town and get crowned as the Rumor Freak.

HOW TO PLAY

     Every player chooses a character and takes the corresponding


Character Card in front of them, along with a marker.

     A random player starts the game by saying, "Rumor has it that... and draws one card from each deck, placing each card faceup in the following order to complete a sentence: WHO > DID WHAT> WHO > SPICY DETAIL. This player is now the active player.
     Start the sand timer. All players (including the active player) have 1 minute to come up with a scenario and a background story for the rumor (why it happened, how things ended up that way, secret motives, or whatever you like). Be as bizarre and disturbing as you can be. Keep notes on your Dry-Erase Card so that you don't forget.
     Once the sand timer is up, each player unravels their story, starting with the active player and moving clockwise. Try to be as convincing as you can and persuade everyone you have the real story.
     Then, every player votes for the best story by writing their favorite character name on their card. And no, you cannot vote for yourself.
     When everyone is ready, reveal your votes. Every player gets as many points as the votes he/she has received. If someone is voted by all, he/she gets one extra point.
     Circle the number of points you received on the numberline of your Character Card and update it after every round to keep track of your total points.
     With each new round, someone new becomes the active player.
     The game ends when all players have been the active player once (for 5-6 players) or twice (for 3-4 players).
     The player with the most points is deemed the absolute Rumor Freak of the Town.


FUN FACT: If the WHO card starts with "my" (example, my mom) we're talking about the active player's real mom. Not their character's mom. Their real mom. Enjoy the chaos.

SPICE UP THE GAME
Looking to spice up the game even more?
Instead of drawing the first WHO card, the active player can come up with a person they know (famous or not) and write it out on the WHO Dry-Erase Card.

—description from the publisher

Roll 3d6 to find out what King Charles would like to eat then deliver it (tiddlywink it) for points! You get three food items per turn and the player with the most points - after a set number of turns - is the winner!

This is a 30-page booklet containing 10 game situations to solve based around the cards contained in the Initiation Pack (formerly known as the Starter Kit). For each scenario, you are shown what cards you have access to and the layout of your opponent's board. From there, you must figure out how to defeat the opposing "player" with the limited resource pool provided. If you get stuck, you can also find the solutions to each challenge in the back of the booklet.

While helpful, you don't actually need to own the Initiation Pack or any of the Clash of Decks stand-alone expansions to play and solve the game situations that are contained within this Grimoire. If so inclined, you can also create your own scenarios using an online creation tool found on the Clash of Decks website.

Expansion game for Mr. Gameshow.

This is an additional tape to add to the base game tape that includes more specific trivia regarding Hollywood. Gus Glitz also is the host for this expansion game as he was for the base game. Game play is not changed by the expansion - just more questions!

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Terrors of the Deep Enemy Theme Pack
description: A Pharrox vampire mini-boss.

Guilds and Gambits is a fantasy battling card game that draws inspiration from auto battler video games, deck-building board games, and trading card games. In this world, two players assume the role of rival guild masters, recruiting guild members, acquiring powerful items, and battling it out until one remains. Be the first player to eliminate your enemy's health points to bring glory to your guild!
Three new badges for TRAILS. These give you points for each of your 0-point photos.

Kitchen Chaos pits two teams of chefs against each other to serve dishes to the customers first! But be wary of traps! Delay the other team with strategically placed traps, or by taking away their ingredient!

The small kitchen can only accommodate a few chefs at each station, so chefs must plan on using their time and resources wisely. Your chefs can perform different actions each turn, from getting ingredients and trap cards, to cooking dishes, and serving food. You can also place strategic traps which trigger whenever your opponents move to a targeted station or perform a certain action.
​
Don't let the chaos of the kitchen get to you, and deliver your dishes before your opponents!

—description from the publisher

In an attempt to become the most-famous, highest-paid, awesomest alchemist ever, you need to get to work.

Your lab is a mess. There is one clear set of shelves that already holds a Storage Container, little bowls for solids, and traps for focused energy. First, plan your day. Second, do something like buying raw materials or storing energy. Then, after everyone else does something, do it again, then again. Since those three projects will fill your day, clean up the lab for tomorrow.

Eventually you will have the materials required to create Compounds, then Potions. After that, you can do really cool stuff like convert a compound and use its effect to transmute raw materials, or drink a potion and alter time. Or, best of all, fulfill the requirements of a Prize to get a really cool trophy for your shelf. The first player to get three trophies on their shelf wins.

—description from the designer

The Kingdom of Delriah has a little trouble with some goblins! They need the help of an adventuring party! Will you answer their call?

Miniature Adventures is an exciting co-op adventure card game inspired by old-school 16-bit RPGs and designed to be portable while still challenging!

Choose a Hero and overcome four Levels of challenges in order to complete the entire Adventure Level.  Adventure solo, or with up to four players!

Players take turns performing actions or Rolling for Adventure.  Players will slot dice rolled into their three Points value locations on their Character cards.  These values will help determine their Initiative (speed), Power (ability), and Actions.

There are several types of Encounters players will need to overcome such as Mobs (enemies) and Challenges (perils).  Survive through every Encounter in a level to progress to the next level and gain a reward!  Once all four levels are complete, the Players win and are victorious!

Battle in a magical realm with a rich and deep history where many practice the arcane arts but only the best can master it. Explore our diverse characters and craft their fates.

In this magical battle board game, eliminate enemy characters to earn points, collect Magic Tokens to activate special abilities, and use strategic character placement and fighting power to win!

Every team is a trio of characters who have set stats for attack, defense, movement, and range. Each character has a special ability that is unique to them as well as a passive ability based on their class (striker, guardian, maven.)

Dice combat is done through attack dice and defense dice. The enemy loses health equal to the number of successful attack dice minus defense dice.

Who will you choose to forge your victory?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Roll Camera!: X-mas Movie Story Pack
description:
'Tis the season once again! You've got big plans to capitalize on the genre that everyone loves (or loves to hate): X-mas Movies! But for some reason you thought it would be a good idea to film your holiday film over the holidays, posing a serious risk to your crew's mood during this moody time of year. Luckily, the spirit of the season is all about the giving and receiving of material possessions, and you've got some special ones wrapped and ready to go in case things get grinchy. ROLL CAMERA!

Roll Camera! Story Packs give a little thematic cinematic boost to your madcap movie-making, with all-new components and a small rules twist. The X-Mas Movie Story Pack introduces a Cheer Tracker to monitor the holiday mood of your crew: let it fall too low and you'll incur grumpiness penalties, but give other players (or yourself) new Gift cards to keep spirits high.

This pack includes 12 Gift cards, 10 holiday-themed Scene cards, 3 Idea cards, 1 Equipment card, 4 Script halves, 1 Cheer Track, 8 holiday prop tokens, and 3 new"X-mas Ghost" cards - a new twist on Problems with a Dickensian flair. The X-mas Movie Story Pack can be played as an isolated module, replacing certain main game components for a highly thematic one-off game, or simply mixed in with the rest of the game and any previous expansions.

—description form the publisher

description:
A party card game of action, complete your dare tasks to collect points & win the title of MOST SHAMELESS! This fun game for friends, couples, or family combines an old-fashioned card game with our modern world of social media. Are you brave enough to share your dare on social media?

Simply separate the SHAME and LESS card decks. You’re ready to go! Players complete card tasks & collect points until someone reaches 13 points & wins! Keep the adult party going & play all through game night!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Scarlet Envelope VII: Tale of a Golden Dragon
description:
This is the seventh game in the series of "Scarlet Envelope" games.

Solve escape room-style enigmas and become a hero of a Medieval fairytale! Crush puzzles together with the Dragon Trainer, the Wizard, and the Witch to figure out how to stop the legendary Golden dragon. This treasure hunt puzzle game mixes interaction with physical cryptic items and a digital multimedia experience (videos, audios, and a chatbot.)

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Scarlet Envelope VIII: Dinner with Anonymous
description:
“First course - peanut stew, main course - your dirty lies with a tahini dressing.” Five honorable guests have been blackmailed into dining with Anonymous, a charming psycho claiming to know everyone’s dark secrets. In a twisted turn of events, you find yourself in Anonymous’ basement, kidnapped and challenged to answer two questions: "Who is Anonymous? And what have these five people done to piss them off?"

Expect CREEPY VIBES (we're talking videos with Anonymous wearing a TV head helmet, cooking a pie out of shuttered glass, and filming puppet shows.) You will peel off, decipher, fold, and investigate. Roughly 2h of gameplay, linear structure, puzzles unlock pieces of the story until the big reveal.

boardgame: Scarlet Envelope IX: Ashes of Persepolis
description:
Travel to Ancient Greece to solve a mystery of Persepolis, the priceless Persian city burned to ashes by Alexander the Great. The hero’s secrets are interwoven into an intricate puzzle guarded by Olympian Gods and an all-seeing Oracle. Only the mightiest can read Oracle’s cards to find out what really happened in Persepolis.

Ashes of Persepolis is an epic adventure with familiar scenes from the myths and legends of the Ancient Greeks. Based on historical events and a lot of research. We hope you’ll learn a thing or two about the quest of Alexander the Great!

PUZZLES:
Expect to solve a variety of visual and logic puzzles. The tringle-shaped cards provide a unique playground for puzzling! You’ll build a maze, cut paper, invent the unique decoder wheel, investigate constellations, make sacrifices to Gods, and of course, you’ll THINK and HAVE FUN!

STRUCTURE:
Roughly 2.5h of gameplay, linear structure, with all puzzles unlocking major pieces of the story (in video format) until the big reveal.

boardgame: The Last Bottle of Rum: Draconic Cookbook Promo Card
description:
Promo card included in the Kickstarter copies of Taverns & Dragons.

Card ability: Double your Loot Points

In Knight Fall, detailing an important event that launches into motion the events that will take place in Rise of the Dread Moon. This thrilling quest is packed with exciting twists and turns – you won’t want to miss it! We recommend you play this quest before embarking on the adventure contained within the Rise of the Dread Moon expansion. Enjoy!

—description from the publisher

We’ve created a cooperative board game for adults that serves as an introduction to cooperative game models, movement strategy building, and last but not least, key principles in nonviolence. It serves as a creative way to build positive, empathic, and meaningful connections between diverse individuals.

Designed for 3-5 players, the game time runs approximately 90 minutes. Cosmic Peaceforce invites us into a whimsical and profound world at the edge of the inner and outer realms of existence, where we have a duty to work together, face down temptations and adorable, chaos-loving goats, embrace conflict as an opportunity for drawing closer together, and learn from our most bitter experiences.

How to achieve the mission (win): The Cosmic Peaceforce must unlock 4 portals to Harmony 3 through strategy, conflict, and chance. The Team does not achieve the mission (loses) under three conditions: The lotus reaches 0 petals, any player loses all of their Soulforce tokens, or the Disharmony Yuga freezes shut 2 portals to Harmony 3.

—description from the publisher

A fairly traditional trick-taking game which has its roots in Hearts.  It is a "must follow" game, where the winner of the trick will collect all cards played to it and lead to the next trick.

The game plays over 4 rounds and the player with the lowest score will win. Points are acquired by winning the 9 of any suit or any cards of the red suit.

In オウムガエス, players are attempting to remember the locations of face down animals cards.  On their turn, a player will look at two cards; if they match, they will collect them and take another turn, but if they do not, they will be returned face down and the next player will take their turn.

However, the cards are never shown to the other players. Instead, the player makes the sound of the revealed animal.  A few cards show a parrot, and if a parrot is seen, the player makes the noise of any other animal in the game.

The player with the most pairs collected when only parrots remain is the winner.

Secret Ranking is a party game about ranking your co-players in a certain category. One player leads the round, and gets a randomly selected category (e.g. "Most likely to have been class president.") The player then ranks the other players according to their view on this category.

The other players do not know which category the ranking is based upon. Rather, they know it is one of six which are visible on the table.  These players discuss which category they think they were being ordered according to, and then, competitively, choose.

Emoman is a game about discussing your emotions and expressing empathy for others. The game includes a deck of cards showing facial expressions on one side, and describing a certain situation on the other. A card is revealed from the deck and players use an emotion wheel to express how much they feel the emotion shown in the situation described.  Once everyone has set their wheel, the values are revealed and discussed. The players who have values closest to each other gain points.

boardgame: I SPY: Find It Fast – Dice Game
description: Roll the dice, find a match and find it fast! Players roll nine dice with realistic pictures on each side. They try to find a match on an overturned picture card filled with a variety of different objects. Be the first to find the most matches and you win!
family: Versions & Editions: Dice Versions of Non-Dice Games

boardgame: Cauldron: Bubble and Boil – Familiar Pack
description:
This Familiar Pack Mini-Expansion is an 8-card deck featuring familiars. These cards remain in play from turn to turn, each with their own unique abilities.

—description from the publisher

Roll & Move property trading game released in 2005. Players compete to become “Money Giants” by winning a million dollars.

—user summary

It's avocados versus imposters in this game of speed matching madness. Pick a team and be the first to get all of your cards into the crates by matching cards, but watch out for action cards and rotten fruit.

Any cards with the same color background are a match!
Match Avocado cards with the same illustration.
Imposter cards need matching characters.

There's no waiting for your turn, everyone plays at once, so be speedy! The fastest matchers win!

—description from the publisher

You've laughed, you've cried, and you've survived all kinds of video call shenanigans and lived to tell the tales. Dare to join the meeting?

Roll the dice to set the scene, then use the cards in your hand to tell the story of a video experience. Was it cringeworthy or hilarious? Disaster meeting or best first date?

Best story that fits the scene wins!

—description from the publisher

Soundtrack Trivia is a new Movie Trivia board game with a companion app that challenges players to guess the movie based on music from its soundtrack. This upbeat party game gets your toe’s tapping and fingers snapping while stimulating conversations about Movies and Music. This family friendly game is for movie buffs of all ages and can be played by 1 to 12+ people. Since the game is supported by a companion app, expansion packs can be easily updated as they are released keeping the game fresh and current. Prior to starting a new game our flexible settings allow you to select the movie ratings, genres and decades of movies that suit your playing style. This allows you to tailor the game to best suit your movie knowledge. You can also vary the games’ difficulty. All of this helps create a nice balance so the game can be enjoyed by players of all skill levels.

Various spaces on the board will have players guessing individually, collaborating (or competing) with a partner or a group. If a player is unsure of the correct answer, they will be able to use one of their six "Cut Cards" which will provide additional hints including the cast, the plot, and a piece of dialogue from the movie. There are also "VIP Ticket Cards" that affect player movement. The goal is to make your way around the board starting at "Action Starts Here!" and successfully ending at "That's a Wrap!" After landing on the final space, you will have to answer one last question correctly before earning the title of the biggest movie buff!

—description from the publisher

History Heroes: Romans will challenge your knowledge of the ancient Romans and their Gods. Discover and learn more about the most significant Roman characters and their (12 most important) Gods on 40 fantastically illustrated cards, packed with facts about them. Learn your Caesar from your Claudius and your Jupiter from your Juno. Discover which emperor made it a capital offense to mention a goat in his presence…and why!

In the game are a great range of Roman characters from rulers like Julius Caesar to the first Roman emperor, Augustus, the sickly but cunning Claudius, who conquered Britain, Hadrian, and the first Roman emperor from Africa, Septimius Severus, who died in York. Foreign leaders, who challenged the Romans’ rule are in Romans too: Cleopatra and Boudica, leading the way! Gladiators, male and female, are in the game too, including the iconic Spartacus: learn more about how he became such a legend in Roman history.

As the Monty Python sketch once said, "What have the Romans ever done for us?" Well...play History Heroes: Romans and discover how much of our present world, knowledge, and history comes directly from those ancient times.

How to Play
There are many ways of playing History Heroes quiz card games. Each pack gives suggestions on how to play, or you can make up your own rules if everyone playing agrees to them!

Win the game by winning the character cards, and you win the cards by guessing/knowing the facts about each History Hero. You can use the images as clues and you can use the different levels of facts to make it as easy or as hard as you want to play the game.

—description from the publisher

Across the far-flung worlds of the Imperium of Mankind, in a galaxy riven by warp storms and unceasing conflict, the armies of Humanity battle for survival. With the Imperium already standing upon the brink of annihilation, the Tyranid hive fleets emerge from the cold void between the stars. The Space Marines stand as the last line of defence between this ravenous alien menace and the beleaguered worlds of the Imperium.

Take the plunge into the 41st Millennium with the Ultimate Starter Set for Warhammer 40,000. This box gives you a complete introductory experience, with loads of incredible models, a battlefield to fight over, helpful guides, and all the dice, tools, and rules you need to play your first games. Pick the noble Space Marines or swarming Tyranids, give control of the other army to a friend or family member, and battle it out for hours of entertainment. There’s no better way to get into the Warhammer 40,000 hobby – this box is also an excellent way to expand your existing collections.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

Across the far-flung worlds of the Imperium of Mankind, in a galaxy riven by warp storms and unceasing conflict, the armies of Humanity battle for survival. With the Imperium already standing upon the brink of annihilation, the Tyranid hive fleets emerge from the cold void between the stars. The Space Marines stand as the last line of defence between this ravenous alien menace and the beleaguered worlds of the Imperium.

Take your first steps into the 41st Millennium with this Introductory Set for Warhammer 40,000. This box is a great way to get familiar with this amazing hobby, as you build and paint incredible models, then play your first games. It includes two sets of push-fit models, a set of hobby tools and paints, a game mat, helpful guides, tokens and terrain markers, plus all the dice and tools you need to play. Pick the noble Space Marines or swarming Tyranids, give control of the other models to a friend or family member, and battle it out for hours of entertainment. If you’re thinking about giving Warhammer 40,000 a try, this is your chance – it's also an excellent way to expand your existing collections.

—description form the publisher
