description: Accumulation is a Taiwanese original boardgame based on Darwin’s definition of evolution, descent with modification, from his masterpiece, On the Origin of Species. Players as biologists which are transported to a primitive world, aims to make the world diversified. They may manipulate the evolution of species by accumulating the length of limbs, either lengthening or shortening. And players may help those animals to survive the catastrophic event by observation and research. The level of the game can be adjusted by the age of players and the circumstances, which makes it suitable for family, friends and class uses.

Where do shooting stars go? Maybe someone's collecting stars?
In star fall, the player is the guardian who makes constellations in search of shooting stars. Players can enjoy cute and lovely stars and beautiful night sky through simple action.

—description from the designer

Skies of Victory is a tabletop wargame that allows players to command squadrons of bombers and fighters. Set about a century before "Flight Command", the player is put in the shoes of a commander in charge of establishing a new airbase to wage war against the enemy. You must make decisions, such as repairs, replacements, and aircraft management, that will impact your overall campaign.

Each turn, you will control squadrons of bombers, and possibly their fighter escorts, moving them along a flight path towards the enemy targets. You gain points by destroying targets. The more points you gain, the better the chance of a successful mission, and ultimately, a successful campaign.

Reign of Hades immerses players in a visually stunning and modern interpretation of Greek Mythology with its captivating blend of dungeon crawler elements and action-driven storytelling. In this enthralling, co-operative campaign game, you will take on the roles of fallen heroes who were once mighty Olympian Gods. Imprisoned in Tartarus by the cunning Hades, you and your fellow players must set yourselves free and navigate the treacherous realms of the Underworld together. Starting as mortal shadows of your former selves, you will fight to reclaim your divinity and strength, with one ultimate goal in mind: confronting Hades and taking back what's rightfully yours—Olympus.

Reign of Hades introduces a novel Dice-Grid System, which allows you to enhance your attacks by turning a combat die to a better facing or 
upgrading it to the next level. You will interact with the game by playing cards into your limited number of card slots, which allow for infinite variability and interesting choices while keeping the gameplay simple, intuitive, and easy to pick up. While the core gameplay focuses on the cooperation between teams of two, there will be a dedicated three-player variant and a thrilling solo variant for those seeking solitary adventures.

Will you and your fellow gods overcome the trials of the Underworld, reclaim your divinity, and restore balance to Olympus? The fate of the gods and the very fabric of the mythological world hang in the balance. Embrace your divinity within and embark on a quest you will never forget!

—description from the publisher

DNGN is a 1-2 player, diceless dungeon crawler with a minimalist style and branching campaigns. Players each take control of an Adventurer, and work together to explore dungeons, defeat monsters, and complete missions, using an innovative stamina assignment system to plan their actions each turn. Over a series of missions the Adventurers will increase and customise their abilities, improve their skills, and purchase new equipment, to be able to take on the greater threats to come, and make decisions affecting their party's path through the campaign.

DNGN is a Print and Play Expanding Card Game; this core game pack contains everything you need to play the game, including a 9 mission fantasy campaign, The World Rune, while expansion packs add further campaigns with different themes, more equipment, and new monsters. Each campaign is played separately with a new set of Adventurers each time.

—description from the designer

SWOP and score! A fascinating and innovative tactical game with 66 colour dice on board. 
Score by forming combinations of dice on the gameboard. The more combinations you realize and the more complex they are, the more positive points you score. The most valuable dice combination is a SWOP - a specific combination of six dice, which is not easy to achieve.
Best play with 2 players, or with 4 in 2 teams, or play alone.

Before playing, you place the 66 dice in a random arrangement on the gameboard. When it is your turn, you exchange the positions of 2 dice and thus form new combinations of dice. Dependent on the value of these new dice combinations you score positive points and move your meeple on the scoreboard. Be the first to reach the target field and win the game.

Box size: 25 x 25 x 4 cm. Weight: 730 gr

—description from the designer

Choose your fighter and play your combo cards to KO your opponent before they KO you. Super Dice Battle uses a custom set of dice to mimic a video game controllers D-Pad and Buttons to create your combos. Utilize the Super Die for more powerful attacks and unleash your most powerful combos to become the Super Dice Champion!

—description from the designer

This is an expansion to the army card sets. It contains unit cards and an optional command action card.

The set will include the following 16 cards:

British: The commanders of Rorke’s Drift (Lt. Chard, Lt. Bromhead, Colour Sergeant Bourne, Pvt. Hook, Surgeon Major Reynolds), rules for bayonet section, double mielie bag walls and field hospital.

Zulu: Zulu ibuthu key, a sangomas (witch doctor), mystic hut and an optional command action card.

Boer: commando key, bethel commando pom-pom gun, field nurse Betty Wessel & field hospital.

The Rorke’s Drift Expansion Plus will fit with your existing cards into the card boxes which come with your Zulu Skirmish Army Card Sets!

—description from the publisher

A promotional giveaway pack for Ultimate Treehouse.

This pack includes unique tiles (two styles with two copies of each) that can be added into the store deck of the base game to provide additional options to build during gameplay.

The new tiles are a tent and a mineshaft and are intended to be placed in the lower left and right corners of the player mat.

Both promo tiles have a high VP value of 5 points.

—description from the publisher

An Escape Game on the Louvre… for the whole family!

Extend the adventure with 3 different scenarios, which correspond to 3 levels of difficulty.

Discover the fascinating works of the Louvre, and solve the three mysteries that hover in the galleries and the different departments.

In partnership with the Louvre Museum.

New box with hard case.

boardgame: Task Force Ranger: Somalia to Afghanistan
description:
Task Force Ranger (TFR) is a Solo or Multi-player “team” level rule set for up to five players.  It has been designed to simulate combat operations in the middle east from Somalia during Operation Restore Hope (1992-1994),  to Afghanistan 2022.  Enemy forces are Automa (automatically card driven) units.   Campaign play with the included campaign system and Tournament play usually consists of  two to four players on the same game table.  A fifth player may run any special ops teams and helicopters (helos) or play the Leader of the Enemy Forces!

CONCEPT OF PLAY

Task Force Ranger (TFR) is an immersive card driven game. Cards provide you with the majority of the team and equipment information needed to play. The easy to reference rule book walks you through the rules in detail. To begin play, you choose a mission. Your Ranger Chalk is then inserted by land, sea or air and moves out to accomplish it. That mission, like a Snatch & Grab (capture an enemy figure), will enable you to gain victory points (VPs) if you choose keep your teammates in a campaign. Otherwise, just being able to accomplish your mission with good team decisions and a low causality count will be your reward.

During game play each of your chalk teams takes two actions.  After each team completes their actions, draw an enemy activity card.   As you play, these cards add situational flavor.  Add up their different point values.  A total of five causes an enemy attack card to be drawn.  These cards allow enemy teams to enter play.  Who will engage first?  Will you make it to your objective?  Will you complete your mission?  From solo to larger games you will be challenged!  At tournament level, chalks may operate together on the same mission!  “Blackhawk Down” could be that mission!

A CAMPAIGN SYSTEM WITH 6 SCENARIOS
Including Blackhawk Down, Somalia  
&  Roberts Ridge, Afghanistan!  
Can your rangers accomplish their mission against the Muj in Fallujah? 
Will your troops survive outside the wire in the Sandbox?

—description from the publisher

Investigations Files is coming to Paris! The League, an international detective agency, has been contacted about the death of Hannah Dubois.

It immediately seems obvious that his death was not as "accidental" as the Paris police would have us believe.

However, when it comes to such a wealthy and respected family, the truth is not easily uncovered.

In the investigation game Investigation Files - Murder in Paris, you will have to decipher codes to solve puzzles, read police interrogation reports and witness statements, analyze clues, unlock new stages thanks to advance of your deductions.

The mystery awaits you! The League needs you!

The investigation files arrive in Berlin! The League, an international detective agency, has been contacted about the death of Laura Werner.

The Berlin orchestra lost its first violin, Laura Werner, the day before their winter festival. Rumor has it that she was murdered by another member of the orchestra.

Join the league, an international detective agency and find out what really happened. Who killed him ? What was the motive? And comment?

In the investigation game Investigation Files - Murder in Berlin you will have to decipher codes to solve puzzles, read police interrogation reports and witness statements, analyze clues, unlock new stages through advance of your deductions.

The mystery awaits you! The League needs you!
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Dawn of Battle is a hex-and-counter war game system that allows players to experience battles before the modern era. The game spans 3,000 years of combat, from the age of Greek generals to the invention of gunpowder.

The game system is straight forward and action packed. Players  depend upon the quality of their army's leadership to provide orders needed to maneuver their armies and strike against the enemy. The better an army's command, the more flexibility it has to move, rally, and attack.

Also included are supplementary rules that cover free deployment in scenarios, barbarian charges, command control and impetuosity, elephant panic, and random events.

Each scenario notes the disposition of the armies that engaged at a historical battle and the terrain that encompassed the battlefield. Units include a variety of infantry (including hoplites and the Macedonian phalanx), cavalry (including chariots and mounted archers), missile units (including slingers, longbows, and early gunpowder weapons, and special units such as elephants and war wagons.

The game's primary engine is comprised of an action deck used to determine command, the randomly determined outcomes of actions, and melee combat. Additionally, action cards provide special effects that players can use to enhance their units' abilities as well as the narrative of the play experience. The deck ensures that no two games are ever the same.

The base game includes twenty scenarios:


     Megiddo, 1457 BCE
     Che, 717 BCE
     Marathon, 490 BCE
     Gaugamela, 331 BCE
     Ipsus, 301 BCE
     Heraclea, 280 BCE
     Lake Trasimeno, 217 BCE
     Raphia, 217 BCE
     Illipa, 206 BCE
     Zama, 202 BCE
     Sabis River, 57 BCE
     Pharsalus, 48 BCE
     Hastings, 1066 CE
     Dyrrhachium, 1081 CE
     Fornham, 1173 CE
     Bouvines, 1214 CE
     Kalka River, 1223 CE
     Crêçy, 1346 CE
     Poitiers, 1356 CE
     Aljubarrota, 1386 CE


In addition to these battles, the game includes comprehensive guidelines on how to create your own scenarios, making "Dawn of Battle" a complete system usable to recreate virtually any battle from the ancient world to early gunpowder.

This Designer's Edition of Dawn of Battle is an update to the original Dawn of Battle released by Worthington Games in 2021. This edition includes all new scenarios as well as 120 double-sided terrain tiles.

—description from the publisher
family: History: Wars in the Ancient Near East

description:
On your turn play one or two cards, if there are 4 cards of the same color or the same number on the table, you take them to your scoring area.

To savor victory, avoid the spicy ones or manage to collect as many of the same color as you can by using the special cards in your favor.

Immerse yourself in the epic adventure of Spirited Earth as you uncover the remains of human civilization that existed before Earth's Great Extinction.

Spirited Earth is a medium-complexity euro-style game for 2-4 players, with card drafting, worker placement, resource and dice management.

A story-rich cooperative board game for 1 to 4 players, Omens of Destiny provides an addictive blend of strategy and luck that pulls you along its fantastical quest and combat-laden lands.

Each of the seven characters from which you can choose have several unique racial and background abilities to draw from. During the course of any game you will have opportunity to increase your character's stats, gain new gear and items, and even learn to wield powerful magic by gaining control of the Mystic Forces.

There are a host of Main Quests available that support both one-shot sessions and legacy/campaign play!

Achieve victory by completing your quest objectives before the Corruption takes over the realm.

—description from the publisher

The cardgame from the Netherlands where three cards form a lizzard (Dizzy Lizzy). To win the game you need to get rid of your cards before the other players do. The rules to create a Dizzy Lizzy are as follows:


     All colors are the same
     All colors are different
     All patterns are the same
     All patterns are different


—description from the publisher (translated)

Het kaartspel uit Nederland waarbij drie kaarten samen een duizelingwekkende Lizzy stapel vormen die altijd aan tenminste één van de volgende vier voorwaarden moet voldoen:


     Alle kleuren zijn hetzelfde
     Alle kleuren zijn anders
     Alle patronen zijn hetzelfde
     Alle patronen zijn anders


—description from the publisher

Medis, with its strange feudal politics and reverence of those capable of mastering magic have districts set up in most great cities of the world and Harrows is no exception. These districts search out talented wizards and provide allies as well as an outlet for their assorted strange products. Wherever there is a Lotus District, magic and mystery go hand in hand. Exotic recreations and unknown dangers abound and can cause a societal rift at the drop of a hat.
 The Lotus District expansion for Dark Domains provides new challenges and new opportunities to challenge even the staunchest ally of the Necromancer. A new mini-board is included, the Lotus District, which allows a wider variety of elements with one minion, as well as the Wing Kong Market where everything is for sale, but the currency is evil instead of coin. New buildings inspired by the architects of Medis are available and new monsters can be imported to lair in your accursed domain. New fortunes are revealed by the mystics of that foreign land. Finally, new adventurers and henchmen walk the streets of the district eager to seek glory in your defeat or provide their own unique aid to your cause.

—description from the publisher

The world is full of Supes. These super powered people are more famous than celebrities, revered as gods and backed by Vought International. Some Supes abuse their powers rather than use them for good, and a group of non-powered vigilantes bent on punishing corrupt Supes look to expose the truth. They are The Boys.

Vs. System 2PCG: The Boys is a standalone game that can also be combined with other titles in the Vs. System 2PCG game system. In the game, each player chooses a main character, builds a deck with exactly sixty cards, and attempts to stun the other player's main character.

This set allows you to use the powers of the mightiest heroes from The Seven and the abilities of the cunning vigilantes from The Boys. Build up you deck with iconic characters like Homelander, Queen Maeve, Butcher, Hughie and more.

BarbeCubes is a cooperative dice game about barbecue for the whole family!

Pick up the orders, roll the dice to match the ingredients, and avoid burning the food with the coal!

Your goal is to deliver 7 orders correctly and the whole table works together to make the barbecue a success!

Vs. System 2PCG: AEW is a standalone game that can also be combined with other titles in the Vs. System 2PCG game system. In the game, each player chooses a main character, builds a deck with exactly sixty cards, and attempts to stun the other player's main character.

Vs. System 2PCG: AEW' features 12 new Main Characters and 28 new Supporting Characters, such as Sting and Darby Allin. Will you claim victory with the charismatic mastermind Chris Jericho, the unparalleled force of Kris Statlander, and battle-tested Jon Moxley on team Babyface? Or will you be unstoppable with the fearless and the relentless Dr. Britt Baker D.M.D. and the dominant powerhouse Jade Cargill on team Heel?

Meme Your Life is a party card game. Your life gathered in a game. Each round, one player takes the role of judge and plays a photo card, after which everyone else plays a caption card to complete the meme. The judge decides the funniest pairing, and whoever played the winning caption card wins the round.
Find in 30 seconds the largest number of images with the letter or theme drawn on the roulette wheel. Exercise attention, memory and language.

Grave Trigger is an apocalyptic sci-fi miniatures skirmish and campaign game where players control humans terminally bound to Animus Reactors—sentient, techno-organic armor that feeds on those who wield it in exchange for terrifying power.

Unlimited Movement - Experience the most dynamic movement system ever used in a miniatures game—no tape measures required.

Diceless Combat - Leap off buildings with aerial attacks, throw terrain across the battlefield, and rely on your skill rather than the roll of a die.

Mutate The World - Summon living terrain to give you cover and even animate it to fight by your side.

Living Weapons - Evolve in response to damage and unleash hidden Damage State cards to turn the tide of battle.

—description from the designer

description:
The Card Game about finding family members.

"I wonder who the son of the Takanara family is with?
And the mother of the Mato Fino family?
And the grandparents of the Bom Fim family?"

The cards are shuffled and dealt to all players.

The object of the game is to form a complete family of 5 cards. To complete the family on their turn, the player simply asks who has "that person from family x". Whoever has the letter trades with you. And so the game continues until the first one completes his family. A quick and easy game to teach, ideal to play with children from 3 years old. Stimulates reasoning, memory, and intelligence.

Promo card that was part of the Pixel Tactics Deluxe Kickstarter.

Card text:
You can only recruit this hero into the Vanguard.

Vanguard: This hero does not block melee.
Flank: This hero cannot be attacked in melee.
Rear: Spell: Remove this hero from play to draw 6 cards.

Leader: If there are 8 corpses in your unit, you win the game.
Find all the parts and assemble your own Potato Head before your friends do. But be careful, other players will try to be faster than you! The player who rings the bell first wins the game.

Oggin is a sea route building game where players must face each other and the board!

Your objective is to reach the treasure island faster than the other pirates.

"Oggin" controls the entire sea and uses it to hinder his plans. Always at the end of the rounds he comes into action.

Use bombs and islands to your advantage and watch out for sea creatures. Show who is the best pirate captain and not even Oggin can stop him.

—user summary

The child will learn to associate each animal with its habitat, where the pairs of cards are an association between the animal and the region where it lives.
A fun way to test children's memory.
and the adults!

GAME DESCRIPTION

The game is played in rounds. In each round, the first to play (who will be called the leader) must draw an environment card from the bag and show it to all players.

The game offers 2 ways to play:

SIMPLE RULE - where the objective is to get the highest number of associations.

ADVANCED RULE - where you can bet points when you are sure that the animal you are looking for is on the card to be turned

The player with the highest number of points, forming pairs of animal cards and environments, wins the game.

description:
Somewhat flustered inmates are once again trying to escape from prison. Taking advantage of the guard's nap, they manage to dig a tunnel, and are close to achieving their objective. But will it work, as each thinks more about getting in the way of their colleague than escaping?

In the first part of the plan, they must build a tunnel…

… And in the second part, it's time to escape!

Prisoners must now escape through the constructed tunnel, while being chased by the prison guard. Disturb your colleagues, and reach your long-awaited freedom before them!

boardgame: Pixel Tactics: Wondrous Game Designer / Antoine Bauza
description:
Promo card that was part of the Pixel Tactics Deluxe Kickstarter.

Card text:
Vanguard: Spell: If your unit's total attack strength is great than the rival unit's, apply 3 damage to that unit's leader.
Flank: Spell: Put a hero from your hand into play in any empty slot in your unit.
Rear: Spell: Discard a card to remove 2 damage from your leader and give it [+1 attack].
Order: Discard up to 3 cards to remove 2 damage from your leader and give it [+1 attack] per card discarded.

Leader: Players may not look at their hands, but rivals can. Limited free action: choose a card in your hand and a zone (vanguard, flank, or rear). A rival must tell you the text of that zone's ability. Any player may use this action.

The Battle of Lützen 1813 is a grand tactical wargame simulating the confrontation of May 2, 1813 which marked the beginning of the first phase of the German campaign. The Russian-Prussian coalition army is in an offensive attitude, Tsar Alexander and his entourage considering a victory over a desperate Napoleon as probable, his army of conscripts suffering from a weakness in cavalry.
The objective is therefore to obtain a significant victory which will seal the fate of the Confederation of the Rhine under French influence since 1806. Ney's III French corps plays the role of perfect prey in the initial coalition plan which is a kind of Ambush Aiming for the Partial Destruction of the French Imperial Army.
Napoleon, aware of the coalition's intentions, is looking for a decisive victory to put an end to this coalition, the aim of which is to overthrow him has not yet been announced. He must destroy the opposing army. But such a providential victory rests on a bold maneuver of refusal from his right to attract the enemy, take Leipzig and fall to his rear. But without effective light cavalry to inform him about the coalition movements, Napoleon advanced in a certain fog...

Lützen 1813 (Vae Victis 169) uses the same system as Bautzen 1813 published in VaeVictis 150.

One round = 1 hour
One counter = 1 division or brigade
Complexity: 6/10
Solo playability: 8/10
Duration: 2 to 3 hours

—description from the publisher

Estrela arrived with another game from the Academia da Mente line, meet Um a Um.

The objective of this game is to exercise logical reasoning, numerical perception and mathematical problem solving.
This game is perfect for amusing children and helping them to stimulate their memory, attention and concentration.

Although most of the games are aimed at children, the Academia da Mente line, by Estrela, is also aimed at the elderly. By stimulating senses and functions such as attention, memory and language, they help keep the mind healthy and fight symptoms of degenerative diseases.

This new Estrela collection aims not only to have a fun aspect, but a slightly more “medicinal” side, stimulating the brain more and more to avoid future health problems.

Exercise your numerical perception, calculation, logic and problem solving in this game.

From mining to crafting - become the best jeweler you can be!

In 1905, the world's largest diamond, mined in Transvaal, South Africa, arrived at the British royal court.
The world's greatest jewelers were invited to join the royal family to create a crown for the Queen's coronation.

As a jeweler invited to the royal court, you must create the most elegant and spectacular jewelry in the world.
Open the boxes, cut the gemstone pieces, and create beautiful jewelry that will be chosen by the Queen.


             Components ***
        
    


- 112 Jewel Fragments
 - 8 Skill Tiles (3Shovels, 3Pickaxes, 2Pincers)
 - 1 Cloth Pocket
 - 1 Manual


             Set of Game ***
        
    


1. Put all the jewel fragments into a bag and mix well.
2. Mix the function tiles and place them face down next to the table.
3. Each player should take 2 pieces of jewel fragments randomly from the pocket.
4. Place 1 of the 2 pieces you took in front of you.
5. Hold the remaining piece in your hand so that no one else can see it.


             Playing the Game ***
        
    


1. Play rock-paper-scissors, and designate the winner as the first to go.
2. On your turn, draw 1 piece of jewel fragment from the pocket and add it to your hand.
3. On your turn, choose a piece from your hand and put it down.
(The pieces of jewelry can be turned in any direction you wish, but at least one of the sides must be put together.)
4. When your turn is over, pass the turn to the person to your left.
5. When the jewel is complete by collecting 6 pieces, take 1 skill tile to use that skill.
(If a gem is completed using a skill tile, you cannot import additional skill tiles.)


             End of Game ***
        
    


A. If someone has used the last functional tile or 
B. if someone has used the last gem fragment in the pocket,the game will end when the next person and the others all put down their gem fragments.


             Scoring ***
        
    


At the end of the game, the points for each completed gem are added together. Whoever scores the most points wins.

- Diamond : 6points
 - Ruby : 5points
 - Emerald : 4points
 - Sapphire : 3points
 - Topaz : 2points
 - Amethyst : 1points

—description from the publisher

Step into the world of Round Table TCG, where you take on the mantle of a ruler in Arthurian Britain. Build your kingdom, rally legendary knights, arm them with powerful gear, and bestow noble titles — all in a bid to conquer the land and cement your legacy.

The game includes 254 cards — 30 regions in England and Wales; 20 Demesnes, which generate your kingdom's resources; and 204 main cards: knights, equipment, titles, quests, and redirects — and players each construct a demesnes deck and main deck. The region deck represents the contested lands of Britain; players battle for control of these regions, each of which grants renown, the key to victory.

Gameplay unfolds across rounds of four seasons. Each season, you decide which knights to recruit from your hand, which knights should have what equipment and titles, and which regions your knights will attack. When a battle begins for a region, you can sabotage your rivals by sending their knights on quests or using redirects to bring your own back into the fray. The strongest force seizes the region and the glory.

Lands are upgraded during play, e.g., you can improve a farm by adding a manor, then to a large manor, and so on. Each type adds more resource point to that ruler's pool, which lets you recruit more knights, etc.

When the five regions in play have been conquered or a player can no longer draw a card, the game ends, and the ruler with the most renown wins.
family: Theme: King Arthur / The Knights of the Round Table / Camelot

Children's game based on fairy tale "The Six Servants" collected by the German Brothers Grimm.

Dear children, do you know the fairy tale about the "Long, Broad and Sharpsight" who helped the prince to rescue the princess from the captivity of the evil wizard? The play is about the fairy tale characters' difficult journey to the prince's castle. The one who makes the journey full of obstacles and dangers the fastest wins.

Each Player represents one of the six characters. These characters are the Princess, the Prince, the Magician, the Long, the Wide and the Sharp-eyed. Their mission is to get to the prince's castle as quickly as possible.

There are two dice in the game, the first with pictures of the characters, the second a classic numeric 6-dice. Players take turns rolling both dice. The dice with pictures determines which character will progress, the second dice determines by how many square. Thus, it is very common for a player to move another player's piece around the game board.

However, if he is lucky enough to have his own piece appear on the roll, then he advances by double the number of points rolled on the other dice.

On the black squares, players progress according to the dice roll. However, if they land on a red square, they are forced to perform the action associated with that square. These actions may delay them or even return them to squares they have already passed through. However, they can make them move forward faster in certain cases.

It is up to the players' agreement when the game ends. It may be when the first piece reaches the castle. Its owner wins and the other players lose. However, it is possible to end the game after all but one of the pieces have reached the castle. In that case, we know the order of the other players in addition to the winner who got to the castle first.

Velbloudí karavana is a quick and easy card game where you become a merchant travelling through a desert and trading with other players. You trade cards for precious coins, but transactions can be affected by other players' cards, hence nothing is certain. The player who crosses the desert as the wealthiest becomes the winner.

The game is quite swift and full of interactions between players, so you must stay on your toes.

Combat Arena: Lair of the Beast is a great way of playing fast and furious games in the world of Warhammer 40,000.

Graceful aliens, sturdy robots, and genetically-enhanced super-soldiers have been tossed into an arena to fight to the death. To ensure maximum carnage, there’s also a massive, frenzied beast to take on! Team up with your rivals to eliminate the threat, but be careful… Only one champion can ultimately win the match.

Descend into the lair of a deadly alien Ambull for glory, revenge, or profit in Combat Arena: Lair of the Beast – an action-packed game for two to four players featuring an eclectic collection of Warhammer miniatures. Pick from one of 10 diverse characters – from Space Marine Sergeant Vennaro to the oddly self-aware ‘robot’ UR-025 – each with their own unique skills and talents, to battle both the Ambull and each other to emerge victorious.

With fast-paced, brutal gameplay, anything can happen. You’ll need to stay on your toes – or get incredibly lucky – to win. Play with just one friend, or get four people together for an epic slugfest. Games can be finished in about a half hour, which means you can get a few rounds in to try out different tricks, or get revenge.

This box includes: 

     A rulebook with background material about the fighters and the galaxy in which they live, assembly guides, 8 pages of game rules, and a variety of playing modes and missions to try out

     Everything you need to play – dice, cards, a game board, tokens, and profiles for each fighter

description:

The game material familiarizes children with the numbers 1 to 100 by completing various tasks. As well as this, they also have the opportunity to consolidate their knowledge with various game ideas.

In the heart of the Espinhal Mountain, in the municipality of Penela, the schist village of Ferraria de São João is called so because it is likely that a small iron mine may have existed there. The shepherd’s horn echoed through the vast oak grove in the past, calling the community’s flock. After a day of grazing in the mountains, the goats would return to the village where they spent the night in communal goat pens, now preserved to bear witness to memories of the past. There are no longer wolves in the forest, but the biodiversity is varied and, at night, the village is visited by deer or wild boar. At the same time, the existing flora provides bees with nutritious food to produce high-quality honey.
It’s a beautiful experience to walk through the village streets, observe the architecture of the now-restored schist houses, and try to spot the traditional Maia flowers hanging from the doors in spring.
To protect themselves from fires, the villagers replaced eucalyptus trees with cork oak trees. Under their shade, the goat herds work meticulously as they graze to help prevent fires.
In this game, you experience the dynamic of a lively village, which you are invited to visit on foot or by bike on the mountain biking trails. Players take turns by playing cards with bees, oaks, or boars to collect goats to make collection sets that match the objectives.

—description from the designer

Incubo is a cooperative deck-building game designed for 2 players, where they take on the roles of a child "Tommy" and his best friend cat, "Alpha", as they confront their fears to rescue their sister, "Nina". Together, they must build their decks by passing cards between each other, eliminating weaker or negative cards, and facing various adventures within the dream world. The game is divided into three R.E.M. phases, each culminating in a confrontation with a terrifying monster, such as a ball of yarn, a half-opened wardrobe, a pigeon, and more. These monsters are confronted simultaneously by both players on different parts of the creature, and every victory strengthens the characters. Throughout the game, players will manage their resources, represented by the ticking of time to ensure they can save Nina.

—description from the publisher

What happens when a biology teacher, a NASA JPL educator, and an award winning artist get together to imagine astrobiology in our neighborhood?

This new game puts a unique twist on the original Ecologies concept by including new “Life Requirements,” and “Celestial Bodies” instead of biomes.

It’s all about building healthy food webs on different worlds. If you create a healthy ecology you get the perks and powers of that Celestial Body. Each organism card lists what it eats and what it’s eaten by. You arrange them with plants on the bottom, predators on top. There is a trade mechanic where you trade with others based on what you need and what they need. It is a race to get 12 victory points and different organisms give different points. Like a typical Role Playing Game you have a character sheet, roll for saving throws, fight monsters and find treasure BUT when your character dies in the game you have to chug your drink in real life! It adds a real level of tension and excitement to the battles. Everything matters.

The new Sidekick Mechanic makes for new character customization and powers!

The battles are a series of one-on-one fights to the death. You either kill the monster earning coins and XP or your character dies and you must chug your drink!

The cards act as the GM (So everyone can drink) and the heroes go through four themed quests in each game - each getting progressively difficult and more likely to drink. However as this is a light RPG and a light Drinking Game there is a one chug per quest maximum which prevents Drinking Quest from being a "Pass out in 10 minutes" kind of drinking game. Over the course of 2 or 3 hours, a typical game averages 2 or 3 chugs.

Whoever gets the most XP wins! Whoever loses had to chug the most so it's sort of like everybody wins.
An additional set of 98 cards

boardgame: Silent Night, Deadly Night: The Game – Expansion Pack #1
description: 10 new cards with 'new actions' and 'group actions', and 4 more players.

boardgame: Silent Night, Deadly Night: The Game – Expansion Pack #2
description: When Billy is about to win, players can now slow him down: they draw one of the new sealed trivia cards, read the question, flip the timer and try to answer within 15 seconds.

boardgame: Beers of Ballard: The Board Game
description:
Welcome to Ballard! You are a beer connoisseur brewery-hopping in Seattle.  Each round, breweries host events and release new beer. Visit popular breweries to gather beer and power-ups, but don’t forget to stop by the food truck in between the breweries.

The game consists of twelve rounds.  An event and supply card are revealed each round to determine which breweries have beers and events.  Players secretly decide which brewery they will visit this round; then all players reveal their chosen destination simultaneously.  Players collect the beer at the selected brewery and must share beer supplies if multiple players chose the same brewery.   Over time players’ buzz level increases and they will need to strategically visit the food truck to reduce their buzz level.  At the end of the game, the player who accumulates the most beer wins!

—description from the designer

Spirits of the King is a 1v1 open-drafting card game with hand management and dice rolling elements. Players conjure Spirits by offering their Blood, Soul, and Sanity cards in an effort to destroy all three of the opponent's lifelines.

Players begin the game with 4 or 5 of each resource card in their hand. On a player's turn, they select a spirit to conjure from a shared pool, paying its cost and resolving its effect. As players conjure more spirits throughout the game, they gain experience and may conjure stronger (yet more costly!) spirits. However, players must be careful with what they offer the spirits, as becoming too low puts them at risk of losing their lifelines!

Will you overcome the costs of power? Or will you fall victim to sacrificing too much?

—description from the designer

Kandy Crush is a two-player game that portrays the Kingdom of Kandy (on the island of modern day Sri Lanka) and how they fought (and allied with) neighboring kingdoms as well as three colonial powers.

The game is played in up to three turns (called epochs) with each epoch having one active colonial power. They are, in order, Portugal, the Netherlands and Great Britain.

Each epoch consists of three phases of card play where the two sides take turn trying to manipulate the game state by using events from the cards as well as their Action Point Value to resolve actions.

At the end of each epoch a winner of that particular epoch is declared. The first side to achieve two Epoch Victories is declared the victor of the game.

—description from the designer

The Reset Kit contains one additional copy of the Expedition Logbook and one additional copy of the Camp Archives.

There is one copy of each of these books in the base game; these additional books can be used when playing the campaign more than once, if a player doesn't want to re-use the stickers or they are no longer re-usable after wear and tear.

Approx. 17×22×0.7 cm, 220 g

Huradrim is a party game where 2 to 10 players are brewmasters looking to gather the right ingredients to brew the best beer for the king of dwarves!

The gameplay combines mechanics of collecting components and discarding the right cards for fast rounds!

You keep your score with a points card or try it as a drinking game! (please drink responsibly)

Grab your hard hat there are hazards ahead! This Happy Little Dinosaurs Expansion Pack comes with 2 new characters and 3 new game components. Hazard tokens can force your opponents to take a Hazard card and face new misfortunes, while Lucky Day cards will help you dodge disasters. You might come face-to-face with Bigfoot or get sucked into a pyramid scheme, so let's hope a shooting star is just around the corner!

—description from the publisher

The NEW Happy Little Dinosaurs Expansion: Hazards Ahead shakes up gameplay with Hazards (as if meteors and bad haircuts weren’t bad enough) 
Adding certain Disaster cards to your Disaster Area will allow you to add a Hazard token to another player’s Escape Route!
When a player’s Dinosaur meeple reaches a space with a Hazard token, that player must add the top card from the Hazard deck face up to their Hazard Area. Hazard cards feature an effect that gives players disadvantages throughout the game. (Hazard cards stay in your Hazard Area unless a card effect allows you to remove them.)
Chaos awaits ☄️ Pick up Happy Little Dinosaurs: Hazards Ahead today!

boardgame: Legendary: A Marvel Deck Building Game – Ant-Man and the Wasp
description:
This 200-card expansion brings all three Ant-Man films from the Marvel Cinematic Universe into a single, deluxe-sized set! Scott Lang, who started out as a cat burglar, grows into something bigger (and smaller) than himself as he and the Wasp battle Yellowjacket, Ghost, and Kang. Make use of new keywords, such as Heist, to overcome new Schemes and gameplay possibilities! This is the perfect way to start a new collection or grow your existing library of Legendary: A Marvel Deck Building Game expansions!


     Relive the epic battles of the Marvel Studios trilogy of Ant-Man movies from Ant-Man to Ant-Man and the Wasp: Quantumania with characters and images straight from the films!
     8 New Heroes including Ant-Man, Wasp, Cassie Lang and more!
     3 New double-sided Masterminds including Yellowjacket and Kang!
     New and returning keywords like Heist and Conqueror!
     The 36th expansion in the vast Legendary landscape. This requires a Core set to play including the Marvel Studios Phase 1 base set! 


—description from the publisher

You want to get rid of those soul tokens as quickly as possible, but the other players have the same goal. It goes from island to island and the lucky 7 always helps you. All other numbers can have bad consequences. Let the sharks circle!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Inspired by Säbel & Schädel.

Things are getting hectic in this legendary "seaman's chess" of the Caribbean South Seas. Positioning on the right planks of wood is crucial; Groups of 4 help. With the highest score you will receive glorious reputation, eternal fame & highest honor at the end.

—description from the publisher (translated)
A Smurf rarely smurfs alone - seven funny Smurfs show on 36 domino cards that a well-known and popular children's game with these funny figures is particularly attractive.

+  1 action

Now and at the start of your next turn: +2 cards
Then discard 2 cards from your hand

Originally planned as correction card for the original Verlies out of the Abenteuerer-Box.

The strechgoal from the kickstarter campaign.
Adds to the game:
30 new terrain tiles
35 new event cards
7 new scene cards
1 new barrier

Comets are now approaching the Asteroid Belt. They will offer valuable resources for your corporation and will help you reach more distant Asteroids as they orbit around the sun. Of course there is always a chance they will explode if they plunge into the fiery embrace of the sun. Will you take the risk to send one of your probes to them?

In this expansion, Comet tokens are placed on the Asteroid belt and they travel, from round to round, from belt to belt, towards the sun. Send there a probe using the same rules as for Asteroids and get advantage of their resources and victory points. Afterwards you may move your probe at no cost to an available neighbor asteroid or risk to stay on it. There is a chance that instead of providing more resources and victory points they get exploded by checking the explosion cards and earn less than expected.

—description from the publisher

Mars, amongst the numerous institutions of its thriving colony, facilitates the headquarters of the Asteroid Mining Assembly of Corporations.
 The A.M.A.C. council has always had a deep bureaucratic structure consisting of a number of different unions and federations serving mostly their own plans and agendas. It expects your unwavering commitment to integrating their requirements by fulfilling their contracts.

In Mars Contracts, you get to follow certain ways of development in your corporations trying to fulfill contracts (objectives) of different unions. Dealing with these unions will offer you new actions combinations and rewards.

In this expansion the union tiles are introduced. Players may use their leader tokens to take contract cards from the union tiles that provide them with some small objectives that can be fulfilled by following certain paths of development or use specific actions through the game. For every contract they fulfill, each union provides more victory points to all players who contributed by completing their contracts.

—description from the publisher

Rediscover the pleasure of Dixit with the Dixit: National Museum in Krakow expansion and play with 84 new cards from a selection of art pieces from the museum’s collection.

Dixit: MNK includes 84 entirely new cards, which you can use as an alternative deck or mix them with the set from any base game. They feature fragments of artworks gathered in the collections of the National Museum in Krakow, including "Lady with an Ermine" by Leonardo da Vinci, "Girl with Chrysanthemums" by Olga Boznańska, "Young Poland" by Jacek Malczewski, and "Wernyhora" by Jan Matejko. This unique expansion will not only allow you to discover a world of unconventional associations, but also to explore the galleries of the MNK and marvel at art from various eras.

Use the cards from the Dixit base game or those from the expansion. You can also mix them together! The game ends when one or more players have reached or exceed 30 points on the scoring track.

—description from the publisher

Windborne is a connection game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the spaces (cells) of an initially empty hexagonal grid of hexagons (board).

Black plays first, then turns alternate. On your turn, if there are less than three pawns on the board, place a disk of your color on an empty cell and a neutral pawn on an empty cell or onto a friendly disk that does not yet have a pawn on it. Otherwise, follow these steps:


     Move a pawn zero or more times onto adjacent cells in the same direction. Each of those cells must be empty or occupied by a friendly disk only. (You cannot move onto cells occupied by pawns or enemy disks, but an enemy disk on the pawn's starting cell does not impede its movement.)
     Place a disk of your color on a cell that does not yet contain a disk. If the cell contains a pawn, place your disk under it.


You win if, at the start of your turn, there is a group of interconnected disks of your color such that no contiguous area outside the group contains more than half of the pawns (i.e. two or three). Such areas may include any mix of empty cells, pawns and disks of either color. You lose if, at the start of your turn, your opponent has such a group and you cannot place a disk.

To make the game fair, White will have the option, on their first turn only, to swap sides with Black instead of making a regular move.

				
				
					NotesAs an alternative, a different number of pawns can be used. A small odd number of pawns is recommended. Even numbers may result in connection races. The optimal number might be directly proportional to the size of the board.

Windborne is Omny with moving targets. In Windborne, a player with a massive group running through the center may still lose if, for example, two pawns end up trapped on top of adjacent enemy stones away from the center. This is less likely in Omny. Note that pawns are most easily trapped in the corners of the board.

Miniature garden management & defense board game to protect your city together
Somewhere not here. Someday, not now.
A world where swords and magic are commonplace.

In this world, humans and "demi-humans" such as elves and dragonewts coexisted.
Each race formed its own cultural sphere as a "nation", and repeated exchanges and conflicts.

You are dispatched to be in charge of reconstruction of a section of the fortified city "Rhineland", which is adjacent to many nations.
However, at the time of the inauguration ceremony, an astrologer warns that "Ajin will attack this city."

The remaining period is 2 years.

In order to fend off attacks, it is necessary to cooperate with the residents of the city and sometimes team up with demi-humans.
Also, if his activities are evaluated, it is not a dream to be promoted to "Lord" who has control over the entire Rhineland.

For the sake of territory, people, and one's own honor.
Use your skills to protect the city and rebuild it.

boardgame: ゴールデンフェニックスブラスター (Golden Phoenix Blaster)
description:
The three modern Phoenix Squadron Horangers and the two ancient Phoenix Squadron Orangers join forces to finally corner the Demon King Black Giraffe to the final battlefield. The hot and long battle is now about to reach its conclusion.

There is only one way to defeat the Demon King. It is a super special move in which all the heroes unite their hearts.

Scream!! Golden Phoenix Blaster!!

Deliver true bonds that transcend words and communicate with hearts to children all over the country!

Emmet and the Spiders Web is a puzzle game. Using three cards in your hand, tell you what symbol configuration within a 2x2 box you need to activate that card. That card lets you score a point/bug and then swap two symbols. It also generally flips a symbol to the spider side, making it harder to make configurations in the future.

—description from the designer

Bumblebee is a solo pattern recognition, route building, 9-Card game about a bumblebee pollinating a set sequence of 6 flowers (1 x Flower per Card)and returning to the nest before the cards run out.

—description from the designer
Кассиопея ("Cassiopeia") has 98 new illustrations. All of them are bright, cheerful and not complicated at all.

Одиссея ("Odyssey") is even more recognizable images in a new interpretation: superheroes, monsters, Papa Carlo and even Lenin. A real challenge for those who think outside the box and see the new in the familiar!

Персефона ("Persephone") is an addition to the Imaginarium that will help you take your mind off the usual maps and make the game even more diverse and interesting. References to famous books and literary works, recognizable characters and pictures with a philosophical plot - all this is here.
Прайм-Тайм ("Prime time") is an expansion to Imaginarium that illustrates the latest global events and trends.

Saga of Soldiers & Heroes begins with one simple mechanic: When a player takes their turn, they draw one card from their deck, then they play one card from their hand. That's all.

The play area is divided into three battle. When a card is played, it is added to one of the battles, where its force value will be added to its side.

The game is played over five rounds. Each player plays with a deck of units: 9 basic soldiers and 3 heroes. You get to choose which heroes you want to include each round. Heroes can be stronger than Soldiers, but more importantly, each hero has a unique special ability.

The game includes 15 different heroes. Both players have an identical collection to choose from.

When both players have played all of their cards, the round ends and victory points are awarded for winning the battles.

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Riders of the Storm Enemy Theme Pack
description:
An Enemy Pack themed for the Hell Train and the infamous Mutant Outlaws known as the Scafford Gang. This is the second set of a new type called an Enemy Theme Pack. It's a great way to add a bunch of exciting new Enemies, cards, and Missions that are all themed for a specific World or faction in the game.

The Scafford Gang are the most notorious mutant outlaws in the Southwest! Making their hideouts in the old caves and mines around the ruins of Brimstone, they have becoming grossly mutated by the cursed radiation of the Dark Stone. As their infamy grows, so too do their ranks, welcoming in any fellow mutants that have been ousted by the locals or that are just looking to make a dishonest buck!

Led by Colonel Scafford and his original gang of confederates (including his miscreant sons), the Scafford Gang mostly loots travelers on the road, knocks over frontier town banks, and raids caravans for their gold and precious Dark Stone cargo. But when it comes to bigger heists, the Colonel’s daughter, Captain Belle Scafford, rides out with her mutant Outriders to race down Trains in search of the big scores.

The Riders of the Storm Enemy Theme Pack comes with a host of new Scafford Gang Enemies to challenge your Heroes, as well as new Scafford-themed Missions for the Hell Train, and over 40 new Game cards with assorted counters.

Captain Belle Scafford is the favorite daughter of the Colonel and an expert horsewoman, deadly duelist with a sabre, and a rogue of the highest order. She has a twinkle in her eye, and a cutthroat smile, with a ruthless disposition. Many men have died underestimating her skill, both on horseback and with a blade. Quick on the draw, she never shoots a man in the back… they are rarely fast enough to turn and run.

The Scafford Outriders are mutant train robbers that descend from the hills to hit the rail lines that run through the valleys and gulches near Brimstone. Riding thunderous horses tainted by the Void energy of the black rock, these wild outlaws use their weird and wondrous mutations to great effect during a heist. With slippery skin to squeeze through the train’s windows, flailing tentacles to climb onto the rooftops, or extra limbs to hold up several passengers at once, they hit and run, leaving only a slimy trail and the faint echo of cackling laughter in their wake.

When extra muscle is needed to tear the doors off a train or rip a steel safe to pieces, the Safecrackers always get the job done. These hulking mutants can charge up alongside a train, running at full bore before making a massive leap to land on the rooftop. With meaty barbed claws and rippling muscle, they can shred right through anything that stands in their way, whether it’s the side of a train car, a reinforced bank safe, or even the unfortunate marshals that try to stop them. No cage can keep them in, no wall can keep them out.

Though afflicted with a hideous troll body and diminutive size, Mortimer J. (Morty) Scafford makes up for his shortcomings by being the best heist planner and safe breaker in the West. Armed with the tools of the trade and a custom stethoscope for fine detail work, he often rides into battle perched atop one of the massive mutant Safecrackers to get him where he needs to be. Planning every heist down to the last detail, Morty Scafford leaves nothing to chance. If you think you know what he’s going to do, he’s most likely already been and gone, and he probably stole your wallet too.

boardgame: Conquering the Valley: Cross Keys/Port Republic
description:
Stonewall Jackson's Valley Campaign comes to an end when they face two Union armies on consecutive days. If he can defeat the Union forces, Jackson's men can rejoin Lee near Richmond.

This game is the second entry in the "Shattered Union" series of American Civil War battles. It combines the smaller battles used by the Blind Swords system with the card system used in the "Black Swan" system. It uses regimental units due to the small size of the opposing armies so it should play quickly.

This series, like the other two, have Hermann Luttmann's finger prints all over it so don't worry if you don't see his name on the box.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: UNO Ultimate: Spider-Man 2099 vs. Venom Add-On 2-Pack
description:
Take your game night to new superhero levels with the UNO Ultimate Marvel Spider-Man 2099 vs. Venom Add-On 2-Pack. Each package includes two never-before-seen decks with special cards and unique rules for each character. The Spider-Man 2099 and Venom cards contain comic-inspired artwork and special Venom symbiote-infected packaging that visually emulates the characters’ narratives.

UNO Ultimate Marvel Spider-Man 2099 vs. Venom Add-On 2-Pack 
Set of two UNO Ultimate decks featuring characters from the Spider-Verse  
First UNO Ultimate decks with all-foil cards 
Can be played together or added to any UNO Ultimate deck 
Special comic book designed packaging with a transparent vinyl sleeve overlay 
Colors and decorations may vary.

Batman
The World's greatest detective, Batman is always prepared for anything and so is his character deck. Be prepared to change the active color when possible - even when it doesn't help you - to gain valuable information on other players and the Danger Deck. Keep track of cards played to maximize "Search For Clues" and "Caped Crusader."

Included in this add-on pack:


     One Uno Ultimate Batman Deck!
     Character specific Wild Cards!
     Danger Deck Cards with Villains and more!
     2 Chase Foil Cards

boardgame: UNO Ultimate: Add-on Pack – Harley Quinn
description:
Harley Quinn
Let's get dangerous! Harley Quinn likes to get wild, and so is her playing style. Play with reckless abandon by using your "!" Cards as much as possible and always keep track of how may cards players have in their hands. Strategically select a player to draw cards or just go after anyone who looks at you wrong.

Included in this add-on pack:


     One Uno Ultimate Harley Quinn Deck!
     Character specific Wild Cards!
     Danger Deck Cards with Villains and more!
     2 Chase Foil Cards

boardgame: Uno Ultimate: Superman – Impervious Promo Card
description:
SDCC extended art holofoil Promo card

Character Power Card

Card Text:
Superman
Impervious
When you lose your turn, choose a player to burn 2 cards.

A super-exclusive Custom card made just for your game! You'll get custom cards plus three copies of the card to use in your game or share with friends. Feature your pet, your foe, or yourself! Surprise someone with this special card - it makes a very special cards are only offered through our initial Kickstarter campaign, and never again!

Originally intended to be an expansion for Ninja Sloths allowing 2 extra players, the Sorcerer's Expansion pack was upgraded to a fully playable base game that can be integrated with the original Ninja Sloths base game. This expanded stand-alone expansion includes the Backer's Choice expansion Action Card, and numerous backer suggested cards.

Each player plays a mad scientist, but, of course, there is only room for one. The game is one part Engine Building, Set Collection, and Area Control. It is up to you, as the maddest scientist, to use your laboratory, robots, and trusty assistant Igor to gather the resources and inventions you need in order to build your death ray and incapacitate the others before they incapacitate you.

Players that have been hit will have chances at reviving themselves using various means while continuing to play their turns, only losing the use of their death ray and assistant Igor while they’re down. However, when a player is the last mad scientist left standing, they win the game.

—description from the publisher

A highly challenging version of Komarc's award-winning tummple! This time there's 3 different size bricks (6 different sides), yellow tumps, white tumps and now RED tumps.

In addition, there's a new Brick Shuffling Mobile App with timer and point calculator. Download to your phone and press START! The Shuffler App will indicate what brick to grab and what side to place, or what tump to use... the timer begins automatically... only 3 to 9 seconds to make your move! Quickly make the placement before the time runs out! If you hear the alarm before you're able to place the brick, keep the brick, lose your turn, and tap the X (fail) on the App. If the placement is successful, tap the check mark (success), and points will accumulate automatically... the longer you play the more points you get with each successful placement! If the tower falls at any time, take the fallen bricks.

Deduct any fallen bricks from your point total. Highest points is the winner!

—description from the publisher

Timber! is a nice and smooth building game, the rules of which are so simple that you don't even have to explain them.

Everyone first selects blocks according to a draft system. And then the building begins. Always give a block of the same color or shape as the top one to your left neighbor, who will place it on the tower.

If you manage to pass a block that causes the tower to fall over, you score a drop of sweat. Whoever gets 3 first wins.

The beauty of Timber! is that the challenge is exclusively in building, and everyone really likes that. You probably recognize the hilarity of shaking hands and the (just not) falling over of a tower.

—description from the publisher (translated)

boardgame: Thunderbolt: The Ancient World, Vol. III – 218-208 B.C.
description:
Prior to his passing, Richard H. Berg was hard at work completing the third volume in his The Ancient World series, Thunderbolt, covering the titanic 2nd Punic War between Rome and Carthage. Mark Herman has teamed up with series developer Alan Ray to finish the game.
 Thunderbolt brings The Ancient World system to the mammoth 2nd Punic War. The game covers the action in the Western Mediterranean on updated Italy and Africa maps published in the prior two volumes, plus a connecting full-size map of Spain and western Mauretania, the last map design of Richard’s long and illustrious career.
 The Ancient World system uses a chit pull mechanic to drive the action with the better leaders having more opportunities to act. There are no movement allowances; instead attrition and the ability of a leader to continue to conduct operations govern how forces can move. Battles are straightforward, resolved with a couple of die rolls and an extensive list of modifiers to account for cavalry superiority, elite status, and leadership, as well as numbers of troops. The Battle Results Table features an “Unpredictable Result” which can turn the tables on the best laid battle plan. All the trappings around the decisive moment are there as well: Interception, Avoidance, Coordination, Retreat, Pursuit & Butchery, and after battle effects. Sieges are covered in detail, including rules for attrition, assaults, repair and reduction, treachery, and surrender. And what would a Richard Berg game be without random events—Auguries in this system.

Thunderbolt will feature several smaller scenarios (most playable on a single map) along with the 2nd Punic War grand campaign played on all three maps. The planned scenarios include:

2nd Punic War: The entire 2nd Punic War. Three maps, 16 Game Turns. A wealth of special rules to bring this conflict into sharp focus.
    Thunderbolt: Hannibal's Invasion of Italy. One map, 3 Game Turns (218-216 BCE). A reappraisal of the scenario from Rise of the Roman Republic. An excellent introductory scenario.
    Italian Campaign: Hannibal’s campaign in Italy. One map, 16 Game Turns (218-203 BCE)
    The Spanish Campaign I: The Roman Invasion of Spain. One map, 8 Game Turns (218-211 BCE).
    The Spanish Campaign II: Scipio Strikes Back. One map, 5 Game Turns (209-205 BCE).
    The Island Contest: The Carthaginian Invasions of Sicily and Sardinia. Two maps, 5 Game Turns (215-211 BCE)
    The African Campaign: Scipio Africanus’ Invasion of Africa. One map, 3-4 Game Turns (204-201 BCE)
    Iberia: Carthage’s Conquest of Spain. One map, ~16 Game Turns (237-220 BCE)

—description from the publisher

18ARG is a stock market and railroad building game set in Argentina where the players take the roles of railway investors and company presidents. In these roles, players try to accumulate wealth. Players do this through the ownership of shares of stock in the railroad companies. When a company earns money and pays dividends, the dividends are paid to the players owning shares in the company. As shares increase and decrease in value, so does the wealth of the owning players.

The players' wealth is the sum of their cash on hand, the current value of their public company shares, and the face value of any private companies they own. The object of the game is to have the most wealth at the end of the game.

A trivia game where you have to answer with a number ranging from 0 to 100. Answers can be in length, width, height, temperature, speed, size, age and given in units like centimeters, years, hours, crowns etc. The game differs from other trivia games which are often limited by given answer options.

The game is played in three rounds, consisting of seven questions each. The player with the lowest score at the end of the game is the winner.

There are more than 1000 unique questions.

A trivia game where you have to answer with a number ranging from 0 to 100. Answers can be in length, width, height, temperature, speed, size, age and given in units like centimeters, years, hours, crowns etc. The game differs from other trivia games which are often limited by given answer options.

The game is played in three rounds, consisting of seven questions each. The player with the lowest score at the end of the game is the winner.

There are a total of 1064 unique questions.

A trivia game where you have to answer with a number ranging from 0 to 100. Answers can be in length, width, height, temperature, speed, size, age and given in units like centimeters, years, hours, crowns etc. The game differs from other trivia games which are often limited by given answer options.

The game is played in one round, consisting of seven questions. The player with the lowest score at the end of the game is the winner.

There are a total of 175 unique questions.

A trivia game where you have to answer with a number ranging from 0 to 100. Answers can be in length, width, height, temperature, speed, size, age and given in units like centimeters, years, hours, crowns etc. The game differs from other trivia games which are often limited by given answer options.

The game is played in one round, consisting of seven questions. The player with the lowest score at the end of the game is the winner.

There are a total of 175 unique questions.

They were the world's greatest heroes in the 1980s and 1990s. They might be older and slower now, but they still can kick your a**!

In this storytelling card game, players must use action movie tropes and cliches to build their epic story, taking turns adding scenes and choosing the best resolution for those until the explosive grand finale!

In Peach Brawl, you play as divine beings and mythical creatures invited to the Peach Party by the Queen Mother of the West. Your goal is to snatch high-scoring food and secure victory, but you must at least grab one peach, or you'll be eliminated.

The game revolves around playing cards to obtain food. Each divine being or mythical creature card possesses powerful magic. Knowing when to strategically use this magic is crucial. Can you anticipate others' strategies and make the right moves at the right time?

A card based dungeon crawl with rogue-like rpg elements for 1+ players.  Players pick a character from the melee fighter, tactical scout or magic using sage. Dungeons are randomly created from a map deck so cards are placed to create a sprawling dungeon.  Each map card contains a chance of treasure or monsters, while a fast and simple combat system allows for high-threat encounters with a ton of loot to find as rewards.  Quests and Adventures give objectives and opportunities to each game, but following these are your choice!

What makes this game stand out is that the Dungeons can be left at any (almost) any time so the character returns to town to sell their treasure and prepare for the next adventure - as long as you have the coins to pay for lodging!  A full town phase allows the player to develop a settlement that gives them more opportunities and options in the next dungeon.

Characters develop, gaining skills and new items as they progress up to level 5 and fight the Fire Drake.

—description from the designer

In turn, each player rolls the die and then, depending on the color shown on the die, picks up a tile of that color from the center of the table and places it on his own board.

The first player to complete his board with all 6 balloon tiles wins.

Ants are one of the most amazing insects on the planet! This is evidenced not only by the ability to lift 50 times its body weight or the presence of the largest brain among other insects, but also by a high level of social communication and self-organization. Thanks to this, insects build anthills of extraordinary complexity, strengthen their power over territories and ecosystems, and during wars with other colonies use sophisticated military tricks that real generals would appreciate.

Players are invited to plunge into the interesting world of ant life and take over the management of an ant colony - build an anthill, organize the collection and extraction of resources, provide an army to protect and spread their influence. Victory will be achieved by striking a balance between the economy and the military, cunning, good tactics and unexpected decisions.

A distinctive feature of the game is the maximum of communication and interaction, dynamism and speed of the games. Each new turn the player gets new opportunities and can play a unique combination of moves and cards that radically affect the course of the entire game.

The goal of the game is victory! The game offers 4 different scenarios. Depending on the choice, players have to fulfill the appropriate conditions for victory: building all levels of the anthill faster than others, capturing the enemy's colony or destroying his anthill.

—description from the publisher

description:
FLIP TRICK WRITING is a trick-taking game where a player wins by having the most points. It is a "flip and write" game, where cards are revealed from a standard deck of cards, and the players each draft a card. Drafted cards are either retained for later or consumed to activate a skill (such as the ability to declare a trump suit.)

Then, after each player has selected 13 cards, the players can again consume cards from their hand to activate victory skills  (such as declaring a certain bid.)

Afterwards, the players complete in a must-follow trick-taking game with their remaining cards. The game ends after one hand.

boardgame: A Feeble and Negligent Action: The Battle of Stoney Creek, June 6, 1813
description:
British prospects in Canada were rather gloomy in the Spring of 1813 as American forces began invading that British colony once again, with their main efforts concentrating on wresting control of the Niagara Peninsula away from the Crown. On May 27th British forces near the north end of the Niagara River were forced to retreat into the sanctuary of Fort George by an American force led by Generals Henry Dearborn and William Winder. After successfully withdrawing nearly all of the other outposts along the river following the American invasion of the Niagara Peninsula, Brigadier General John Vincent began planning for ways to strike back at the Americans, whose force was joined by another led by General John Chandler. Local informants, along with a daring night-time reconnoitering by Colonel John Harvey of the American encampment at Stoney Creek near what is today Hamilton, Ontario, disclosed the lackadaisical nature of the American deployment. Although outnumbered by nearly a five-to-one margin, General Vincent ordered a risky night assault, counting upon superior knowledge of the local terrain, surprise, as well as training with his regulars. The battle that resulted was one of the most intense and lop-sided battles fought in Upper Canada.

Can you do as well, or better, than your historical counterparts?

Each copy of A Feeble and Negligent Action is composed of the following:

•    One 11 by 17 inch map with Turn Record Track.
•    100 double-sided counters.
•    8 pages of rules.

Players will also need a six-sided die and a standard deck of playing cards to play the game.

Each hex is approximately 35 yards across, a regular or militia infantry unit is 150-150 men, and artillery units represent 1 cannon or mortar. A turn represents 15 minutes of time. The types of units in the game are Infantry (Regular, Militia), Dragoon Cavalry, Artillery and Leaders.

—description from the publisher

A 2-4 player game in which you work together to build the Roman Fortress at Traiectum (or, the Dutch city of Utrecht as it is known now). Make sure you finish before the end of the fifth round as the Germanic Tribes will attack. And without the Castellum to help with the defense, you will all die.

Roll dice and play matching cards to collect the needed men to build the fortress. Use your wood and stone resources to build up to four different support buildings. Perform additional tasks for the Roman government, or gain favor from the Gods. All these things will get you victory points. And if you succeed together in finishing the Fortress, the player with the most victory points will reign supreme in the fortress.

Na een lange mars komt een groep Romeinse legionairs aan bij de zuidelijke oevers van de Rijn. Ze hebben de opdracht gekregen een fort te bouwen aan de natuurlijke noordelijke grens van het Romeinse keizzerrijk. De Germanen in het noorden hebben een leger bijeen gebracht om de Romeinen te vernietigen. Alleen als Castellum Traiectum af is voor de Germanen aanvallen hebben de legionairs de kans om te overleven.

Lukt het de spelers om Castellum Traiectum te voltooien voor de Germanen aanvallen? Of gaan we allemaal roemloos ten onder!

Naast het bouwen aan Castellum Traiectum zijn er nog meer punten te verdienen in het spel. Doe in het geheim missies, of zet een eigen bouwwerk neer door een vicus, schip, wachttoren of barak te bouwen voor meer hulp. Zorg dat je principia punten verdient en kom verder met de hulp van de Batavieren of zet deze tegen je medespelers in...

boardgame: Tiny Epic Crimes: Crooked Capos Mini Expansion
description:
A new breed of well-funded criminal is loose in the streets—the Capos. These fat cats are villains-for-hire, and it is your job to take them into custody. However, it has come to your attention that corruption has infected your precinct. Several of your more shady comrades have been racking in dirty money to
look the other way. Can you truly trust your fellow officers to meet duty’s call?

Crooked Capos has 2 separate modules that can individually be added to the game: "Crooked" and "Capos."

In the Crooked module, players are dealt Loyalty Cards which state whether they are “CLEAN” or “CROOKED.” Players can
investigate whether another cop is crooked, getting either big rewards (if they are right) or big penalties (if wrong).

In the Capos module, the Capos represent an extra challenge and reward for the officers! Capos are treated like mobsters while in the city, but are not like regular mobsters. Instead, each can only be arrested by following the requirements listed on their card.

Each player get three destinations they must get to. The first player to visit these places wins.

You move by rolling a die and can choose to move forward or backward. There are balloons that you can use to fly to other balloon stations.

A quick and crazy game where you need to get rid of your deck while remaining calm.

To remove one of your cards, you need to be seen and heard as much as possible to find other players that have the same card as you. When two players find matching cards, they may remove that card, but only after celebrating by running round the table while howling, shaking hand and switching places or winning a game of rock paper scissors.

But wait, even if you get rid of all of your card you still need to spin a wheel. If it stops on the wrong spot, you'll end up loosing instead.

The game is set in the fantasy world of The Cursed Empire. As the leader of a Guild of thieves and assassins, you will recruit powerful agents, accumulate gold, hatch subtle plots, and garner influence with both sides of the war that rages in Thargos: Empire and Darkun.

Compete to take down Marks and reap the lucrative rewards, but beware the City Watch of Kurnor, lest your guild members end up in the dungeons! description:
Monkey Mayhem is the wild and wacky card game. In this game, you will draw cards and use their special effects to manipulate the game in your favor. Steal bananas, swap hands, and unleash Monkey Mayhem on your opponents! But be careful, as they'll be doing the same to you!

To win, you need to collect as many as point cards possible. If one of the player's hand or the deck is out, the game over. In your turn, you must draw and play a card as point, then play an action card (optional).

Moon Trek is based on the Kariuchi design. The rules are almost the same as the basic Kariuchi.

Changes to the rules were created to make it for 4-player or group competitions. The changes are as follows.

Players can play individually with 2, 3 or 4 players, or in groups of 2-2, 2-1, 2-1-1, 3-1, etc.
Group pieces can be stacked. There is no limit to the number of pieces that can be stacked. Therefore, players cannot remove their own group pieces from the board and returned to the reserve.
If a player's own piece stops at an enemy piece already on the board, the enemy piece is removed from the board and returned to the enemy's reserve position. If multiple pieces are stacked on the space, all the pieces are returned to their respective reserve positions.
If an enemy piece on the circle is removed, the "Kari" die can be cast again. However, if the throw was a roll of 4 or 5, the throw is not repeated twice. Also, if an enemy piece is removed during a short-cut move in the circle, it cannot be re-thrown.

—description from the publisher

il Gioco della Guerra Simulata is a comprehensive set of tactical wargame rules for the late World War II period (1944-45), written by a retired Italian Army officer.

Also included are rules for minefields, bridge laying, weather and logistics (with fuel, ammo, water and food consumption).

The rules use OO/HO models with a reduction scale of 1:3. The appendix includes detailed orders of battle for German and U.S./Allied units listing the number of men and vehicles needed in the above scale.

The rules may be played by two players but are thought for a larger number of players and with the help of a judge.

—user summary
