boardgame: Defeat the Threat!   THE FIRST GUNSHIP
description:
Defeat the Threat! THE FIRST GUNSHIP is a SOLITAIRE wargame of operational and tactical command.  At the game's core is a patent pending Threat Assembly that simulates an attack by a mysterious alien Threat, making this a truly SOLITAIRE game-playing experience.  The Threat Assembly is tied to varying Intelligence Reports and can be adjusted to varying difficulty levels.  The player commands the maneuver of aircraft, armor, and infantry divisions to repel the Threat attack on the ground and to clear the launch of the First Gunship to move global defense to the next strategic phase.

During each round of play, the player as the Ground Forces Commander orders divisions to maneuver and engage Threat Elements that deploy throughout the game, which represents an operational campaign of 22 months, based on the Threat Assembly.  A Reference Card summarizes all major features of play for quick reference.  The player must achieve the game's objectives, which include successfully defending certain city regions and clearing certain regions of Threat Elements so that the strategic breakthrough of the launch of the First Gunship is achieved.

The game usually lasts 8-9 rounds.  In each round, the Threat deploys based on using the patent pending Threat Assembly.  The player then collects income and purchases new units for deployment at the end of the round.  The player then moves units and conducts Offensive Combat.  The Threat then moves based on the game board configuration, after which Defensive Combat is conducted.  Each round ends with new unit deployment.

The patent pending Threat Assembly is designed to deploy the Threat based on various Intelligence Summary reports that are provided to the player.  The player can thereby control units with advance knowledge of at least some of the Threat's expected movements.  However, the patent pending Threat Assembly also varies the deployment of the Threat so that there is always an element of surprise that varies with each game.  That is, the Threat deployment always varies significantly between games, although the Threat attack proceeds generally based on the provided Intelligence Reports that may change between games.

description from publisher

boardgame: Biathlon Crystal Globe: Piste du Grand-Bornand
description:
Piste du Grand-Bornand provides a new terrain board for a harder challenge (8 new rules for simulating this famous ring).

—user summary

Signez de nouveaux exploits sur cette piste mythique inspirée du stade de biathlon d’Annecy – Le Grand Bornand, l’étape française de Coupe du Monde. Situé en Haute-Savoie, le Grand-Bornand est le rendez-vous incontournable où bat le cœur de tous les amateurs français de biathlon. Seuls les plus grands biathlètes s’y sont imposés. Face aux difficultés techniques proposées par ce circuit exigeant de la station des Aravis, les biathlètes pourront compter sur les encouragements de plus de 60 000 spectateurs. Il leur faudra grimper l’impressionnante montée tant redoutée, résister aux faux-plats et avaler les bosses avant de rejoindre le pas de tir. C’est là que la victoire choisira son Champion au rythme des acclamations du public de la grande tribune. Découvrez les 8 nouvelles règles de jeu qui viennent pimenter ce circuit : passage des bosses, ascension du mur, cardio au tir, finish au sprint… Pour des parties encore plus intenses !

—description from the publisher

Krapules is an ambiance game where you fight with your gang (2 to 3 people) to harvest the best trashes in the collective dumpster on the table. Try to get food from the same family and with the most flies, and pass your inedible trashes to the opposite gang.

You can play as Birds of anarchy, Rocket Racoon, Kitty Blinders or Bloody Rats gang. Each gang is composed with 3 characters: a petty thief, a collector and a scout ; they have different distribution of actions, in those: dig (return/move a trash in the dumpster), collect (take a trash, visible or not, in the dumpster), give a trash to a player, steal a trash from a player.

Trashes are cards with trash bags drawn on the back, they are all spread hidden in an unorganized mountain of garbage, and you can only interact with the one where the bags are not covered by other cards. So you may dig a card and move it on top of a nice trash to block opponents from taking it.

Every character comes with 4 cards numbered between 1 and 12, with two different sets of actions. At every round, each player get to choose one of them and put it hidden on the table. Then the boss (the player with most trashes in their hand) get to call every number in ascending order, then players called (starting from the left of the boss, if many) choose one of the two sets of actions to play.

At the end of every round, when everybody played, teammates can exchange up to 3 trashes (to assemble families, or muddy the waters), then the boss and the rookie (the player with the least trashes) are defined for the next round.

At every moment, players can communicate freely and openly, but might feel the need to get some common codes to avoid disclosing strategic information to the opponents gangs. The two forbidden actions are: showing your cards, and whisper to a player.

At the end of the 4 rounds, players reveal their collected trashes and count their points individually (number of flies, plus 2 for each item in a family, starting from the second) then sum them up to have team score.

boardgame: Fallout: Wasteland Warfare – Mojave Companions: ED-E, Rex & Veronica
description:
ED-E was battle damaged, but a few repairs make it fighting fit and ready for action once more (albeit with new priorities).

Rex is a cyberhound with a past in law enforcement and Caesar’s Legion. He is still a powerful fighter and firm friend.

Journeyman Scribe Veronica comes from an offshoot of the Brotherhood of Steel. Equipped with a custom power fist, she is a strong ally for those who can earn her trust.

boardgame: Fill in the Game: Origins 2023 Promo

Both portions have an Origins Game Fair logo.

Origin Story has you monologue about your villain origin story for one minute, whereas O-H... has other players respond to that phrase as directed by the active player.

GUILD booster pack: Not only does this pack feature 5 new Request cards and 1 new Decree (all give new scoring conditions), but it also features 7 Guild Favor cards (a new card type!). Guild Favor cards can be added to any game of ICE for some extra variety and strategic gameplay. Players choose a new Guild Favor card each new Day (round), and during their turn they can turn the card over to use that card’s special action. These can have a special synergy (when using a Favor card from the same Guild you’re playing), or allow for special combos when playing Favors of other Guilds.


EDIFICE booster pack ** As you know, Edifices are the final layer of tiles in the game, and when you discover one it offers a special variant for your next game. But there was no easy way to track this before. Now with the Edifice deck, you have three useful ways to use these cards:

One new EDIFICE tile.

The Great Mill is a mini expansion for Dorfromantik: The Board Game and contains two cards and a mill standee. Unlocking the Mill achievement lets you score additional points for Grain Tasks and Yellow Flags by cleverly placing the mill piece.

Special Detective is a game in which the police attempt to capture the bank robber before he can escape from the city.
The red car is the bandit's car; the remaining cars which are blue, green and yellow are police cars.
The bandit has first turn to play and starts at space "Bandit Starts Here." Once his car enters a street he must go forward to the next intersection unless he lands on a space indicating otherwise. Upon reaching an intersection, he may turn in either direction.
The police decide among themselves the order in which they will move; they will start separately from the spaces indicated on the board. Once a police car enters a street, it must continue to go in a forward direction until reaching the next intersection. Each police car moves in turn before the bandit's car moves again.
If a police car lands on the same space already occupied by the bandit's car, the bandit is considered captured and the game is over. If the bandit lands on the same space already occupied by a police car, then the police car is out of the game. If any player's car lands on a marker telling him to go back to another street or location, he immediately goes back as instructed; then on his next turn he has the option of starting again in any direction from that space.
The bandit must land on the space marked "FREE" by exact count to win the game. If he is six spaces from the "FREE" space, and throws seven, he is taken one space past and the chase continues.

—user summary

White Noise is a card game of survival and adventure in deep space. You are a space traveler who is lost and must find your way home. But the journey is full of dangers and mysteries. You must use your wits, skills and luck to overcome the challenges and obstacles that await you.

The game is played with a deck of 100 cards, each featuring a Jump number, a Challenge number, location flavor text and an event. Card number 1 is placed face up in the play area, and the rest of the cards are shuffled and divided into two piles. You choose one pile as your playing deck (the other pile is not used this game).

You also choose a character card and place it in front of you. Each character has four statistics: Health, Reputation, Energy and Intelligence. You have 6 character points to distribute among these statistics as you wish.

On your turn, you draw the top card from the playing deck and place it next to the previous card in the play area. You must guess whether the Jump number on the new card is higher or lower than the Jump number on the previous card. If you guess correctly, you follow the event on the new card. If you guess wrong, you lose 1 Health and must face the Challenge on the new card. To pass the Challenge, you must roll 2 dice and add the value of one of your statistics to your (gold) dice and add the challenge number to the event (white die). If the result is higher than the Challenge number, you succeed. If not, you lose 1 health.

The game ends when one of the following happens:

You find the Home card in the playing deck. You win!
You run out of Health. You die!

White Noise is a game of suspense, strategy and chance. Can you make it home alive?

—description from the publisher

The object of this game, as in a stock car race, is to be the first player to complete five laps around the track. 
To begin, each player selects a car and lap markers of matching color. The lap markers are put in the center of the board where indicated. Each player then throws the dice. The one with the highest throw puts his car in starting position on the inside lane (lane nearest pit). The player with the next high number puts his car in the next lane, etc., until all cars are in position at the starting line.
First player again throws the dice and moves his car the number of spaces indicated. Each time a player completes a lap, he takes one of his colored markers from the board. Players may, if they wish, move their cars from one lane to another; however, a move to another lane must be made from adjacent squares of the same color. For instance, a player may move his car from a red square in his lane to an adjacent red square in the next lane.
When a player's car stops on one of the spaces indicating mishaps, he must do what the space instructs immediately. 
One car may not pass another car in the same line. All passing must be done by changing lanes as explained above. If a player throws a number that allows him to move his car past a car ahead, but it is impossible to do so by changing lanes, the cars are said to collide; they are out of the race. 
A lap is completed only when a car has crossed the finish line; then a player may take one of his lap markers from the board, indicating he has completed a lap. The first player to complete five laps is the winner.

—user summary

The Golden Oaks, led by Rocco & Bessie, have crossed the border from Canada to bring their own whisky to Chicago. Don't be fooled by their appearance: they won't hesitate to stain their elegant suits with blood to control the city.

—description from the publisher

Red Dragons Tong adds a new gang to the fight for Chicago. Led by Charlie Gong, the opium kings will use their strong distribution networks to take control of the streets. If you lose sight of them for even a second, it will already be too late.

—description from the publisher

KeyForge: 2 Player Starter Set gives players all that they need to start learning KeyForge, then discover what makes the game special.

More specifically, this box contains two pre-constructed decks, tokens, and a learn-to-play booklet that guides you through a game session turn by turn. It also includes two decks from the latest KeyForge set: Winds of Exchange. Each deck is uniquely generated from a fixed pool of cards, and no two decks are the same, so once you know the rules, you can open these sets and discover something unique to you.

In more detail, KeyForge is played over a series of turns in which you, as the Archon leading your company, use the creatures, technology, artifacts, and skills of a chosen House to reap precious Æmber, hold off your enemy's forces, and forge enough keys to unlock the Crucible's Vaults. You begin your turn by declaring one of the three Houses within your deck, and for the remainder of the turn you may play and use cards only from that House. For example, if you take on the role of the Archon Radiant Argus the Supreme, you will find cards from Logos, Sanctum, and Untamed in your deck, but if you declare "Sanctum" at the start of your turn, you may use actions, artifacts, creatures, and upgrades only from Sanctum. Your allies from Logos and Untamed must wait.

Next, you must strive to gain the advantage with a series of tactical decisions, leveraging both the cards in your hand and those in play to race ahead of your opponent. If you wish to weaken your rival's forces, you may send out your allies to fight enemies on the opposing side, matching strength against strength. Otherwise, you may choose to use your followers to reap, adding more Æmber to your pool.

Notably, no card in KeyForge has a cost — choosing a House at the start of a turn allows you to play and use any number of cards from that House for free, leading turns to fly by with a wave of activity! Yet balance is key. If you simply reap more Æmber at every opportunity, your rival may quickly grow their team of minions and destroy yours, outpacing your collection and leaving your field barren. But if you focus on the thrill of the fight alone and neglect the collection of Æmber, you won't move any closer to your goal! If you succeed in finding a harmony within your team and have six Æmber at the start of your turn, you'll forge a key and move one step closer to victory. The first to forge three keys wins!

Shakespeare’s First Folio has players taking the role of printers in the early Seventeenth Century competing to print the first folio of Shakespeare’s plays.

Players will utilize a combination of trick-taking and worker placement to collect as many plays as possible.  The plays are suited - Histories, Comedies and Tragedies.  There are also historical patrons and personages who will help the players out.

A game ends immediately when the last Play is taken by a player. The most points wins and will print Shakespeare's First Folio!

Welcome to Gearwrights, the innovative and immersive board game that puts you in the shoes of a budding inventor in a steampunk-inspired world. With resource management, engine building, and a unique pachinko dice tower featuring custom d6 dice, Gearwrights offers a rich and engaging gameplay experience that will have you striving to become the greatest inventor of all time.

In Gearwrights, you'll start with a dream of creating the next great invention. As you work to build your engine and manage your resources, you'll have the opportunity to design and build a wide range of gadgets, contraptions, and gearwrights, from simple machines to complex inventions that will change the world.

But the competition is fierce, and you'll need to work quickly and efficiently to beat out your rivals and claim the title of the Greatest Inventor of All Time. By carefully managing your resources and utilizing your engine to its fullest potential, you'll be able to create more and more elaborate inventions, earning prestige and acclaim along the way.

And with a one-of-a-kind pachinko dice tower featuring custom d6 dice, every roll of the dice in Gearwrights is a thrilling and unpredictable event. You'll never know what fate has in store for you, but with strategic planning and a bit of luck, you'll be able to overcome any obstacle and achieve your goals.

So if you've ever dreamed of being a great inventor, or if you just love the unique dice towers, Gearwrights is the game for you. With a deep and engaging gameplay experience, endless possibilities for invention and discovery, and a rich and detailed game, Gearwrights is sure to become a favorite among board game enthusiasts and budding inventors alike.

—description from the publisher

The U.S. Postal Inspection Service is the oldest federal law enforcement agency in America – and they've seen it all. They've uncovered letter bombs, recovered millions in stolen money and taken down an entire mafia ring. Now, it's time for you to unravel some of their most high-profile cases... and crack them yourself!


     Take on a mafia kingpin in SOCIETY OF THE BLACK HAND (1/5 Difficulty)
     Bring down a corrupt art gallery in OPERATION BOGART (3/5 Difficulty)
     Unmask a criminal mastermind in DIGGING FOR GOLD IN PARADISE (4/5 Difficulty)


NEW MEETS OLD
This adult party game may feature America’s oldest law enforcement agency, but using phones, email and the internet makes it a strategy game for everyone too!

(Internet Connection Required)

–description from publisher
family: Theme: Mail / Stamps / The Post Office

description:
A world summit is approaching and you have been selected as one of the main chefs in charge of the banquet. You and your fellow chefs will travel to the summit together aboard a diplomatic ship. On your way there you will go through a Riviera famous for its exotic flora and fauna. This will be the place where the ingredients for the banquet will be collected. While the ship cruises the Riviera you will plan the banquet menu as well as collect the necessary ingredients to prepare it.

Riviera is a dice drafting game where the dice can be used either for their color or their number. You use the color to set up end-game scoring opportunities, in the form of recipes to be fulfilled, and the number to collect ingredients from the Riviera to fulfill such recipes.

In each round of the game the ship will move along the Riviera and new dice will be rolled. Each player will draft one of them and will use it for one of the available actions. Such actions include:


     Using the number to move on land to collect ingredients. The movement will start from the current location of the ship. During this action, you may use the special powers of the ship's crew to assist you. 
     Using the color to add a dish to the banquet menu. You score points at the end go the game if you have the ingredients to prepare the planned dish. 
     Using the number to fish for seafood ingredients. This is done by drawing tiles from a bag. 


—description from the designer

Zauberhafte Würfelorte is a wooden family game for 2-5 players, ages 8+, made in the Urdonautal in the heart of Bavaria. In a magic contest, you have to roll the dice for the ingredients for all the required potions. Whoever brews the best potions will win the game.

The player whose turn it is takes the 5 colored dice and rolls them up to four times. He may put any dice aside to brew a potion with as many ingredients as possible of one color or a given combination. An extra die, extra rolls or the restart of the turn can be conjured up with spellpower. Spellpower is gained when it takes less than 4 throws to brew a potion. The fewer throws you need, the more spellpower you get. You can also get spellpower in any player's turn if the player rolls at least 3 of the same ingredients in his roll and you are the first to notice this.

Once a player has brewed each of the required potions, the game is over. For each potion that is not brewed, the player gets minus points. The more ingredients a player has used to brew his own potions, the more points he gets.

—description from the designer

When the World is literally going to Hell in a recovered shopping cart, working together as a team is incredibly important...and getting _everyone else_ to work as a team on your behalf is even more so!

Over 13 rounds on the night of Friday the 13th of an unspecified month in an unspecified year, you - alone - will attempt to shepherd, sacrifice and/or destroy, both zombies and survivors alike in order to survive until help arrives in the morning.

Each turn, you will draw 3 cards and postpone one of them (put it to the bottom of the deck) and then must put the others into play. Various effects can be triggered - resolved in the order of your choosing - which might result in gains and/or losses before you compare your Survivor Total against the current Zombie Threat Level: if you are being overwhelmed by this threat, your community will suffer!

Simply make it through the 13 turns of the game - see the Sun rise on a new day - and you will win...

Note: Dies Irae is the third game in Tony Boydell's "Infinity" solo game trilogy (along with Lux Aeterna and Aleph Null)

—description from the designer

boardgame: Die lustige S u. F Reise
description:
The players take turns to roll and move. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. The players start in Offenbach at space 1. The first player to reach the central office of Schade & Füllgrabe in Frankfurt a. M. at space 120 wins the game.

The game was published for the 50th anniversary of Schade & Füllgrabe.

—user summary

Everyone's an Expert is a fun card game with a serious touch where 3-6 players try to come up with creative (and most likely ridiculous) solutions to different global problems.

However, there is a catch. They must base their solutions on two completely random keywords found on their solution cards.

Each round one player acts as an investor, investing money in the ideas they like the best. The player with the highest total investment when the game ends wins.

—description from the publisher

The players take turns to roll and move. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. The first to reach space "Ziel" wins the game.

The game was published to promote the encyclopedia "Grzimeks Tierleben".

—user summary
Promo deck for 2nd edtion of Hands. More gestures, more fun!

boardgame: On Bloody Ground: The Norman Conquest
description:
On Bloody Gound - The Norman Conquest is a 64 page, perfect bound rulebook, with common sense core rules, including brutal combat and a system of orders that makes the game dynamic, exciting, fun and challenging.

The rules are designed for use with individually based 28mm miniatures, but can easily be adapted for use with any scale or basing.

This first book in the series covers the Dark Ages in Britain between the 8th and the 11th centuries and includes army lists for Saxons, Vikings, Normans, Irish, Scottish and Welsh.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players
Series: On Bloody Ground (WiP Games and Miniatures)

description:
Find the Fun: The Game is a fast-playing, simple to learn card game for one player about making a game good enough to pitch to a publisher.

It focuses more on fun than education but offers up lots of game design nuggets of wisdom during play.

The box and cards can also be repurposed and used to create a prototype of your own creation.

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Trun Outcast Hero Pack
description:
The Trun Outcast is a special Hero Class that we put together for the Kickstarter to let Brimstone veterans and new players alike dive even deeper into the crazy world of Shadows of Brimstone!

Part of a brutish warrior race of reptilian soldiers, the Trun tower over humans, standing around 10' tall. With rippling muscles and a thick scaled hide, the Trun are the ultimate hunters in the galaxy, using brutal weapons and wearing heavy armor plating. They seek out deadly prey to test their skills, living and fighting in tribes of armored warriors.

Sometimes, when a member of a Trun hunting pack becomes disgraced or fails in a critical hunt, they are banished from the tribe, an outcast to their people. These warriors often seek out a glorious death in combat or travel great distances to stalk the deadliest prey alive. But some... some choose to take on a new life. A life of adventure...danger...the life of a Hero! Or maybe just a mercenary. If the pay is high and the chance of glorious death is higher, a Trun Outcast can rarely say no.

This new Hero Pack lets players take on the role of a Trun Outcast; an alien brute from another world, able to pick up and throw smaller Enemies around, smash through combat with a meaty fist, and blast his way through the toughest opponents using his alien weapons and martial prowess.  He can even learn from his prey, getting better at hunting and fighting them over time, developing new tactics and techniques to bring ruin on his enemies! The Trun are not subtle... but when you play a Trun, you're not really looking for subtle. You're looking for a battle monster that can get the job done, or die gloriously trying in an epic way!

—description from the publisher

Backlash! is an expansion kit for The Lash of the Turk, The Lash of the Turk (ATO #30).

Backlash! covers the multi-year campaign for the crown of Hungary which lasted from 1684 until 1699 (sometimes known as The Great Turkish War).  The fighting concluded with most of Hungary, Croatia, Slovakia and Serbia becoming part of the Holy Roman Empire.  Now the Imperial player (the Holy Roman Empire) can turn the tables somewhat and be the aggressor against a reeling Ottoman Empire!

Backlash! is a bonus game published in 2023 in the ATO (Against the Odds) Annual 2020.
family: History: Classical Age of the Ottoman Empire

boardgame: Boss Monster: Birthday Party Promo Card
description:
A promo card in celebration of Boss Monster's 10th Anniversary.  This promo card will be given away at Gen Con 2023 as well as at Brotherwise Games' featured retailers.

This card is a Monster Room, with the following ability: This Room has +3 damage if your Boss has Leveled up.

Achtung Jabo! is a solitaire wargame about the 371st Fighter Group, which flew ground attack missions on D-Day in the P-47D Thunderbolts (known for its famous "Razorback"). Gameplay depicts the Group's missions on D-Day, June 6, 1944, and the book includes extensive research into the techniques, history, and practices of Allied fighter-bomber units at the time of D-Day.

All Game Maps are historically accurate paintings -- 14 in all -- created meticulously with reference to photo reconnaissance images from the time, featuring all of the targets assigned that day. The game focuses on replayability and targets will vary each time you play. Your pilots should expect to encounter German Tiger tanks, Panthers, "Hetzers", armored half-tracks, Opel Blitz trucks, artillery pieces, and Wehrmacht infantry. You will fly along railroad routes looking for locomotives and rail cars. You will bomb bridges and target gun emplacements, "tobruks" and pillboxes. Beware, the Germans will respond in force with their famous 88 flak guns and rapid-firing quad-20 mm guns for close-in defense. You will be armed with bombs and a full load of bullets for each Thunderbolt's eight .50 cal machine guns.

Just as in history, your Fighter Group's three squadrons will rove across the Normandy countryside in the areas located behind UTAH and OMAHA Beaches. Your overall mission is to prevent the Germans from reinforcing their forces arrayed in defense against the Allied landings, but you never know what targets will present themselves as you fly your assigned patrols. Your mission orders are clear -- and they are exactly the same orders as the 371st Fighter Group actually received that day.

Victory requires you to complete ALL of your assigned missions and hopefully not lose too many of your planes and pilots to enemy fire. It's time to paint D-Day invasion stripes on your planes and get into the air!

—description from the publisher
family: Series: Air War Book Games (Historic Wings)

description:
Simply include the Cosplayer cards in the Level A pile and the Food Truck cards in the Level C pile.

Cosplayer
In flag possession: Lose the flag against Level A cards and Newcomer.

Food Truck
From the bench: Your cards with base power 4 have +2 during the attack.

Supersonic Jet is a two-player card game set in a new golden age of air travel.

You and your opponent lead rival airlines seeking to expand across the globe. You compete to control the most airports and carry the most passengers. In this zero-sum game, remember to pursue frequent fliers at every opportunity. These high-value travelers can unlock decisive strategic advantages, including the ultimate prize — the supersonic jet.

—description from the publisher

The Marrakesh: Camels & Nomads expansion adds new content to Marrakesh, including the camels and nomads modules; new oasis, luxury, and provision tiles, which offer more options during play; and a solo gameplay mode.

By August 1905 the Russo-Japanese War had been raging for 18 months, with over a million soldiers, sailors, and engineers in arms,  it had cost the lives of between 100,000 to 200,000 participants and brought death and destruction to civilians. The war had seen the sinking of the Russian Pacific and Baltic fleets, and the great sieges at Port Arthur and Mukden. The world was shaken by the emphatic rise of Imperial Japan as a land and naval power. Russia had been blooded and humiliated.

But Russia was not yet done. Although both sides were financially exhausted it was Russia who continued to funnel troops into the war, while Japan’s manpower reserves were depleted. The war might yet continue, with further loss of life and property, and protracted instability in the Far East. With this would come the risk of further Russian humiliation. Both sides needed a way out. The door to that way out was opened by US President Theodore Roosevelt when he invited delegations of both sides to Portsmouth, New Hampshire - to a peace conference through August 1905. But with the sides so far apart on numerous key issues - the Russian Czar had stipulated no territorial concessions or reparation payments, and the Japanese Emperor wanted exactly those things and much more - what real hope was there that both sides could find a mutually satisfactory resolution?

In history, the answer is found in the Treaty of Portsmouth, a complex and significant diplomatic achievement, for which, for his part in facilitating and assisting, President Roosevelt was the first American to receive the Nobel Peace Prize. In Peace 1905, you - the players - will have Roosevelt’s assistance, but the work of successfully negotiating is down to you. Will you end the war, and bring a peace your governments approve of, or will the conference end in failure, and the war ignite again?

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Post-Impact Earth does not suffer many to live on the surface:  only the mutants can survive in the dense, orange haze that seeps and spreads from Ground Zero, in Antarctica.  Whether the meteor brought the mysterious hot catalyst with it or simply thawed it is unclear and unimportant.  The world's remaining superpowers wage war over the rights to Antarctican airspace and the freedom to purchase this ore from the mutant miners below.

You are the mutant miners.  You dig, you sell, you dig... while you collect and repair the bullet-ridden wreckage that falls from above the haze.  After all, you don't plan to stay down here forever.

Heirs to the Air is a resource-management game in which every load of ore that you sell to the first-world factions overhead puts you one step closer to building your own dirigible fleets so you can fight for control of the 12 territories.  On the other hand, every load of ore that you sell makes one of the first-world factions stronger.

You will race against your fellow mining factions to assemble an air force, time your ascent, and then try to hang tough.  When the final first-world faction is eliminated, the game ends, but beware:  you can all lose if a first-world faction runs amok and establishes dominance over Antarctica.

—description from the designer

boardgame: What Do You Meme? Bigger Better Edition
description:
They say it's impossible to improve on perfection.... Couldn't be us. Introducing the Bigger Better edition. Packed into a sexy new black box and featuring over ONE HUNDRED brand-new cards in addition to all your classic faves, this version of What Do You Meme? is about to electrify your next game night. Gather 'round your funniest friends (or your family, if you think they can hang) and take turns matching outrageously funny Caption Cards and Photo Cards to make the most iconic pairing. We asked someone good at math, and they told us there are literally countless combinations to be made with this new edition.

Adults Only: This game contains adult content and was created for ages 17+. There are some things you just can’t unhear. Trust us on this one.

Note: This edition is the 2016 version of What Do You Meme? + 100 new cards.

—description from the publisher

In Stargather, you will race to create stars as you collect colorful stardust and form nebulas full of marvelous discoveries.

On your turn, you will draw 3 tokens from the Galaxy bag. Collecting stardust will grow your nebulas. Discoveries will help your nebulas become stars faster, like the Comet that lets you draw an extra token on your turn. When the nebula is big enough, it will "ignite" and become a star.
Each star has a special one-time effect. If the color of the star matches the color of your nebula, it will have a more powerful "brighter" effect.
Stars' effects can cause big chain reactions, letting you ignite several nebulas on your turn.
Collect 7 stars to win the game.

—description from the designer

In this unique game, the boundaries between reality and fantasy blur. Thanks to the unique combination of game materials and augmented reality (AR), you can transform any place of your choice into the magical world of wizards and create an incredible gaming experience.

Discover magical artifacts, hidden treasures and secret messages with your fellow players. You will only be able to solve the 16 main puzzles and break the ancient curse by working as a team.

Reign Absolute: Umbrous Edition is the compact, streamlined version of the Ultimate Rapid Strategic Fantasy. Imagined as the love-child of fantasy tabeltop miniatures games and "explosive" party games, Reign Absolute: Umbrous Edition is about bringing the fun and excitement of competitive fantasy-themed conquest to an audience that would otherwise be put off by a grueling set of rules and complicated setup requirements.

The game is a grid based conquest game where each player has an unique set of Faction units, and the objective is to locate and defeat the opponent's Faction unit, at which point that player is eradicated from the game.

Players take alternating turns playing an Action followed by drawing a Reinforcement Card. Reinforcment Cards provide varying tactical benefits and supplement individual unit's combat strength. Aside from random draw of units during setup and the drawing of the Reinforcement Cards.

—description from the publisher

Prigozhin's March of Justice is a solitaire wargame from Conflict Simulations LLC covering the attempted Wagner coup of Russia.

The player controls the Wagner forces as they attempt to move assets around, outmaneuver Russian forces and attempt to get units to switch sides, the AI controls the Russian side. The game is developed to be extremely difficult.

The game takes about 15-20 minutes to play and can be played multiple times in a row fairly easily. A two player option allows another player to take over the Russian side.

—description from the publisher
A mini-expansion for Sea Salt & Paper, presented as a booster pack: 8 new cards with 5 new effects.

1883. The French have decided to crush the renegade Chinese Black Flag army. The Eastern forces have taken control of Tonkin and the illustrious Red River. The Black Flag army, along with its Vietnamese allies, have been illusive and put up a fierce resistance. A plan to capture the fortress of Son Tay is put into place in the hopes of a quick end to the war. The shadowy army has fortified Son Tay and is confident that the French will be repulsed. The stage is set...

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players
Series: Battle Series of World Battles (Historical Game Company)

description:
In Energize Duel, every player embodies a superhero and engages in thrilling battles to determine the mightiest, swiftest, or most clever hero of all.

As they traverse the room, players challenge one another through the use of Duel cards.

By triumphing in quick duels, like rock-paper-scissors, thumb wrestling, or trying to evoke laughter from the opponent, players claim their opponents cards.

Ultimately, the player who claims the greatest number of cards emerges victorious.

Energize Duel can be played in workshops, as a team building exercise or with friends/family.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Converge: Aspects of Vision – Solo Mode
description:
The mercurial Creators are masters of light and sound, improvising entertaining displays to break through the gloom. Can you use these unpredictable artists to achieve your goals?

This 6-card solo expansion for Converge works with any combination of factions. With only 5 turns to play, it’s a race to achieve goals and earn stars before time runs out! On every turn, one of the opponent’s cards will be revealed, serving as both one of their faction members and a shared goal. Play 2 cards each turn to counter their steady advance to victory.

Each of the solo cards features an ability on one side and a goal on the other. At the start of the game, pair 3 ability cards with 3 face-up goals. You may use the ability at any time as one of your actions, or complete the paired goal to gain the ability to use as an additional action when you complete the paired goal, giving you an extra chance to turn the tides in your favor. By managing the artifact and shrewdly using your abilities, you might just save the day.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Heroes of Barcadia: Con Crud Promo Tile
description:
A promo monster tile available at all conventions that Rollacrit attends.

This is a level 5 monster that causes 5 HP of damage if not defeated. If anyone leaves the room, this monster flips back over and can be fought again. Don't be the one to bring Con Crud back to the group!

Players separate the cards into 2 piles, 'Categories' and 'Letters'. One player (anyone) flips over a category and reads it to everyone. Then she flips over a letter card. The first person to call out something that fits the category and starts with the revealed letter takes the letter, and that counts as one point. 2nd letter is then revealed (for the same category), and again, the first person to call out something that starts with the letter and fits the category takes the letter as a point. Then a new category is revealed, and play continues, playing 2 letters for each (1) category. Play continues until all 52 letter cards are gone. The player that has collected the most letters at the end, wins!

Sometimes, a letter comes up with a category with a category and no one can think of anything. Anyone, at any time, can call "Katootsie!" and start the Katootsie! timer app (a free app you can download for your phone). This starts a 10 second timer. If no one thinks of an answer before the time runs out, the person that called Katootsie! gets the letter. But if someone does think of an answer before the timer runs out, that person wins the letter and also steals one letter from the person that called Katootsie!
Other rules: Q, X and Z can appear anywhere in the word (not just at the beginning), and it's shown on the card for clarity.
If there is a tie, ONLY the people that tied pull a 2nd letter and compete for that, and the winner takes both letters. 
Proper nouns are generally allowed unless otherwise indicated.

—description from the designer

The robot city of Gearland is under attack! The evil semi-truck ROLLAR is damaging the city and tormenting the wonderful robot citizens.

Players will take control of a superhero train and help save the city by delivering gears and passengers to specific locations, upgrading their train, solving emergencies, completing missions and battling Rollar. Once Rollar is defeated the Super Train with the most stars at the end of the game is the winner!

From the designer of the critically-acclaimed The Quest Kids comes a fast and fun pick up and deliver train game for young conductors 7 years old and up.

Super Trains features giant colorful train miniatures that players will move around the colorful city of Gearland. The main resource in the game are the magical gears, which are generated every turn by the rolling of the Gear Die. What gears a player receives is based on how they have upgraded and organized their unique train player board.

But watch out, because the Rollar Die is rolled every turn as well, which determines where an emergency will appear on the board.

—description from the publisher

Death Path: Solitary Assignment is a solo/one-player card & dice game for ages 12+.

The Death Path takes the violence and brutality of a gladiatorial fighting pit combined with a challenging and complex labyrinth.

Citizens watch the action unfold live on their holo-terminals while chosen Subjects enter the Death Path ready to battle for their survival. Within the Death Path, Subjects will also encounter Pathfinders: Government-controlled robots whose sole mission is to challenge Subjects by rigorously testing their combat skills — and to provide even more brutal entertainment for the revelers viewing at home.

During their journey through the Death Path, Subjects are presented with many options within the maze representing multiple routes. The correct path of survival could be a Hidden Path that leads directly to the end of the maze. The incorrect path may just turn out to be a Dead End with no options but to retrace one’s steps.

A Subject’s prize for winning the Death Path? The Subject gets to live and enjoy all of the fame, glamour, and riches of a celebrity, while the Government logs another elite soldier for their impending war for control of the world.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Boxtop Pinball: Welcome to Las Vegas
description:
Family-friendly dexterity game about flicking dice down a cascading boxes of a Vegas themed pinball machine.

Players will flick dice over the course of 3 rounds aiming for bonus zones multiplying their pip totals by the area they land on.   You can also try and knock opponents out of bonus zones on subsequent dice flicks but bumping them off the zone they are in.

Bonus games include collecting the best blackjack hands by bumping into cards, betting on the outcome of roulette spins and more.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Keeper of the Sacred Flame Hero Pack
description:
As part of an ancient and secret order of Egyptian warriors, the Keepers of the Sacred Flame have taken an oath that has passed down for generations. It is their sworn duty to prevent the ancient enemies of mankind from returning to power and casting the world back into Darkness.

With whispers and rumor of the fabled Lost Tombs being unearthed, and the cursed Dark Stone rediscovered within, the Keepers have begun to gather their forces, sending agents to stop those that would seek out the relics and rituals needed to resurrect the corrupted sorcerer Khamuntet by the followers of his fanatical cult. Or perhaps their greatest fear, that the long-dormant gates to the Sunken City have been found...and opened, reawakening the ancient and terrible Kingdom of Pharrox.

This new Hero Pack contains everything you need to play as a Keeper of the Sacred Flame. Trained from birth as saboteurs, warriors, and assassins, the Keepers of the Sacred Flame have been standing against the Darkness for millennia... and they are ready to fight!

Deep in the forests at the heart of Mysterra, a once sparkling lake has deteriorated into a swampy bog. Many years ago, an evil witch stole 5 magic gems from the Queen, only to lose them in the lake’s depths as she made her escape. Over time, the witch’s magic has seeped into the waters, contaminating them with her evil. Gnomes, long known as the Keepers of the Forest, seek to return the waters to their former glory. To do so, they must enlist the help of the fairies, building mushroom huts for them to live in. In return, the fairies will use their magic to find the gems and save the lake, restoring beauty to the forest once again. Will you be able to eliminate the quagmire and earn the Queen’s favor?

The Quagmires of Mysterra is a 1-4 player game of shifting strategies and sinking maps, involving dice, lily pads, and mushrooms! Puzzle your way through an ever-changing swamp as you beat your opponents to attract the most fairies. Only with their magic can you collect the sacred gems and restore the lake to its former beauty, earning favor with the Queen at last.

On a player's turn, they must match their available Wishing Dice with an action card in their hand. Once a card has been used, it is no longer available to play (until all discarded cards are returned to their hand via the Rest action). Then, the player re-rolls their dice to prepare for their next turn.

Actions include: 

     Exploring the map
     Collecting resources
     Building a mushroom fairy hut
     Buying tools and upgrades from the market to build their engine and enhance future turns
     Applying bonus fairy magic
     Mirroring another player's top card
     Resting to reset your hand


Points are scored for various accomplishments during the game. When the final gem has been found, all players finish the current round and end-game scoring begins. Whoever has the most points wins!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Holistic Review: A Game of Choosing Winners
description:
Holistic Review is a gateway+ weight auction game in which players control universities recruiting prospective students. They will use multi-use cards to build an engine, auction for students with their administrators, and try to fill the most prestigious majors to capacity. After five auctions, the most prestigious university wins!

Gameplay:

Reach round, players choose two strategic plan cards to play to their player board. These are multi-use cards that can each perform four functions. Then, players use their administrators in an auction for students. Players must divide their limited available efforts between reaching out to more students and putting more effort into recruiting them. The students will choose the university that put the most effort into recruiting them!

After five waves of students have applied, players score prestige from their completed majors, completed private objectives, their ability to teach well, and subtract score for any students they were unable to place.  The most prestigious university is the winner!

—description from the designer

Obey the Clay sees two teams use their clay to complete Aardman-inspired challenges as fast as they can. As soon as they’re done, they flip the Time-o-Matic to begin their rival’s turn to complete a task. The faster you go, the more pressure the other team is under--for if you run out of time, the other team wins the round.

Picture This! consists of 50 cards with close up photographs of common objects on the front and the name and photograph of the object on the back. One player displays the front of one card to the other players and they have one minute to guess the object. The first player to identify the object from the close up image gets the card. If no one can identify the object the card is placed back in the deck. The player to the left takes the deck and displays the front of the next card. The first player to get 5 cards is the winner.

—user summary

The faerie land of Tír na nÓg is split into two realms: the Realm of Eternal Spring, where the Seelie fae are governed by the ethereal Queen Titania, and the primeval Forest Arden, where the King of Autumn, Lord Oberon, rules over their Unseelie kindred. Oberon and Titania have decided to end their historic rivalry and join their kingdoms by getting married. They have each invited delegations from among their various vassal faeries to come and celebrate the royal wedding at the grandest feast ever seen in a wooded glade.

The players take on the roles of faerie nobility who stand to lose the most from this arrangement, as their position in this new political order is anything but certain. But if you're willing to indulge in some well-timed mischief, you can try to help things move along to your own advantage. The king and queen will be hearing toasts and compliments all night, and faeries are known for their penchant for practical jokes. If you can end the night aligned with the correct factions who managed to look good to the king and queen, you can come out with your position assured.

Mischief is the first title from Dan Cassar's Dream Cult Game Studio, an imprint he hopes to use to share a unique brand of games that is deep, elegant and accessible.

Raketou do Vesmiru (English translation "Rocket Into Space") is a space race board game for 2-4 players from earth to mars and back. Unknown publisher, this game was produced in Czechoslovakia before manned space flight in 1961.

The game see players with 2 wooden rockets each. One of the rockets is an auxiliary rocket used only if needed when reaching a space station. Players leave earth and progress along a path to the space station (space 95), then mars (space 132) and back to earth (space 183), landing at each place by exact count. The wonderfully futuristic space graphics on the large board (68 x 45cm) has red numbered spaces that cause delays for the traveler, such as Oxygen leaks, magnetic interference and rogue rockets.

Most interesting with this game is instead of a dice to decide player movement a "Rocket Range" determines the count. This is similar to the Carnival Strongman game using a hammer to hit the bell. In Raketou do Vesmiru a wooden rocket slides on a steel rod when released from a spring. The top of the rocket continues along the rod and stops at a number(1 - 10)that determines a players move. If a player launches the rocket too hard, it hits a bell at the top of the "Rocket Launcher" and the player has to move back 10 spaces.

The winner of the game is the player to get to mars and return to earth first.

—user summary

Időfutár ("Timerunner") is a young adult radio play that had gone through many adaptations in Hungary. Novel, stage play and board game had been created from it, so it craved for a card game adaptation. A competition was hosted in 2019 to design a card game related to Időfutár, so my colleague Gergely and I submitted our entries: his solution won the special award, mine won first prize.

A company of friends find a seemingly useless, worn-out, old object. The “compass” – as it will be revealed later – leads its user to a hidden treasure. Our story does not continue with treasure hunt but a desperate escape, as mysterious, dark figures in suits appear and start a wild hunt for the tool of fortune.

The Időfutár card game is an asymmetrical game for 2-6 players. Asymmetrical means – in this case – that a player handles the chaser suited enemies, while each of the other players play the role of a seventh grade student, who are the protagonists of our story. One against all. The adults aim to lure the children into a trap and get their hands on the compass. The latter ones, however, have to outmaneuver their chasers by dropping fake clues and creating diversions.
The game keeps up a slight tension throughout the chase for both parties. While the students have limited communication (as the opponent hears everything), trapmaking doesn’t always end up the same way, so sometimes we give away the position of the compass with our desperate fleeing. The player controlling the ‘suited ones’ cannot sit back either, as time is working against them, and it is easy to run out of time if the students play their tricks strategically.

—description from the designer

In a magical and remote archipelago, a sorcerer helped by invading goblins enslaved the Goddess of the Island, generating discord between the 4 warrior tribes, responsible for protecting the island's temples.
Each tribe has magic crystals that can awaken a great warrior, capable of opening a magic portal and saving the Goddess, sending invaders to another dimension.
Now at war, the 4 tribes believe that only one of the tribes can save the fate of all.

In Portal, players use action points to move their warriors, seeking to collect diamonds from other tribes and take them to their own temples. For this, in addition to movement, players spend action points to acquire action cards, which allow special actions and even attacks against goblins and against warriors from other tribes.
Additionally, each time a diamond is taken to a temple, a random event is triggered, generating randomness with each new game.
The game is won by whoever manages to collect diamonds from all the other tribes, awakening their great warrior, and taking him to the ceremony site for opening the Portal.
Includes the Melvin Purvis miniature to replace the G-Men token from the Bloody Business expansion. The pack includes 3 exclusive G-Men cards.

Gun and Gun: W SHOUT (read “Double Shout”) is the standalone sequel to Gun and Gun.

Double Shout features 4 new gunners, 6 new guns, and a new overclocking mechanic.

It can be integrated with the original Gun and Gun and its expansion Gun and Gun: Overheat for a huge variety of combinations between gunner, two guns each, and special ability, so no two matches play out the same way.
family: Players: Games with expansions that add solo play

description: Ultra Premium deck of the core game 60 Associate cards, but with new and interesting skills and mechanics. The look has been redesigned and produced with metallic inks for an astonishing collectors edition look.

H.Y.S.T. (How You Steal Things), is a board game for 2-6 players. Each of you is a thief that needs to steal the diamond for a mysterious wealthy buyer. This particular diamond is housed in a museum that has "state of the art" security. Also, the museum's halls are a maze of passageways that can be arranged in over 14 billion ways using 9 tiles to create the board.

In the middle of the museum is the target of your heist, the diamond. You will use action cards that assist you to bypass security, hinder opponents, or gain movement. Regular movement is taken using dice rolls. Get to the diamond first to steal it, Be warned, other thieves can steal the diamond from you using luck or action cards. Choose your exit wisely as another thief may catch you.
 Be the first to get out with the diamond to win the game.

With monoliths, Skytear Games introduced a new system to set the difficulty level of Skytear Horde games.

Each horde card has now 3 variations with 3 different power levels, indicated by a color at the bottom of their card.

This system allows you to tweak the difficulty, and even the consistency, of the horde deck by selecting cards with 1 or 2 specific power levels out of the 3 available.

The challenge expansion makes it possible to use this system with the original 4 hordes. It basically contains 2 variations of every non-mythic horde card from the original Skytear Horde games.

Mythic cards are an exception where they only have 1 version which is always strong.

This pack includes 80 horde cards to play the 4 hordes from the original Skytear Horde game using the new difficulty system.

Hexagods is an exhilarating skirmish game that brings the strategic depth of tactical video and tabletop games to life, combined with the accessibility of the Print & Play genre. Players build and lead teams of intricately designed miniatures, each possessing unique abilities, to engage in fast-paced, tactical battles. The game emphasizes strategic decision-making, where positioning, timing, and team synergy are essential for achieving victory.

Designed for competitive play, Hexagods challenges players to outmaneuver and outthink their opponents in intense skirmishes. With a wide variety of units and abilities, the game offers a wealth of strategic options, ensuring that no two battles are ever the same. Regular updates and expansions keep the gameplay dynamic, introducing new miniatures, abilities, and mechanics to continually refresh the experience. Enter the world of Hexagods and prove your tactical prowess in a game where every decision can turn the tide of battle.

A pack of 23 tiles (10 pairs and three solos) from all previous promotional offers or former iterations of our premier Profile System game, The Duke.

Specter (single tile)
Dragon (single tile)
Mountain (single tile)
Lancer
Swashbuckler
Jester
Flag
Dragoon
Assassin
Longbowman
Shield Maiden [not shown]
Duchess
Oracle
Blank (+ sticker sheet)

Thrust! is A Space Combat Microgame Inspired by Very Bad Chess by Zach Gage and the Upcoming Ship Combat Rules From the Mothership Shipbreakers’ Toolkit. It also features secret board gameplay, giving it a vibe best described as Space Battleship.

To play, you randomly choose four space ships that will be your fleet for the game. Combat is quick and brutal, running out of fuel is as big of a threat as violence. Each time a ship is damaged, there are permanent consequences. Every game will be unbalanced and unique, and this is by design, you need to think on your toes using the ships available to you. The winner is the last person with a surviving ship.

—description from the designer

This expansion was part of the Volfyirion Guilds Kickstarter and contains:

12 Command Cards
6 Building Cards
6 Troop Cards
3 Wonder Cards 
3 Agent Cards

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: The Scientist Hero Pack
description: A Pulp Adventure staple, the mad scientist with his gadgets and crazy ideas was too fantastic an archetype not to add to the game. His starting Upgrades let you choose how ‘mad’ you want him to be, as a Gadgeteer inventor, using Fringe Science, or with an endless supply of Master Plans. He also has a Techno-Gadget that can be redirected for different purposes throughout an Adventure, and can be leveled up along with your Hero, using its own mini-Upgrade Tree to unlock the power of Super Science!

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Wasteland Warrior Hero Pack
description:
This is another Hero players have been asking about for years, and the time has come for him to make his prison break—a playable alien Hero from the Blasted Wastes—the Wasteland Warrior. This is another special Hero Class that we put together for the Kickstarter to let Brimstone veterans and new players alike dive even deeper into the crazy world of Shadows of Brimstone!

Hailing from the deadly sulfur deserts on the alien prison world of the Blasted Wastes, the Wasteland Warrior has mastered the art of survival above all else. Able to customize and trick out his armor, weapons, and wargear by Scavenging for Scrap and wrecking other items for Scrap and Tech, his gear is always changing to suit his needs. Armed with Rust Blades and a Scavenger’s Rifle, this alien fighter can Snap Fire as he closes with Enemies, shredding them with desert ferocity, then falling back to get set up for a sniper’s shot!

The Joneses just bought a new car! What are you waiting for?! You must keep up with their expensive lifestyle!

When the Joneses buy something, you and all your neighbors have to buy the same thing too!

Experience the thrill of the chase for new stuff to keep your status in the Joneses' affulent neighborhood while you struggle to stay out of bankruptcy. Take on new income opportunities and hope you don't get some unexpected expense like a broken appliance to drain your bank account!

Playing against the Joneses can be taxing. Yet, your goal is simple: Stay financially secure while trying to sell your house and relocate. But while the goal is simple, the cards may or may not be in your favor.

In this game of chance, just try to Keep Up With The Joneses.

—description from the publisher

From an early age, you gazed in awe as intrepid warriors journeyed through your quaint, slumbering hamlet. Against your parents' instruction, you stealthily observed them, acquiring a few clandestine skills during their fleeting visits. Now, the moment has arrived to transcend your abilities and forge the path of a legendary adventurer, leaving your mark on places far and wide throughout the realm.

Effortless is a lightweight worker placement game with elements of set-collection and a dash of take-that for 1-5 players. On your turn, you will place an effort at one of the 6 locations randomly selected during set up and add a card to your growing hand. Once all players have exhausted their effort, they will gain Greatness, based on the cards in their hand and where they have applied their efforts.

Due to a randomized setup, no two games will be the same! From a Hidden Coliseum to an Eerie Market, where will your next adventure take you?

Can you make becoming the greatest feel Effortless?

—description from the publisher

Released via the Japanime Games patreon.

Chaugnar Faugn is promo card # ROTOOP006

Act: Banish 1 Warrior or Disciple from your Hand: Add 1 Energy to your God.
React: Pay 1 Energy: Warriors or Disciples who Defend this turn are returned to the player's hand instead of their Discard Pile.

Released via the Japanime Games patreon.

Vexoria is promo card # KBP-005

If Vexoria does not Attack during your turn, you must Banish a Warrior or Disciple from your discard pile, if able, at the end of your Discard Phase.
Act: A God damaged by Vexoria this turn cannot use a Warrior to attach Vexoria on their next turn.
React: When a Disciple is played, give it Act: Attack 1. Banish this Disciple at the beginning of the Discard Phase.

Released via the Japanime Games patreon.

Cultist Zolon is promo card # KBP-006

Act: Attack 2.
React: If Cultist Zolon is Banished from play, shuffle him into the Warrior Deck instead.

Includes randomizer, card separator, and 5 copies.

boardgame: Cities of the Damned: Aachen and Cassino
description:
Cities of the Damned (published in ATO #60 magazine) features two challenging solitaire games in which the player must lead his Allied forces to victory in two of the WWII’s most intense battles.

Cassino:  In early 1944 the western half of the German “Winter Line” in Italy was anchored by the town of Cassino high up on Monte Cassino, which also featured a centuries old Benedictine Abbey atop the peak.  The position utterly dominated the surrounding valleys, so much so Allied troops HAD to take it to continue advancing on Rome. Facing some of Germany’s best soldiers, the area was only captured 5 months later after four separate assaults, with the vast majority of the city was destroyed by aerial bombardment and vicious street to street fighting.

Allied units (brown) are companies. German units (blue and grey) are KGs of 150-180 men—and tank units represent 10-12 vehicles. The map scales are printed on each map. Each turn represents 1 day of time. D6 combat resolution.

Aachen: Five months later the world would see yet another account of the terrible cost of audacity.  Fresh from a string of victories across the length of France, the US Command set its sights capture of the supposedly weakly held German city of Aachen.  The Americans confidently made plans to surround the city, isolate it, and accept its surrender. But being the former capital of the First Reich, and the first true German city to fall into Allied hands, the city’s propaganda value was simply too great: Hitler forbade surrender.

Unit level counters for both ground and air, d6 combat resolution. The game is nine turns long.

Aachen uses the solitaire system designed for the game: Stalingrad: Advance to the Volga, 1942
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Monsters Outnumbered is a math-based card game that uses strategy, deception, and a team of monsters who have taken on number form.  Players sabotage each other's Hordes (equations) with calculated assaults while tactically working to strengthen their own.  Only the most cunning will be able to command their monsters to victory by achieving the highest Horde in the deadly arena.  Players accomplish this using addition, subtraction, multiplication, division, and ability cards. Games can be played in teams or free-for-all.

—description from the publisher

Wolfsrudeltaktik is a card game where players take the role of World War II German U-boat wolfpack commanders.  The objective of the game is to prevent the Allies from shipping enough supplies to Britain to end the war early, and to sink more enemy tonnage over the course of the war than your opponent.

Manage your submarines, utilize your special skills, and choose carefully which convoys to attack; the Allied destroyer escorts are always on the lookout to sink a German U-boat.

—description from the designer

In a world ravaged by plague, only the sharpest minds survive.

The Dark Order is a confrontational, tactical card game for two players, set in a unique dark fantasy world, featuring illustrations created by talented artists. Step into the role of a commander, leading a unique faction to victory. Deploy your units, issue tactical orders, and deliver critical support — medkits, traps, or weapons — when the tide of battle demands it. With resources dwindling and the battlefield constantly shifting, you must choose wisely: who will go to the front lines, and who will be sent to scavenge the locations? Beware — exhaustion and wounds will take their toll, eventually forcing even your best troops to stand down.

At the heart of the game lies a unique mechanic: Battle Reserve Management.
Every action depletes your reserve, and the longer the battle lasts, the more your forces weaken. Your Battle Reserve fuels every move — but once it’s gone, defeat is inevitable. Carefully balancing aggression and endurance is the key to survival.

➡️ manage your hand & resources to plan every move,
➡️ fight through back-and-forth combat resolution,
➡️ play multi-use cards in clever ways,
➡️ outsmart your opponent with bluffing,
➡️ adapt thanks to multiple win conditions,
➡️ take risks with push-your-luck decisions,
➡️ explore asymmetric roles & factions,
➡️ and enjoy a quick setup…

…all under the pressure of a unique Battle Reserve Management — your greatest resource, but also the clock that can kill your commander!

Industrollization is a print and play, roll and write game for one to four players. In this game you will deliver goods to factories. The order in which you will place goods is crucial to score the most points. You can deliver goods by train or by truck.
The game lasts six Rounds. Each Round is divided into two phases. First phase is Loading Goods and the Second Phase is Delivering Goods to factories. There are four trains and a truck where players can place their goods. Each train will deliver goods to the specific factory while truck can deliver goods to any factory.

—description from the designer

Welcome to the Conquest of A.I. Board Game, where you must create your own artificial intelligence by 2023 using cutting-edge technologies. The exciting task you have set for yourself is to gather the six innovations needed to make it.

You'll come across a lot of thrilling opportunities in this game where you can freely move around the board, come up with methods, and create plans. This board game provides a true sandbox experience where players' imagination and ability are crucial.

Using the 30 reward cards and 54 fascinating technology cards, demonstrate your ability to manage your research points and action points properly. Investigate all of your alternatives so that you may best develop and protect your own servers.  The option to assault other players' servers gives you another way to engage in rivalry with them while playing.

Every choice counts, and the exciting competition and strategic components guarantee that you'll never get bored. To achieve a deserving win, go out on this expedition and build the most cutting-edge artificial intelligence!

—description from the designer

Drawing Fire is a pen and paper skirmish wargame for one player. Players lead a small squad of soldiers on a dangerous mission behind enemy lines.

In each game, players draw a map on a piece of paper. The map is then randomly populated with "features" like enemy soldiers, tanks, buildings and rough terrain.

Players move their soldiers around the map, attacking and interacting with features, in order to complete the objectives of the mission.

Players win once they have completed their primary objective. Players lose if they run out of time or all their soldiers are killed.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Crimetime: Fall 003 – Schweigen ist Silber, reden der Tod
description: Case 003: A murdered journalist. Did she know too much? Take on the role of investigator and enter a swamp of lies, corruption and power. Travel from location to location, identify new suspects and collect evidence in multiple locations. Follow the trail of the victim and hunt down the evil! All you get for the solution is the collected evidence. The lines between reality and fiction will blur.

Promo from Board&Dice when you pre-ordered the game at their website.

Content: four level 2 corner buildings and two level 3.

Set-up: Mix in with the others.

Description: When constructing a Building you can use the new corner Buildings to overbuild. Contrary to the normal rules, these must be built in order: the level 2 corner Building may be placed only over level 1, and level 3 over level 2. Also, you must use the depicted citizens to do it, putting them in their respective tracks as per normal rules. Place your marker
on top of the new Building and score Victory Points, but you do not get any extra reward or penalty for constructing
them.

Promo from Board&Dice when you pre-ordered the game at their website.

Content: 4 alternate building bonus tiles.

Set-up: Replace all 4 of the Building Bonus tiles with the Alternative ones. Place them face up, at random, in their dedicated spaces on the main board during step 1 of the setup. Then, proceed with the setup normally.

Description: Their effects are:

     Gain 1 Cloth and 1 Victory Point
     Gain 5 Victory Points
     Advance 2 steps on the Cerdà track
     Advance one step on your Sagrada Família track

This fun-for-all game is designed to bring entertainment and endless frustratation to your gatherings, parties, and game nights. Bring together both family and friends, and prepare for a savagely thrilling experience! Your objective is simple: be the first to get rid of all the cards in your hand. But don't be fooled by its simplicity! BLEEP! will challenge your wit and test your ability to outsmart your opponents. Will you use your cards strategically to gain an advantage, or will you play an aggressive action card to shake things up? The choice is yours! BLEEP! combines strategic thinking, several twists and turns and a dash of mystery to create unlimited fun. The game includes a set of numbered cards ranging from 1 to 12 and a collection of aggressive action cards that will let you wreak havoc upon your opponents! But be careful, what goes around, comes around!

—description from the designer

Jesters is a 1-card expansion that shuffles into the game.


Shuffle into the base game.

Juggle: When placing a card, you may place it in a spot already occupied by the Jester card. Then place  the Jester card in a different, valid position.

H.D.P.: Dibujame (Draw me) is a great party game, with good H.D.P. humor but, this time, you're going to create a lot of drawings that are crazy, bizarre, with double meanings and a little ill-intentioned. Each card is going to have a funny situation or prompt for you to draw. When you're done, another player will try to guess what you meant and put it in a sentence, which someone will later use to make a new drawing, and so on for several iterations.

The game reimplements and combines the core "Telephone" mechanics present on Eat Poop You Cat (AKA Telephone Pictionary) with the ones from H.D.P., while maintaining the characteristic H.D.P. humor intact.


Game mechanics: 
Setup: 
- Each player draws:

     A tablet with a number (1 - 8).
     A reusable drawing sheet.
     A paper binder.
     A marker.


- Additionally:

     Prompt cards are shuffled and placed on the table, facing down and accessible to all players.
     Prize cards are also shuffled. Five of them are randomly (blindly) selected and placed on the table, facing down. (The remaining prize cards will not participate in the game).


Round mechanics (summary):
1) Each player draws a prompt card from the pile and chooses one of its 3 options to draw.
2) Once all the drawings are completed, they are passed to the player to the left (), who has to interpret in word/sentence form- the drawing they receive.
3) Once everyone has written down their interpretation of the original drawing, they once again pass it to the player on their left (concealing the original drawing).
4) Now everyone receives a new word/sentence that hey need to turn into a drawing. Once everybody's done, they pass the new drawing to the player on their left (concealing the previous drawings and interpretations).
5) The drawing-interpretation iterations continue until everyone receives back their original number tablets.
6) All the drawing-interpretation sequences are revealed and price cards (worth 1 point each) are distributed -by consensus or vote- among players. The player with the most points is the winner (obviously).

Innkeeper is a colorful, fast-paced, card-slinging, game of mercantile! Players will take on the role of Proprietors competing to earn customers in a busy fantasy setting! To be the best Proprietor you’ll need to use business smarts, cunning, and treachery to stay one step ahead of the competition. Bid on assets, orchestrate treacherous plots, and renovate your inn to attract customers. Outwit the other Proprietors and stake your claim as the greatest Proprietor in Bittersburgh!

Innkeeper is a competitive game in which you and your fellow Proprietors must compete to earn the attention of customers. To win the game, you have to be the Proprietor with the most gold in your Vault with no Debt cards at the end of 4 rounds. You'll find yourself biding on powerful assets, and sabotaging your competition with powerful Treachery cards!

The gameplay of Innkeeper is designed to be a fast, chaotic, and treacherous experience for you and your friends!

—description from the designer

Murder trials have never been so much fun!
Trial! Murder on the Beach is a card game with a English trial theme. Each player is a Defendant on trial for the murder of Victor Tim. The aim is to place Guilt on the other Defendants so that they are convicted of the murder and you are not.
In the first part of the game, Defendants use Solicitor Cards to win Trial Cards and build their hands for the Trial.
In the second half of the game, the players use the Trial Cards to win control of the witnesses and raise each other's Guilt.

In Suspicious Behaviours one player is a Detective trying to figure out who did what on a mysterious crime while watching the physically and verbal cues that other players will do regarding their secret role, and all in real time.


This game was distributed as promo material and also included inside the box of Recreo Arcano preorder and June 2023 Ludoty Box games.

Enter the circle of rocks, in the center of the ring of mushrooms, behind the trees of eternal autumn, hidden in the valley of sister souls, there is hidden the treasure that will give you freedom.

Soulmates Who is the dreamer and who is the shadow?

Palin & Drome is a board game for 1 to 4 players that is very quick to learn and play.

Each turn the player must decide how much to move, knowing that each step he takes will be reflected by his opponent, as if a shadow or spirit were stalking him... By placing traps and taking advantage of the walls, they must try to collect as many points as possible before any of the two fall exhausted. The player with the most points will be the winner.

They must be careful what traps they place, where and how they take advantage of the traps that their own opponents set in their favor.


The game was originally devised during the Global Game Jam 2014 as DoppleGanger in Córdoba, Argentina by the team of Débora Schvartz, Carlos Kirincic, Emmanuel Rubio, Lucio Estevez.

Have fun playing the It's In The Groove Country Trivia Card Game. Hear song lyrics from classic country tunes from Garth Brooks, Kenny Chesney, Tim McGraw, Brooks & Dunn and many many, more.

Two or many players have fun taking turns reading the lyrics cards while the other players shout out the song title and or artist. Many ways to play. 107 cards in every deck will test your recall as you race to answer.

Cards can also be played with the It's In The Groove Deluxe Board Game.

—description from the publisher

Engage in the exhilarating world of horse racing and strategic investments! Your objective is to amass the greatest fortune by acquiring horses, participating in races, and purchasing shares in various companies. Success lies in making smart moves with your horses, companies, and other ventures you are involved in.

Two games in the box: Classic Jägersro with the original gaming experience for enthusiasts and Modern Jägersro - a modern, fast and compact family game.

The game contains a double-sided game board that allows for both game modes. A race book with achievements that allows you to follow your horse's career and see your results in the Hall of Fame and also a legacy variant that changes the dynamics of the game over time.

—description from the publisher
