Double or Nothing adds a plethora of additional content that seamlessly integrates into 3000 Scoundrels, such as:


    Advanced Technologies


Technologies now grant unique abilities to give you an edge over your rivals both during the game and at the end of it.
Clear mod cards provide even more variability to 3000 Scoundrels, ensuring endless ways to employ a winning strategy!


    Double the Scoundrels


New job and trait cards offer new abilities and double the number of possible scoundrels to 6,000!
Double or Nothing also introduces a brand new type of scoundrel: the Maverick! Mavericks shake up 3000 Scoundrels as they activate during your opponents’ turns, forcing you to keep a keen eye on every move your rivals make.


    Robust Solo Mode


Sitting alone atop the West?
Double or Nothing offers a dedicated solo mode, with four unique leaders to square off against.
Each enemy requires a different strategy to defeat, keeping your approach to victory fluid and varied.
Enemies also offer an expert side, increasing their difficulty when you’re ready for a new challenge!


    Debts


Players can gain a debt for a quick infusion of cash, but must be careful as unpaid debts could cost precious tech points.


    New Locations


Visit the Newspaper for a leg up on leads for valuable tech, or stop by the Loan Office to rid yourself of dangerous debts.


    Extended Leader Boards


Players can now hold two additional scoundrels for bigger posses and even bigger combo opportunities.


    And even more!


—description from the publisher

Doo Doo Kangaroo is a game of silly fun and action where kids feed the kooky kangaroo donuts until he does doo-doos! Then it becomes a game of fast skill and observation as they quickly try to grab the donuts but dodge the doo-doo! This fun party game is sure to make kids giggle as soon as they see Doo Doo Kangaroo sitting on his toilet waiting to be fed! Players take turns by spinning the spinner to see how many donuts to feed the kangaroo. When Doo Doo Kangaroo is full... Watch out, he's gotta go! He'll start to shake and shimmy all around the table causing donuts or doo-doos to fall out of his toilet! Race to pick up the donuts and place them on your plate but don't pick up the doo-doo! The first player with 5 donuts on their plate wins!

—description from publisher

Give each player two dice and deal a standard deck of cards into four face-up piles in the center.

Then, on a count of “1, 2, 3, dash!” players start rolling their dice, in real-time.

After each roll a player can take all the cards from the center that equal the sum of their dice (Jacks are 11 and Queens are 12; Aces and Kings are only used in variants). If they take any cards, they must reroll both dice, otherwise they can choose to reroll one or both of their dice.

Keep rolling, as fast as you can, until two of the piles run out.

Scoring
2s and Queens are 3 points, 3s and Jacks are 2 points and every other card is 1 point each.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: River of the Dead Enemy Theme Pack
description:
River of the Dead is a new type of product for Shadows of Brimstone called an Enemy Theme Pack, and this one is about the nefarious Cult of Horus and their devotion to finding and resurrecting the scattered relics and mummified remains of their ancient master, Khamuntet!. It's a great way to add a bunch of exciting new Enemies, cards, and Missions that are all themed for a specific World or faction in the game.

River of the Dead
The great god Osiris was lord of the dead, ruler of the afterlife, and master of resurrection. The fields would grow at his command and the souls of the dead would be judged in his name, worthy or unworthy to pass on into the afterlife. Killed by his jealous brother Set, Osiris’s body was cut into pieces and spread throughout the land, before being re-assembled and resurrected by the power of the goddess Isis, his wife.

It was in this tradition that the Arch-Villain Khamuntet, after finally being defeated millennia ago, was also cut into pieces and sealed away in the hidden tombs of his own dark sorcery, buried deep in the desert sands. Now the fanatical Cult of 'Horus, son of Osiris', seek to reassemble and resurrect Khamuntet in the same way, so he too may pass judgement on mankind, spreading Darkness across the land!

The River of the Dead Enemy Theme Pack comes with a horde of new Egyptian Enemies to challenge your Heroes, as well as new Cult of Horus themed Missions for the Lost Tombs, and over 40 new Game cards with assorted counters. You get 1 Priestess of Judgement, 3 Large Ushabti Warriors, 1 Large Ammut - Eater of the Unworthy, and 6 Osirian River Guards.

Priestess of Judgement
A powerful sorceress in the Cult of Horus, the Priestess of Judgement follows the sect of Osiris, charged with performing the sinister rituals and harnessing the dark magik that will re-forge and resurrect their master, Khamuntet! Possessing the Scales of Truth, her harsh judgements can bring death and destruction to anyone that stands in the way of their master’s glorious return, as she unleashes Ammut, devourer of the dead, on her foes.

Ushabti Warriors
Carved of stone and placed in burial chambers and tombs, Ushabti were created to be servants and minions of the dead. They come to life at the call of their master, taking up tools and weapons for the task in need. Though many are created as legions of small figurines, the most terrifying Ushabti Warriors are those sculpted at great size and adorned with hieroglyphics. Crafted in the likeness of Osiris, and armed with hefty Egyptian war axes, these stone warriors are formidable foes when called to war.

Ammut, Eater of the Unworthy
A monstrous creature that combines the front of a lion, hind-quarters of a hippopotamus, and the head of a vicious crocodile, Ammut is a divine beast that feeds on the hearts of those deemed unworthy. When the scales of judgement tip not in your favor, Ammut is ready to devour your soul!

Ammut is a nightmarish Large creature that ruthlessly hunts those found unworthy of joining the blessed dead in the afterlife.

Osirian River Guard
These skeletal warriors guard the River of the Dead. Armed with Dark Stone-tipped spears and heavy shields, they bear the ceremonial dress and armor of the Pharaohs. Nearly unstoppable in their ghastly resolve, they rise back up from the dead to fulfill their sacred duty and bring ruin on any that would defy the judgement of their master.

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Fury of the Reich Enemy Theme Pack
description:
Fury of the Reich is a new type of product for Shadows of Brimstone called an Enemy Theme Pack, and this one is about everyone's favorite Pulp Adventure villains to punch! It's a great way to add a bunch of exciting new Enemies, cards, and Missions that are all themed for a specific World or faction in the game.

Fury of the Reich
As a buildup to war in Europe seems all but inevitable, the fascist Reich have begun scouring the globe for ancient and powerful occult relics that could help further their ambitions. Founding an elite and secretive branch of the military known as the Trident Korps, they embark on expeditions into the far reaches of the world, using experimental technology and ruthless cunning to achieve their goals. Researching tales of the lost city of Atlantis, the Trident Korps has dispatched a detachment to the desert sands of Egypt in search of the key to unlocking the power of this ancient fallen kingdom.

The Fury of the Reich Enemy Theme Pack comes with a host of new Pulp Adventure Enemies to challenge your Heroes, as well as new Relic-hunting themed Missions for the Lost Tombs, and over 40 new Game cards with assorted counters. You get 1 Colonel Stein, 6 Reich Soldiers, 1 Tressa - Femme Fatale, 3 Trident Kommandos with Harpoons, and 3 Trident Kommandos with Lightning Guns.

Colonel Stein
A veteran of the Great War, Colonel Stein has seen his share of horrors on the frontlines, both manmade and supernatural. When his unit’s trench was overrun with tentacled monstrosities, only his ruthless nature allowed him to survive; but the evil of the Void creatures left their mark, corrupting his flesh with hideous mutation. Rising through the ranks, he now leads a division of the Trident Korps, seeking out occult artifacts and ancient relics that could bring him more of the raw power he felt on that dark night so many years ago.

Reich Soldiers
Reich Soldiers form the backbone of the Trident Korps’ expeditions. These soldiers are hand-picked from the military ranks for their cold demeanor and fanaticism. Equipped with gear for exploring ancient tombs and temples, the Reich Soldiers carry the deadly MP.36 submachine gun to ensure that they have the upper hand in a fight.

Tressa, Femme Fatale
Using her beauty and cunning to take advantage of her adversaries, Tressa is a cold-hearted killer. Second in command to Colonel Stein, she is in charge of tracking down leads on the locations of the lost tombs and temples in the deep desert. Her disdain for the heat only serves to make her more sadistic in achieving her aims. Fast and agile, Tressa depends on subterfuge and deception, striking the killing blow without warning or remorse.

Trident Kommandos
The Trident Kommandos are elite troopers armed with powerful advanced weapons and armor, harnessing the power of the Void to their own evil ends. Their suits are engineered for exploring OtheWorlds and harsh environments like the deep sea. They are at the forefront in the hunt for the Lost Kingdom of Pharrox and the priceless treasures and technology that could be found in the Sunken City.

Chaos has broken out, and people are running in fear! A Zombie is roaming the city in search of delicious brains.

In Zombie Flip , your goal is to guide him to the victims with the juiciest brains... in points, of course! If you have the highest score at the end of the game, you win!

The objective of Underflow is to be the merchant with the most objective points at game's end. Each Objective card and additional abilities award points to players. The game is primarily resource collection with dice drafting and a bit of engine building mechanics.

The game is played in four phases in each round. These include the Flow phase, a collection phase, a second collection phase, and finally the objective phase. Players draft dice which represent positions of locations or cards on the 9x9 grids.

Objective cards give players points and abilities including end game goals and in game actions. The player with the most objective points after 6 rounds is the reigning merchant.

—description from the publisher

Encore is the expansion to the spooky boss-battling campaign game, Vagrantsong, that will take players to new heights, new lows, and everywhere In Between.

 
Going ‘round the second time with Vagrantsong: Encore, you’ll find:



        * A fourth door to open that leads to a new Chapter with 5 new Scenarios called Bridge of Leaves.
        * 6 Save Your Soul Scenarios to bring your friends back from having one foot in the grave.
        * Short Trip Mode, which gives players the opportunity to explore the Silver Ferryman in Chapter-sized chunks.
        * New In Between cards, Skills, and Junk.
        * Smoky Bones, special dice that players earn and find to help turn their bad luck around.
        * And enough twists and turns to make your head spin. 




It’s time to hop aboard the Silver Ferryman one more time with Vagrantsong: Encore.

—description from the publisher

Vagrantsong: Off the Rails offers players the opportunity to revisit Scenarios from the core game and its expansion, Encore, with new and exciting challenges along the way.
Included in Off the Rails:

     A new game mode that increases the difficulty and replayability of over 40 Scenarios.
     New Objectives tailored for each Vagrant called Devious Deeds that provide more character customization as Rewards.
     Smoky Bones, special dice that players earn and find to help turn their bad luck around.
     And a few spooky surprises along the way.


Vagrantsong: Off the Rails requires the core game of Vagrantsong to play.

—description from the publisher

In February 1945, the Japanese executed Operation Kita ("North"). Two hybrid battleship-aircraft carriers and four escort ships dashed home to Japan from Singapore. The Allies detected the Japanese task force, but all their tries to attack it with submarines and aircraft were ineffectual and failed. These were among the last IJN warships to safely reach Japan before the war ended.

This number 28 in the Pocket Battle Games series from LPS Inc.

boardgame: 東のポテトマン (Potato Man of the East)
description:
Potato Man of the East uses a deck of cards of 1 through 5, and a few wild cards, and is played as a memory game. The cards are shuffled and set out on the table in a grid.

On a player's turn, they reveal cards one at a time. When they reveal a third card, if they have revealed three of a kind, three in ascending order, or three in descending order, they can choose to stop and score or keep revealing cards.  If any time time the revealed cards don't satisfy one of those criteria, the player's turn is over and the next player takes their turn.

The player with the most points wins.

Gaia Project: The Lost Fleet is an expansion for Gaia Project, containing:


    Four new factions: Darkanians, Mowyed, Space Giants, Tinkeroids, corresponding to 2 new planet types: proto planets and asteroids
    New map layouts, featuring holes where the ships of the lost fleet and some new planet types will be placed
    New smaller sector tiles ("interspace" and "deep space" sectors) containing the new planet types
    New ships from the Lost Fleet to explore, allowing access to new action spaces, technologies, technology tiles and federation tokens. No standard Q.I.C. actions anymore - Q.I.C. actions are now only available on ships
    3 new standard tech tiles and 6 new advanced tech tiles
    4 new round boosters
    3 new round scoring tiles
    3 new final scoring tiles
    Some faction balancing (start resources, income, additional actions or different costs to explore a ship); Lantids get cover up tiles for their PI (1-3 player)


A webapp implementation of The Lost Fleet expansion solo Automa is available.

It's Complicated is an obtuse game for 2 players that includes parts of chess, poker, snakes & ladders, resource collection, dice rolling, worker placement, and more! It intentionally takes longer to explain than to play.

Players roll dice, use the dice through worker placement to take actions in each of 4 sectors. There are 4 ways to win, but most often winning depends on the control of the four sections: Warfare/Chess, Politics/Poker, Science/Snakes & Ladders, and Economics/Resource Collection.

The board-game-in-a-poster edition of the game includes all components, even papercraft dice and chess pieces, in one 18x24 inch poster.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Dog Park: Dogs of the World Expansion Pack
description: Dogs from Kennel Clubs across the world are coming to Dog Park! The Dogs of the World expansion includes 29 cards and 3 new abilities: Friendly, Fusspot, and Globetrotter.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
In Trailblazers: Sasquatch Expansion players cooperate together to build hiking, biking, and kayaking loops in a shared area. But be careful, one player might secretly be a traitorous Sasquatch seeking to sabotage your trailblazing efforts!

Sasquatch wants to be left alone and will thus do anything he can to keep pesky humans out of his woods — including undermining trail construction. If there is a Sasquatch in the game, then the other players (humans) want to find him!

How to Play:


     Deal out the secret identity cards. All players are humans, but one player might secretly be Sasquatch.
     On your turn: Play 1 trail card, discard the other facedown into a discard row of your choice. Then draw back up to 2 trail cards.
     If you are Human: Cooperate together to build hiking, biking, and kayaking loops through new hybrid camps to maximize your points… and keep an eye out for the sneaky Sasquatch.
     If you are Sasquatch: Blend in with the Humans while subtly sabotaging their trails. Reveal yourself when you think you can win, or ride it out until the end-game scoring to determine the victor.
     If Sasquatch is revealed, then Humans must race to build a loop through his location before their turns run out. If Sasquatch is not revealed (or there was no Sasquatch), then Humans tally up their loop points to see if they won.


The game ends after a Sasquatch Hunt or when a discard row is full.

This expansion is compatible with any edition of Trailblazers.

—description from the publisher

Dinosaur Train Card Game is the sequel to the Whale Train Card Game similar to Mah Jong or Rummy
The goal of the game is to make a set of all 9 cards in your hand. The rules are like Mahjong, you connect pictures on the cards to complete a dinosaur or train.

Two carnivorous dinosaurs and three herbivorous dinosaurs make one set. There is no set number of trains, steam locomotives, and bullet trains. Vehicles can be chained together to make them longer and longer.

If you arrange them in the same colour or make a long train, you can play a role.

Do you aim for a big role and wait patiently, or do you aim to get up early?

boardgame: Monster Hunter World: Iceborne – The Board Game: Overpowering Hunger
description:
Overpowering Hunger is an expansion containing new monsters, new weapons, new armors, new quests.

Fulgur Anjanath: Feared for its sheer strength and thunderous power, this Anjatiath subspecies electrifies its jaws and dorsal membrane to strike down its foes.
Brachydios: This marine-blue brute wyvern uses its saliva to prime the sticky slime coating its massive arms, rendering it highly explosive.
Raging Brachydios: A large and unusual Brachydios variant that is even more aggressive. Its body is covered in highly explosive slime that's quick to detonate.
Gold Rathian: A rare gleaming gold Rathian species with exceptionallv strong scales. When wreathed in hellfire, its breath grows more lethal and its tail swipes more broad.
Savage Deviljho: A mutated Deviljho variant, driven mad by its overpowering hunger. It will attempt to feed on all it sees.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Monster Hunter World: Iceborne – The Board Game: Ruiner Nergigante Expansion
description:
The Ruiner Nergigante expansion adds this XXL spiked elder dragon (120mm base!) and all its adrenaline-pumping quests, rare weapons, and powerful equipment crafting options to your game. That means:


     New campaign quests
     New arena quests for one-shot boss battles
     New choice-driven narrative
     Every craftable Ruiner Nergigante weapon type — for all 14 hunters — from the video game


It also introduces a whole new way to win your campaign, because it includes a 5-star quest! Will you withstand the onslaught to take this formidable threat down?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Monster Hunter World: Iceborne – The Board Game: Blackveil Vaal Hazak Expansion
description:
The Blackveil Vaal Hazak expansion adds this unsettling XXL elder dragon (120mm base!) and all its skin-crawling quests, rare weapons, and powerful equipment crafting options to your game. That means:


     New campaign quests
     New arena quests for one-shot boss battles
     New choice-driven narrative
     Every craftable Blackveil Vaal Hazak weapon type — for all 14 hunters — from the video game


It also introduces a whole new way to win your campaign, because it includes a 5-star quest!

—description from the publisher

#inconversible: Magnificent Yomi a collaborative reasoning game where two or more players can play.

Yomi, a girl with red hands, has become an old man and traveled to the fantastic world of "Magnificent".

Now Yomi in bringing together local children to show them her album of memories and tell the story of "Magnificent" that she experienced.

But be careful because her album contains a fake memory. If you mention that fake memory (that is, if you flip it over), everyone loses.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Pacific Rim Breachfall can be played with two to four players. One team sides with the Pan Pacific Defense Corps (PPDC) and can build mighty Jaegers. The other team plays as the alien Precursors who create the dangerous Kaiju who want nothing more than to destroy humanity.

The two different groups have a different goal throughout the game to achieve victory. The Precursors must destroy all the cities around the Pacific Ocean and the PPDC must find a way with their Jaegers to close the 'THE BREACH' so that no more Kaiju can come to Earth.

When one of these two goals has been achieved, the game ends. The team that completes the goal wins!

During the game you can always consult with your teammate to determine your tactics. You can also show each other your cards. But be careful, because your opponents can of course hear you.

—description from the publisher

The King has a widely-known passion for gourmet food. As the royal chef, you must not only prepare the king's favorite dishes at the right time but also keep an eye on your competitors to avoid being overshadowed. It's alright if you want to sneak a bite, just make sure you don't get caught!

In the game "The King's Feast," players take on the role of the royal chef, providing meals for the king through three rounds of gameplay: "Breakfast, Lunch, and Dinner." During each round, players take turns playing five culinary cards based on the king's preferences, earning points based on the quantity of each type of food in their ranking. Additional effects can be triggered if certain linking conditions are met.


It is time to give your offerings to the King to ensure that you’re in his good books, if he likes what you’ve offered, the King shall duly reward you! However, what you’ve prepared smells so good that you really want to keep some for yourself, be really careful that this is not spotted by the King as he has a really sharp eye! A keen lookout on your fellow players is also important, do not allow them steal the limelight!

The King’s Feast is a card game that’s intuitive and easy to learn. Decide between keeping food for yourself and competing for the rewards of the King. Play the Food cards in the right order to highlight their flavors and receive bonuses!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Bestiary of Sigillum: Collector's Edition – Out-of-Control Chaos Promo Campaign
description:
Out-of-Control Chaos campaign is a mini-expansion for Bestiary of Sigillum: Collector's Edition that consists of 3 consecutive two-player missions with unique mechanics. Each mission changes the core rules to set up a narrative and create a new playing experience.

In the Chaos of Space mission, hexes adjacent to the Citadel (central hex) change their terrain type every time the Time Dial is rotated. The Chaos of Time mission allows characters to use Abilities of their allies with a one round cooldown penalty. Finally, in the Chaos of Events mission players get Event cards that can be played in a special way, with their effects applied only one time and only to one character or one suitable condition.

Whenever a mission is completed, both players score Victory Points: the winner gets 3 VPs, while the loser gets VPs based on the number of damage points the opponent’s Castle has suffered. When the campaign is over, the player with the most VPs wins. In case of a tie, both players share the victory.

Take advantage of chaos to battle against your opponent under unpredictable conditions!

Gueroy is a strategic battles board game in a fantasy world. 
Choose one of the two Heroes in one of the 4 available Clans and face your opponent in a tactical fast-paced match. 
Use Essence Crystals, an in-game currency and Victory Condition token, to summon creatures from your Clan to help you in the confrontation. 
Move your pieces through the hexagonal board, perform Attacks, Actions and Effects and collect Treasures in this competitive PvP.
Fulfill your Essence Well, destroy your opponent's Well or kill your opponent's Hero and you will prevail.

—description from the designer

description:
The North American Raccoon shouldn't be in Europe, but it is.  Play as one of four different roles as they try to minimise the impact this invasive species has on the native species, and human population of Europe.

Semi-Cooperative (Coopetitive) game for 3-4 players.  Work together to reduce the Impact or everyone loses.  Succeed and 1 player will have pushed the agenda in their favour enough to be the overall victor.

Propose and Vote on Policies that try different approaches to managing the Impact.  Policies are played to a Queue for resolution, Action cards can be used to manipulate the queue and the votes.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Yellowstone Party Game is a gritty Crisis and Solution board game with the hit show’s attitude and style. Become one of your favorite characters and step into the Yellowstone world. Experience the fierce battle to protect the Dutton Ranch as you solve crises, prove your loyalty, and outmaneuver those with hidden motives. Perfect for true Yellowstone enthusiasts who “ride for the brand.” This social deduction adult party game feels straight out of the Bunkhouse. Prepare for things to get loud, heated and plenty rowdy. “This is going to be the end of us. But we’re gonna do it anyway.” - John Dutton

THE YELLOWSTONE SOCIAL PARTY GAME. Become one of your favorite characters from the Dutton Ranch and step into the Yellowstone world. The goal is to figure out who is the disloyal player betraying the Dutton family while defending your loyalty to “the brand”.

A GAME OF LOYALTY AND DECEPTION. It’s a game of hidden motives. Each round, players must solve a common crisis. Use your personal decoder to reveal the crisis at hand. Disloyal players have a dysfunctional decoder that does not reveal the crisis on the card.

SOLVE THE CRISIS. Play down one of your Solution Cards to solve the crisis. Between the limited options of Solution Cards and, for some players, the lack of knowing what the crisis actually is, players must pick their Solution Card wisely!

THE REVEAL. Get ready to defend your choice and prove your loyalty. Using the communal decoder to reveal the crisis to the entire group, it’s time to accuse, defend, strategize, bluff, and challenge players’ choice of Solution Cards. Things get wild here, so tap into your inner Beth Dutton.

FOR YELLOWSTONE FANS and their friends and family. No need to have seen the show to play (but it sure helps!). Perfect for a game night, pre-drink, party, bachelor or bachelorette party, or just for a chance to be Rip for a while. The game contains mature content; it’s a card game for adults ages 17+. 4-8 Players.

WHAT'S INCLUDED. 100 Solution Cards, 50 Crisis Cards, 12 Character Cards, 9 card decoders, and 2 voting tokens, along with a rulebook to show you the ropes.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Ancient Terrible Things: Expedition '23 Promo Pack
description:
Ancient Terrible Things: Reawakened Expedition '23 is a promo pack of extra content from the 2023 Kickstarter campaign for Ancient Terrible Things: Reawakened.

Contents: 5 Encounter cards, 2 Swag cards, 1 Character/Starting Swag card, 3 Feat cards, 1 Rules/advertisement card, 1 character Disc, 1 3D Tainted Tide tile.

—description from the publisher

Simulation game with athlete card and dice. 
Athlete with two types of rating: Skiing and Shooting (Stand and Prone).
Each World Cup Race has 3 to 6 rolls of each athlete (depends on race type: sprint, individual, Pursuit or Mass Start)

Super-G 2018 is the third (of four) offering in our Alpine Skiing series. Slalom 2018 and Giant Slalom 2018 are already out there, work will start on Downhill 2018 as soon as Super-G goes off to be published.

All four events are similar but different! Super-G could have been called 'Mini Downhill' but 'super' is a more appealing term than 'mini' so they went with that. The differences (from Slalom and Giant Slalom) are sufficient to require another re-think and re-design of the basic game engine, hopefully reflecting the difference in the events themselves, but that is for you to judge.

Skiers still have three Ratings, but Start is of less importance as the race times are longer, and we have added Technique to the ever-present Control and Speed. In game terms Technique is the skier's inherent ability in the approach to the turns and in the turns themselves. And because this is an in-built ability the Rating for Technique isn't subject to movement up and down during a run although there may be marginal changes for a particular Turn as part of the Performance Roll. But those changes are temporary, for that Turn only.

Because run times are usually between 60 and 90 seconds there is only one run per skier in each event. And to make the simulation as playable as possible a run consists of just six Turns, two each of Top Section, Middle Section and Bottom Section. One run rather than two helps to simulate the lessened predictability of the event and to reinforce that we let each Turn for each skier use the current Control Rating plus either the current Speed Rating or the static Tech Rating. More of that later.

Once again we have supplied Ratings for the top thirty in the World Cup tables for both Men and Ladies, plus twenty All-Time-Greats in both categories. Details of how to generate your own Ratings are included as before as are very similar solo rules using the familiar Mindset concept. Enjoy.

—description from the designer

The Temporal War is fought in an arena built at the beginning/end of time itself. Warriors from across time and space are gathered to battle in the arena. Their reward is a brief moment of near infinite power. With this power, the winner can grant almost any wish imaginable.

This is a collectable card game for 2-6 players. You can play a free-for-all rumble or form teams of two or three.

Each player begins with one summoner in the arena, a deck of cards which represent their summoner's memories, and various warrior pieces and tiles to put into the arena.

The game ends after a certain number of rounds have been completed (4 or 6). If a winner hasn't already been decided through combat, the player who controls the most energy sources claims victory for themself or for their team.

—description from the designer

boardgame: 凡庸ロボットのネガイゴト (Negaigoto of mediocre robots)
description:
凡庸ロボットのネガイゴト ("Negaigoto of mediocre robots"), also referred to as "The Wish of a common robot", is a growing Must "Knot" Follow trick-taker. If you are to play a card of a suit already in play it will be ranked lower than all other cards played in the trick.
Can your hand become stronger or, conversely, become weaker? What's more, you can select one card from tricks each round and carry it over to the next round...! You can also get batteries by getting cards with tricks! If you raise your skills with batteries... you can get strong cards...! There is a chance to score additional points...! Strategize your way to victory!
publishers: ぽてきゃっ党 購買部 (Potkyattou Purchasing Department)

description:
Invincible: Escape from Mars is a social deduction game where your objective is to find your teammates, get on the rocket, and escape from Mars. But loyalties can change at any moment, including your own!

Based on the animated series, this game puts you in the position of either a hero or an astronaut attempting to escape Mars and make it back to Earth without bringing a world-destroying parasite home with you. No pressure!

Each round players will be faced with an obstacle that they must overcome as a group. On their turn, a player may contribute a card to the obstacle, place a card on their own personal setback, or play a card for a special ability. At the end of each round players will resolve the obstacle in either an astronaut victory, a sequid victory, or a neutral outcome. If the astronauts or sequids achieve 3 victories they immediately win. If neither team reaches 3 victories before round seven, players have a chance to launch the rocket. Every player that has completed their personal setback boards the rocket and attempt to make it back to Earth. Before the launch though, players will have one final chance to vote off any infected players that have infiltrated the rocket.

Depending on skill level, players may elect to play a simple game as an astronaut trying to escape Mars, or a more challenging game as one of 9 unique superheros attempting to protect those astronauts. Superheroes have more power, but they’re playing a tougher game. You know what they say about power, responsibility, etc. Each player’s choice to be an astronaut or hero makes no change to the overall balance of the game!

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
A quick and snappy dueling game for 2 players focusing on wit, timing, and misdirection all wrapped in a fantasy rpg theme. Players will have a a hand of 6 cards. Each turn, both player's will secretly choose a card from their hands to play and reveal/resolve them simultaneously.  These cards have a variety of effects, but generally will either be dealing damage, blocking damage, or healing HP.  Players start with 10 HP and the goal is reduce your opponent to 0.  After each card is played, it waits for three turns in a "Queue" before it returns to its owner's hand.  Players will also know at all times every card in their opponents hand. So they must choose what card they think will counter their opponent the most.

—description from the designer

How well do you know your logos!? This amazing game of quick brand recognition, which is fast and frantic, is the ideal game for the whole family to join in with — but do you know your Chupa Chups from your Cornetto, Colgate from your Aquafresh? A simple game to play — each player must collect brands that match the mission cards being read out. Collect the most brands to win!

Logo cards are spread across the table between players and each player has a section cup stick to collect logos. There is a deck of cards which give you a criteria for each round (e.g. laundry brands or logos with green in them) then it’s a mad dash between players to grab the correct logos.

boardgame: Design Your Destiny: Running Out of Time
description:
Read a fictional story about Charlie and Natalie as they are whisked away to the future to try to stop the invention of time travel itself! Every couple of chapters you get to play as Charlie and Natalie in a game where you help them progress through the story. Unfortunately, each game is broken and needs your help to fix it.
The next few pages after a game will guide you on ways you can fix the game, but you are given complete freedom to fix it however you want. Each task you complete earns you a sticker that helps put the time machine back together. If you complete enough tasks and fix the time machine, then you unlock the final chapter to see how the story ends.
It's a sci-fi story but also a game design tutorial that's great for kids or for people just getting into game design.

—description from the designer

Lants & Lot is a cooperative game of rivaling marionette casts. Players attempt to "K.O." advancing opponents using a common poll of Sicherman dice. By manipulating the type of dice in the pool and playing the right pair of hand cards, abilities can be gained and odds can lean in your favor.

The cast has Chip Points which are players health. Each player chooses a character with their own traits, These traits determine how many dice may be rerolled when compared to enemy traits.

If players defend their sage from the last rival with at least 1 Chip Points left for each player, they are victorious!

—description from the publisher

Gorge is a silly 2 player game of Clans competing in a competition for fishing rights. Cards act as levers to launch objects over and into the gorge in order to achieve a set number of Feats.

The game combines worker placement on ever changing actions and a bit of understanding of fulcrums and levers crated by the two ends of the Gorge. Even Clan members can be launched but a player must have at least 1 clan on their side of the gorge at all times.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: One Page REV: Guns in the Summer Wood
description:
One Page REV: Guns in the Summer Wood is a solo roll and write American Revolution wargame. Players act as a colonial militia in upstate New York during the height of summer 1777. Six 6-sided dice are needed to play the game. The militia starts at one end of a map, with terrain generated randomly by dice rolls and drawn onto the map.

As the militia enters each hex, rolls to determine enemy units are made and combat follows. Different types of weapons (rifles, pistols, etc.) are affected by terrain and weather. Victory points are assigned to the player after defeating British units in combat, and British victory points are gained when the militia takes damage. Combat is resolved through dice rolls with the number of rolls depending upon the player's weapons, stamina and morale. Rolls exceeding a certain number count as hits, and the number of hits determines the attack's result.

If the militia accumulates sufficient victory points, it wins immediately. Otherwise the militia continues to an objective hex and if the militia has more victory points than the British, the player wins. Recordkeeping for the game is made on the map page and each game is played on a new page. The book contains a printer-friendly game page as well that can be copied and printed. The player could also use tokens and counters from other games to keep track of the game's progress.

Who's the biggest smarty pants? Try out 200 challenges including number-crunchers, brainteasers, catchphrase conundrums, and word puzzles. Take turns answering a range of brain-busting questions and see who will be victorious in their quest to be crowned the ultimate smarty pants!

—description from the publisher (paraphrased)

Two tough Guys on the hunt for scoundrels and scoundrels! With BeanS BooM BANG! you will experience the adventures of Bud Spencer and Terence Hill firsthand, and you can even reinvent from scratch. Take on the role of a director and invent crazy stories, defeating criminals with thunderous blows. Right jab, left slap in the face or the infamous hammer fist: anything is allowed, it...

Each player starts with 5 BEANS cards on the table. Each BEANS card represents one kind of blow (left punch, right punch, double punch, left slap, right slap). The cards must lie covered on the table, in front of the player.

Each turn, one of the players performs the role of the director. The director chooses randomly a BOOM and a BANG card from the stacks on the table. BOOM and BANG cards describe places and situations. The sum of the points printed on the two cards are the points earned by the player who will win the turn.

The director, following the suggestion provided by the cards he picked, invents a story. He cries out "Action!" and all the players must put their hands on their knees. At a given point of the story, the director will name a blow at his choice, such as "Right punch", or "Double punch". The players should beat the table performing the right action. The first one who succeeds wins the turn, and therefore removes the BEANS card in front of him corresponding to the action requested by the director.

The game ends when a player get rid of all his BEANS cards or if a player have eaten too many sausages or drunk to much beer. Sausages and beer cards are part of the BOOM and BANGS decks. If a player has more than one beer or sausages, the game ends early and the winner is the one with the highest number of points.

Galactic regions are defined based on the age of their stars
Stars explode and perish at the end of the 6th galactic year of their life
The regions of the galaxy are being divided
The emperor has chosen you to rule these areas
choose the best areas for yourself
Blame the older areas on others
Who will be the ruler of better regions?

—description from the publisher

Compete against rival coffee store owners in this fast and fun card game. Fulfil orders quickly and add special ingredients to score big, but watch out - the thief might steal your coffee!

Coffee Thief is a 2-6 player card game involving strategy, a little bit of luck and straight out theft. Players take turns to collect ingredients, fulfil orders and steal from their opponents.

Play for 10min or 10 hours, its up to you! Coffee Thief is easy to learn - offering a unique mechanic that heightens the intensity of the game as you play.

But beware, you never know when the thief is just around the corner.

—description from the designer
A baseball game from 1937 with the results of each play determined by the consultation of charts corresponding to six-sided die rolls with no differentiation per player.

Captain’s Log #7 includes the fiction story What Price Victory? about a battle between the Yorktown and the Klingon cruiser Pitiless. Captain Merrin Hunter, Star Fleet’s only female cruiser captain, is locked in a mortal test of wills with Klingon Captain Keng. The fate of a vital Federation outpost hangs in the balance!

Also included are five new scenarios, four new ships (Federation battlecruiser, Hydran Apache light command cruiser, Klingon MD5 war cruiser mauler, and Lyran Single-Tooth Jaguar war mauler), information about officer ranks and a list of the officers on a Klingon D7 battlecruiser, the first and only trivia contest, an example about carrier drone storage, the first Battleforce 500 article, tactics, and the original version of the SFU Index.

The F&E section includes Q&A, The Molasses Attack (strategy), and The Breeze, which is a new F&E scenario.

Supports Star Fleet Battles and Federation & Empire.

Delve into the depths of your mind and test your gaming knowledge with Did You Know Gaming’s new, dungeon-crawling trivia card game. Alongside a group of fellow heroes, you’ll be tasked with using your wisdom and insight to answer gaming-related questions as you progress through the depths, killing monsters, acquiring gear, and becoming stronger, all in the hope of defeating one of several deadly bosses and escaping the Trivia Dungeon. Choose an iconic player character and either band together with friends to take out the dungeon boss, or go it alone and escape solo. It’s now time to prove whether you really Do Know Gaming!

—description from the publisher

Some of the food has been gathered, realising that in the freezer it can last longer and not go out of date. But there is little room left in the freezer... Who will be the lucky one to take that place?
Players play cards to get matching sets and advance going up in the refrigerator to the freezer. The first to get in the freezer, wins.

A culinary card game that feels like you’re competing on your favorite cooking show - without destroying the kitchen.

Grab your starting hand - Every player starts the game by grabbing five ingredient cards and five dish cards for their own hand, along with a piece of paper and a pencil.

Pick the Head Chef - One player takes a turn as the Head Chef each round. The Head Chef starts the round by picking a card from the Special Occasion Deck and a card from the Ingredient deck (which is now the secret ingredient).

5 minutes to create a dish - All the players now have 5 minutes to write down a unique recipe for a dish that utilizes the cards in their hand and the secret ingredient, something they would bring to that round’s special occasion.

Present and judge - Once the five minutes are up, all the players take turns presenting their recipes to the head chef. The head chef picks one person to win, and that winner accumulates points for the ingredient and dish cards they used in their recipe.

Repeat - Players continue playing rounds by redrawing cards and taking turns as the Head Chef. First player to reach 25 points wins!

—description from the publisher
publishers: Jordan Gayle Johnson Digital Media, LLC

description:
Rule the Waves, Britannia is a fast-playing card game where you take command of a British warship during the Age of Sail. Set your course across multiple journeys in Europe and across the Atlantic, sail around Jamaica, and cruise through the Lesser Antilles. Encounter bad weather, Spanish, French, and Dutch warships, sometimes their formidable flagships, even some of the most notorious pirates!

Each journey only takes 5-15 minutes, so your whole odyssey can take just over an hour.

Includes an historical mode, along with 2 player versions that are either cooperative or competitive.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
In Tides of War: 1944, two to four players command their soldiers over a shifting battlefield, strategically adapting to evolving terrain and reinforcements of troops. At the start of the game players choose how to arrange the board and objective, but over the next 60 to 90 minutes, explosions and flames will alter the landscape and leave the terrain different than how it started. Along with the variety of Troops, Terrain, and Victory Conditions, Tides of War also offers a unique set of rules that remains simple, yet entirely different from more generic mechanics, offering many more possibilities which allow for a unique game experience each time.

—description from the publisher

Verge is a two-player strategy game played on an initially empty hexhex board. The players take turns placing a stone of their color on an empty hex. The first player unable to place a stone has won.

     A partitioning group is a group separating two hexes outside the group, in the sense that all paths between the two hexes go through the group.
     You're not allowed to place adjacent to a friendly partitioning group.
     When a group becomes partitioning, remove all nonpartitioning groups adjacent to it.


The game is balanced with the pie rule: One player decides what player 1's first placement will be, and the other player decides whether to play as player 1 or player 2.

—description from the designer

Enter the fascinating world of business negotiations and prove that you are the master of strataccor.

Walk the boardgame, buy tiles, buy properties and ask rent.

A variation of monopoly but the properties power depends on the tiles you own.

In Halloween II: The Game, players will roll dice to move their characters around the board, collect weapons, draw cards, enter dangerous 'Shape Zones,' work around unpredictable obstacles, and strategize together in an effort to stop Michael Myers from reaching and killing his intended target, Laurie Strode.

—description from the publisher

Can you stop killer miner Harry Warden from reaching the Valentine's Day dance and slaughtering the town youths? As Warden stalks his way to the big Valentine's Day dance, one to four players (that's you!) move around the board collecting weapons, drawing cards, working around roadblocks, using the secret mine tunnel, and trying not to get their hearts broken. But watch out – players who land on Harry's space will confront the pickaxe-wielding slasher, which means certain death for either Harry or the player.

—description from the publisher

The spin on the game is that it will be themed around July 4th. These games are played on holidays, at least for now, and we needed to make it different than the movies. So we’re creating a brand-new story: It’s July 4th weekend, and this little town of Clarion, Pennsylvania, is having a big drive-in for the holiday. They wake up to a newspaper report that these Ghost Face killers are running around and killing people. The idea is that the police officers have figured out that this Ghost Face (or Ghost Faces – key!) is headed to the big drive-in to kill a bunch of people. And it features nine masks in the line. So you’re gonna get the gold Ghost Face, the devil Ghost Face, the orange, the green, the scarecrow, the aged one. The idea is to celebrate the line of Ghost Face masks that Fun World has been cranking out but aren’t in any of the movies … it’s a brand-new adventure for Ghost Face. He’s still killing.

Unlike the other Stop the Killer games, we have a multiple-killer angle, which you find out throughout the game, and it’s different with every game, whether there are one, two, or three killers you have to stop. And there’s a whodunnit angle.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Feathered Serpents Enemy Pack
description:
The Feathered Serpents of Kotak are a new Enemy group themed for the lush, rain-filled jungles and island outposts of the Spanish Fort. As Myth Enemies, fighting in the name of their ancient master, the cosmic deity Kotak, they are equally at home gliding through the murky Swamps of Jargono, where many ancient ruins dedicated to Kotak still rise up from the watery marsh. On feathered wings, the Serpents of Kotak descend from the heavens to hiss and strike at those who would dare intrude on the temples dedicated to their ancient master. With long, needle-like fangs, the Feathered Serpents deliver a powerful venom to their foes, piercing armour and flesh alike. Highly agile, the winged serpents dart from target-to-target, lashing out with their tails and using their venomous bite to strike with deadly accuracy. But perhaps their most dangerous asset is the protection afforded to them by Kotak himself; a powerful death ward that can mend even the most grievous of wounds with the cosmic magik of the Ancients.

Includes 6 Feathered Serpents of Kotak, Cards, and Rules

—description from the publisher

While it’s impossible to include everything, we jammed all the Lethbridge we could into this box. It’s full of outdoor adventures, family attractions, rich traditions and of course…Nikka Yuko Japanese Garden - a little something for everyone! Looking for a fun way to experience The Windy City? Buy your favourite Lethbridge properties, increase your rent by collecting City Blocks and trading them in for Keys to the City. Sound easy? Well, add in some traffic jams, property tax and parking fines and it gets a little more difficult and a lot more fun! So choose your token and advance to GO Lethbridge! You may soon be the Mayor…or out of the game for eating too many Blues Burgers from Coulee Brew Co.!
publishers: Late for the Sky Production Company

boardgame: Age of Steam Deluxe: Expansion Volume IV
description:
A collection of Age of Steam Deluxe expansion maps:

     Balkans (3-4) by John Bohrer / England (4-6) by John Bohrer
     Ukraine (3-5) by John Bohrer / Trisland (3) by Ted Alspach
     Blizzard of '77 (3-6) by Dylan D. Phillips / South Carolina (2) by Ted Alspach / Death Valley (2-3) by Randal Lloyd


Each Expansion Volume of the set contains multiple maps on mounted game boards, plus the rules and any extra components needed to play each one.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Super fast and simple, conventional point-to-point game about Patton's historically failed attempt to grab logistically strategic Breton ports and liberating Brittany. (Simpler, sleeker system than Postcard Brittany but retaining the essential gameplay objectives.)
German units, roadblocks, and blanks begin game hidden - to stay so in a 2-player game until revealed or until the end of the first Patton turn in a solitaire game.
6 turns.  As for Postcard Brittany, 11 US, 3 French FFI (having special abilities), 11 German infantry and extra-resilient parachute units.
Lotsa dice combat system - defender firing first.  Push-your-luck No Retreat! rule.
(Note also the Brittany side game coming out with Phalanx's logistical system Keep'Em Rolling.)

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Why is it called The Holiest Crap? Cuz self-righteous people are... holy pieces of crap!

This strategic card game is inspired by the silly, self-righteous crap you and your friends do every day! It’s a game about people, not poop, where every card is like a meme making fun of the crap we all do. Just like in real life, you're competing to look better than everyone else – what fun!

Hide your Vices (like your Bull Crap) by covering them with Virtues, so you look holy instead of crappy. Make other players look bad by attacking their Vices and exposing their crap to the world. Lift yourself up, tear others down, and get the most points to crown yourself The Holiest Crap!

Created and illustrated by the human Erik Robinson (not A.I.)

—description from the publisher

Okuno is a game similar to UNO. Players race to empty their hands. In turns, players attempt to play a card by matching its color, number to the topmost card on the discard pile. If unable to play, players draw a card from the draw pile and pass their turn. The special cards can spice things up a bit.

It’s only 7 days until code freeze (the business’s deadline for code changes), so it’s crunch time for you and your fellow project managers. Bribe developers with coffee, buy your new User Interface from a vendor, and acquire Management Support as you try to get everything done before the deadline. Don’t forget to watch out for bugs in your code, though! A single Null Pointer Exception can ruin an entire Ticket (not to mention Stupid Mistakes). You’ll need strategic planning, management, and probably synergy (or something) to succeed, but god help you if the build breaks. Whoever impresses upper management the most will win!

—description from the publisher

Original Czech escape game follows events from the time of Assassination of Reinhard Heydrich. In this story you will act as an unknown member of Czech resistance, trying to make his best to stop the Czech Occupation.

This escape game is a variation on popular escape games where players are locked in a room and are trying to get out of it.

The game is made for the police of Zeeland (a Dutch district) to promote their work for a safe neighborhood.

You start with 6 card board tiles in your hand. On your turn, you may place a tile adjacent to the space where your pawn is on a map of Zeeland provided the design of an adjacent tile fits. 3 times 4x4 tiles show the same village with houses, streets and  a police station. If you have another tile that fits to the placed one, you may put it as well. If you don't have a fitting tile, throw the special tile to move your pawn the respective number of spaces across already placed tiles.
If you have no tiles left, you may alternatively move neutral pawns like citizens and policemen across the spaces. If you are adjacent to a neutral pawn, keep it for scoring. When all neutral pawns are taken, player with highest score wins.
Play Hvad er jeg? mad & drikke, a.k.a. What am I? food & drink, with up to four friends by placing cards on your forehead, then taking turns asking questions to discover what food you represent.

“Are we Klingons, or are we spineless worms? We may die, but death in battle brings glory to us all.”

Read all about the betrayal at Oxvind V where the crew of a Federation battlecruiser fight for their lives in a Klingon trap in Romulan space.

Enjoy new play aids: the particle cannon energy record form for Star Fleet Battles and Klingon and Lyran record keeping forms for Federation & Empire.

Star Fleet Battles players will find new ships (Klingon G2C police leader, Klingon F5W war destroyer, Kzinti war destroyer carrier, and Kzinti fast carrier resupply frigate), Ask Kommodore Ketrick, Ask Uncle Ardak, information about tractor beam auctions, scenarios, a Seltorian seminar, Victory at GenCon 1993 by Alex Pundy, SFB Rules cross index, and Term Papers.

Federation & Empire players will find Federation & Enquire, Tactical Notes, Alliance strategies, rules notes, and The Tholian Web, a new scenario.

As is usual, there is information about the company, Star Fleet Awards, Q&A, humor, background information, and information on Prime Directive and Star Fleet Warlord.

boardgame: Die drei ??? Kids: Chaos vor der Kamera
description:
The players slip into the roles of Bob, Peter and Justus and use their brains and a good memory to try to find out who is innocent and who is responsible for the acts of sabotage.

20 cards are placed face down on a game board, two for each of the ten people. One of these is collectively identified as the "culprit". The players move their pawn across the game board (between the character tiles) using dice and may then look at one of the neighboring pawn tiles. In this way, they try to find memory-like pairs of figures until only the "culprit" remains.

—user summary
A new map for 1817 that shortens the game, and makes it more suitable for 3 players

1817 Volatility brings new private companies and a new auction structure to 1817. 1817 veterans will appreciate the variety of synergies that can be explored with each of the new privates. The new auction structure adds variability and turns what was a pretty static auction in 1817 into a more interesting game.

How weird can you act in front of other people without laughing?

The art of behaving strangely in front of others without laughing or blushing requires a tremendous talent or years of practice.

Knäppgök is a party game consisting of 100 cards with tasks that are guaranteed to be difficult to perform without laughing. The more you try to keep from laughing, the louder the laughter will be! The cards can be handed out to the party participants but can also be used as seating cards.

Math + memory + fun are intergrated in this innovative game and activity card deck. Who said math has to be boring?
 "Hurry up" is a combination of addition, subtraction, multiplication, division, memory games and activities.Everybody will have endless hours of fun.
 It is time to unplug and have real interpersonal bonding time. With "Hurry up" everyone can improve their math skills and cognitive memory

Each team has rating on Serve and Rally which must be subtracted  respectively opponent's Pressure rating and opponent's Defence Rating. 
 Once these two ratings have been established, the teams alternate serving and Rally (with d100 roll), as in real volleyball, until one team scores.
Moreover, the game has a quick system for creating the matches of the other teams, if you don't want to play them set by set, but you want a result that is faithful to the real strengths of the national volleyball teams, with only one roll per Set per team (Quick Play System)

Simulates Field Hockey competitions with just a few dice rolls.
Each Game has 10 phases per quarter. 
In each phase you roll to determine who has possession (the attack range is calculated for each team minus the opposing team's defense range).
A second roll (it rolls on the team chart of who won the previous possession) can result in a shot on goal (three different types of shots), or in a Turnover or Counter-attack.
Resolve the Shot on Goal with another roll, and with a 3rd roll you see the marker.
This game also has a one quick method to decide other games with a single roll.

Play archery competitions with just a few rolls.
Each Athlete card has a rating for each arrow shot (and the points it hits on the target), from 6 to 10 or missed.

Pair the athletes, and have them challenge on the distance of 3 arrows and whoever gets the highest score wins, and qualifies for the next round.

This is a climbing/shedding card game where each round players will collectively create the victory points pool that is allocated to each person as they play all their cards. The deck is made up of cards 1-7, seven times each, or 1-8, eight times each, depending on the player count. Each card has tie-breaking numbers on them to allow creative options for players to play all the cards in their hands.

The size of the meld dictates the strength of the cards played. Peaks are 1 or 3-card melds, and the higher values on cards are stronger. Valleys are 2-card melds, and the lower values are stronger.

The player who best climbers the peaks and valleys of the wilderness will win the game by getting points equal to or above the goal!

—description from the designer

Boardame based on the very popular Belgian tv-series/soap "Thuis".
You have to choose one of the main characters and answer quiz questions about the series (running for 25 years on television). The one who gets the most correct answers gets to organize a Thuis-party and wins the game. Best suited for fans of the series.

In order to survive the fierce competition for survival, we repeatedly fought over territory.
Tired from the long battle, we all decided to take a moment's rest while playing a card game. Unfortunately, some of the cards are so dirty that they cannot be seen.
it can not be helped. Let's enjoy the game by supposing what the dirty card is.

This was a KS exclusive that adds 5 additional village locations:

Trading Post
Stables
Outpost
Barracks
Barricades

boardgame: Second World War at Sea Midway: Aftermath
description:
We know what happened after the American victory at Midway. What might have happened had the Japanese won instead? We pick up the story after the mutually destructive Battle of Midway. SNLF troops have taken Midway in days of bitter fighting and the Japanese must decide what to do next.

Midway: Aftermath is a Campaign Study, with eleven scenarios depicting Japanese operations in the Hawaiian Islands against a diminished but not eliminated U.S. Pacific Fleet built around the carriers Saratoga and Wasp plus the battleships of Task Force One.

You’ll need Midway Deluxe Edition (just Midway Deluxe) to play the scenarios.

—description from the publisher
family: Series: Second World War at Sea (Avalanche Press)

description:
Level up, fight nasty internet monsters and secure the crowns of dominance. Teaming up with your fellow players is crucial, as well as dealing the highest possible reward out of every situation. But who are your friends in the next fight? Maybe you shouldn't have stabbed them in the back before.

There are two piles of cards waiting to be discovered in this game. Players draw items and potions from the safe pile and collect symbols showing their capabilities in combat. 
In order to win crowns, players also need to explore the dangerous pile of cards where monsters and curses lurk. Monsters can be defeated with the symbols on the items - often, this can only be reached by teaming up with a fellow player. Of course: Help is not for free so you need to negotiate the loot, as well as staying aware of the other players - they might have curses or monster-boosters on their hands that may cause a desaster.

In order to win this card game (containing more than 120 different cards), Players need to sense opportunities when to team and when to break up with their fellow players.

—description from the designer

Så det kan låta: Schlager! is the "Melodifestivalen edition" of Så det kan låta.

By solving four "musical" tasks, the teams must move their game marker from Start to Finish on the game board. First to finish wins!

boardgame: Heroes of Stalingrad: War Stories #02 – Aleksei Naumov
description:
January 1943, supplies for the encircled German 6th Army were flown to the Pitomnik airfield.

To close this channel, Rokossovsky ordered the 91st Tank Brigade to seize the airfield. The 344th Tank Battalion was sent against the Bezymyannogo Hill and the Novaya Nadezhda Farm, both located on the outskirts of the airfield near Stalingrad. During five hours of intense fighting on January 21, 1943, Naumov and his crew destroyed 5 tanks, 24 armored vehicles, 19 artillery pieces and mortars, 15 machine gun nests, 5 bunkers, and took out up to a hundred German soldiers.

—description from the publisher
family: Series: Heroes System Tactical Scale World War 2

description:
Game that brings the figure of the saints closer. It shows them as people who follow God with great commitment and support.

During the game, participants remember the details of the (short) stories they hear, find pictures from cards for large plans, give other players a task or have to associate a saint with an event themselves.

boardgame: Heroes of Stalingrad: Battlefield Stories #01 – All Quiet on the Eastern Front
description:
This expansion allows you to play in two new environments, a fiercely contested Kolkhoz on the outskirts of Stalingrad, as well as the docks on the west bank of the Volga. With this, you can relive the dangerous crossing of the river by the soldiers of the Red Army.

Battlefield Stories are new products containing Terrain Boards (Brand New) and Scenarios (Brand New).
family: Series: Heroes System Tactical Scale World War 2

boardgame: Monster Hunter World: The Board Game – Turf Wars Upgrade
description:
A new mechanic introduced in Iceborne, turf wars sees some monsters go head-to-head on the board — often while you're caught in the middle!

This upgrade deck adds the new turf wars mechanic and more to your Monster Hunter World board game. It comes with more than 50 cards, including:


     Turf war behaviours for 6 World monsters
     Handy player reference cards explaining turn sequences and status ailments
     Potion and whetstone cards


—description from the publisher

In Flow of Fate, players draft powerful Character, Weapon, and Resource cards to assemble the most synergistic tableau they can manage in 3 short rounds.

Every Fate card has multiple actions, and every Character, Weapon, and Resource card has multiple purposes, from being upgradeable, to helping other cards upgrade, to contributing to colored sets which are worth bundles of bonus points. The result is that your strategy is never set in stone; you can always pivot to outmaneuver your opponent!

Flow of Fate has an expandable player count. With two copies of Flow of Fate, you can play with up to 4 players, and with three copies, you can play with up to 6 players!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Escape Game Party: La Malédiction du Temple Maya
description: Could you escape those two adventures? Items have been stolen from the Calakmul from and the curse of Maya's gods is about to strike: the end of the world is announced. Get into the mysterious temple in order to attempt to ease their anger.

The ancients have granted you the charter you need to found a settlement on the distant shore. It is up to you to journey there, and turn the boatload of workers, resources, and food that will accompany you on your voyage into a sprawling settlement, and a thriving community.

Yonder is a mid-weight worker placement game. Over the course of five rounds, you will collect resources, gather food, and build your settlement at the edge of a mysterious city. Place your workers carefully, harnessing each species' special power to its fullest extent. Direct the expansion of your settlement, constructing the buildings you’ll need in order to thrive. Dispatch workers to other player's settlements where necessary, trading manpower for the use of their facilities. Meet with a steady stream of envoys, each of whom offer lucrative contracts, and cash in on as many as you can while trying to avoid unsustainable commitments. There are the challenges that await you over yonder.

—description from the designer

Enter the world of the 1st expansion for ERUNE and roam through its darkest dungeons... New Adventurers, new epic quests, new monsters and thwart the Master of Shadows' evil plans!

Erune is a Dungeon-Crawler for 1 to 5 players.

The Spirit of Erune is the privileged companion of your adventures. Guardian of the ancestral knowledge of the world of Erune, it resides in each and everything. Impartial, it does not take part in the conflict between the Adventurers and the Master of Shadows.

The Spirit of Erune is the narrator of your adventure
It allows you to speak with the characters you meet during your adventures.
It punctuates your quests with all kinds of events.
Beware! Your choices have consequences that influence your story!

The Spirit of Erune is a source of knowledge
It will answer all your questions about the rules of the game and the objects of the world of Erune down to the smallest detail.

The Spirit of Erune is luck
It unveils the treasures, the traps and the effects of items among thousands of possibilities!

The Spirit of Erune makes you evolve
It reveals your earnings when you level up, your quest objectives and the winner of the game.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Birdie Pro: Fox Run Expansion Course
description:
Disc Golf Destination: Fox Run, Jeffersonville, VT

Fox Run, the sister course to Brewster Ridge at Smugglers’ Notch Resort, was first opened in 2013. Designed by Steve Brinster (2013 USDGC Champion) and Jeff Spring (DGPT CEO), it quickly became revered as one of the most challenging courses on tour.

Fox Run is a fan and player favorite on the Disc Golf Pro Tour. It has hosted three PDGA Majors, including Pro Worlds twice (2018, 2023).

You will need to focus to keep the disc in bounds. Be smart about when to utilize your skills and make sure to save a little for the signature 18th hole.

Do you have the skill, perseverance, and karma to conquer Fox Run?

Components: Two game boards.
Board 1: Holes 1-9
Board 2: Holes 10-18

A strange Dowager lives outside of Derby in a sprawling manor distastefully done up like a Scottish castle. More distasteful yet is that her estate is simply crawling with odd characters, refugees from around Europe and eccentric, if pedigreed, characters from closer to home. Respectable people shun the great lady for her appalling taste in acquaintances, but one must confess that her rabble is spectacularly interesting...

The Characters expansion for Obsession brings thematic new personalities to Derbyshire who provide unique actions for managing your country estate: Constable Bucket, a fair hand at running off all manner of unsavoury guests; a Mr. Higbee who can find domestic help in a pinch (“References? I am sure they are here somewhere…”); the wise Reverend Grantly, a dispenser of sage advice; and an Eccentric Dowager Marchioness, who will connect you with a Prussian major, Swedish noble, and a Russian count as quick as a wink.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Lunar Rush: Plasma Thrusters Promo Card
description:
Plasma Thrusters is a promotional card first included with purchases of Lunar Rush at GenCon 2023, and also available to Kickstarter backers.  The publisher provides the following card description:

For this card, you may "boost" your ship that is in the final Medium or Slow route, whether it's Up (to the Moon) or Down (to Earth), forward and immediately arrive to the destination.  You can do this for the Fast route too, but it generally doesn't help as much.  Some clarifications below:

     You may only use this card once per Turn, and must spend an Ore and Shard to do it.
     You may use this card any time during the Moon Phase, except in the Game End Final Scoring period, you may use the card ONCE just before moving the ships to the market.
     When used on the Up route, you may immediately get the resources from the ship you "boosted." The resources for this ship may be used to build Gold Modules.
     When used on the Down route, the ship that is "boosted" sells FIRST in the Market Sale Order, ahead of any other ships.

In the board game Shadowrun: Takedown, you and your friends assume the roles of runners, serving as mercenaries and deniable assets for powerful megacorporations. The corps have hired you and your crew to handle the covert tasks they prefer not to have on their official payroll. Engage in these missions, survive, and strive to achieve legendary status with 1-4 players per copy of the game.

Over the course of the campaign, players will have the opportunity to evolve their runners to fit their play styles, whether they're hacking, sneaking, or brawling. Players can gain gear to augment their abilities or enhance their bodies through integrated cyberware and magical upgrades. The game offers a range of balanced character and gear advancements, allowing characters to develop over the course of a progressively challenging campaign.

Players select their roles, each providing unique stats that influence the gameplay for all characters. This game introduces the Mastermind role, the runner who keeps an eye on it all, seeing things the others can't. The Mastermind can grant special bonuses to players during missions, along with their own role-specific benefits.

—description from the publisher

Objective in Kitty Kakes is achieve your cats eats more cakes than others, it is 2 different zones, hand zone and towers zone. Cat cards are numbered with 1, 2  or 3. In towers zone each turn this cards have to be played to build towers (one above other) until achieve specific quantity of points to exchange by box cards, 6-7 for 1 box card, 8 points for 2 box cards and 9 or more for 3 box cards.

All box cards have to be dispersed but face down on the center of the table . When a player exchanges a cat tower for box cards this box cards have to be put in their respective towers zone where player can open it using cat points from their hand zone cards.

In order to open box cards, you have to use 6 cat points from the cards in your hand zone. Be aware some boxes might be empty. Although there is dog cards and mouse cards, these cards are used to difficult your rivals advance, like scaring (dropping a card) your rivals towers cats or stealing closed boxes from other players.

A player wins when he achieve the next objective, which one will change depending the number of players. 10 cakes to win in 2 players mode, 7 cakes in 3 players, 5 cakes in 4 players, 4 cakes in 5 players and only 3 cakes in 6 players mode. It also exists a simple player mode but the rules changes a little bit.

—description from the designer

Tiletum Tantrum House Character Tiles from Board&Dice available through the Season 9 Of Tantrum House Board Game Media Kickstarter.

Includes three new character tiles to be used with Tiletum.

Changes in Gameplay

     You can take the new tiles from the offer and place them on your player board as per normal rules described in the main rulebook.
     They can be placed with any other Character tiles in the same building, then you can perform the action depicted in the upper left corner. For all other purposes the placed tile should be treated as if it were the same type as other Characters in the same building.
     They cannot be placed in a building with only one room.
     There can only be one promo Character tile in a building.




Tiles description

     Pay 5 gold to move your marker on King Track to the same position as the first player (place it on top of their marker).
     Pay 3 Gold to place a Pillar from your supply onto any Cathedral on the map. There must be an available Pillar space at the chosen Cathedral, and you cannot place the Pillar at a Cathedral that already has one of your Pillars.
     Pay 3 Gold to place a House from your supply onto any Town on the map. There must be an available House space in the chosen Town, and you cannot place the House in a Town that already has one of your Houses.

This fine motor game is a ramen rumble! Kids build new preschool fine motor skills as they snag oodles of noodles and toppings to make bowls of ramen in the Noodle Knockout! Fine Motor Game from Learning Resources. Draw one of the 10 double-sided order cards, pick up a pair of fine motor chopsticks, and grab the noodles and ingredients needed to take a delicious victory! In addition to fine motor skills, these fun skill games also help kids build early preschool counting and color recognition skills. Designed for 1-4 players, there are even multiple ways to play—test your fine motor skills with a solo bowl, or compete with up to three friends! This versatile preschool skill development tool also comes packaged in a sturdy, reusable plastic bowl that acts as both the game board and storage!

Enjoy hours of sun-drenched fun at sea without ever leaving home when you play this Caribbean Cruise Domino Game. A traditional domino set with a twist, the game comes with a set of double-six dominos, special 3D electronic-hub calculator, score pad and instructions.

Passengers on this cruise try to be the first player to get or break 100 points, keeping track of their scores with the unique Life Preserver Hub calculator. Anytime a passenger plays a domino to make the point total a multiple of five, the Life Preserver Hub automatically plays the tropical Caribbean Cruise song and the player score all of the points displayed. The 3-D Life Preserver Hub calculator shows the current point total at all times.

boardgame: Great War at Sea: The Wine-Dark Sea
description:
In one of the First World War’s best-known naval escapades, the German battle cruiser Goeben eluded her British and French pursuers in August 1914, escaping to Turkey and helping to widen the war into entirely new theaters. That would only be the first of many naval operations on the Mediterranean Sea and the adjoining Adriatic and Black Seas with naval action continuing well after the official end of the war.

The Wine-Dark Sea covers all of the naval action in the Mediterranean between 1914 and 1918. The game includes 280 “long” playing pieces representing major warships, and 280 square ones representing smaller warships, aircraft and fleets. There are two operational maps, covering the entire Mediterranean Basin from Batum on the eastern end of the Black Sea out past Gibraltar into the Atlantic Ocean on the western edge.

The ships and planes of all the participants are present: Austria-Hungary, Britain, France, Italy, Greece, Ottoman Turkey, Romania, Bulgaria, Germany, Japan, Spain, Imperial Russia and the United States.

There are over 100 scenarios included, organized in our popular “story arc” format that places them in historical context.

—description from the publisher
family: Series: Great War at Sea (Avalanche Press)

description:
A "second-price auction" bidding game where bidding certain amounts (or using certain bidding cards) allows you to see the value of some of the unexplored planets up for bid.

Bidding is done with a small set of 11 bid cards, and players cannot bid the same amount, so it sometimes feels like a resource-allocation game (or even a worker-placement game, in a way.)

boardgame: ねこカフェ KASANEKO (Cat Café KASANEKO)
description:
This is a game where you look for the one cat that matches the theme from a pile of overlapped cat cards, and quickly grab the correct card. This is a super simple game where the rules can be understood in a minute, but it tests your observation skills, search abilities, spatial recognition, and more, as you find the correct cat and grab the right card. It's an ultra-easy and super exciting game! This new sensation of a cat card game is a contest of the keenest eyesight.
