—description from the publisher (translated)

『ねこカフェ KASANEKO（カサネコ）』は、重なりあうねこの描かれたカードの中から、お題となる１匹を探して素早く正解の取り札を取るゲームです。1分でルールが分かる超シンプルなゲーム性に、正解となる猫を探し、そして正しい取り札を取るための観察力、探索力、空間認識能力などが試される、超カンタンで超盛り上がる！　目ヂカラ勝負の新感覚ねこカードゲームです。

—description from the publisher (Japanese)
publishers: Studium Mundi (スタジオムンディ)

description:
Introducing Underground Dice Racers, an adrenaline-pumping push your luck dice game designed for 2-4 players. Step into the shoes of a Hotted up Street Racer, where each player takes control of their own racing machine and sets out to conquer the tracks. In this thrilling tabletop adventure, the key to victory lies in rolling your Engine Dice and daring to push your luck to the limits. Roar down the lanes, manoeuvring through challenges and obstacles, all with the ultimate goal of being the first car to cross that coveted Finish Line. Only the boldest and most strategic racers will claim the glory and bask in the well-deserved triumph. So, rev up your engines, brace yourself for heart-pounding moments, and get ready to embrace
the thrill of Underground Dice Racers. The race to victory awaits!

boardgame: La Defensa: El Asedio de Cádiz 1625
description:
La Defensa – El Asedio de Cádiz 1625 is a solitaire game played on three maps: Strategic, Operational (Map of Cádiz Bay) and Tactical (Map of Cádiz city) where the player will take the Spanish side and the game will take the actions of the Anglo-Dutch allied side (allied). During a certain number of turns and following a game sequence, the Spanish player will carry out the actions on the corresponding game maps on the Cádiz Bay and Strategic Maps during the first part of the game, and on the Cádiz City and Strategic Map if the allied assault on Cádiz occurs.

The objective of the Spanish player is to avoid its defeat in the game. The defeat can be produced by four possible options:

     If the 'Cádiz Blockade' is maintained for 4 consecutive turns.
     If the allied side reaches the center of Cádiz on the tactical map (Map of Cádiz city).
     If the allied side conquers the four cities of the bay (except Cádiz) at the end of the 'Assault on the cities by the allies'.
     If the Spanish morale drops to 0 at any time during the game.


If the end of the last game turn (turn 10) is reached and the assault on Cádiz has not taken place, the Spanish player wins the game.

—description from the designer

La Defensa – El asedio de Cádiz 1625 es un juego en solitario en tres mapas: Estratégico, Operacional (Mapa de la Bahía de Cádiz) y Táctico (Mapa de la Ciudad de Cádiz) donde el jugador llevará al bando español y el juego llevará las acciones del bando aliado anglo-holandés (aliado). Durante un número determinado de turnos y siguiendo una secuencia de juego, el jugador español realizará las acciones en los mapas correspondientes de juego. En los Mapas Estratégicos y de la Bahía de Cádiz durante la primera parte del juego, y en el Mapa Estratégico y de la Ciudad de Cádiz si se produce el asalto aliado a Cádiz.

El objetivo del jugador español es evitar su derrota en el juego. La derrota se puede producir por cuatro posibles opciones:

     Si se mantiene durante 4 turnos seguidos el ‘Bloqueo de Cádiz’.
     Si el bando aliado llega al centro de Cádiz en el mapa táctico (Mapa de la Ciudad de Cádiz).
     Si el bando aliado conquista las cuatro ciudades de la bahía (excepto Cádiz) al final del ‘Asalto a las ciudades por los aliados’.
     Si la moral española baja a 0 en cualquier momento del juego.


Si se llega al final del último turno de juego (turno 10) y no se ha producido el asalto a Cádiz, el jugador español obtiene la victoria en el juego.

—description from the designer (Spanish)
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: City of the Great Machine: On the Verge
description:
The On the Verge long-lasting City Events create even more tension between the Heroes and the Great Machine in the endgame. Each On the Verge card specifies special conditions both for the Heroes and the Great Machine. Each of them adds a uniquely sharp feeling of everything hanging by a thread!

The On the Verge promo-expansion consists of 4 cards of the same name and the Rule cards. They can be added to any OvM games (not S&C). These cards are also part of the Confrontation mini-expansion.

—description from the publisher

The pre-Christmas rush is already underway in the city, but Spy Guy has another case to solve! He has to stop Bert as soon as possible before he can get to his machine and use it to steal all the baubles from the city. Will Spy Guy find the clues fast enough to lead him to Bert's trail?

You take on the role of Spy Guy - a superdetective who upholds law and order in the city. Your task is to catch Bert before he reaches his machine and uses it to steal all the baubles from the town! If you manage to catch up with him - you win!
To catch Bert, you and Spy Guy must find as many clues as possible hidden throughout the city. The more clues you find, the faster your pawn will catch up with the escaping Bert. The items you need to find are shown on the clue cards.

Spy Guy Winter Adventure is a co-operative game in which you all direct the movements of the detective. The gameplay consists of a series of alternating turns between Spy Guy and Bert. The game begins with Spy Guy.
In Spy Guy's turn:

     Reveal one clue card from the deck. It indicates the item you must search for on the board this turn.
     Turn the hourglass as soon as the card is revealed.
     You all look out for the object you are looking for on the board. It will appear in more than one place. If any of you of you manages to spot it, he places a clue marker on it.
     When the sand in the hourglass has settled, the search is over! You cannot add any more markers.
     Count how many wanted items you have found. Move Spy Guy's pawn the same number of spaces in Bert's direction.


In Bert's turn, look at the clue card revealed in Spy Guy's turn. Move Bert this many spaces forward (towards the machine) how many shoe marks appear on the card. Then, if Bert has not yet reached his machine, start another round of Spy Guy.

If Bert reaches the last box on the board, he steals all the baubles from city! Unfortunately, you lose. If Spy Guy manages to catch up with or chase Bert on his way towards the machine, you have successfully caught him and you win!

—description from the publisher

W Treflikowie trwa już przedświąteczna gorączka, jednak Spy Guy ma do rozwiązania kolejną sprawę! Musi jak najszybciej
powstrzymać Berta, zanim ten dotrze do swojej maszyny i za jej pomocą ukradnie wszystkie bombki z Treflikowa. Czy Spy Guy wystarczająco szybko znajdzie poszlaki, które naprowadzą go na trop Berta?

Wcielacie się w postać Spy Guya – superdetektywa, który strzeże prawa i porządku w Treflikowie. Waszym zadaniem jest złapanie Berta, zanim ten dotrze do swojej maszyny i za jej pomocą ukradnie z miasta wszystkie bombki! Jeśli zdołacie go dogonić – wygrywacie!

By złapać Berta, razem ze Spy Guyem musicie odnaleźć jak najwięcej poszlak poukrywanych w całym mieście. Im więcej
ich odnajdziecie, tym szybciej Wasz pionek dogoni ciekającego
Berta. Przedmioty, które musicie odnaleźć, umieszczone zostały na kartach poszlak.

Spy Guy Zimowa Przygoda jest grą kooperacyjną, w której wszyscy kierujecie ruchami detektywa. Rozgrywka składa się z serii naprzemiennie występujących po sobie tur Spy Guya i Berta.Grę rozpoczyna Spy Guy.

W turze Szpiega:

     Odsłońcie z talii jedną kartę poszlak. Wskazuje ona przedmiot, którego w tej turze musicie poszukać na planszy.
     Od razu po odkryciu karty obróćcie klepsydrę.
     Wszyscy wypatrujecie na planszy poszukiwanego przedmiotu.


Będzie występował w więcej niż jednym miejscu. Jeżeli któremuś z Was uda się go wypatrzyć, kładzie na nim znacznik poszlak.

     Gdy piasek w klepsydrze się przesypie, poszukiwania się kończą! Nie możecie już dokładać kolejnych znaczników.
     Policzcie, ile poszukiwanych przedmiotów udało się Wam znaleźć.Przesuńcie pionek Spy Guya o tyle samo


pól w kierunku Berta. Zdejmijcie wszystkie znaczniki poszlak
z planszy. Jeśli Spy Guy nie dogonił lub nie przegonił Berta, rozegrajcie turę Berta.

W turze Berta spójrzcie na odkrytą w turze Spy Guya kartę
poszlak. Przesuńcie Berta o tyle pól do przodu (w stronę maszyny), ile śladów butów widnieje na karcie. Następnie, jeśli Bert nie dotarł jeszcze do swojej maszyny, rozpocznijcie kolejną turę Spy Guya.

Jeśli Bert dojdzie do ostatniego pola na planszy, kradnie wszystkie bombki z Treflikowa! Niestety przegrywacie. Jeśli Spy Guy zdoła dogonić lub przegonić Berta w jego drodze
ku maszynie, udało Wam się go złapać i wygrywacie!

—description from the publisher (Polish)

boardgame: Arkham Horror: The Card Game – On the Road Again: Parallel Investigator
description: On the Road Again is a print and play rules set for Arkham Horror: The Card Game that provides an alternate version of “Ashcan” Pete, previously released in The Dunwich Legacy: Investigator Expansion. In order to play this investigator, a copy of the Arkham Horror: The Card Game core set is required.
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: Arkham Horror – The Card Game
Game: Arkham Horror TCG – Challenge Scenarios & Parallel Investigators
Series: Arkham Horror Files (Fantasy Flight Games)

description:
Immerse yourself in the serene beauty of nature with Meadow: Adventure Book, an expansion for the beloved game Meadow. Embark on a journey through diverse landscapes, each illustrated with stunning watercolor art, as you hike into nature and observe its wonders. This expansion captures the relaxed, cozy, and inspiring atmosphere of the base game, offering new ways of exploring Meadow surroundings.

Meadow: Adventure Book introduces changes in base game mechanics with each scenario, providing unique puzzles and varying difficulty levels that enhance long-term gameplay. Rediscover the game with new twists while enjoying the tactile pleasure of the beautifully designed book-format board. This expansion is perfect for 1-4 players, ages 10 and up, and offers a captivating gameplay experience lasting 60-90 minutes.
Deck of 12 cards to play with Deadpool as Deadpool (X-Force). This was given out as a free promotional item during San Diego Comic Con 2023.

boardgame: Marvel United: Mister Sinister Promo Deck
description: Deck of 12 cards to play with Mister Sinister as a hero! This was given out as a free promotional item during Gen Con 2023.

Expansion for the The Dead Eye. 
Contents includes: 24 Hope, 24 Strength cards, 9 Story cards, 3 Character cards, 1 Rules card, and 1 Sneak peek card.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Relations between China and Portugal during the colonial period were complex and often conflictive. Portugal was among the earliest European powers to venture into China, establishing a colony in Macau in 1557. This colony marked the initial point of contact between China and Europe, playing a pivotal role in fostering commercial and cultural.

The historical context of "Captain's Log China & Portugal" will be enriched with new improvements and enhanced roles, aiming to gain fame and recognition. Stay tuned as the rest of the content unfolds gradually, revealing an engaging and intriguing narrative.

—description from the publisher

Shark in the Water is a competitive card game for 2-4 players ages 7 and up.

     play time is approximately 5-10 minutes per player
     Goal= first player to 20 victory points wins
     in Shark in the Water, players use their one action per turn to build up their fleet of fishing boats, play fishermen to catch fish or send the shark or pirate card to another player in order to capture their fish or destroy their boats. 
     as players draw up their hand limit of 5 cards, any fish cards drawn into their hand are immediately played to the center of the table and become available to catch. This does not count as your one action and is mandatory. 
     with any remaining cards left in your hand you may choose to play a boat card into your fleet in front of you or (provided you have an available boat to place a fish on), you may play a fishermen card to catch one of the available fish from the center of the table.
     When a boat has the required number of fish on it, that boat can immediately be "docked" for it's victory points. 
     once you've taken your one action, play continues clockwise to the next player who draws one card at random from your hand before drawing up their remaining hand limit of 5 from the central draw deck. 
     The shark or pirate cards can show up in any players hand at any time and if that player chooses to, they may use it for their one action to attack another players fleet.
     The shark is able to destroy another players boat and cause that boat to be discarded along with any fish on it. 
     The pirate can be used in a similar way however instead of destroying the boat, the pirate is used to steal the fish on that boat. 
     In a defensive play, if the player being attacked is holding the "chum" card, that player can send the shark to attack another player instead of themselves. 
     Pirate and Shark attacks can both be defended against by playing the Jed Fishworthy (dynamite Fishermen) card. In this defensive play, the player being attacked discards the Jed Fishworthy card in order to immediately stop the attack and send the Pirate or Shark to the discard pile, which is then reshuffled back into the central draw deck. 


—description from the designer

A collection of eight promos for a selection of David Turczi games. This promo pack was originally offered through a campaign on Gamefound to celebrate the birth of David Turczi's first child, and featured seven new baby-themed promos and one reprint.

1. Tekhenu: Obelisk of the Sun – Warmth of a Cat mini-expansion
2. Perseverance: Firstborn Adventures Promo and Tramping to School promo cards
3. Imperium: Progeny Promo Pack
4. Voidfall: Stellar Nursery
5. Vengeance: Roll & Fight – Boss Baby Promo Card
6. Venice: Nursery Promo Tile
7. Trickerion: The Secret Workshop
8. Tawantinsuyu: Children of the Inca

Drive through all 9 road cards and cross the finish line with the best possible score. 
Roll 3 dice to determine your movements and make strategic choices to optimize your speed and safety.

SUMMARY

    Game is played in turns until you go through all 9 roads.
    In each turn you will roll 3 dice and must place a number of dice equal to your current gear. 
    The rest may be used now or reserved for the next turn without being rolled again (max. 1).
    You have the option to reroll up to twice. However, after each roll, including the initial roll, at least one die must be utilized.
    Then, once there are no more dice to roll, move your car. The placement of your dice dictates the car’s trajectory on the road.
    Each road card will have 2 to 3 lanes; Left (L), Middle (M), and Right (R) with each lane consisting of up to 4 spaces.
    Each lane has a dice requirement: L (1-2), M (3-4), and R (5-6).
    End your turn by drawing a new road card to replace a road you passed. If there are no more roads, you win the game.

This unique, fast-moving board game captures the excitement and danger of the Carabas, the jewel-studded hunting ground from Jack Vance’s classic sci-fi novel The Dirdir. Up to five players control crews of desperate humans, striving to uncover precious gems and return with them to safety. Meanwhile, the vicious alien Dirdir stalk the landscape, hunting the humans tooth and claw. The more ground a crew covers, the more they attract deadly alien attention.

Move your crews across the colorful map, draw Sequin gems from the cloth bag, and roll special three-sided dice to find out how close the hunters come to you -- or perhaps you can lure them to attack a rival crew along the way.

Beware the Excellences, keenest hunters of all, and the eventual wave of aircars that will shut the whole terrain down! Two to five people can compete in about 15 minutes per player. Whoever escapes alive with the most valuable haul of Sequins wins the game!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Rolling Realms: Lost Ruins of Arnak Promo Pack
description:
Expand your copy of Rolling Realms with this set of 6 promo cards, inspired by Lost Ruins of Arnak.

To play this realm, you must use your dice to ascend your magnifier and your research journal to uncover new parts of the ruins and gain rewards. Explore and discover smartly to complete this realm.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Rolling Realms: Roll Player Promo Pack
description:
Expand your copy of Rolling Realms with this set of 6 promo cards, inspired by Roll Player.

To play this realm, you use dice to shift your alignment to lawful, good, chaotic, and evil. For every new aspect of your alignment, you earn rewards. Discover all aspects to complete this realm.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Rolling Realms: Millennium Blades Promo Pack
description:
Expand your copy of Rolling Realms with this set of 6 promo cards, inspired by Millennium Blades.

To play this realm, you must use your dice to mark cards and gain their rewards. These rewards include new dice to use elsewhere. But you will have to spend resources to manipulate your dice, as the cards go from 0 to 9. Mark all cards to complete this realm.

—description from the publisher

With this mini expansion, you can boost your games of Rolling Realms.

Each round, players can use a number of Rewards that give additional possibilities for gaining resources and scoring stars.
The pack comes with 6 unique new abilities (of which 2 are used each round), and player cards in all 6 player colors.

—description from the publisher

"Only the strongest rat clan will reign!"
Follow the Prophet's call into the shadowy world of 'RimRats'!

Take control of a mutating rat clan! With clever deck building and strategic map control, guide your swarms. Only one clan will fulfill the prophecy and rule over all.

Join the battle for dominance!
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Blast off for adventure with Star Schlock—the tabletop miniatures game inspired by sci-fi of the 1970s and 80s!  Use miniatures from the growing line of Star Schlock figures—who may bear an uncanny, yet legally distinct resemblance to familiar sci-fi tropes—to wage intergalactic war on your kitchen table.

Fortunes can swing dramatically in Star Schlock! Mount sweeping charges against your enemy's flank! Risk it all with a plan so crazy it might just work! This isn't a passive game where you set your figures up and spend two hours seeing if your deployment decisions worked. You'll need to think hard to use the limited resources at hand to outwit your opponent, push your units to the limit of their capability, and exploit the vulnerable spots in your opponent's strategy.

Play a nail-biting game against a single opponent on a 3'x3' battlefield, or bring the game to your club and play an epic six-player confrontation on a sprawling 4'x6' table (or larger!). Star Schlock is a flexible system built to handle a variety of gaming group sizes.

Use the rules for your universe, or come play in ours. The Star Schlock rules emulate the adventures of the long running Canadian sci-fi series of the same name (it ran for 20 years—surely you've heard of it?). From the Explorer Corps boldly going where none have gone before, to the madhouse that is the Space Ape home world, the Star Schlock universe is full of space princesses, mystical crystal knights, infectious mutants, plucky robots, and a host of other familiar sci-fi tropes. The Starter Set is the perfect place to jump in as we kick off the Galactic War that would be fought onscreen, in comics, novels, and trading cards for two decades!

Dozens of character and unit profiles already detail the denizens of the Star Schlock universe, with more to come. Gather  troops with abilities that synergize with each other to unleash epic combos that will leave your opponents reeling!

—description from the publisher

Oh No! Alphazed has taken all the letters from the signal! Help Xyzavier fill in the pyramid to make sure the signal is sent.

On each turn you will roll two six-sided dice.

One die determines the pyramid row that you will write into, the other die is used to choose a letter to write using the letter chart.

The game ends when all spaces on the pyramid are filled, or when all circled letters are filled in.

Proceed to scoring according to each word that was formed. Words may be in a horizontal or diagonal straight line, and may be written forwards or backwards.


First Breakfast Shy game, include in April 2023 Board Game of the Month Club Postcard package.

Very light game rules, a game that could be on the back of a cereal box!

Triax is a 2-player abstract strategy game where you control three Mages that you will move along the hex paths and through warp gates. The Mages are at their strongest when they are together so the player who can place all three of their Mages in the same hex at the end of their turn wins the game.

—description from the designer

boardgame: BIG 1: Pack O Game Micro Expansion
description: This deck of 30 cards adds new and extended gameplay for each of the titles in Pack O Game SET 1. 1-6 cards enhance: HUE, TKO, GEM, FLY, TAJ, LIE, SHH and BUS.

boardgame: BIG 2: Pack O Game Micro Expansion
description: This deck of 30 cards adds new and extended gameplay for each of the titles in Pack O Game SET 2. 1-6 cards enhance: ORC, GYM, RUM, DIG, BOX, SPY, SOW and BOO.

boardgame: Second World War at Sea: Java Sea
description:
Immediately after the attack on Pearl Harbor, Japanese forces began their “Strike South” against the American, British and Dutch colonies in South-East Asia. The combined American-British-Dutch-Australian fleet resisted as best they could, but would be crushed in February 1942 in the Battle of the Java Sea.

Java Sea is a Second World War at Sea series game based on this bold Japanese aggression. There are 180 “long” playing pieces, depicting the major fleet units of both sides: battleships, aircraft carriers, cruisers and fleet destroyers. There are also 340 square playing pieces, half the size of the ship pieces, mostly depicting aircraft but also smaller warships and markers. The game uses the maps and playing pieces from the old Strike South game, but otherwise is completely new with additional playing pieces, a completely new set of 40 scenarios, and Second Edition series rules with full-color play aids.

The two operational maps depict the South China Sea and the East Indies, including all the major ports and airbases. The area covered stretches from Formosa on the north to Australia on the South. The maps overlap with that from Eastern Fleet on the west edge, and stretch to New Guinea on the east where they overlap with those from South Pacific.

All of the battles of the Japanese Strike South are included: the battle of Java Sea and Sunda Strait and Badung Strait, the last cruise of the British battleship Prince of Wales, and many more. There are some what-if variations, like the addition of an aircraft carrier to the British Force Z. The Royal Thai Navy is fully present, along with the French squadron they battled at Koh-Chang in 1940.

—description from the publisher
family: Series: Second World War at Sea (Avalanche Press)

description:
Step back in time to the dazzling world of the roaring 20's, where the city's destiny rests in your hands! As a visionary real estate developer, your mission is to achieve fame and recognition by strategically placing your buildings in the largest metropolis on the East Coast. Welcome to Gilded Heights, the thrilling city builder card game that will test your wits and strategic prowess.

Construct magnificent buildings every round and watch the cityscape transform into a testament to your ambition. But beware, fellow developers are also vying for fame, and they won't hesitate to place their buildings strategically to diminish your success. The competition is fierce, and every decision matters. Only the savviest minds will rise to the top.

Discover four distinct types of buildings at your disposal: Residential properties, steady point collectors; Landmarks, immovable monuments to your vision; Industries, influencers of property value; and Influence cards, the perfect tools to annoy your rivals. Master their unique abilities to shape the city in your image.

Are you ready to immerse yourself in the allure of the 20's, embrace the thrill of building a skyline, and outwit your adversaries in a game of strategy and cunning? Gilded Heights awaits you, where fame, fortune, and architectural legacy are within your grasp.

—description from the publisher

A dimensional rip has allowed monsters to enter the worlds of Arkcantrix. Adventurers have entered the forbidden crypt with the hopes of conquering the new evil. The path is long and full of peril at every turn.

In Dungeon Path, each player chooses one of 12 characters that have their own weapon proficiencies, monster masteries and unique skills.

Each player receives 5 cards to start their Path as well as their first Miniboss. Players will draw cards from their Path or go to Town to purchase weapons and spells, increasing their power, defeating monsters and Minibosses until they are strong enough to defeat the Big Boss encounter!

Other players can stall you on your quest by placing Nets or Obstacles on your Path. You can build your hand in many different ways to gain the power needed to finish your journey.  With over 150 cards in the Path deck, every play is different.

First to vanquish the Big Boss wins the game and saves Arkcantrix.

boardgame: Pillage: Skirmish Rules for the Dark Age
description:
Pillage is a game written to be played in a relaxed setting, with friends, over drinks and other treats.
These rules were written to meet a need specific to the JHP (Jeux d'Histoire du Ponant) club, for which we had a project to play the Viking period but where no rules seemed to fit.
They were therefore written for this purpose.

You should know that these are simple rules, in order to be able to quickly play skirmish games on a period going from late antiquity to the Middle Ages, let's say, to be more precise, from 793 (looting of Lindisfarne Abbey) to 1066 (battle of Hastings).

The period is vast, of course, but the relative similarity of the methods used to fight has made it possible to establish here common rules for all warriors of this period.
As you will discover when you read the rules, they are essentially based on the characters' equipment.

—description from the publisher

1880 Saigon Monopoly features historic buildings and some of the city’s original street names from the era before the city’s name changed to Ho Chi Minh City.
It incorporates an old 1881 map of Saigon with some traditional Vietnamese art into the design to give the game an antique feel.

boardgame: Market: A Dice Game with a Twist
description:
Created by 9-year-old Oliver in 2022, this 2 to 5 player game is structured around dice battles called face-offs. There are four levels of dice, each more powerful than the previous.

On their turn, challengers can choose to merge and/or unmerge their dice to change their power levels before rolling. Challengers can also play the Market and sell a dice pair in hopes of drawing a higher powered die from the bag. Playing the Market is a gamble though, because you could lose your dice and have nothing to show for them!

Strategy matters, but don't discount luck in this game, because special elements are activated when you roll doubles, and then weaker dice are able to overpower even the strongest of dice!

Defeat your opponent by rolling the higher value and you get to take one of their dice. Win when you are the only one left with 2 or more dice!

—description from the designer

boardgame: The Automata Initiative: Liberation – Part 1 (fan expansion for Android: Netrunner)
description:
"Brazil has long been a haven for androids, with human rights extended to both clones and bioroids within its borders. Legislation has been introduced into the Brazilian parliament that would strip bioroids of those very rights and protections. This “Automata Initiative” would remove their legal personhood from the constitution and cement the status of bioroids as robots without soul or sentience. With tensions building between corporate meddlers, a protesting populace, and sympathetic Runners, the stage is set for a conflict that questions the very nature of what it means to be a person."

The first half of the Liberation cycle for Netrunner. Contains 65 new cards and implements new mechanics.

boardgame: 週末へのカウントダウン (Countdown to the Weekend)
description:
This is "Neko Neko Nyanpany", a company in a certain town.
You are a company cat working here.
You are very busy at the company, doing lots of work every day! But the cats are looking forward to their weekend off so much that they don't want to do much work.
“I don't need the works!”
They don't want to work on their holiday!
Let's make it a fun weekend with no work by skipping work in every possible way, or using the cats hands!

Game overview:
The game consists of 10 tricks, divided into [Week 1 (Tricks 1-5)] and [Week 2 (Tricks 6-10)].

Players play the cards from their hands, and the winner of the trick is determined by looking at the color and number of the cards.
The condition written on the "Clock card" where the "Cat pawn" is  placed is the winning condition for that trick.
In order to win/lose, each player can （1） place "Task chips" to advance  "Cat pawn" (change the winning condition) and (2) choose any cards  to play in their hand. Make sure to handle "Task chips" well to advance  the "Cat pawn" so that you do not have too many "Task chips" left over  at the end of the weekends!
Score Calculation: The score will be calculated twice. Once on the first weekend (after 5 tricks) and once on the second weekend (after 10 tricks).
Scores are given minus points according to the color and number of  "Task chips" each player has. Scores are listed on the "Count card".
Winner: At the end of the game, the player with the least minus points wins the game/

Mythic Commander is a fast-paced two-player wargame that allows each general to lead an army of warriors, monsters, mages, and siege weapons in a clash of blades, arrows, and magic.

Build your army from an array of different choices and test your tactical skill against an opponent on the battlefield. Achieve victory by outwitting your enemy and destroying their forces.

Issue orders to control your forces and unleash powerful magics using the game’s Command and Spell Cards.

Customise your army by mixing your choice of 7 factions with 6 civilisations, each of which contains different upgrades that alter the abilities of your army’s soldiers on the battlefield.

This game comes complete with a double-sided map and army tokens to get you playing right away – you just need some six-sided dice. Mythic Commander is also compatible with any fantasy miniatures you have – bases all the way up to up to 40mm can be used on the maps provided, but any size is possible on your own battlefield by following the guidelines included in the rulebook.

—description from the publisher

Dr. Frankenstein is a reimplementation of Frankenstein published in 2005 with expanded player count up to 4 players.

Dr. Frankenstein is a game where the doctor and Igor try to get sets of three ingredients from a large 5x10 ingredient rack. During a turn, the player has three action points to manipulate the ingredient columns, trying to get the exact set of three ingredients they are looking for, either on top of a single column or on three adjacent columns.

Re-stacking columns is quite intuitive: you can move stacks of any size, but you can never move them up, only the same height or down. The first player to collect five sets wins the game.

The Advanced Adaptive Combat System (AACS) is intended for real freaks, who’re looking for an advanced battle experience. The AACS gives the players more tactical control over combat against strategic targets (towns). In determining their Risk Tolerance players can now, through careful consideration of their chances of beating the defenders, risk nearly everything to make their Battle Rolls a success. Of course, the endeavour can fail. For the defenders will then prepare countermeasures, which again will endanger the success of the Battle Roll.
Also, for the first time in Eysenhudt, with the AACS the players risk actual battle losses – either in damaged goods or in absenteeism or even in the loss of whole units.
The AACS builds on the Adaptive Combat System (ACS) and is meant for very advanced players (or freaks), who love the battle experiences and who’re willing to accept an expanded duration of playing time and a way more extended and advanced combat system in exchange for more tactical options and better control of the battle procedure.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description: A 10-card mini-expansion with 6 new Human cards, 3 new Hissy Fit cards and the 1-card game, Hissy Fit: Scratch Attack!

How well do you know Switzerland and its 26 cantons? In this exciting racing game, you can prove your knowledge, discover lots of new things, and steal cards from your fellow players. The one who collects the most coats of arms and cards wins. This card game is suitable for children aged seven and above, and 2 - 4 players can participate together. The set includes 78 question cards, 26 coats of arms, 1 game board, 4 game pieces, and 1 die.
A small free Demo Game version for Noobs in Space with two scenarios on 30 cards.

Snacks is a fast-paced visual speed game where players race to spot matching snack combinations. With simple rules and quick rounds, it’s perfect for both casual gatherings and competitive play.

How to Play:

In each round, one player shakes and tosses a set of snack tokens onto the table. Players then compete to find a row on any of the eight snack machine cards that matches the exact combination of images shown on the tokens. The first to spot the correct row must either point to it or call out the card's letter and the row's number (e.g., "F-2").

Correct answers earn a snack figure as a point. Incorrect guesses result in a penalty—losing a snack figure if you have one, or sitting out the next round if you don’t.

Game Modes:

- Easy Mode: Use the red side of the cards and four specific tokens.
- Hard Mode: Flip to the gray side for a tougher challenge with five tokens.

End of Game:

The game ends when all 10 snack figures have been claimed. The player with the most figures wins. In case of a tie, a final showdown round decides the winner.


Snacks es un juego de agudeza visual y reflejos rápidos donde los jugadores compiten por encontrar combinaciones de snacks iguales. Con reglas sencillas y partidas veloces, es ideal tanto para reuniones informales como para desafíos más competitivos.

¿Cómo se juega?

En cada ronda, un jugador sacude y lanza un conjunto de fichas de snacks sobre la mesa. Luego, todos los jugadores buscan una fila en cualquiera de las ocho cartas de máquinas expendedoras que contenga exactamente las mismas imágenes que salieron en la tirada.

El primero en encontrar la fila correcta debe señalarla con el dedo o decir en voz alta la letra de la carta y el número de la fila (por ejemplo: "F-2").

¿Cómo se gana?

Si la respuesta es correcta, el jugador gana una figura de snack como punto. Si se equivoca, pierde una figura (si tiene) o se queda sin jugar la siguiente ronda (si no tiene figuras).

Modos de juego:

- Modo fácil: Usa el lado rojo de las cartas y cuatro fichas específicas.
- Modo difícil: Da vuelta las cartas al lado gris para un desafío más complejo usando cinco fichas.

Fin del juego:

El juego termina cuando se hayan entregado todas las 10 figuras de snacks. Gana quien haya acumulado más figuras. En caso de empate, los jugadores empatados compiten en una ronda final para definir al ganador.

Welcome to planet Ratyl.
Thousands of years after humans abandon an exhausted trade planet, rats now thrive. Centuries of progress sees significant technology and cultural advancements, however rat identity continues to clash with the previous human influence.
A toxic but necessary monarchy of cats surface to keep rat numbers under control and protect the civilisation known as Ratyl. With the rat community becoming too advanced; King Apollyn bans all human culture and bi-pedal affections. A resistance of outlaws form an uprising to regain control of their planet with a desire for knowledge of the humanoid past.

Ratyl is a dexterity/strategy mini game console for up to two players lasting 20-40 minutes. It's the cornhole/X-com/warioware crossover everyone has been asking for...

Ratyl is perfect to play solo or with a friend. It's incredibly snappy while creating countless 'oh my gosh!' moments of success and failure. Easily thrown in a bag at around the size of a VHS case.

The meaty dexterity layer will have you tossing, placing and moving diamonds that satisfyingly (I mean like really satisfying) rattle into a 5x10 grid. There are twenty mini games from super cozy to super crunchy. Can you escape the room as an assassin, build a successful veggie crop or invade your opponents castle?

A layer of strategy has you drafting a hand of outlaw rats that allow you to alter the game state with various powers. Rewind an action, steal an outlaw card or move a diamond at will to name a few.

Finally place the powerful polyomino pieces to persuade the diamonds to fall in your favour and increase your chances of dextrous success.

Ratyl is a competitive mini game console that is continually evolving with it’s own community. 'Plug in’ the twenty different solo and two player mini games included to enjoy a variety of mechanics and ideas designed to spark your imagination.

Have a great idea for a mini game? Then join our community online. Share scores, stories and download an endless number of new games and mechanics.  Support each other as you help create new game ideas for the ever evolving game system.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Into the Wild Supplement
description:
This 15 card Game Supplement for Shadows of Brimstone includes new Gear cards, Darkness cards, and Norse Lands Encounter cards, as well as a brand new Adventure Card that lets you make virtually any Mission to being set on an expedition Into the Wild.

Note that although this Game Supplement comes with Norse Lands Encounter cards, they are used with the Into the Wild Adventure Card, regardless of what World the Heroes are currently in. This card pack works just as well with the Old West, Forbidden Fortress, or Valley of the Serpent Kings sets as it does with the Gates of Valhalla set.

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Rifle Bandits Enemy Pack
description: This Enemy Pack contains everything you need to fend off raids by rowdy Rifle Bandits. Included are 6 Rifle Bandit models, the large Enemy Record Sheet for them, and all of the Cards needed to add them to your games of Shadows of Brimstone.

Each player has one or more forbidden cards. The object of the game is to discover the shape, color and number of each of the forbidden cards. Players will take turns creating clues using their cards in hand to deduce a forbidden attribute, never showing a forbidden shape, number or color.

If a player must draw a card from the central deck, but there are none left, all players whose card has not been completely uncovered have lost.

Be careful, the cards disappear quickly! Will you be resourceful with your clues, or will you lack clarity and time?

—description from the designer

Through strategic moves, surround and capture enemy fungi and their mushroom king to dominate the field.

Based on the 2000+ year old Roman game “Latrunculi” however mushroom-themed with added mechanics and pieces.

—description from the publisher

Biohazard is a competitive strategy game for 1 to 4 players.

During the game, players need to manage resources to protect the world from a spreading biological pathogen. Players are assigned different areas and objectives at the beginning of the game and, by turns, perform different actions to create synergies and score points.

Easy to learn but hard to master, Biohazard brings some innovative mechanics to the euro genre by adding a discrete level of randomness that forces you to collaborate with your opponents and adapt your strategy as the game evolves.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Colosseum: Ave Titus is a new standalone game that is being built from scratch—out of pure love for the classic Colosseum. The original designers will return with the addition of Jonny Pac. Ian O'Toole will do all of the illustrations and graphic design work. The goal of the game is to bring a fresh, highly-thematic, mid-heavy gamers’ game to your table, with a familiar setting of organizing great spectacles in the Colosseum. This time the game will take place in the specific era of Emperor Titus, who celebrated the inauguration of the Colosseum in 80 AD with a series of games and spectacles that lasted for 100 days, often referred to as “The Games of Titus.” These games were a lavish display of entertainment and were intended to demonstrate the wealth, power, and generosity of the emperor. They showcased a diverse array of entertainment and were among the most significant and memorable events in the Colosseum's history.

As a master impresario, you will have the chance to carefully orchestrate all possible means that you have, to organize the most epic and glorious grand spectacles of the Colosseum. Your moment in the sun has come...

—description from the publisher

Made by the Spanish website 3DJuegos, in Gamers Quizz you can challenge your friends to victory and defeat your rivals. Not only will you have to have a little bit of luck, but you will also have to fine-tune your tactics and test your knowledge of video games. Up to 6 players can fight for victory by correctly answering more than 700 questions grouped into four categories (characters, universe, curiosities and gameplay) with themes that address both current events in the sector and the history and legacy of the industry.

boardgame: Betrayal: The Yuletide Tale – Evil Reigns in the Wynter's Pale
description: A festive-themed expansion for Betrayal at House on the Hill 3rd Edition. Adds two new Rooms, Items, and Events, and two unique characters controlled by a single player: Jingleklaws and Elf, who start the game on the Upper Landing and have unique abilities. Comes with a new Scenario card featuring 5 new Haunts based on Jingleklaws and the holidays.

Who's a Good Dog? is a deduction and memory-based board game where players take on the roles of dog enthusiasts trying to identify their opponent's hidden character card. The game revolves around the excitement of discovering and eliminating characters through strategic questioning, all while attempting to outwit the opponent.
Each player has a board with slots to place character cards facing away from the opposing player. The cards represent different dog characters. Players take turns asking alternate questions about the appearance of the opponent's chosen character. The questions help narrow down the possibilities, leading to the eventual identification of the hidden dog.
The primary objective of the game is to be the first player to correctly identify the opponent's hidden character. Players do this by asking questions that help eliminate potential options based on the appearance of the characters. The winner gains a point and marks it on the slider to keep track of the score. The game combines deduction, memory, and strategic thinking as players try to unveil the mystery dog using clever questioning and reasoning. The catch is that specific questions about dog breeds are not allowed, but questions about fur color and other attributes are permitted.

In Rank Up! the players are competing to win the most cards through a series of rounds where all players compare a statistic chosen by the round leader. The core of this game builds on the classic “war” card game that most kids already know how to play.

Design features added in this game include: 1) rolling a game-specific wooden die that will determine whether the highest or lowest stat wins the round, 2) players (usually) get to look at 2 cards each round and choose which to play, 3) a memory game element of needing to remember the cards looked at, and 4) the lead player choosing 1 of 4 stat categories for that round’s comparison rather than the simple comparison in “war” of the traditional card rank value. The winner of a round is the lead player for the next round.

Another significant difference from “war” is that this game is “once through the deck” and see who has the most, rather than the “win all the cards” victory condition of traditional “war” card games. This significantly shortens the game play and allows for a predictable playing time. The publisher says 20 minutes, although that may be a bit optimistic when playing with younger kids.

There are 100 game cards with 4 factual statistic categories on each. Also on each card is a fun fact which is not used in play of the game. Initially the publisher is releasing two versions of this game of “Amazing Animals” and “Countries of the World”.

Legends of Nara takes you on a thrilling journey where you step into the shoes of aspiring adventurers, seeking to establish your reputation and become legendary Heroes in this vast realm. With captivating RPG elements and a rich fantasy aesthetic, this game offers an unforgettable gaming experience.

On your turn, you'll have several options. You can move to explore new locations, fight formidable monsters, make a tactical retreat, or rest to regain your strength. Each action has its own consequences, so choose wisely!

As you explore, landing on a small location will present you with an event card. These cards may reveal monstrous creatures, potential companions, or other surprising encounters, adding an element of unpredictability to your journey.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Warfighter: Fantasy Expansion #1 – Lanolar: Elven Battle Mage
description:
This Warfighter Fantasy Expansion adds 48 cards and 6 Counters to your Backpack!

Lanolar uses both physical combat skills and Mana spells to overcome the forces of evil!

This expansion offers 48 cards including:

6 Player Hero cards
6 Squad Hero cards
16 Skill cards
8 Spell cards
2 Armour/Shield cards
4 Item cards
5 Action cards + 1 Location Marker

boardgame: Thunderstone Quest: Risen from the Mire
description:
This is Quest #3 for Thunderstone Quest, initially released as part of the Thunderstone Quest Base Game. In 2023 it was released as a separate expansion.

Each Quest consists of a selection of dungeon tiles and monster cards that create an engaging storyline. Each Quest includes rules for selecting Village and Dungeon cards to create a variety of Adventures. After you've mastered each Quest you use random setups of Quest cards to create new Dungeons and Villages - build your own Quests and Adventures!

Contains 6 dungeon tiles, 25 paper & plastic dividers, randomizers, and 378 cards, including 6 heroes, 6 monsters, 12 support items, treasure, 1 prestige class, 1 Guardian Board and three card versions of the Guardian, each of slightly different difficulty, meaning that different Adventures within the Quest have a scaling challenge.

Combine correctly - The little ghosts have to scare the right people in the right room. But watch out, there are night and day ghosts, which one is on the next card?
Pick-a-Boo is ideal for 2 to 4 players from 5 years. If you are the fastest to put the right spirit on the card, you will win it. Whoever then collects the most cards wins the game.
Fun learning effect - In addition to a quick comprehension, the exciting card game Pick-a-Boo also playfully trains the logical thinking and reaction time.

Deckt eine Karte auf. Bildet nun mit den Geistern und den dazugehörigen, farbigen Sockeln den richtigen Geist. Platziert euren Geist möglichst schnell auf der richtigen Spieltafel. Aber Achtung: Die Geister fliegen entweder nur nachts, oder nur am Tag. Überlegt euch also gut, auf welche Karte ihr euren Geist setzt. Wer die meisten Karten sammelt gewinnt.

Cyber-city is a cyber-punk themed game based on a map with a maze and a deck of cards.
The mechanics of the game involve exploration of the map coupled with the Flip and write game mechanic.
The aim of the game is to track down and defeat all the criminals living in the city before the time runs out.

—description from the designer

Klaisto is a Print & Play dice game for 1-4 players.

The players must try to turn all the plates into the goal to win. Only a few dice are needed. Pay attention to the event cards. They can help or harm.

Game Overview: A board game for 1 to 4 players. The goal is to use cards to get the drone to the goal point.
Game Genre: Grid movement, card draw, cooperative game.
Game Rules: Each turn, place a card and flip it simultaneously to check the number. Move the drone by comparing numbers. Success when the drone reaches the goal.
Game Components: A deck of playing cards required on the print page of the PnP file (jokers excluded). Includes areas for placing cards and moving the drone. Part of the print page can be used as a Meeple.
Game Features: You can't directly say the card numbers. The game progresses without speaking the numbers.

Everyone can download this game's Rule Book below.

English Rule Book : https://drive.google.com/file/d/1ERd9ljVPGdj0gkclpn3YnlhDMhAc3rmO/view?usp=sharing
Korean Rule Book : https://drive.google.com/file/d/1gos43Kbj7cN8rZpOP_N_y3kYnUD9zBfE/view?usp=sharing

And Print page Download here
https://drive.google.com/file/d/11Ibf1Wovwa_QyHx_YIdVgxSXGhhbeL_V/view?usp=sharing

Game Of Drone is optimized for printing on A4 paper.

—description from the designer

The Numerals appeared out of nowhere, destroying your mother ship in seconds. Your starfighter managed to launch just in time, and now you must face overwhelming odds to defeat the alien digits. You have one chance to defeat the Numeral menace using the power of mathematics itself!

D6 Starfighter is a dice manipulation and placement puzzle where you'll use the variable abilities of your starfighter to defeat enemy waves. Defeat all enemies to win!

Each turn you'll face a row of four enemies and roll five dice. Use abilities from your ship, pilot, and weapons to manipulate the enemy row and your dice so you can defeat each enemy with the exact damage values necessary. Each turn presents a new puzzle as you figure out the best path to victory!

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
The expansion pack of Game of Drone can be played as a stand-alone game.

Game Overview: The expansion pack for "Game of Drone" brings a shift from the cooperative gameplay of the base game to a competitive one. This expansion is provided as a PnP, with the base game available for free, while the expansion is offered to Patreon paid subscribers.

In this expansion, two new competitive modes are introduced: Open Field Mode and Hidden Field Mode. In Open Field Mode, players compete by placing cards and moving all players' drones. In Hidden Field Mode, players compete by hiding their movement coordinates and aiming for the highest score.

Through this expansion, Game of Drone offers players new strategic challenges and diverse play styles. Now, players can enjoy a new experience of competing with each other to move the drone to its destination.

The rulebook is available here, but the pages for printing are provided for a fee to Patreon subscribers. Below is the download link for the rulebook.

English RuleBook : https://drive.google.com/file/d/1rSJRJTEYP2g_4-Lwiqqn5o6vE245ZVDz/view?usp=sharing
Korean RuleBook : https://drive.google.com/file/d/1DV3jMkLfivPKUFFq6ia1CSy0HZpQgmSy/view?usp=sharing

And here is the link to the Patreon subscribers-only page.

https://www.patreon.com/posts/23-july-game-of-86945903?utm_medium=clipboard_copy&utm_source=copyLink&utm_campaign=postshare_creator&utm_content=join_link

—description from the designer

Angelo Alberti can be used only with The Geological Factor expansion, He can avoid the extra or replacement costs illustrated on the left part of the Geological tiles, at the cost of 1 Engineer. You can activate this effect twice in the same round by placing the extra Engineer on the XO action space (you still have to place Engineers during the main action on your turn).

If, during your turn, you decide to copy this power with Mahiri Sekibo you can avoid the extra or replacement costs of a Geological tile. You don't need to place other Engineers than the one you use to activate Mahiri Sekibo.

boardgame: Las Ligas de la Ñ: El juego de las fuerzas primigenias y existenciales
description:
Las Ligas de la Ñ: El juego de las fuerzas primigenias y existenciales ("The Ñ Leagues: The Game of the Primal and Existential Forces"), is an asymmetrical fighting board game for 2 to 12 players that includes classic characters of Spanish literature in card form, with their name, work, author, representative quote and unique powers (there are no equals). The time has come for Don Quijote, Sancho Panza, Celestina, Lazarillo, Fortunata and Jacinta, Segismundo, Bernarda Alba and others!

In this new Ñ game (the fifth in the series), players have to control one of the playable characters in order to score more points than their opponents (after summing up the points for final position and for eliminated elements). Each turn, the players use their cards or powers to maneuver, attack or defend, displace enemies and make other actions. There are several types of playable characters: some have allies, some are a pair of heroes, some undergo evolutions and transformations, and some go on their own.

The game's main source of inspiration is the Super Smash Bros. video game series and it maintains important similarities with both the Unmatched board game series and battle royale style games.

—description from the designer

For scientists from all over Europe, the time has come to move to Italy, a country rich in culture and stimulating discoveries, but you have to be careful! Time is limited and it is important to visit each place at the right time!

Newton Italy Grand Tour is an alternative map for the Newton game.

The Barrage: The Colorado Connection expansion includes a specific map set on the Colorado area providing a new mechanic: the Bridges.

The Colorado Map includes some new elements: the Construction Sites for Bridges and the Areas for Productive Settlements.

The rivers represented on the Map flow along deep canyons creating clear physical separations between the delimited regions: the presence of bridges built on the crest of the Dams will allow an easier connection between the Productive Settlements of each region; on the other hand, a Dam with a Bridge cannot be further elevated for the rest of the game. Choose carefully when to build them!

The Productive Settlements are new action spaces (4 in the Mountains Area, 3 in the Hills Area, 3 in the Plains Area) that can be activated by placing the required number of Engineers. Activating a Productive Settlement has a cost in Credits that depends on the presence or absence of Bridges along the route from the Construction Site (located on the left edge in the Mountains and Plains Areas and right in the Hills Area) to the Productive Settlement you intend to activate.

You need the base game Barrage to be able to use it. You can also use the Barrage: Leeghwater Project expansion in combination with this expansion.

—description from the rulebook

The Barrage: Duel expansion includes a new map specifically designed for 2-player games and the Duel board, which provides a new mechanic that enhances the 2-player games experience.

Barrage Duel can be played in two ways: you can simply use the new map to have more competition in 2-player challenges, or you can use the Duel board along with the other additional components. This last option changes the rules of the game!

The new map has fewer basins, and some building spaces will cost more. These spaces will change from game to game, always creating variety in this map.

The Duel board adds three conditions on which to compete with your opponent, different in each game. At the end of each round, the player who best satisfies a condition will move the relative marker to their side. Mark the opponent tightly on each condition; otherwise, they will get decisive advantages!

You need the base game Barrage to be able to use it. You can also use the Barrage: Leeghwater Project expansion in combination with this expansion.

—description from the rulebook
family: Versions & Editions: Two-Player Versions of More-Player Games

boardgame: HeroQuest: Hero Collection – Path of The Wandering Monk
description:
“Know not the path, but the reason you walk it.”

Walk in the wise Monk hero’s shoes and expand your HeroQuest adventure with the Path of the Wandering Monk figures! The graceful Monk hero is a student of unarmed combat techniques formed in reverence to the 4 elements. When you play as this hero, you lack the ability to use metal armor and shields, but you may wield daggers, crossbows, handaxes, shortswords, and staffs. This collector's edition item includes 2 highly detailed Monk figures with powers and skills not seen in HeroQuest characters. It also includes 7 game cards and a scroll.

Artisan dragons can make such wondrous things. Drop a coin at this shop to see the heavens — or take a peek at another shop. Expand your Flamecraft town with the Cosmic Scale promo shop featuring new art by Sandara Tang.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Star Wars: Legion – Strike Me Down Battle Pack
description:
Recreates the pivotal moment in Return of the Jedi when Luke Skywalker and Darth Vader duel.

Contents:
2 resin miniatures
1 throne room display
2 unit cards
6 command cards
1 token sheet
1 insert sheet

As a hamster owner in HAM-FARM, you are in charge of "running a company by hamsters for hamsters". You scout for new hamsters from the "ham farm," a recreation area on the outskirts of the company, and sometimes build useful facilities in order to create the best company for hamsters!

Hamsters have "effect icons" and "scores," and the player with the highest total score wins. However, hamsters with lower "score" are easier to scout and have stronger "effect icons". Players should also actively target "facility cards" that have a lasting effect!

Players can strengthen their decks with cards that have strong "effect icons," or they can aim for cards with high "score," or they can aim for bonuses by building lots of "facilities. The interesting point is that each player's strategy is unique!

You are a dragon, and you have realised that your cave is too cluttered and disorganised. You need help - you need kobolds!

In Too Many Kobolds, the objective is to recruit kobolds to join your cave. Kobolds have needs, and when you fulful those need, they turn from being shy to being happy! Happy kobolds give victory points at the end of the game, while shy kobolds take away points.

Kobolds come in a variety of colours, representing the various resources they bring to your cave. Kobolds can help satisfy the needs of other kobolds nearby, and in doing so can help each other become happy too!

The game ends when all players have completed their cave - a 3 x 5 tableau of cards. When the last player has completed their cave, each player counts up the number of victory points indicated by their kobold cards. After checking for bonuses, the dragon with the highest victory points - the happiest cave - is the winner!

The dragons are all fired up to have a picnic at the lake and see their feathered friends. Expand your Flamecraft town with the Lucky Lake promo shop in participation with Lucky Duck Games and featuring new art by Sandara Tang.

—description from the publisher

Cooperate is an all-or-nothing team game in which you must predict - in 30 brief Seconds – how many words your team will shout out that meet the Cooperate card challenge. A soon as you have predicted your score turn the timer again and you’re off for another 30 seconds to actually shout out the predicted Number of words – but if you don’t reach that number you score Nothing.

—description from the publisher

Players place shields on a 4x4 grid. If the center of the shield or the wood of the shield make a line of three in a row of the same color then those shields are collected for points. Play continues until all 72 shields are used.

—user summary

Do you like soda? Drink it in moderation, and don't exceed the maximum intake of 13g of sugar! Bluff your way through to avoid getting caught, as some Soda cards aren't really the drinks they appear to be.

The last player to drink a soda starts the game. On his turn, the first player draws a card and adds it to his hand. He then chooses a Soda card from his hand and places it on the table in front of him, either face-down or face-up, around the Scale:

Face down: bluff. The player does not mention the actual value except to himself.

Face up: power activation. The player announces the actual value of the card and immediately activates the power indicated on it, if it has one.

Before drawing a card, the player whose turn it is can say "Too much sugar!" The previous player then turns over the Soda cards lying around the Scale. If the sum of the cards exceeds 13g, he loses the round. Otherwise, the player who said "Too much sugar!" loses the round. All players except the player who lost the round win the cards placed in front of them.

A new round begins. When the deck is exhausted, players end the game. Each player adds up the actual values of the cards they have won.

—description from the designer

Comrade Gov’nor, welcome to this game of lies, deceit, corruption and murder. You are to navigate the murky waters of the upper-echelon of the Russian policy while ruling over your oblasts. You start your political journey under the watchful eye of Moscow oblast - which is a Non-Player-Character. 
You will have access to riches beyond your wildest dreams, directly out of the bosom of Mother Russia: timber, oil, steel, gold, diamonds and, of course… potato. Spend them wisely to acquire power, because yachts are an impoundable illusion. You will gain resources in each turn, but half of everything goes to Moscow. Play your hand close to the chest and take maximum advantage of both alliances and traitors. When the time is right, you will be able to declare your independence and from then on, ALL of the riches stay in your regional budget. Moscow, of course, will try to ”denazify” you and ”liberate” your province from under your rule as soon as possible - so choose your timing wisely.

—description from the publisher

Snowblind II is the second edition of Snowblind: Race to the Pole, featuring renovated and an updated version of the original game, as well as featuring an entirely new, second adventure game, on the reverse side of all the components.

La guerre de Jugurtha (also known as "the Jugurthine's war") is a strategical game covering the war fought by the king of Numidia, Jugurtha, against the Republic of Rome. 
Jugurtha met initially successes against poor roman leaders and a poorly trained roman army (-111 to -110), but had later to cope first with the aristocratic general Quintus Metellus (-109 to -108) and then with the famous Caius Marius, head of the Populares party (-107 to -105).
Finally, he was betrayed by his father-in-law, king Bocchus of Mauretania and fell in roman hands.

La guerre de Jugurtha is published in Vae Victis 170.

One turn = around 2 months
One counter = around 4000 infantrymen or 2000 cavalrymen
Complexity: 4/10
Solo playability: 4/10
Duration: 2 to 3 hours

Red Riding Hood is a game of decisions, chances, and luck!

Red Riding Hood defeats the Wolf...who defeats the Wood...who defeats Red Riding Hood...but perhaps to score points and make winning combinations, you'll have to lose some challenges.

A trick-taking game for all ages in which you always take, but sometimes you must take what your opponent wants! Will you use your cards to score points or to form valuable combinations? And will you play to win right away or to benefit from the mysterious "fog" card?
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Little Red Riding Hood

description:
The Sirius System... A system brimming with life and conflict...

Four factions struggle between planets, space stations, and moons for dominance. Stuck in the middle are the independent ship owners, like you - the Dog Star Shippers!

Dog Star Shippers is a one-to-two player game of risky outer space encounters. Earn credits, buy the best ship, discover new bases, and complete dangerous missions. Maximize your Valor to become Top Dog of the Sirius System! Woof.

Start with a small ship and, with skills and a little luck, buy the best ship and discover incredible new bases!

Play against a friend or challenge your abilities against Two Solo Opponents of different difficulties.

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Games with expansions that change player count

description:
The Time Trigger is the second expansion for Adventure Realms.

In The Time Trigger, players will need to travel back into time to collect the Time Stone. There are also new ways of gaining Victory Points and Treasures. Speaking of which, Treasures have been completely re-balanced. Treasures are now placed and LEFT in front of a player given the player new abilities during their turn. These can be very powerful just so long as you meet the requirements to wield them.

There are also new types of cards. Random Encounters are monster cards that get shuffled into the Guild Deck and may filter into the Guild Row. These can be Defeated (recruited), the player will gain the effect, then the Monster is banished. Elemental Techs are a new type of card that will give players more to do with their Elemental Stones. Once attained, the player may (as a free action) socket their gem to gain a powerful one time effect.

Travel through time is the newest and biggest part in the game. Each player will move their vessels through the Overworld collecting time shards. What dor you do with the time shards? Travel through time of course! At the end of the Passing Phase, players may then travel through time to different Eras. Each Era will have one time powerful abilities or effects that can grant players victory points, new heroes, or even the Time Stone!

There is now the new SOLO MODE! The player will face off against the Shadow who is trying to collect points, the elemental stones, and spread Darkness throughout the Realms. The Solo Mode is extremely low upkeep while also being quite difficult to beat.

—description from the publisher

A card based party game for people who like to debate why they should be the winner!
In standard play, a judge draws a prompt card.  Players take turns playing a character card with a modifier card and pitching to the judge why they are the clear winner.  Other players are encouraged to voice their own opinions at any time.  The judge will select a winner and the player is awarded a point!
To add add more fun there are additional ways to play, such as drawing an additional random modifier from the top of the modifier deck as you make your pitch.
In arena mode, you face off against a single opponent and the rest of the table votes on the winner!

—description from the publisher

Black Sea Marines is a Panzer Grenadier Campaign Study, with 11 scenarios based on the land battles of the Black Sea Fleet Marines in the dark days of 1941 against German and Romanian invaders. There are two chapters, each with a battle game to link the scenarios together. You’ll need Panzer Grenadier: Fire in the Steppe, Armata Romana and River Battleships to play all of the scenarios.

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
A quick card game where players are spies who make words out of signals they intercept. Players make words by getting the best cards, but beware for stealing spies!

Players can swap a consonant for a vowel, and try to make words with the 4 cards they have every turn. Players are able to change words, or steal letters from each other.

The game ends when there are no cards left to draw. The players count the cards they have and withdraw points for cards they cant make words with.

—description from the designer

The annual All-Valley Karate Tournament is about to begin and the pressure is on! As a Sensei, you must train your students before the tournament begins. Each Fight your students win takes you one step closer to ultimate victory. Draft the best cards to help your students and claim the Grand Champion trophy.

Players draft cards: both training and flashback cards to build up their fighters skills for the tournament phase. The cards they draft affect their fighters speed, dice rolled in the fights and overall ability to sustain damage/defend against their rivals.

During the fight phase, players face off rolling dice and using their drafted skills to advance in a tournament structure to be the top fighter overall.

Hope or Despair - Aim to escape from the academy by choosing fate!

A simple reasoning game based on the very popular game Love Letter, which has received many board game awards. Co-designed with Danganronpa game designer Kanaye-Ishii, enjoy the combination of Love Letter and Danganronpa!

Each of the 20 characters taken from the Danganronpa: Trigger Happy Havoc videogame has a Hope and a Despair ability, and is associated with one of those two fates. The Hope abilities are identical to the original love letter, allowing you to play with classic love letter rules.

Playing the Monokuma mode - named after the key villain from the video game - players first set up an active fate for the game by randomly choosing between Fate and Despair. Characters played trigger their abilities based on the active fate. However, once per game when a player discards a card with an opposing fate to the active one, they then use that new fate for the rest of the game.

Use the combination of Hope and Despair abilities to aim for victory and outlast your opponents!

Immerse yourself in the world of video game development as you lead your studio in the creation of the next gaming sensation - or an overhyped flop.

"Pixels and Polygons" is an original take on the worker placement mechanic, featuring modular hex tracks that vary in every game. Workers, or developers, can compete and move on these tracks to collect specialized bonuses for your game in development, such as genres, fans, and features. Researching the tracks can increase the maximum range to which the developers can move, representing your strategic commitment.

Cards are played in games to add specific features with special effects, unlocking the true potential of your projects. Released games are scored based on specific trend requirements and then compete on the hot list for reputation and revenue.

Gear up for launch! Will you become a legendary game creator or a heartless profit-chaser, exploring every dark aspect of game development?

—description from the publisher

Qwadro is a roll and write game for 2-4 players, 10+ years, playing time about 20 minutes.

On his turn, the active player rolls 4 dice and is entitled to 1 re-roll.
All players then write the sum of 2 dice on their player sheets.
The colors of the dice determine the row or column in which the number is entered.. The numbers must be entered next to already entered numbers.
The active player may enter a 2nd number as well, but must use the 2 dice that he did not use in the 1st entry.
Numbers in rows and columns must go from lowest to highest - left to right and top to bottom..
If a player has 4 of the same numbers orthogonally connected, he gets bonus points and may no longer enter that number.
If a player cannot enter a number, he gets negative points.
The player with the most points wins.

—description from the designer

A promotional pack initially offered with pre-orders of the Ex Libris: Expanded Archives expansion, comprising 6 "bookend" artifact cards & a rules card.
Note that this promo pack is not usable without the Expanded Archive expansion.

Ankhitects is a tile laying pyramid building game for 2 players, ages 10+, playing time 15-20 minutes.

The players are rival architects in ancient Egypt who have been tasked by the Pharaoh with creating blueprints for the construction of the Pharaoh's pyramid. 
They will try to do this by placing polyomino tiles on their own player boards in order to connect as many groups as possible of the same elements - air, water, fire and gold.
The player who scores the most points at the end of the game wins.

—description from the designer

You and your fellow comrades are going down into forgotten dungeons full of loot to collect. Avoid cursed treasures and fearless ghosts in your way throughout dungeons. Be quick and be smart while collecting treasures. Prove your scavenging skill and become the richest adventurer of all times.

Loot&Roll  is print and play game for one to four players. Your goal is to collect treasure and score the most points. But don't be too greedy. Each treasure has its own value that changes through the game. So, pick the right time to collect each treasure type. Overtake your opponents and collect the most valuable loot.

—description from the designer

Operation VAULT is a dice drafting, dial manipulation board game for 2-4 players, ages 10+, playing time 30-60 min.

The criminal organization "MEDUSA" is planning the biggest
bank heist in history – Operation VAULT.
The crew is set, only member missing is a safecracker.
That's why they have invited the best safecrackers to a competition.
The fastest player to crack 7 combinations will get the job and be the winner of the game.

—description form the designer

This mini-expansion for Aleph Null contains four new Interference cards at Level 4.

You can add them to the choice of Interference cards on the Circle of Master, Grand Master, or Prince but beware: they are highly punishing!

—description from the publisher

Turn up the volume at your next party with this hilarious game of raunchy setups and some of the internet’s most viral audio clips. Draw a setup card and have all players play their best reply with QR code facing up. Use the free The Audio Game app to scan the cards and reveal the hilarious sounds. For more laughs, we encourage you to play with 2 or more people. Spend the night competing with friends and family to see who can create the funniest combinations

A trick-taking game based on the 'Dick Tracy' comic strip. The 35-card deck is divided into three "Gang" and three "Detective" suits, each of a different color. The Detective suits are numbered 1-5, while the Gang suits are numbered 1-6. There is also one card each of the Gang Boss and Dick Tracy, each worth 15 points.

Object of the game is to take as few tricks of Gang suits as possible, while avoiding the Gang Boss and trying to get the Dick Tracy card.

The Dealer starts by dealing a "Mystery" hand face down in front of him, which will remain until the final trick has been played. Dealer then deals six cards to each player, three at a time. Each player looks at his cards and discards two. This continues until only the Mystery cards remain undealt.

Player to the Dealer's left leads by playing a card, then each player in turn must play a card of the same color (suit) if possible, otherwise he may discard any card. Highest suited card wins the trick. A player who cannot follow suit may play the Dick Tracy card on any Detective suit and win the trick; likewise the Gang Boss card wins any Gang suit trick. The player who wins the trick leads the next one. If a player leads with either the Dick Tracy or Gang Boss card, players may discard any card in their hand.

The player who wins the last trick collects the Mystery cards. Players then count all the Gang cards in their possession and score two points for each one. The player holding the Gang Boss card adds fifteen to their score, while the player holding the Dick Tracy card subtracts fifteen. Game ends when one player reaches or exceeds one hundred points, and the person with the lowest score wins.

—user summary

A cooperative game for three or more players. One person acts as the "Game Master", who sets up the puzzles but does not participate in solving them. The object of the game is to solve a set of four puzzles, each of which reveals to the players one of the symbols needed to unlock the combination lock. The players have only one hour to find the solution.

The combination lock has four rotating wheels, each with four different symbols. Before the game starts, the Game Master sets the combination to open the padlock, and chooses the puzzle for each wheel to give the correct answer. Once the game starts, the Game Master has the option of giving the players clues if they are struggling with the puzzles.

—user summary

Two players or teams compete to answer questions about each state (capitol city, flower, bird, nickname), marking correct answers by placing a colored magnet -- either red or blue -- on the state in question on the map printed on the inside of the metal travel case.

Players need only answer one of the four facts to place a magnet. If they are able to answer more than one question, they may continue playing until they make a mistake or cannot answer. If a player cannot answer any of the questions on the card, it is placed on the bottom of the draw stack, and play changes to the opposing player or team.

The winner is the player or team with the most magnets on the map once all the cards have been used.

—user summary

boardgame: Spy Code: Ultimate Operation – Escape Room
description:
Bring the excitement of the Escape Room Challenges into your home with Spy Code Ultimate Operation Escape Room. This interactive family game tests your spy skills by challenging your team to work together to solve 7 exciting tasks in order to set the captured player free from the Timer Strap before time runs out! The game challenges your skill, strategy, luck, dexterity, deduction, and knowledge in various levels of difficulty over and over again.

Spy Code Ultimate Operation Escape Room features seven challenges to test your skill, knowledge, and strategy--Challenge 1: Break Free from the Handcuffs Challenge; 2: Fiddle the Key Out of the Cage Challenge; 3: Solve the 3 Puzzles to Get the Key Challenge; 4: Balance the Ping Pong Ball Challenge; 5: Guide the Marble Through the Maze Challenge; 6: Create the Path for the Marble Challenge; and 7: Find the Key Challenge.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Spy Code: Operation – Escape Lab Disaster
description:
