Spy Code - Operation: Escape Lab Disaster offers another batch of Escape Room Challenges you can play at home. This interactive family game tests your spy skills by challenging your team to work together to solve exciting tasks in order to find the keys and stop the Chemical Lab Timer before time runs out! The game challenges your skill, luck, and talent in your quest to prevent a terrible lab disaster.

—description from the publisher

Spy Code - Operation: Escape Lab Disaster features three challenging puzzles--Challenge 1: Run Through the Test Tube Maze; 2: Master the Atomic Clock Spinner; and 3: Solve 3 Puzzles and Get the Key.
Expansion for Trivial Pursuit with 500 new question cards.

Qubik is a combination of the classic Tic-Tac-Toe and the Rubik’s Cube that makes a new, challenging, and fun game for people of all ages. The rules are as simple as taking turns to place a cube into a 3x3x3 space until a player creates a straight line of one color to win.
Qubik can be played by 1-7 players. A typical game of Qubik takes around 5-15 minutes. There are 3 modes of play available for you to enjoy: Casual, Competitive, and Solo.

THE DUST is a rapid duel between two players or two teams.
It happens in a manga-styled world. Players choose their weapons and accessories to use in the battle. These items provide different abilities and styles. Many items have two ways to use. Switching the way at the right time can be vital to win the duel. Some items provide powerful one-time use ability but require dice to be consumed to charge it.
Every turn, players manipulate dice to trigger skills and perform attacks on their opponents. Whoever knocks down his or her opponent first is the winner.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
This is the first stand-alone expansion to The Dust: Empire, a rapid battle game between two players or two teams. This expansion adds new artifact to choose from and tactic cards to the base game.
It happens in a manga-styled world. Players choose their weapons and accessories to use in the battle. Every turn, players manipulate dice to trigger skills and perform attacks on their opponents. Whoever knocks down his or her opponent first is the winner.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: King of Glory: The Card Game
description:
King of Glory: The Card Game consists of a 54 card deck, with each card numbered 1-54. Each card depicts a different scene from the book, KING of GLORY. In the game you are attempting to play cards sequentially and make connections between cards to score points.

On your turn draw 2 cards either from the top of the deck or the  discard area. You may then play either both cards, one of the cards, or none of the cards. Each card you play will score 1 point at the end of the game. So why wouldn't you always play both cards you might ask?

Because the one rule when playing cards is the card must be lower than all your previously played cards, or higher than all of them. It can't be between any of them.

There is one exception. If a card you want to play has a symbol on it that matches a symbol on a card you've already played, you may ignore the one placement rule. Simply place the card in your played cards row so that all your cards remain in ascending order. At the end of the game you get 2 points for each symbol match.

The game is based on the book KING of GLORY. You do not need to be familiar with the book to play the game, but those that are will enjoy identifying the thematic symbol connections between different cards.

Anunnaki: Trade with Atlantis promo contains a set of 4 Commercial Pacts tiles with the Gaia icon.

You may fulfill a Commercial Pact with Atlantis if you control at least 1 Territory of it (the center of the Gaia board). This will give you the some rewards: to summon a Minor God, take an Artifact card, or even move your Action pawn to get an additional action.

boardgame: Rats of Wistar: Guest Mice Set
description:
A promo set of 4 Guest tiles.

Explore the map in order to find guests to invite to your lair.

boardgame: Rats of Wistar: Objective Cards Set
description: A promo set of extra 3 Objective cards.

Welcome to Pargate, a mysterious village hidden within a lush forest.

Here, you will encounter a plethora of unique characters and wondrous creatures, who... who... well, who will do what you decide!

In The Chronicles of Pargate, you are the one creating the story.

Start with a basic storyline structure, or mix and match the ones provided, or even create your own from scratch, and envision - alone or with others - a story full of twists, unexpected events, and laughter...
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: もしも勇者がいるのなら (Moshimo Yusha ga Iru no Nara / If there is a hero)
description:
Defeat the unreasonable royalty!　A new SATSUGAI (murder) -style card game!
You only play one card at a time, but the game development changes every time.
Will you rob with the royalty, stop with the heroes, or laugh with the ...... clowns?
Make full use of 7 different characters to achieve the victory conditions!

This game is designed to be "easy to play", "moderately strategic" and "flashy game development".
Only 7 types of character cards and 3 types of chips are used.

All you have to do is "select a card in your hand and reveal it.

Depending on the effect of the revealed card, you gain or lose chips.
After you have done this four times, the game ends when you reveal the last card in your hand.
You win if the last card in your hand satisfies the win condition.

All you have to do is reveal your cards four times and fulfill the victory condition at the end."

That's all there is to it, but it doesn't go very well.

The game "If there is a hero" is a recreation of such a slapstick game.

boardgame: Black Powder: Epic Battles – The Hundred Days
description:
Napoleon Bonaparte, the Corsican Tyrant, plunged Europe back into war once again by leading an emboldened French army against a resolute England and her allies. The Hundred Days campaign raged, witnessing numerous battles both before and after that decisive day at Waterloo.

Authored by Adrian McWalter (Albion Triumphant Volumes 1 & 2; A Clash of Eagles), this supplement for Black Powder Epic Battles presents 18 new scenarios covering the entire breadth of the campaign. Alongside the new units, rules, ‘what if?’ scenarios, and commanders is a system to link all your games in a grand campaign that follows the events of the Hundred Days, one battle at a time.

Finally, full orders of battle are presented for all the French, Prussian, English and Allied forces involved in the fighting.
The entire panoply of the Hundred Days awaits within.

—description from publisher

A fast paced, action, battle royale game where you face off against 1 to 3 opponents incorporating characters from the Monogatari series and power them up using any standard 54 deck of playing cards! Activate special skills and be the last one standing to win!

Players take turns drafting heroes (from the 17 character cards included in the box) that they will use to battle their opponents. Each player shuffles a 54 card standard playing card deck (sold separately) and draws a starting hand equal to the value printed on their current character. They also draw cards equal to that character's LIFE value. Players will then pick one card from their hand to represent their starting POWER (1-10 cards have power equivalent to their numbers, while Jacks, Queens and Kings equal a power value of 11,12 and 13 respectively).

Players then take turns playing different suits and numbered cards in specific combinations to activate their character's own unique battle skills. These skills will either allow you to charge up your current battle power value even further, increase the rate of damage dealt to losing players at the end of the round, or any number of special abilities (like drawing more cards or dealing instant damage to an opponent).

Once all players PASS, everyone counts up who has the current highest power value. That player wins that round, dealing damage to every other player, equivalent to the current rate of damage! Those players must discard life cards equal to the damage rate. If a hero loses all of it's health, that player must switch to one of their reserve heroes. When all of your opponents heroes have been reduced to 0 health, you win the game!

Sand Stacking is a pocket-sized dexterity game for 1 or more players that rewards precision and patience. To be successful, you will need a steady hand, an eye for balance and nerves of steel.

Stack tiny metallic cubes as high as you can (or as fast as you can) and see who comes out on top. You can build with either large (4mm) or small (2mm) cubes.

In the standard head-to-head game, you can go against any number of opponents, taking turns to create one tower - with the player who knocks the tower over as the loser. You can also play as a co-operative team to get as high as you can!

Alternatively, you can attempt to set records by building one tower on your own (official records are kept on sandstacking.com).

For most, Sand Stacking is a fun party game. For others, it can actually be a tool to help with meditation and focus. The game requires such a degree of concentration that meditation can actually be a part of the strategy!

One thing is for certain - sand stacking is for everyone!

…except kids under 12.

—description from the designer

Set during the time of Cities of Venus, Survivors continues the story with the search for survivors on the surface by the brave mining mech operators.

The object of this stand-alone polyomino-dice manipulation game is to find as many survivors as possible while discovering fuel for the re-ascent vehicle. Once the launch sequence is initiated, players utilize the resources and engineers that they had rescued to secure their place as the captain, and hero, of the re-ascent ship.

The winner is the player who has the best dice combination at the end of the Launch Sequence.

—description from the publisher

NOH combines strategy and art, bringing the magic of traditional Japanese mask theater to your gaming table. In NOH, players take on the role of leaders of a troupe of Japanese Noh theater artists during the Edo period, where they are invited to present the most beautiful and harmonious sequences of performances at a festival celebrating the birthday of Shogun Ieyasu Tokugawa, a great enthusiast of Noh theater. In NOH, players will compete against each other to determine which theater company will gain the most prestige among the nobles in feudal Japan, ultimately becoming sponsored by the Shogun himself.

An elegant card game combined with the use of custom dice, featuring the exquisite illustrations of one of the greatest masters of Japanese woodblock printing: Tsukioka Kōgyo (1869-1927).

boardgame: Black Rose Wars: Dungeonology Rocca Civetta Promo Cards
description:
This set of four promo cards for Black Rose Wars was included in the Dungeonology Rocca Civetta SG box

Basilisk Beast Promo Card:
2 Movement
3 Attack
8 Health

Nina Manticore Beast promo card:
Move 2, Attack 2
If Nina is in your room, her attack becomes 4.

Andromeda Scholar Construct promo card:
2 Movement
0 Attack
3 Health

Petrifier Promo Card:
Summon Basilisk in the Target Room. It activates immediately.

boardgame: Sine Tempore: Dungeonology Rocca Civetta Promo Cards
description:
Theses promo cards were included in the Dungeonology Rocca Civetta SG box.

Chrono's Crown Accessory Promo Card:
Psionic
3AP RNG:4 - Once for Mission you can target another Hero.
Move the Target Activation Token up to 5 Sectors backward on the Momentum.
The chosen Activation Token cannot be placed before the Sector where the Activation Token of the owner of this Equipment is located.

Yocca Promo Card:
Yocca can move using his master's AP with the Runner Skill.
Yocca can end his movement in a Square occupied by an Enemy (Troop).
If an Enemy starts their turn in the same square as Yocca, the enemy gains a stun token.
When his Master kills an Enemy (Troop)in the same Square as Yocca, he automatically gets a Resource card.

The game simulates the battle of Arsuf that took place on 7 September 1191, which was during the Third Crusade. During this battle, a larger army of the Ayyubid Sultanate, led by Saladin, was defeated by Crusaders, led by Richard I of England.

A tactical skirmish game for 2 or more players. In this competitive wargame, the heroes of Firekeep battle the diabolical forces of Icepeak for domination over the realms of men and beast, bringing Frank Frazetta's unfettered primal imagination.

Also includes rules-lite mechanics for continuing the adventure through narrative-driven roleplay.

—description from the publisher

Exact revenge against your clones in a biopunk, body horror setting.

In Personal Vendetta, each player is a clone fighting for revenge against their duplicates. Cards and game actions reflect the physical and psychological toll of confronting your own worst enemy: yourself. Attack your foes to inflict damage, react to their schemes to turn the tides of battle, and modify your state to improve your position over time.

The game takes place in a world where biology and technology entwine: machines are organisms, and organisms are machines. At the center of all innovation lies cerebrium, the fundamental cells that compose all brains and computational devices alike. In a culture of quick fixes through elective brain surgery, will you improve yourself by sculpting your mind into a new, better form? Or will you carve away the cerebrium that holds you back?

Personal Vendetta is a competitive, high-conflict, drafting and hand management card game for TCG admirers, comic book fans, and junkies for painful decisions. With too many options and too little time, you must strategically alternate between drafting, playing, and activating cards on your turns. Most tabletop games idealize a journey of increasing numbers, options, and power through a gradual drip-feed of increasing resources, but here the majority of your resources are present at the start of each game, making more options available from the outset, while forcing you to weigh each cost heavily. The result is a frenetic race to the bottom, where the goal is not so much about winning or even surviving, but about being the last to die.

boardgame: War of the Ring: The Card Game – Fire and Swords
description:
This second expansion for Ian Brody's War of the Ring: The Card Game introduces new scenarios to the base game, including one that allows to play with up to six people.

In addition to that, War of the Ring: The Card Game – Fire and Swords features 60 faction cards, 9 new paths, 9 new battlegrounds, and 13 skirmishes, a new type of battleground to represent the smaller settlements and fortifications of the War of the Ring, places like the Fords of Isen and Bree. This expansion adds three new factions to the game: Northmen for the Free Peoples; and Easterlings and Ruffians for the Shadow.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Series: War of the Ring (Nexus/Ares Games)

description:
Saifu Aisu is a game about vowel patterns in Japanese words. The game includes two decks of cards, with each card showing a different three syllable word.

One of the two decks of cards are turned face up on the table.  Then, a card is revealed from the other deck and read aloud. The players race to find which of the revealed cards has the same vowel pattern.

As an example, if the revealed card is "Saifu" (さいふ, sa-i-fu), a matching card would be "Aisu" (アイス, a-i-su).

The winner is the player who find the most correct matches.

This game can be played cooperatively or competitively. Either way, the procedure is generally the same. The "Amendment" cards contain the text of the first ten amendments to the United States Constitution. These should be read aloud to the players beforehand to familiarize themselves with the purpose of each amendment. The "case" cards are shuffled and placed in a stack face-down on the board. Each Case card contains a dilemma relating to one of the amendments, and asks a question about the actions of the people described.

Team/Player 1 draws the top card and reads the case to Team/Player 2. Team 2 answers either "Yes" or "No". Older children are encouraged to name the amendment referenced, and explain their answer based on its text. If their answer is correct, they place one of the provided horse tokens in their "corral" on the board. If their answer is incorrect, no token is awarded. Then Team 2 draws and reads a case card to Team 1, following the same procedure. The first Team to reach ten horse tokens is the winner.

Cooperative play follows the same steps given above, with the Team/Player attempting to accumulate ten horse tokens.

Toritiba is a trick-taking that lasts one trick, and to which each player contributes 5 cards. It is themed around a horse race, though some of the animals are zebras or deer.

Cards can be played face up as an animal to enter the race or face down to set the conditions of the trick, such as trump suits and are high or low cards stronger.

The highest ranked card played to the trick earns the player 3 points, second place 2, and third place 1. It is suggested, as a variant, that players could play more than one hand. Additionally, if a player finishes 1st, 2nd, and 3rd, it is an auto-victory.

Year 22XX. Two centuries of fierce competition in the delivery industry have encouraged massive strides in Earth's transportation system. Private ownership of trucks, ships, airplanes, and even spacecraft are now common.

Humanity can now pass the Milky Way, far into space, and reach their destination in the timeliest of manners. We have reached civilizations innumerable. Many of them desire to make use of Earth's delivery technology and efficient fuels. As a result, a deluge of orders has been sent to the corporations of Earth, and the age of rocket deliveries begins. There is opportunity for all, but only one can succeed.

In Venture, you must manage your corporation while competing against others for deliveries. In each of three rounds, players need to collect resources, hire personnel, upgrade their rocket, take orders, and make investments, after which they launch into space, moving to various planets to make deliveries, hire alien employees, and more before returning to Earth.

The Red Mask and her friends race trough the city to catch the villain Wolfgang. The 2-4 players take on the role of one of the superheroes. They have use their powers to pass obstacles. The fastest of the superhero win, unless they all run out of power. In that case Wolfgang get away and the players all lose.

The players roll dice to move trough a city of round chips. The chips make up a randomized path. When hitting a chip with an obstacle the player has to pick a card with a superpower. If the card match the obstacle the player can pass and the card is discarded, otherwise it's a stop and the card is turned face down. In that way the cards add an element of memory to the game.

Play this fun game with the whole family and help kids develop their planning and thinking skills. Lay down the correct cards based on the natural order and remember to use your action cards. The first player to discard their cards three times wins The Golden Egg and the game!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Mini Escapes: Last Journey to Antarctica
description:
Emily Mound is on the hunt again, but this time she must do so under extreme conditions, among the desolate glaciers of Antarctica. An alien mystery is hiding, waiting to be revealed....

Mini Escapes: Ultimo Viaggio in Antartide is the third episode of Mini Escapes and contains 3 foldable Escape rooms of 45-60 minutes each. They are playable individually, but all of them together will immerse you in a compelling story! Check the difficulty of the scenarios on the back of the box, gather with your group of friends, and scan the QR code to start your game.

Each Mini Escapes is structured around a large "folded" sheet that fits in a pocket, to be carried and played anywhere! Open it following the instructions, with the helpful app at hand, and get the wheels turning!

Important: You need to use your smartphone, PC or tablet to play Mini Escapes.

–description from publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Stand up. Form a circle.
Shuffle your 12 cards.

Everybody will at the same time look at their top card, shout out its name and show the move on the card. All the while looking out for other players doing the same.

When you have eye contact with another player doing the same move, you both drop the card and do the same with the next card.

First player to drop their last card wins.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Chick Maze is a maze and write game where you take the place of an angry chick who explores a double-sided map depicting a farmyard and the underground, to look for the Hen and bring her back to the chicken coop. As you explore, you will find items that can help you overcome obstacles, and you will also encounter enemy creatures to defeat.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Send It!: The Snowsports Board Game
description:
A snowsports board game complete with skiing/riding, crashing, skill building and, of course...SENDING IT!

SEND IT! is for those times you want to hit the slopes, but can’t — maybe it’s the middle of summer, maybe you’re waiting for your next vacation or maybe you’re just tired from a day on the mountain — because no matter what, even when you can’t ski/ride, you should be able to SEND IT!

On each turn, riders choose two actions, which can be the same or different, from the following options:

     Ski/Ride - roll the ski/ride die and move 1-3 spaces along the singletrack.
     Train - roll the two training dice to gain the skill points you need to send the next feature.
     Prepare - draw a Prepare card to gain points (which help you recover from a crash and send the next feature) or special skills.
     SEND IT - if you are on a run and have the necessary skills, play Prepare card points and roll the 20-sided die to try to send it and pass on to more difficult trails.


Just like real snowsports, crashing can happen in a variety of ways - you can crash while training, while skiing/riding or while sending it - and until you recover from your crash you can't do anything but prepare, giving the other skiers/riders the chance to get ahead.

The first skier/rider to send the final run (the "Hall of Fame") and reach the Golden Hot Chocolate wins the game.

20 NEW Scenario discs for Tiwanaku.
These are long scenarios only (45 spaces).

Stand-alone (combinable) sequel for Psychic Links.


It's nice to play a round of golf on a brand new course. But if it's a new course on every stroke, you know the psychics must be hitting the link again.

Players first take turns to draw cards and place them building the course (until the hole card is placed). Each player starts with his token (ball) on a solid green square of the starting card.

Players will then take turns making shots (shown on the top of each card). To make a shot, choose any of the four face up options and move the ball as shown in the diagram. You may not choose the same option twice in a row. Sand and slope squares affect your shot. If the shoot includes an action, for example modifying the course, perform it as stated.

The first player to end a shot with the ball exactly in the hole wins the game.

—description from the publisher

Breakfast Shy postcard game, include in May 2023 Board Game of the Month Club Postcard package.

Very light game rules, a game that could be on the back of a cereal box!


Fill in squares with icons to form contiguous groups. The bigger the groups of your icon, the more points you’ll score.

Each player chooses one of the 4 icons.

Players take turns adding icons to the board. On your turn, draw your icon on any one unfilled square. Then, draw another player’s icon on any other unfilled square. Play passes to the next player.

Once all squares have an icon, the game ends and each player scores points for contiguous groups of their icon. The player with the highest total score wins!

In Ciudad de 15 Minutos (15 Minutes City), you are going to design a city focused on its inhabitants’ well-being and accessibility. You will develop VRT lines, bike paths, pedestrian zones, and railroads. You will help your citizens move around in a safe and sustainable way to their destinations, so they are not stuck in traffic for hours to access their necessities.

Ciudad de 15 Minutos is a short and fun game where you will be choosing cards in a closed draft, and build little by little the city of your dreams. Each player will only be able to place 24 cards during the game, so choose wisely how to design your city’s mobility.

Capture enemy discs by moving your own disc onto a stack of exactly the same height. Connect sides of the board to win.

You can play Adere on different boards, such as a standard triangular "Y" board or a rhombus "Hex" board.

Acknowledgements: The capturing mechanism was used previously in Escabel by Luis Bolaños Mures, and is also used in Stairs by Kanare Kato. Adere was inspired by Knightvision by Christian Freeling, a prior connection game with a capturing mechanism. Andy Juell and Dale Walton contributed significantly to playtesting.

As the dawn breaks on a new era of Cards Against Humanity, an enormous new expansion emerges on the horizon. Bathing the world in its glorious light is the all-new Hot Box, featuring 300 scorching cards to set your deck of Cards Against Humanity ON FIRE!

300 fresh cards to spice up your deck of Cards Against Humanity.
Brand new for 2023.
Features a massive image of the sun.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Cards Against Humanity: Culture Wars Pack
description:
Every corner of American media is making huge profits fanning the flames of outrage, and Cards Against Humanity wants in on that action! Here comes the Culture Wars Pack.

30 all-new cards about the most important issues that America is totally incapable of having a productive conversation about.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Cards Against Humanity: Family Edition – School Sucks Pack
Cards Against Humanity: Family Edition – Smarty Pants Pack

description:
Chrono Crawler: Time Crystal Quest is a single-player dungeon crawler, consisting of only one card and no other components. It is played using a new mechanic that uses time to modify and vary the game, while leaving ample decision-making space during the game.

The player plays an adventurer who must explore a dangerous dungeon in search of 3 special crystals. The dungeon is made up of several rooms, each of which contains may contain monsters, traps or treasures.

The game is difficult and challenging, but also very rewarding. You have to use your skills and intelligence to overcome obstacles and reach the crystals. Chrono Crawler: Time Crystal Quest offers a unique and immersive experience, which is suitable for all types of players.

—description from the publisher

Create your own hockey team and compete against your friends. With new cards coming out every year, you can replicate any action or situation from hockey. It is as fun and exciting as hockey. Score, Save, Fight, and bodycheck. All this and more are available in Breakaway:CCG

—description from the publisher

In this fast-paced game, aim for your faction, build your forces and claim Grogu! Players split into two teams -- The Mandalorian vs. the Galactic Empire -- and each take a character disc that matches their allegiance. After placing Grogu in the centre of the table with 15 Ally cards spread a good distance around it, each player then takes it in turn to throw their character disc to land on and win Ally cards! The aim is to collect enough points to then aim for and land on Grogu, with the first player to do so being the winner.

A quick to learn and easy to play game that's perfect for The Mandalorian fans, it comes in a Mandalorian helmet case and is suitable for ages 8+, 2-6 players and has an average gameplay of 10 minutes.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Sagrada: Promo – Gen Con 2023 Window Pattern Card
description: Foil promo card given out at the Floodgate Games booth during GenCon 2023.

boardgame: Overboss Duel: Gen. Khan Promo Card
description:
Promo boss card "Gen. Khan" handed out with purchase of Overboss Duel at GenCon 2023.

This Boss card allows the player to "Switch the locations of up to 2 tokens on your side of the board with the same amount of tokens on your opponent's side." upon reveal.

Additionally, it provides the player with the scoring objective of "For whichever token type you have the most of on your side of the board, gain +1 point for each token. (Heroes count as a token type).".

The game simulates the Battle of Preussisch-Eylau, East Prussia, on 7 and 8 February 1807, between Napoleon's Grande Armée and the Imperial Russian Army, who were reinforced by Prussians.

The game box also contains a few additional maps (and counters) for other smaller battles that took place at that time in that area: battle of Pułtusk 1806, Gołymin 1807, Bergfriede (Barkweda) 1807, and Waltersdorf (Włodowo) 1807.
Adds the new pigeon Pigeon Stool to the family. The new pigeon can lock down any face down card in any crime scene.
Rainbow Kite Card promo provided with game purchase at GenCon 2023. This card allows you to flip any one sand timer of your choice (including the white timer).

boardgame: Epic Spell Wars: Magus Minion & Manual
description:
Additional pieces to allow Annihilageddon 1 and 2 to be played with new mechanics introduced in Annihilageddon 3.

The following is from the official listing:

This EPIC storage box looks like a REAL Spellbook and includes 22 Spellbook cards for every oversized Wizard in Epic Spell Wars: ANNIHILAGEDDON and ANNIHILAGEDDON 2!! Plus 3 train tokens and bonus oversized Wizard, Familiar, and Spellbook!

And HOLY HELL, this box is roomy: It can store ALL the oversized Wizards, Familiars, and Spellbooks for the first two games in the series plus the upcoming ESW: ANNIHILAGEDDON 3 — Satanic Panic!

KEY FEATURES
EPIC storage box for all the oversized Wizards, Familiars, and Spellbooks for Epic Spell Wars: ANNIHILAGEDDON 1, 2, and 3 
Includes Spellbook cards for very oversized Wizard for the first three ANNIHILAGEDDON titles
Includes train tokens
Custom box that looks like a real Spellbook! 
CONTENTS
22 Spellbook cards for Epic Spell Wars: ANNIHILAGEDDON 1-2
1 Promo Oversized Wizard
1 Promo Familiar
1 Promo Spellbook
3 Train Tokens

"A hugely horrible game for hugely horrible people in a really very bigly way!"

Play a famous dictator from history or an aspiring one in modern day! Build your empire with Industry and Military. Score Victory Points for accumulating Opulence while watching your Subjects starve! Score more Victory Points by committing Atrocities against your subjects! But having all this much fun has its consequences… Keep your Civil Unrest in check or you’ll face assassination! Run PR campaigns to lower your International Notoriety to keep the UN from invading! Use Play Cards to affect other players or yourself to adjust Unrest or Notoriety tracks, lose or obtain Industry, Military, or Opulence, or temporarily affect what they can do. The first player who reaches the end of their tracks triggers End Of Game then all count their victory points.

DicTaters is a potato themed, fact based card game with hugely amounts of history spread throughout. Cards resemble things and actions taken by real-life dictators and those who oppose them. With a satirical tone, DicTaters uses humor to make the topic of horrifically cruel regimes approachable for conversation. Players learn how dictators operate and recognize the same patterns in today’s leaders.

If you're on Facebook you can check us out here too: https://www.facebook.com/groups/3273865592943765

HQ: https://whatweretheythinkinggames.com/

Unleash your inner caveman on this new egg-citing cardgame! Will you be able gather a team of cavemen and go up against epic events and dinosaurs and claim your victory?

In Caveman Rumble, players compete for the title of Caveman Champion! Hunt for delicious eggs and dinosaurs by stealing, color matching, snatching and lots of strategy and take that mechanism.

The objective of the game is to match colors on cards and snatch eggs and dinosaurs before your opponents. The player with the most points wins.

—description from the designer

Hyper Hacker is a cyberpunk-themed board game that challenges players to hack their way through a dystopian city. The city is plagued by cybercrime and corruption, and only the most skilled hackers can make a difference. 
Players compete as hackers who use their equipment tiles to create a network of connections that can bypass the security systems of the criminals. The goal of the game is to score the most points by placing tiles strategically and connecting them to the most valuable targets. 
Hyper Hacker combines elements of puzzle, strategy, and bluffing, as players have to balance their own network expansion with disrupting their opponents’ plans. It is set in a rich and immersive world that explores the themes of technology, society, and morality in a futuristic setting.'
publishers: 星空桌游 Starry Sky Games

description:
Zurvivor Zchool (Z refers Zombies) is a semi-cooperative game in which players take the role of a survivor with difference abilities to against obstacles by test type of status.

Each turn, one player takes on the role of leader and chooses 1-3 members that will face off against the crossroads. If you pass the test, leader gets a card, and members get to level up. In other words, if you don't, you could get hit and injured, which could infect you.

Some cards have mission items to send missions. Once complete, you'll go to the next mission, and if you complete all 3, you'll face the boss! That's the final battle.

When you defeat the boss, the game ends. Check objective and score points. The player who most points win!

This is just a great game for zombie world enthusiasts.

—description from the designer

Start as Entrepreneurs. Earn money, hire staff, and expand to new locations to win the game!
Scale Up! is about the ups and downs of building your own business, from an Entrepreneur all the way to an Empire!
There aren't any overnight successes here, so start small and build your way to the top! Compete against other players to build the biggest business at the table. Are you ready to Scale Up!?

—description from the publisher

Who built such an amazing sandcastle? Turtle Hilda is impressed but needs your help! Roll the die and work with Hilda to identify which shapes were used for the sandcastle. Place them on the matching spot on the game board and name the shape and color. If you place all the shapes, you win together but if all three water drops are placed on the sandcastle. Now it starts to rain. Unfortunately, the children have lost.

—description from the publisher

Wer hat denn da so eine tolle Sandburg gebaut? Schildkröte Hilda staunt nicht schlecht und geht gleich auf Entdeckertour. Die Kinder würfeln und finden gemeinsam mit Hilda heraus, welche Förmchen zum Bauen verwendet wurden. Sie legen sie auf die passende Stelle des Spielplans und benennen jeweils deren Form und Farbe. Schaffen sie es, alle Förmchen einzupuzzeln, gewinnen sie gemeinsam.

—description from the publisher (German)

Your adorable bunny Snowball has been kidnapped and you must set off on a heroic adventure to rescue her. Solve puzzles, explore rooms and find clues to track down Snowball and bring her home safely. Are you ready to save your bunny?

Save Snowball is an escape room-style game that will have a group of players cooperatively traversing a deck of cards. These cards will progress the story and challenge the players with exciting puzzles. The game is accessible to all ages. Unleash your inner detective.

—description from the designer

Each Hyperspace expansion civilization comes with a Homeworld tile, 4 unique Citizens, 2 Super Units, and 2 Super Techs.

The Automata are efficient, well-balanced, and effective as befits a machine civ. They can draw on tech powers without researching them, and their abilities are evenly divided between early- and late-game advantages.

The Technotron is a highly-flexible ship with controlling computers that can access and instantly activate a wide number of effects. When encountered, Technotrons can manifest many different results. They are not just for combat, either, and are often seen helping with manufacturing projects, scientific research, and other such efforts. As with all Automata ships, the Technotron has no “crew”. It is, itself, a single machine, and as such is precisely a single “Automata” resident, though a large and authoritative one.

Rules allowing up to 4 players to play a series of 3 games against each other.
Each game uses 1 of the 3 Great Facades expansions.

GridWars are set in an alternate future where society, technology, and reality itself are interconnected in a sprawling digital matrix known as The Grid. Multiple cyberpunk factions wage a relentless war amidst hypnotic neon lights and humming data streams. Exploit every bit of data as one flip of a binary switch can decide who will control the vast digital expanse and seize the overwhelming power it promises.

The alarms go off in the middle of the night: the Numeri gang has robbed the mall again. As you are the only witness of the robbery, Zumm the policeman asks you to identify the thief among all the suspects.

Diez Ladrones ("Ten Thieves")is an educative speed game based on the number partners that add up to 10 (9+1, 8+2, etc).

One clue card with tons of suspects (numbers) will be shown in the middle of the table, the first player to put his hand on the correct guilty number will win the card. 
There are three possible options:

     If there is an odd amount of numbers you have to make pairs between number partners. The number left alone without his pair is the guilty number. For example, in the card there are: 3,6,7,8,4. Number 8 is the guilty one.  
     If there is an odd amount of numbers and also Zuum (policeman) on it, you don't have to put your hand on the number left alone because Zuum has already caught him. You have to put your hand on his pair, the missing number. For example:3,6,7,8,4 and Zuum. Number 2 is the guilty one. 
     If there is an even amount of numbers, all of them have their pairs, you have to put the hand on Zuum's card. 


—description from the designer

BGC Málaga 2023 - Best Spanish Educational Game (Finalist)
family: Organizations: Board Games Association of Creators in Spain (LUDO)

description:
An amazing educational game based on rock, paper, scissors for any lover of dinosaurs ages 5 to 65 million.  Learn about fascinating dinosaurs and the Mesozoic periods in which they lived.  Saurus beats paper, paper beats rock and rock beats Saurus in a game of Dino-domination!
Deal each player 10 cards and place the remaining cards face down in the middle of the table.  The dealer will then flip over the top card of this deck, revealing the card you must beat.
Everyone shouts “Rock, Paper, Saurus!” and lays down their card.  Cards have values from 1 to 10 and ranked mesozoic periods; Jurassic beats Triassic and Cretaceous beats them all.
Saurus beats paper, paper beats rock and rock beats Saurus.  If there are two dinos of the same value the carnivore will always beat the herbivore. 
Players earn 1 point for each round won.  Once players have played all 10 cards in their hand the game is over and whoever has the most points wins!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Kreel Manor: The Path of the Dead
description:
Become a Necromancer and summon defeated enemies to fight for you with this dark new hero.

The expansion for Kreel Manor: The Dungeon Crawl Card Game adds a new hero to play as (and allows up to 4 players, 5 with The Path of Song).

According to your alien research team, the creature abducted from Earth is called “Steve.”  Continue to study the Steves by collecting disguise pieces in order to hide in plain sight.  Avoid suspicion, sabotage your competition and complete 3 matching disguises for the win!


     A game for 3-5 players.
     Avoid combining non-matching Disguise Cards to avoid suspicion.
     Steal disguises, sabotage opponents and block attacks with Action and Reaction Cards.
     Includes: Alien Cards, Disguise Cards, Action Cards, Reaction Cards and Suspicion Tokens


Everyone is dealt 3 Alien Cards, 1 Rule Card and 6 cards from the Hello My Name is Steve deck.

Collect Disguise Cards and match the symbols to move amongst the various groups of Steves. Will you blend in with the hipsters outside the coffee shop? How about the future Olympians down at the gym? It's up to you!

Use Action and Reaction Cards to block your opponents play or to get the upper hand and avoid suspicion.  If an alien gains too much suspicion, they must flee, discarding their disguises and starting anew.

The first player who successfully disguises their 3 aliens to infiltrate Steve society wins! How hard can it be to just blend in?

boardgame: Upin & Ipin Ke Sana Ke Sini
description:
The game is based on Malaysia longest-running animated series of five-year-old twin brothers name Upin & Ipin.

In Upin & Ipin Ke Sana Ke Sini, players will embark on various adventure with Upin, Ipin and friends in their village Kampung Durian Runtuh.  Players will move any character depends on the scenario objective and collect as many Ayam Goreng Tokens as possible.

The base game use a unique worker placement, action selection and hand management mechanics.  Players choose 3 out of 10 possible scenarios where each scenario present different objective and additional twist.  Scenarios challenge players differently like involve in a race or areal control battle between two teams or pick-up and delivery competition or deduction or 1 vs all or even a cooperative objective.

Players accumulate the Ayam Goreng victory points token and after 3 scenarios who have the most is the winner.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Marvel Champions: The Card Game – Deadpool Hero Pack
description:
Oh yeah, we’re doing this. You know him, you love him, it’s the wacky and wonderful Merc with the Mouth, Deadpool! This fourth-wall-breaking hero has finally arrived in Marvel Champions: The Card Game, and he’s coming in a larger Hero Pack than the others! Deadpool’s all about risk-reward gameplay, with an ability that makes him nearly immortal but also adds acceleration tokens to the main scheme. His pack also comes with a brand-new aspect for the game: the ‘Pool aspect! In this 78-card Hero Pack, you’ll find Deadpool, his fifteen signature cards, and a bunch of ‘Pool aspect cards for you to play around with, including some extras in the back of the pack for you to experiment with!

No Marvel game would be complete without Deadpool, and Marvel Champions would not be complete without the Deadpool Hero Pack. This expansion comes with a 40-card pre-built deck, giving you the chance to start playing from the moment you open the box.

—description from publisher's website
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: Marvel Champions – The Card Game

description:
Mon Dieu, quelle horreur! The inhabitants of Orléans have been hit hard as the plague has come upon them, bringing suffering and misery to the population. There are many dead to mourn, and even the plague doctor can bring only limited relief and hope to the long-suffering inhabitants. The clergy also has little to offer in the way of relief. And so, in the end, everyone is on their own and must try to protect their followers as best they can...

When you use the Orléans: Die Pest / The Plague expansion, the game conditions are made tougher from the start of play thanks to the "rat cards". Sometimes there are fewer monks in the game, sometimes no gears may be placed on place cards or the building of trading stations must be paid for with an additional coin.

New events are now also linked to the "corpse tokens" which players must place a certain number of in their bag at the end of each round. These tokens might be randomly drawn from the bag at the beginning of the round and occupy valuable space on the marketplace because they cannot be used for any action. To get rid of them for good, a player can fulfill indulgence cards through pilgrimage, donations, or other conditions, which also bring victory points at game's end. Fortunately, each player has a "plague doctor", a new figure that is available in (almost) every round and can be used universally.

—description from the publisher

The Last Catch is a card drafting, hand management, pick up and deliver game for 2-4 players, ages 10+, playing time around 30 min.

The players are fishermen and they are trying to catch as many fish as possible and deliver them to villages before winter.
But this won't be an easy task.
The villages have specific demands on what kind of fish they want. Also, the players are restricted by the cards in their hand, they can only add cards to the right or to the left and can only deliver cards from the right or from the left of their hand.

The player who collects the most points by catching fish and delivering them to villages will be the winner of the game.

—description from the designer

In Boonlake: Artifacts, you send out your treasure hunters to find treasures and mysterious artifacts on a new map.

Have you always wondered about the vases scattered around Boonlake? Recent findings show that they are loaded to the brim with variable atomic system energy, or V.A.S.E. for short. Put your found vases into the new artifacts and secure an advantage toward victory.

Five Twists is a semi-cooperative trick-taking game with hidden roles & variable card ranks. Player actions determine card ranks while revealing information that can aid players deducing partner identities. Using limited information to identify hidden partners, players vie to score the points with their partner and by also fulfilling secret individual objectives. Each round partners, secret objectives, & variable card ranks change; setting up the perfect storm for betrayal and intrigue. 
 Five Twists is played over 5 rounds, with the winner being the player with the most points at the end of the 5th round.

—description from the designer

Below The Torii Gate is a competitive trick-taking game where players make offerings to nearby forest spirits and earn points based on how many spirits they please. Playing cards allows players to attract spirits & fulfill different offerings. Fulfilling offerings affords players points and sometimes another special bonus. Card suits are ingredient types for various offerings. Players play single cards where suit and rank determine lead in future hands of play. Players have a limited number of visiting spirits they can entertain at once, so gaining the lead is important for attracting the most easily pleased spirits.

Below The Torii Gate is played over 5 rounds, with the winner being the player with the most points at the end of the 5th round.'

—description from the designer

With the help of the Avengers, defend Manhattan against Thanos and Loki!

Your aim of the game is creating the biggest group of Avengers Tiles depending on the symbols drawn on your Identity Token. Shuffle the Identity Tokens face down and draw one of them each. Do not show your token to the other players!
Choose one tile each from the tiles you have received and pass the remaining tiles to the neighbouring player. When all players have selected 1 tile each and passed the rest on, place the chosen tiles face up. Then, one by one, add them to the previously
placed tiles. The player who has the tile with the highest number lays it out first, followed by the others in descending order of the numbers on their tiles.
In addition to the squares with the image of the Avengers, you will also find 5 types of special squares on the tiles. Special squares can be added to any other square in the game, and any square can also be added to them.
Special squares can make your game easier or harder for other players.

When you have played 2 or 3 rounds (depending on the number of players), reveal your Identity Tokens and count whick of the Avengers has created the largest continous area. The fewer players have owned the same Avenger, the more points you get for its area. Then add up the scores for both of the Avengers you have on your Identity Token.
The player with the highest score wins!

—description from the publisher

Hunters play cards to complete rituals to banish demons, but one or more among them turns out to be possessed by a demon. Will the hunters succeed or will the demons?

Demon Hunter is a semi-cooperative trick-taking card game with hidden roles & open bidding. Players play cards to win tricks; trick-winning cards count as fulfilled portions of rituals for each player on both sides. With a limited book of rituals, each player bids on which of their rituals they think they can complete each round. Each ritual being completable once per game. When a player completes a ritual, they fulfill a portion of the group ritual which determines how Hunters & Demons score. The more the Demons win, the more they corrupt the group ritual and the more points their wins are worth. Hunters are working to beat the demons to the top, but since no one knows who the demons are, this battle of good and evil comes down to wits.

Demon Hunter is played over 5 rounds, with the winner being the player with the most points at the end of the 5th round.

—description from the designer

Starving Artists is a game of abstract thinking and shrewd resource management for 4-6 players that takes about 10 minutes per player. Manage a supply of unique magnetic pieces, using them to represent abstract titles and trading them in for new title cards if the ones you have aren't working. One player is the art critic, who must try to match each artwork with its title, avoiding decoys. Doing this successfully earns both the artist and the critic points. The player that communicated their vision and managed their resources best will be declared the winner!

Objective:
Become the most successful artist by having the most points at the end of the game. Score points by guessing others’ artworks correctly, having your own artwork correctly guessed, and having the most pieces remaining of each color at the end of the game.

boardgame: Sagrada: Promo 22 – Game Boy Geek 2023 Window Pattern Card
description: A promo window pattern card for Sagrada included in the 2023 Game Boy Geek - Season 11 Kickstarter that features a level 3 and 6 pattern on opposing sides.

You might think you know your friends well, but do you know how they’d respond to a controversial question like, "What superpower is not very super?"

Loaded Questions: Greatest Hits is a hilarious Q&A party game for you to figure out who said what with 250 of the franchise's top-rated questions!

Each round, you make your friends anonymously answer an absurd question from the deck: "If you started a rock band, what would you call it?".

You then have to decide which friend gave which hilarious response–which might reveal more about them than you knew! With each round, the role of the judge rotates so everyone has a chance to become the Loaded Questions Champion!

—description from the publisher

Fantasy Fishin’ is a game for 2-5 players with a focus on gameplay that’s easy to learn, quick, and engaging! A game typically lasts only 30 minutes and is recommended to anyone 8 years or older.

In Fantasy Fishin’ players take on the role of an angler in a breathtaking fantasy setting. Enjoy catching legendary fish, epic treasure, and even some useful trash as you compete with your friends to become the ultimate angler.

On each turn, players will go fishing and choose between moving to a new lake, using 1 of their 3 unique tool cards, or exchanging their old tool cards for new ones.

There are a total of 19 unique tool cards and 16 ever-changing lakes to fish from. Only by mastering all the lakes and tools available to you will you become the ultimate angler! Do you have what it takes?

—description from the designer

The aim of the game is to construct a forest together before the witch casts his spells and cages all the animals. The game is played in turns and the players develop strategies together to ensure that the witch does not win the game.
If a player manages to form the full forest, all the animals are automatically freed and the fairy and the animals win the game!

In their turn the player rolls the dice and does whatever is shown on the face:
BUILD: take the number of forest pieces shown on the dice and place them on the game board following the illustration to create a forest of trees.
MOVE THE FAIRY: move the fairy forward as many squares  as shown on the dice. 
MOVE THE WIZARD: the player moves the wizard figure along the number of squares on the path shown on the dice and tries to do the action on the square the wizard has landed on.

If the players manage to place the final piece of the forest on the board, all the animals are automatically freed and the fairy and the animals have won the game.
If the wizard manages to turn over the 7 spell pieces, or traps 12 animals in cages, then he wins the game and the players must start again from the beginning.

An adult party game where the active player draws a card and reads the question, like "If I were a tasty Chicken which would I be?" Five choices are given and then players simultaneously pick the option that will be the most like the active player.

Players matching the majority opinion get a point and play continues around the table with a new active player.

Earn your way to the top of the podium by getting rid of your cards the fastest.

The deck consists of 60 cards: 10 tens, 9 nines, and so on down to 1 one card, and 5 special cards.

The person who leads can play either a single card or a set of two or more cards of the same value. As play proceeds, each player must play a set with the same number of cards, of a lower value, or pass.

Hands continue until only one player has cards remaining in their hand. The first player to go out wins a gold medal, worth 3 points, the second player gets a silver medal worth 2 points, and the third gets a bronze medal, worth 1 point.

After seven rounds, The player with the highest point total becomes the Dream Team Soccer Champion!
Make an unbroken group of your own pieces linking opposite edges of your color. You can capture opponent pieces by moving your own discs onto stacks of exactly the same height.

Those naughty squirrels are back at it and peskier than ever in this nuttier version of nut nut Squirrel! Collect the most cards to win this game of lickety-split decisions, chance, and strategy. What kind of player will you be?

Are you meek and mild mannered, an impulsive risk-taker, or a slow, calculating strategist. Find out in this breakout game of surprise and wild choices. The object is to flip over as many cards as possible without unearthing a squirrel which will steal your stash!

Exciting New Additions

     The Golden Acorn (oooh! ahhh!): If you are able to bank this card by the end of the round, add 5 points to your final total.
     Squirrel Cage: Cage the next squirrel you flip over and keep going!
     Winter Is Coming: It’s time to gather more nuts. You must flip 3 more cards.
     The Nut Thief: Steal 1 nut from each player.


—description from the publisher

Stack up presents in this easy, fast-paced card game full of giggles and gifts as teams try to guess their way to the win. Extra challenges may have clue givers spinning in circles to fulfill this outrageous gift-gathering quest. But whatever you do, don’t land on the naughty list!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Wild Tiled West: The Surveyor Promo Card
description:
All Pre-orders directly through the Dire Wolf web store, FLGS+ retailer program or an affiliated retail partner, are eligible for the exclusive  “The Surveyor” promo card.

This is an additional Partner card to add to your games of Wild Tiled West.

It provides 4 for each of the following:

     5 or more buildings
     5 or more tombstones
     A pasture with 5 or more cows
     A second pasture with 5 or more cows

The secrets of Grind House deepen in this new expansion that promises greater rewards…if you can stand the risk.
The Host has opened a new wing of the mansion for you to come explore, and your invitation included a little something extra this time around. But did the other players receive another message too? You’d better stay sharp if you want to win Scythes Out.

New Thematic Mechanic: Promised Inheritance tokens. The Host has invited you all with a promise, but you’ll soon find, that some promises are better than others. Rooms offer the ability to swap tokens between yourselves and other players in hopes of ending as the favored player.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Hero Realms: Parsons the Insider Promo Card
description:
This card was given out at Origins and GenCon 2023

Parsons the Insider
Champion - Human, Mercenary        Cold Cost: 5

No Faction, choose a faction when you play this card. It has the chosen faction until it leaves play.

Expend: Gain 2 Gold. You may put a card from your discard pile onto the bottom of your deck.

Shield/Defense: 5

boardgame: Sweet Mess: Pastry Competition – Ice Cream Module
description:
A separate deck of 15 delicious ice cream recipe cards, played with their own set of rules.

SETUP

     Shuffle the 15 Ice Cream Recipe cards into a face-down deck.
     Place the Ice Cream deck beside the other Recipe decks.
     Draw one Ice Cream card and place it near the main Recipe display, face up.


In general, Ice Cream cards are treated like other Recipe cards. You may Prepare, Reserve, and Complete them as such.
When you Prepare or Reserve a Recipe, you may choose the face-up Ice Cream card from the display (following the usual rules).
Refill the Ice Cream Recipe display at the end of your turn, if necessary.

Note: If the Ice Cream deck is empty, ignore this step.

boardgame: Lyrically Correct: A Music Trivia Game – 90's & 2000's Hip Hop and R&B Edition
description:
Lyrically Correct is a music trivia card game that tests how well you know the lyrics to some of the hottest songs. This game will have you laughing, arguing, singing, and most of all enjoying time with friends and family.

Are you ready to turn up at your game night? Be prepared to rap your favorite rhyme or sing your favorite R&B line.

We have created a game so HOT that the VIBE will shift with every new card. Some cards are INTENSE FACT CHECKERS, some will MAKE YOU DANCE, and others will lead to a STRAIGHT UP KARAOKE SESSION. This is a UNIQUE EXPERIENCE like nothing else on the market.

Grab two or more players (pick your teammates wisely) and take turns answering questions about 90's and 2000's Hip Hop and R & B hits.

Hold up, that’s not it! Just when you think you have the right answer, a challenge card can be issued at any time which will test everyone's knowledge a little more.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Lyrically Correct: A Music Trivia Game – Mix Tape Edition: Funk, Hip Hop, Pop, and R&B
description:
Are you ready to get funky fresh, spit-dope rhymes, and hit those fly notes? Grab ya homeboys and homegirls and get ready to partaaaayyy. It’s time to see who knows the lyrics to all those 80s jams we love.

We have created a game so HOT that the VIBE will shift with every new card. Some cards are INTENSE FACT CHECKERS, some will MAKE YOU DANCE, and others will lead to a STRAIGHT UP KARAOKE SESSION. This is a UNIQUE EXPERIENCE like nothing else on the market.

Grab two or more players (pick your teammates wisely) and take turns answering questions about the 80's Hip Hop, R&B, Funk, and Pop hits.

Hold up, that’s not it. Just when you think you have the right answer, a challenge card can be issued at any time which will test everyone's knowledge a little more.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Lyrically Correct: A Music Trivia Game – 60's and 70's Oldies But Goodies Edition
description:
Lyrically Correct is a music trivia card game that tests how well you know the lyrics to some of the hottest songs. This game will have you laughing, arguing, singing, and most of all enjoying time with friends and family.

Are you ready to get your groove on? Get ready to sing your favorite lines from ya momma and em' favorite songs!

We have created a game so HOT that the VIBE will shift with every new card. Some cards are INTENSE FACT CHECKERS, some will MAKE YOU DANCE, and others will lead to a STRAIGHT UP KARAOKE SESSION. This is a UNIQUE EXPERIENCE like nothing else on the market.

Grab two or more players (pick your teammates wisely) and take turns answering questions about the 60s and 70s Oldies but Goodies.

Hold up, that’s not it. Just when you think you have the right answer, a challenge card can be issued at any time which will test everyone's knowledge a little more.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Lyrically Correct: A Music Trivia Game – Revival: The Gospel Edition
description:
Lyrically Correct is a music trivia card game that tests how well you know the lyrics to some of the hottest songs. This game will have you laughing, arguing, singing, and most of all enjoying time with friends and family.

Are you ready for a game night full of praise and worship? Be prepared to sing, dance, and shout your favorite gospel songs.

We have created a game so HOT that the VIBE will shift with every new card. Some cards are INTENSE FACT CHECKERS, some will MAKE YOU DANCE, and others will lead to a STRAIGHT UP KARAOKE SESSION. This is a UNIQUE EXPERIENCE like nothing else on the market.

Grab two or more players (pick your teammates wisely) and take turns answering questions about the gospel music we all love. Whether in your living room or in the holy sanctuary, we got you.

Hold up, that’s not it. We have created all New Challenge Cards for this deck. Are you saved and sanctified? Just when thou think you have it all figured out, a challenge card can be issued at any time that checks to see who was really paying attention at Sunday School.

—description from the publisher

Each Hyperspace expansion civilization comes with a Homeworld tile, 4 unique Citizens, 2 Super Units, and 2 Super Techs.

Over time the Azota enervate and weaken other aliens – gradually, but permanently, removing colonies from play. But the Azota weaken over time as well, making the game into a race. The Azota must “spread the love”, gathering colonies as they go. They are more powerful in the early game, and lose oomph as time goes on. They must maximize their advantages before they fall apart.

The Skeleship is designed for one purpose only – to kill. If you can afford to accompany it with a single puny corvette, it is certain to score a kill in every fight – possibly more than one. It is deadly, and the Azota ability to engage in extra battles makes it even worse.

Each Hyperspace expansion civilization comes with a Homeworld tile, 4 unique Citizens, 2 Super Units, and 2 Super Techs.

The Collectors must gather copies of every different species, every ship type. They must
own them all, or as many as possible. As they improve their collection, the Collector
threat grows as well.
Their Athenaeum is not just a vessel, but a repository of valuables and wealth. It
transports relics that the Collectors hold dear, and as their collection grows, they
become more obsessed and more eager to see it progress. As a result, the larger their
museum’s assortment, the more fanatical and fixated become the Collectors about
these vessels.

Each Hyperspace expansion civilization comes with a Homeworld tile, 4 unique Citizens, 2 Super Units, and 2 Super Techs.

The Daldath are a terrifying civilization which can desolate the map. They leave hollow spots in their swath, from which they can later harvest precious supplies. The destruction is expensive, however, and other civilizations can often profit through this same destruction.

The Daldath can create gigantic biomechanical structures, deeply rooted in a planet, and powered by energy from its core. These structures project destructive energy across
the light-years of space and destroy entire worlds. Their planet smashers are the core of Daldath power. Though they can be destroyed, it is difficult.

Each Hyperspace expansion civilization comes with a Homeworld tile, 4 unique Citizens, 2 Super Units, and 2 Super Techs.

The Dacians are a terrifying threat. When the Dacians produce, they drain extra resources from their neighbors. Their Vorax tech makes them both fearsome and flexible in battle. At least they prefer to keep your colonies alive, as feeding grounds,
and they must carefully plan just when they are going to receive a secret, to manage their vampirism.

Their super-ship Lancets is part and parcel of your Feed action’s success. It ensures a Feed’s success, letting you drain your foe and bump up the battle track without risk.

A new expansion for one of the best Deck Builders of all time! Quest number 14: is your party ready for the Raging Seas?

There is a new power wreaking havoc on the long-standing peace that has stood for decades among the nations and Islands of the Great Sea!

Raging Seas introduces a new way to move through the Dungeon that doesn’t use Light. To explore the ocean tides, you’ll need to chart your journey and draw a number of new Voyage cards; that is the number of moves you can make in the Dungeon that turn. Each of these cards has a requirement that, if met, will provide a bonus to your endeavors that turn. But beware! If you fail to complete a Voyage card, you may receive a penalty that makes your quest that much harder.

The high seas can be an exciting and fearsome place. You never know what islands you’ll come across or what dangers you’ll encounter. A chance meeting with a merchant ship can provide you with a tremendous boon … for the right price. A sudden squall can come upon you in the night, putting the entire voyage at risk if you are caught unaware. So it’s best to come prepared!

Texts From Your Ex is a fast-paced party game where you race to get rid of all your ex’s texts first! Get rid of all your cards and then Block the conversation to win a round. The first player to play all of their Block tokens wins!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Rock, Paper, Scissors: The Card Game
description:
Bringing together two classic games, "Rock, Paper, Scissors" and "War" in this fun and fast-paced game. There are, of course, a few twists. This is a game the entire family can enjoy! It was inspired by my own kids and their love for card games, but especially their favorite game, "Rock, Paper, Scissors!"

QUICK TO LEARN: The rules are simple and easy to learn!

SMALL AND COMPACT: Bring this game on the go, it is small and easy to travel with!

BOREDOM BUSTER: This game brings the fun, and is sure to beat boredom any day!

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Card Versions of Non-Card Games

description:
Shinobi no Gi is an "information concealment card game" in which players combine two Shinobi to play against each other.

Use your ninjutsu and companions to uncover hidden scrolls in your opponent's deck.

Psychological warfare of deception and reasoning, and card strategy. Win both and aim for the strongest shinobi.

—description from the publisher

Each Hyperspace expansion civilization comes with a Homeworld tile, 4 unique Citizens, 2 Super Units, and 2 Super Techs.

The Glazaqtoq start with an extra homeworld, their major advantage. Their sloth and laziness means they don’t benefit from salvaged items, but this also acts as a sort of protection, since they are less likely to be on an interesting continent. They make up for this with their huge baseline production. Their high-handed approach to secrets lets them deny rewards to other civs as they seek to maintain their ancient hegemony.

The Glazaqtoq ship was once the most powerful vessel in the universe and enforced their will on hapless minions. Now these ships are obsolescent, though still striking in appearance. The Glazaqtoq put great faith in them still.

Each Hyperspace expansion civilization comes with a Homeworld tile, 4 unique Citizens, 2 Super Units, and 2 Super Techs.

Deadly in battle, with an ever-growing economy, the Mendeku have the fascinating weakness that they cannot take any action they have not seen. Early in, this is a consideration even for other players. They think, “I want to attack, but if I do, the 
Mendeku will, too.” Eventually the balloon goes up and the Mendeku horde is unleashed in its fury.

The super-ship Dromon is the apex of the Mendeku war machine. These vessels are partly organic, a giant Mendeku caste hatched for this purpose. The huge creatures are not complete, however, until they are attached to faster-than-light engines and an enormous railgun, firing straight down the being’s body, and aimed by unerring instinct.

Captain's Log Myths and Legends will incorporate fantasy into the game board, now not only the living are present and not all acts are equally honorable. Every act can have a consequence, and this will be revealed as time goes by... the story continues.

We have separated from the main plot, now we can find mermaids, harpies, flying ships and much more... little by little will be revealed all the content that will incorporate this expansion.

—description from the designer

Those pesky squirrels are after your hard earned stash of nuts, seeds, and pinecones! Be the lucky person with the most cards at the end of the round to win this game of choice, chance, and strategy.

Shuffle the deck of stash cards with 8 squirrels. The first player begins flipping over as many cards as he/she dares from the deck (each player MUST flip at least one card on each turn). The player may choose when to stop flipping and pass to the next player at any time. The object is to flip over as many cards as possible without unearthing a squirrel which will steal your stash! If any of the other players have accumulated stash cards during a round where someone flips over a squirrel, they get the reward of banking their nuts, seeds, and pinecones. After the deck is depleted, players count their cards to determine the winner. For more challenging play, add more squirrels! We've included 15 total squirrels to amp up the fun, or should we say frustration!

—description from the publisher

Each Hyperspace expansion civilization comes with a Homeworld tile, 4 unique Citizens, 2 Super Units, and 2 Super Techs.

The disgusting Nomian pests are a difficult problem to solve. Other civs are forced willynilly to evacuate worlds when they arrive, and their ability to spread their infection even to 
unit pools is aggravating and enervating. Their hand is against every man and every man’s hand against them.

Nomians, as an intelligent disease-based organism, gain effectiveness the better they understand their opponents’ defenses, biology, and technology. The Plagueship is the apogee of the Nomian infectious cycle. It carries within it acid spores, energy pods, and is equipped with blasters, plague-beams, and anti-metal virus casters. The real threat of the plagueship is that the more it battles, the more the Nomians learn about your weaknesses, and the stronger the ship gets. After a lengthy war, the plagueships become almost unstoppable in efficacy.

Each Hyperspace expansion civilization comes with a Homeworld tile, 4 unique Citizens, 2 Super Units, and 2 Super Techs.

The Old Ones grant wisdom and lore to other species. They suffer from their current decadent state, which makes them unable to boost colonies, but they can make up for this by the blessings they spread selectively.

The Oracle super-ship is an amazing vessel. When its main armament fires, it can either inflict vast damage, or create a portal into hyperspace, granting strange energies for the Old Ones. Either result is useful to their species and makes them a particularly knotty problem to crack.

Each Hyperspace expansion civilization comes with a Homeworld tile, 4 unique Citizens, 2 Super Units, and 2 Super Techs.

The Salps are cheap and cheerful. They spread like wildfire, and soon thrive throughout the star cluster, but their feeble ships are not difficult to destroy. Their bases and colonies are another matter.

Salps are mainly oriented towards reproduction and dispersal, so of course their superunit is not a ship, but a gigantic colony, designed to host trillions of salps. It is equipped 
with machines to provide food, housing, and even funeral services. It is armed as well, for such a large concentration of the salp race needs protection from their many predators.

Each Hyperspace expansion civilization comes with a Homeworld tile, 4 unique Citizens, 2 Super Units, and 2 Super Techs.

The Starspawn plunge through the sky, gain the secrets of Cthulhu’s wisdom, and must slumber periodically. They can devour enemy ships before the latter get off a shot, and they can instantaneously gather rewards. They are fearsome, but fortunately at least in Hyperspace terms, not unbeatable.

The most powerful Starspawn weapon is of course Great Cthulhu himself. But since his rise, he underwent mitosis, and now two are available. Perhaps this was always the point of conquering Earth -simply to enable Cthulhu’s duplication.

Each Hyperspace expansion civilization comes with a Homeworld tile, 4 unique Citizens, 2 Super Units, and 2 Super Techs.

A death-obsessed species, the Thoth’s powerful psychic curses blight and hamper other players, and often lead to violence. Meanwhile they benefit from the glories of oblivion, firm in their knowledge of their complicated afterlife.

The Sphinx super-ship is a light-sail, which elegantly swings through space at the minimum energy cost needed. It is built of so-called “resurrected matter”, a highenergy form of normal matter. As such it is indestructible by normal weapons, though the attached devices and sensors on the Sphinx’s outside surface can of course be burned off and need re-affixing.

Each Hyperspace expansion civilization comes with a Homeworld tile, 4 unique Citizens, 2 Super Units, and 2 Super Techs.

A species of gigantic kaiju-like monsters, only a few exist. They dominate entire parsecs of space, as well as the worlds they inhabit. Powerful in resources, they are few in numbers and obsessed with their complex reproductive cycle.

The Urumak have two different forms they can take. The most commonly-seen are the colossal planetary invaders. But they can also grow (or attach – the process is not clear to outsiders) gigantic cavorite crystals around their bodies and rise into space, orbiting worlds as defense bases and shipyards

Each Hyperspace expansion civilization comes with a Homeworld tile, 4 unique Citizens, 2 Super Units, and 2 Super Techs.

The Valkar are invaders from another dimension. Early, they are comparatively few and easy to push back. But in the later game, with techs prepared, and their defensive advantages manifest, they are a redoubtable foe.

The Valkar’s mightiest vessel, the Phantasm, perpetually fades in and out of existence. Its internal vibrations (it’s not even made of atoms) have two phases, and thus can either fuse enemy circuits, or alternatively intercept and nullify energy blasts and attacks. Thus, it has either a defensive or offensive value, and the Valkar can adjust the effects according to tactical needs

Each Hyperspace expansion civilization comes with a Homeworld tile, 4 unique Citizens, 2 Super Units, and 2 Super Techs.

The Vorck have a strong work ethic and never lack for resources. They love to build alongside other civs and this booms the Vorck economy. Eventually they can turn those extra resources into a terrifying fleet incorporating the dreaded ramships. The Vorck have no strong instinct for battle. To them, war is just another industrial project. Most of their weapons are converted tools. Instead of blasters, their troops carry overclocked welding torches. Instead of sergeants and officers, they have foremen and supervisors.

Their super-ship, the Ram, is a converted wrecker. The back half of the ship is the “real” vessel, basically just a small control compartment and huge engines. On the front is a powerful magnetic clamp, which the Vorck use to lock bits of flotsam and destroyed ship parts. This giant spiky surface, they then directly ram into the target enemy. Of course, the crash itself can harm the Ram, but it has done its duty.

Each Hyperspace expansion civilization comes with a Homeworld tile, 4 unique Citizens, 2 Super Units, and 2 Super Techs.

The XK-13 are obsessed with war, as befits their ancestry as war machines. The more they fight, the more they benefit from fighting. They have only one real resource boost, their Heat Death tech, which is best to take opportunistically, probably 6-9 VPs into the game – don’t fall into the beginner trap of waiting till you have a lot of VPs, because you won’t need organics badly then.

The XK-1 is an earlier model of the Xk-13 war android, less sophisticated, less intelligent, but more powerful. It broadcasts a suppressive effect throughout its system 
preventing other civs from building or even salvaging in the area, and in battle can convert its own ships into bolts of energy to wipe out the foe.

Each Hyperspace expansion civilization comes with a Homeworld tile, 4 unique Citizens, 2 Super Units, and 2 Super Techs.

The Yaddith start with the best homeworld of all. Unfortunately, they are due to lose it, so must maximize its use early on. At game end, they earn VPs from enemy starbases, don’t need to conquer these – this knowledge can mean opponents sometimes let the Yaddith colonies alone.

The Stingship doesn’t require a key action to build – with enough resources they can amass a deadly fleet faster than any other civ. Keep this in mind; the Yaddith is not about slowly growing over time, but about quick reflexes and adaptability

Each Hyperspace expansion civilization comes with a Homeworld tile, 4 unique Citizens, 2 Super Units, and 2 Super Techs.

This species is the hardest of all civs to play. Not because they are inherently complex, but because of the mindswap which adopts abilities, weaknesses, and technology of another species. They start as “vanilla” Yithians, then might become Dacians, then Xk-13, and so forth, forcing their rivals to adapt to the new situation. Of course, the Yithians must adapt too – learning or figuring out how to use their new powers.

The Yithian battleship is designed with internal nodes and weapon ports for a gigantic array of devices and systems. When they swap minds with a target species, they adapt the technology best-suited for their new bodies into the battleship, and so the battleships take on a variety of roles
