INTRODUCTION  Halfcut is a two-player game played on a square board of any size, initially empty. The two players, Red and Blue, take turns placing their own checkers onto unoccupied squares, one checker per turn, starting with Red. Passing is not allowed, but if you don't have an available placement, your turn is skipped. Mark Steere designed Halfcut in August 2023.

OBJECT  Red must form a path of red checkers (interconnected via horizontal or vertical adjacencies, or both) connecting the two red sides of the board. Blue must form a path of blue checkers connecting the two blue sides of the board.

GROUP  A group here is a monocolored group of checkers interconnected horizontally or vertically, or both. Diagonal adjacencies are irrelevant in Halfcut.

CROSSCUT  A crosscut is comprised of four adjacent checkers arranged in a square. Two of the checkers are red and two are blue. Like-colored checkers are diagonally opposed in the square.

CROSSCUT GROUP  A crosscut group is the group that contains a crosscut checker.

CROSSCUT RULE  You can only form a crosscut if by doing so you create a new crosscut group which is larger than at least one of the enemy crosscut groups of the crosscut.

CHECKER REMOVAL  Having formed a crosscut, immediately remove the enemy crosscut checkers which are part of enemy crosscut groups which are smaller than your newly formed crosscut group, concluding your turn.

SIMULTANEOUS CROSSCUTS  In order to make a placement which would form two crosscuts simultaneously, you would have to satisfy the crosscut rule for both of the crosscuts, each considered separately.

INTRODUCTION  Clump is a two-player game played on a square board of any even size, initially filled with red and blue stones interspersed in a checkerboard pattern. Mark Steere designed Clump in August 2023.

OBJECT  Red must form a path of red stones (interconnected via horizontal or vertical adjacencies, or both) connecting the two red sides of the board. Diagonal adjacencies are irrelevant in Clump. Blue must form a path of blue checkers connecting the two blue sides of the board.

PLAY  Players take turns swapping one of their stones with an orthogonally adjacent enemy stone (subject to swap conditions described below), one swap per turn, starting with Red. Players will always have a swap available. Passing is not allowed.

GROUP A group here is a monocolored, orthogonally interconnected group of stones. Groups can morph without any change in size.

SWAP CONDITIONS  On your turn, make a swap subject to the following conditions:
 
     At least one of the swapped stones must be part of a larger group after the swap than it was before the swap.



     The largest group so formed must be larger than the largest group that would be diminished by the swap. (If no groups are diminished, only condition 1 applies.)

INTRODUCTION  Crisscross is a two-player game played on a square board of any size, initially empty. The two players, Red and Blue, take turns placing their own checkers onto unoccupied squares on the board, one checker per turn, starting with Red. Passing is not allowed. Mark Steere designed Crisscross in July 2023.

OBJECT  Red must form a path of red checkers (interconnected via horizontal or vertical adjacencies, or both) connecting the two red sides of the board. Diagonal adjacencies are irrelevant in Crisscross. Blue must form a path of blue checkers connecting the two blue sides of the board.

SEGMENT  A segment is a straight (horizontal or vertical), contiguous sequence of like colored checkers, of length one or more.

NEW SEGMENT  When you place a checker, you form a new segment. Your newly placed checker will either form a segment of length one, or it will lengthen an existing segment (or two perpendicular segments), or it will combine two existing segments into a larger segment, or some combination thereof.

FLANKING (DIFFERENT FROM REVERSI)  A blue segment is flanked if it is sandwiched between two red SEGMENTS in a straight line. Conversely for a flanked red segment.

CAPTURE  If your new segment, together with another of your segments, flanks an enemy segment, replace all checkers of the enemy segment with your own checkers, concluding your turn.

PERIMETER  Consider the board to be surrounded by an invisible perimeter of squares. You can never place a checker on this imaginary perimeter. But, on your turn, assume that the perimeter is entirely occupied by your checkers.

DRAWS?  It’s unknown at the time of this writing if a deadlock can occur. If it is possible, it’s extremely unlikely.

INTRODUCTION  Swaptimum is a two-player game played on a square board of any size, initially empty. The two players, Red and Blue, take turns placing their own checkers onto unoccupied squares, one checker per turn, starting with Red. Passing is not allowed, but if you don't have an available placement, your turn is skipped. Mark Steere designed Swaptimum in August 2023.

OBJECT  Red must form a path of red checkers (interconnected via horizontal or vertical adjacencies, or both) connecting the two red sides of the board. Blue must form a path of blue checkers connecting the two blue sides of the board.  It’s possible that a player may win on the other player’s turn.

CROSSCUT  A crosscut is comprised of four adjacent checkers arranged in a square. Two of the checkers are red and two are blue. Like-colored checkers are diagonally opposed in the square.

PLAY  You can place a checker on any unoccupied square not ruled out by the placement restriction (see PLACEMENT RESTRICTION). If your placement forms a crosscut, then make a swap:

a. between any two checkers in the crosscut, OR
b. between any checker in the crosscut and any other checker not in the crosscut which is horizontally, vertically, or diagonally adjacent to said checker,

which kills the crosscut and doesn’t form a new crosscut, concluding your turn.

PLACEMENT RESTRICTION  If your placement would form a crosscut, and if every possible crosscut killing swap would form a new crosscut, then said placement is illegal for you.

SWAPTIMUM DESIGN NOTES  Swaptimum was inspired by Reneo (2023) by Luis Bolaños Mures. Luis is a world class abstract game designer and the leading authority on OOSCGs (orthogonal only square connection games. That is, only horizontal and vertical, but not diagonal, adjacencies count as connections.)

Swaptimum is my most recent in a series of OOSCGs after a continuous, obsessive, months long design odyssey. Of my last 18 attempted OOSCGs, only five (Halfcut, Clump, Crisscross, Flipstop, and Swaptimum) survived Luis's scrutiny. Most either didn't work or were very similar to what had already been done, many by Luis himself.

Each player take a notepad and find a pen.
     Each player draw a card of each color and place them in order from 1 to 4.


Simultaneously, players must use their 4 cards to create a story by choosing one of the two options on each of the cards.

Each player illustrates his story in the form of a comic strip. No pressure: the drawings can be as bad as the story.

When everyone has finished, each player shows their comic created from the drawn cards.

We vote for the most ridiculous story. At 3,2,1 we point to the chosen comic. The cartoonist with the most votes wins the game. In the event of a tie, they share the victory.

—description from the publisher (translated)


     Chaque joueur.euse prend une feuille du bloc-notes et trouve un crayon.
     Chaque joueur.euse pige une carte de chaque couleur et les met en ordre de 1 à 4.


Simultanément, les joueurs.euses doivent utiliser leurs 4 cartes pour créer une histoire en choisissant une des deux options sur chacune des cartes.

Chaque joueur.euse illustre son histoire sous forme de bande dessinée. Pas de pression: les dessins peuvent être aussi nuls que l'histoire.

Quand tout le monde a terminé, chaque joueur.euse montre sa bande dessinée créée d'après les cartes pigées.

On vote pour l'histoire la plus ridicule. À 3,2,1 on pointe la bande dessinée choisie. Le.la bédéiste qui a obtenu le plus de votes gagne la partie. En cas d'égalité, ils partagent la victoire.

- Descrtipion from the publisher

In Silos, players are given the roles of farmers who need to sort and collect seeds. Farmers take turns dropping seeds and hoping they land in their hoppers. As the seeds fall, they are deflected by obstacles. Predicting which hopper collects each seed is key to winning.

Silos is a game of predicting where the “chips may fall” with two game modes.

Game Modes

     Prediction: drop seeds and collect a coin when the seed land in your hopper. Collect the most coins to win.
     Collection: drop a seed such that it lands in your hopper. Collect the most seeds to win. Maximum of four players.


—description from the designer

boardgame: Snap Ships Tactics: Scout Ships Card Pack – Lance & Locust
description:
All the chassis, part, and AI cards for the Lance and Locust Snap Ships kits.

36 cards total. Intended for players that already have a Lance and/or Locust kit in their collection.

—description from the publisher

The first expansion for Northern Pacific is covering the Southern Pacific Railroad.

The Northern Pacific expansion: Southern Pacific game uses the components found in both the original Winsome Games production of Northern Pacific and the later licensed Rio Grande Games production of Northern Pacific. It may be played by owners of either game.

Provided are two 11x17 inch maps that must be trimmed on the short edge, resulting in an 11x33 inch game board.

The Northern Pacific expansion: Southern Pacific is for 3-6 players and takes as long as a Northern Pacific game. There are no rules changes.

Trial by Battle is a set of wargames rules for playing games set in the Medieval period of history. They are designed to be easy to learn and to play and are ideal for a quick game. They are ideal for players who are not very familiar with wargames rules or who don’t have hours to play with. Sometimes this type of ruleset is called a “beer and pretzels” game. Neither beer nor pretzels are required to play, but a game is always more enjoyable with good refreshments.

The rules aim to be representative of how medieval armies fought but aren’t an exact simulation. They don’t provide for all the different variations in types of soldiers and armies during the Medieval period. For instance, there is an Archers unit that can represent crossbowmen or longbowmen. The rules provide the framework for an enjoyable Medieval wargame that captures the feel of battles in the Middle Ages.

The rules include six scenarios and some sample army lists to help you get started.

It is the year 2368. You and your gang of criminals just committed a burglary and stole a spacecraft to return to your hideout.

Unfortunately, after takeoff, the board computer detected the tampering and locked you out, leaving you unable to navigate. You are on the wrong course, which will surely get you caught; and your resources are running out.

There are tools on board that will help you regain control over the ship, change your course and escape. But if greedy companions want to steal these, will you be able to make it?

The game ends when either your gang takes control of the ship (and the player with the highest value of cards wins), or when one of the resources runs out (and the player with the lowest valued hand wins). Either way, only one winner!
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
A free downloadable solitaire postcard wargame portraying a German armoured incursion into Soviet Union in 1941.

You’re in command of a German Armored formation, in 1941, during the early stages of Operation Barbarossa.

—description from the designer

Fortuna et Victoria is a family game where players compete against each other to become the richest and most powerful being in a legendary universe. To accomplish this goal, players will hoard money and obtain properties to grow an empire, but also acquire troops, capture monsters and fulfill technological advancements.

The game is based upon a classical square design where players cast dice to move their pawns. Players can buy properties to extend their empire but they also have to keep an eye on other players, as opposite troops may attempt to shatter your power. Players may enter into conflict with one another and this is solved through card combat. You can hire troops or attempt to capture monsters that fight for you. You will have an edge to this combat if you can manage to discover one of the 18 technological advancements of the game. If you win, you keep your troops or monsters plus a large sum from the defeated player. Luck also plays a role in the game with plenty of random events, that may be of benefit or not.

There are several ways to win the game. First, players decide if they want a short or a long game. Short games have five different possibilities of victory including when all properties are bought or when a player captures the dragon. The long game has four possible outcomes including leading to bankruptcy to all other players or by possessing castles in all properties. The winner is the player that obtains the highest amount of money adding properties and other card values.

Based on the popular game show category "before and after". Each card contains an answer made up of two different parts that share a word which connects them together. Players take turns being the clue giver each round. The clue giver must come up with clues for both parts of the answer and tell the group. The group will guess the answer. The first player to guess correctly wins the round! Full of vulgar, taboo, and naughty cards that will have your friends blushing and laughing at the same time. The uncensored version offers a unique twist on the classic that’s best suited for adults.

Milestone is a game consisting of a roll of washi tape and a special "dicil", a pencil with printings on the sides such that it can be used as a die.

In the game, players pull out two lengths of tape, and place the, parallel to each other on the playing surface, with a fist's width between them.

On your turn, roll the pencil towards the lengths of tape. The number showing on the pencil is the die value for the turn. The two places on the tape that the pencil overlaps affects how you can modify your die result.  Then, write the resulting number on a blank space on one of the pieces of tape.

The game ends when both pieces of tape have a number written in each of the blank spaces.  To win, numbers must be decreasing from one end to the other.

Line Frame is a game consisting of a roll of washi tape and a special "dicil", a pencil with printings on the sides such that it can be used as a die.

In the game, players pull out a length of tape based upon the die roll and place it adjacent to or on top of specific places on tape which has previously been placed.  Then, they must write the number from the die in a blank space.

The game ends when a certain amount of tape has been placed.  Players score for numbers written in boxes where one of the surrounding colors is in a group with a size at least that of the number.

updn is a trick-taking game using domino-style cards with two values, where either number can be the rank and either number the suit.

It is a "must follow" game, where you must play a card that matches the suit of the lead card if you can. However, the card with the highest sum of its two values will win the trick, regardless of suit. The winner will take all of the cards played to the trick, with some cards worth positive points and some cards worth negative points.

The game also uses a row of bonus cards to effect certain conditions, with the bonus cards taking effect every two tricks (2, 4, 6, 8, but also 9). These cards do things such as reverse rankings (so that low sum wins), and are also worth points themselves. The card which is available in round 9 will reverse the player's score - making a score of -x worth +x, and vice versa.

The player with the most points wins.

boardgame: Q.E.D. 猫=液 (Q.E.D. Cats are Liquid)
description:
In Q.E.D. Cats are Liquid, players are exploring the rheology of cats through Deborah numbers.

In practice, players have a container of size 21 and a hand of cat cards. Players draft a cat card, place it in front of them, and pass the remaining cards. Some other cards may affect the size of the container or cat values with algebraic operations.

Once a player's container size equals the sum of their cats, they are finished with the round.  Other players continue until all cards are drafted.  Generally, the player with the largest matching cat and container size wins.

A pirate trick-taking game where you're trying to capture your opponents' pirates and earn points.  Each round brings different scoring criteria.  Whoever accumulates the most points and take the WANTED card wins!
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Educational game for the Finnish schools. Promotes STEM studies as players try to produce enough resources for world population.

Simplified version of Kyoto Protocol. Played over three rounds and at the end of each round players with least production receive small penalty. Goal is to have most cards in play after three rounds.

When the next Allied offensive began on September 12, its goal was Rimini and San Marino. The Germans knew perfectly well that the loss of Rimini would cause the Allies to exit the Po River Valley and so they had to do everything to slow down and stop this attack on the coast. But the Allies also attacked in the direction of San Marino – straight across the hills. They also used ar­mo­red divisions, which were expected rather on the coastal plains.

—description from the publisher

The game simulates the military actions that took place on the Italian Peninsula in the area of San Marino in Autumn, 1944.

Units represent: regiments, battalions, and brigades.

The game uses the WB-95 system by Taktyka i Strategia.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players
Series: Wielkie Bitwy 1939-45 (Dragon / Taktyka i Strategia)

description:
Three extra cards to add to your Plants deck:

     Rubber Tree [Floor]
     Pink Oyster Mushrooms [Shelf]
     Trailing Jade [Hanging]


Each require one of each resource (water, food & light) to get 2 and then 6 points.
Micro expansion with 9 (1 black + 8 white) new cards.

The great heist was executed in the city of London. You and your fellow PIs are recruiting your assistants to gather clues to find the eight stolen goods. However, if you pay your assistants too much, your agency will close. If you don’t pay them enough, they will reveal their knowledge to your opponents as revenge for you being a miser. Can you find the stolen goods before your opponents without closing your agency, and without revealing too much of your clues?

The Great heist is a board game that uses a companion App to play the game.
Using the App the players ask for clues (at a cost) and the App respond with answers as to how many goods are placed in the clue area (but not exact location).
At the end of the turn, the clues the were paid the least are revealed to all players which will give them more information.
Every other turn there is a police raid to one location of the game that also help figuring the real position of the stolen goods.
Using known relation between the objects and the answers from the App and police raids players can figure out where the goods are placed on the map.
At the end of the game, the player who paid the most for their clues (in order to avoid them from between revealed to the other players) will lose the game since his PI agency will close. All other players will score points for the stolen goods they found and how fast they found them.

—description from the designer

Atomic Kaiju is a deck-building game, which means you start with a fairly small, weak deck and over the course of the game you accumulate more and more powerful cards. The object of the game is to use your military and your monsters to decimate your opponent’s city, bringing their city points from 75 down to zero.

Spend Science and Material Points to buy cards from a market of over 100 cards to bolster your military might, mutate gameplay, and establish buff locations.

Military Cards: Use these to buy new resources
and attack your opponent.

Location Cards: Play these next to your Kaiju Nest
and reap their benefits every turn.

Mutation Cards: Use these to change the fate
of the game on a moment’s notice.

Research Cards: Use these to buy new mutations
that change the game, or...

Hatch a Gargantuan Kaiju! Draw 7 Science Points in one hand and you can hatch the ultimate weapon - a gigantic Kaiju beast to lay waste to your enemies! There are 8 fearsome atomic beasts in the game, and you never know which one you’ll hatch!

But be cautious! Too much atomic research can lead to a deck full of Toxic Waste!

—description from the designer

Legends of Signum II: Warseer is an innovative skirmish wargame set in the  universe of Signum. In this hexagonal battlefield, players command squads of unique heroes and their loyal followers, engaging in tactical battles that are as thrilling as they are visually stunning. Each miniature represents a character with distinct abilities, allowing players to craft their strategies around their heroes’ strengths.

One of the standout features of Legends of Signum II: Warseer is the introduction of the Hexagonal Grid system. Unlike previous versions of Signum that relied on rulers for distance measurements, this new mechanic simplifies movement and combat, creating a more intuitive and fluid gameplay experience. The hexagon is not just a shape; it’s the foundation of a dynamic battlefield that encourages creative tactics and strategic depth.

The world of Signum continues to evolve, with the rise and fall of realms as war remains a constant force. Players will witness the clash between two powerful factions: the bustling Free City of Vallor, known for its rich trade and diverse populace, and the zealous Empire of Iron Nest, steeped in fervent devotion. As the narrative unfolds, only the strongest will seize victory and claim the spoils of war.

Legends of Signum II: Warseer not only expands upon the lore of its predecessor but introduces a wealth of new fantasy miniatures, an enhanced economic system, and a revamped battle mechanics framework. Adapt your strategies, harness devastating magic, and outsmart your opponents to emerge triumphant. In this realm, cunning and strength are your greatest allies.

—description from the publisher

Tornaloco is a totally CRAZY game!

Place all your cards down as fast as you can - but make sure the order and direction are correct!

Welcome to the Kung Food World Championships! You, the crème de la crème of Ninja chefs,will need to prepare the best recipes in the fastest time, since only one can be crowned Grand Master of the Wok. Wok’up!

On the signal, all players simultaneously throw an Ingredient token so that it spins a couple of times and falls in the Wok.

     If no Recipe card is completed, the players must simultaneously throw in another token. A Recipe card is complete when its conditions are met using one or more of the Ingredient tokens inside the Wok.
     Once a Recipe has been completed, the first player to cover the card with their hand wins it, and then places it in front of themselves. Next, the player turns over a new card from a different stack so that there are always 3 cards visible on the 5 decks. The players all take back an equal number of tokens (it doesn’t matter which ones), placing them at the bottom of their respective piles, and then the next round begins. It is possible to complete several Recipes with the same throw. If players cover different Recipe cards simultaneously, they each win their respective card. It is also possible for a player to win two completed Recipe cards at once, by covering them simultaneously.


3. If a player mistakenly covers an incomplete Recipe card, they won’t be able to play for the rest of that round. The Ingredient tokens and Recipe cards stay where they are, and the other players continue to play the round, by throwing their next token.

A game about witchcraft published by Parker Brothers of Salem, reportedly withdrawn within a few years due to local sensitivities.

Description from the Illustrated Catalogue of Parker Brothers' Standard Games (1889):

"The most popular card game published this season. An excellent game to play, and suited for all ages. The cards are of different tints and are illustrated with well executed etchings of several characters of old witchcraft times. Every card is thus illustrated. The object of the players is in emulation of the good old spirit of 1692 to capture as many witches and their able assistants, the black cats, as possible. Retail Price, 25 Cents."

The game presents all phases of the Battle of Kock, the battle for the Brest fortress, as well as a hypothetical scenario of the German-Soviet conflict in 1939. Although the game is a standalone title, it can be combined with other games of the September 1939 system (W-39), thus creating one big game about the entire Polish Defensive War of 1939.

—description from the publihser

The Battle of Kock was the last battle during the invasion of Poland in 1939. It took place between 2 and 5 October, 1939, near the town of Kock, Poland. During the battle, the Polish Independent Operational Group Polesie, commanded by General Franciszek Kleeberg, fought against the German XIV Motorized Corps, under the command of by General Gustav Anton von Wietersheim.

This game uses the W-39 system developed by Taktyka i Strategia.


     Game counters: regiment-battalion-company
     Board: 2 x 50/70 cm
     No. of scenarios: 4
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
The Denlands are under siege! Floods, fires, rockfalls… no creature is safe. It’s up to you, the newly-appointed Scouts, to protect your home and neighbors from disaster. You must get to the bottom of the catastrophes by exploring treacherous terrain, investigating strange structures, and driving off the mysterious GREM creatures who are growing in number (and mischief!)

In Boundless Stride: Into the Denlands, you’ll assemble a team of 4 Scouts out of 6 playable critters. Lead your team to explore the land and complete scout missions. As the story unfolds, permanently unlock new locations, maps, and missions in this unique, co-operative legacy game. The game itself evolves over the course of multiple plays, with map stickers, location modifiers, new abilities, and hidden codes.

The innovative open movement mechanism in Boundless Stride creates a feeling of exploring the wilderness. Each scout uses a unique set of track tokens and a special ability to move across the map in a freeform way. The game includes four colorful maps that are completely free of grids or pre-defined movement spaces. Instead, you’ll use track tokens to construct a path for your character, carefully matching each track token with the terrain beneath it. On the way to objectives, you’ll reveal encounters that may grant resources, offer special actions, or trigger new content from the encounter book.

In Boundless Stride: Into the Denlands, the rules and components of the game will permanently change through repeated plays as you place stickers, solve puzzles, mark progress, and record your findings. As Scouts, you'll explore the vast Denlands, charting the stars, learning new animal languages, and uncovering the mysteries of the GREMs.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Delta Six Patrol: D6 Solitaire Wargame
description:
Delta Six Patrol is a solitaire D6-based tactical wargame system in which players use D6 dice to represent friendly and enemy team composition and firepower to complete challenging missions while engaging randomly generated enemy units. Players recruit team members, specialists, and equipment based on mission parameters and points. Teams traverse geomorphic maps to reveal and attack enemy units of unknown number and disposition. Player Teams maneuver and attack enemy units of unknown number and disposition to achieve mission objectives.
Players will need at least six D6 dice, preferably of two different colors to distinguish friendly and enemy units.

Game Features
Unit Scale – Teams/Squads
Map – Hexagonal. One hex=50 meters
Turns – Minutes
Playing Time – 10-20 Minutes
Players – One (Solitaire Play)
Dynamic Enemy Bot system.
10 Missions plus 2 Bonus Missions.
Comprehensive Play Aid.
Easy to follow Two Page Rulebook.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Advanced Squad Leader: Starter Kit Expansion Pack #3
description: ASLSK Expansion Pack #3 includes two new 8" x 22" geomorphic mapboards (h and g) and eight new scenarios set in locales such as Poland, Sicily, Italy, Russia, the Gilbert Islands, and the Marianas.
family: Series: Advanced Squad Leader Starter Kit

boardgame: First in Flight: Clement Ader Promo Card
description:
Originally available through Tantrum House Season 9 Kickstarter.

1 double sided card pilot card

Blue side: You may not use skill cards until you are famous.

Ability: Instead of your turn, other players ignore your pawn for the next 2 actions, then resume play as normal.

Pizza King is a family-friendly card game where you score points by collecting the right toppings to complete pizzas.

In a typical round of the game:
Each player is dealt four Topping Cards and one Pizza Card is revealed.  Then each player decides on one Topping Card to discard.  This is repeated twice more, ending up with three Pizza Cards revealed and each player having nine Topping Cards left in their hand.  Then each player gets the opportunity to score points by completing as many of the revealed Pizzas as they can with the Toppings in their hand (as well as the "Free" Topping or an opponent's discarded topping).
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: The adult party game where you try to submit the worst reactions to bad situations. Roll the die to select and read a bad situation, then ask the other players how to make it worse. They submit answers from the Make It Worse cards in their hands of 7 cards each. Read the answers aloud and award a point to the player who made the bad situation the worst.

"Football, Bloody Hell!" (Game's tagline)

Set building card game based on professional football/soccer.

Each player has a 'Manager' card with two preferred formations. The goal is to build either formation with 'Player' cards. 'Players' have specific positions they play in, a point value from 5-20 points and also 'Managers' that they do not work well with. A manager is limited to the number of such players he can have. Each turn, a player draws a 'Player' card from either the draw deck or from the 3 face-open cards or discards a 'Player' card on top of one of the three face-open cards (thus taking the card below out of the game).

Once a formation is completed, a player may end the round by revealing his lineup. Points are scored for players in their proper position and bonus points for completing a team first and there's a deduction for having players who don't work with the manager. The player with the highest score wins!

Apenparadijs is a sociable family board game for 2 up till 6 players. The age limit is 8 years and older, as there is a significant factor of strategy and thinking ahead in the course of this game. Apenparadijs is a race to the finish between the red colobus. The players take off in an epic battle full of obstacles and ways to oppose their opponents. Players and also speed up their movements by building an banana farm for example. Until the end of the game it will be fierce fight. The length of a round will take about 30 until 45 minutes, however if you have more or less time available, the board is adjustable to your preferences. Extra flexible!

In Apenparadijs the players will journey on an epic adventure through the jungle of Central Africa. As each player attempts to reach the promising paradise (finish) first, they are bound to face serious challenges. There are multiple obstacles along the way and meanwhile your rivals will attempt to sabotage you. All ingredient for a fun and competitive family board game, surrounded by funny looking monkeys.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
An unofficial fan expansion for Catan.

Placing The Siege on an opposing player will give one control over the Settlement or City in question. But at a cost...
With just one game piece, various new challenges and strategies arise. The expansion provides more ways to initiate attacks in the game while maintaining it well balanced.

The Siege introduces scenarios for both the base game of Catan and its expansions; Seafarers, Cities & Knights, Traders & Barbarians, Explorers & Pirates and Treasures, Dragons & Adventurers.

The defense of Westerplatte went down in history thanks to the heroism of 216 Polish soldiers attacked by over 2,500 Germans. Attacked from the air by Stukas, shelled from the sea by the battleship Schleswig-Holstein, as well as by artillery and heavy machine guns, they lasted seven long days, inflicting heavy losses on the attackers.

—description from the publisher

Westerplatte is a peninsula in Gdańsk, Poland. Between 1926 and 1939, it was the location of a Polish Military Transit Depot. The place is famous for the battle in 1939 and a long Polish defence (7 days) against German forces, with Polish defenders being outnumbered (216 Polish soldiers vs approx. 2500 Germans) and outgunned.

The game offers two scenarios: one historical and the other one more hypothetical.

boardgame: Resident Evil: The Board Game – Into the Darkness Expansion
description:
Tiptoe through the Third Floor and the underground cave system in tense new scenarios where you’ll face rotating tiles, boulder traps, and poison. Watch out for the enormous Yawn’s gaping maw, and the ensnaring webs of Black Tiger and her spiders...

Dare you step into the darkness and take on these two huge new bosses, so big they engulf multiple squares?

Including six gripping new scenarios, this expansion adds 8 hours of extra gameplay to your Resident Evil: The Board Game campaigns. Or, play each scenario as a terrifying standalone experience.

boardgame: Resident Evil: The Board Game – Bleak Outpost
description:
Enter the Guard House and underground Aqua Ring in tense new scenarios featuring sinister new mechanics. You’ll encounter flooded tiles, undead insects, the ferocious Neptune, and the constant threat of Plant-42’s searching vines dragging you into the depths...

Are you brave enough to explore this strange world and face these two huge new bosses, so large they take up multiple squares?

Including six gripping new scenarios, this expansion adds 8 hours of extra gameplay to your Resident Evil: The Board Game campaigns. Or, play each scenario as a terrifying standalone experience.

Get ready to excavate dinosaur bones! Diggin' Dino Bones is a puzzle game that will have you digging for bones and assembling the skeleton of your favorite dinosaurs. You are the paleontologist commissioned to find the bones of a specific dinosaur. Search the excavation site and be the first to find and assemble your Dino bone puzzle to win!

—description from the publisher

The object of the Insider Trader is to manipulate the stock market by acquiring, selling and stealing information in order to position yourself in the right place at the right time to outsmart your competitors. The players travel around the outside of the board forcing stock prices to go up or down. In addition to this, players can buy inside information, which they can use to gain an advantage over the other players. The way in which each player controls their movement on the board, will ultimately see them prosper, bankrupting their opponent or face huge, personal financial losses.

—description from the designer

This expansion for Gem Getter Pro includes 8 additional dig sheets, and 2 new roll sheets. New dig sheets all feature inner edges for an added level of interest, and plenty of variety to offer gem getters a fresh puzzle every time they play!

An updated campaign sheet is included to accommodate the additional roll sheets.

—description from the designer

Ahoy! Welcome to the Golden Age of Piracy where reckless adventurers love to raid all over the Cariddean Sea assaulting merchants’ fleets and plundering any kind of valuable goods … while avoiding unfriendly cannonballs from well-disposed corsairs and some other ominous threats.

Then, who is the greatest Raider of the Cariddean nowadays? At the end of today’s challenge only the one that will have looted the most precious booty will gain this title, grabbing and wearing the unique and prestigious “Reddhat of the Raiders” till next competition starts over!

So, hit the waves and may the winds blow your gritty sails mates! AArrgh!

Overview of the game scope and versions:
Each Player is a Raider of the Cariddean, greedy for riches and prestige, competing with other felon mates. Game scope is to raid the merchant ships convoy aiming to collect sets of goods cards which will end up in assigning Prestige points according goods majorities and Ship cards collected.
Game can be also played in Solo mode with different difficulty levels.

• Travel edition is cheap and compact, gives you all what you need to have fun with the Designers’ original concept.

• Deluxe edition includes all expansions and promo cards developed before the game launch which can be added one-by-one or bundled all at once in order to increase the playing group up to 5 Players; adding variants with new ingredients (cards and tokens) and increasing the scoring opportunities as well. Moreover, this edition is much fancier in terms of materials (and we know how complacent and greedy our Pirates are when holding a real treasure chest with golden doubloons! Yaarr!)

note: simplified Rulebook will be uploaded soon!

-- description from the Publisher --

boardgame: 仲良シふれんず5人用拡張  (Nakayoshi Friends: 5th Player Expansion)
description: An additional suit of cards for Nakayoshi Furenzu which allows for a fifth player. It also includes one additional rule.  The expansion is available for both the first edition (large numbers) and the second edition (smaller numbers).

It was that time of year again, where all the sheep on the farm got their wool trimmed. As they herded into the barber shop, one by one they got freshened up, and went on their way. That was until BzZzZz " I SAID LEAVE THE TOP NOT BUZZ IT!" And that folks was the start of the biggest brawl in barnyard history.

As the first year was snatched from the barber and turned against them, the struggle to get revenge resulted in wool being trimmed in places no sheep would want bald. It was total mayhem.

So grab a shear, it's a shave or be shaved world, hopefully you won't have to leave the farm out of shear embarrassment.

—description from the designer
A Japanese wargame on the campaign in 1944 in France.

boardgame: Katacrash: ¡El loco juego de las columnas chifladas!
description:
Place rings to put columns on top. Once complete, place another ring and put more columns on it, until it falls.

See also Tower Collapse.

How about some more heroes? With more heroes you can get a lot of variety out of your games. You can also play them in solo (either as or against them) and even play 3-4P team games.

Includes:

     Griyan Hero set and AI sheet (for solo play).
     Mub Zane Hero set and AI sheet (for solo play).
     Imperator II solo expansion.



What is the mark of a true champion? Legendary heroes clash across the Neververse to find out.

Battlecrest is a card-based tactical skirmish game for 2 players (1-4 with expansions). With perfect information, players must truly outwit their opponent to win.

Characters are composed of 6-card sets featuring a hero card, a health tracker, and action cards. Occasionally a minion or item will replace an action card.

The map is built of 6 cards as well, with the cards randomly placed in a set pattern.

Players take turns maneuvering their characters around the dynamic, and often dangerous map while unleashing powerful attacks. As action cards are used they open up new possibilities and empower the character’s battlecrests. Build up to decisive strikes or refocus to get powers back. Every character has a unique set of actions and preferred tactics, so no two battles play the same.

—description from the publisher

In One Card: A Las Chapas you will compete to be the fastest by creating three-word sentences with the three letters of each patent, respecting a slogan. Whoever gets 2 victory points will win the game!

Suggested Components:

     1 6-sided Dice
     4 Chips of the same type for each participant (pieces of paper, legumes, coins, etc).

The Drawing Game Where ANYONE Can Be An Artist! No art skills required!

Discover a world of artistic fun with Picablo, an inclusive drawing game for everyone! Whether you're a professional artist or you've barely doodled, Picablo ensures everyone can join in the excitement.

Inspired by a unique shape die, players create imaginative drawings based on prompt cards. The game caters to diverse play styles, offering both competitive and cooperative game modes. Draw, guess, and party on with endless combinations and endless laughter.

Each game is a new creative journey, no two alike, offering tremendous replayability. Picablo is more than just a game - it's an innovative way to unlock your creativity, engage with friends and family, and create unforgettable memories. Add Picablo to your collection today for an exceptional drawing game experience!

A balloon ride ends on a small island without mobile receiption.

As stranded, you have to escape from the island with your two friends. The box contains 50 cards which have to be turned one by another. They contain information and small puzzles to solve and questions to answer. The solution of the puzzle is each on the back side of the card. After reading the cards and solving the puzzles, you escape from the island and win the game.

Deep Dive: Wild Food Tiles Mini-Expansion is a set of KS Promo tiles included with KS pledges. These tiles can be used in any game if Deep Dive by shuffling them into their respective depths during setup. Wild tiles act like any other tiles except they can be any of three colors (assigned to a specific tableau column when collected). If collected before all if the tableau columns have been defined, then they must be placed on the first row/set of the tableau and their color determined once the other two colors have been added to that row/set.

The Mines of Pekari is a strategy game for three to six players. Each player takes on the role of the chief of a tribe of goblin miners under the rule of Zafrat, the Goblin King. During the game, you will make decisions on how to allocate your resources (ingots) by providing good working conditions for your miners, paying tolls to the Trolls of Tuuk for the use of their shifting tunnels and mountain passes, paying for mining reports, or buying items from the goblin merchants. This will be done in the face of uncertainty regarding mining conditions, the demands of the trolls, gem price fluctuations, and testing availability.

Each round, you will have the opportunity to dig from one of four mines and then sell gems that result from your dig. As you dig deeper into the mines, conditions will become more treacherous as the threat of a cave-in or the release of dangerous gasses increases. Your opportunities, however, will also increase. Word of the mines' riches will quickly attract Goblin Merchants with valuable wares to sell to your miners, and the Goblin Engineers will offer to sell you information about the likely bounty of your efforts before you dig.

At the end of the game, the goblin chief with the most ingots is the winner.

—description from the publisher

Using the typical Family Pastimes cooperative game mechanics, most similar to "Snowstorm", Community Food Bank sees players cooperate to drive the lorry truck around the city gathering collections to the warehouse, before then distributing them to those in need - but cautious of closed streets, traffic accidents and other perils that could cause them to run late and miss the day's quota.

Welcome to Pengy Island where you and your friends take on the role of Antarctica's greatest heroes - yes, we're talking about superhero penguins. Pengy and his Pals need your help to fight off the Walrus Gang, the Albatross Alliance, and more. The easy to learn mechanics help you get new players to enjoy the cooperative combat genre, while experienced gamers will find the strategic depth rewarding.

Pengy Pals is a cooperative, dice rolling, combat game. Each Pal comes with two unique dice that are added to a community dice pool. The unique symbols and dice create challenging and fun decision-making for you and your pals. But be careful! The Enemies will use the dice you choose to instantly react.

Take on the role of four unique penguin superheroes. Each penguin comes with unique action cards, dice, and abilities. Choose your favorite playstyle, and discover the hidden ways that every Pal can kick some Albartross butt. Let's just say, even the healer can pack some serious punch once you learn how.

Characters must also balance the amount of Fish they have in their community Fish Storage. Players work together to build up the supply and will need to cooperate on the best ways to use the Fish available. Not only will you eat Fish to perform abilities, you will need to make sure you have enough Fish to keep your Pals well fed.

The three unique Enemies create new challenges and new ways to play the game. This game isn't just about combat. Unique win conditions, like securing resources or surviving a killer whale attack, keep players on their toes. Challenging variants also keep the game fresh and rewarding for more experienced gamers!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Žižka: Reformation and Crusade in Hussite Bohemia, 1420-1421
description:
Bohemia, 1420. The King was dead. Less and less able to resist the encroachments of higher nobility and ever more prone to leaving the matters of state in the hands of others, Wenceslas IV of Bohemia had passed away that previous August. Allegedly, a stroke killed him upon hearing the news of the defenestration in Prague of anti-Reformation councillors. Wenceslas’s brother Sigmund (or Sigismund) of Luxembourg, King of the Romans, expected to succeed to the Bohemian throne.
 Enter the Hussites. By early 1420, the Bohemian adherents of early-Reformation scholar and preacher Jan Hus, who died a martyr’s death in 1415, had grown in number and confidence. Radical Hussites even founded communes, awaiting the Second Coming of Christ.
 Sigmund, frustrated by his stalled succession and obligated to the Catholic Church, organized a crusade against the Bohemian heretics, supported by his Catholic loyalists. The Hussite factions rallied, fought the crusaders, and in June 1420 soundly defeated them before the gates of Prague. When Sigmund’s rivals in the Holy Roman Empire launched a second crusade, the Hussites beat them again in late 1421. In the first two years of the Hussite Wars, the one-eyed haytman Jan Žižka and other Hussite leaders had managed to secure two great strongholds—Prague and Thabor—along with most of Bohemia, making the Hussites a power which would soon threaten all of neighboring Europe.
 In Žižka, designer Petr Mojžíš (The Bell of Treason) brings Volko's Levy & Campaign series to the heart of late Middle-Ages Europe. The iron Lords of the Holy Roman Empire can withstand even crossbow bolts, and gunpowder is in its infancy. A new army emerges to collide with the seemingly invincible knights: peasants with no training but fanatical religious faith and therefore no mercy.
  
The Hussites start with almost no Strongholds, cannot obtain Coin from the populace, and are slowed by the vulnerable flock of women and children accompanying their flailmen. They must build their capabilities step by step and use unorthodox tactics and weapons like war wagons and various firearms. Or perhaps their religious songs can even scare away the crusaders!
 The seemingly strong Catholics have challenges too. The number of crusaders joining the cause as well as the timing of the crusade are uncertain and the logistics daunting. Two mighty fortresses and a fortified hill complicate the struggle for control of Praha (Prague).

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Dark Deeds: Loot of the Gods Expansion
description: An expansion for Dark Deeds, Loot of the Gods contains six Loot cards for items infused with the magic of gods, saints, and sorcerer

boardgame: Cursed Empire: Heroes of Thargos – Undead Expansion
description: This is an expansion for the Heroes of Thargos card game that allows players to play Undead Anti-heroes or play a survival game against them. Additional rules mean that you can slay your opponents heroes and turn them to join your shambling ranks of zombies and undead heroes!

Sequel of A las Chapas, with new cards and categories.


In this game, a card with license plates (3 letters) is drawn, as well as card with a category (movies, people, etc.). Quickly, players must create sentences using these letters and the given category.

The player who creates a valid sentence faster earns a point, and after a given number of rounds, the player with the most points is the winner.

Some variants are included.

boardgame: League of Dungeoneers: The False Prophet
description:
This expansion makes a huge game even bigger. It introduces 2 new playable species as well as 2 new professions. There are 22 new double-sided tiles allowing the heroes to explore natural caverns and mines all over the Kingdom. New treasure and enemies awaits those who dare venture forth!

With a new campaign and 17 new quests, this expansion provides hours upon hours of exciting dungeon crawling adventures.

—description from the designer
In One Card: Jardín Japonés you will seek to score points by making small decisions that make a difference, rolling dice to draw decorations and freely drawing ponds.

In Fours to Heaven is a strategic level board wargame about the first campaign of the Second World War. Battle for Poland, that took place September 1st to October 6th, 1939.

The game portrays dramatic clash between Poland and Hitler's Third Reich. It was the first introduction of what will be later called "Blitzkrieg" tactics. From military point of view, Poland was doomed from the start due to many reasons. Because of this, this story cannot be told without taking them into consideration. The biggest issue for Polish defenders was stubborn implementation of defensive "Plan West". It assumed Allied intervention or opening of the second front days after the potential attack. Many flaws of the plan and pointless actions, if the circumstances were different, caused quick downfall of Polish defenders.

The story in this game is told mostly from the Polish perspective, introducing Polish propaganda, morale and General Staffs standpoint. All of this combined creates many layers of the campaign beyond simple hex&counter wargame, which is crucial to the gameplay and makes Polish player really decide, how to play.

The game covers the whole campaign from it's start on the September 1st to the bitter end on October 6th and can be played in many ways, as a whole campaign or in series of smaller scenarios. Unit scale is division/regiment, 1 hex covers 10km of the real terrain.

—description from the designer

One card version of Lunes.


Lunes ("Monday") is a fast-paced, single-player board game in which you must avoid being caught by your boss and cut out of the office once you finish the essentials for the day.

In each round, you move your peg through the corridors of the office (which is a modular board), and your boss will perform movements through an AI comprised of action cards and automated movement. Before you can leave the building, you have to reach the printers and get reports to deliver to your colleagues, who will then cover your escape and reward you with useful objects to achieve your goal.

In the city of IURE many people are deprived of their dignity, but some young people have decided to change the situation. With their actions they will try to assert their rights, but to succeed they will have to cooperate. A story and a board game to build a city, where dignity and rights for all rule. Produced together with Amnesty International.

Players will needs to play action cards, use different city buildings cards and follow news card instructions in order to improve the value of their city layout and gain a specified amount of points.

Expansions of the Cariddean features 5 different modules plus a set of promo Characters to enhance the experience of the base game, making it every time different.

It includes all expansions developed before the game launch which can be added one-by-one or bundled all at once in order to increase the playing group up to 5 Players adding variants with new ingredients (cards and tokens), increasing the scoring opportunities as well.

You can enroll shady character and exploit their special abilities; or gather map fragments and keys to obtain more prestige; grab precious stones being aware of the cursed amulet; assault opponent ships to steal their goods...

o Exp.1: Welcome to Shella        (10 cards, Town folks, double side) 
o Exp.2: Smugglers and Strugglers (10 cards, Onboard crew, dual types)
o Exp.3: The lost treasure        (6 cards, Treasure map fragments)
o Exp.4: The cursed loot          (6 cards, Shipwrecks’ presents)
o Exp.5: Flying knives            (10 cards, Raiders’ stabbing )
o Special promo cards: Castaways  (4 cards)

—description from the Publisher

After the fall of the Berlin Wall and the division caused by the Cold War, the world opened up gradually  to a period of globalized economy in which the modern powers began to vie for world hegemony.

In Statecraft, you will manage the destiny of one of those great powers in its struggle for hegemony. Your first choice will be to select a leader for your power. Then, it will be time to prepare your agenda: use your votes in international organizations to secure their support, obtain allied governments with the help of your influences and have your spies ready in case of conflict. But try to get a good public image achieving your leader's objectives in each round... or everything will become more difficult.

Statecraft is a deeply interactive political board game that reproduces some of the most relevant situations of recent history. It is played in four rounds plus a final bonus points round. At the end, the power with the highest score will be declared the winner of the game.

—description from the publisher

The game with lovable trash! Be the first to sort your cards in sequential order. Avoid the garbage monsters so you don’t lose a turn!

Players begin the game with a grid of 10, facedown cards. The start player draws a card from the deck. If the card is a number card, and that number’s corresponding space in the player’s grid is still facedown, the player replaces the card in their grid with the drawn card. If the card replaced can be placed into the player’s grid they may do so. This may continue until they replace a card that cannot be placed into their grid or until they reveal a garbage monster. Any player who completes their grid in the round (all other players get one more turn after the first player achieves this) then starts the next round with one fewer card in their grid. The first player to complete the 1-card grid wins the game.

Beat the voice commands and pass the level. You have to be fast. 7 commands and 4 difficulty levels. Complete the commands correctly and pass the level: red inside, blue outside.

Commands can be:
Throw in the air, grab for a color, touch a color on the outside, touch a color on the inside, spin 5 times.

Rummy like never before! Scorzo brings a new strategy with every round. Try to win big, but don’t get stuck with too many points!

Players attempt to make their entire hand full of melds (3 or more of a kind or 3 or more in a run). Once one player achieves this, they yell “SCORZO,” the round ends and players score. Cards in a players hand that are not a part of a meld are added to that player’s score. Lowest score wins after 11 rounds.

We live in a world where the demand for boomies is growing because collecting them has become fashionable. Yes, as if they were stamps. But it's not so easy to get them, and the boomie hunters are overwhelmed with so many requests… Who we gonna call? 
The scares they cause can't compete with the thrill of adding one more to the collection.

In this fun game of auctions and chance, you'll need to acquire the most complete and valuable collection of Boomies to win the match. To do so, you'll bid with viscous and use all the tools you can wisely.

boardgame: Boss Monster: Dungeon Kart Promo Cards
description:
Three promo cards for Kickstarter backers of Dungeon Kart.  These cards can be used in any of the Boss Monster base sets.

Cards included are:

Go-Kart (Universal Item) - When equipped to a hero: The hero only enters the first and last rooms of your dungeon.
When used as an item: Move a hero at the entrance of any dungeon to the last room of that dungeon.

Haste (Spell) - Play after building your first room this turn. Reveal your first room and build another room on top of it following normal building rules.

Finish Line (Advanced Trap Room) - [4 Damage] If a hero survives this room, destroy this room.

A legend speaks about some ruins that hide mystical mechanical treasures, that have amazing gems and the more you open the more powers they will offer you!

Mechanical Treasures is a game from the Minikin Saga series. In this game you must keep the most valuable treasure to win the most points and the player that gets a certain number of points will be the winner! But beware, if more players have the same value treasure as you… they will be worth nothing!
On your turn, you must decide to open a mechanical chest or to pass.

If you open a chest, you are going get information of what type of treasure your opponents don’t have, and you must activate that chest's ability if you can.

The more mechanical treasures that get opened, the more powerful the abilities will get!
The round will end in these 3 scenarios:


     All players pass consecutively.
     A player reveals the Value 1 Mechanical Treasure; that player gets eliminated for the round.
     There are no Mechanical Treasures available to open in the Ruins.


When one of these scenarios happens, all players that are still in the round, reveal the Mechanical Treasure they own, the highest value treasure (That doesn’t repeat) wins that many points!

If no player got the points to win, prepare a new round. The player with the lowest treasure and least points, starts the new round!

—description from the designer

boardgame: London: Twilight's Game – variations on a theme
description:
LONDON: ‘Twilight’s Game’ – variations on a theme is a suite of Cold War themed strategy games using progressively building, but eminently simple, rules in which two or more adversaries seek to outmanoeuvre and/or best their opponents on a squared grid nominally representing the London of Len Deighton’s and John Le Carre’s twilight world of spy fiction.

In the basic game, two Players take turns to place and move their agents on a 54-square grid comprised of six 3-by-3 tiles.  There are three types of square:

     ‘Normal’ locations (in the basic game there are forty these).
     ‘Black bag’ squares (there are eight of these, more than one on some of the tiles and none on a few others)      
     ‘Tube’ (London Underground) locations (the basic game has just six of these, all on different tiles.)


More advanced variants of the game include the following additional types of space:

     Taxi spaces
     London bus spaces
     Telephone box spaces
     Safehouse spaces


The basic game is for 2 Players. The first variant allows 3 or 4 Players to play with accordingly different board configurations and some simple changes to the basic two-player rules.

Have you got what it takes to be the ultimate spymaster in the treacherous and murky World of Deighton and Le Carre’s twilight city?

Components (the items needed to play):

     The Print & Play gameboard tiles (available in the files section)
     24 wooden playing pieces (tokens, pawns or blocks etc) [six each of four different colours]
     The rules.
     A set of numbered 'stickers' to differentiate an adversary’s agents for some of the advanced variants in the Suite.


Optional: a printed playmat picturing a map of central London onto which the gameboard tiles can be placed.

Objective of the game and how to play:
In the basic two-player game the winner is the Spymaster who can manoeuvre three of their four agents off their adversary’s edge of the board.  These spies are deemed to have infiltrated their opponent’s agency (SIS or KGB). The 3/4-Player versions of the game bend these objectives slightly – although a choice is provided in the rules as to how this is done. Players take turns to move one of their spaces. The regular move is one orthogonal space. However, this can be extended in one of three ways:

        A spy moving onto a ‘Black bag’ location has to move on quickly (they do not want to get caught!) – such a piece can move a further space, again only orthogonally.
        A spy moving onto a ‘Tube’ (London Underground) space may on a subsequent turn move from that space to any other ‘Tube’ space.  They will then have the choice to remain in that new location or move a further space/spaces before completing their turn per the normal rules for moving.
        A spy can make a ‘jump-type’ move over an adjacent agent. Such moves still need to be orthogonal but can be ‘chained’ for a spy to move longer distances and the pieces being jumped over can be one’s own or an adversary’s.


Spaces spies are moved to must be vacant. No spy – friendly or otherwise – can share the same space.  (That would be poor Tradecraft!)

No diagonal move is allowed in the basic game and Players are not allowed to “Pass” their turn.  Nor can a spy's move be ‘reversed’ in the immediately following turn.

A little information about the variants
One of the more advanced variants of the game described in the rules allows for the elimination of enemy agents. A Spymaster skilled, or lucky enough, to kill two enemy agents automatically wins that variant – although there are further variants that allow for even this to be otherwise.  Spies can only be killed adjacent to ‘Black bag’ locations.

Players cannot move an adversary’s spies in the basic game.  One of the variants described however allows for this.  Further variants introduce additional types of space – for example the ‘Taxi’ space allows a spy to move to any space within the same tile or to any space, depending on the value associated with the ‘Taxi’, up to 1-2 tiles away.  Another variant introduces “values” for the spy pieces and a further variant introduces the idea of “missions”.  There are twelve of these. For example:

      'One of your spies must visit two safehouses.’ 
      ‘Each of your spies must travel at least once by bus.’ 
      'None of your spies must enter the central square of a tile.' 


Both the above variants provide options for introducing additional game mechanics reflective of a need for bluff and counterbluff intrinsic to the murky world of Cold War espionage activity.  For instance, the variant introducing the attribution of values to spies allows for pieces passing over an enemy agent to gain “intelligence” on that agent's value.

Players can choose which different variants and/or optional rules they wish to incorporate in their game play.  The game is suitable for Players aged 8 to 80+.  Several games can be played inside 30 minutes even using combinations of the different variants.

The rules of the game and each of its variants can be found in the files section – as can several different versions of the gameboard tiles, the “Mission” Variant cards and other sundry items that can be used to assist in playing the game.

—description from the designer

In the family-friendly co-operative board game Guardians of Matrimonia, you are an elite event-planning team ensuring that a fantasy wedding goes as smoothly as possible.

In their role of bardfluencer, necromanager, holistic cleric, or ale-chemist, players each have their own unique abilities to tame the magical emergencies that threaten the big day, whether the client spouses are goblins, dwarves, or eldritch hiveminds. You need to put out fires, keep the guests partying, and make the spouses happy. The more successful the celebration, the better your rating at the end of the festivities.

Guardians of Matrimonia includes a competitive mode in which you collect tips, as well as multiple levels of difficulty and rules variants to keep gameplay fresh.

Fill your tub with squeaky clean dinosaurs in this fun colour, counting and observation game with three ways to play!

Spread the cards in the middle of the table, spin the spinner and race to find a dirty dino that matches by colour and type. Once everyone has a tub full of clean dinos, count the number of bubbles... the player with the most wins the game!

Hey ladies, it's time to have a little fun without leaving the house! It's the perfect addition to any girls’ night or bachelorette party, so don't miss out on the hilarity of girl code!

The girl code card game is a Cards Against Humanity type question and answer game. Look out for wild cards including: shares, dares, callouts, and immersive challenges. With 350 cards, you'll have a new game each and every time you play.

This game was created for ages 17+. Some topics in the games are intended for adults. Remember, girl code is NSFW!

—description from publisher

1804: Louisiana. Can you find a waterway to the Pacific?

"Lewis & Clark" is a free mini-scenario to discover the Cartaventura game!

Cartaventura is a collection of immersive historical adventures in pocket size. Use cards that offer different options, travel, explore, and meet the right people to provide the best possible ending for the hero you play.

Each scenario takes place in a different place and time in history.

—description from the publisher

1804: Louisiane. Parviendrez-vous à trouver une voie fluviale jusqu'au Pacifique.

"Lewis & Clark" est un mini scénario offert pour découvrir le jeu Cartaventura !

Cartaventura est une collection d'aventures historiques immersives au format poche. À l'aide de cartes qui proposent différentes options, voyagez, explorez et faites les bonnes rencontres afin d’offrir la meilleure fin possible au héros que vous incarnez.

Chaque scénario se déroule dans un lieu et une époques différents de l'histoire.

—description from the publisher (French)

Who will seize the title of the most daring pirate?

DeckHand is an immersive pirate-themed deck-building card game. Craft your unique deck from a diverse set of 63 cards, each offering distinct effects. The dynamic board state influences your choices as you engage in cunning plays and interactions.

Players take turns accumulating gold coins and strategically purchasing cards from a constantly changing 6-card supply. Your goal is to acquire the most powerful cards, build synergies, and outmaneuver opponents to possess the cards with the most prestige. Time is of the essence, requiring you to execute your strategy efficiently in a race against the clock.

Easily brought to the table, DeckHand offers a playtime of around 30 minutes for 2 players. With minimal rules and only cards as components, setup is quick, allowing new players to dive in rapidly.

Every card in DeckHand is different and therefore plays a role in contributing to the unpredictability and depth of each playthrough.

description:
Electric 4 in a row Game - The first 4 in a row board game that features an electric counter. It is a strategy game that requires fast reactions and is suitable for all ages. Ready for some strategy, fun, and excitement!

Get 4 in a row - Simple and Unique game rules for 2 players: win the game to create 4 same-coloured tokens in a row in any direction within time limitation.

Electric Counter - Provides 5 levels count time, maximum 10 seconds for Beginner Level and minimum 2 seconds for Expert level. Increase the excitement of the game and provide more fun then classic one.

Choose Your Own Difficulty - Allows 2 players choose their own difficulty at the same time. Make your game more challenging, enjoy the game together no matter you are beginner or expert.

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Electronic Versions of Non-Electronic Games

description:
For centuries, Bees of a particular hive have lived in harmony and respected the division of labour amongst them. Things have changed! There is chaos in the hive. Who will come out on top! There is fight in the hive!

Fight in the hive is an elimination card game that is played 2 rounds. Players gather points during each using various ways and tricks. The player with the highest point after each round wins.

Mechanics:
Kill steal
Alliance
Battle card driven
End game bonuses
Matching
Player elimination

—description from the publisher

In The Battle for Sampo, you lead a village as a hero. You have combat and rune scores, and unique powers, and aim to become the legendary hero by claiming twelve heroic deeds. Deeds require different tasks, like battles or helping the village. Manage skills, companions, and resources while balancing different actions. Search for the Golden Sampo for bonuses, but watch out for dark deeds that can tarnish your honor. The player with the most deeds wins in the end.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Unlock!: Attack on the Grand Express
description:
This exclusive demo adventure will allow you to learn Unlock! quickly and without reading the rules.

Unlock! is a cooperative card game inspired by escape rooms that uses a simple system which allows you to search scenes, combine objects, and solve riddles. Play Unlock! to embark on great adventures, while seated at a table using only cards and a companion app that can provide clues, check codes, monitor time remaining, etc.

Note: Unlock! requires a free application to be downloaded from the App Store or Google Play. Once downloaded, an internet connection is not required during game play.

Step into the world of pizza, profit, and a pinch of crime. In this pizzeria-themed card game with an underworld twist, each player runs their own restaurant.

Ingredient cards let you collect tokens—meat, cheese, veg, and staff — needed to fulfill table orders and earn points. Item cards can boost your restaurant’s efficiency or sabotage your rivals.

Clever combinations of special items unlock bonus points at the end of the game.

The player with the most successful (and most cunningly managed) pizzeria takes the crown.

-description from the publisher-

boardgame: Flying Colors: Beat to Quarters, Vol IV
description:
Beat to Quarters, Vol IV (Under the Southern Cross) is an official expansion to Under the Southern Cross by GMT Games, LLC and requires a copy of that game to play.

One of the most common criticisms of the Flying Colors system is the clutter of status markers on the map and that they may detract from game play. This concern can be minimized somewhat by using status sheets and a handy pencil to keep track of things on paper. This, however, adds a separate record-keeping aspect that the design of the game goes out of its way to avoid.

Beat to Quarters provides the ultimate way by which to keep track of the status of individual ships through the use of individual tracking cards that are kept conveniently near the map and that are laid out to conveniently hold a variety of status markers such as Broadsides Fired, Sail State, Rigging Damage, and Hull Damage. The cards are designed to be printed at a standard 3.5 by 2.5 inch size so that they can be slipped into card protectors for extended usage.

Individual tracking cards are included for all 214 individually named ships included with Under the Southern Cross. But that’s not all!

As a bonus, Beat to Quarters also includes the print-and-play components for an entirely new way to play Under the Southern Cross. Drawing from games such as Naval War, Modern Naval Battles, and (in particular) Enemy in Sight, Beat to Quarters can be played with just the ship tracking cards, a special set of markers and 8.5 x 11 inch tracking board, and the status markers from Blue Cross, White Ensign. The ship cards are organized into squadrons that maneuver and fire against the opposing fleet within a relatively small playing area. With Beat to Quarters, you can play the huge battles without the space requirements for several game maps.

NOTE: This volume of Beat to Quarters only includes marker sheets for the Argentinian, Brazilian, Chilean, Colombian, Peruvian, Portuguese, Spanish, and Uruguayan fleets.

With Beat to Quarters, you can either enhance or completely change your enjoyment of Under the Southern Cross!

—description from the publisher

Tribulation is a scenario-driven cooperative tactical card game with a dark, biblically-inspired fantasy setting for 1-2 players. You play as an agent of the Lord holding the forces of evil at bay at the end of the world to give humanity one last chance.

In Tribulation you succeed if you vanquish the forces of evil before they defeat you and your teammate. In a solo game, you control two agents of the Lord. If both are destroyed before you can vanquish your foes, you lose. The primary mechanism of Tribulation is tactical combat via card play and action management. You move your character through 3 zones, play cards from your hand to resolve actions, and rest to regain action points, cards and health at strategic intervals. Tribulation can be played as a one-off battle game or a progressive campaign-style game with an advancing narrative and character progression between battles.

—description from the rulebook

Four modular expansions tweak the classic gameplay of Through The Desert with fun twists without diluting the elegant nature of the gameplay. Add one for a slight change, or bring out all four for experienced players looking for a challenge!

Rival Nomads:
Based on a deck of Nomad cards, players will embark on a mysterious journey as they reveal specific and intriguing scoring conditions that challenge them to adapt their strategies quickly.

Djinns:
Visiting oases awakens mischievous djinns, who use their magic to make crossing the desert more difficult.

Special Watering Holes:
Take a camel piece so you can place an extra piece. In a game where the turns are so tight, this extra pace is crucial! Trading is a way of life in the desert. Buying a commercial good is worthless, but finding someone to trade with while having a pair? That's ten points!

Bazaars:
Form valuable trade routes from desert bazaars to distant villages.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Star Wars: Shatterpoint – Witches of Dathomir Squad Pack
description: A group of enigmatic Dathomirians enter Star Wars: Shatterpoint in this pack! Mother Talzin leads one of the largest Nightsister clans on Dathomir, offering her Force-sensitive witches and assassins to those seeking hired killers. This pack gives players a full squad of Mother Talzin, two Nightsisters, and Talzin's eldest son Savage Opress to test their skills against Jedi, Sith, or whatever other enemies may cross their path. No matter if these characters are fighting together as a squad or joining other characters, this pack also contains all the Stat, Order, and Stance cards players need field them in their games of Star Wars: Shatterpoint!

boardgame: Star Wars: Shatterpoint – We Are Brave Squad Pack
description: An iconic Queen of Naboo and her entourage enter Star Wars: Shatterpoint in this pack! Committed to public service from a young age, Padme Amidala fights for her people on the floor of the Galactic Senate to the streets of Theed and beyond. She is joined by her courageous handmaidens, including her decoy Sabe who often protects Amidala by impersonating her. In addition to a full squad of four miniatures–Queen Padme Amidala as a Primary Unit, Sabe as a Secondary Unit, and two Naboo Royal Handmaidens as a Supporting Unit–that can be fielded together or alongside other Star Wars: Shatterpoint miniatures, this pack also includes the unit, stance, and order cards to get these characters into the fight.

boardgame: Star Wars: Shatterpoint – This Party's Over Squad Pack
description: A legendary Jedi Master crashes the party in this pack for Star Wars: Shatterpoint! Few can challenge the prowess of Mace Windu and this pack brings this legendary swordsman to Star Wars: Shatterpoint along with Clone Commander CT-411 "Ponds" and two ARF Troopers. Players can use these characters together as a powerful squad or have them join other characters in their own custom teams. No matter what they choose, players will also find all the Stat, Order, and Stance cards they need to add these characters to any Star Wars: Shatterpoint squad.

boardgame: Star Wars: Shatterpoint – Fistful of Credits Squad Pack
description: The galaxy's most ruthless bounty hunters enter Star Wars: Shatterpoint in this pack! The clever and fearsome Cad Bane has built his reputation working for the Hutts and even Darth Sidious himself and this pack adds him to the game as a Primary Unit. Joining him are the deadly Aurra Sing as a Secondary Unit and a Bounty Hunter unit consisting of a Chadran-Fan, Todo 360, and a Devaronian as new Supporting Unit. In addition to beautifully sculpted miniatures of these characters, this pack also includes all the Unit, Stance, and Order cards for players to make full use of them in Star Wars: Shatterpoint!

The Flyers & Scavengers expansion brings you eight new cards, giving secret goals for each player to complete! Maybe you must keep all your dinos alive, maybe they all need to be extinct, or perhaps, something else! This is a great twist to keep the game fresh for repeated plays.

—description from the publisher
family: Organizations: Game Designers of North Carolina

description:
Escape Room Tycoon beckons you into the captivating world of escape room management, where every decision you make shapes your destiny.

In this thrilling medium-weight euro game, you don the hat of an aspiring escape room entrepreneur. Your mission? To craft, curate, and conquer the escape room scene. The game unfolds over four intense rounds, each mirroring a distinct business season. Your expertise, represented by cubes, is your currency for action.
