Spend it wisely to construct ingenious rooms, adorn your venue with exclusive enhancements, host unforgettable events like graduations and birthday parties, hire talented Game Masters, run promotional campaigns, and strive for the most glowing reviews. But beware, as the rounds progress, the cost of your ambitions escalates. Keep a vigilant eye on your rivals' strategies while advancing your own to secure your place at the pinnacle.

Escape Room Tycoon is the world's first board game that immerses you in the riveting realm of escape room management. It's your chance to become the ultimate escape room mogul.

—description from the designer

The players take turns to roll and move. Each player has his own track from space 1 to space 100. Between spaces 50 and 60 the players may chose to exchange their meeple (pedestrian) for a car. Each turn the traffic lights of the traffic tower are changed. Whenever a meeple gets to cross a street the player has to roll a color die (red and green) to determine if it can do so. The meeple has to look left and right when crossing the street or has to suffer a penalty. If a meeple gets on an orange ringed danger space it has made a mistake and the player has to look up a penalty in the mistake chart.  
The first player to reach space 100 wins the game.

—user summary

A restless spirit haunts this house. The home's new inhabitants call on you, a group of spiritual mediums, to perform a séance to channel the entity and seek answers for the five enigmatic questions that keep it tethered to this world.

Spirit Session is a cooperative word-crafting and deduction card game for 2 to 5 players. On their turn, players will either draw a letter card into their hand or play a letter card down to the table to help craft the five words necessary to release the spirit. But be careful, sprinkled within the draw decks are 'Darkness' cards that threaten to permanently alter the words being spelled or 'Candle Flickers' cards that threaten to blow out the three lit candles on your table. Nothing is safe from the growing darkness.

Spirit Session is a limited communication game in which all players will create a different compelling story with each play. For added difficulty, the game also includes additional game play elements that increase the difficulty for seasoned mediums by adding a cooperative puzzle element to each session.

Can you channel the spirit and decipher the answers of its fate before your candles flicker out? Gather your fellow mediums and prepare for a Spirit Session.

—description from the designer

Dig 'em All is an engaging and family-friendly board game that centers around the exciting premise of a crashed asteroid laden with valuable precious metals in the heart of a desert landscape. This event triggers a modern-day gold rush, where people control drones and robots in their pursuit of gathering these valuable resources. The game is designed to cater to a wide range of players, from beginners to more experienced gamers, thanks to a scalable rule set that allows for increasing levels of complexity.

The gameplay revolves around players taking on the roles of drone and robot operators, each vying to collect as much of the precious metals as possible from the asteroid crash site and surroundings. The board is likely to depict the desert landscape and the crater left by the asteroid impact, with paths, obstacles, and various locations representing different resource-rich spots.

Players can expect to engage in a mix of strategic planning, resource management, and tactical decision-making. They might have to navigate their drones and robots through treacherous terrains, avoid hazards, and outmaneuver their opponents to secure the most valuable deposits. The game could involve elements of player interaction, such as competing for resources, blocking opponents' paths, or even forming temporary alliances.

What sets this game apart is its adaptability to different skill levels. The rule set can be adjusted to accommodate players' experience, allowing beginners and kids to jump in quickly while still offering deeper strategies for seasoned players. This versatility makes the game accessible and enjoyable for families, casual players, gamers and enthusiasts alike.

—description from the designer

Go on an adventure and discover hidden objects in Le village de Nathalie. Fill in your board with your objects and race back to your starting tile to win the game.


Partez à l'aventure, venez découvrir les objets cachés dans "Le village de Nathalie". Complétez votre carte d'identité pour revenir à la maison de votre personnage préféré, et gagnez la partie!

FORTNITE-INSPIRED MONOPOLY GAME: Flip. Outsmart. Survive. In this Monopoly Flip Edition: Fortnite board game, it's not about what players own—it's about how long they can survive

FLIP TILES TO CHANGE BOARD: The dynamic gameboard includes 8 island tiles that flip to change the board and reveal well-known Fortnite locations, hazards, and more. Watch out for a damage trap!

BE LAST PLAYER STANDING: In this battle royale, players earn and lose Health Point (HP) chips instead of Monopoly money. Players who lose their last HP are out! Be the last player with HP to win

BUILD A LOADOUT: Each player chooses a Loadout card with a Permanent Item on it that lends them an advantage over other players. Store Loot Chest items there and use them to attack or heal

ACTIVATE SPECIAL ACTIONS: Roll the action die to boost Health Points, target opponents, and draw Game Mode cards that unleash the Storm

COOL GIFT FOR FORTNITE FANS: Looking for gifts for Fortnite fans? This Fortnite-themed Monopoly game is a great choice, and it's an exciting family board game for teens and adults

—description from the publisher

Get ready for some wild fun in Predators & Prey! There are two parts to the game. First, in the Capture Phase, you try to get the coolest animals and make them stronger. Then comes the Battle Phase, where you use smart moves to fight against others and see who's the last animal standing. The game is super funny and exciting, and even though it's silly, you can use sneaky tricks and smart strategies to win!

—description from the publisher

In Peek a Beak, you are a newbie birdwatcher partaking in an inaugural 8-day birding competition in Sungei Buloh. Your goal is to snap a picture of the 15 elusive birds that call the nature reserve their home, with each one having a unique quest that can be completed for a handsome reward. But there's a catch - bird-eating snakes roam the untamed wilderness and it is your job to make sure that your prized possessions survive long enough for a picturesque snapshot to be taken. Every turn, you roll a set of dice to determine the trail you can take, though be sure to keep your distance from the birds lest you scare them away. So what are you waiting for? Print out a copy of this single-player board game and showcase your awesome birding skills now!

-description from designer

With Volts, there will be electricity in the air. The current may not pass between you. Keep up the pace and pump up the volts. The first to reach 21 volts cuts the current and then points are distributed.

Volts, the game that electrifies your evenings!

Cycling Legends is a strategic racing game in which you, as a manager, will try to get the most points with a team of four cyclists to win the game. With your Team Management Board, you will be able to defend your cyclists against incidents, upgrade the skills of your team leader and to control the energy status of your team. Nine pre-defined stages will keep you busy for a while, but you will be able to make your own!

A unique game mechanism will provide players to do additional actions during their turn which will influence the game. Reacting to unforeseen actions of other players on three different road types where you will need to use your cards wisely and escape on the right moment. Manage your team, use energy and power-ups to perfect your strategy.

The "Elite version" will take you to the next level by adding individual skills per cyclist and more tactical options during the game.

This dynamic game has been designed to maximize the interaction between players.

—description from the publisher

"A lonely Little Ghost appears before us, looking for a friend. Who will it be?"

Bluffing card game where players take turns to play one or more cards face-up or face-down to a central discard pile. Face-down cards may be bluffs; face-up cards affect which cards can be played onto them. A challenge, a 10 or a sequence of passes clears the pile and takes the action into a new suit.

First player to empty their hand wins.

Deck of 51 cards; 1-10 in five suits, and a first-player marker.

—description from the designer

At Keukenhof, the festival of tulips in the Netherlands, the tulip fields wash the countryside in gorgeous colour, dotted by the iconic windmills. As a tulip farmer, you want to have the best, most valuable tulip fields. Will you bloom your way to victory?

A very portable & easy to learn tile laying game of tulip fields, greenhouses, & windmills.

Lay one of two dual-coloured tulip tiles on your turn and place windmills to claim fields for points. Bonus end game points are awarded for public scoring goals selected randomly at the start of the game, and you can add another "greenhouse tile" scoring goal during the game that only you can score. Can you maximise its scoring *and* placement bonuses?

—description from the publisher

Krypto-nine is a strategic 3-5 player family-style card game.

How to Play: The game is played in two parts. Players take turns playing combinations of cards from their hands in sums of 9. (See example images). The goal is to play all the cards in your hands before your opponents, so using a higher combination of cards per turn is recommended. 
As soon as a player manages to play all their cards, they win that round and are awarded with a Victory Card.

Victory Cards allow players to manipulate the game by adding their own personality and creativity to gameplay. Players build their own unique strategy, create/change rules, and destroy any opposing strategy when they're used the right way.

The first player to successfully play 9 of these Victory Cards wins the game!

Krypto-nine features ambidextrous playing cards for both-handed players, basic math skills to help the younger players have fun learning, and offers a wide range of playability to ensure it’s a great time for all fun-seeking individuals!

DustCollectorz - Champions Unleashed is a collectible trading card game introducing monsters from the world of Seraphia. The monsters have been trapped and turned into cards.

Players of the game have several game mode options. A two player fighting mode allows players to clash on any surface. A player starts the clash by attacking with a monster or monsters from a specific tribe and declaring which stat value will be used. The second player counters the attack with cards that can defend against it. Each card can have special abilities that can alter the course of the game. The player with the highest stat value wins the round and their cards are captured. The game is played until one of the players runs out of cards.

A second game play mode is a 2v2 four player team mode. In this game mode, there are three clashes per round. The team that wins two clashes wins the round. Cards are played until all of one player’s deck and hand are exhausted.

The goal of the game is to have the highest number of points in a player or team’s capture pile. Points can be earned by capturing creatures in clashes.

boardgame: My First Adventure: Tracking the Dahu
description:
Today, you’re setting off to find a legendary animal.
Today, you’re going to climb the mountain to photograph the Dahu.

Who do you want to be for this hike?
Aïvy, the sportive girl?
Will, the swiss-knife boy?
With ...
Barry, the mountain dog?
Peregrin, the tamed hawk?

Choose your duo and enter this exceptional story, where each of your choices will affect your chances of photographing the Dahu!

Tracking the Dahu is the 8th book in the "My First Adventure" collection of stories in which you are the tiny hero. Choose your character and plunge in this rather unusual story, a story where each choice you make will change your adventure.

—description from the publisher

Somewhere along the line, you made a dangerous enemy. The Exarchy themselves have sworn vengeance upon you. Now their chosen champion stalks you at every step. Prepare yourself for the fight of your life as you face the mighty Imperator.

Imperator solo expansion enables gameplay for one player to play as any hero against any other hero using these 6 solo Command cards and that hero’s AI rules insert.


What is the mark of a true champion? Legendary heroes clash across the Neververse to find out.

Battlecrest is a card-based tactical skirmish game for 2 players (1-4 with expansions). With perfect information, players must truly outwit their opponent to win.

Characters are composed of 6-card sets featuring a hero card, a health tracker, and action cards. Occasionally a minion or item will replace an action card.

The map is built of 6 cards as well, with the cards randomly placed in a set pattern.

Players take turns maneuvering their characters around the dynamic, and often dangerous map while unleashing powerful attacks. As action cards are used they open up new possibilities and empower the character’s battlecrests. Build up to decisive strikes or refocus to get powers back. Every character has a unique set of actions and preferred tactics, so no two battles play the same.

—description from the publisher

Behold, a world forever changed, where humanity's remnants struggle to survive amidst the ruins of a once-great civilization. They call them Slayers, the heirs of mankind and scourge of abominations, masters of the eternal power of blood.

Rebirth: Carved in Blood is a Living Card Game set in an alternate world ravaged by the battle for control over Earth's last precious resource: blood. Each player takes charge of a group of Slayers, the elite descendants of humankind, and leverages their abilities to subjugate opponents and strengthen their rule over the Battlefield.

An evolved resource management, a structured Battlefield, and the presence of unique dual-natured characters and powerful neutral creatures make the gameplay distinctive and highly strategic. Master harnessing the fury of abominations to your advantage, gather blood, and unleash the power of Transmutation to awaken the true strength of your Slayers.

Three game modes are available: 1vs1, 2vs2, and Draft. To infuse more intrigue and strategy into the Draft experience, players can manipulate Draft piles by spending Blood Points.

—description from the designer

How about some more heroes? With more heroes you can get a lot of variety out of your games. You can also play them in solo (either as or against them) and even play 3-4P team games.

Components:

     Griyan Hero set
     Griyan AI Sheet: for solo play, requires Imperator expansion.



Battlecrest is a card-based tactical skirmish game. With perfect information, players must truly outwit their opponent to win.

—description from the publisher

How about some more heroes? With more heroes you can get a lot of variety out of your games. You can also play them in solo (either as or against them) and even play 3-4P team games.

Components:

     Mub Zane Hero set
     Mub Zane AI Sheet: for solo play, requires Imperator expansion.



'Battlecrest is a card-based tactical skirmish game for 2 players (1-4 with expansions). With perfect information, players must truly outwit their opponent to win.

Players take turns maneuvering their characters around the dynamic, and often dangerous map while unleashing powerful attacks. As action cards are used they open up new possibilities and empower the character’s battlecrests. Build up to decisive strikes or refocus to get powers back. Every character has a unique set of actions and preferred tactics, so no two battles play the same.

—description from the publisher

Calling all backyard inventors! Reveal a creative idea card, then grab an assortment of wheels, gears, pipes, food and other fun items to build a device that matches the prompt MAGNIFICENTLY.

200 sturdy cardboard pieces can be combined in infinite ways to make innovative inventions and wacky contraptions.

When time is up, budding inventors explain why their invention is the MOST magnificent. Fun character pieces (Girl, Boy, and Dog) can help illustrate a device's function.

Use the award ribbons to recognize Best Story, Most Inventive, Brainiest Solve, and more!

This game is based on the book "The Most Magnificent Thing" by Ashley Spires.

Smart, clever or downright genius … are you a maker of magnificence?
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
New York City is one of the most iconic and influential cities in the world. While the city was founded in the 1600s by Dutch settlers, over the next few centuries New York City transformed from a small trading post into a global center of commerce, culture, and innovation.

In Grand Plan: New York City, players are urban planners who build this city from the 1600s through the early 2000s against the backdrop of historical events. A unique system of historically based chronicle cards allows new rules to come into effect and expire over the course of the ten turn game.

Players lay out the grid plan of Manhattan across the island and into the sky, developing new neighborhoods, placing influence, and engaging with the news of the day.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Earth's Mightiest Core Set
description:
Marvel: Crisis Protocol is a tabletop hobby miniatures game set in the Marvel Universe. Players assemble, paint, and collect an ever-expanding line of highly detailed plastic miniatures representing iconic Marvel characters. Players choose characters from their collections to form their own Marvel-inspired dream team, then pit their chosen forces against each other on an interactive tabletop where the very terrain itself can be thrown, crashed into, and destroyed during the super powered showdown.

Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Earth's Mightiest Core Set serves as a starting point for new players, a revision of the 2019 Core Set that features updated characters, more dice mitigation in those character powers, four character affiliations instead of two, and other changes.

All previous Marvel: Crisis Protocol expansions are playable with Earth's Mightiest Core Set.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Don't you forget about them!  The Teens are here to take part in all the radical action from the 80s.  Will you put together the perfect Brunch Club to stand up against the dweebies?

—description from the publisher

Each player takes 11 pawns of his color and distributes them to the magnetic board in some specific spaces. Players take turns, moving their pawns according to the rules, trying to kick their opponent's pawn outside the board.

boardgame: BarRoom Brawl: The Festive Advent Calendar Game!
description:
Behind each of the 24 doors you’ll find a daily surprise, which builds all you need to play the fun festive game of tavern mayhem – Bar Room Brawl.

Get the food and drink back to your friends, avoiding spillages. Avoid the bouncer and don’t go shoving the other guests! The first ‘party’ to fill their table wins the game. Outdo the other players to be named pub champion!

This is a one-print game, containing everything you need to play:

    6 limited edition, festive-themed, highly detailed Mantic resin miniatures – 4 players, the barkeep & his bouncer!
    All the TerrainCrate furniture you need to build your Tavern & a unique, holiday themed game board.
    The game tokens & dice needed to play `BarRoom Brawl’.
    Complete game rules – these are in the box, not behind a door – but no peeking until the 24th or you’ll ruin all your surprises!
family: Misc: Advent / Christmas / Jule calendar game

description:
Ivion combines the intensity of a fighting game with the strategy of deck-building card games to create an experience unlike any other. In Ivion, your deck IS your character, and you build it from a variety of classes and specializations. Each character type has numerous cards from which to choose, along with wildly different playstyles. Mix and match them to create your own, unique character!

THE RUFFIAN tumbles through life, never staying anywhere too long. In her wake, she leaves behind broken jaws, empty purses, and filled hearses. Instead of working for her coin like an ordinary sap, she'll get hers with a growl, grab, and a club to the gut.

THE FRIAR roams the countryside, bringing aid and cheer. He claims the Lady herself guides his humble path, though if that's true, she seems to have a soft spot for taverns. He’s not versed in violence, but he does believe in beginner’s luck.

—description from the designer

Ivion combines the intensity of a fighting game with the strategy of deck-building card games to create an experience unlike any other. In Ivion, your deck IS your character, and you build it from a variety of classes and specializations. Each character type has numerous cards from which to choose, along with wildly different playstyles. Mix and match them to create your own, unique character!

THE EXECUTIONER delivers heads for the Baron. By decree, the townsfolk gather to watch his handiwork. The condemned, however, has broken free. And it's laughing at him. He whets his axe; it will start shrieking soon enough.

THE POACHER only follows the laws he can read. As he puts it, can't none get the rule outta him to save the king, mind. Not so. He just live by his code, skin the deer and sell the pelts for shoes, snacks, and trap stuff. Now that's make sense, traps... Couldn't even say how many he hid all about the Kingswood. Might as well be the Trapswood even. This new headsman tho got weird. Couldn't as fun to escape from as the last one. Too stiff to fluster. Nothing a log to the face won't do in though.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Hong Kong Wet Market Board Game
description:
Integrating Hong Kong's daily life into the bilingual board game, The Hong Kong Wet Market introduces players to the other side of Hong Kong's market in a fun way!

Players follow the instructions in the "Gourmet Recipes" card and travel through different markets in Hong Kong, Kowloon and the New Territories to get what they need, as each dish requires different combinations of food.

Sometimes, same types of food may have different price point in different market — just like in real life! In the fastest time possible, and with limited budget on your hands, buy the relevant ingredients, and return to your home!

The fastest player to return home wins the game!

The game feels different every time because of the randomness in the assignment of where home is, and what market card is selected. Players will face new challenges every time!

Through this Hong Kong Wet Market board game, young players can learn simple additions and subtractions, mental arithmetic, route-planning!

Delve into something different this festive season - an escape room game with a sprout-themed twist. The perfect way to get into the Christmas spirit and have fun with all the family.

Successfully complete all 10 tasks in 45 minutes to escape those dreaded sprouts this Christmas.

—description form the publisher

Bon Bon is a card game where cards have a number and various possible symbols - from among three types of symbols, with three different potential colors.

Each player gets a hand of cards, and in the center of the table the players see a card that is on offer this round and the card that will be next round.

Each round players have 3 turns, and on their turn they either draw 1 card or play 1 card face down.  Then, players reveal the cards they played. The person who played the most value in the shape and color combinations shown on the card on offer take that card as points.

The first person to 21 points wins.
publishers: Super Hige Club (スーパーひげクラブ)

boardgame: Host Your Own Escape Room: Museum Edition
description:
When the museum guard tells you about a secret exit and a legend of creatures coming to life at night, you can't resist the challenge, With clues hidden within the exhibits, you set out to solve the mystery and find the exit before it's too late.

This escape room is perfect for the whole family to play, Let your imagination take hold as you embark on an exciting adventure together, all from the comfort of your own home

—description from the publisher
family: Theme: Fine Art and Art Museums

description:
Back to Start is a "roll" and move game, where players' movement is determined on cards they play from their hand - numbered 2, 3, 4, and 5. They will begin on Start, travel to the Turning Point space, and return to Start.

The spaces in between are randomized and begin face down.  When a player moves, they count the spaces, reveal the final card they land on, and activate its effects - such as advancing 2 spaces; causing the player on your right to discard a card; swapping locations, etc.

The first player to return Back to Start wins.

Ding Ding‧HK Tramways Board Game, is a board game base on Hong Kong Tramways which is one of the prominent tourist attractions in Hong Kong.

The tram is an icon of Hong Kong and one of the best, enjoyable ways to explore the metropolis. The tram runs along some of interest and historic regions of Hong Kong island. It moves at a relatively low speed, so travelers can experience all the attractions of each neighborhoods.

Player will play as tourists and use the "Ding Ding Cards" travel around the northern to the southern section of Hong Kong Island. The fastest player to collect the "Fun Cards" will win the game. Through the "Fun Cards", players not only could obtain to know the geography of Northern and southern Hong Kong Island, but also would receive a better apprehension of the tourist attractions, as well as the other places to see along the tramways route.

boardgame: ねぇ、なに着ていく？ (Hey, What Are You Going to Wear?)
description:
Hey, What Are You Going to Wear? is a game about not going out in the same clothes as someone else.

Players draft the colors and types of clothes they'll wear, and then try to be the only trendy one this season. If another player is also wearing the trendy style, points instead are awarded to the person wearing the nicest clothes.  If this is also a tie, the players who just put on a t-shirt score!

The player with the most points after two rounds wins.

Heart of Venice is a roll-and-write game for all the puzzle-lovers. Each turn, you draw a waterway and a house according to the dice, then the river runs! The rivers, or numbers, fill the port as the game continues and allow you to unlock powerful skills. Fill the port with JUST-RIGHT numbers, connect houses, distribute numbers wisely, and the victory is on your way!

This game spotlights a Japanese traditional game: amidakuji, in which you have several vertical lines to pick from, and navigate from the top to the bottom while following horizontal lines connecting the vertical ones. Traditionally it's played similarly to Plinko, but in this game you are building it!

Take the risk, but plan carefully. Glory of this great city is on your shoulders! The heart of Venice is certainly beating.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Kingscraft: Zwergenkönig Borik / Borik der Große Promo Card
description:
Double-sided promo card, included with Zwergendorf.

Front: "Zwergenkönig Borik"
Power: 12, 3×D6
Speed: - (The special ability cannot be ignored using speed.)
Special Ability: "Beer Enjoyment" - Before a battle against "Borik", the active player rolls 1×D6. According to the result of that roll, a different ban is enforced (1 = shoe slot, 2 = armor slot, ..., 6 = upgraded items).

Back: "Borik der Große"
Power: 15, 4×D6
Speed: - (The special ability cannot be ignored using speed.)
Special Ability: "Beer Enjoyment" - Before a battle against "Borik", the active player rolls 1×D6. According to the result of that roll, a different ban is enforced (1 = shoe slot, 2 = armor slot, ..., 6 = upgraded items).

Welcome back to Crime City.  You have already solved numerous crimes and sent many criminals to prison.  However, there are still cases left unsolved.  Once more, you will take to the streets of this crime-ridden metropolis to get to the bottom of its final mysteries.

The Bonus Box contains 14 cases with 136 cards in total.  The 3 "Epic Cases" are spread across several chapters and all four game maps of Crime City.

Also included are 11 bonus cases.  Some of these were previously unpublished or only available on a limited basis at events and in retail stores.  The Bonus Box completes the Crime City series.

-- per publisher's description
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
A night at an exclusive movie premiere in the heart of Kyoto takes a deadly turn when a dark force awakens from the cursed ground. You'll need to work together to solve the puzzles and prove your virtue before time runs out.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Host Your Own Escape Room: New York Edition
description:
Locked inside Central Station by an infamous criminal gang, it's a race against time to uncover their plan for the heist of the decade, and foil their scheme before it's too late.
Work as a team to solve the sequence of puzzles and input the answers online to discover whether you have been successful.

The difficulty level of this escape room is moderate. Hints can be found throughout to help you catch the bandits and complete your mission.

—description from the publisher

The game pits creatures from 8 factions against each other. The creatures and their fate are in your hands, in the form of a deck of cards.
Creatures' Battle is a challenge-based card game. At the beginning of the game, each player draws four cards from their own deck, which they must replenish at the end of each round so that they has four cards in their hand again. Each round starts with a different player. The starting player uses one of their creatures to challenge the others, who, if they have the right cards, may beat the challenger. The last player to beat the cards with their own wins the round. When the deck runs out of cards the battle is over and everyone counts the points they have collected. Whoever has the most points wins the battle. But be careful, to win you need quality victories, not quantity.

—description from the designer
Party Game Trilogy combines three favourite games: Sketches - Pantomime - Find the word. Draw, describe and pantomime different words for your teammates to guess.

boardgame: Θα πεις και ένα τραγούδι (You will sing a song)
description:
How many games will you find with a word?

Sing any song with the word "love" in the lyrics. Now try the word "holiday". Or do you prefer the word "heart"? I think we just found another musical talent!

boardgame: Wheels of Change: The US Auto Industry of the 1950s
description:
“Always give more than you promised.” –Motto of the Studebaker Corporation.
The 1950s auto industry was a fascinating and unique period in USA economic history. Transitioning from a seller’s to a buyer’s market following the post-war economic boom, the automobile market in the USA was marked by tremendous changes inside and out of the industry. New automotive massed-produced technologies such as automatic transmissions, overhead valve V-8 engines, air conditioning, power brakes and other accessories competed for buyers' attention along with new makes (hardtops and station wagons in particular), annual styling changes and new model lines. New marketing techniques were also developed in the face of rising consumer demands, new communication technologies (especially television), and a growing middle class with increasing cash to spend, as well as a growing need for transportation as millions moved from the cities to the expanding suburbs.

In this game players take on the roles of the independent auto makers Studebaker, Packard, Nash, Hudson, Kaiser-Fraser and Willys-Overland (henceforward referred to as Willys) and try to survive and hopefully even grow during this turbulent decade. The rules and card play cover the actions of the "Big Three" (GM, Ford/Lincoln/Mercury/Edsel, Chrysler/Plymouth/Dodge), as well as national events that impacted the overall economy and automobile industry. A variant adds an additional player to take on the role of the Tucker Automobile Company. Players must allocate resources to marketing, product development, new technologies, dealerships and their various car lines (luxury, family, sport and economy models) and live/adapt with the results.

Each turn represents a year, with each side of the game board that players must transit representing a season of the year. Cards that represent various company assets as designers, new products, dealerships among others are played. Event cards are drawn at various points that can adversely affect, or benefit, one of more players during the turn or game. There are three scenarios (1951-53, 1954-56 and 1957-59) that concentrate on key periods of the decade for the auto industry, and a "campaign game" that covers the entire period. Players win by earning profit and gaining market share, and lose by going bust. Can you do as well or better than your historical car manufacturer?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Fit To Print: Game Breaking News Mini-Expansion
description:
Kickstarter Promo Pack. Includes

9 Breaking News cards
2 Centerpiece Tiles

From the rules: The Game Breaking News Mini-Expansion adds tiles and cards that bend and break the rules of play, creating new spatial challenges and adding completely new gameplay elements!

COMPONENTS:
Cardboard:
2 Centerpiece tiles (63x50mm, double-sided)

Cardstock:
9 Breaking News cards (57x87mm): 3 different Friday, Saturday & Sunday cards

Rules / Title sheet (75x120mm).

Bouquets combines elements from Set, Rummikub & Dominoes to create an elegant tile-based game of pattern creation to test your visual perception!

Multiple players compete to place all of their tiles from their hand out onto the table the quickest! Not only are you creating, building and designing beautiful little patterns of 3+ tiles, you can also take and rearrange anything that has been already played on the table along with tiles from your hand.

While Bouquets can be enjoyed with multiple friends, it also totally rocks as a solitary experience! Test yourself to get rid of all your tiles quickest OR try and make the most crazy rearrangements and patterns you can imagine!

The players take turns to roll and move. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. The first to reach space 100 wins the game.

—user summary
publishers: Verlag von Auerbachs Deutschem Kinder Kalender

boardgame: Слово за слово: Вечеринка (Word for Word: Party)
description:
The game Слово за слово: Вечеринка ("Word for Word: Party") is suitable for a big noisy company, and word games lovers, and for family gatherings! Take a card, choose a word, roll two dice and explain the word without saying both letters. No one will be bored, because while one tries to explain the word, the others have to not only guess the word, but also see if he made a mistake. Get victory points and have fun guessing words and catching the speaker on blunders!

Who the game is suitable for:

     Fans of educational board games, as it offers great opportunities to develop vocabulary and eloquence.
     Family companies, because it has simple rules, fun gameplay and bright components.
     Fans of compact games, because it does not take up space on the table, easily fits in a pocket and conveniently unfolds in any situation.
     Large companies, because it accommodates an unlimited number of participants and the more of you there are, the more fun the party.
     Fans of diverse parties, as the rules provide several game modes suitable for any company.


Improve your vocabulary with your friends and the board game Слово за слово: Вечеринка!

—description from the publisher (translated)

From the first human-carrying kites in China through to the designs of Leonardo Da Vinci to the first modern aviation pioneers – the Wright brothers, human desire to reach the sky has been limitless.
However, the last few decades have been difficult for all airline enterprises throughout the world. In FLIGHT players will take on the role of the Head of Operations. 
You will deploy aircraft fleets to airport hubs around the globe and take part in creating new aircraft. You will manage day-to-day events and lead your specialists to OUTSMART YOUR COMPETITION.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Cthulhu Wars: Nine to Eleven Player Earth Map
description: Some might question the sanity of anyone who desires to play a game of Cthulhu Wars with this many players.  Even the publisher says anyone who wants to do this needs a lobotomy.  Includes a large neoprene mat with an enlarged map of Earth officially for 9-11 players.  The Ritual of Annihilation track, however, has a track for up to 12 players!

A combat-memory cards game where sasquatches lurk near every logging camp.

Lumberjacks spend the daytime chopping and swapping: Chopping adds cards from the deck to the forest grid, while swapping conceals them as they cycle through the players' hands.

In the evening, players must choose face-down cards from the grid one at a time. Find a fallen tree? Live to log another day. Reveal a sasquatch? Lose a life!

Lose three lives and you're out. The last lumberjack standing wins!

—description from the publisher

T-Rex Rumble is the dinosaur-themed dexterity challenge. To play, a player rolls the die and draws one of the 100 challenge cards in four hilarious categories: Prehistoric Pickup, Dino Drawings, Jurassic Charades, and Tiny Arm Challenges. Then, they strap on the tiny T-Rex arms and use the claws to complete the challenge on the card – no hands allowed. The first team to use their T-Rex arms to secure ten challenge cards avoids extinction and wins the game.

—description from the publisher

FRUIT is a tree-shaped board game about the 9 Fruit of the Spirit as shown in Galatians 5:22-23.  
Starting at the root, climbing up the trunk, and going to the ends of each of the 9 branches, be the first player to collect 1 of each of all 9 Spirit Fruit cards. This is done through reading Bible verses based upon the Fruit of the Spirit and by making wise choices, collecting Wild Fruit cards, and going through penalties.

—description from the publisher

The Elegy of Atlas is a skirmish-engine builder tabletop game.

Two players build their civilizations on the backs of massive giants called Atlases. These creatures are the size of a continents and can range from man made monstrosities to natural wonders.

Each turn the player upgrades, researches and evolves their Atlas to become the most powerful entity of the land.

—description from the designer

Triplettes et Caméléon ("Triplets and Chameleon") are two games designed for a "tridimensional" 96-card deck. Each card represents every combination of 3 properties: rank (1, 2, 3, or 4), symbol (spade, heart, club, or diamond), and colour (red, yellow, green, blue, black, and white).

The game includes rules for the following games:


     Caméléon: A trick-taking game for 2-7 players. Players bid the number of card points they must win in tricks with a possible partner. The highest bidder gets to exchange cards with the talon, pick the trump colour, and play with a potential partner.


When playing a card to a trick, a player must follow both colour and symbol; if unable, follow symbol; if unable, follow color; and if unable, may play any card. Highest ranked trump card wins, otherwise highest ranked card that followed the led card's symbol, color, or both wins regardless of how well the card followed the led card. If there's a tie, then the card that followed the led card the best wins the trick (following both is the best, then symbol, then colour). Otherwise, the first card played wins.


     Caméléon Croisé: Like Caméléon, except it's a 4-player, fixed-partnership game. Players include their intended trump colour in their bids and there's no talon.



     Triplettes: A rummy game for 2-7 players. Players meld their cards by playing triplets, which are 3 cards where 2 out of 3 of their properties are all the same. Players can exchange one card from a played triplet with a card from their hand provided that there's still a valid triplet after the exchange.



     Patience du Caméléon. The player deals 20 cards face up and looks for 4 cards whose 3 properties are all different.



     Poker Caméléon: Describes new combinations to use to play any poker variant. Each combination consists of only 4 cards; players break ties by replacing the a card in their combination with a fifth card from their hand.



     Partisans: A 4-player, fixed-partnership trick-taking game where the player who played the trick-winning card doesn't necessarily take the trick. There are only three colours in the deck, one for each partnership and a neutral one. Players must follow symbol, otherwise play any card. The card that wins the trick is the highest ranked card that followed symbol that's not the neutral colour. The colour of the winning card determines which partnership takes the trick.

Soldiers of Napoleon, a set of card-driven, tabletop miniature wargames rules for the battles of the Napoleonic Wars, 1805 to 1815, ten years of almost constant conflict across Europe and a huge subject for one book.

This is a set of rules for battles, not skirmishes. 
It pits battalions, regiments and brigades against each other, but even at this roughly ‘divisional’ scale, it can only recreate part of the many, many, far larger battles of the period. 
It was an era of European history that saw many of the largest battles ever fought on the continent, which helps explains the period’s lasting appeal to wargamers (always useful to have a huge war to refight) as well as providing an historical backdrop to wargames unlike most others for sheer visual spectacle, with all those men, horses, guns and colourful and decorated uniforms."

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Masters of The Universe: Fields of Eternia The Board Game – Enter the Dragons!
description:
With every expansion to Fields of Eternia comes a wealth of new content to explore, and Enter the Dragons is no exception. This new box includes its own unique scenario, quests, and bosses to keep players engaged and on their toes. However, the real highlight of Enter the Dragons lies in the addition of outpost tokens.

At the beginning of each game, players will place one of these tokens face-down on each outpost on the map. When a hero enters an outpost, the token is revealed, revealing a variety of potential benefits. Outposts may generate extra Eternium, controlling it may earn the player extra Victory Points at the end of the game, or the outpost may provide a burst of inspiration to the heroes, granting them unique and powerful abilities.

Outpost tokens introduce another new mechanic: Inspiration cards. When a player reveals certain tokens, they may be rewarded with an inspiration card. These cards may not look like much at first glance, but don't let their unassuming appearance fool you. The power of inspiration cards lies in their ability to surprise your opponents. Since your enemies won't know what cards you’re holding, you can catch them off guard with unexpected moves. Whether it's healing your hero at a crucial moment, playing an additional card during an epic battle, or teleporting your soldiers across the map, thanks to these cards, gameplay in Fields of Eternia becomes even less predictable and even more challenging!
family: Game: Masters of the Universe – Fields of Eternia

description:
IRONMASTERS OF YORKSHIRE is an economic, strategic resource management game set during the Industrial Revolution in the late 18th century in the county of Yorkshire (West Riding). Inventions and improvements in manufacturing processes, advantageous geology and geography, and well-established local practices and traditions determined the progress of industrial change. During this period, the British Empire became the first global industrial power, producing much of the world's coal, iron, steel and textiles. The industrial revolution consisted of technological, scientific and industrial innovations (e.g., mass production, steam engines, railroads, sewing machines, gas and electric light, the telegraph) that led to a tremendous expansion of production, particularly through the factory system.

In the game of  IRONMASTERS OF YORKSHIRE players take on the role of the eminent Yorkshire's ironmasters in the times of the industrial revolution. The goal of the game is to score the most Industrial Points (IPs) at the end of the game. Players will use their own mines and explosives in order to extract resources such as coal, iron ore, minerals and gold. All these measures will be taken with a view to producing the steel using their own Bessemer converter. During the game each player can score immediate in-game  Industrial Points by developing, extracting  resources, producing steel, establishing various companies and constructing railways, docks and bridges. At the end of the game, Industrial Points for the investments, established companies, architect and bridge tiles along with the end-of-game bonuses for the industrial majority in the Yorkshire's towns (Sheffield, Doncaster, Leeds, Bradford and York), will be added to the points players scored over the course of the game. The game is played over a maximum of  seven rounds, each divided into nine phases.

On their turn (Action cards phase) players may perform following actions:

Development - Pay required resources and develop up to two sections of the Player Board.
Mining - Use the explosives from the personal supply to extract the resources (coal, iron ore, minerals and gold).
Smelting  - Use the resources from the personal supply to produce steel.
Investment - Invest in one of the five City Markets.
Establish - Establish a company using previously purchased Company Deed card.
Construct - Construct a Railroad, a Dock or a Bridge.
Collect or Pass - Collect a resource printed on the Action card (it is always optional)or Pass.

On their turn, players may also perform (in any order) following free actions without playing an Action card:

Purchase - Pay 3 gold and take one face-up Company Deed card from the Deed Market on the Main Board.
Loan - Take 5 gold from the general supply.
Recruit - Pay 1 Gold and discard one Action card from the hand, then EITHER draw one Action card from among the four available face-up Action cards OR draw two Action cards from the top of the Action cards deck, choose one Action card to keep and place remaining Action card on the bottom of the Action cards deck.

The specialists (Action cards): Engineer, Miner, Smelter, Ironmaster, Investor, Mayor, Watchman, Financier, Architect, Docker, Swindler and Powderman may enhance the main action.

During each round players go through following 9 phases:

1. First player - a First Player tile is passed clockwise.
2. Event card (optional) - an Event card is revealed (a Telegraph connection might be built).
3. Dividend and Trading House - the players may generate the resources depending on their progress in the game.
4. Seaport of Hull - new resources are placed on Game Board.
5. Action cards  - this phase is divided into two turns, in each turn you preform action(s) playing two Action cards from the hand (each player has five Action cards on the hand). At the end of this phase each player refill their hand, drawing the Action card from the top of the Action deck.
6. City Markets - players with a presence in the city (established company or/and built infrastructure) filled with the invested resources score Industrial Points.
7. Consumption - players with already established companies may consume resources from the Game Board (Docks and Railways might provide resources) and/or personal supplies. Players may lose Industrial Points, if they are not able to consume required resources.If a player has taken a Loan, it must be partially paid back from the personal supply. 
8. Storage - players select the resources from their personal supplies they wish to move to the next round. It always vary and depends on the player's personal storage. If the storage is exceeded, it will be penalized.
9. End of the round/game.- (refresh phase) 


The end of the game is triggered:

1) when the 7th Event card has been revealed (optional).
2) when any player during the round places their last Industry tile from the Player Board on the Game Board.
3) when there are no more Company Deed cards available to purchase.

In any case, players continue to play this round until the Storage phase is concluded.

Scoring:

1. Investment track position.
2. Established companies.
3. Collected tiles (Architect and Bridge tiles).
4. Personal end game tiles.
5. Industrial majority bonus tiles for each city.

Included are four different games. Three boardgames and one quartett card game.

One game is a variation of the wellknown Malefiz.
On the second game the players work as detectives, to find and arrest some bad guys, shown on "WANTED" cards.
The goal of the third boardgame is to be first on specially illustrated event points.
All three boardgames are dice driven.
The fourth game is a standard quartett card game, where the players have to look for and complete sets of 4 cards. The player with the most card sets is the winner.

One can play the 4 games as a contest with winning points given for each game and added after all 4 games have beeen played. Or one can play just one game only.
All characters shown on pictures on the game boards are based on puppet characters from the German Puppet-on-strings Theater
Augsburger Puppenkiste.
A nice game for puppet addicts, and rather rare to find.

The quick question game of “What am I?” is back with Hedbanz Lightspeed! From Spin Master Games, Hedbanz is fun for the whole family. Play your favorite quick question game at lightspeed! Rules change in a flash as your electronic headband lights up one of three colors. Guess as many cards as you can before your headband sings, signaling your time has run out! The player with the most points wins! When it turns GREEN, act out a scene! At lightspeed ORANGE will flash, answer the questions fast! When you see BLUE, give 3 clues!

—description from the publisher
New combinations on the block create additional challenges and demand new strategies.

TimeStrike: Shadow of Fear is a Boss Battle Royale for 2-∞ players. Leave your world behind in this build and battle sci-fantasy adventure as you make your mark across a modular and destructible landscape.

Journey through time itself with TimeStrike, a first-of-its-kind boss battle royale and the first of many games designed to be played on the SOURCE system. TimeStrike integrates with SOURCE's modular system to create immersive and destructible worlds for endless replayability.

At the end of time, the numberless universes collide. It is here that history ends and your adventure begins. Draft a team of legendary characters to journey across this timescape. Scavenge for lost artifacts, mine the map for materials, complete quests to augment your characters, build fortresses and roads to gain tactical advantages, fight and tame mythical monsters to unlock powerful combinations, go fishing for your next meal, and adapt to an ever changing world - all while battling the other players to be the last team standing. At the center of it all a world boss, and master manipulator of time, seeks to erase you from time itself. You’ll compete to control this boss, driven by a deck of unique abilities, and to wield its massively destructive power to control the battlefield and lay waste to your enemies.

Defeated players become one with the game and take control of the boss and monsters to exact their revenge on the survivors. Either one player wins, or the game defeats you all. Experience exhilarating battles with your friends as you command a team of legendary characters from ancient civilizations and distant futures to survive the onslaught of the world boss and a map as shifting as the sands of time.

—description from the publisher

A two to four player game where players compete to be the first player to collect three tokens and bring them to the center of the game board. Players take turns rolling two four-sided dice, one featuring the numbers 1-4 and another with the cardinal directions (East, West, North, South). Players then move one of their pawns accordingly. Landing on an opponents pawn will return them to their starting position, and if they were carrying a token, you can pick it up.

The game has no theme other than a name and celtic knots on tokens and along the edge of the game board.

In Seven Dragons two to four players compete to be the first to collect seven colored tokens from the map, knocking their opponents home and using the features of the map, such as portals, to arrive first.

boardgame: YES or No: The Game Conspiracy Expansion Pack
description:
The most anticipated follow-up to the best-selling game is finally here. With 110+ new cards, you can find out even more about your friends and family, and judge their stances on the most hotly contested conspiracies in American life. Because the original game wasn't controversial enough.

—description from the publisher

Boloa is a connection game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the intersections (points) of an initially empty square grid (board). The top and bottom edges of the board are colored black; the left and right edges are colored white. There is a sufficient supply of stones and pawns in each of the players' colors.

				
				
					DefinitionsA party is a pawn along with all pawns one can reach from it through a series of steps onto orthogonally adjacent pawns. Note a party can include pawns of both colors.

A party is said to be smothered if no pawn in it is orthogonally adjacent to an empty point.

				
				
					PlayBlack plays first, then turns alternate. On your turn, perform exactly one of these actions:


     Place a pawn of your color on an empty point.



     Place a stone of your color on an empty point or on a point occupied by a pawn of either color. If the point is occupied by a pawn, move said pawn to an orthogonally adjacent point that is either empty or occupied by another pawn. The latter pawn is removed from the board. 


Next, replace all pawns in every smothered party with stones of the same color as the majority of pawns in that party. If a smothered party contains the same number of pawns of each color, replace them with stones of your opponent's color.

At the end of your turn, for any two diagonally adjacent stones of your color there must be another stone of your color adjacent to both.

You win if there is a chain of orthogonally connected stones of your color touching the two opposite board edges of your color.

To make the game fair, the second player will have the option, on their first turn only, to swap sides with their opponent instead of making a regular move.

—description from the designer

A Bitcoin Mining Game

Travel the time chain from the Genesis block in 2009 through 2140 when the last bitcoin - ever - is finally mined ... Hodl, hodl, hodl. May the best holder win!

GAME NIGHT FELLOWSHIP Energize family and friend gatherings with this simple to learn, difficult to master game. So grab your favorite snacks, sit down and just play. Laughter, friendly competition, and excitement take center stage. In 45 minutes, you will travel 131 years on the time-chain!

QUICK TO LEARN (15 MIN), ENDLESS STRATEGY (AVG 45 MIN TO PLAY) Learn the game in just 15 minutes, but enjoy the subtle changes in incentives as you progress from one epoch to the next. Each block presents new challenges and decisions, offering fresh experiences throughout the game.
EVERY BLOCK LINKED TO PRIOR BLOCK. The premise is deceptively simple. Add transactions to blocks. Mine blocks to the time-chain. Earn block rewards. Tick tock, next block. Along the way, you build an immutable ledger of how the game was won!

ENDLESS REPLAYABILITY The dynamic, fast-paced gameplay ensures multiple opportunities to fight for and against other players. No two games are alike, making it a go-to choice for fun, unpredictable entertainment that never grows stale. Apply elements of game theory, e.g., opportunity costs of investing in assets, negotiating with fellow players to influence their decisions, or tradeoffs of pursuing aggressive versus defensive postures.

STAY HUMBLE & STACK SATS. It’s not just about winning — It’s about skillfully navigating on-chain-inspired challenges. Adding bitcoin, or "stacking sats", is only half of the story. Beware! You also have to protect your bitcoin from other players who will be only too happy to take yours.

THE BEST, MOST VISUAL WAY TO UNDERSTAND BITCOIN LAYER 1 Beyond being lighthearted and fun, HODL UP is STEM.org certified, so you'll be honing your math and problem-solving skills while you play. It's a 45-minute game, perfect for busy schedules or even as a quick and engaging classroom activity.
PERFECT FOR STRATEGY GAMERS & BLOCKCHAIN FANS Whether you love strategy or have a passion for blockchain, this game strikes the perfect balance between entertainment and education. It’s suitable for both tech-savvy players and casual gamers alike, young players and age-challenged, the timid and the bold.
An exciting game to get to know the euro, learn to manage small amounts of money and make successful purchases and sales!

Chameleons no longer find their way in the jungle! To help them, get rid of all your cards to advance the chameleons from vine to vine, without exceeding the imposed playing area.
 But beware, chameleons have gone crazy! They change colors and direction without warning... Stay alert not to leave the jungle! Joker cards will allow you to trap other players (and save you!) by giving you the opportunity to change the color requested, the direction or both.
 Crazy Léon is a family game that will appeal to the youngest. Contains 54 cards and the game rule printed in the box.

—description from the publisher

Pizza Rush is a fun game in which you deliver pizza!
Move around the board, play action cards, and block opponents. Remember to return for a new order.
Beware of Aunts and Cats, who want their own favorite foods, not pizza.
Be the first to deliver 4 pizzas to their destination and win!

—description from the publisher

Papa Tango gaming rules were designed to give players a feel for the PT actions that oc­curred in the "Slot" around Guadalcanal during World War II.

No attempt was made at a naval simulation but instead the main design goal was that of a fast playing, fun, "Beer and Pretzel" type of game. A game that would give you an excuse to collect and paint up some PTs and Japanese naval craft.

A couple of different design techniques were use. As PT pat­rols and actions almost always happened at night, part of the game would involve just trying to locate the enemy. This could best be accomplished having all of the players on one side and having the Japanese side run by a game master. The other unique design technique was the use of darts as the hit mecha­nism for firing. I had watched a naval game at one of the first Little Wars conventions that use darts thrown at a silhouette tar­gets and it was obvious that the players were having a good time tossing the darts. It just seemed that the game might be fun in­corporating another popular game into the wargame.
 Game players are members of the "Lucky Seven" PT squadron, a fictitious squadron operating in the "Slot" during the Guadacanal and New Georgia campaigns during World War IL The squadron consists of 3-6 players operating with six PT boats. There are two replacement boats for boats lost in combat.

Each game consists of 10 patrols with each patrol lasting up to 20 turns. The squadron has been assigned the mission of protecting the Guadacanal airfield from attack by the Japanese navy and to interdict supplies and rein­forcements from reaching Japanese held islands. So check your ammunition and fuel, man your craft and get ready to tango with the "Tokyo Express".

—description from the publisher

A trading card game series using DMM GAMES IPs, such as Orphans Order, Kamihime Project and so on.
In this game, players should construct a deck with 50 cards to fight with the opponent, like pokémon trading card game, each character card has skills which use energy to deal damage to opponent's character. The player who defeats all of the opponent's characters will win the game.

APP COMES TO LIFE: Experience the legendary app now as a board game in your living room!

MOST TREASURED CHARACTERS: Challenge each other with your favorite characters from the Subway Surfers franchise such as Jake, Tricky, Fresh and Yutani on the three iconic subway tracks.

EXCITING GAMEPLAY: Boost your surfing skills with items, collect coins and dodge oncoming trains in this agile and adventurous game!

—description from the publisher

Cut The Wire is a fun game of deduction and luck. Players are challenged to defuse the bomb as quickly as possible by testing each of the wires. The aim of the game is to figure out which wire is the special defuse wire. Players take turns reviewing the clues that flash on the device and then having a go at cutting a wire.

A variant of the game turns Cut The Wire up a notch by switching from Classic mode to Timed mode. While playing in timed mode, each player has only 15 seconds to take their turn. If a player doesn’t make a move in time, the device will explode and the player will be eliminated. Players must stay calm, listen to the clues, and defuse the device before it explodes.

boardgame: Rise of Babel: Fall of Man
description:
The Fall of Man Expansion adds: 5 Unique and upgradable characters with asymmetric capabilities. 30+ market cards that add more tension. Confusion tiles and player tiles may be moved, blocking opponents, confusing the tower construction. Store Gold between turns. Acquire more powerful resource tiles. Thrive in the new expansion Rise of Babel: Fall of man!

—description from the publisher

A party game for 3 or more players.

How to play:

5 Picture cards are placed face-up in the middle, and each player gets 5 Phrase cards.   Players get 60 seconds to place one of their Phrase cards face-up under one of the Picture cards, trying to create the funniest meme.

The winner is determined by a vote with all players simultaneously pointing to their favorite.  First to five wins the game.

—description from the rulebook

Little animals play on the beach. But how to separate them so that some swim and others sunbathe? Take multi-colored fences
and place them in such a way as to separate some animals from others!

How to play?

     Look at the task cards
     Pick a fence for this round
     Find and capture the right animals!


—description from the publisher

Mice really like to visit their forest friends, but their houses are all very similar. I would not mix up the invitations. Help the mice get to the right house by analyzing their signs and highlighting them.

Мышкин дом ("Mouse House") is a series of fast-paced logic games featuring little mice who love to visit. Each game can be played as a team or as a competition, including older children.

Before starting the game, look at all the signs on the cards - different colors of roofs, characters near the houses, shapes of windows, and a fence around each house. Then choose from four options for the game rules.

     "Find the Mouse": The mouse has gone to visit one of its friends. Players must guess which house it is in.
     "House Lost": Players have to memorize a row of houses and then identify which one was removed by the host. 
     "Where do the little mice go?": The little mice take turns going for a walk and visiting their friends. Players have to guess which houses the heroes of the game visited!
     "The mouse house": Each player chooses a mouse and tries to take his mouse to the mouse house with the help of arrow cards.


—description from the publisher (translated)

Elsa wishes to uncover the mystery behind her magical powers - but will she find out the truth? Fortunately to her, she can count on her sister Anna and their amazing friends. Join your favourite characters in the world of Frozen II, be observant and test your memory!

There are two variants int this game: lottery and memos.

In lottery variant players try to collect a set of all characters by pulling out forest tokens and getting corresponding character tokens.

Memos variant is similar, but more challenging. After pulling out a forest token a player needs to find a matching character token from a pool of face down tokens.

In both variants the first player to collect set of all 6 characters is the winner.

The players take turns to roll and move. Before the game begins, a token is placed on each city (black dot), but 3 tokens are placed on the towns of Brenner, Reit im Winkel and Garmisch-Partenkirchen. Whoever touches these places first during the game may appropriate the tokens on them, but never the one who jumps over the cities at high speed to be the first to be back at the starting point in Lindau.
The winner in this game is not the fastest who first reached Lindau, the start of the journey, but the one who owns the largest number of tokens.

—user summary

Two cards that are part of Spielbox 04/2023.

They are:

     An early-game helper: Cost: 3 blueberries. Gives you in each income phase 1 production of a helper.
     A late-game order: It starts at 2 reputation. Fulfillment requires a value 1-die of each of the 3 different colors. The reward is 3 blueberries.

boardgame: The Binding of Isaac: Four Souls – Tapeworm Promo Cards
description:
7 different foil cards for The Binding of Isaac with tapeworm theme.

- 4 treasure cards
- 1 monster card
- 1 character card
- 1 loot card

They were part of the Tapeworm Kickstarter and free for backer.

Castles of Aragon is a print at home roll and write game for one to unlimited number of players. In this game you will be shaping the land of Aragon as you please. Your goal is to create the most habitable land by putting forests, fields, pastures and houses around six Aragonian castles.

Game is very simple but challenging. During each turn you have to either draw a given terrain type (forest, fields, pastures, town) on the game sheet or add another castle. Terrains will be placed around castles, and each castle has its own rules for scoring.

Solitaire & Co-op game in which the players command USN submarines in the Pacific Ocean during WW2.  The game can be purchased from WargameVault online. A boxed version is in production by BluePantherLLC and will be available from their Web Site in December 2024.
 Starting as a Subron Commander, with six boats, each sends his subs hunting enemy shipping.
Die rolls decide all outcomes. There are War Events, Passage Events, Patrol Events, and Random Events plus many optional rules to enrich gameplay.
The objective is to earn promotion to Admiral, Commander Submarines Pacific.

—description from the designer

The march of humankind’s progress threatens the survival of the American Red Wolf. It's up to you and your team to save them!

Work with your fellow conservationists to save American Red Wolves, challenging negative stereotypes, and gaining the support of landowners.

Historically, thousands of American red wolves were found throughout the southeastern United States. Today, only a small population exists in the wilds of North Carolina, making them the most endangered wolf in the world. Their fragile population has grown through a carefully managed breeding program. In 1987, they were reintroduced in Alligator River National Wildlife Refuge. As of 2023, red wolves are still released there, though their population area now expands outside the refuge. Because a large portion of their recovery area encompasses privately owned land, local support is critical for coexistence.

—description from the publisher

Welcome to Dusk Runners, a cooperative roguelike deckbuilder, set in a torn apart world of years long days and nights ruled by death. 

Take up the role of Dusk Runners, survivors and explorers who stay a few steps ahead of the night. Gather forgotten technologies so powerful they look like magic, battle monsters in the night to get stronger, make allies or enemies of survivor factions and learn ancient secrets in order to defeat the ultimate threat of the darkness: the Nightstalker.

Playtime: 45 min / player (average 2 hours) | Players: 1-4, built-in solo mode


    In Dusk Runners your ultimate goal is to defeat the Nightstalker, the monster of the night that hunts your group. You'll travel from Zone to Zone, each time setting up a different part of the sprawling map of the semi-open world. 



    When you are in a Zone, your goal is to gather as many resources and powerful artifacts as you can, while you make allies (or enemies!) with factions, battle monsters and eventually, when the darkness of the night catches up with you, find a way to escape the Nightstalker.



    Play the game in Turns, where each turn Players must explore Locations, reveal Secret cards hidden under them or fight with enemies. Challenges are faced with your unique deck of at least 14 cards. At each Test you must draw cards with enough resources on them to succeed.



    You can play your cards during a Test to build deadly and amazing combos and utilize secrets and artifacts you gathered to boost your chances and combat power.



    Once out of cards, you must Rest. But with each Rest, the darkness comes closer and you must draw Darkness cards. Eventually, you’ll stumble into the Nightstalker and must flee… Unless you are strong and prepared enough to finally face it and win the campaign.

This is a cut-out-and-play game with a food/cooking theme.

This extension comprises an extra deck of cards and a new task to Dicing with Dinner, allowing random events to change game play and scoring, usually round by round.

Events can affect recipes, scoring, and ingredients. Balance short-term gains and losses against realizing the long-term value of ingredients in your 'fridge.

—description from the designer

boardgame: じゃんけんゴリラ (Janken Gorilla)
description: You are a gorilla. You want to win the best bananas. Each round, you can play a rock, paper, scissors, or gorilla. Note that you are a strong ape, but also, still an ape, so you cannot play a card again until you return the other cards to your hand. The highest unique card wins the banana on offer, following RPS strength, but Gorilla beats all others.

Doomsday is a fast and dynamic card game in which the goal is to collect the least number of card points and force your opponents to collect as many as possible. The number of players is limited by the amount of cards (a maximum of 8 per game is recommended), several game boxes can be combined to increase the number of players (e.g., 16 players with 2 boxes).

The arena is packed and every pass counts. Defend and attack with strategy and precision in your strokes, in a game that emulates volley-ball to perfection.

An adaptation of the original Set & Match, this special Volley-ball version uses the flick to render a better pace in the passes within one side, while its unique dice-based mechanism helps players accumulate strength and precision before hitting the opponent's side of the court.

boardgame: The Battle of the Sambre: 16–19 May 1940
description:
Commanded by Rommel, in May 1940 the 7th Panzer Division was one of the major units playing a major role in the success of the German plan to isolate the Franco-Anglo-Belgian army group on the North Sea coast. It forcibly crossed the Meuse in Belgium on 13 May at Dinant, breaking through the French 18th Infantry Division after a day of fighting. The French hastily committed their reserves in an attempt to contain the Germans. Two first-rank divisions were therefore sent to block the Panzer route: the reserve 1st Armoured Division and the 4th North African Division, which was stationed between Anor and Trélon, on the line of French fortifications near the Belgian border. Coming up against the 1st DCR, the 7th Panzer was surprised by the powerful and numerous French tanks (B1bis and H39), but after a few severe losses, the German tanks, which were more mobile and better coordinated, avoided a confrontation and left it to the well-directed German artillery to destroy most of the B1bis one by one, paralysed by a lack of information on the enemy's action and the rapid exhaustion of their fuel tanks. Faced with the 1st DCR, it was more the 5th Panzer Division that was engaged and momentarily halted. The latter therefore followed the advance of the 7th panzer division westwards, a little further north and with a slight delay. The 4th North African Division fought and sacrificed itself between Dinant and France; it more or less disappeared on 15 May, although some elements managed to return to France. At the end of the day on 15 May, the remnants of the three French divisions (18th DI, 4th DINA and 1st DCR) arrived at Beaumont and, retreating, crossed the French border again.

After clearing his route on the morning of 16 May, Rommel placed his forces between Beaumont and Sivry. It was then that the soldiers of the 84th Infantry Regiment, part of the fortified sector of Maubeuge, learned that the Germans were only a few kilometres away. Despite their initial optimism, they hardly had the means to stop the Germans, as this south-eastern section of the fortified sector consisted only of a line of light blockhouses, mostly equipped with automatic weapons. To the east of Maubeuge, only the fort at Boussois, although not completed, was a significant obstacle to the German advance.

After the 84th RIF's thin curtain of blockhouses, the Sambre was the last possible barrage before Rommel's panzer division and the 5th panzer division that followed a little further north reached Cambrai and crossed the rear of the Franco-British expeditionary corps engaged in Belgium. The French high command therefore decided to recall several major formations to try and stop the Germans on the Sambre. These included several well-equipped North African divisions and a motorised infantry division, but above all the 1st mechanical light division, the elite of the French cavalry. While the French arrived in scattered order to plug the gap and avoid being surrounded in Belgium, Rommel pushed westwards with his reconnaissance battalion, sometimes taking ill-considered risks.

Would the French arrive before Rommel on the Sambre, perhaps even managing to capture the fiery Wehrmacht general? Would the French tanks be up to the task of fighting the Panzers?

—description from the publisher

Ukraine 2022 is a fast play combined arms game, aimed at using printable unit chits provided along with the rules. 6mm or 15mm miniatures can also be used if desired. 
The game covers all the main units used in the 2022 invasion of Ukraine, from infantry armed with a variety of support weapons such as NLAWs, Stugna-Ps, RPO-As, and SA-7s, to the usual BTR and BMP variants, to tanks, APCs, helicopters and UAVs. Fixed-wing aircraft and larger SAM systems are not represented, as the focus is on ground combat, with support from helicopters and drones.

The unit chits provided allow for easy reference of move distances, weapon types and ranges, as well as situational awareness. This game does not use a grid, and is still a traditional wargame in the sense of measuring distances and freedom of movement. The chits are 1" x 1.5" in size, making for easy handling and good readability.

Ukraine 2022 uses a novel order system, where players write orders on slips of paper and place them in a bag. Orders are randomly drawn from this bag, so you don't always have direct control of your forces - they may be reacting to orders from long ago, or you may see the enemy activating instead of your own forces. This injects chaos and uncertainty into the game, and creates a kind of "hands off" approach to unit activation. There are 4 orders: Move, Attack, Defensive Ambush, and Cautious Advance, and each order must have a direction such as "North", Northeast" etc. Units can conduct movement, detection and attacks in a 90 degree cone pointing in the direction of the order.

Solo play rules are provided, using the same chaotic order system, but done on a single sheet of paper and using a D6 to randomize which order is selected.

A printer-friendly version of the rules is also provided. Note that the printer-friendly version does not have the printable unit chits in the PDF - these should be printed from the full color PDF, on to thin card.

Play mat is not provided. A 4' x 4' play area is recommended for smaller games, and 6' x 4' for larger games.

—description from the publisher

Feud is a two-player strategy game set in a kingdom at war. Players take turns to occupy counties and seas, conquer towers, walls, castles, and cathedrals, and strive to earn the highest amount of nobility points to conquest the fatherland throne.

During the game, players roll three dice and drive them on the kingdom map with a flick of their fingers, aiming to occupy regions and seas with their troops. After the invasion, they create counties within the regions and place troops according to the dice values.

The game unfolds as players conquer towers, walls, castles, and cathedrals, earning points for each of these conquests. Additionally, players can use the special powers to earn points for having the majority of regions crowns and conquering the lordships of the Sea,North, South, West, and East.

The game ends when all the towers have been assigned or when both players have exhausted their Grim Reaper icons. At the end, players calculate their final scores based on their achievements.

—description from the designer

boardgame: My First Adventure: The Infinite Library
description:
Today, a nasty wizard has stolen the Book of Knowledge of your peoples !
Today, you will have to follow him and fight in his infinite Library.

Which magician do you want to play?

Lilon the air elf, a flier and healer of all ills?
Camille the enchantress, the never chilly mistress of the ice?
Lucien the fire wizard, an everyday tamer of flames?

Choose your character and enter a story like no other, a story where each of your choices changes your adventure !

The Infinite Library is the 9th book in the "My First Adventure" collection of stories in which you are the tiny hero.

—description from the publisher

The only thing more powerful than a witch, a wizard or a dragon is the combined sorcery of all three!
