While these spellcasters and fire breathers are an ominous force alone, a koven of witches, wizards and dragons can together yield unspeakable magick. It is up to you to unite these divided mythics and create the most powerful koven the magical world has ever seen!

Koven, a solo card game by Galaxy Goose, demands strategic card placement. Each turn, you play one of the two cards from your hand into the playing area, always adjacent to an existing card. Your objective is to group as many like-minded mystics as possible in rows and columns. The game also includes two exciting expansions: Witch Trials and Monster Trials.

—description from the publisher

Let’s find out who has the sharpest eye and the quickest reactions! Gather your friends at the farm and hurry up to feed the animals with their favorite food.

Speedy Feedy is a fun card game for adults and children to develop attention skills. Reveal the food cards, count which food appears the most, and slap the card of the animal that likes this food!

Each round, players take 3 simple actions: reveal cards from the top of the deck, count which food appears the most, and slap the card of the animal that likes this food. There's no time to lose—the player that slaps the card first is the quickest to feed the animal. And the speediest feeder wins the game!

To make the game more challenging, add in new rules and pay close attention to details—don’t miss the flies, crows, lollipops, and pieces of cheese on the cards. Get your friends together and find out who has the sharpest eye!

Today, you’re going to live a mythical odyssey!
Today, you’re setting off to find the helmet of the legendary Jason!

Which hero do you want to be for this adventure?
SAM
PATTIE
CHICKOS
Make the right choices! In this exceptional story, each of your choices will affect your adventure!

Choose your character and enter a story like no other, a story where each of your choices changes your adventure!

Argonuts is the 10th book in the "My First Adventure" collection of stories in which you are the tiny hero.

—description from the publisher

Fleet of Foxes brings all of the fun of battling your friends with spaceships to roll and write games!

Roll 3 dice, then take turns placing your dice completing actions. Players will be able to upgrade their abilities, attack their opponent, regain health, and shield from incoming attacks. Attacks are tallied at the end of the turn less the shield values. Players will gain bonus effects each time they deal 3 or more damage to their opponent.

Players will gain energy to upgrade abilities allowing for stronger effects. The top most row can have up to 3 dice on them while upgraded abilities may only have 1 die on them.  Players can learn abilities to the left, right, or below from a previously learned ability. This will give the players plenty of options for upgrading their fleet.

Do you lean into shields and hit point regeneration? Can you stall your opponent's attack and gradually wear them down? Or do you go for the big blasts and push for that  super nova win of running your opponent out of hit points as quickly as possible?

With 4 different character sheets, players will find plenty of variability and options for battling their foes! Will the Explorer be your guide? Do you pick the Mechanic for her dice changing abilities? What about the MP who can block just about any attack headed her way? What about the Space Marine with his ability to deal huge damage to himself and his opponent? Each character presents their own play style!

Plus no need for holding sheets back with stretch goals or asking backers to pay extra, nope, not here. We want you to have all 4 for just your pledge amount! Join the Fleet and get ready to battle!

Players will find additional challenge with being able to play with up to 3 friends playing 2 vs 2 or free for all with 3 players!

Even more challenges await, we're also including 9 cards for solo or cooperative play! You can even chain multiple challenge cards to battle against the Wolf and his minions.  Its time to take back the controlled space and free the Foxes!

Get ready to roll, write, and win!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Ashes Reborn: Red Rains – The Blight of Neverset
description:
The Red Rains have swept further across Argaia, making their way to the paradise garden-city of Neverset. Home to Leo Sunshadow, this land of eternal sunshine faces its darkest days with the birth of this next Chimera. Join forces with Leo, Astrea, Saria, and Maeoni as they fight to dispel this plant monstrosity in The Blight of Neverset!

This new Chimera fights the Phoenixborn in two aspects, Bloom and Thorn. Watch the Bloom aspects blossom into a display of beautiful glory, while the Thorn aspects ruthlessly strike from the darkness and wither away all that the Phoenixborn can muster. Fight back with an all-new charm magic deck, befuddling the Chimera’s attacks, bypassing its key defenses, and bringing it to fatigue.

—description from the publisher
family: Game: Ashes – Rise of the Phoenixborn

boardgame: A Return to the Islands of the Damned
description:
Expansion kit for Islands of the Damned: Wake Island and Peleliu issue (ATO #43).  The expansion kit is named A Return to the Islands of the Damned.

For the Wake Island: Not Dead Yet expansion game, in which the US solo player is defending against the Japanese attack, the expansion kit includes an addition eight (8) US counters to arrive if there is a Second Japanese Invasion, and represents the hypothetical event of US Task Force TF-14 getting close enough to Wake to unload some or all of its supplies (extra ammo, fuel, aircraft, radar, and more marines).

For the Peleliu: Utmost Savagery game, in which the US solo player is invading the island, the expansion kit includes: 
(1) three US Marine Wardog counters which boosts the US side; plus 
(2) five extra Japanese units, modelling the hypothetical event that a significant part of the 25,000-man Japanese force on the nearby Babeldaob Island actually had been successful in reinforcing the Japanese defenders on Peleliu.

A Return to the Islands of the Damned includes one page of Rules, and 16 die-cut counters.
This expansion for Monster Pit adds the Sand Dragon and Dwarf Necromancer Monsters.

Mistery English is similar to the Game of the Goose. Players have to complete an itinerary. In order to do so, they roll a dice and, every time they enter a box something happens. Sometimes, they have to answer questions, other times they advance 2 boxes extra or they re-roll the dice, etc.

The board is divided into 4 different rooms and, before leaving any room, each player must recover a token with his colour (green, yellow, red or blue).

A player keeps rolling until he misses a question or ends up in a box where he cannot do anything else. The questions in the cards are all related to language and are very basic. Things like: “How do you say (WORD) in English?” “What does (WORD) mean?”, etc.

—user summary
Pictures: USA is the third major expansion for Pictures. Two new material sets and 55 US-themed cards with 110 new photos provide a fresh take on the award-winning creative family game.

Children game based on building couples and securing them with love potions. Rivals may break them up with toads. Players can steal a member of a couple from their rivals aswell. The overall value of the couples can be boosted with cards.

LGTBIQ+ friendly and racially diverse game.

boardgame: Earthborne Rangers: Stewards of the Valley
description: The Stewards of the Valley adds a new Ranger background set, a new specialty set, and it deepens the card pools of each of the background and specialty sets from the Earthborne Rangers core game, giving you even more options for character creation and variability.

boardgame: Heroes of Barcadia: Coin of Magic Pourtals
description:
Hey, did you drop this coin? It seems to have a slightly risky magical aura… maybe you should try using it the next time you go through a Magic Pourtal? Who knows what may happen… We know what’ll happen — when a player uses a pourtal, they’ll have the option to flip this coin for either a reward… or a punishment. Get either +1 Loot Card or -5 HP. Is it worth the risk?

This Heroes of Barcadia Coin of Magic Pourtals adds a fun new mechanic to the game that's 100% optional and 100% awesome! Clinging on to life is something you'll need to get used to while brawling in the Barcadian dungeons, so why not take this coin with you?! It may just be the extra oomph you need, or that final blow that sends you back to the starting point...

Product Specifications:


     Heroes of Barcadia Coin of Magic Pourtals
     Game Add-on
     Available exclusively from Rollacrit
     Size: 1" diameter
     Material: Iron
     100% waterproof
     Heroes of Barcadia game not included.
     Made by Shire Post Mint


—description from the publisher

Ion Award Winner 2023
        Ciutat de Granollers 2024 Semi-Finalist
        BostonFIG 2025 Finalist
        Hippodice 2026 Semi-Finalist
    


In Gumball World, you are a teenager who has just been hired for their first part time job at a candy store!  Sounds pretty chill, right?  Not with Mr. Gimble running things!  You only get paid for filling the gumball machines in the way that Gimble insists and lose money if even one gumball is out of place.

You will fill your gumball machine over multiple rounds where you will draft gumballs and fulfill cards.

You will take turns fulfilling cards with one or more gumballs matching specific patterns that Gimble approves of.  One two stacks of the four cards are gone or there aren't enough gumballs to draft, the game ends at the end of the round.  Also, bribe Mr. Gimble with quarters to gain powerful special abilities that allow you to steal gumballs, take extra during the draft, make any color a wild or score a card if gumballs fall into a scoring area of a card due to gravity!  As you fill your gumball machine, you must follow specific gravity rules.  Do you want to fill the sides and bottom first to create a nice base for the rest?  Or do you want to build up the middle and leave the sides for gumballs that are harder to place?

—description from the publisher

Hole Up is a "holey" word game for two or more players, generally aged 7+. Players take turns stacking up letter cards to make three-letter words.

The cards have one or two letters and one or two holes. The holes line up with missing letters such that when two or more cards are stacked, underlying letters appear through the holes and a new word becomes visible.
             
COMPONENTS 

     72 double-sided letter cards
     Six dry erase score boards  
     Two dry erase markers.


OVERVIEW 
The goal of HOLE UP is score the most points from the words stacked.

MODES 
There are three ways of playing HOLE UP:
• Stack - single stack
• Square - four-sided square stack
• Crosswords - like Scrabble.

All modes can be played solo or with two or more players.
  
COMMON SETUP 

     Shuffle the letter cards.
     Place the cards within easy reach of all players.
     Draw the the top card and place it between the players as the base card in the “heap”.
     Give each player one card.
     Give each player a score card.


GAME PLAY 
On their turn, a player can perform the following actions:
Try to form a word on the heap.
Draw a new card if they cannot make a word.
Stack one of their cards to make a word or combine two of their cards (if available) to make and stack a word.
Players can lay either side of a card. The stacked card must form a valid three-letter word using the letters beneath and on their card. If not a single stack, all words formed must be valid.
At all times, at least one hole will be present and the letter in the hole will be used to form the next word.
 
FORMING A WORD 
A three-letter word is formed using a combination of the letters beneath (as seen through the hole) and those on the card played. 
For example, with the stack showing H A T
and the current card showing [  ] I P , the new word would be H I P, scoring 8 points. The "H" in the hole is used.

SCORING 
Add up the point values of the three letters in the word made. Add the value to the score card.

ENDING THE GAME 
The game is over once the draw pile has been exhausted and no more words can be formed by the player(s).
 STRATEGIES 

     Examine both sides of a card to optimize a score.
     Hold on to a valuable card to use later, for example one with high point value letter like X or Z.


—description from the publisher

In the melon field
A group of orangutans were fighting over who was the wisest gorilla in the world
At that moment, the orangutans pulled out a little green box...
It's a never-ending conversation starter
It's also a friendship detector that tests tacit understanding
It's a thought-provoking thought experiment.
And a philosophical debate.
It can get you caught up in the glories and bleaknesses of human nature
Or it can make you obsessed with digging up other people's secrets.
It's ....

There's been a goose killing in Goose Village.
The first goose witnessed the incident.
But as geese, we can only send messages through body language
And there are some evil geese forces hidden in the Goose Village.
Can we help the police goose to find the last murderer?

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Echoes of Death 4
description:
Echoes of Death 4 includes 4 new survivors that embody memories from another world. These memories can be unlocked by reaching unique milestones in the story of your campaign.

This set includes prototypes of Strain System mechanics. Add your Strain Milestone cards to your game, if you reach their unique conditions, your gameplay changes forever. A new fighting art borne from distant dreams is permanently added to your game.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Longclaw Promo Cards
description:
Longclaw Lenore is packaged in a box that includes:

1 x Hard plastic Longclaw Lenore miniature
1 x Hard plastic Kingdom Death 30mm base and scenic insert
Large matte art print
Indomitable White Lion resource card
Pattern card
Pattern gear card
Install guide

At the end of the longest hunt, Lenore faced the hoary beast. Distracted by the dancing flame in her eye the monster reached out to pluck the bauble from her skull, exposing its soft belly. It was just enough.

The indomitable monsters that stalk the darkness unchallenged tend to grow features not normally found in younger specimens of the species. Survivors brave and lucky enough to survive encounters with these monsters gain indomitable resources. These unusual components inspire new configurations of gear worthy of these hard won treasures.

LUNE is packaged in a box that includes:

1 x Hard plastic Lune miniature
1 x Hard plastic Kingdom Death 30mm base and scenic insert
Large matte art print
The Gaiety character card
Character install guide

The settlement struggled, constantly raided by packs of centiwolves, survivors would often return with less than they departed with. Then, a survivor was born dancing to the wistful tune that emanated in strains from her lantern. Despite the struggling means in which she grew, Lune danced free of care. One day, she heard a song in the darkness that compelled her to spring, swaying into the darkness in search of the melody. Impoverished, and downtrodden, the legacy of Lune still persist in the settlement, brightening their otherwise miserable existence.

Lune is a character who's memory has an enduring affect on the settlement.

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Cresent Tessen Promo Card
description:
This is a set of two promo cards, the Cresent Tessen Gear card, and the related Fan Specialization Weapon Proficiency Card. They were included with the Sen miniature, which itself is not part of the game.

The Cresent Tessen is a weapon with Deflect 1, Barbed 1, and Unfurled: While you have any deflect tokens, this weapon has +2 speed and -4 strength.

The Fan Specialization gives the following bonus: At the start of your act, you may lose all deflect tokens. At the end of your act, if you are standing and have a fan in your gear grid, you may Deflect 1 for free.

Simple tile-laying game consisting of 33 hexagonal tiles. Each tile depicts a part of a huge monster. Playing the tiles from your hand on to the table and connecting them into one or more monsters will score you points, but finishing the game with parts of unfinished monsters will be penalized.

—description from the designer

The Mediterranean naval war includes more intense action in the air and on the sea than we could stuff into our game on the topic, La Regia Marina.

The Middle Sea is a very large expansion set for La Regia Marina: 630 new pieces (210 double-sized “long” pieces, 420 standard-sized square ones) and 40 new scenarios.

It covers the action from the summer of 1942, when La Regia Marina’s story ends, through the surrender of Italy in September 1943. And there’s also the brief 1940 naval war between France and Italy.

You get the full French order of battle for the Mediterranean fleet, Italian ships commissioned during the last year of the campaign or planned to join the fleet, and the American and British ships that participated in these operations and were not included in La Regia Marina. Plus many, many aircraft.

—description from the publisher
family: Series: Second World War at Sea (Avalanche Press)

description:
You awake in the dark with nothing but a candle and...have you done this before? The déjà vu is less troubling than the change it implies. Was the ground always this unstable around you? When the tunnels crumbled into pits, did they always scream? And why does it feel like those screams are getting closer?

The Shrieking Hollow is an expansion that adds a new threat and a new space to explore within The Night Cage. Pits now connect prisoners to the Hollow, where The Other lies in wait. Over the course of play, The Other will rise up out of the Hollow and attack the prisoners, destroying any part of the maze it encounters. If the prisoners are brave, they can leap into a pit and fall to the bottom of the Hollow where the dim light of their candles will stop its advance, temporarily. However, they can't hold it back forever, and prisoners that stay too close to The Other will be attacked when it lurches toward the flickering light it despises.

If you are looking to increase the dread and horror of the original game, The Shrieking Hollow is for you.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Welcome to Jammington, a charming neighborhood filled with extraordinary residents and skilled artisans. What sets this community apart is a fascinating twist - all the homes were constructed using a remarkable ingredient: jam! Yes, you heard that right - jam, it works better than glue!
Thanks to the innovative Jammer 2.0, we have access to the finest quality jam right here in our neighborhood.

In Handyman Jam 2-4 players ages 12+ will repair homes by collecting and using Jam. You will select 1 of 6 unique Handyman characters to play as. On your turn you will collect Jam and repair homes to earn as much money as you can. The 1st player to repair their 9th home ends the game. Use your expert skills to repair homes and earn the most money to win the game.

—description from the designer

Котоквест ("Escate") is an exciting quest game with 42 logical riddles and cunning mysteries. This is the first game in series of Mr. Cat’s fun adventures.
Escate is a great entertainment for family and friends who love riddles, both for beginners and experienced players.
How to play?

     Read the task card.
     Inspect all rooms.
     Find a clue.
     Solve all the riddles to complete main quest.


—description from the publisher

An Abstract Strategy Game for 2 players utilising dice movement in an unusual way.

Be the first to push 5 of your opponents 6 dice off the edge of the board in an strategic game of cat and mouse, whereby the mouse can easily become the cat.

Your dice's strength and value will change after movement based on it's top value prior to movement. With a 6 benefiting from high movement potential but weak strength, to a 1 being the strongest on the board but with vastly restricted movement. As you can guess the numbers in between offer their own benefits and weaknesses.

The sector is in turmoil as 2 or more UFO fleets strive for total domination in this fast paced tabletop game.

And... all rules on a single A4 sheet!

Two or more players have a fleet of UFOs, this can be any number but to start 3 to 5 is recommended.

The UFOs are moved like most other tabletop games by measuring the distance they fly. Important to note is that the UFOs have four fixed speed values. 
The faster a UFO flies the harder it is to hit but also the harder is to target an enemy UFO. Shooting makes use of a simple straightforward gunnery chart using a single D6.

The last UFO (fleet) standing is the winner!

In this expandable card game, players use pre-constructed and highly-thematic decks representing crew among unique starships, illustrated through gorgeous art by Norwegian artist Raymond Heggheim There were three primary pillars of development for Astra during the design process:

•Embracing a unique, interactive game system and departing from standard attack/defense mechanics.

As passionate and active card gamers ourselves, the two founding members of Paper Planet Games have played countless card games at a competitive level. We noticed parts of each of these games that we loved and wished there was a way to mix it all together. That's where Astra: Vestiges of Deep Space came in. What we came up with was FTL meets Magic: The Gathering. We left behind the idea of attack points and health values and, inspired by Android: Netrunner, imagined something entirely different. Instead, players manage the actions of the Crew Members on their starship while equipping their vessels with powerful weapons to take down the opposing crews.

We also loved the fast, single-turn action from Ashes: Reborn, as well as the expandable nature of Summoner Wars. We took our favorite aspects from the games we love and implemented them with our own unique system, creating Astra: Vestiges of Deep Space.

•No Tokens.

We wanted to design a game that can be easily put in a jacket or backpack pocket. Whether you're two friends at a lunch table on a field trip or a couple playing at the gate of an airport, Astra was going to be a travel-friendly game. Once we settled on the idea of abandoning health points and attack values, we knew there was design space to abandon tokens altogether. Cards, after all, are double-sided, which ended up being the key to our main currency and health system. Crew Members have a Wounded and Healthy side, while rooms of your starship have an Online and Offline side. Players will win or lose by their ability to manage the status of their Crew Members and rooms.

•No Deckbuilding.

While deck-building has great reason of being among the fondest card game mechanics, we took the theme and storytelling of Astra too seriously to add deck-building to the mix. We want each faction of Astra to feel unique, with their own goals and their own play style. Additionally, by eliminating deck building, we knew that balancing the game and expanding the game would be significantly easier at the size of our operation. What resulted is a more narratively focused and fine-tuned strategy game.

—description from the designer

Open your Wobbly Pirates stacking block game to begin a seafaring adventure in balancing brilliance. Remove the chocks and watch your shaking ship battle against the seven seas. Roll the dice to see who will be next to walk the plank, as 12 wooden characters line up for wobbly waves of wonder.

As the ship’s bowels are lashed by ever growing currents, your fellow eye-patched pals are struggling to stay on board. It is your job as ship captain to maintain law and order on the high seas. Keep everyone from going overboard or there may be mutiny. Good luck, me hearties!

With Jaques of London’s Wobbly Pirates balancing game for kids, scallywags and scurvy dogs alike are invited into a world of Montessori toy magic, with self-discovery and personal learning leading to the perfect pirate bounty.

Features:

Includes Wooden Boat, 2 Wooden Chock pieces, 12 bright and engaging pirate-themed wooden balancing blocks characters
Montessori Play - Encourages free exploration to build confidence in independent play
Develop hand-eye coordination and fine motor-skills
Discover cause and effect through balance
Crafted from Sustainable Sources
 All hands on deck! Take turns rolling the dice before stacking the corresponding character on top of the pirate ship. Be careful, because one wrong move could send your shipmates plunging into the deep blue below.

Seadogs old and new will revel in the benefits of fine motor skill development, sorting, stacking, and problem solving, with our solo or competitive gaming adventure proving ideal for assisting in the growth of both individual emotional intelligence and cooperative gameplay.

Whether stacking solo of balancing with buddies, Jaques Wobbly Pirates Game is readymade to bring the treasures of learning and play back home.

—description from the publisher

Many will sink. One will survive. Face off with up to 6 players and compete to be the last afloat in the Battleship Royale game! In this exciting party game take on the beloved Battleship search-and-find game, all players share one Battle Grid to mark hits and misses. Instead of placing physical ships, they're dealt Ship cards that reveal their fleet and where their ships live on the grid. The cards slip into handheld Command, so players can secretly track hits. Roll the die to fire to fire on opponents. The last one with a ship left on the grid wins! Ready to level up? Play in Advanced Mode and create an elite fleet with the addition of submarines, which can share coordinates with ships and have ship-detecting Sonar capabilities! The Battleship Royale gameboard stores easily and has built-in trays for pegs. It's a thrilling game for Family Game Night and makes a fun gift for kids ages 8 and up!

BATTLESHIP PARTY GAME FOR GROUPS: The Battleship Royale game is the first-ever Battleship game that lets players face off with up to 6 opponents! Who will be the last afloat to win?
    SHARE THE BATTLE GRID: Inspired by classic Battleship search and find gameplay, this fun family game features 1 shared Battle Grid: players track ship locations by marking hits and misses on it together
    SECRETLY TRACK HITS: Each player is dealt Ship cards revealing their ships' locations on the grid using coordinates. The cards slide into handheld Command Centers that let players secretly track hits
    MANY WILL SINK, ONE WILL SURVIVE: Roll the die for the number and location of shots on the grid, and fire! Mark a hit or miss with a peg (or two). The last player with a ship left standing wins
    LEVEL UP WITH AN ELITE FLEET: Amp up the strategy in Advanced Mode with the addition of submarines, which can share coordinates with ships and have ship-detecting Sonar capabilities

—description from the publisher

In the sunlit expanses of the savannah, not all lions are carnivores. Enter the world of "Vegan Lions", where you're a unique lion with a penchant for plant-based delights. A table top 52 card games where you roam the vast plains, gathering a diverse array of vegan foods from crunchy kale to protein-packed lentils. But it's not just about the food; it's about the friends. Recruit a squad of animal allies, each bringing their own perks and quirks to your journey. But beware! The savannah has its challenges, and you'll need to navigate obstacles that can hinder your progress. The goal? Amass the most points through your vegan collection and animal alliances, proving once and for all that plant power reigns supreme in the animal kingdom. A delightful blend of strategy, humor, and vibrant themes, "Vegan Lions" promises a roaring good time for players of all ages 8+.

Based on the "Assassin's creed" series, take the role of a professional assassin who make is steps in the streets of a full-of-life medieval town, trying put his hand on a high-priced wanted head. Watch-out! your not alone. few experienced assassins want his head too and will do all the necessary, even by take you down...
Make your wise choices to assassinate disturbing guards, get some 'clues' from civilians, find useful objects and treasures and locate the Wanted before them.
But remember, one wanted won't make you a legend...

Instructions(summary):In a small medieval town shown on the game board and divided into zones, the participants distribute the various game tokens, 6 in total: citizens, guards, objectives, event, bargain and tower.
The 'Citizens' and the 'Guards' are placed with their faces up and the others upside down. Each player in turn has the right to choose a face-up token and act according to the roll of the dice. Civilians will reveal info to you so you could flip a face-down token in the same area but be aware - an bad die may also kill them (3 kills of a civilian will get you out of the game). Killing 'Guards' will get you victory points according to the guard rating. 'Bargain' are also victory points but they can be used for bribery. 'Target' will allow you to choose another player and try to rob or eliminate him but also to locate the 'wanted'. An 'Event' will allow you to cause an 'Accident' and instantly eliminate a 'Civilian', 'Guard' or other player (can be useful) and the 'Tower' will allow you to peek at face-down tokens.
The game ends when one of the players finds the 'Wanted' or alternatively if the other players are disqualified (killed too many civilians or were killed somehow). A player who found the 'Wanted' will win 5 victory points.
At the end of the game, victory points are counted and the winner is announced.

boardgame: Burn the Witch: Houses and Heretics
description:
Houses and Heretics expansion to Burn the Witch offers players unique house abilities and introduces a third faction: the heretics. Includes hidden identity cards for innocents, witches, and heretics to maximize the stoke the flames like never before!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Second World War at Sea: Ironbottom Sound
description:
The Solomons Campaign of 1942 saw the American and Japanese navies engage in the most intense naval combat of World War II. For months the two sides fought over Guadalcanal, a miserable jungle-covered island with a vital airbase.

Ironbottom Sound: A South Pacific Story continues the tale that we began in Second World War at Sea: South Pacific. You get 36 new scenarios for South Pacific, covering more of the operations around Guadalcanal including Tassafaronga, Operation Ke (the Japanese evacuation of Guadalanal) and the campaign for New Georgia. The battles of Tassafaronga, Kolombangara and Kula Gulf. And a deeper look at the campaigns we already covered in South Pacific, with still more scenarios.

South Pacific is a rich mixture of game play and history, but there is more to the story than we could fit in the box (which is already filled to the top). The U.S. Navy wrote its most glorious chapter in the Solomons, and it’s a story that deserves the attention we give it in Ironbottom Sound.

—description from the designer
family: Series: Second World War at Sea (Avalanche Press)

description:
AltarShock is a miniatures bloodsport skirmish game inspired by Unreal Tournament and Quake Arena.

With no stats, lists, or points, AltarShock is extremely fast to learn, set up, and play. 15 weapons, ammo, HELTH, and more are placed as pickup tokens around a terrain filled battlefield. Frag, Get Fragged, Respawn, Frag again. AltarShock includes 4+ game modes and can be played with up to 10 people in death match or team game modes.

Prepare yourself, and your friends, for the ultimate bloodsport on the table.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Flesh and Blood Round the Table
description:
Round the Table is a stand-alone game set designed for the multiplayer format of Flesh and Blood.

It contains 4 ready-to-play hero decks with unique playstyles, including three brand-new heroes:

     Professor Teklovossen (Mechanologist class)
     Melody, Sing-along (Bard class)
     Brevant, Civic Protector (Guardian class)
     Ira, Crimson Haze (Ninja class)


The decks featuring over 100 new cards with complete new game mechanics for multiplayer games.

It also contains an exclusive rubber play mat (for one player) and a reusable storage box (for up to 1.000 cards).
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
In this game, a group of thieves, led by a "Mastermind," must collaborate to plan and execute heists. Their targets can include banks, museums, jewelry stores, casinos, corporate companies, government buildings, armored cars, check cashing locations, art galleries, and wealthy people's mansions.

The objective of the players is to accumulate the highest number of points possible throughout the game to emerge as the winner.

The game comprises 10 rounds, with each round revolving around a specific location.

Warning! Sneaky aliens are attacking Earth... and not only that, they've sabotaged our Robots. They are totally out of control! It's time for us, the Galactic Heroes, to fight back and face this enemy invasion. Let's rescue our planet!
Galactic Heroes, a social deduction adventure for 3-6 brave humans. And robots. And aliens.

In this Social Deduction Game, players joining secretly 3 teams and try to outsmart the others.

With easy to teach rules, this could be the 1st Social Deduction game for your Family. Child friendly!

—description from the publisher

Player holding 3 cards. The majority of their color will show their true identity. They could be Human, Robot or Alien.
Players can investigate cards from others to see their faction options and if they think they know who is the enemy, they could grab a boxing glove or a Banana-Thrower from the table and attack the enemies. Thanks to Update cards players can become more powerful, so maybe it is better to collect more updates.

Humans must defeat all Aliens and Robots t win the game as a Team.
Every Robot is a lone-wolf and must be the last man standing.
Aliens wind together if all Humans are defeated.

Every game needs 10-45 minutes is unique thanks to new Update cards, other weapons and other hidden Role skills.

boardgame: Murder at The Magic Mirror Casino
description:
Disgraced magician and ex-convict, Charlie Young, was found dead in a dumpster behind the Magic Mirror Casino in Las Vegas, with multiple gunshot wounds to his torso. Young had upset many people in his time and most recently, he'd been on the run from the law after cheating in a game of high-stake poker. Now his time is up, but there is a killer at large.

The evidence has been collected for you, it’s your job to piece it all together and work out who it was that killed the victim.

27 PIECES OF PHYSICAL EVIDENCE - Inside the case file, you'll find newspaper articles, CCTV images, prison record, social media profile, police reports, blueprint, phone/text records, a coroners report, a map, witness statements, and more.

SEARCH THROUGH A POLICE INBOX - In addition to the physical evidence supplied inside of the box, you'll need to access an online police officer's inbox to prove the suspects innocent or guilty - but it's not easy...you'll need to be a smart investigator to gain access!

A GREAT NIGHT IN - With approximately 2.5 hours of game play (depending on your skill level) and a difficulty rating of 3.5/5 this "whodunnit" style thriller will provide a fun night in for all who choose to participate. If you can't crack the case without a secret clue, hints and answers are available online.

MediLuck Medicines were in the process of developing a new sleeping pill when their formula was leaked online. Nell Phryp, a Private Investigator, had been hired to find the leaker so that they could be brought to justice, but in the course of her investigations, Nell was brutally murdered inside Lab 7G-12.

The evidence that Nell gathered before her death has been sent over to the Police for further analysis, along with new evidence that has come to light during initial Police investigations.

The evidence has been collected for you, it’s your job to piece it all together and work out who it was that killed the victim.

Eliminate as many of the suspects as possible, so that we can find the killer (or killers) and bring them to justice.

 33 PIECES OF PHYSICAL EVIDENCE - Inside the case file, you'll find police reports, blueprints, CCTV images, text records, a coroners report, witness statements and more
.SEARCH THROUGH A POLICE INBOX - In addition to the physical evidence supplied inside of the case files, you'll need to access a police officer's inbox online to prove the prime suspect innocent or guilty - but it's not easy...you'll need to be a smart investigator to gain access to the online portal!

A GREAT NIGHT IN - With approximately 3 hours of game play (depending on your skill level) and a difficulty rating of 4.5/5 this "whodunnit" style thriller will provide a fun night in for all who choose to participate. If you can't crack the case without a secret clue, hints and answers are available online.

On July 4, 1927, Astrid Pelham fell from the top floor balcony of the luxurious Merivale Manor, impaling her body on a statue below. The cause of her death remains a mystery, shrouded in whispers of murder, suicide, and even supernatural intervention. Armed with new evidence, it’s your job to piece it all together and work out who it was that killed the victim.

Eliminate as many of the suspects as possible, so that we can find the killer (or killers) and bring them to justice.

30 PIECES OF PHYSICAL EVIDENCE - Inside the case file, you'll find police reports, suspect photos, witness statements, a manor guide and more.

SEARCH THROUGH AN ONLINE INBOX - In addition to the physical evidence supplied inside of the case files, you'll need to access an online inbox to prove the prime suspect innocent or guilty - but it's not easy...you'll need to be a smart investigator to gain access to the online portal!

A GREAT NIGHT IN - With approximately 3 hours of game play (depending on your skill level) and a difficulty rating of 4/5 this "whodunnit" style thriller will provide a fun night in for all who choose to participate. If you can't crack the case without a secret clue, hints and answers are available online.

In this 2-4 player game, players sail from island to island gathering cards.
The board consists of three islands, each made up of a stack of cards. The cards act as resources, contracts, and upgrades to your ship, and are slowly wittled away as the game progresses.

Resources are just that: resources. They have no inherent value unless accompanied by a contract.

Contracts list what resources -- and what combination of resources -- score points at the end of the game.

Upgrades change what you can do on your turn, whether it be stealing cards from your opponent or gaining an extra action.

The game is over as soon as an island is used up. The player with the most points scored by their contacts wins.

boardgame: RUN: Barcelona and Copenhagen Promo Maps
description:
Two additional maps for RUN.

Barcelona features interesting city blocks with lots of rooftops and tunnels, while Copenhagen introduces ice as a new map feature.

—description from the designer

Includes the following new cards for Imperial Miners: 
6 Mine cards, 1 Event card, and 1 Progress board (Bureaucracy/Journey)
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Shards of the Aegis is a light strategy/take that/party card game that is simple to learn and inspired by Greek Mythology. The deck is made up of different Gods, Goddesses, and Creatures vying to restore the Aegis of Zeus.

Each turn you will draw one card and play one card and use the unique ability of the god or creature you’ve played. Some cards will give you the opportunity to claim a sought after shard or steal them, and others are disruptive or sabotage other players by discarding or rearranging cards. Other effects include exchanging cards, peeking at the cards upcoming in the deck, and much more!

Find the right combinations of cards and the right timing to collect the needed shards in order to be the first player to play an Aegis card and ultimately win the game.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Body of Evidence: Best Served Cold
description:
When notorious local Chef Christain LeClair is found dead on the eve of his restaurantʼs grand reopening, Thornhill P.D. needs your help to crack the case. Bring all of your detective skills to bear in this interactive murder mystery - with a grisly twist!

Study the evidence, gather clues, and sharpen your scalpel as well as your wits to perform a forensic autopsy in order to bring the perpetrator to justice. Conduct your investigation solo or with friends in this fully-offline experience, fit for veteran sleuths and rookies alike. Itʼll take a keen eye, an iron stomach, and a steady hand to make the cut in Body of Evidence, THE murder mystery autopsy game!

Your story begins as all good mysteries do - with a question: What happened to Christian LeClair?

Body of Evidence will guide you through a deductive investigation across several files, challenging you with questions as you build a picture of the case. However, it's not just your field work and evidence from the scene that will lay the truth bare - you'll be heading down to the morgue for an investigation like no other .. To categorically solve this case, you'll need to conduct an autopsy and follow the grisly clues towards the truth.

As your understanding of the case deepens, the documents, clippings, and physical evidence revealed in each new fill will either strengthen your suspicions, or turn the case on its head. It's up to you to keep a close eye on every new development, motive, and alibi. Definitive proof of the matter awaits to be uncovered by the coroner's scalpel, so make sure you wield it wisely.

Follow the clues, exonerate the innocent, and confront the culprit.

-description from Kickstarter campaign

Experience the precision and discipline of 18th century warfare with Battle Formation! Test your strategic prowess as you lead your army into battle, outwitting and out maneuvering your opponent. Unleash devastating volleys into the enemy lines or spread out and pick them off one-by-one. Will you claim victory with your tactical brilliance, or succumb to the enemy's cunning?

Battle Formation is a two-player competitive strategy game. Carefully maneuver your troops, make calculated attacks, and advance your forces across the board to kill the enemy general.

—description from the publisher

Hazards Pack is an expansion for 9-Card Rally Run that adds more challenge.

To ramp up the difficulty, this pack introduces 2 hazards: Water and Crests. These two hazards change the lane requirement to a specific value. Water hazard requires the lowest dice value of that lane while the Crest requires the highest value of the lane.

Road cards from this expansion have Caution on their card back.

Quick Rule:
Water: Use the lowest dice value of the lane
Crest: Use the highest dice value of the lane
Swap out 1-7 regular road cards with hazard road cards. Card backs must match.

—description from the designer

Food Truck Rally is a card drafting, economy game. Pick a spot to park your truck, combine resources to complete customer orders, and travel around in your truck as you gather a crowd of loyal customers.

Watch out though, your competitors may try to crash your truck, pay off an inspector or steal resources from you. Keep up on your insurance to protect your truck or take a chance without insurance in hopes to save money as you race to the top for the title of TOP FOOD TRUCK.

boardgame: The INWO Book: Rules, Variants, and more
description:
A complete guide to Illuminati: New World Order (INWO).

Included are:

     The World Domination Handbook V1.1, the complete updated rulebook.
     Every Card Described: All 412 cards from the Limited Edition with the complete text and in full color.
     Illuminated Strategy: Deck-building hints, tips, and twists for every Illuminati group.
     Variant and Optional Rules: New ways to take over the world including the "One With Everything" rules for playing with one big set of cards and the notorious Cheating Rules.
     Designer Schemes
     Stupid INWO tricks and more.


152 pages

You are a dwarf and you are digging a hole, not just any hole, you are digging a mine. Along the way you will find treasure, hopefully enough to make you rich; but no promises. Pay attention to where treasure is as you dig deeper and deeper into the mine. Everyone can be a miner in Card Caverns!

Card Caverns is a solitaire roll and write game, that uses cards to help you build a map, and also shows you what you are allowed to draw on that map. Over the coarse of 9 rounds, you will be carving out a mine, using a dry erase marker. Collect gemstones, and items along the way that will lead you to being the best miner out there.

Once you have played the classic version, be sure to try out Challenge Mode. This will give you set objectives to try as you play the game.

Card Caverns was originally designed for the 15th Roll and Write Game Design Contest. The designer (Wayne Koenig) wanted to create a game that was played mostly in hand, which is why all the "rock formations," also show you what you must draw on the mine. We hope that you will print the game out and enjoy it as much as we have enjoyed designing it.

—description from the publisher

Camino a Xibalba ("Path to Xibalba") is an abstract strategy game for two players, where in asymmetric chess-like movements you must carry a ball through passes between your pieces to reach the hoop, the player who gets 3 out of 5 points will win the game.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Hard to Get: Essen Spiel 2023 Promo Dilemma Cards
description: Three additional board game themed dilemma cards.
family: Mechanism: Give a Clue / Get a Clue

description:
A standard research mission goes awry when your crew crash lands on an unknown planet, launching you into a desperate hunt for resources to repair your ship and return home. However, someone amongst your crew may have a different agenda—even you. Are you eager to find a way home or to plant the seeds of a new society on this planet? Will your choices be for personal gain or the good of humankind? With an entire planet to explore and only so much time before your Rover's engine becomes overloaded, you must do whatever it takes to go big and go home… wherever "home" may be.

Go Big And Go Home is a tile-placing and push-your-luck game for 2-5 players divided into two teams. Each team will use the same Rover to explore the map and transport resources to their respective Team Objectives. Players will draw cards from the Rover Deck to find Solar Energy Cards that allow them to perform actions such as Explore (place new Hexes on the map) or Move the Rover among the Roads, but be careful if you reach 3 Failures of the same type, you are out of the Round.

Each team has a Team Objective: Blues want to transport Resources to the 2 Blue Team Objective Hexes to repair the ship, while Reds want to transport them to the Red Team Objective Hex to Build a Settlement. Rush through your Team Objective, or Upgrade the Rover as a crew to try to explore more and earn more Victory Points by completing Personal Objectives or your Origin Quest; but be careful, volcanoes may erupt as a response to overexploiting this planet and destroy everything you built together.

The game ends at the end of the Round in which either team completes their Team Objective, and the Player with the greatest VPs wins the game.

—description from the designer

Welcome to Sophistry, a strategic 1v1 card game of philosophical debate set in the ancient Agora. Play as a famous philosopher, synergize your Arguments, and make Objections to your opponent’s Arguments. In Sophistry, Subject Icons on the Discussion cards set the winning condition at the start of the game. Collect the required Subject Icons on your table before your opponent to win... but watch the Popularity track, an alternative winning condition.

Spend Focus to play new Argument cards from the Common Knowledge Pool. Choose Arguments that match the current Discussion’s Subjects to score points, but be careful, some of them will cost you Popularity! Place hidden Objection tokens on top of the cards in the pool to guess your opponent’s next Argument. If your opponent takes an Argument with your Objection token, flip it, and if they fall onto your trap, play powerful Objection cards, adding any Subject of your choice to your table. Change Subjects, disrupt the table, and manipulate the winning conditions. Stay Focused! Argue, object, and adapt!

Everything is valid, in the game of Sophistry

—description from the designer

Super Flipop is a dexterity and precision game suitable for the whole family. Similar to shuffleboard but with a twist. Shoot your wooden pucks from an elastic band to get to the number markers on the other side of the game board, by rebounding against the rails. Your opponent must do the same while trying to displace your pieces. The game ends when a team reaches 1000 points.

With this starting landscape and its 15 tile holders you can store your tiles from the Carcassonne mini expansions in an orderly manner. You can use the 15 tile holders individually as dividers and remove each tile holder with its tiles individually.

The 3D starting landscape replaces the starting tile or the starting tableau.

—description from the publisher

Place the landscape on the edge of the Table. So you don‘t place tiles behind the starting landscape during the game and everyone can see all the land tiles without restrictions.
Place the empty box with the open side down on the table.
Now you see a small gap on each of the long sides of the box. Put the castle panorama into one of these gaps. Make sure that the panorama is between two layers of paper and that the illustration is facing the bottom of the box.

—user summary

boardgame: Terraforming Mars: Directed Heat Usage Promo Card
description:
Autumn Season Promo 2023 of FryxGames, which you can buy on its own, or get for free if you order something at their webstore during Autumn 2023.

It is a blue project card with a cost of 1 M€.

Effect: Pay 3 heat to either gain 4 M€ or 2 plants.

Promo card X48.

Take on the challenge of solving six completely new puzzles in this free mini-version of OTLO Stones. Punch out the tiles and cards, then set a timer to determine which of your friends is the ultimate brainiac. It's not as straightforward as it appears; the tiles feature holes and are arranged in two layers.

The game can be played on its own or by using the better tiles from OTLO Stones.

TONKS is a fast and fun game of miniature mechanised mayhem, where each player controls an individual tank battling it out in a free for all. The game features fun upgrades and lots of opportunities for customisations, kit-bashing and wild colour schemes. For very brave hobbyists, there is the ‘TONKS OF LEGACY’ campaign, where tank commanders can risk it all seeking fortune and glory.

Masala Lab has not 1, not 2, but 3 games inside, all of them based on delicious Indian food! Inspired by the best-selling book Masala Lab by Krish Ashok, you will prep and serve various Indian dishes while trying to rack up the highest score.

Game 1 - Tiffin, is a set-collection game, where you have to collect food "Items" to make your "Prep", in a way such that the Items match the recipe of the Prep. An interesting card-overlap mechanism means you need to collect the Items in a particular sequence shown on the recipe. The game ends when the one player collects 7 Prep cards, but the winner is the one who scored the highest! Watch out for penalties for incomplete Prep. A game lasts 20-30 minutes.

Game 2 - Sadya (pronounced sadh-ya, meaning "feast" in Malayalam), kicks things up a notch, and you're competing to serve the best meal at the feast, on banana leaves, as is traditional in some parts of India. The rules build upon Game 1, and add a few more rules and mechanisms to it, namely engine-building and worker-placement. You have access to a "Pantry" in this game, which you access with your workers. Once you've made your "Prep" with "Items", just like in Game 1, you can use your Prep and your Pantry to "cook and serve" Dishes on to your leaf. Making "Prep" builds up your engine and you can re-use this Prep for several dishes. Prep and Dishes, both score you points. The first player to reach 40 on the score-track triggers the end-game. Resolve any penalties for players, and the highest scorer will win! A game lasts 45-60 minutes.

Game 3 - Dessert, is played with the reverse side of the playing board, and the "Dish" cards held upside down! This is a pattern-building game, that will evoke a slight tinge of nostalgia for fans of Tetris. In this game, you're in charge of serving a bunch of fussy guests, each of whom places an order for 4 dishes. But they will only accept your order and give you points, if you place their orders in any of the 5 Tetris shapes! This is a 2 or a 4 player game, that takes about 30 minutes.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Kreel Manor: The Path of Song
description: This expansion for Kreel Manor: The Dungeon Crawl Card Game adds a new hero to play as (and allows up to 4 players, 5 with The Path of the Dead) and includes over 60 cards.

boardgame: "Give Fyre": Skirmishing and Battle in the Renaissance Period
description: Miniatures wargame rules for Renaissance skirmishes.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
The gods have descend to the Mortal Realm, choosing their candidate, to represent them as their Avatar in the great "Pantheon War".

Play the ability of the respective gods in the form of cards, strategically plan your cards around to defend or create chaos to other Avatars within the battlefield. Destroy all other Avatars and allow your god to be the Grand Diety.

Pantheon War: Olympus is a fast-paced free-for-all battle card game designed for 2-4 players, where you play as the Avatar of your chosen gods. During your turn, you may play up to 2 cards against other players to destroy them , or play it onto yourself. When another Avatar attacks you, you may play Reaction cards against them.

The game ends when all players except you are eliminated. So come join the chaos and let your god to be the Grand Deity.

Conspicuous Cultists is a Lovecraft inspired, pocket sized, solitaire game of resource management, cult building, and Eldritch secret seeking. 
You'll shuffle up a deck of randomly selected event cards that will be slightly different every time you play. Will you uncover dark secrets and hidden knowledge, or will your dark obsession get the best of you!

To set up a game of Conspicuous Cultists you'll make a semi-random deck of 14 cards from the 43 present in the game box. You'll pick a starting resource pool, and then you can begin. The resources are divided into 5 types: Relics, Money, Cultists, Persons of Interest, and Suspicion. You then will reveal cards from the top of the deck and pick one of the two to three options available on the card. This will cause you to do things like pay or gather resources, remove the card from your deck, add new cards to your deck, or roll a dice to see what might happen. 
Some of the cards will lead you through a chain of events (by adding new cards to your deck) that will ultimately allow you to unlock a dark secret, such as a pact with the Deep Ones.

When you get all the way through your deck, you'll shuffle it and keep going. This means that part of the challenge of the game is remembering which trials and obstacles are in your deck that you haven't come across yet, so you can try to save your resources for them. Or alternatively gambling that you'll be able to replenish your resources before you encounter those trials. You may remember the cards in your deck, but you'll never know what order they'll come up.

You'll also have to balance your resource income and expenditures. Too many resources and the gods may curse you for your gluttony. Too much suspicion and law enforcement may raid your cult. But too little of anything, and you won't be prepared for what comes next. As bad as a curse of greed or a law enforcement raid may be, the wrath that the Old Ones pour out upon those who approach their secrets unprepared can be far worse!

—description from the designer

Goal
Be the first to complete your 4x4 grid of cards with each row and column in increasing order. Duplicate numbers can be placed next to each other, Aces are low, and a card’s suit doesn’t matter.

Setup
Deal 4 cards to each player. Each player places their 4 cards along the diagonal of a 4x4 grid in any order.

How to Play
On your turn, either:

     Draw the top card of the deck, or
     Take a card from the discard (all previously discarded cards are available)


Then, with that card, you can either:

     Discard it, or
     Place it anywhere in your grid. If you place it on top of a previously placed card, discard the previous card.


If you place a card diagonally adjacent to another card of the same number, take another turn.

You can’t place a card that would break the increasing order of its column or row.

The first player to complete their grid wins!

—description from the designer

This bundle contains four different Hero Realms: Journeys expansion packs:
Conquest, Discovery, Hunters, and Travelers.
There are 48 cards in total.

In the "Letterbox" expansion, you will find 150 new Clue cards, 2 new characters, 1 secret role, a 4th-category token. The box also becomes part of the game – it is a true Letterbox, via which the players now send letters to the Ghost.

description:
Welcome to the City of Myrkesh. Five districts, once divided with different goals and agendas for the city, must now stand together against the threat of destruction. Each player starts with a deck of ten ally cards and, on their turn, will send out their people to move through the city, recruit new allies and attack the monster that’s wreaking havoc.

The inhabitants of the City, although besieged, are mighty and resilient. Align yourself with the people of the 5 districts:

In the Merchant’s Row, Coin does the talking. Utilize your ability to procure cards better than anyone else, rush cards to the top of your deck and even utilize other players to broaden your card selection. Who will you barter with?

Those of the Royal Courts value flexibility and resourcefulness, utilizing both Coin and Power, Raise your levies, gain noble prestige and protect yourself from disruption. How will you show your status?

Those of the Industrial Grounds have one true motivation: Progress. Having the ability to draw cards like no one else and gaining cards that support multiple options, you will be able to adapt to any given situation with innovation by your side.

Those of the Wilds see Power as strength, and those that are weak will not last. Weather the destruction and ignore anything that would halt your ferocity. Brush off your wounds and inspire others to join your pack!

Those of the Undercity benefit from being anywhere in the city they want, utilizing speedy movement and sneaky tactics. Slander your opponents, cause their allies to revolt against them and slow down their movement so you can secure victory.

Whoever you align with, the unforgiving city will welcome you. Will you seek to have the most allies? Take on the monster before your opponents can? Or will you use the resources you have to spread your influence over the city and the people within it?

In the City of Myrkesh, Prestige is yours for the taking!

—description from the designer

Semi-cooperative card games for 3 to 6 players with an average duration of 60 minutes, Beyond the Bottle propels you into an offbeat, chaotic adventure.

In this biblical universe, rescue the wandering souls who have lost their way through the tunnel, limbo, and purgatory by repelling the attacks of the monsters roaming these desolate lands.

Build the challenges together, face them as a team, and share the loot, but keep in mind that only one of you can have access to paradise!

Goal of the game

You're dead and it's bad luck! But what's worse is having to wait in line for 423,673,417,998 minutes before you can pass into the afterlife. So you make the decision to become an interim soul smuggler.

Your job:

roam the tunnel, limbo and purgatory to find wandering souls. Beware of the events and creatures of these doomed places.

Cooperate to avoid the pitfalls, but beware, only one of you will win the premium skip-the-line pass giving access to the afterlife.

For the others, it's back in the queue.

description:
You are a gladiator mouse competing with other mice (and a stalking cat) for cheese and glory.

Take turns to draw from a common pool of dice to move your mouse, or the cat, or to distribute cheese over the game arena.

Use your wit and cunning to be the first to collect 7 wedges of cheese and be crowned the winner!

boardgame: From the Earth to the Moon
description:
From the Earth to the Moon is a novel by Jules Verne published in 1865, in which some adventurers, after the end of the American Secession War, embark on the adventure of reaching the Moon. To do so, they build an immense cannon and a bullet that will be their means of transport.

This "flip & write" board game for 2 to 6 players represents the essence of the novel. We will have to collect and produce resources, build the cannon, the base on which it rests and the bullet in which the adventurers will travel. In addition, the mechanics of gambling and dueling, concepts that appear repeatedly in the novel, are represented.

—description from the publisher

Fast 8-turn game about Operation Market Garden 13-29Sep44, from south of Eindhoven to north of Arnhem. 12 Allied and 15 German pieces.
"Monty Fantasy" 50% possibility the German SS panzer divisions *won't* be there at game-start to confront the paradrops.
Basic 25%-50%-25% combat odds allocation/resolution system.
Weather 50% good after Turn 1.
Hidden city garrison units.
Print off onto A4/8.5x11 for the units and A3/11x17 for the map, for more comfortable gameplay.
As with my other postcard games, anyone is free to print off (unaltered) copies en masse in postcard A6/4x6 or A5/5x8 size and format, to mail/give to family and friends.
Anyone interested in commercially publishing/selling this for profit, I have publication rights - this is not public domain - so check with me about licensing.

—description from the designer

description:
Where has Jareth hidden Toby?

Granting Sarah’s hasty wish to be rid of her baby brother Toby, Jareth the Goblin King has hidden the infant away somewhere in a strange and elaborate maze! The journey to find him is rife with curious characters that can help, but Jareth has beguiled one of them to hinder Tony’s rescue.

Taking on the roles of Sarah, Hoggle, Sir Didymus, Ludo, The Wiseman, and Worm, players navigate different parts of the Labyrinth to solve WHO Jareth secretly has control over, WHAT cursed object is keeping them from saving Toby, and WHERE Jareth has hidden him!

—description from the publisher
family: Game: Cluedo / Clue (Parker Brothers, et al)

description:
A drawing party game for 3 to 6 players, themed around the ancient NAZCA Lines.

Players each draw a picture to match a theme card.
On their turn, players cover their drawings with six "cover tiles" and reveal one square per round.

Players score points for being the first to guess the correct answer while only seeing part of the picture, and even more points for guessing with fewer clues.

boardgame: Wardelve: Ichor of the Chitin Lords
description: Wardelve is a ready to play out of the box, the game takes only a few minutes to setup and you can start to play pre-laid out maps or a dungeon explore and crawl mode. Setup is easy and fast, typically in minutes as you build as you play. Play can range from minutes to hours depending upon choice of game mode, number of players, etc. You will recruit minions, creatures from a large quantity of low level dust devils to fewer and fewer upper level creatures. While defending your dungeon from the pesky dungeon raiders… humans.. elves… dwarves… halflings… stealing your treasures.

Up to 5 people play as detectives to gather the clues to a major theft of a precious treasure!

Each round starts by rolling the die to determine how many cards will be played. Players will play their cards facedown in front of them, simultaneously flipping them over in order. If two or more players play the same card they cancel each other out, however if you are unique in your choice, you receive the associated clue. The round then continues with the next card.

The game is over when someone collects all 5 clues and catches the thief!

—description from the publisher

The Lahnexpress is a dice game for 2 to 4 children from the age of 4 to 5. The game is played on a large wooden board with a central turntable, the railway. Each child gets 5 pieces of one color made of wood. 4 of these are set up on the base plate at your own station. The dice are rolled in turn. The player with the highest score may then turn the game disc so that the first wheel of the locomotive is at his station. Then the starting player rolls again. With the number of points now rolled, the game is played as follows:

     First, a token is placed on the rails in the direction of the arrow for the number rolled.
     Then the locomotive continues in the same direction for the same number of points.


If the wheels of the wagon of their own color are on the same track space as their token, the player with that token may board the wagon. This also applies equally to all other players. The child who was first to place four game pieces in the wagon of the same color wins.

—user summary

Record your own custom BOP IT moves, then keep up with the game to score as many points as you can! It's the Bop It game you know, with a twist. Included BOP IT moves mix with moves you create, for a unique BOP IT game challenge. Record your move and repeat it when it's called out. The game senses if you are doing it correctly!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Jim Henson's Fraggle Rock The Card Game
description:
Pit your skills against your friends and family in the Fraggle Rock Card Game. This exciting game features two games in one: “snap” and “Where’s that Fraggle?”

In “snap,” players must race to be the first to shout out “snap!” when they spot a match between two cards. It’s fast-paced, fun, and perfect for players of all ages.

In “Where’s that Fraggle?,” players must use their memory skills to match the location of a Fraggle with the corresponding card. It’s a great game for younger players, but challenging enough for adults to enjoy as well.

With colorful artwork and easy-to-learn rules, the Fraggle Rock card game is sure to bring back fond memories of the classic TV show. And with two games in one, it offers hours of fun for the whole family.
family: Series: Jim Henson's Board Game Collection (River Horse)

description:
Navigation Portal is a game that delves into the intricacies of mathematical prowess as players journey through a vast expanse of about 63 square parsecs in nearby space. Players will be challenged to tackle diverse navigational scenarios, utilizing the Navigation Portal to traverse the vastness of interstellar realms. With each play, players will hone their skills, mastering the art of space navigation.

The game features two modes: Educational and Racer.
In the Educational mode, kids explore trigonometry through gameplay. To grasp the basics of trigonometry, players will typically need a maximum of 10 hours to complete Level 1 and Level 2. Level 3 delves deeper, covering trigonometry as taught in secondary schools. This level can be mastered by players as young as 12.

The Racer mode is designed for experienced players, starting even from Level 1. Each game can accommodate 1-8 players and allows for a mix of Levels 1, 2, and 3. Players are finding their way home.

Quick Start notes
First moment...
When you kick off the game, start timing yourself using a watch with a seconds feature or your phone's stopwatch, and keep it running the entire game. During your turns, you can set your course based on the seconds passed. Remember, don't pause the timekeeping throughout your gameplay. Later, you can tap into this recorded time for game stats, like gauging the average time taken per move or figuring out the overall game duration.

...a moment later
When you begin the game, you'll venture out from the solar system, positioned at the heart of the game board. Your initial direction is set by timing seconds on a stopwatch. Take that count, multiply it by six, and then add the number you roll on the dice. This combination maps out your path and sets the stage for your in-game decisions and strategy.
    
Gather The keys
Throughout the game, your route is charted using the navigation portal keys, represented by cards you gather along the way. Crossing the polar grid on your journey from start to finish rewards you with a navigation key for every point where you intersect the grid.

Fuel
Throughout the game, your route is charted using the navigation portal keys, represented by cards you gather along the way. Crossing the polar grid on your journey from start to finish rewards you with a navigation key for every point where you intersect the grid.    Your game's dashboard starts with a fuel gauge of 21 units. Your journey's length hinges on this fuel reserve. Think of each fuel unit as the power to travel one light year. On your game board, this distance is shown as a single centimeter. So, when considering a scale where a centimeter equals a light year, a span of 9.3 light years in the game translates to 9.3 centimeters. In terms of fuel consumption, you'd spend 9.3 units for that journey.

Settlers
When the game begins, you're equipped with two settler modules, each loaded with 25 settler units. Should you journey to a star or star cluster, you get the chance to colonize adjacent sectors. Think of a sector as a 1/5 ly² square, where you can place one settler unit. Now, if you discover planets in these clusters, keep them in mind, as you can revisit them later to boost your settler count based on the planets present. For a quick illustration: if there are 3 settler units represented by 3 occupied sectors near a planet, you can top up 3 of those groups in your game dashboard's module. And if you come across a system with four planets, that's a potential of adding 12 settler groups back to your resources.

2 new scoring tiles:

1 Modernisme tile
1 Cerda tile

Essentially the same Uno but with several Pixar characters and a new rule: The Pixar Pop Quiz (applied to 4 wild cards). The person who plays this card chooses any other player and asks them to name a Pixar movie. The player holding the wild card must name as many characters from that movie as they can (up to five characters). The other player then must draw one card for every character named correctly.

Loopz M3 is the music, memory and motion game that can be played anywhere! This handheld unit captures all the fun of the original Loopz, but this time, it's totally portable. Players use four trigger buttons and motion sensors to play a variety of games where the winner is the one with the fastest reaction time and best memory for sequences. Includes 4 different games for up to 2 players.

Your toddler will build fine motor skills, color matching, direction following, turn taking, and decision making with Bit City Builders! Pick up materials from construction sites, roll the die and move your truck to the matching road, and deliver materials to the right site to collect stars.

Ravensburger preschool games grow with your child! Big City Builder includes multiple ways for your child to play on their own and for you to play together. Since 1883, Ravensburger has built a reputation across the globe as a trusted brand for parents and children. Ravensburger’s toys, games, and puzzles encourage discovery of what really matters through play.

Solo & Family Play!
Flexible Play for Growing Children – This game grows with your child! Explore multiple ways for your child to play on their own and for you to play together. Variant rules allow you to adapt the complexity of play.

—description from the publisher

Your toddler will build fine motor skills, color matching, shape recognition, direction following, and turn taking with Rainy Ranch! Roll the die to lead animals into the barn, watch out for raindrops, and get everyone safely inside before it rains.

Ravensburger preschool games grow with your child! Rainy Ranch includes multiple ways for your child to play on their own and for you to play together. Since 1883, Ravensburger has built a reputation across the globe as a trusted brand for parents and children. Ravensburger’s toys, games, and puzzles encourage discovery of what really matters through play.

Solo & Family Play!
Flexible Play for Growing Children – This game grows with your child! Explore multiple ways for your child to play on their own and for you to play together. Variant rules allow you to adapt the complexity of play.

—description from the publisher

Shoot your swarm of skillful robots straight through the gap in the solar system to complete their daring space mission! Battle your friends in this rapid-fire riot of hurtling cyborgs. Whose entire team can breach the gap first?

—description from the publisher

Two players sit on opposite sides of the board and take turns using the built-in elastics to shoot their counters through the slot in the center of the box to the other player's side. First player to get all six of their counters through is the winner.

—user summary

Everyone has their own opinion and Up For Debate is a game that allows you to prove your opponent is wrong in a series of wacky debates. Challenge their answer, give your opinion on why your answer is better, and let the other players decide who wins that round. Be the first to get rid of all your cards to win the game.

Up For Debate is a fun and fast-paced game for family and friends. It can also be used as an icebreaker at gatherings or as a way to get everyone talking at your next party. Suitable for players aged 8 years and over, you will need three or more players for gameplay.

—description from the publisher

A children's game where the players draw cards with either a color or a picture, then move their astronaut piece either forward or backward to the nearest matching square on the board. Some squares have instructions to allow players to take short cuts to move farther along the track, while others will send them backwards. First player to reach the finish star wins.

—description from the publisher

Sentient drones weave through the treeline. Cybernetically enhanced alien warriors comb the mountainside for fuel and shelter. And in the center of it all, proud native warriors prepare to protect the life force of the planet itself.

War is coming to the small green planet of MD-0715, and it’s time to pick a side.

Manage resources, upgrade technology, and bring your army to battle in this epic deck-building game for up to 4 players.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Magic: The Gathering – Wilds of Eldraine
description:
This booster expansion for Magic: The Gathering revisits the fairy-tale world of Eldraine (previously featured in Throne of Eldraine) in the aftermath of the New Phyrexian invasion, which devastated the five knightly courts and forced most of the human denizens of the plane to retreat to the wilds, where all manner of fairy-tale dangers await. To make matters worse, the Wicked Slumber spell that the defenders of Eldraine used to repel the Phyrexians didn't end when the invaders were routed, and it continues to spread. Meanwhile, Will and Rowan Kenrith, the heirs to the throne, are at odds with each other over how to repair the realm, and a young hero named Kellan is taking his first steps on an epic journey...

Wilds of Eldraine is sold in draft boosters, set boosters, collector boosters, and bundles. Two preconstructed commander decks, "Fae Dominion" and "Virtue and Valor", were also released alongside the main set. The set features the return of the popular Adventure mechanic from Throne of Eldraine, alongside the new mechanics of Role Tokens, Bargain, and Celebration.

—user summary
