description: This is a modern variation of checkers. A hexagonal board that will force the players to think, analyze, and play differently. In this game, draws are fewer, kings are dangerous, and no space on the board is off limits.

This is a modern variation of checkers from Africa. It is checkers with an exciting twist; a circular board that will force the players to play checkers as if for the first time. It is no longer the same game you know …

just like in traditional draughts, players capture opponents by jumping over them. the only difference is the board is not connected from both sides.

In the realm of Slime Saga, a lone adventurer, the elemental Slime, sets off on a exciting journey. Armed with courage and wit, they face formidable foes, challenging obstacles and explore peculiar biomes and levels.

To play this PnP board game, you will need two 6-sided dice, one pen, printed and cut-out markers, a printed level sheet and a printed character sheet with one slime selected from six different elements (earth, fire, water, wind, frost and electro).

The goal is simple: You must survive through each level and biome and go from the starting point on the map to the "next level" field to continue in the saga. At the start of every level, you add map hazards by rolling a die. 
You roll two dice to move around the map, overcome events and traps, collect items to aid you and fight in combat. Each chapter has their own unique events and setup.

Will you join the saga and become the ultimate Slime Adventurer? Prepare for unforgettable adventures!

boardgame: Das Sommercamp: Die Jagd nach der gestohlenen Fahne
description:
Time for summer camp

But the good mood around the campfire is gone. Someone stole the flag!
Head into the wilderness in this adventure game. Explore remote forests, moors and rocky landscapes. Combine different items you find on your journey. Meet different people. Some want your help, others stand in your way. Open secret envelopes and listen to exciting audio book chapters to bring back the stolen flag.

Whether it's a great family game night, a kid's birthday party, a kid's play date, or even for solo adventurers, this game delivers a unique adventure-packed experience for everyone!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Zeit fürs Sommercamp

Doch die gute Stimmung am Lagerfeuer ist dahin. Jemand hat die Fahne gestohlen!
Macht euch in diesem Abenteuerspiel auf in die Wildnis. Erkundet abgelegene Wälder, Moore und Felslandschaften. Kombiniert verschiedene Gegenstände, die ihr auf eurer Reise findet. Trefft unterschiedliche Personen. Manche wollen eure Hilfe, andere stehen euch im Weg. Öffnet geheime Umschläge und lauscht spannenden Hörspielkapiteln, um die gestohlene Fahne zurückzubringen.

Ob bei einem tollen Spieleabend als Familie, einem Kindergeburtstag, einem Kinder-Spieltreffen oder auch für Solo-Abenteurer: Dieses Spiel liefert jedem ein einzigartiges Erlebnis voller Abenteuer!

—description from the publisher

Beastcaller is a mix of traditional Trading Card Game and a lot of Miniature Tabletop:
A 1v1 where two rivals bashing their heads in using summoned creatures. Additionally they have many spells traps and reaction cards at their disposal.

You take on the role of Beastcaller:
someone who has the ability to call Beasts - inspired by middle european folklore - from their Gromoire and send them into battle.

Before the game, each player picks one Beastcaller with his own unique gamechanging abilities and prepares a deck of at least 20 cards (spells, traps, reactions) and a Grimoire of exactly 10 cards (Beasts).

The game is divided into 2 phases that repeat themselves until one beastcaller faints.
In the first phase, the Beastcaller takes over - here the player prepares, calls Beasts onto the battlefield, cast spells, lay traps and more.
In the second phase the Beasts are activated turn after turn and move across the battlefield.

Powerful Spell cards can only be played if a Beastcaller has enough Beasts of a certain level on the battlefield.
Furthermore dissolved Beasts are not put in an discard pile - they linger on the Soulstack and can be brought back to your Grimoire. But be wary, the more Beasts are in your Soulstack the tinier the chance to get back what you need right now.

Beastcaller combines the strategy of tabletop war games and the exciting creation of your own card decks from a trading card game.

A hand management game, where players play with 1 of the four available civilizational decks in a turn based battlefield strategy game. Additionally, there is a Battlfield event deck which is utilised for common impacts on all players at the beginning of every turn.

The objective of the game is to defend your Commander with your Units on the battlefield, while trying to take out opponent's Line of Defense and take out opponent's Commander(s) to win the game.

Each deck has historically inspired units, resources, and special ability cards. Unit types are placed on the field in Line of Offense, and Line of Defense to attack the opponent and fend of opponent's attacks respectively.

In each turn, players draw cards to their hands, and perform upto 3 actions among placing a unit on the field, moving units between Line of Offense and Defense, using a Resource card or a Special Ability card, and launching direct attack on a Commander if the opponent has no Line of Defense.

The game winning condition is Last Commander standing and if decks run out before this happens, then the game is drawn.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
The ultimate card game for knowledge-hungry geeks and enthusiasts! Get ready to delve into the depths of niche interests and expand your geeky horizons in this unique twist on the classic Top Trumps game.

Just like its traditional counterpart, Topic Trumps follows a simple yet addictive gameplay formula. Each player receives a hand of topic-themed cards, with each card containing open-source researched stats.

The objective of the game is to outdo your opponents by comparing specific attributes or statistics of the topic cards. These attributes may vary depending on the theme, but they could include factors such as speed, weight, firepower or even something completely odd and obscure. Players take turns challenging their opponents by selecting a specific attribute from their card and pitting it against the corresponding attribute on their opponent's card. The player with the highest value for that attribute wins the round and collects the opponent's card.

However, what sets Topic Trumps apart from its traditional counterpart is its inherent educational value. All stats and values in the deck have been researched in open-source material - arming you for any pub-quiz or trivia night!

Players get to explore and learn about various geeky topics that they may not have encountered before. Whether it's discovering fascinating facts about rare chemicals, fantasy characters, curries of the world or taking a crash-course in wine or maybe unraveling the intricacies of micro-computers of the 1980s, Topic Trumps is an excellent way to immerse yourself in new, old and exciting subjects.

In addition to its quick gameplay and educational value, Topic Trumps takes learning to the next level with an innovative feature: each card is equipped with a QR code that links to a wealth of information about the specific topic it represents. By scanning the QR code using a smartphone or tablet, players can find out more about their favourite card.

—description from the designer

Compound 49 is a thrilling survival horror game where players navigate through chapters, taking on the roles of the Occupants as they delve into the secrets of their surroundings. Played in the dark, this game involves light and sound, creating an immersive experience of mystery, suspense, and desperation for 1-6 players.

—description from the designer

UMS: Modern Age is the first adaptation written using the “core” of UMS, a set of rules originally developed in Italian and then translated into English whose ambition is to cover the conflicts of the contemporary era (2015+) and other modern historical periods as well.

The scale is 28 or 20mm and the base unit is the Squad/Team or the single vehicle. The structures and equipment of the units mirror the ones from armies of NATO, Russian (former Warsaw Pact), Middle-East, Africa and irregular/insurgent ones. D12s are used to resolve firefights and all others checks such as moral and activation ones. The minimum table size is the standard 120x180Cm, but larger ones can be used too!

Clashes between superpowers armies, local conflicts between Warlords, symmetrical or asymmetrical operations, raids … all this can be played on using the guidelines offered by the rulebook.

UMS: Modern Age gives the players rules for managing a ground battle with vehicles, aircrafts, helicopters and artillery supports. Players will also find generic army lists with a point system and scenarios, or missions, to be used for a quick game among friends. This does not take away the possibility for players to being able to create custom scenarios inspired by historical events of the last decade.

—description from the designer

Use your wits, cunning and dexterity skills to escape a boiling pot of water before your "friends."

In Lobster Pot you are one of 4-6 lobsters trying to build dominance over the other lobsters and climb out of the boiling pot of water first to win the game.

You will use dice rolling to increase your dominance, or lower the dominance of others.  You will use those same rolls to climb up the walls of the pot and escape.

Be careful not to land on the same space as another lobster.  If you do you enter into a conflict and must complete a conflict against that lobster.  These conflicts test your dice rolling, drawing and dexterity skills.

The Computer Is Your Friend — this was the first thing you heard when you came out of the clone tank. It'll probably be the last thing you hear when you're lasered down like the Commie mutant traitor that you are.

Athenium is a head to head summoner battle game in line with most popular trading card games.  What sets Athenium apart is that it is permanently simplified to the most basic elements of gameplay.

Starting life points are determined by the number of players.  Each player involved adds 5 life points to the starting life point total of every player.  It's meant for 2 players, starting at ten, but it's still really fun with 4 players, starting at 20.

There are still resource cards, called Macguffins, that are used to motivate a character into the battle, but instead of 4-7 colors, there are 3, and they are based on genre.  The green cards are Fantasy characters, the blue cards are Science Fiction characters, and the red cards are Horror characters.

Each character card has one number, which represents attack, defense, and casting cost, which go up or down based on their type matchups.

They attack and block left to right, just like reading a book.  When the defending characters run out, the rest is damage dealt to the defending player.  When the defending player runs out of life points, they lose.  Last librarian standing wins.

Everyone plays from one deck which may be supplemented with special cards, or later expansion decks.  All of the art, graphic design, writing, gameplay architecture and web design were done by Ben Butcher, though the expansions have other illustrators on payroll in the near future.

—description from the publisher

description:
Adult fans of UNO card games will love this daring take on the classic game. It plays like regular UNO matching color and number, but if a Dare Card is played, the player must decide if they are willing to risk doing the dare versus the penalty of drawing cards. There are four levels of dares: mild, medium, spicy, and fuego!  A chance roll of the die determines which dare players must perform. A card with a “reverse” icon on it forces the player who played the Dare Card to perform the dare! But the party doesn't end there, these cards are durable, waterproof plastic cards to make them completely spill-proof and comes with a handy clip to keep them together! Makes a great gift for fans and game lovers ages 18 years old and up.

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Adult Versions of Family-Friendly Games

description:
NICE TRY: Team Edition is a competitive party game in which teams of at least 2 people face fast, crazy and funny challenges in the areas of cleverness, skill and physical awareness.

The special thing about it is that at least 2 people per team compete together in each challenge. With simple household objects such as spoons, coins or toilet paper, everyone can show what he or she is capable of.

In the bidding phase before the game, each team bets on how well they will do. The players have to assess their own skills as well as those of their teammates in order to earn points. 
Each team that succeeds in its bid earns points. Those who overestimate themselves earn minus points.
Special games and jokers bring additional elements into the game.

The team that has earned the most points after 8 rounds wins. 
Do you earn the respect of your fellow players, 
or do you just get a gleeful "NICE TRY!"?

—description from the designer

NICE TRY: Team Edition ist ein kompetitives Partyspiel, in dem sich Teams aus mindestens 2 Personen schnellen, verrückten und witzigen Herausforderungen aus den Bereichen Cleverness, Geschick und Körpergefühl stellen.

Das Besondere dabei: in jeder Challenge treten mind. 2 Personen pro Team gemeinsam an. Mit einfachen Haushaltsgegenständen wie Löffeln, Münzen oder Klopapier kann jeder zeigen, was sie oder er drauf hat.

In der Gebotsphase vor dem Spiel wettet jedes Team darauf, wie gut es abschneiden wird. Die Spieler müssen dabei ihre eigenen aber auch die Fähigkeiten der Mitspieler gut einschätzen, um Punkte zu verdienen. 
Jedes Team, das sein Gebot schafft, bekommt Punkte. Wer sich überschätzt, sammelt Minuspunkte.
Sonderspiele und Joker bringen zusätzliche Elemente ins Spiel.
Das Team, das nach 8 Runden die meisten Punkte erspielt hat gewinnt.

Verdient ihr euch den Respekt der Mitspieler, 
oder kassiert ihr nur ein schadenfrohes „NICE TRY!“?

—description from the designer (German)

In Formworm, each player tries to fold and adjust their 8-piece cardboard worm into the shape shown on the formcard. The fastest one to complete the shape scores a point!

The game includes 50 two-sided formcards, with an easier and harder sides and four different colored foldable worms.

Formula 1991 is a simple Formula 1 racing cardgame/boardgame paying homage to the epic 1991 season, all housed in a deck of 55 cards. The cards in the deck represent parts of the track that you will ride, drivers and strategies that will be applied. A pocket strategy and competition game that provides a lot of fun, and can be even more exciting with the addition of a second deck, allowing more players to participate and the track becomes even bigger.

—description from the designer

Formula 1991 é um cardgame/boardgame simples de corrida de Fórmula 1 em homenagem à épica temporada de 1991, tudo dentro de um baralho com 55 cartas. As cartas do baralho representam partes da pista que você vai montar, pilotos e estratégias que vão ser aplicadas. Um jogo de estratégia e competição de bolso que proporciona muita diversão, e pode ser ainda mais emocionante com a a dição de um segundo baralho, permitindo que mais jogadores participem e a pista se torne ainda maior.

—description from the designer (Portuguese)

Create your own crossword using words from any paperback book.
A solo dry erase game on a single card.
Also available as in print and play format.

GOAL: Use all 26 (A-Z) letters from the alphabet to create and solve your own crossword puzzle using any book of your choice.

SETUP: Open any (paperback) book to a random page. Select eight random 3- to 8-letter words and write them horizontally in the highlighted boxes next to each arrow.

GAME PLAY: Insert your own words with three or more letters, connecting them vertically to the eight horizontal words. Write each letter downwards within the squares and mark off the used letters in the pool at the top. Leave an empty column between your words.  Repeat until all 26 letters have been used. You may reuse the letters as needed to complete the challenge.

WINNING: 
Use all 26 letters to interconnect all the horizontal words with the vertical words. 
Leave no “orphans” (unconnected words).

—description from the designer

Our first post-WW2 historical simulation game in the "Fierce Fight! Army Group Manstein" series, set during the Korean War of 1950.
Can you win the largest ground battle since the end of the Second World War?

Published in Game Journal #88 with 激闘！ロンメル軍集団 (Fierce Fight! Army Group Rommel)

Wild Duo is a game collection that includes 5 games for 2 players. You can play these games with two players or in larger groups (4/6/8/10), in which case the games are rotated between the groups of two. There is definitely enough variety, as each of the games is a unique gaming experience.

The games are perfectly playable for both children and adult players, as the gaming experience depends entirely on your, respectively, your fellow player's skills. That's why some games can be decided after only 5 minutes, while others last 30 minutes.

Each of the games deals with a different animal species or a specific behavior of this animal species and thus takes the players on a journey into one of 5 worlds. Each of the five rule explanations therefore always includes an exciting, thematic text on the corresponding animal species.

—description from the publisher

"In a world where multiverse collide, players wage a war for their own universe. 
The stakes are the characters called "applicants" who are important to the universe... 
Players can call on characters from all the Multiverse to join their forces to form the strongest squad and win the war!"

Build War is a deck-building game with varying action phases and innovative mechanics for 1 to 4 players .
The four-player simultaneous deck-building phase proceeds at a very high speed and high tempo: 

     Each player receives 10 cards, assigning 5 cards to the initial deck and 5 cards to the initial hand.
     Build Draw System: drawing two cards at the same time allows for speedy deck construction.
     Cards can be acquired at no cost, so powerful cards can be played from the beginning. However, there is a space limitation and managing it is an important part of gameplay.
     Color and number coded cards, each with its special ability, can be combined in powerful combos. 
     All players play the war phase simultaneously.
     Revenge system: if you lose, you can take over 5 cards from the deck.
     The war lasts four rounds, or until someone loses all their lives, and the player with the highest life at the end wins!

A blitz-krieg strategy game for two players. The players move elementals of earth, wind, fire and water in turnorder on the board. They may also use their tower to block the angry elementals.

Each player have the same kinds of game pieces, which have individual movement patterns. The mountain runs straight on, the river can run dioganally, but only forwards, the fire moves dioganally both forwards and backwards, and the wind may move in any direction.

To win you need to enter the opponent's camp with two of your elementals.

—description from the designer
18xx set in Arkansas from Reconstruction to the Great Depression. Combines elements of 1817, 1841, and 18Ireland.

boardgame: Fly Fight Win: MiG Alley – Fighter Combat in the Korean War
description:
Fly Fight Win: MiG Alley is a hex-based, miniatures rules set for aerial combat. It emphasizes the importance of speed, altitude, and pilot skill, and minimizes much of the tedium typically associated with air-to-air combat games. It includes statistics for many frontline fighters that saw action during the Korean War and a pilot advancement system for campaign games.

MiG Alley is a standalone rules set. The unique period of early jet fighters and reliance on guns over missiles sets this title apart from other games in the Fly Fight Win series. This series is designed for playability but retains sufficient detail to recreate historical outcomes (with a nod towards the physics of flight). Most scenarios can be played in under 60 minutes.

—description from the designer

description:
The Magic: The Gathering Starter Kit 2023 is ideal for any pair of new Magic players that walk through your doors, as they give newcomers the opportunity to dive into their first game in-person (and continue the fun online with redeemable Magic: The Gathering Arena code cards in select regions).

Learn the essentials or teach friends and family with the included Play Guide booklet, then enter the fray with two pre-built 60-card decks—Day of the Dragon (a Blue-Red deck) and Backup Beatdown (a Green-White deck).

—description from the publisher

Introduction:Anda (from Proto-Germanic: anþą, which means "breath") is a drawless annihilation game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the hexes (cells) of an initially empty hexagonal board. The recommended size is 7 cells per side, but boards of 5 or 9 are also valid. There is also a place outside the board called the prison. Each player has access to a sufficient supply of stones of their own color.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A group is a maximal set of connected stones of the same color. A single stone is also a group.
     A breath of a group is an empty cell adjacent to the group, along with all other empty cells connected to it by a free path to that group that may include zero or more empty cells.
     A group is smothered if each of its breaths is adjacent to exactly one enemy group.


				
				
					Turns:Black plays first, then turns alternate. On your turn, remove an enemy stone from the prison or perform the following actions in order:


     Place a stone of your color on an empty cell so that it is not the only one of its color smothered at the end of your turn. If it is your first turn, simultaneously place two stones of your color on empty cells separated by at least two intermediate cells.



     Remove from the board all smothered enemy groups, then remove all smothered friendly groups.


If the prison contains stones of both colors, remove pairs of opposing-color stones until only stones of one color or none remain.

				
				
					End of the game:You win if the last enemy group is eliminated from the board.

To balance the game, before starting, the first player places several black stones in the prison, and then the second player chooses a side. In handicap games, the weaker player chooses Black and begins by placing a number of stones in the prison proportional to the difference in skill between the players. This balancing method is called the komi pie rule.

				
				
					Notes:Special thanks to Luis Bolaños Mures for a material contribution to the game's rules. Originally, a group was considered smothered if all its breaths were adjacent to the same enemy group. In the current version, a group is smothered if each of its breaths is adjacent to exactly one enemy group, without requiring all of them to be the same.

—description from the designer

A new Camper joins the fight for survival in this 54-card Expansion for the Psychopath Killer Card Game!

The 5th Camper Expansion adds new cards to both the Camper deck and the Psychopath deck, introduces new Situation cards that can be used to modify each game, adds a new Location (the Graveyard!), and enables play for up to 6 players!

Every copy of the 5th Camper Expansion comes with the 2nd Promo Card: the 'Skewer' card featuring alternate photography from that in the expansion. Just add it into the Camper deck!

—description from the publisher

Plank is a 2-5 person competitive treasure-hunting game where players must battle for control of supplies while they attempt to assemble a crew and claim a treasure for the win. Use your crewmember's unique abilities to roam across vast seas and plunder your opponents as you collect resources. Don't like your current crew? Make them walk the plank and pick up new ones from sand bars.

As you visit islands, build your crew, and plunder other players, you will begin to collect resources that will help you form your strategy and win the game. Your ultimate goal is to collect two identical themed keys and discover which island that key's treasure is buried on to claim that treasure. Other players will almost certainly be going after the same treasure as you, so you must always be on guard because they can plunder you and steal your keys for themselves. Each crew member's unique abilities and traits will allow you to move, attack, or go fishing. A players ship is fully crewed when they reach four crewmembers. If they want to pick up another crew member, they must first make someone walk the plank! The more crew you have, the greater your options each turn.

Once you have two identical keys and know what island your treasure is buried on, it becomes a race for time as you attempt to sail to the island and claim it before anyone plunders you! A player with at least one treasure may return to Skull Island and dock their ship to celebrate their successful treasure hunting and end the game. Once a player with a treasure has docked at Skull Island, all remaining players get one final turn, and then all players count their points to determine who wins!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Star Wars: Legion – Ewok Warriors Unit Expansion
description:
An iconic race joins the Rebel cause in this new pack for Star Wars: Legion! Natives of the forest moon of Endor, Ewoks may be short of stature and limited to primitive technology, but they are quick learners who cannot be underestimated. This pack adds a unit of six Ewoks and two unit cards that let players decide if they're armed with slings or spears to players' Rebel armies or the new Bright Tree Village Battle Force. Joining them are an Axe Ewok miniature that can fill the unit's heavy weapon slot and an Ewok Trapper miniature that can fill its personnel slot. Rounding out this pack are five upgrade cards that give players options for customizing their Ewoks to fit their own play style.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Star Wars: Legion – Logray and Wicket Commander Expansion
description:
Two heroic Ewoks come to Star Wars: Legion to help bring down the Empire in this pack! The brave and resourceful Wicket W. Warrick and Logray proved instrumental in helping the Rebels bring down the Imperial shield generator during the battle of Endor and this pack adds these iconic Ewoks as new Commander choices for Rebel armies and the new Bright Tree Village Battle Force. In addition to miniatures of these characters and their unit cards, this pack also provides players with new unit cards for C-3PO and Chewbacca that reflect their time on Endor during Return of the Jedi, giving players even more ways to use them in battle! Finally, five upgrade cards round out this pack, inviting players to further customize their Ewok forces.

—description from the publisher

While exploring an old abandoned house, you have stumbled upon a book and some odd looking dice. Suddenly, a devil appears offering you and your friends a wager. Do you accept?

Devil's Dice is a lightweight dice rolling game for 2-6 player's. A twisted cross between Coup, and Liar's dice. Lie, steal, and chance your way to victory by being the first to summon the devil!

Each player starts the game with one skull token and two dice. The dice are hidden behind a cover screen. Each die has 6 unique symbols.
Satan's pool is the board in the center which dice will be added to throughout the game. 
When you've assembled all 6 symbols between your hand and the public die in Satan's pool, you declare Satan's set and win!

On your turn, you can take any of the actions listed below, regardless of whether you have the required symbols:

Imp's set: If your all your dice match, with Imp's counting as wilds, you may gain a die from the common pool.
Skull: If you have a Skull, take two skull tokens from the common pool. 
Scythe: If you have a Scythe, pay two skull tokens to try and steal a die. 
Pentagram: If you have a Pentagram, re-roll the dice in Satan's pool, and if you roll any pentagram you gain up to one. Also block Scythe's steal. 
Trident: If you have a Trident, Take up to three skull tokens from another player. Also block Trident's steal.
Flames: If you have a Flame, Gain 1 skull token, then re-roll your current hand.

Any player may challenge your action or block(claiming you are lying about having the requisite symbols). When challenged you must then reveal your dice hand. If you are caught lying, the action or block is cancelled, and the challenger steals a die from you. If you were telling the truth, instead the challenger loses a die and rolls it into Satan's Pool, and play continues after.

—description from the publisher

Pishtaco is a social deduction game where you will test your ability to read other players. Whether you're a fat-stealing Pischtaco, or the community trying to defend themselves against their attacks. You will have to win everyone's trust and discover who is lying to make your team win.

This is an adaptation of the traditional Russian game Mafia, later popularized as Werewolf. This edition of Ponte Mosca includes a Peruvian Dog as an additional character.

The "Pishtaco" is a character from the Andes who disappears people to steal their body fat. This character has many versions depending on the region and time in which his stories are collected. He has been a landowner, "misti", african-peruvian, "gringo", US government worker. The fat that he collects is used to be sold in Lima, greasing machinery, oil for cars, beauty products. He is a character that has been studied by José María Arguedas and who does not stop reappearing in modern tales, a character that is used so that children do not play outside the house until late, and today we will use as adults to continue playing.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Unfair: CHKO Kickstarter Sneak Peek Cards
description:
This pack of 8 playable sneak peek cards from future expansions for Unfair, was available to backers of the CHKO expansion. These are Kickstarter temporary exclusives marked with an asterisk symbol in the bottom right hand corner. The included thank you card had instructions for use:

Cards with an asterisk symbol for their theme pack are promotional cards. You can add up to 1 copy of any promo cards you wish to use in your game, unless that card is already in the chosen packs.

A Caccia di Conchiglie is a game based on the mechanics of remembering and matching the same shapes and colours: the shells are defined by a unique double code of shape and colour to facilitate their recognition.
Throw the shells on the beach, memorise them and then hide them. And now roll the die: do you remember where the corresponding shell is? Whoever collects the most wins!
The frustration of failing to match images, a typical problem in classic memory games, is replaced here with the amusing uncertainty of what the consequence of the error will be, while still rewarding the right behaviour.

Test your language and logic skills: have fun while developing attention and concentration.
Ruba Parola is the game of words connected in a logical sequence. Each player has three minutes to add a new word to the sequence of words on the board that is logically connected to the word at the top or the word at the bottom, and then to steal one that is in the middle, replacing it with another. For each word placed, the player places one of his own loot tiles next to it. The winner is whoever manages to place all 5 of their loot tiles first!

The Rivalry expansion includes:

2 new playable God + Board & Miniature (Hera, Dionysus)
18 Conflict cards
12 Enhancement tiles
15 Standees
20 Spark cubes
42 tokens (Location, God, Marker)
2 Aid card
6 Temples

Content for Cataclysm expansion

And more!

—description from the publisher

100 years after the events of Hybris, Disordered Cosmos, Ancient Greece is once again in turmoil: no Aegis remains and the Achaeans, led by Zeus, attack the last stronghold of the Eye of Nyx artifact: Troy.

But this fortress is protected by other Olympians who wish to eradicate Zeus' madness - the city of Troy must not fall! But that's without counting on the observation of this legendary war by the Mystics, who choose no side and simply watch, impassive.

Hybris, Twilight in Olympus is an epic game of area control for 2 to 6 players, scheduled for crowdfunding in 2024.

The game is played in 10 rounds, corresponding to the 10-year siege of Troy. During a turn, each player secretly programs his actions through his own game system:


     The Achaeans program their actions using a card system
     The Trojans program their actions using a worker placement system.
     The Mystics program the potential actions of the other two players!


The action phase then starts, with the Achaean player revealing his first card, then spending a time value to carry out his action. The other players will use the Achaean's time value to carry out their actions.

The Achaeans win if Troy falls, either by breaking the wall, stealing the nyx eye, or if Troy falls economically before the 10 turns.
Trojans win if they survive 10 turns.
Mystics win if they achieve their secret objectives before either of the other two players win.

—description from the designer

The 54 card and 12 token minimalistic game which simulates conflict. The game is build around of the idea that the board can also change. The players have to balance heir overall strategy with enough flexibility for tactical decisions in order to secure the win.

Players move their own tokens and push neutral and opponents tokens on the field cards to secure a good position. They can change the scoring cards and field cards to impact overall situation on the board.

You don't like where your opponent stands? Push him!
You don't like the way points are scored? Change it!
You don't like the ground you fight on? Convert it!

The tuggle is always, the tuggle is everywhere.

—description from the designer

the game comes with the full 32 pieces minus 1 pawn for each team. the goal is still the same; checkmate the opponent king to win the game.

Rumors are abound in Noctenburg: the townsfolk whisper of sights of strangely humanlike animals in their homes, the hospital and even the court. Ominous omens indeed...

description:
This L-chess board is a modern variation of chess that let’s you start the game beside your opponent instead of facing him. With a unique board, players will think, analyze, and play differently forcing them to forego any old openings or strategies and discover new ways to play.

Chess 90Degrees is faster and easier compared to standard Chess, but full of unexpected moves. However, pawns in this game hold a lot more power since the road to promotion is easier.

The game comes with the full 32 pieces. The goal is still the same; checkmate the opponent king to win the game.

—description from the publisher

This is a promotional item and it requires the base game of Nucleum to play.

Components:

     4 Acrylic Nucleum markers
     4 Nucleum Bonus tiles


Changes in setup:
Exchange wooden Nucleum markers with acrylic ones.
Randomly assign 4 Nucleum Bonus tiles to cities with Nucleum power plants (covering the bonuses printed on the map).
During setup when you assign one of the Nucleums, flip the corresponding Nucleum Bonus tile.

Changes in game play:
After you place a new Nucleum marker in one of the power plants– immediately gain the bonus and flip the corresponding Nucleum Bonus tile.

This is a promotional item and it requires the base game of Nucleum to play.

Components:

     4 Contract Space tiles
     5 Action Fragment tiles
     1 Offer tile


Changes in setup:
Place the Offer tile above the board. Place 5 Action Fragment tiles on the Offer tile. Deal one Contract Space tile for each player.

Changes in game play:
•Gaining Action Fragment tiles:
During the game while performing the Contract action you can place the newly gained Contract on the Contract Space tile. This will immediately grant you one of the Action Fragment tiles from the offer. You can have only 1 Action Fragment tile at a time. If you already have one and want to gain another, you can exchange the tiles – taking the new one and returning the old one to the offer (this also applies to “take a contract with double reward” action).

•Using Action Fragment tiles:
When placing Action tiles above your player board or on the board, before you perform any of the actions you can decide to cover one of the actions with the Action Fragment tile. Then perform all visible actions and discard the Action Fragment tile that you used back to the offer. If you placed an Action tile on the board and covered an action that was not adjacent to another tile or a city, leave that Action Fragment tile on the map (it is still considered yours and counts to the 1 Action Fragment tile limit) until another tile is placed adjacent to it, then discard it (after use if triggered).

Here are eleven swashbuckling scenarios to play with the All For One set of rules for Three Musketeers games.


     In the courtyard of the Carmelite Conrcnt
     The fight in the laundry and dyeworks
     Milady and D'Artagnan 
     The ambush at the river
     Duel in the dark 
     Returning the jewels to the Queen 
     Milady and Constance 
     Breakfast in the ruins
     The kitchen 
     No such thing as a free lunch 
     The inn yard


The sharp eyed and even the not so sharp eyed may spot adaptations of events not only from the books but also from Richard Lester's films of the three musketeers. However because these are games and the baddies have to have some chance of winning, the outcome may not fit with the expectations set up by the films. Some games are limited in terms of time thus allowing several games to be played in the course of an evening so that several participants can have a chance to swap characters or try different strategies with the same game without becoming bored.

The first 7 of these scenarios can be played as a mini-campaign. If you so choose to do this then it should be considered that light wounds take a week to heal and serious wounds will become light wounds in the same time. The time between the first and second scenario can be considered as longer than a week. Scenarios 2, 3, 4 and 5 will be fought within a few days of each other. There will be at least a week between scenarios 5 and 6.

—description from the designer

Slice, Dice and Deliver is an I Split, You Choose, Roll, Write and Deliver game where players compete on a shared map of New York to draw and move along routes that connect to deliveries for pizzas that collect via splitting a dice pool.

Each round the start player rolls the dice pool, splitting the dice into sets based on the number of players. Each player chooses a set of dice with the start player choosing last. Play then proceeds from the start player, drawing roads, moving your vehicle, delivering pizzas and collecting tips. The player who delivers the most pizzas and collects the most tips is the winner!

Slice, Dice & Deliver was included as a Print and Play reward for the Tantrum House Season 10 Kickstarter.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Galactic Era: 2-Player Variant with Passive Automa
description:
This is an improved variant for 2 players will full graphics to match the rulebook.

It uses a passive automa instead of the puppet from the rulebook for the third faction. After setup, it is a lot quicker to play with almost no effort for it because it is not active. This variant is also suitable for beginners to the game.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Old School Tactical: For The Empire
description:
For the Empire is an expansion for Old School Tactical Vol IV The Italian Theater 1943-45. You must own Old School Tactical Vol IV to play For the Empire.

When Great Britain once again found herself in war, she sent out a call to her Commonwealth allies.  They stepped forward and answered the call, as they always had. New Zealand was in the thick of the action from the beginning.  The Kiwis fought bravely in the Balkans and Greece, the battle for Crete and were a key force in the North African campaign.

The New Zealanders came to Italy with the same amount of fight.  Among the many battles they faced, they were tasked with a difficult struggle to take the fortress at Monte Cassino.

The Canadian Navy fought vigorously in the battle for the North Atlantic, but the army was static, stationed in the British Isles.  But they were itching to get into the scrap. They got what they wanted when their soldiers and tankers were added to Operation Husky, the invasion of Sicily.  They were bloodied and they learned.

They carried on to the Italian boot and fought their way North through difficult terrain against a determined enemy that always had the advantage,  Many battle honors were earned, one being the terrible Christmas in Ortona.
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players
Series: Old School Tactical (Flying Pig Games)

description:
A print and play game where you grab predefined letters to make words inside a 3x3 square grid.

Six three-letter words must be made.
Three words will be horizontal.
Three words will be vertical.
Share nine letters across six rows and columns.

SETUP:
Print the grid and letters on the included page.
Avoid viewing the solutions and hints pages.
TIP: Glue the grid and letters onto cardstock,
after printing but before cutting.
Cut out the grid and letters.

TO PLAY:
There are 63 challenges.
Collect the letters for a challenge.
Arrange the letters to form six words.
If stuck, view a middle letter or hint for a random word.

—description from the publisher

These rules are suitable for a wide range of actions, from small skirmishes to large battles. By adjusting the ratios you can utilise the figures you have available.

I have deliberately avoided laying down tedious minutae of law. Your interpretation of history is what makes for an enjoyable replication of events as you understand them. If you are lucky, you will find a like mind or at least a tolerant collaborator.

You will notice that the tables have been kept very basic; for example, superior armour. This way you or an umpire can decide by comparing the units engaged, without recourse to pluses or minuses for breastplate, helmet, tassetts etc. If in doubt ignore it.

The ratio of pikemen to musketeers always causes controversy. If you do not have any accurate idea for a particular battle, then it may be helpful to know, that it is generally accepted that Parliament as early as 1642 reached 2 musketeers to I pikeman. The Royalists probably attained this by late 1643. In Scotland the ratio of 3 musketeers to 2 pikemen appears to be more common.

Finally, nothing is written in stone here, but keep to the spirit of the period, avoid gamesmanship, be prepared to lose graciously or be generous in victory.

—description from the designer

Tantrum House inspired, fan-made player board for Roll To The Top: Journeys roll & write from Allplay. This print & play expansion was made available through the Tantrum House Season 10 crowdfunding campaign on Kickstarter.

Promo card for Flamecraft from Cardboard Alchemy. This new & super-awesome promo was made available through the Tantrum House Season 10 crowdfunding campaign on Kickstarter and as well as through various social media channels.

Full text on card:
End of Turn: Place 4 (coins) seven spaces ahead of you on (influence) track. Next turn, reach space to gain (coins), or remove them.

boardgame: Pike & Shotte: Epic Battles – Push of Pike
description:
The 17th century saw the recruitment of vast armies across Europe that clashed against each other in bloody wars that decided ultimate temporal and divine authority.

The Thirty Years’ War was one of the longest and most destructive wars in European history. Ostensibly about religion, the conflict ultimately shifted the balance of power between nations on the continent.

The English Civil Wars saw Royal authority violently challenged by Parliamentary imposition in a series of wars that rent Britain asunder. Nobles and lords flocked to one side or the other to fight neighbour and brother in bloody war.

Pike & Shotte Epic Battles: Push of Pike provides you with all the information you need to refight the wars that raged across Europe in the seventeenth century in an epic scale, with period specific additions that capture the tactics, conditions, and prominent personalities of both these cataclysmic conflicts.

—description from the publisher

The Hunt: Lupus is a social deduction party game, where two main factions try to prevail on each other by eliminating the other one, by using their powers or by votation, while other independent characters work in the shadows to attain their own purposes and reach victory.

The game is set in a dark horror atmosphere, where an unspeakable entity has doomed the inhabitants of a village, changing them and causing some of them to betray their kind.

Each player has a powerful role, unknown to other players, and must try to guess what other players' roles are by looking at their playstyle and seeing through their lies. Each role has a big impact on the game (no more villagers or dull roles), allowing for strategic decisions and granting always unpredictable and fun games.

The Hunt is on.

—description from the designer

Deluxe Hero Pack #2 is a set of Hero and Minion cards in alternative color palettes so you can pit them against themselves using two sets of hero cards.

Contains one alternate card for each of the following Heroes/Minions:

     Korvu
     Pavl
     Hype

The goal of the game is to survive by building walls, attack your enemies, and avoid the storm.
Health Points are the currency of the game, and it usually gets paid to/from the bank.
Players roll one action dice and one normal dice. Properties are collected as safe areas for yourself, and you only collect HP if you own a full set.
Passing go provides 2 HP and reveals a storm card that usually increases the damage dealt when landing on that space.
There are 10 metal figures of popular characters from the video game.

Tiger Banana Pancake is a crazy and chaotic fast-reaction card game for kids, families and parties.

Players race to do silly actions, but don't fall for the sneaky imposters!

—description from the publisher

Now preschoolers can get in on the mystery-solving fun!  This mystery board game for kids ages 4 and up includes a double-sided gameboard for 2 levels of play. On the Level 1 side, kids work together to solve a Playground Mix-up. One of the characters mistakenly put an item in their backpack. All players work as a team, exploring the playground by searching for clues in seek-and-find picture tiles. Together, they must figure out what item is missing and who took it to solve the mystery.

When junior detectives are ready for a bit more challenge, they can flip the board over to Level 2 and compete to solve the Science Lab Mystery. Who set off the volcano, what did they pour into it, and where should they have been?
family: Game: Cluedo Junior / Clue Junior (Parker Brothers, et al)

description:
Trading property has never been more fun in the giant version of this iconic Hasbro kids board game. This classic Monopoly board game made big is perfect for an outdoor game. Play indoors or outside with this board game for kids 8-12 (or anyone ages 6+). The rules to play this fun game are slightly different than the original game. Roll the Giant Die and move your Giant Token that number of spaces on the Giant Gameboard. If you land on an empty property space, you MUST buy it. Mark the space with the green side of your Property Card. When another player lands on the space, they pay you the amount shown on the gameboard using their Million Money Meter. The player with the most wealth at the end of the game wins! With these new speedy rules, you’ll get the classic Monopoly experience in less than 30 minutes!

—description from the publisher

Herbivores or carnivores, dinosaurs had to struggle to feed and reproduce! In this chain tile-taking game, you recreate the foods that existed 100 million years ago to give birth to lots of baby dinosaurs.

An intense and fast-paced game in which tactics and choices will lead you to victory!

n the middle of the table is the game board, this is divided into several zones which are positioned in front of the players. Dino tiles are spread out on the board. Simultaneously, players draw the tile of their choice from the area of ​​the board facing them and integrate it into one of their food chains in order to feed one of their dinosaurs.

As soon as a dinosaur has eaten, it lays an egg! Each player then takes the eggs that their dino has laid, corresponding to their points at the end of the game.

Once all players have drawn a tile and integrated it into their food chains, they rotate the board so that each player draws from a new area of ​​the board, and so on.

The party ends when there are no more eggs or Dino tiles available. Players turn over their eggs to reveal the number of baby dinos: the player with the most baby dinos wins!

Outrun the others in eight brain-teasing puzzles from the world of Disney - even for the little ones!

Cortex Disney is a fast-paced observation-logic family party game for children. From card to card, players can test their speed in eight types of puzzles that use different areas of their brain. Cuckoo eggs, memory, matchmaking... and special, touchable cards! Can be combined with other Cortex Kids board games.

The Green Valley is the place to be for Goats that could collect Victory Point Tokens in Mountain Goats, but the stack of Tokens already has dried out.
Move your Goat to the Green Valley and gain at least half the points in game end scoring.

—description from the publisher

Dragons fly and eat donuts. Who will win and catch a gold?
Welcome to the little dragon race for the golden donut! And you can speed up or slow them down with the help of colorful donuts.
How to play?

At the beginning of the game, each player receives a secret objective card with three dragons. The winner will be the player who brings the most dragons from his card to the finish line and receives more victory points.
All the dragons are at the beginning, and 5 stacks of flock cards are placed in the center of the table. On his turn, the player performs 2 consecutive actions:

     Selects a flight map.
     Moves the dragon figurine.


The scales on the flight card always show what color the dragon and donut on the front of the card might be. The player moves the baby dragon shown on the flight card to the nearest donut of the indicated color.

—description from the publisher

Tetralchemia is a stand-alone card game for 1-4 players about alchemist girls who roll dice to capture creatures and use them to fight and craft.

The Master Alchemist of the kingdom of Evenfall has called a contest that will decide who her new students will be. Teams of alchemists compete for the title by capturing the souls of strong enemies, summoning them at the right time in battle, and molding them into powerful magics. Will you be the next students?

—description from the publisher

Showdown in Slingshot Canyon! In the midday heat, the players take on the role of crooks on nimble prairie dog paws on the prowl. And neither carriages nor the vaults guarded by the sheriff are safe from them. Which of the players will manage to scam the most money through cleverly planned moves, good card management and a little luck?

In Showdown players either use their turn to take a new card or play cards from their hand. A card always show 2 out of 5 possible actions. When playing cards players can play a number of cards of their choosing as long as all played cards share the same symbol and that symbol is visible at least 3 times on all cards played.
The active player then activate ALL symbols on the played cards, grouped and in order of their choosing.

The symbols have a variety of possible actions and let you move your bandit to the next spot or send the sherif off to guard the next vault of the bank in the middle of the board. Players can steal from a carrier or from other players by rolling dice and players can even save some of that well earned money so it can't be taken away from them ever again.

But the sheriff of Slingshot Canyon is always vigilant and once he reaches his lookout, players not in hiding or in disguise will lose some precious coins. 
As the game progresses the path the sheriff takes gets shorter and shorter and players have to play their hands smart to not lose it all. The player who leaves the Showdown of Slingshot Canyon the richest, wins the game.

Merchant Trails: The Silk Roads is a competitive Print & Play strategy game for 3 to 5 players.

Players are curious merchants who, excited by the bustling exchange made possible by the Silk Roads, are racing to collect various goods and trade them in the diverse cities along the renowned trading route.

During each trading season, players will make trade agreements, send out camel caravans to collect goods and sell these goods in different cities on the Silk Roads. Gain VP and special powers by selling combinations of goods in each city.

Optimise your trade, but don’t make too many enemies; as your caravans meander through the Silk Roads’ harsh terrains, the other players determine your route – and the goods available to you!

—description from the publisher

The Clutch is the first and only board game that will put you in the shoes of a basketball team Coach.

As a Coach, your mission will be to select the most suitable players to build a highly competitive team that will face other Coaches. Understanding your team's strengths and weaknesses will be the key to success. You will make crucial decisions during intense 3 vs. 3 player games with the goal of scoring 5 points before your opponent.

Use bonus cards strategically to gain valuable boosts that will help your team secure victory.

The ultimate goal: Accumulate as many wins as possible to ensure that your team qualifies for the playoffs and finally emerges as the season champion!!!

Are you ready to win The Clutch?

—description from the designer

Raket - Flug is a post war rocket launching game from Sweden. The game sees players roll 5 colored balls at a target board that has 5 openings at the base. The Target board shows a post war military rocket base. From the  back of the board 5 rockets are held in place under tension by rubber bands. The base of the rockets are seen through the openings in the target board base.

Players roll all balls one at a time and a direct hit to the rocket base launches the rocket. Each launched rocket has a certain kilometers to which a player receives i.e 30,000 Kilometers earns 30 points. Points are doubled if a player can launch a rocket of the same color as the ball rolled.

The winner is the highest score after a round by all players.

Most interesting about the game is the similar graphics and game play to the rare 1932 "Race to the Moon" game produced by "All Fair". The objective and scoring differ but the graphics have similar rocket launch pad.

—user summary

Three fun and educational games. In this game, jolly gnomes invite children to have fun while learning about colours, patterns, shapes and observation. There are three different ways to play.

—description from the publisher

Find the Gnome – All the gnomes are placed in a circle face up and the picture cards of the gnomes in a pile face down. One by one the picture cards are revealed, and the players try to find gnomes that match with their colours and shapes to the picture. The goal is to find as many gnomes as possible.

Patterns and Colours – All the gnomes are placed randomly face up and the picture cards in a pile face down. Each player takes a card from the pile and tries to find as many matching gnomes as possible. The game ends when all of the gnomes have been collected and the player with the most gnomes wins the game.

Hide-and-seek Gnomes – All the gnomes are placed randomly face down and each player is dealt four pattern cards. The aim is to find gnomes that match the player’s pattern cards by turning one gnome at a time face up. If the gnome doesn’t match, it is turned face down and it is a next player’s turn. When one of the players has found three gnomes the game ends.

Colour Crazy is a mind-bending game of brilliant colours. Race against your opponents to connect colours and claim victory. Colour Crazy will twist your mind, test your reflexes and leave you craving for just one more game.

To begin, set up enough matching piles on the table for the player count and then deal out each player's cards face down. Once the dealer shouts "Start," each player quickly grabs their top seven cards and must match them with the cards on the table. Matches can be made by pairing the word on one card with the colour of another. Once you place all the cards in your pile you win the round. Everyone else keeps the remaining cards in their hand for the next round. After three rounds whoever gets rid of all their cards is the winner!

In what seems like a simple matching task will split your brain in a mad rush to the finish. Use wild and block cards at opportune times to break up your opponents rhythm or string together a run of matches to grab the victory.

—description from the designer

In this children's game, you will move around on a track trying to collect the six items depicted on your player board. The winner is the first player to collect all their items.

Some spaces have face-down chance tokens. If you land on a chance token, you will perform the associated action (roll again, skip a turn, move five spaces, etc.)

You have the gift! As a MEDIUM, you can enter thoughts into the minds of others and make them name certain terms. But not everyone is born to be a MEDIUM. Maybe you are much more successful as a RECIPIENT? Whoever manages to build mental connections and read minds will win this game. But maybe it's enough to form obvious associations, without any telepathic abilities?

—description from the publisher

GAIA, the super AI designed to protect the Earth, has identified the biggest threat of them all - HUMANKIND.

In this semi-cooperative game, each player controls 2-6 characters with which they journey across the Earth and space. Your mission is to shut down GAIA by discovering a special reboot code. This is done by navigating the Earth while GAIA pours her wrath over it, and by finding the Code Tiles hidden there. For each active Code Tile discovered the players earn Victory Points. The players have to cooperate to successfully perform Space RUNS to the different destinations: the Moon, Mars, and the GAIA Space Station. Code tiles are hidden on these as well.

Each turn a player chooses a character to move on the Earth or one of the destinations, trying to reach the Code Tiles and play Action cards that also give them Victory Points. While moving they have to steer clear of the spaces destroyed by GAIA's attacks. Reaching a functioning space vessel and gathering a suitable crew is also essential.

There is no time to lose, as GAIA is wreaking havoc on the Earth with natural disasters and deadly drones. If the reboot code is not discovered in time mankind is destroyed and GAIA wins. If the reboot code is discovered, the single player with the most Victory Points earned wins the game!

—description from the publisher

One mystery . . . 6 suspects. It’s the classic game of Clue with a giant twist! Can you figure out who’s responsible for hijinks in the mansion? Nine mysterious rooms are laid out in a circle, each holding dark secrets inside to discover. Spin the spinner and move to a room by standing beside the room’s large vinyl mat. Inside, you can pick up 2 Evidence Cards – but you can keep only one. Or you might spin and take possession of another big piece of evidence: one of 6 large, foam Tools. Your goal: Move from room to room, collecting matching colored Suspect, Room, and Tool cards, along with the matching foam Tool, and you’ll solve the case! Was it Professor Plum with the Rubber Mallet in the Library? Or Mr. Green with the Trumpet in the Billiard Room? No case is too small to solve with Clue: GIANT Edition!

—description from the publisher
family: Game: Cluedo / Clue (Parker Brothers, et al)

boardgame: BLASTER: Game Magazine 05 – Dead by Lead
description:
Welcome to Bloodstone, a booming and lawless frontier town where danger lurks, and fortune corrupts. Dead by Lead is an immersive experience that transports you to the untamed world of the Old West. Prepare for epic showdowns, compelling campaign play, and etch your name in the annals of history.

In this thrilling miniatures skirmish game, will your gang become notorious gunslingers on a quest for glory or nameless corpses in a shallow grave? With every step, you'll encounter opportunities, but only the quick and the cunning will survive. Do you have what it takes to rise above the rest and build a legendary gang?

Immerse yourself in Bloodstone's open world, brought to life through your miniatures and scenery. Engage in heart-pounding shootouts, daring robberies, and high-stakes jailbreaks. With intuitive game mechanics designed to capture the tension and excitement of the Old West, Dead by Lead delivers Western skirmish in bold new ways.

What’s in the book:


     A complete set of skirmish rules 
     Detailed character and gang creation 
     Ongoing campaign rules
     Six gameplay scenarios


What do you need to play?


     Six to twelve Western miniatures or figurines to represent your gang of characters.
     One twelve-sided (d12) and several six-sided dice (d6) for each player.
     A tape measure or ruler for measuring distances in inches.
     A standard deck of playing cards.
     Dead by Lead is best suited to a flat 3' x 3' playing area to represent the battlefield.
     Various western terrain or model scenery (red dirt, train tracks, rivers, rocks, city buildings and crates) for visual and tactical interest.
     A pencil and tokens to help you keep track of various game mechanics. We recommend using six-sided dice as counters, as several dice are easier to manage than a score of tokens.

Welcome to a life-sized land of rainbow roads and sweet surprises: Candy Land Giant Edition! Unfold the large, 64” x 64” vinyl gameboard and open up your kids’ imaginations with illustrations of a colorful, magical Candy Land. In a game as big as this one, players become the movers! Spin the large Lollipop Spinner and move to that color on the board. If you reach the Peppermint Forest, Lollipop Palace, or one of the other Neighborhoods, collect a Card. Once you’ve collected 4 Cards, make your way up the Rainbow Road. The first player to reach King Kandy’s Castle wins and gets to wear the King’s foam crown! With simple rules and no reading required, Candy Land teaches younger kids color recognition and turn taking while letting them play in an imaginative, magical world.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Game of Life: Giant Edition
description:
Life is anything but boring in the giant version of this iconic Hasbro kids’ board game. The Game of Life is giant sized and extra fun. Take a trip through The Game of Life: Giant Edition- a futuristic world so big that you become the mover on the board! This classic game made big is perfect for outdoor get-togethers or birthday parties. Play indoors or outside with this board game for kids 8-12. Whether it is summer or winter, you can play this kids’ game wherever you like! The large, illustrated vinyl game board makes for big fun in the house or as an outdoor game. The rules to play this fun game are simple and easy to play. The player with the highest score wins. Collect a family, pet, vacation, & career to complete your adventure card! Then retire in your dream home. This family game for kids and adults is for 2-4 players, ages 8 and up. It is super convenient to be able to play as a 2-player game or as a multiplayer game. Cards in this iconic game for kids can range from “I celebrated my 100th birthday” to “Become a famous artist” to “I went on an exclusive vacation to a private island”. Get ready for tons of fun and imagination in The Game of Life: Giant Edition.

- description from publisher

Get rid of your cards to win. Deal 5 cards to each player.  Person to the left of the dealer goes first.  Match the first card flipped over from the deck.  Match by color, number, and numbers in sequential order (1,2,3,4,5, or 5,4,3, 2,1…etc).  Follow the instructions on each card with images (force cards).  Stacking or dropping doubles, triples, etc. of numbered cards are ok. When it’s your turn to play, you can drop a force card on anyone even if they are not sitting next to you.  For example, I can skip someone’s turn even if they are 5 seats down from me. Force cards are the cards with images, pictures, or words written on them.  The force card tells you what to do next once played. All Force cards are also wild cards.  You can simply use them to change the color if you desire.

Welcome to Flex Office! At Flex Office we offer our employees hybrid work options: work from home or in the office. Each player is a Team Leader of eight enthusiastic colleagues.
As a Team Leader, your job is to keep your team motivated. For best team morale, you provide the best workplaces in order to score the most points.

Every workplace is worth points, because the available workstations in the office are limited. The Team Leader with the most occupied workplaces (laptop cards) in an office space, gets the points associated with that office space. However, the competition for the best workplaces at Flex Office is so fierce, that your laptop may end up with the Clean Desk Officer if you haven't protected it!

Flex Office is a game that takes place during an 8 hour working day. Every hour is a game round and after 8 game rounds the players run out of cards in their hands. Then the points are counted, after which the Flex Office closes its doors for the day.

—description from the designer

Tawan Cards is a Peruvian card game The goal?
Conquer Tawantinsuyu (the Inca Empire) and become the new Inca!
Your soldiers are the Power Cards, aided by Special Cards such as the Apuskipaq and Chakra Runa which have different abilities and powers that will help you to advance and win in the game.

The "Cards of the Ancestors" expansion pack transforms the fundamental dynamics of the base game, altering known strategies through the skills of the villagers. Instead of the game revolving around two dominant institutions controlled by players, it now emphasizes the villagers, who bring varied trades and strategies. These villagers challenge your understanding of the game, let you meticulously monitor the game's cards, and get increasingly unstable as accusations fly.

Contents:
This pack features 7 scenarios (3-9), 21 new villagers (7 factions),and 2 Deck Cards for each side.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description: The object of the game is to play all 18 championship holes, in the least amount of strokes (shots) as possible. The layer with the lowest stroke total at the end of the 18 holes is declared the winner. On each hole your ball starts from the tee and is shot down the fairway until you put it on the green. Once on the green you begin to putt, to put the ball in the hole.

Recreates the air battles of the Iran-Iraq war from 1980 to 1988.  It follows the trajectory of the was as new systems and tactics evolved.  From the opening stages with Iraqi MIGs vs Iranian American made aircraft such as F-4s and F-14 Tomcats.

As the war progressed both the Iranians and Iraqis acquired new modern aircraft such as the Mirage F-1 and the MiG-29.

The book covers 9 campaigns over the eight year span and includes clashes outside the direct conflict with engagements by American and Saudi forces.

Also new or updated data on 30 aircraft involved in the conflict.

Bonfire Big Box includes Bonfire base game and Trees & Creatures Expansion.

The bonfires are sources of light, energy and warmth created by the guardians of light in order to brighten the cities on the otherwise dark planet. The residents of the cities however, took the bonfires for granted and exploited them for their personal gain. Disappointed the guardians of light retreated and let the bonfires extinguish. The citizens could no longer live in the now dark cities and were forced to leave.

You are a group of gnomes living close to the cities and you also need and the light of the bonfires. Missing it now, you try yourself to visit the cities and learn how to ignite the bonfires once again: You must visit the guardians of light on their holy islands and ask for tasks to prove your good will. For each completed task, they will re-ignite one extinguished bonfire. Whoever manages to earn the greatest trust from the guardians and manages to brighten their city the most will win the game.

The engine for Bonfire are the three-coloured tiles you will be puzzling onto your player board. When you manage to place the same colours adjacent to one another, you will receive more action tiles of that specific kind. This will allow you to specialize in certain types of actions and pursue different strategies.
You can use the tiles to perform the following actions:

     Move your ship to an island
     Receive a task from an island by spending two resources
     Invite a guardian of light into your city
     Trigger a procession of guardians through your city and gain resources.
     Add a landscape tile to your city (this is where the processions take place)
     Recruit a gnome gaining a special ability or victory points
     Find support by the last bonfire, gaining portals, resources or action tiles


You will play in turn order until a fixed number of tasks has been solved, after which each player has 5 more turns. During final scoring, you will receive points for your completed tasks (the bonfires) and any improvements made there (portals, landscapes or guardians).

—description from publisher

boardgame: My Very First Games: Sound Game Collection
description:
The music game collection is an addition to the range of MES games. We fill with it a gap to the topic music and/or sounds.

The game contains 5 different game ideas to meet the character of a game collection.

Sternschnuppern has a beautiful rotating game board, which implements the memo principle particularly exciting and challenging. The gems to be searched for are located under little hats. If the children guess the correct position of the gems they are looking for, they use the magic wand to conjure up the little hats and see what is underneath. Currently, this "magic moment" has not yet been implemented well enough. We have already talked about ideas to have the hats lifted up by a magnet built into the magic wand. These and other ideas are intended to make the moment when the child looks under the hat and sees whether he or she has guessed correctly even more magical.

—description from the publisher

King of the Dice: Junior is the new junior version of the successful board game from HABA. Children aged 4 and up try to build a castle as quickly as possible for the coronation of their new king of the dice. To do this, they must use three dice to achieve the most varied conditions on the playing cards. As a joker help with unsuccessful throws the crowns made of wood. These can be helpful to get hold of the 5 playing cards as quickly as possible and thus close the castle courtyard! Who in this imaginative world full of elves and orcs at the end of his castle completed first is the winner and dice king!

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Junior Versions of Grown-Up Games

description:
How does the little squid Kalmario grow up so quickly? In the HABA game for children aged 4 and up, the respective tentacle tiles must be placed in the right order. This requires skill and a good memory. Whoever has the necessary bit of luck and completes the sea creature with the matching octopus tentacle tips first, wins the fun children's game in bring-along format.

—description from the publisher

This memory game is a memory game with a certain (dice) luck factor.

Game content: 4 wooden boats + 2 wooden dice + cardboard tiles

The object of the game is to uncover the exact number of insects you have rolled.

You roll both dice. The number rolled on the white die indicates how many corners you may move on. The green die indicates how many insects you have to search for.

—description from the publisher

In the fun card game Stichelkönig from HABA, players gather around cactus king Miguel de la Espina, turn his spikes and try to play their cards wisely and skim off neat patches. Whoever can show the most of them wins. A fun card game from HABA for the whole family and cactus lovers from 8 years.

—description from the publisher

Desperate creatures are creeping around the village ... Can you provide them with food before they break down the gate and devastate everything?

As villagers, the children have to feed hungry monsters that would otherwise devastate the village. To do this, they practice worker placement, using their game pieces to perform different actions. While some plant vegetables in the garden, get milk from the cowshed or eggs from the chickens, others can turn the milk into cheese or, with a little luck, get coveted goods at the market. Always with an eye on the fence or the village gate - because this is where the hungry monsters gather! If they are not fed in time, they want to get their food themselves in the village and demolish the fence! Here teamwork and planning is needed to keep the monsters in the fence!

"Monster Hunger" trains concentration and gets the children to interact to achieve the game goal.

-description from publisher

Dial M for maybe? A crime committed in a stately manor (possibly), and as the world’s greatest detective (probably) it’s yours to solve. Five suspects will help (or hinder) your investigation – can you link a suspect, location, and weapon together, before time runs out?

A single-player micro-game – part solitaire, part interactive fiction.

boardgame: Cosmic Portal 2: Exploring the Multiverse (fan expansion for Carcassonne)
description:
Brave followers throw themselves into the mysterious swirling vortex. What will they find on the other side? Winter or desert? Cave men or cowboys? Discover for yourself!
With this fan expansion you can enter the multiverse, interrupting the base game and playing mini-games with several spin-off of the Carcassonne family waiting to close the Portal and to go back to the original game.

Evergreen: Pines and Cacti is a set of two modular expansions for Evergreen that introduces two new kinds of plants that interact with light and shadow in interesting ways.

Each modular expansion introduces a new power, and the game uses exactly six powers, so remove one power from play for each expansion module you add.

PINES
Small pines long for the kiss of sunlight, so they spontaneously grow into big pines when they are in shadow at the end of a season.

CACTI
Cacti can grow very quickly, but they need a lot of light to survive. Make sure they are not in shadow at the end of a season or they will die!

Test your railroading skills with the Archipelago Board, divided into four 4×4 Islands that may be connected to the neighboring Islands through Bridge spaces! It also features a new Special Building, the Warehouse, which allows you to “store” routes and save them for later use.

The was first available at Essen Spiel 2023.

Test your Quicksand  skills with Danger Mode!

This mini-expansion allows you to replay all of the levels from the base game with a higher difficulty level thanks to a Dangerous sand timer that triggers game over as soon as it runs out of sand.

Do you have what it takes to beat the DANGER MODE?

—description from the publisher

Join the incredible force of university-sponsored space commanders and cadets on a three-day adventure to save a nearby mining planet from a major volcanic event. In this journey for survival, you will rise to the challenge of an unfortunate crash landing, navigate volatile terrain, fend off agitated native scavengers and craft radio parts. As dusk falls on the third day, you either radio home and triumph over adversity as a team, or you die a hot, miserable death under volcanic rubble.

Radiocraft is a medium-weight, cooperative strategy game designed by Cathedral Games. Each in-game day contains multiple rounds. During each round, commanders will each choose a primary and secondary action set to take - giving direction to cadets, negotiating trades, producing key materials needed for survival, planting and harvesting fruit, extinguishing fires, upgrading abilities, and attracting new recruits to the cause. Actions you take are required for survival and also for experience-building in crafting the radio parts required to radio home. Each action set is distinct and requires planning ahead to produce a successful outcome. After each player takes their turn, the round ends and end-of-round events occur - fire from volcanic activity spreads, scavengers move from tile, to tile, which initiates combat, and gardens grow. By sunset of each day, players must ensure that cadets are under their care or protected at supply camps and enough fruit is collected to survive the next day.

—description from the publisher

In the game of "Impromptu," players are in for a whirlwind of creativity, quick thinking, deduction, and a dash of luck. Each round presents players with a unique challenge card, with tasks like improvising stories, sharing dreams, uncovering secrets, or stepping into the shoes of talk show guests.

Players aim to race their pawns to the finish line on the game board. To progress, they must complete challenges using word cards. The game proceeds with players taking turns, following a clockwise order, until one player's pawn successfully reaches the end of the track.

A typical turn involves a player selecting a challenge card, executing the challenge as directed, moving pawns based on points earned by the players invlolved in the challenge, and placing the used challenge card face-down.

The game features 5 different challenges, each offering unique twists and creative opportunities: Talk Show, Once Upon a Time, Secret Word, Who Are You?, and Crazy Dream.

This fast paced card game lets 2 players engage in a chase between the monster and Mr. U.

Mr. U uses his path cards to run as far away as possible, and the monster tries to chase him down. Roles change every now and then, and the player who first wins 3 chases wins the game! But beware, bombs and dynamites stir things up.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Damnation: The Gothic Game – The Night of The Vampire
description:
