Night of the vampire is the first expansion for Damnation: The Gothic Game, which adds new cards, characters and deathknell events to the core game.

Beware Nosferatu, a powerful new vampire has entered the castle.

boardgame: Dying Message: The Novelist's Last Sentence
description:
A famous novelist was murdered by someone. At the crime scene, there was a dying message left by the novelist.
From the novelist's dying message, can you determine the killer's occupation, weapon, and motive?

This is a cooperative deduction game for 2 to 6 players.

2-5 people can take part in the game. The individual lanes are filled by drawing lots. The racing cars line up at the start. Racer l begins to roll a die, the others follow in turn. The cars advance as many spaces as the number of points rolled. If a car comes onto a black space, it has a breakdown and loses a turn. Space W means to roll the die again.
On the + and - spaces, the car must go forward or back the specified number of spaces.
If a car comes to a stop on space A, it is eliminated from the game due to machine damage or an accident. 
The winner is the one who passes through the target first.
The players an spectators may bet on the winner of the race.
The first expansion to Everything Ever including 15 new category cards.

Nosh is the world’s first charcuterie board game. In a world where charcuterie boards are ubiquitous (and sometimes don’t even contain charcuterie), they have become a staple for any kind of get-together. But how much do we think about the food on the board and the people eating from it as we enjoy a charcuterie board? Nosh was created to encourage intentionality around the charcuterie board as we think about the pieces we pick up and the people around the table with us.

As a unique offering that straddles the line between board games and kitchen products, Nosh is positioned to cover multiple purposes and markets. As a product, it can be used as a board game or as a keepsake charcuterie serving board.

Nosh is a board game - charcuterie display board hybrid. The board itself will be a wooden display board of near-keepsake quality, and will come with high-quality wooden pieces. The NOSH game will include:

Wooden playing / serving board
Wooden pawns
Nosh conversation cards
Plastic die
Nosh playing instructions

Because the game involves eating, it will be up to the players to provide the food that goes on the board, but a list of suggestions will be provided. In markets where we will do hyperlocal marketing, we will also partner with local cheese shops and retailers that sell charcuterie items to encourage local consumption.

The game is collaborative and not particularly competitive - the joy comes in building the board with your friends, sharing your thoughts on the discussion prompts, and enjoying a convivial environment to enjoy good food. As far as gameplay is concerned, comparable games include Chutes and Ladders or Candy Land. It is not intended to be particularly difficult, with the main focus being on the conversation and food rather than the game itself.

—description from the publisher

Players choose their poop personality and plop them into toilet bowls. Twenty game cards form the outer track that changes from game to game. Fun and quick to play. It takes strategy and good dice rolls to avoid getting flushed and remain the last poop floating.

boardgame: Marching Order: Quick and Dirties #5 – Let Me Out!
description:
Madame Toadlicker needs you to clean out her boarding house, but the House of Fading Flowers has always attracted strange visitors. Sometimes, you gotta worry if it’s worth the coin. But the good madame has always been kind to you and your fellow Rogues, so it’s time to repay the favor.

Oh, and if you’ve never played Marching Order before, don’t worry—this is a great opportunity to get into it.

Marching Order Quick and Dirties are no-nonsense adventures meant to be played in a single sitting. Each one comes on a twelve-panel, fold-out brochure that fits real nice in your Marching Order box. They’re a great way to expand your collection without breaking the bank.

—description from the publisher

Plan your tours to the most emotional places in the world.

The goal of the game is to be the first player to reach 100 points and win the game. In order to collect points, you will move around the world and perform specific actions. Cards will allow you to move over land taking a bus, car or train. Flight tickets let you travel faster using connected airports. Reaching a required secret or public destination earns you points and lets you leave your flag there. Every time another player is reaching or crossing a destination with your flag on it, this will earn you additional points. Now isn’t that nice? Being the first to visit a desired spot is not the only option to score points: For each continent traveled you will get your passport stamped. A passport page full with stamps gets you extra points. And don’t forget to pass by the Betting Office every now and then: Try your luck and place bets on the outcome of a match, the number of goals, or even the exact final score. Correct bets also get you extra points. But beware: Sudden events might throw off your plans and force you to reschedule your route.

So, plan your trip wisely, collect tickets and points, and be the first Groundhopper to reach 100 points and win this exciting journey around the world!

—description from the designer

Create rooms, let customers move in, and compete for the rent money.

Draw the pieces in the envelope at random and arrange them on your site to make a room.

Once you have created a room, have guests who meet your requirements move in.

You can make many rooms and invite many guests,

You can make a lot of rooms and invite a lot of customers, or you can make a big room and have rich people move in!

—description from the publisher (translated)

部屋を作ってお客さんに入居させて家賃収入を競うゲームです。
封筒に入ったピースをランダムに引いて 自分の敷地に並べて部屋を作ります。

部屋を作ったら条件の合うお客さんに入居してもらいましょう。

たくさん部屋を作ってたくさんお客さんを呼ぶもよし、

大きい部屋を作ってお金持ちに入居してもらうもよし、 たくさん家賃収入を稼ぎましょう！

—description from the publisher

GAME ABOUT CATS: The Monopoly Crazy for Cats board game is for cat lovers and those who know them! In this Monopoly game with a twist, players collect cats, buy cat properties, and win Cat Fights.

PLAY FOR CATS NOT CASH: Forget the cash in this Monopoly game. Instead, cats are currency. Scoop 'em up or give 'em up throughout the game. Purr Power die gives players a special boost to their turn.

CHOOSE A CHARACTER: Move around the board with one of the crazy cat lover tokens. Each character features a unique ability that can be activated when landing on the Cat Treat space.

CAT FIGHT: Pass Go and trigger a Cat Fight! Get a shot at fighting shady breeders to rescue the cats. The game ends when the final Cat Fight is over. The player with the highest score wins.

Includes: gameboard, 4 Character tokens, 4 Character cards, 4 Reminder cards, 8 Cat Fight cards, 16 Title Deed cards, numbered die, Purr Power die, parts sheet containing 95 Cat coins (55 single-Cat coins, 40 five-Cat coins), and game guide.

If you can answer yes to one or more of the questions, you have every chance of winning this game. Liverpostej, jantelov og klaphatte is a entertaining game where you gain or lose points in relation to how typically Danish you behave... for better or for worse.

The game contains more than 300 questions, which are about, among other things, liver pate, box wine, potted plants and red sausages.

boardgame: Meowchelin Stars:  A Game of Cat Chefs & Cuisine
description:
Welcome to Meowchelin Stars, where strategy meets whimsy in a tabletop card game.  You will be competing and negotiating with your friends to hire mischievous Meow Chefs and create mouth-watering menus with Delicious Cuisine sets!

As you play the game, 9 Meow Chefs will join your team - use their special abilities to seize Victory in a game with your friends.

Play Coupon cards that feature a double-function for an action or money, as you create your menu cuisine.

Your goal is to collect as many 'Meowchelin Stars' as you can to be the winner at the end of the game!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Unstable Unicorns: Golden Glazed Unicorn Promo Card
description:
Golden Glazed Unicorn is a new mini in the Unstable Unicorns Rainbow Apocalypse Vinyl Mini Series which comes with a promo card.

Card Text:
Golden Glazed Unicorn
Magical Unicorn Card
When this card enters your Stable, DRAW 2 cards. Your Hand limit is increased by 2 cards.

boardgame: Unstable Unicorns: Soul Sucking Unicorn Promo Card
description:
Soul Sucking Unicorn is a new mini in the Unstable Unicorns Vinyl Mini Series which comes with a promo card.

Card text
Soul Sucking Unicorn
Magical Unicorn card
When this card enters your Stable, you may SACRIFICE a Unicorn card, then STEAL up to 3 Upgrade cards.

Your wizards gaze across the mystical planet of Sygon. Volatile volcanoes have blazed away much of the world, except for a precious clearing of land. Here lies the various landscapes of Sygon: meadows, swamps, dunes, mountains, forests, and caves.

Each landscape hosts a unique spell component crucial to developing civilization on Sygon. Collect gold and the various spell components - flower, elixir, spice, powder, mushroom, and crystal - and maneuver your wizards to conjure towns, workshops, and markets to quickly advance your civilization. Use alchemy or forage to obtain components outside your grasp.

Sygon relies on you to bring prosperity to its ravaged lands. However, you are not alone. Wizards loyal to other civilizations plot to loot you and compete for control of the planet. Be wary of where you place your towns and wizards and build walls to protect them against thieving wizards and volcano eruptions. Rise from the ashes and bring glory to your civilization on Sygon.

For the game setup, the players will construct the randomly generated hexagonal game board of flower, elixir, spice, powder, mushroom, and crystal hexes and randomly assign a gold value to each hex. Players will begin the game by placing 2 towns in snake draft order. Each town is placed on an unsettled hex of choice with either 1 or 2 walls in the same hex. A player places their 3 wizards in hexes with their towns on their first turn.

On a player's turn, they will move their wizards, conjure buildings, and cast spells in order. Each building type (town, workshop, market, and wall) contains a unique resource combination, and a player's wizard must be in a hex for them to build there. Action spells consist of wizards foraging component cards from the various landscapes of Sygon, stealing gold from nearby opponents, and protecting from stealing. Additional spell cards (charm, fissure, vanish, blizzard, power trip, & foresight) may be added to this base 2-4 player game.

The goal of Hexes of Sygon is to accumulate enough and the most sorcery points (SP) of all players at the end of a round of turns to win, where SP are awarded through buildings.

—description from the publisher

The object of the game is to travel around the board, playing each game, trying to win money and collect money. This money is used to buy your dreams. 
Games include Poker, Odds & Evens, Two Up, Crown & Anchor, Horse Racing and Blackjack.
Players must buy three prizes from the bank:

     One for $800 'luxury motorcars', 
     One for $1000 'Toys for the idle rich' and 
     One for $1500 'Wonders of the modern world'


These can be bought from the bank at any time during the course of the game. When a player has bought three of these, then a Pacific Island may be bought. The WINNER is the first player to buy the Pacific Island.
A player may sell prizes back to the bank at face value if the player is running short of money. Players may borrow money from the bank upon the consent of the other players.

Likely timeframe of manufacture, mid 1980's.

—user summary

Hincksville is quite the perplexing town! As a resident, and avid reader of The Hincks Gazette, you know it’s up to you to unwind the enigmas hidden within this puzzling newspaper.

Each issue of The Hincks Gazette is a self-contained game which includes a front-page article filling you in on the debacle currently turning Hincksville upside down, and a path of connected puzzles which will ultimately help you solve the central conundrum and save* the day! (*wherein “save” can be defined as, but not limited to: successfully  troubleshooting your houseplant that won’t stop sassing you, preserving the historical record of astronautical dinosaurs, or uncovering the truth behind the suspiciously helpful robot bellhops).

The Hincks Gazette is best played with 1-2 players, and has a playtime of 60-120 minutes per issue. The rules are simple, just start at START and you'll be on your way. While this game is mostly played offline, players can visit the Hincksville website to solve the mystery or access hints.

—description from the publisher

Hincksville is quite the perplexing town! As a resident, and avid reader of The Hincks Gazette, you know it’s up to you to unwind the enigmas hidden within this puzzling newspaper.

Each issue of The Hincks Gazette is a self-contained game which includes a front-page article filling you in on the debacle currently turning Hincksville upside down, and a path of connected puzzles which will ultimately help you solve the central conundrum and save* the day! (*wherein “save” can be defined as, but not limited to: uncovering the secret behind the perfect dancing of rival town Fhincksburg, ascertaining the fate of a favorite town icon, or exposing the nefarious plot of a meddling brother in a robotic contraption).

The Hincks Gazette is best played with 1-2 players, and has a playtime of 60-120 minutes per issue. The rules are simple, just start at START and you'll be on your way. While this game is mostly played offline, players can visit the Hincksville website to solve the mystery or access hints.

—description from the publisher

The color of the pieces represents one of the four symbols on the GO-G0 board. Each player places his four pieces in his inner starting zone from which all pieces must go over the entire GO-GO track - in the direction of the arrow — into the outer goal zone. To start into the GO-GO-track a player must
throw either a two, four or six. The piece advances as many spaces as the die shows. A player with a six is allowed an extra throw with which he can either advance his starting piece or start a second piece on the G0-G0 track.
If a piece lands on a occupied space the piece on this space is knocked out and must start from the beginning again. An exception is a piece on a crossed space, these cannot be knocked out. Further barriers can be built on the eight crossed spaces of the track. No pieces are allowed to jump these barriers.
The winner is the player who brings all four pieces into the goal zone, in which the die must equal the exact number of moves required to reach the goal zone.

—user summary

The Siege of the Bastille is a game for two. Each person has four stones with which he must try to reach the Bastille in the middle of the board before his opponent. Players take it in turns to move their stones, advancing only one square at a time, but in any direction. The first person to occupy three of the positions on the fort is the victor.

—user summary

In this fast-paced and educational board game, players gain important knowledge and lots of laughs through fun physical challenges and clever questions on their way to the top of the mountain.

Till toppen was developed by three young girls who had an idea for a children's game that reduces sedentary time and screen time and that turns knowledge and social interaction into something fun. The questions and challenges have been developed together with children in the target group to make the game fun and just the right amount of difficulty.

A mind-bending social card game in which players respond to a hysterically tricky combination of colours and words. No matter how quick-witted you think you are, this game will catch you out. Designed to bumfuzzle the brain!

Compete against family and friends to see how quick-witted you are! Say the word on the card, or the colour of the word, but NEVER, EVER….that’s NEVER, say the word 'BUMFUZZLE'.

AIM OF THE GAME! The first player to get rid of their cards wins!! Sounds easy? Not in this game. Be the first to respond correctly and victory could be yours! Get it wrong and you’ll be picking up all the cards.

Fast-paced game that blends strategy, speed, and special attacks.

A card-driven game about the most popular sport in the North Wasteland. Play cards to hit the ball, and don't forget to use spell cards to hinder your opponent! Observe your opponent's  moves and play smart to become the best Guardinton player!

Officially licensed from the creators of the How to be a Mind Reaver comic series.

—description from the publisher

An explicit expansion deck. From outrageous world records to melting eyeballs, this deck brings you 200 exclusive cards that you won't find anywhere else and can be played stand-alone or with the main deck.

boardgame: Detecteam Kids: Tales of the Plush Forest
description:
In childhood, every day is filled with interesting events! Meet Christopher and his plush toy friends: Little Bear, Piglet, Rabbit and other animals. Together they always find a way how to have fun: they can invent a tale and bring it to life with all their unusual ideas. What should they do this time? Maybe arrange a costume ball, a tea party or go hunt for a bizarre beast? However, even the best plans can go awry… But Christopher and his friends are not afraid of any difficulties ― the unexpected circumstances make their games even more exciting!

Tales of the Plush Forest is a co-operative adventure for young detectives. Your goal is to piece together the full picture, just like a jigsaw puzzle, and re-create the events. Find out what actually happened in this stand-alone game from the Detecteam Kids range!

—description from the publisher
The exclusive 50-card expansion about the man that started it all! Can be played stand-alone or with the main deck.

boardgame: Host Your Own Murder Mystery: At the Manor
description:
The newest addition to our Host Your Own collection, Murder Mystery at the Manor is a role-playing detective game which transports players to the Scottish Highlands where there has been a terrible murder. Solve the case and work out whodunnit.

We include online content such as audio clues and playlists to help set the mood, and hosting tips to help things run smoothly. All our murder mysteries come with three alternative endings so they can be enjoyed again and again.

Our Host Your Own collection creates immersive experiences for the players to enjoy. From the moment the host sends out the downloadable invites with the players character information, till the last dessert spoon hits the table, our game features all is needed for a great night of amateur sleuthing.

—description from the publisher

Why watch a gameshow when you can play one live? Bring the fun into your own home and put your skills to the test! Gather your friends and family round the table and get ready to buzz your way to victory through several frantic rounds of mind bogglers and brain teasers!

Let the games continue… This product has been designed to keep - please enjoy, replay and give away for further play.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: LOOP: Life of Ordinary People – The Future Expansion
description:
What will our world be like in 50 years? Will our next generation be able to live happily and sustainably, or still be struggling with our old paths while paying our old debt?

—description from the publisher

The future (expansion) adds futuristic careers, new challenges and several challenging special events.

Get ready for a thrilling game of cunning and wit with Wuzzled! the ultimate word puzzle game for 2-4 players! Unleash your inner wordsmith and compete against friends and family in a fast-paced game of deduction and strategy. Wuzzled! will have you attempting to create the perfect word to outsmart your opponents while frantically trying to uncover theirs. 
So grab your letters and prepare to flex your brain muscles with Wuzzled! Can you be the last word standing?

Wuzzled starts with each player creating a secret word and hiding it under their tile holder. 
The game is then played in 2 phases. 
The first is the GUESSING phase, where you target an opponent and try to guess their word. 
The second is the CHECKING phase, where you check the words guessed against you, and place coins on your alphabet sheet to show which letters were guessed right and which are not in your word. 
If your word is guessed, you are out! 
Play rotates between the two phases until only one player remains - the last word standing!

—description from the publisher

A game of Krieg Eterna is divided into three separate rounds of play, where your objective is to win more rounds than your opponent. Each player's side of the field is split into three rows: melee, ranged, and siege. At the end of each round, the player with the most total strength across all of their rows wins that round. Players take turns playing cards until all players say "pass", after which they score the field, discard all cards on the field to the graveyard, and then start the next round.

—description from the publisher

Restless Spirits is an easy-to-learn tactical card game where you play as a Spirit Guide, and you're helping Lost Spirits find their way home. Combining elements of set collection, tactical card drafting, ability combos, and push your luck, it's a race against your opponent(s) to free the spirits.

There are 30 unique Light and Dark Spirits to encounter, with a different set of 3 Totem requirements listed at the bottom of each card. Every turn, you must decide where best to use your 2 Action Points: attracting Spirits to your board, buying Totem cards from a marketplace, and playing them to tick off a Spirit's requirements one by one.

You'll also be using powerful Spirit abilities to gain an advantage over your opponent - Light Spirit abilities will help you win even faster, whilst Dark Spirit abilities will slow down your opponents instead! But Spirit abilities can only be used once-per-game, so tactical timing is important to get the most out of them!

If you can meet all 3 of a Spirit's Totem requirements, it will be released into the Spirit Realm, becoming one of the cards you need to win the game! The first player to released the required number of Spirits (changes with player count), wins!

—description from the designer

Delve into the mysterious and expansive world of Olroc, a planet on the brink chaos and destruction. Here you will find the likes of post-cataclysmic dramas, tales of exploration, monsters, mechs and all-powerful ancient beings known as Forelords. Explore deeply human themes like existential dread, overpopulation, love, and the threats of technology. Dive into the world by reading the corresponding novels, then come here to play the characters.

Forelords is a 1v1 dueling trading card battle between competing biomes. Play as one of many lands, whether they be high, low, wet or frozen. Move your cards into spaces like chess pieces, and gain favor by collecting one or more of the five terrain tokens. To win, you must destroy your opponent's king or collect the most points in battle by the showing of the final monolith — whichever comes first...

During the game, you will add land and ally cards to your hand, pay the cost to put them into play, then use them to battle your adversary. Land cards will equip you with valuable resources, such as metal, batteries, credit, knowledge...even coveted terrain tokens. Ally cards will maneuver and melee at your command to overcome foes as well as the wilderness.

Will you amass resources and knowledge to wage a strategic battle, or go straight for the king? Only the Forelords know for sure...

Game Mechanics

The game features popular TCG mechanics like hand management, player elimination and deck construction, but sets itself apart with a distinct mix of mechanisms that allow players to:

• Explore a hostile and unpredictable wilderness (exploration)
• Move cards on a grid to tactically position allies for battle (grid movement)
• Unlock unique “fortes" during play (variable player powers)
• Gain surges in power by capturing one or more terrain tokens

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Black Powder Epic Battles: The Waterloo Campaign – Bonaparte's French Army Starter Set
description:
Sunday, 18 June 1815; on an unassuming rain-soaked field in Belgium; two great generals of the age finally met in battle; ending fifteen years of continuous warfare on the continent, and bringing to an end the reign of Napoleon. The Battle of Waterloo, arguable the most famous battle in all military history, provides more than a fitting subject for our second foray into the 'Epic' scale of wargaming. Black Powder Epic Battles: Waterloo is upon us.

Bonaparte's French Army starter set provides a solid core of a French army fighting in the Hundred Days campaign - 10 units of line/light infantry, 3 units of skirmishing voltigeurs, units of each type of heavy cavalry (Cuirassiers, Dragoons and Carabiniers) and light cavalry (Hussars, Chasseurs a Cheval and Lancers), supported by 16 pieces of artillery and brigade commanders. Also included is an MDF building of the Decoster House and a bespoke A5 softback rulebook with Napoleonic specific content.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Black Powder Epic Battles: The Waterloo Campaign – Blücher's Prussian Army Starter Set
description:
After Napoleon entered Paris and started the Hundred Days campaign, King William II of Prussia recalled Blücher from his retirement in Silesia and set him the task of facing down the upstart Frenchman. He was placed in command of the Army of the Lower Rhine and quickly marched west to join the Anglo-Dutch allies, hoping to consolidate their forces.

Unfortunately, the Prussian army was hastily assembled and pressed into the field ill-equipped and poorly trained. Over a third of the force was Landwehr (militia), some of which marched barefoot to war. At the Battle of Ligny the Prussian army faced Napoleon directly and was roundly beaten, Blücher himself being trapped injured under his dead horse for hours. The Battle of Waterloo provided a chance at redemption.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Intervention 1967: A Sword of Israel Expansion
description:
In June 1967, Israel launched a pre-emptive wear against her Arab neighbors, crushing the armies and air forces of Egypt, Jordan and Syria. The Soviet Union refused to intervene on behalf of her Arab allies, but the Israeli high command could not be completely sure that Soviet paratroopers might not appear in the skies over Tel Aviv.

Intervention 1967 is a small expansion set for Panzer Grenadier (Modern) 1967: Sword of Israel, based on how such an operation might have played out. The Soviet Army’s 103rd Guards Airborne Division is a well-trained and well-armed outfit, in the midst of preparations for the Dnepr 1967 exercises - a complete contrast to the Arab armies the Israelis face in the standard scenarios.

The Soviet parachute battalion of 1967 is very much a light infantry outfit - air-droppable armored carriers would only start to arrive the next year, converting the airborne formations into far more capable all-arms units. To make up for that lack, the battalion wields a large number of support weapons: Sagger anti-tank missiles, heavy mortars, recoilless rifles and the diminuitive ASU-85 assault gun.

Intervention 1967 includes 24 playing pieces representing these troops, plus a couple of new Sword of Israel scenarios for their use. Intervention 1967 is an expansion set, not a complete game. It’s a download; you’ll have to assemble the pieces yourself.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: U-Boat Captain: Solitaire WW2 Wargame – Advanced Edition
description:
U-BOAT CAPTAIN, Advanced Edition is a solitaire, tactical-operational level game covering individual German World War 2 U-Boat patrols and combat encounters, 1939-1942.

This edition (44 pages) comes with full rules, optional and advanced rules, eight U-Boat Displays, map, logsheet, 37 counters, and Reference Cards, along with with several historical scenarios and campaign games (of various lengths), extensive examples of play, historically rated U-Boat "aces", and Designer's Notes.   
The game has the feel of a miniatures rule set, but with boardgame format.  Utilize  various historical German U-Boat Types (Type VIIA, VIIB, VIIC, IXA, IXB, IXC, IIB, and IID), each rated for fuel capacity and consumption, number of torpedoes carried, deck gun characteristics, and durability/survivability.  
Conduct monthly partrols to various locations (North Atlantic, Norway, the Arctic, Spain, West Africa, etc.) from 1939 through 1942 and try and achieve the most tonnage sunk you can, without being sunk yourself.  In each patrol, you wiill sail to your area of operation... 
conduct daily patrols/searches...
if you have an encounter, decide when/if to attack (watch out for escorts)... 
if you attack, perform the attack sequence and launch torpedoes, or use your deck gun. 
When fuel starts to run low, or your boat has accumulated too much damage, you can sail back to port for refit and rest, before beginning your next patrol.  Sink at least 100,000 tons of allied shipping in your career and earn for yourself the coveted Knight's Cross.

This Minden Battlegame book (Volume 11) emphasizes high playability combined with authentic historicity to produce a game that plays quickly but retains a strong historical and authentic narrative. As such, U-Boat Captain aims to be your "go to" game for highly playable WW2 submarine action covering the Battle of the Atlantic. The game system often reflects the "risk versus reward" nature of the theater, as it places you in the role of a U-Boat commander, working with your crew to employ good tactics in an effort to sink allied shipping, while avoiding being sunk yourself.

This Advanced Edition retains the game system as published in Panzerschreck #19, and is fully compatible with it, but it adds new U-Boat types and additional optional rules, providing players with a comprehensive game that covers the war years from 1939 through 1942, the pivotal period of the Battle of the Atlantic.

Can you be a successful U-Boat Captain?

—description from the publisher

Dark Star Inc: a multi-planetary conglomerate private security corporation for hire.  
If you need a job done quickly and discretely, they are the place to go.

Dark Star has the experts for any job, big or small.  
From quiet rescue operations, to taking over a planet, Dark Star has what you need… for the right price.

Dark Star Inc is a game of random encounters on far off planets or derelict starships between a small rescue team and a race of vicious xenomorphs.  
The ability to use multiple game board layouts, along with randomly generated encounters using cards makes the game unique every time you play.

Turn Sequence:

     Team Member Actions - Team Members use up to two actions to move, use equipment, place traps, whatever
     Opponent Actions - Xenomorphs or Survivors move up to two Zones in this phase
     Team Member Combat - Team Members use a weapon to attack threats using d6 to determine results.
     Opponent Combat - Opponents conduct attacks against adjacent team members


The game includes counters for all combatants, but you can use figures of your choice to add to the visuals impact.

Available as a download at Wargames Vault

boardgame: The Season: Love and Drama in the Regency Era – The Jane Austen Expansion
description:
The Jane Austen Expansion brings the writer's popular heroines and suitors to The Season. Play as the witty Elizabeth Bennet or the beautiful and charming Emma Woodhouse!

As a blooming debutante in the London season, your primary goal is to have the highest reputation points by enhancing your accomplishments, improving your reputation among the social elite, and, most importantly, encouraging proposals and getting married.

You may enhance your accomplishments directly through actions or with choices you make during special events. At the end of the game, each accomplishment point you have translates to one reputation point. Your reputation is improved by socializing and wearing the right outfits at various balls. Debutantes who wear matching dresses and accessories, especially those that match the balls’ themes, receive higher reputation points. You may encourage a suitor to propose by raising a suitor’s affection level to 10 and ensuring you are eligible for that suitor’s dowry and accomplishment requirements.

The lady with the highest amount of reputation points at the end of the game will be the winner of this exciting London season!

Masters of the Game is an expansion for Deadball: Baseball With Dice that replicates the evolution of the baseball across the Twentieth Century, with all new rules that allow fans to simulate the financial side of the sport they love. It requires the second edition of Deadball: Baseball With Dice to play. It includes:


     Updated rules for eight distinct eras of historical play, including the War Time era, the Expansion Era, the Steroid Era, and today.
     Optional rules for everything from spitballs and Astroturf to a war time draft that determines which players stay behind—and which don’t make it home.
     A completely new system of team finances that allows you to carry a franchise to the heights of the game—or drive it into the ground.
     Rules for weather and cross-country travel that change era to era.
     Updated rosters for the Southern Circuit, both in 2023 and 1914.
     Two new campaigns for Nine Game Pennant, a fast-paced campaign mode that tosses you into the heat of a Southern Circuit championship race.
     A host of gorgeous digital supplements, including updated Quick Reference guides for every era in the game.


Welcome to Deadball—yesterday, tomorrow, and today.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Shell Company: Don't Write Me Off
description:
Welcome to the high-paced world of turtle dating! Your goal is to run the most successful turtle dating company, helping to romance turtles and sweep them right out of their shells. But you’re not the only one vying for their attention; you might find yourself at odds with a rival company as you attempt to woo these reptiles. You better make sure you have just the right gifts to make the best impression. If that doesn’t work, you could always donate the gifts and write off the expenses. After all, it’s not just about the love; it’s also about the money.

Shell Company - Don’t Write Me Off is the first in a series of games Born at PAX®. What does “Born at PAX” mean? We at Lynnvander Studios teamed up with the PAX team to run a panel called Design a Board Game Live. Shell Company - Don’t Write Me Off was conceived by the crowd at PAX East 2022. They came up with the combination of turtle dating sim and tax evasion, and we refined their ideas into the board game you’re holding now!

Shell Company is an auction-style bidding game with a touch of bluffing where players will need to manage their finances in order to acquire gifts. These gifts can either be used to romance potential clients, or they can be donated and used as a tax write-off. Both of these things are important because players are scored based on their weaker area.

—description from the publisher

Welcome to the Big Apple, a city rotten to its core. A high-profile politician has just been murdered, and only the Worminators can solve the case. You’re one of the hotshot detectives that have earned the title of Worminator, and you’ve been ordered by the chief to investigate this murder. If the media frenzy around the case weren’t enough to throw you off your game, the mayor’s office seems to be blocking you at every turn. Rumors run rampant that this might be… an inside job.

Worminators: Inside Job is a game Born at PAX. What does “Born at PAX” mean? We at Lynnvander Studios teamed up with the PAX team to run a panel called Design a Board Game Live. Worminators: Inside Job was conceived by the crowd at PAX West 2022. They came up with the combination of buddy cop worms, and we refined their ideas into the board game you’re holding now!

In Worminators: Inside Job, each player is a worm detective assigned to the case. You will build your own deck of Detective cards as the game advances, using them to gather evidence and bring it to the courthouse. Will you be able to assemble the clues and solve the murder before the city becomes overrun with corruption or drowns in criminal activity? Get your badge and let’s find out.

—description from the publisher

It's summer! Resorts, beaches and swimming.  Sounds like a lot of fun.  Of course there are sharks.  Just like humans, there are many types of sharks, some powerful and some not.  Modern sharks are crossing race barriers and "hunting" in teams.  So, which team will capture the most humans and feed their appetites?

Draw cards to roll dice so that you can eat as many humans as possible.

Kalambury De Luxe is a well-known and beloved party game in a new, unique edition. Over eight hundred phrases in five categories, for you to act out or draw! Try to control bursts of laughter as you guess Pictionary with your team!

Gain control of the land! Vanquish your foes! Complete your quest!

Draconicus the Fantasy Boardgame is an engrossing fantasy boardgame based on the Draconicus fantasy setting. It is a game of quests, strategy and adventure for 2-5 players. Playing time is between 3-5 hours and it is best suited for players ages 12 and up.

The objective of Draconicus – The Fantasy Boardgame is to build influence with different factions of the medieval island of Draconica: feudal realms, religions and cults, barbarian tribes and different schools of magic. Players must then use that influence to complete a quest. The first player to complete their quest is the winner.

This boardgame can also be played in tandem with the Draconicus Role-Playing Game.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Shelfie Stacker: Rad Reviewers Promo Pack
description:
The ability on this card is pretty powerful as it allows 2’s to be turned to 6’s. 6-valued dice in the game are wild, allowing you to turn them to any face that you please!

—description from the publisher

Originally made available through the KS campaign for Dreadful Meadows

Time Flies is a head to head asymmetric trick-taking cake defence game for 2 players. One player assumes the role as a fly, trying deviously to get some cake on its birthday, the other, as a child trying to stop the fly landing on their cake and ruining their own birthday!

Time Flies has elements of racing, set-collection, area control, and of course trick-taking. All you need to play is a standard deck of playing cards, one game sheet, and a pen.

—description from the designer

A collaborative game made in France!
Play as a detective: On September 24, 2021, the famous maestro Ernst Ejna was found dead in his dressing room. Re-establish the chronology of events, study the various testimonies and unmask the murderer.
A complete criminal file: Contains testimonies, forensic analysis, press articles, crime scene photos, audio content...
Escape game mechanics: Solve puzzles to unlock 3 envelopes containing additional evidence.
Detective 2.0 : Internet access is required to hack a computer, unlock a phone and open a safe. Also, several levels of clues are available on the site so you don't get stuck

description:
A collaborative game made in France!
Play as a detective: Christophe Cannette, a famous TV actor, is found dead in the pool of his villa in Mykonos. Re-establish the chronology of events, draw up a list of suspects and investigate in order to shed light on this case.
A complete criminal file: Contains testimonies, magazine clippings, a map of the island of Mykonos, and a multitude of other documents...
Escape game mechanics: Solve puzzles to unlock 5 envelopes containing additional evidence.
Detective 2.0 : Internet access is required to access personal data and hack into a paparazzi's drive. Also, several levels of clues are available on the site so you don't get stuck.

Cruelle Fin De Carrière pour Christophe Cannette - Jeu de Société Escape Game - Jeu d'Enquête Immersif et Collaboratif

description:
A collaborative game made in France!
Two tourists are found dead in their room at the Gothic Tales, an allegedly haunted hotel in the small British village of Brandonsbury. Stir up the past to uncover the truth.

Features
    Comprehensive criminal case file
    Escape game mechanics
    A collaborative game made in France
Includes eyewitness accounts, forensic analysis, press articles, crime scene photos, audio content...

INCARNEZ UN DETECTIVE : Le Gothic Thales, hôtel tristement célèbre du village de Brandonsbury, est le théâtre d’un nouvel évènement mystérieux. Rétablissez la chronologie des événements, dressez la liste des suspects et enquêtez afin de faire la lumière sur cette affaire.
UN DOSSIER CRIMINEL COMPLET : Contient des témoignages, des exemplaires des journaux, un plan du village , et une multitude d’autres documents…
DES MÉCANIQUES D’ESCAPE GAME : Résolvez des énigmes pour déverrouiller 4 enveloppes contenant des preuves supplémentaires.

description:
Revengened Story is a battle royale board game in which three teams fight to conceal their true identities.

The setting is the world of a fairy tale. It is the story of Red Riding Hood, who was cut into pieces by a werewolf, and 10 years later takes revenge on the werewolf. Each of the 18 characters becomes a hero, and the game unfolds dramatically.

Simultaneous movement, map exploration, card combos, resource management, dice combat, and more. In addition, there is reasoning and gamesmanship with visualized search chips. The appeal of the game is that a total of 18 characters with two abilities create their own strategies and a different sense of play for each character.
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Little Red Riding Hood

description:
In Tantrum House Roll & Write, the numbers 2-12 are all assigned a representative game-related icon (3 = a meeple, 5 = Kevin Delp’s hat, 10 = a die, etc). Players will use the sun of two dice to place a matching icon in one of the six Tantrum House studios (3x3 grids). Scores are determined by the number of icons that match its studios icons.

This print ‘n play roll & write standalone game was part of the Tantrum House Season 10 Kickstarter campaign.

HOW TO PLAY:

     Active player rolls 4 dice and chooses 2 (gaining benefits if applicable)
     All other players use the result of non-chosen dice
     Icon can be drawn in any box
     Gain bonus icons immediately when a row or column contains 3 matching icons

Lights, camera, escape! Desperate to catch a glimpse of the hottest new action flick, you snuck into the Escape Pictures movie studio to watch the filming in progress. You'll have to make a dramatic exit though, because the brawny head of security has caught you lurking and your only hope of safety lies with a coded note. You'll have to think fast if you want to stay alive to watch the sequel!

There are twelve different puzzles and each solution will translate a single letter of the note. Only once all twelve puzzles have been solved will you be able to make your escape from the Movie studio.

boardgame: A War Transformed: WWI on the Doggerland Front
description: A War Transformed is a skirmish wargame set in a world where World War I was utterly changed by forces far beyond human comprehension. Players command small forces of infantry, cavalry, artillery, and other… stranger… troops on the Doggerland Front. Fast-paced gameplay and a tense initiative bidding system are combined with authentic folk traditions and occult philosophies of the era – it is a game of rifle and relic, of bayonet and belief, of machine gun and magic.

boardgame: Armored Knights: Guderian Crosses the Meuse
description:
The XIX Korps, under the command of Guderian, breaks through the Ardennes Forest and reaches the Meuse River at Sedan.  He decides to make a quick attack against the French forces there and gains a bridgehead.  French incompetent leadership and old battle doctrine lead to disaster and the Germans exploit the foothold into a major breakthrough.
The game is a major upgrade to the Armored Knights game system.  It brings a better combined arms feel to the game and lessens the math skills needed to compute combat factors.  Players are going to like the feel of this operational level combat game.

boardgame: Frontiers on Fire: 18th Century Skirmish Warfare in North America
description:
Frontiers on Fire is a man-to-man skirmish wargame for warfare in 18th Century North America, mostly the French-and-Indian War and the American War for Independence. 
Too many skirmish games are simple systems where dice take a greater role in the game than tactics, decisions, or careful use of fire and movement. 
Frontiers on Fire allows each player to control a half dozen or so men, but takes some of control away from the player through model/character reactions to the players’ plans and to the situation the characters face. 
Skirmish games often have difficulty making a playable and interesting game while depicting realistic ranges, movement distances, and time. 
Frontiers on Fire uses a short turn (the time to load a musket) and ranges experienced by those fighting in the Eighteenth Century, not parade ground and firing range optimums. Percentage dice for shooting means many subtle  factors can be taken into account, with a separate d10 for the effects of an effective shot. Hand-to-hand is by opposing d6, but even melee allows for tactical choices in the fight. 
Each turn, each model is secretly given an order chit such as fire, load, walk (and shoot if loaded or if pistol- or bow-armed), run, etc, although the particulars are defined only when the model activates in a randomized order with other models or groups of models. 
Leadership and group rules allow some co-ordination of actions and timing. 
Special events account for unusual occurrences and can both benefit a player, or interfere a good plan. 
The game system, as written, works for 54mm or 25/28mm figures, and 15mm models can be used if the ranges are adjusted. Frontiers on Fire’s author is a professional historian, specializing in the 18th Century, who also has decades of wargaming experience. 
The rules have been extensively play-tested by both veteran and novice ‘gamers. 
Frontiers on Fire provides realistic, challenging, playable, enjoyable, and even dramatic games.

Sequence of play:

     1) At the beginning of a turn, secretly choose an action chit for each character. Place the action chit face down on the character card or under their base.
     2) Roll 1d10 per force for activation sequence. Record results. Re-roll ties but stay within relative sequence to other forces with different rolls.
     3) Using the sequence just rolled, each force takes turn activating one character ‘on point’ (must be done before any group moves) OR any one group with a leader OR any stragglers (stragglers can only be activated after ALL group moves)


—description from the publisher
Gives Players a chance to randomize or customize their setup of Milestones & Awards. Rules include a complete index of all Milestones & Awards and explanations.

This supplement for the award-winning Bolt Action WWII wargame focuses on the Axis offensives through Eastern Ukraine and into the North Caucasus.

After the failure of Barbarossa to utterly defeat the Soviet Union, a new plan was devised, Case Blue. This plan involved pushing through the southern Soviet Union to reach the Caucasus and secure the oil fields that Germany so desperately needed. While initially there was great success and sweeping advances as the autumn began, the Axis advances began to falter in the wake of Soviet resistance and counter attacks, culminating the battles in and around Stalingrad.

This Campaign Book for Bolt Action contains new linked scenarios, rules, troop types, and Theatre Selectors, providing plenty of options for novice and veteran players alike.

—description from the publisher

The Galactic War was long ago fought between Humans and Vampires, fleets of star cruisers doing battle alongside demigods known as X-Knights.

Now you can go back in time and command the Human Cosmada or the Vampiric Starfleet, and be a general in the Time Wars.

Two players move stacks of cruisers or individual X-Knights across a board littered with star systems to conquer and gravity spaces which slingshot you across the board. Gather command cards to outdo your opponent in battles with three phases: Blast, to destroy; Drone, to repel; and Marines, to capture. Does your opponent have the cards to back up their move? Or are they bluffing?

When you control the central system, as well as the two star systems on your secret goal card, you win the game!

Comes with two-sided board for advanced scenarios.

—description from the designer

The Portable Wargame evolved over several years, and the first book about it was published in 2017. Several more have followed, and the rules have proven popular with wargamers who want to fight battles on a regular basis but who resources of time and space are limited.

Over time, several variants have been written, some of which have been published. The most recent innovation has been the development of the Fast Play 3 x 3 Portable Wargame (FP3x3PW for short), which uses a three-by-three gridded tabletop and a maximum of six units (plus a general) per side. Since this new version of the rules was launched in early 2022, it has sparked a lot of interest and has generated a rapid growth in new sets of period-specific rules.

The Portable Wargame Compendium has been compiled from contributions from members of Portable Wargaming community. The Compendium has been edited by Bob Cordery and includes contributions from David Barnes, Justin Barrett, Bob Cordery, Mark Cordone, Arthur Harman, Marc Pavone, Martin Rapier, and Mike Taber.

98 pages includes - Snakes and Ladders Campaign System and Wargame Rules for Original 3x3, Fantasy, 18th Century Horse & Musket, Colonial & Late 19th Century, WWII Eastern Front Infantry, WWII Operational, 1st Punic War, English Civil War, Little Wars, and Sci-Fi. Plus sections on armies, maneuvers, and terrain.

This expansion to Triqueta introduces a new type of animal: the friendly but shy wolves, who prefer to keep to the shadows. They step out from the shadows at the end of the game and are then worth a lot of points when forming a triqueta. However, since one of each other animal is also hiding in the shadows, collecting shadow animal tokens is never risk-free.
Hidden Wolves adds 15 shadow animal tokens to the base game. As a special bonus, it comes with an exciting 2-player variant that makes the gameplay at that player count extremely enjoyable.

—description from the publisher

The expansion is included in newer versions (e.g. from 2024) of the base game.

boardgame: Black Powder Epic Battles: American Civil War – Gettysburg Battle Set
description:
Fought over the first three days of July in 1863, the battle for Gettysburg proved to be the high tide of the Confederacy and the turning point of the American Civil War. The Confederate army of North Virginia, under General Lee, and fresh from victory over the Union at Chancellorsville, assaulted the Army of the Potomac under Union General Meade at the crossroads town of Gettysburg in the deadliest battle of the entire conflict. The battle ended following the near suicidal attack known as Pickett's Charge which led to the battered rebel army withdrawing from the field. Lee's invasion of the North has been halted.

This box set contains over 2,000 men. Each of the two armies are presented on colour-coded plastic sprues; the push-fit pieces are extremely easy to assemble - enabling you to quickly get to the business of gaming this fascinating era of history on the epic scale it deserves. You’ll also find some MDF scenery to lend your battles extra authenticity.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Shadow Storm: Fantasy Mass Battle Rule Set
description:
Shadow Storm - Mass Fantasy Battle is a fast and fun wargame rule set designed to produce a rich fantasy wargame.

All the ‘high’ fantasy archetypes are covered, and the rules contain sections on heroes, mages, magic, and monsters. Special unit rules create depth that helps define orcs, dwarfs, elves, and humans, amongst others. Spell and magic item lists enhance the fantasy depth of the rules.

Shadow Storm offers a unique movement, shooting and combat system, and the players can use either D10s or D6s to play the game.  
The game itself is easy to learn, but the tactical play it encourages means that it will take time and experience to master.

The rules cater for all figure scales, and players can use small or large forces to play the game. 
You can play a game of Shadow Storm - Mass Fantasy Battle in many ways, be that from a narrative perspective or a matched combat tournament game.

Turn Sequence:

     Check Command Ranges
     Movement - Move Unit
     Rallying - Rally Units
     Magic - Cast Magic
     Shooting - Shoot Units
     Combat - Fight Combat


Once a player has completed all the steps with one command, they hand the initiative over to the other player. That player then selects a command and repeats the process.

—description from the publisher

Boss Up Everything is a game based around the mechanics of the game Rock, Paper, Scissors. In this game you move around 15 cards that are placed in a row in the middle of the play area, trying to collect them. But when you move onto a card or start your turn on a card where an opponent stands, than you can challenge them to a Rock, Paper, Scissors duel. The winner may move the loser(s) exactly 2 cards away from the card the battle has taken place.

—description from the designer
