description:
Devouring Teeth Lurk Underfoot likes being in the same lands as Invaders, so it can use Range 0 offensive and defensive Powers. The first of its Innate Power can give some mobility, if needed.
Has a poor Plays track and potent but expensive Unique Powers, so can be better at handling fewer large threats than lots of little ones.

This is a low-complexity spirit. It’s the same spirit as is included in Horizons of Spirit Island; in Germany, it was published on its own.

boardgame: Spirit Island: Wachende Augen in Bäumen
description:
Eyes Watch From The Trees is good at Defending against Ravages, and at steadily earning Fear.
Its ability to Gather Dahan to fight back when Defending can make a huge difference, changing a stalling tactic into a blow against the Invaders.

This is a low-complexity spirit. It’s the same spirit as is included in Horizons of Spirit Island; in Germany, it was published on its own.

boardgame: Spirit Island: Bodenloser Schlamm der Sümpfe
description:
Fathomless Mud Of The Swamp likes having Sacred Sites where Invaders will Build, but may need to re-create those after oozing outwards with its Innate Power. In smaller games, might be able to cut off the most Inland lands from Explore actions by Destroying Inland Towns/Cities and stopping new ones from being built.
Causes a fair bit of Fear, much of which represents unpleasantness, hardship, and disgust.

This is a low-complexity spirit. It’s the same spirit as is included in Horizons of Spirit Island; in Germany, it was published on its own.
family: Theme: Wetlands / Swamps / Bogs / Fens / Marshland

description:
You command a fleet of pirate ships roaming the Caribbean, looking for treasure and trouble. Rival buccaneers, however, challenge your dominance of the high seas. Use your navigation skills and firepower to send your enemy to the briny depths. Or, find a hidden trove of loot before they do.

In Cutthroat Cannons, you’ll constantly be forced to choose—offense or defense? Use your lookout to hunt them down? Draw cards for special attacks and defenses? Raid the navy to build a supply of handy Doubloons? Or do you need to move or repair your ships? And all the while you’ll need to avoid piratey perils like scurvy, Voodoo curses, a mutinous crew, and a very releasable kraken.

Rouse your crew—it’s time to prove your mettle!

boardgame: Spirit Island: Zunehmende Hitze in Stein und Sand
description:
Rising Heat Of Stone And Sand‘s play stile is Weaken-and-Destroy. Likes having Sacred Sites where there're Towns/Cities, as it makes all Spirits' Damage more effective there.
Has an easier time setting up Sacred Sites in Sands and Mountains, but can do so in any terrain with a bit more time.

This is a low-complexity spirit. It’s the same spirit as is included in Horizons of Spirit Island; in Germany, it was published on its own.

Sun-Bright Whirlwind is good at handling Explorers, clearing newly-Explored lands of Invaders so they don't Build there. Not nearly so good at dealing with Towns/Cities.
Can focus on Energy and largely forego its Innate Power, focus on Plays to aim for mid-to-high Innate thresholds, or strike a more balanced path. Adds at most 1 Presence per turn, so there won't be time to do it all.

This is a low-complexity spirit. It’s the same spirit as is included in Horizons of Spirit Island; in Germany, it was published on its own.

AKA The Original Six-Shooter Challenge Game

Chupito! is a party game for 3-12 players that takes less than a minute to learn, less than an hour to play, and is endlessly repeatable. Players draw challenge cards, wager bullets against one another, and try not to get shot!

Each player starts with an equal number of bullet cards and 3 bomb cards (these are single-use and can affect the outcome of a round). On their go, a player enters a bullet card and draws a challenge card from the pile, which they read aloud. Other players can challenge them by entering their own bullet cards.

The challenge is then played out and the winner gets to fire all the bullets at the losers by flipping the cards over and dishing them out. The player with the least bullet holes at the end of the game is the winner!

—description from the publisher

Android Farmer, Electric Sheep is a roll-and-write game for two players. It is designed to be interactive with take-that actions and plenty of hate-drafting.

The future is now and you’re a farmer that is obviously an Android. You’re working to program all the wonderful Electric Sheep that your android brothers and sisters need in order to dream.

You and your android-nemesis will be programming sheep into a data store that has limited space. If you’ve filled a partition in your data store and attempt to program a sheep into it then you’ll experience Buffer Overflow. Too much of that and the game will end, and you’ll have plenty of negative points to show for your efforts. Additionally, the game can come to an end once a data store has been fully filled (which happens through proper planning, of course).

It's not as simple as placing sheep into the data store, plenty of actions like Hacking, Code Injection, and ICE Breaking will get in your way and make things a little more difficult.

Whichever android has made the best decisions and taken the most timely actions will end up with the most points and be deemed the winner.

Adding a purple color faction to the game. Available only as a kick starter exclusive to complete pledge backers.

It contains 52 purple cards. These cards are used interchangeably with blue. Expanded versions are put in interchangeable with other colors. The purple expansion is intended to create a small elite force and is filled with cards that gain significant benefits by excluding cards. The purple expansion is a great alternative to blue, which is the core of many decks, and will change the whole flavor of the game.

Dice Race is a dice game where you have to match your cards against the rolled dice as fast as possible.

Shuffle the double-sided cards and deal all of them to the players. A player rolls the ten dice (all with different colors distributed between them). Now it is up to each player to match their cards (consisting of eleven suits) against the suits shown by the dice as quickly as possible. You play a card and take matching dice and place them on the card. After all the dice have been collected, they are rolled again and a new round starts.

The game ends when one player plays their last card from their hand or when a dice is not picked, because none of the players has a card with that colour on it. The winner is the player who played the last card or the player with most collected cards if the game ends because no one could take any dice.

boardgame: Around the World with Black Emanuelle
description: Revisit the sensual world of Emanuelle. She was the pleasure-seeking photojournalist – forever embodied by the Indonesian actress Laura Gemser, most frequently in collaboration with writer/director Joe D’Amato – who would uncover an increasingly lurid world of sex cults, snuff films, cannibal jungles, women’s prisons, white slavers, depraved convents and beyond to become the premier feminist icon in genre history. Every spot on the board is location or scene from an iconic Emanuelle film. Can you make it home when so many adventures await?

Barbro is the game that reveals what you and your girlfriends really think. The game where you have to stand up for your opinions - and secrets. Barbro sets out to find out what type of woman you are: Bimbobabe, Bullmamma, Ninjafeminist, Viktigpettra or Dräktdrake?
It's about making moral decisions, performing embarrassing tasks and showing your knowledge of women. With Barbro, the truth comes out. Barbro a game just for women!

The game Barbro is played in two phases. In the first phase, is about to collect character tokens that will determine what type of woman you are. Character tokens are won by answering questions correctly or completing missions. After a jointly determined number of chips before the start is reached, you enter the second phase, where you have to correctly answer questions based on the character tokens collected during the first phase. Whoever succeeds in flipping their character tokens first wins and is named the evening's Barbro.

The game's five female types:
Viktigpettra - "It is cowardly not to explain to others how things are"
Bimbobabe - "With a little femininity and a push-up bra you can go a long way"
Bullmamma - "Hot milk chocolate and mangled sheets help with most things"
Ninjafeminist - "Raise your voice and refuse to shave your armpits"
Dräktdrake - "Life is a business idea that should be profitable"

The goal of the game is to be the first player to complete one's quest...without dying! To complete a quest, one must accumulate a certain number of road cards. Not an easy task as your adversaries will delect in throwing the most violent obstacles your way in order to slow you down or annihilate you. Good adventuring!

This is a card game published using a book format (135 cards and a book).

boardgame: Mafia: Devenez le parrain de la ville
description:
You are a member of one of the 5 largest Sicilian families. Your name inspires respect and fear. The community ranks you among the favourites to take over from the great Godfather. You're so close, all you have to do is prove that you're up to this huge responsibility.

MAFIA literally plunges you into this world of mafiosi, where the big families compete for the ultimate title of "Godfather". Designed for 2 to 5 players, MAFIA is a family strategy game. Adults will quickly appreciate its simplicity and strategic aspect, while younger players will love the twists and turns, the many possible interactions and the game's theme. Your aim is to take control of the city. To achieve this, you'll need lots of henchmen, plenty of cash, a few sticks of dynamite, some well-placed dirty cops, good investments and, above all, luck and determination. Whoever has accumulated the most cash at the end of the game will win the coveted title of "Godfather of the Mafia".

Cover Me! is a game of individual combat between single infantry squads, their support weapons, and the occasional armored fighting vehicle. 
Each player controls a handful of soldiers. 
Infantry for all major combatants of WW2 are included, along with many iconic vehicles.


     Works with any scale using your existing miniatures
     No basing requirements as long as figures are individually based
     Prioritizes player agency in matters of move and morale: you get to decide when it's time for one of your soldiers to take cover and restore your combat effectiveness
     Ammo is a finite resource but you don't have to count every bullet
     Leaders have concrete effects on morale and motivation
     Figures can be assigned unique traits or skills
     Weapons are realistically modeled and playable, including rifle grenades, mortars, antitank weapons, satchel charges, mines, and flamethrowers, as well as firearms and grenades
     Terrain elements are clearly defined and easy to agree upon
     No table clutter; each soldier's status and ammo is tracked on a small card
     Rules for hidden movement, campaign play, and solo/co-op play are included
     Standard OOB or point buy options for choosing your squad members
     Includes German, Italian, Japanese, Soviet, UK, and US infantry squads and many vehicles
     copious diagrams that explain core concepts in visual format
     concierge-level, prompt support from the author and a vibrant online Facebook community
     Separate printables PDF included with base labels, counters, figure cards, vehicle cards, and ability cards


—description from the publisher

Soccard is a card-based strategy game that simulates a soccer match. Players will take the role of a coach and use probabilities to their advantage to score goals and defeat their opponent. May the best player win!

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
In Sano jo se Sana, players compete in teams, trying to reach the end of the scoreboard before other teams. On their turn, a team picks up a lettercard, nominating the letter of the turn and then they start asking question cards as fast as they can.

But in order to ask the question, they must fill the sentence with a proper keyword, starting with the given letter. Each question correctly asked and answered, scores a point on the scoretrack.

There's no wrong answer, but the question is bit trickier.

boardgame: The Forbidden Corner Official Board Game
description:
The Forbidden Corner Official Board Game is a magical way of bringing the weird and whimsical charm of the park to your family tabletop. Journey down the twisting pathways of a fantastic land, outwitting your opponents and racing to be the first to gather the elusive and precious gems.

Use a variety of enchanted tricks, tools and trinkets to best the fiendish creatures that will surprise you on your way- is it trouble or treasure that lies around the corner?

—description from the publisher

Story of Many is a cooperative fantasy adventure board game for 1-4 players about exploring a vast land, combating deadly foes, and delving into mysterious adventure sites. With over 400,000 words of adventure text, and limitless possibilities for creating and advancing your own character, brave a world filled with profound lore and hard choices. Story of Many emphasizes exploration, storytelling and challenging combat, making it an engaging experience for all board game and table-top RPG fans.

Open-world exploration is a core element of Story of Many designed to capture the sense of freedom and discovery. The world map represents the expansive and interconnected regions of Varda - each with its unique landscape, challenges, and opportunities. These regions include dense rainforests, uncharted islands, desolate ruins, sprawling eldritch cities, and treacherous dungeons. As players progress, they will uncover the game's intricate lore, and influence the unfolding story. Their choices will lead to different branching paths and outcomes.

Combat is fast, unforgiving, and delivers a fresh experience every time, as the players will pit against more than 200 unique enemies. Combat is resolved using dice and miniatures. By defeating enemies and exploring, characters gain experience to develop their abilities. Character development is achieved through a massive, interconnected web of nodes encompassing passive traits, abilities, spells, trigger actions, setting it apart from any other game of the genre. No fighter will ever be the same!

CREATE YOUR HERO. EXPLORE THE LAND. CHOOSE WISELY.

—description from the designer

An abstract game given away by the Ducosim game club, Game card #0032, given away to attendees of their conventions.

Try to create the largest globule of your color by placing markets given to you by your opponent.

Game is a single thick paper sheet with pieces that must be cut out from the card.

This ultimate game block is the Christmas HANGMAN edition and offers 160 cheerful and funny terms for lightning-fast guessing fun with the whole family. The aim of the game is to guess the term before the hangman is painted out. Fun guaranteed!

Game flow:
The HANGMAN says a letter and crosses it off the alphabet. The LETTER MASTER now says whether the letter is contained in the solution word and reveals the corresponding position in the solution word. The HANGMAN now enters the letter in the correct position. If the letter does not appear in the word, the HANGMAN must color in a body part. The game is over as soon as the HANGMAN has guessed the solution word correctly or all body parts have been colored in. Should the HANGMAN name a wrong solution word, a body part must be painted out as a penalty.

Dieser ultimative Spielblock ist die weihnachtliche HANGMAN Edition und bietet 160 fröhliche und lustige Begriffe für blitzschnellen Ratespaß mit der ganzen Familie. Ziel des Spiels ist es den Begriff zu erraten, bevor das Galgenmännchen ausgemalt ist. Spaß garantiert!

Spielablauf:
Der HANGMAN sagt einen Buchstaben und streicht diesen vom Alphabet weg. Der BUCHSTABENMEISTER sagt nun ob der Buchstabe in dem Lösungswort enthalten ist und verrät die entsprechende Position im Lösungswort. Der HANGMAN trägt nun den Buchstaben an der richtigen Position ein. Kommt der Buchstabe im Wort nicht vor, muss der HANGMAN ein Körperteil ausmalen. Das Spiel ist vorbei, sobald der HANGMAN das Lösungswort richtig erraten hat oder alle Körperteile ausgemalt sind. Sollte der HANGMAN ein falsches Lösungswort nennen, muss ein Körperteil als Strafe ausgemalt werden.

To win the game, the player who plays after you must no longer have any cards in hand.
During each turn, each player must play one card. As soon as a player has emptied his hand, they are 'DELETED' and the game ends, offering victory to the player before them in the current direction of play.

boardgame: Grave Robbers From Outer Space: Director's Cut!
description:
Resurrected from the dead after 15 years out of print, in Grave Robbers From Outer Space: Director's Cut!, you as a B-Movie director build up your own film with Location, Character, and Prop, and Special Effect cards while defending against Hollywood Creatures.

Differing from the original game, rather than using a single communal deck to draw from, GROS now focuses upon players building their own custom movie decks, CCG style, to play head-to-head. The initial release is made up of ten 16 card Feature Packs, each pack based upon a movie genre — new players and seasoned players alike can simply shuffle two or three together and play. However, those who are savvy can build their own decks from scratch.

Popcorn, the resource system introduced in the Resurrected Edition, has been retained, balanced, and expanded upon. In order to play cards, you need to meet their Popcorn costs by "tipping" cards already played in your movie with a popcorn bucket icon, or discarding cards from your hand.

Finally, Roll the Credits cards - where they still exist for Classic play - have been replaced with the new game-ending mechanic: Plot Twists — secret goals kept face-down on the table that trigger from in-game actions. Bank a Plot Twist to lock in points. Bank the requisite number of Plot Twists and the final scores are tallied.

—description from the publisher
publishers: The Academy of Resurrected Games and Holdings (ARGH)

description:
A doorway opens to another realm, where the greatest inter-dimensional warriors battle to rule the multiverse! I AM CHAMPION!

With your hand selected team of radical warriors you and your opponent go head to head in this epic card game of great magnitude Each card is equipped with a ability determined by a dice roll adding a bit of chance to your outcome.

Warriors cards can be collected with your friends, family or coworkers, battle your neighbors, strangers on the street, the mail man, collect better cards a move up the tiers to be the greatest in the cosmos! The cooler the card, the more rare it is.

Cards can be as rare as one of a kind.

TrapMove is an exciting strategy board game designed for 2 to 4 players, where skill, strategy, and a dash of luck determine the winner. The game includes a 10x10 game board, 100 chips, player game pieces, a die, a drawstring bag, and an instruction sheet. The objective is to outmaneuver your opponents while trapping them and becoming the last player with available moves.

Players start by rolling the die to determine the first player. The game proceeds clockwise, with each player strategically placing their game piece on the board and then selecting one of three chips from the bag to determine their move. The game revolves around five different chip moves, each with its own rules and restrictions. These chip moves dictate how players can move their game pieces on the board, whether it's a simple one-space move, a complex diagonal maneuver, or the ability to jump over obstacles.

The goal is to strategically use your chips to outwit your opponents, block their movements, and avoid getting trapped yourself. Once a player can't make any more moves, they're eliminated from the game. The last player remaining with available moves emerges as the winner of TrapMove. With its combination of strategy, skill, and a touch of unpredictability, TrapMove promises an engaging and fun gaming experience for friends and family alike.

—description from the publisher

A card game to be played with anyone that wants to know what everyone thinks about things without letting anyone else know what they think.
The base game consists of two types of cards, questions and yes/no cards. The questions are always formatted so that they have to be answered with Yes or No. I think you can see where this is going. One person draws a question and asks the group, the group then answers with the Yes/No cards in their hands without revealing how they voted. The person that drew the question guesses what the group answered, then counts the results. If they get it right they keep the question as a point.
In the advanced version there are Boosts and Drops which are used as rewards and punishments.

—description from the publisher

A cooperative game for children in which the players have to guide Little Red Riding Hood faster to grannys house than the wolf can appear there. Once the house is reached the game is over.

In turn order every player rolls the die and performs an action according to the result. One either moves Little Red Riding Hood or the wolf or granny one further step. An additional action if a green color is rolled is to turn over a tree piece and perform the corresponding action such as moving the wolf one step further or to reposition Little Red Riding Hood.

One interesting fact of the game is that it is not a simple race as it is affected by some random effects in the game: The wolf can only enter the house if granny arrived already there. Also Little Red Riding Hood can skip a few steps or repositioned with a special tree piece.

There exists also a competitive variant for two players if each player plays a character.

—user summary
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Little Red Riding Hood

description:
It is late in the 19th century. Slowly but surely Americans have been expanding westward across the continent. Spurred on by promises of fortune, freedom and territory Americans from all walks of life set out for the untamed lands west of the Mississippi River. This rapid expansion brings with it camps, towns and cities, some popping up overnight and disappearing just as quickly. Vast railway networks are built to connect the ever-expanding country. The vast swathes of land prove difficult to control; attracting criminals, runaways and renegade Indians. The expansion westward is accompanied by law and disorder as state and federal lawmen take up the badge to hunt down criminals.

Crime and murder rates are high. Amidst this lawlessness the states earn their nickname: "The Wild West." Justice is meted out with sixgun, shotgun, and hangin’ rope. In the turmoil some lawmen turn outlaw, and some outlaws turn lawmen. Loyalty to anything or anyone is fleeting and life is cheap. Some outlaws and lawmen become legendary heroes and villains in United States folklore.

In Shoot N' Skedaddle players will take control of outlaws and lawmen, battling for supremacy amongst the cow towns, railheads and mining camps of the Old West. Will you blaze a trail of crime and mayhem? Will you take up the tin star
and uphold the law?

—description from the publisher

This is a web-published variant of the Empire Builder system by Mayfair Games that is set in Austria-Hungary.  The geography includes a lot of rivers and a large stretch of Alpine Mountains.  There is an event for "Assassination of the Heir Apparent" that can lead to the game ending before the usual 250 million kronen and all but one major city connected.
This is a web-published variant of the Empire Builder system by Mayfair Games that is set in France.  The map is wide open with plenty of room to build and plays up to six.
This is a web-published variant of the Empire Builder system by Mayfair Games that is set in Italy.  The geography is somewhat limited due to the narrowness of the peninsula, similar to Nippon Rails.

Drug use, personal demons, wild episodes, and more. Side Effects is a "take that" card game where you race to treat all the diseases in your psyche with drugs and therapy.

Expand the pain and the pleasure with Side Effects' first expansion pack. Enjoy new Disorders, Drugs, and actions that add additional functionality and exciting new dynamics!

Hands Full is endless, tangled fun for the whole family! The challenge is to keep your hands full while you jump, high five, shake, dance, twist and more! Hang on tight—don't let go, or you lose! Master your moves in Solo Mode, play together to beat a twisted challenge in Team Mode, or get competitive as you play to be the last one standing in Survivor Mode! With 3 games in one, 15 fun moves and over one million crazy combinations this interactive family game always gets out of hand!

3 Games In 1 

     Solo Mode: Level up as you master the moves. Complete all 6 levels to become a Hands Full master. 
     1 Player Survivor Mode: Compete to be the last player standing. Don't let go or you’re out.
     2-4 Players Team Mode: Complete a sequence of crazy twisted moves. If anyone lets go – it’s game over for everyone.


—description from the publisher

Vitreum is roll and write print at home game for unlimited number of players. You and your fellow players become stained glass glaziers. Your job is to create the most beautiful piece of art from glass.
During the game you will try to create the best stained glass for your benefactor. He will give you twelve guidelines, upon which he will judge you. It's up to you, how well you will follow these guidelines. Good luck!

—description from the publisher

In the old West, a lot of rumors travel far; voices of epic adventures and stories that are recounted at sunset. They tell of heroes, crooks, and lost places in the desert.

BANG! Legends is an expansion that lets you play as a “Legendary” version of the classic BANG! characters and discover new lethal abilities. But be careful: Only by completing amazing feats (or by boasting about them!) can you enter into Legend!

Take on legendary versions of the classic characters from BANG! Perform heroic deeds—or brag about them—to gain fame and enhance your abilities.

This expansion introduces 16 legendary characters (to swap with the characters in the base game), 16 Feat cards, and 35 wooden Fame tokens in 7 different shapes and colors.

“This is the West, sir. When the legend becomes fact, print the legend.” —(M. Scott, “The man who shot Liberty Valance”)

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Draw ten pictures in just one minute but can anyone guess what you drew? Played over three rounds this contains 108 brand new category cards, 8 pencils and a pad of drawing paper.

Category cards come in three difficulties - easy, tricky and almost impossible - and  they may be combined with those from the original Six Second Scribbles.

In Snylt, the players roll dice to move their pieces around the board. At the start of the games, the players decide how many rounds you need to complete in order to win.

On you turn you roll one die at a time, hoping to get a sum as close to, but not over, 7. If you stop rolling dice while your sum is not over 7, you move the sum multiplied by the number of dice. If your sum gets over 7, you go back to the start space.

If your piece lands on top of another piece, you take control of their next move, and in that move they will bring you along with them (you become a parasite (which is the origin of the name (in Danish, "snylter" means a parasite, and "at snylte" means to parasite))). If they bring you all the way around the board, you will also get the point for it instead of them.

As mentioned, first player to reach the agreed-upon number of points wins.
200 all new questions for Herd Mentality. Be the first player to collect eight cows but be warned that if you've got the pink cow the winner can't be you!

Anything and everything can and should be collected.

In the Booster Brat TCG, you will use a carefully curated deck of cards to steal your rival's collection and build up you own. Play as either Spark or Captain, two brothers beginning their journey into the cutthroat world of Card Collecting.

Each 2-Player set includes:
(1) 20 Card Spark Deck (with orientation cards to get you started)
(1) 20 Card Captain Deck (with orientation cards)
(1) 14 Card Bonus Pack - Includes intermediate level cards, 1 Holo Promo and 3 Randomly Inserted Holos (From a Pool of 12)
(2) Build Your Own Playmat Packs
(1) Full color rulebook

Print Run of 500 Starter Sets.
This lot is for 10 2-Player Sets.

Necromunda. This hive world was founded over 15,000 years ago, as a mining and manufacturing colony. In the millennia since, the planet's mountains have been reduced to rubble for the ore within, its seas turned into oceans of chemical sludge, its very air poisoned with radioactive filth. Teeming billions infest its vast hive cities, from the glittering spires to the bowels of the underhive, a maddening mass of humanity that lives, works, fights, and dies without ever seeing the light of day. In the world beyond, outlaw gangs and nomadic tribes war endlessly over trade routes across the irradiated ash wastes.

Life is cheap. The bullet is king. And in the shadows of the hive, only the strongest endure...

This rulebook is the definitive guide to the game of Necromunda – the tabletop skirmish wargame of anarchy and violence, where players control rival gangs battling for supremacy. It contains the full and updated core rules of the game, including new additions like vehicles, founding gangs, and ash wastes weather, along with campaign rules and scenarios. The book also offers an in-depth look at the setting of Necromunda through background lore and stunning illustrations. It's an essential resource for anyone who wants to enter the brutal world of inter-gang warfare!

This 336-page hardback book contains:


     Lore of Necromunda: Learn about the locales and locals of this nightmare world, from the choking underhive depths to the toxic expanse of the ash wastes. Includes a look at each of the major gang factions, maps of Hive Primus and the planet's western hemisphere, and a detailed timeline stretching back to the Dark Age of Technology.



     Core Rules: Updated and revised core rules for the game of Necromunda. You'll learn the basic principles of gang warfare and fighter characteristics, how to play out a full battle of rounds and phases, and advanced rules for elements like psykers or vehicular combat. This book incorporates updates from a variety of supplements and FAQs, collating all the core rules you need in one place.



     Founding a Gang: Rules for creating, equipping, expanding, and updating a gang of your own – including earning experience or injuries, purchasing wargear and Advancements for individual gang members, hiring mercenaries and extra muscle, and creating Skirmish gangs for one-off battles.



     Dominion Campaign: A comprehensive campaign system, allowing you to advance your gangs and fight over valuable territories – including victory conditions, rules for income and challenges, and 26 unique territories to seize control of, each with their own distinct benefits.



     Battlefields & Scenarios: Learn how to set up your battlefields in the underhive or ash wastes, discover special rules that cover everything from loot caskets and booby traps to radioactive desert storms, and fight it out in 11 different scenarios where victory means wealth and glory for your gang.



     Arbitrator’s Toolkit: An expanded Arbitrator’s Toolkit, with rules to help you shape the perfect campaign and story – including multiple campaign variants, optional special rules, new House Favour rules for rewarding Underdogs, and guidelines on using gang tactics.



     Gang Tactics & Skills: A complete set of core Gang Tactics and general Skills, available for any and all gangs to use as they play out battles and develop new abilities.



     The Trading Post: The complete Trading Post, with rules for buying, selling, and using a huge variety of weapons, armour, wargear, bionics, accessories, chems, companions, and more – including a full list of weapon traits. The Trading Post also incorporates items that were previously available only on the Black Market.


—description from the publisher

A trivia game where the players will answer with both a number and a tolerance interval (e.g. 42 +/-10).
The player(s) with the smallest correct interval wins the round and get(s) one point. 
When a player hits spot on with a correct answer and an interval of +/-0, they get a bonus of two points.
The winner is the first to get to five points.
All answers in the game are rounded to the nearest integer.

Sydamerikas Hemligheter is an exciting and unique roll & write game where you embark on a journey throughout South America to discover clues leading to valuable missing objects. In the frantic pursuit, you traverse the terrain using a jeep, boat, and airplane, requiring strategic planning of your route for the best possible outcome. The player who first locates the missing objects and reaches their destination city wins the game!

Free Y is a two-player game that is played on an initially empty hexagonal grid with an odd total number of boundary cells, preferably outlining a near-regular hexagon with odd sides one cell longer than even sides.

On your turn, place a stone of your color on an empty cell. You win if there is a group (set of connected stones) of your color such that any region (set of connected cells) outside that group includes less than half of the boundary cells.

Bill Taylor posted Free Y in 2008 on the rec.games.abstract newsgroup.

boardgame: Stadt Land Vollpfosten: Das Kartenspiel – Christmas Edition
description:
Gameplay

Each player is randomly given 8 letter cards. Now each player must place a word matching one of his letter cards as quickly as possible. Important: The first letter of the word counts and you do NOT have to form a term from the letter cards, as is sometimes the case in other games, e.g. Scrabble.

So it gets exciting: The 60 category cards contain a total of 200 different and funny categories:

Who can put down a "profession" first? Or a "reason for separation"? What about a "city abroad"? Can you quickly think of a "Famous Dead Person" or a "Famous Actor" for your letter cards?

Important now: The FASTEST player wins each round and at the end the player who first got rid of all letter cards wins the game. The 10 additional action cards make the game especially exciting.

Of course, there are also FULL FIX cards in the game that either act as jokers or are used as tricky counters.

Das offizielle Kartenspiel von STADT LAND VOLLPFOSTEN mit 120 Spielkarten für ein unvergessliches Spielerlebnis unter Freunden.
150 kreative und lustige Kategorien bringen jeden Hobbydenker ins Schwitzen. Spaß Garantiert!

Spielablauf:

Jeder Spieler bekommt per Zufallsprinzip 8 Buchstaben-Karten ausgehändigt. Nun muss jeder Spieler möglichst schnell einen Begriff passend zu einer seiner Buchstaben-Karten ablegen. Wichtig: Es zählt jeweils der Anfangsbuchstabe des Wortes und man muss NICHT aus den Buchstaben-Karten einen Begriff bilden, wie es in anderen Spielen z.B: Scrabble manchmal der Fall ist.

Es wird also spannend: Die 60 Kategorie-Karten enthalten insgesamt 200 verschiedene und lustige Kategorien:

Wer kann zuerst einen “Beruf” ablegen? Oder einen “Trennungsgrund”? Was ist mit einer “Stadt im Ausland”? Fällt dir zu deinen Buchstaben-Karten schnell ein “Berühmter Toter” ein bzw. kommt Dir sofort ein “bekannter Schauspieler” in den Sinn?

Wichtig nun: Der SCHNELLSTE Spieler gewinnt jeweils die Runde und am Ende gewinnt der Spieler das Spiel, der zuerst alle Buchstaben-Karten losgeworden ist. Durch die 10 zusätzlichen Aktionskarten wird das Spiel besonders spannend.

Natürlich gibt es auch VOLLPFOSTEN-Karten im Spiel die entweder als Joker wirken oder als kniffliger Konter genutzt werden.

The official card game of STADT LAND VOLLPFOSTEN with 120 playing cards for an unforgettable gaming experience among friends.
150 creative and fun categories will make any amateur thinker sweat. Fun guaranteed!

In the distant land of Terraflip, the ruler has died. The four main families of the land do not wait long before claiming themselves as the new ruler. Will you be the next ruler of Terraflip ?

Terraflip is a competitive, tactical, tile placement and memory board game for up to 4 players set in a medieval land. In this eco-friendly board game you place and flip tiles to earn points. Each player has also access to basic and three special tiles. Once all players have played their tiles the player with the most points wins the game and becomes the next ruler of Terraflip.

Every component of the game, including the box, is entirely made out of engineered wood. It has been deliberately made without the use of plastic.

—description from the publisher

Now you can make the news. Make UP the news, that is. Just like the real tabloids do.

Everyone creates a headline . . . crazy, sinister, or all too true. An instant judging round lets everybody vote on the best headline. And it's on to the next round!

This fast-moving game for three or more players includes 320 cards and easy rules.

—description from the publisher

An immoral, grotesque and mannerless party game.
A game for adults that takes advantage of the multiple meanings of common expressions to generate hilarious situations. Whoever quickly guesses the real meaning of these expressions wins the game. The biggest difficulty? Stop laughing. How can the Portuguese language be so treacherous...

Anonymous is a deduction game, where players are hackers ready to launch their cyber attacks. The objective is to discover the identity, target and location of your opponent's cyber attack without getting caught! Each round lasts approximately 20 minutes.
Don't get caught. Stay anonymous!
Draw cards to add or multiply points and test probability. Surprise cards send scores forward, backward, or back to zero. The first to 500, without pulling a boom card, wins.

Ruleset designed to play small-scale skirmish-style games that are both simple and engaging, thus allowing younger gamers (5-7yrs old) to have fun with friends and / or family.

In Basic Battles 2.0 there are 3 phases to each player turn:

     The Movement Phase
     The Shooting Phase (if your figurines have ranged attacks)
     The Combat Phase

Nebel über Carcassonne: Die Bannkreise ("The Banishing Circles") is a small expansion for Mists over Carcassonne that introduces cats to your ghost-hunting team...although cats can sometimes be less helpful than you might think.

As with Mists over Carcassonne, the tiles in Die Bannkreise can be used in a regular competitive game of Carcassonne.

Roll the die and gather ingredients to make pizzas. The player who first makes pizzas worth 21 Neapolitan lire wins the game! A push your luck game for the whole family that takes about 15 minutes to play. And, of course, it comes in a pizza box!

It's time for kick-off, the referee blows the whistle, and the match is underway. The stadium is buzzing in the sunshine, the stands are packed, and the crowd roars in ecstasy. Your passes are precise, and your dribbling is on point - everything seems to be going as planned.

But then it happens - you stumble over your shoelace and end up being dribbled on the bleachers. Suddenly, the opponent advances on the field while you're still down, biting the grass!

Anything can happen in Dice Dribble - the dice game for those who love soccer! Dice Dribble is a Yahtzee-like dice game for all soccer enthusiasts. Attack and defend using the various symbols on the dice. The first to score three goals wins the match!

COMBO is a battle Card Game where you and your opponents have to use element cards to attack each other. When a player is reduced to less than 0 Energy Points that player enter a Ghost State. The player or team that can reduce all their opponents to Ghost State and be the last one standing will be declared the winner.

—description from the designer

A mini-expansion for Carcassonne inspired by the city of Oudenaarde, Belgium. Contains 9 tiles with drawings of famous breweries from the city of Oudenaarde.

There are 5 breweries in the city of Oudenaarde. When you place a tile with a Brewery, you can place your minion in two ways:

     On the same tile as in the normal game: as Thief, Knight or Peasant.
     Your follower becomes a Brewer and delivers beer to a nearby Town. Place your follower in a connected City. The condition is that that City is accessible via a Road that connects to the tile you just placed. The Road can connect to the Brewery tile itself or the Road can be on a connecting terrain tile, this is allowed even if there are already other meeples in the city.

Footboard is a football strategy game for two players.

It is designed to replicate the experience of playing as an entire team in real-time, with its closest equivalent being abstract strategy games such as chess.

There are no playing cards or extraneous assets, just two dice (each) and your footballing brain!

—description from the publisher

In this fast-paced archery tournament, you try to land the perfect hit yourself while trying to prevent your teammate from doing so. Whoever optimally combines the card value with the dice points will achieve the highest number of rings after 6 shots and thus win this speed bow tournament.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Enquêtes Express: On a Volé La Machine A Remonter Le Temps
description:
On a Volé La Machine A Remonter Le Temps is a mini stand alone investigation card deck for Enquêtes Express.
It was available from Plato magazine 152 as a promotional freebie.

Enquêtes Express is a cooperative investigation game that is accessible, fast and fun! During an investigation, each player is only allowed to consult a part of the clues. It is only by sharing and cross-checking their information that the players can unravel the case together.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Enquêtes Express est un jeu d'investigation coopératif à la fois accessible, rapide et fun ! Lors d'une enquête, chaque joueur n’est autorisé à consulter qu’une partie des indices. C’est donc uniquement en partageant et en recoupant toutes leurs infos que les joueurs peuvent ensemble démêler l’intrigue.

—description from the publisher

Agatha and Larry have reached Bowden, a small town in rural Edinburgh, in a timely manner. Grandfather Godfrey appears distraught when he accompanies them to the shed of Mystery Balloon Ltd, his hot air balloon company. Everything was ready for the annual Bristol Festival Regatta, England's premier competition...but the balloons prepared for the event have been ripped apart! It's not just a puzzle worthy of the best detectives, it's personal!

In collaboration with Atlantyca Entertainment.

—description from the publisher

Far Far Out is a space exploration game where you can win in three different ways.

You will discover planets, asteroids and star systems. Your spaceship and your crewmembers go on a Cosmic Odyssey where no one has ever gone before. Encounter strange lifeforms on planets, dig for items on asteroids or find quests that help you achieve your goal in this competitive galactic journey where you might help or frustrate other players in your hunt for Reputation, Wealth and Exploration.

The goal of the game is to have either 12 Victory Points on 1 track, or 6 Victory Points on each of the 3 tracks (total of 18).

description from the designer

The King has disappeared. When the Othersiders, our sworn enemy, obtained this knowledge, their attacks became more and more frequent. We must not let them take our lands for they will destroy us. It has been decided that each of the three Realms of our Kingdom must present their champion who will unite us under their banner. This is where you come in. Prove that you will be a better ruler than the other pretenders for the throne. Go now! Save our Kingdom!
Tales of Realms and Ruins is a competitive strategy game with slight cooperative elements. The game is for 2-3 players where you take on the role of a Hero campaigning to claim the throne of the Kingdom. You will gain Followers by playing cards, building your deck, conquering Realms and using their effects. Throughout the play, you will encounter various choose-your-own-adventure Events that will shift the odds of success either towards you, your rival players for the Throne, or the game itself. Tales of Realms and Ruins may end in 6 different ways depending on your choices throughout the play.'

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: Guarding the Land: The Battle of Fort Griswold, September 6, 1781
description:
Guarding the Land: The Battle for Fort Griswold is a 2-player postcard wargame that covers the British assault on Ft. Griswold on September 6th, 1781 in Connecticut.  Benedict Arnold (a Limey who became a Yank and then turned Limey again) was in overall command of British forces and this was his last combat action of the war. He was on the west side of the river busily sacking the town. A British detachment landed on the east side of the river to tackle the American defenders in Ft. Griswold. Historically, the Americans were quickly routed and there were claims of a massacre by the British. Several poems were written later to commemorate the event.

This game requires a deck of regular playing cards and a six-sided die (d6) to play.

Summer Tour - The Game takes players on a vibrant, musical journey inspired by the spirit of legendary jam bands. In this board game adventure, you'll embark on a cross-country tour, chasing epic jam band shows and collecting tickets along the way. It's all about embracing the good vibes, camaraderie, and the thrill of the road.

Players choose their vans and strategically navigate the game board, moving towards tour stops to collect tickets. Spin and Roll dice to advance. Special bonus rolls add an extra layer of excitement, offering unique opportunities and challenges.

The ultimate goal of Summer Tour the Game is to collect the most tickets by the time you've visited all 12 cities on your cosmic journey. But it's not just about the destination; it's about the experiences along the way. Will you embrace the free-spirited nature of the tour, chase the shows, and collect the tickets to become the ultimate fan? The stage is set, and the adventure awaits!

AlmaTafl is an abstract strategy game for 2 players of at least 8 years of age, with an average playing time of 20 minutes, influenced by the Tafl family of games, where one player controls the invaders and one player controls the defenders. 
The invading army aims to capture the King, while the defending army aims to lead its King on any escape spot.
The board, as well as the setup of the game are inspired by the star-shaped fortifications and unlike other Tafl games, AlmaTafl is perhaps the only game in the Tafl family of games which features the stacking mechanism and no casualties.

—description from the designer

Mixtape Massacre: Time Warp is the brand new expansion in the long running Tales from Tall Oaks 1980's collection. It is also the final entry in this SIX part collection.

In this brand new expansion for Escape from Tall Oaks (with additional components for use with Mixtape Massacre) Time Warp has players working together as you travel back and forth through time in an attempt to clean up, or mess up the Tall Oaks timeline.

During your travels, you'll be chased by new slashers of Tall Oaks past and future. With new survivors and slashers, new modes of play, and multiple possibilities for endings, prepare for the time of your life... or death.

It's Time to head back to Tall Oaks!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: War in Tokyo: The Hunting Pack
description:
In 1945 Japan did not surrender. The Allies push hard to bring an end to World War II. What ends is nuclear fallout in Tokyo. With civil unrest across the globe, the Allies send special teams into radiated Tokyo to find the Japanese Army and its government to secure surrender and bring an end to World War II.

The story to War in Tokyo uses the reality that during a time of war, technology advances at a faster pace than in a time of peace.

The Hunting Pack is an RPG Tabletop wargame, so what a troop finds, hacks, kills or knocks out affects them after the skirmish and any later skirmishes; that trooper may even level up and find new specialties to branch out into, the decision is yours...

Controlling four units to begin with, players will embark on a journey to find the hiding Japanese government (Allied Forces), stop the Allied invasion (Japanese Forces), or assist the army that pays the most (Mercs), to bring an end to the war.

Which side are you on?

—description from the designer

boardgame: War in Tokyo: Diary of a Volunteer
description:
In 1945 Japan did not surrender. The Allies push hard to bring an end to World War II. What ends is nuclear fallout in Tokyo. With civil unrest across the globe, the Allies send special teams into radiated Tokyo to find the Japanese Army and its government to secure surrender and bring an end to World War II.

The diary of Private Chris Hunt shows what it was like before entering Tokyo, and the realisation of the enormous and dangerous task ahead. This is only the beginning of his story....

—description from the designer

boardgame: Weltkrieg, 1914: Solitaire World War I Wargame
description:
Weltkrieg, 1914 is a solitaire game simulating the opening six weeks of World War I, at the grand strategic level. The player takes the German side in the game, and distributes his armies among the main theaters of the war to make attacks and advances to bring about the end of the war quickly, hopefully with favorable results. The object of the game is, through your application and manipulation of military assets, to get enough Victory Points to win the game, and the war. Setting: National armies are mobilizing across Europe. Britain stands ready to enter the war if Belgian neutrality is violated. You, in the role of Germany—you cannot rely too strongly on your Austrian friends—must put your war plans into action, in search of a quick victory “before the leaves fall.”

—description from the publisher

Blood of Ancients is a tabletop miniature wargame for armies of antiquity. 
The game is d10 based with virtually no references to charts to make for a game that plays quickly with rules that are easy to learn. There are a few key concepts which are different from other systems. The contested ground and charge rules are designed to illustrate the push and shove of battles in antiquity. It was a time when armies charged at each other, clashing with only a few casualties. But each charge is a test of mettle and discipline until one side breaks and runs, whence the real slaughter begins.

The heart of the game is charge and maneuver. Don’t let an opponent on your flanks! But charge his flank mercilessly when you can. Your cavalry can be a powerful offensive weapon, but shaky in defense. Your troops will last longer in combat when massed together, but ranged weapons are most effective when fired at massed troops, so you’ll need skirmishers in your army as well.

Your commander will play a part as well. His army will function best when troops are within his command. Troops which are out of command are unreliable, but your leader can inspire troops under his command to move, defend or attack with greater determination. The leader’s timely actions may save the day on a key hill or important flank.

This game doesn’t require either a specific scale of miniatures or a particular playing surface. You are encouraged to use what you have on hand. Whether you use stands of 15mm, 10mm, or 6mm figures or individually based 20mm or 28mm figures makes no difference. One stand of 15mm figures or 1 individually based 28mm figure still equals 40 to 50 combatants.

We prefer to use a hex grid (less arguments) on our table surface. Our hex grids are big enough to contain a unit. A unit is composed of 4 to 12 bases or stands of figures. If you don’t have or like hexes, we’ll tell you how to easily convert this to free form measurements.

—description from the publisher

Storms have gone, and rain has fallen,
And all our wildest prayers have been said —PowerWolf lyrics

A horde of ravenous undead led by a Strigoi master besieges a small town at the gates of Tismana. The people of the village, terrified, ask for the help of Powerwolf — feared children of the moon, wild army of Armenia, defenders of the True Creed — to free them and embrace the way of Vorcolac for good! Will the werewolves and Powerwolf be able to hunt down the Strigoi master and save the village (and as many inhabitants as possible) before the sun rises?

Alive or Undead is a co-operative card game in which each player takes on the role of a werewolf who will try to co-ordinate their own actions with those of the other players...but as is well known, a pack of werewolves during a frantic assault is not so much a disciplined patrol, able to handle high-profile tactical actions! 
Therefore, during the assault, it will not be easy for the werewolves to understand each other's intentions...especially to the sound of howls.

Assault after assault, the werewolves — with the help of the valiant Powerwolf — will try to annihilate the undead because each of them, if defeated, contributes to the exhaustion of the Strigoi master's vital power. Likewise, every villager infected by the undead will increase the Strigoi master's power, so it will be very important to save as many villagers as possible from the master's claws!

—description from the publisher

The world has been corrupted by the Nephilim, who have left behind their henchman — the Tyrant of the Konstrukts — to reign over the once lush and peaceful lands.

The bloody countryside strewn with iron and steel was not enough, nor were the hills, grass, and trees, now colored red... The soil of the fields will forever taste of the blood of warriors sacrificed for the madness of men corrupted by evil. Kreel has no second thoughts or regrets, nor do the men he has gathered around him for this final march, during which it will be increasingly difficult to distinguish between good and evil...

The March Against the Tyrant is an epic adventure that will lead the players to face a certain enemy, but following a path of choices that will determine their conduct and integrity. Follow blind, instinctive fury; rely on one's own purity; or use the magical arts? These will be the tools with which the heroes may solve the trials they will face.

The March Against the Tyrant is not a co-operative game. Each adventurer follows their own path, dictated by their own way of solving dilemmas, but the combined choices of the players will contribute to the outcome of the quest and provide the tyrant with the key to make their victory a chimera!

—description from the publisher

Q ME UP is a lively party game where the players become music producers. Guided by a theme determined by the next theme card, queue and play your song for everyone to hear and judge. Other players rate your song by giving it one of the four score records: Platinum, Gold, Silver or Cult Classic.

After four rounds, everyone has their producer playlist ready, and the ratings are summed up. The player who scores the most points, is crowned as the Platinum Producer. If another player has collected more cult classics, they win the game by becoming the esteemed CULT PRODUCER!

—description from the designer

The picture books about Sallys father, by Thomas Brunstrøm and Thorbjørn Christoffersen, has been very succesful in Danish children's homes, and with 'Bandekassespillet' (The Swear Jar Game) the universe is turned into a solid board game. In the game Eddie and Sally have made swear jars for everybody, but who will be the first to fill it up with coins?

Primarily a game about rolling the die and following the path, but it allows children to play with language and swearwords in a fund and liberating way within the context of the game.

As magical librarians, protect the sanctity of the Mystical Library.  Co-operate through 50 quests to make words and combat the diabolical wizard Jack Q. Vexez and his evil henchmen Fyp and Pyf.

Each level has unique objectives/restrictions and they start from easy (suitable for learning the game, or for challenging children), progressing up to tricky and ultimately diabolical levels, which will challenge even the finest wordsmiths in the land!

Generally working on 2 words at once, players will take turns to place a card at the start or end of one of these lines, aiming to turn a line into a word.

Each level might see restrictions on what letters you can use, remove letters from the deck, force you to use certain letters to beat the level, or even do something totally different!

—description from the publisher

The Automata is a versatile framework that brings a new level of excitement to your gaming experience. With this innovative expansion, you can simulate up to 2 opponents, allowing you to enjoy thrilling gameplay even when you’re playing solo. Unlock the realm of solo play for the core game as the Automata brings the game to life with its intelligent simulation. Whether you’re seeking a challenging one-player adventure or want to enhance your multiplayer sessions, the Automata is here to elevate your gaming experience to new heights.

—description from the designer

Staring Thumbs is a multi-player card game that exemplifies family fun. Players must choose cards from their hands that correlate to three common games (staring contest, thumb war, and rock, paper, scissors). If any of their opponents have chosen the same game, they must compete in the chosen game for the points of the cards. After all cards in the decks have been played, the player with the most points wins!
This Bayou Bash expansion turns up the chaos with new riders, new game modes, new track elements, and the ability to play with up to 8 players!

Bid. Build. Betray.

Outbid and outplay your opponents as you race to be the first one to complete your robot. Decide when to stockpile your money and when to buy robot parts, minibots, and utilities. Use powerful effects to help you claim victory or destroy your opponents as they try to do the same.

Each game of Machinate creates an evolving marketplace. No card has a set price. Each new game you play changes your approach. As you learn about the card effects and auctions, you change when and how much you spend. Each player gets a fresh infusion of credits and participates in auctions every turn, keeping everyone at the table engaged. And with utility cards that can be played on any turn, you always have an opportunity to influence the game.

Powerful and unique card effects let you play with different strategies every game. Each deck has a total of 100 cards, with 69 unique abilities and art across robot parts, minibots, and utilities. Unique utility and minibot cards allow you to stay in the game, even if you are running low on credits. This creates a natural balance where every player is competing right to the end. There's always a chance for a dramatic underdog victory.

—description from the designer

Play your combinations cleverly to hit the Jackpot or to achieve powerful three of a kinds!

In this card game, players try to align slot machines symbols on their tableau to snag the symbols tokens and the matching powers.

Each card has three symbols and players place them trying to either align three of the same symbols or create a serie of all seven different symbols. The player can either place each card next to another or partially tuck it under/play it over other cards

Three of a kinds gain the player the matching token either from the center of the table or from the player who owned it before, and then apply their powers. If the player already has the matching token, they gain GAIN cards from the bank.

Jackpots gain the player Jackpot cards which are worth a whopping seven points at the end of the game. If they manage to create both a horizontal Jackpot AND a vertical Jackpot during the game, it becomes a Jackpot Royal and gain both a Jackpot card and a token.

Each token has a unique power that activates when gained by a player. Most of them allow the player to steal GAIN cards either from other players or from the bank. They are also two-sided, with one side being more valuable than the other.

After 14 turns, the players add the points from the GAIN cards, the Jackpot Cards and the tokens they still own at the end of the game. The player with the most points win!

Nataterra is an asymmetric territory-control board game, with elements of resource management, technology development, and a unique combat system set in an original high-fantasy world waiting to be discovered.

Nataterra features a unique combat system that acts as a game within the game. When players clash units in a specific zone on the map, each unit is represented on a separate board, allowing for in-depth strategic prowess and sharp decision-making.

Evolve with the deck-based technology tree — each card unlocks a specific new card or component on your main board, allowing you to develop your civilisation further and broaden your faction’s capabilities.

Each of Nataterra’s 17 unique map tiles is crafted from six hexagons, ensuring stability while playing and offering diverse biomes and resources to gather. With nearly infinite configurations possible, these tiles allow for endless replayability that requires new strategic approaches every time you play.

In Nataterra, players can choose from four distinct civilisations, each offering unique characteristics and playstyles. With 11 victory conditions and 16 map tiles to customise your game setup, the replayability is endless!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Happy Little Dinosaurs: Clear the Air Promo Card
description:
Your favorite existentially-distressed dinosaurs are now collectible vinyl mini figures!

The Happy Little Dinosaurs Vinyl Mini Series features eight of your favorite dino characters, plus two new mystery characters. Each box comes with a vinyl figure and a promo card you can use in the game.

Plug the new card into your Disaster deck to play with your Happy Little Dinosaurs base game and expansions.

Buy one figure at a time for a mystery surprise, or get the pack of 18 with a guarantee that you'll find all 10 figures! The surprise is half the fun, so Vinyl Figure Series sales are final.

Bad Luck Bronto vinyl mini figure and Clear the Air promo card.

Card text:
CLEAR THE AIR
If you would add this card to your Disaster Area, move it to the bottom of the Disaster deck instead, then collect 2 points for each Natural Disaster card in your Disaster Area.

boardgame: Obsidian Empires: Warfare in Pre-Columbian Mesoamerica
description:
Obsidian Empires is a tabletop miniature wargame set in the colorful and dynamic Pre-Columbian world of Mesoamerica. Focusing on the Post-Classic period, the rules and armies in this book give you all you need to play games set during the rise and domination of the Triple Alliance all the way to the arrival of the Spanish. With gameplay that is intuitive and easy to learn, any player can jump right in and start their journey through this lush time period and setting, and the rules allow for great creativity in scenarios and campaigns.

Simple enough for new players to grasp but also complex enough to entice veteran gamers, Obsidian Empires use a novel mechanic to represent battles in the time period: Focusing on the Core Forces mechanic, Mesoamerican armies all have a common pool of Units that they can utilize, but each specific Faction brings unique bonuses, strategies, and sometimes, unique Units that make each Faction distinct. Gameplay is also kinetic, there is no sitting around here, and all players are active in each and every Step of the game. Each roll is meaningful, and with a focus on a single die for most rolls, there is no time wasted counting dice or figuring out needlessly complex formulas.

The book includes army lists and special rules for:

Aztec (Triple Alliance)
the largest empire in central Mexico, this Faction emphasizes melee and close-quarter battles, but due to their military expertise, they can bring forces in from flanking maneuvers, and due to their size, they even have a free Unit to capture their dense population.

Purepecha
the second largest empire to the west of the Aztecs, the Purepecha were famed for their keen-eyed marksmen, utilizing range combat to stymie their foes, but each Purepecha bow was so well-constructed, it could even act as a weapon in melee.

Tlaxcala
An isolated City State to the East of the Triple Alliance, after years of warfare, the Tlaxcala were bitter enemies of the Aztecs. Renowned for their fervor in combat, they relied on a balanced force that could maximize advantages both from afar and close.

Huastec
a City State of Mayan descent to the Northeast of the Triple Alliance near to what is today known as the Gulf of Mexico. The Huastec have been slowly losing their southern territories to the Aztecs, but their tenacity and expertise with spears is legendary. They were steadfast fighters that would rather die on their feet than flee than the enemy.

Spanish
the first Europeans to begin the task of conquering, colonizing, and converting the native population, the Spanish were often outnumbered hundreds to one, but thanks to their Tlaxcala allies, they were able to utilize the advantages of gunpowder, steel, and horses. Unlike other forces, the Spanish did not fight to claim captives but rather to seek gold and glory.

—description from the publisher

Four-card expansion for Stoner Fluxx. Adds two new Keepers--Hash (counts as Weed) and Secret Stash (allows you once per turn to hide a Weed Keeper under it and treat it as if it doesn't exist, thus preventing it from being discarded)--and two new Goals: Hash Pipe (Hash + The Pipe) and Stash of Hash (Secret Stash + Hash).

Rolling Trains is print'n'play game in roll and write genre for 1-4 players. Your task will be to deliver goods from factories to correct cities. You play on a map without any railroads built. It is your job to construct them and use them for delivery.

Players will play in turns. During each turn, player will gather funds to build railroads, factories and stations. Each turn you roll 3 dice and assign them to actions. Each die result allows you to choose 1 of 3 actions. Available action types are: building railroads, factories or stations and gathering funds. If you do one type of action more, you will do the other types less.

DOG + DONUT = ?

Players guess spesific measurement, often ridiculous leghts/dimensions. As the title suggest, how long is a dog and a donut combined? Player who guesses closest to the correct answer, scores a point.

A modern interpretation of the first recorded game, inspired by archeological discoveries.

The Royal Game of Ur is an epic ancient board game that has been around since 2600 BCE. It came into being as a court game in the ancient Mesopotamia.

The game has a 3×8 grid board with 20 squares. The dice used are typically D4, with some alternative solutions existing.

An inevitable reality surrounding the game is that due to the historical context, the full rules and the way of playing are largely unknown. Ur: The Royal Game is a project intended to re-interpret and expand upon the existing concept of the Royal Game of Ur.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Star Wars: X-Wing (Second Edition) – YT-2400 Light Freighter Expansion Pack
description:
A classic light freighter returns to Star Wars: X-Wing in this pack! A fast and resilient light freighter, the YT-2400 features a variety of weapon emplacement points, making it an attractive vessel for smugglers who favor a heavily armed “transport.” This pack adds one of these ships to players’ collections along with new ship cards for Rebel hero Dash Rendar and his droid companion Leebo. Joining these two pilots are two standard loadout cards that give players everything they need to jump into action and reprints of 8 upgrade cards that invite players to outfit their YT-2400 to their specifications.

description:
As in ancient times, join a faction and take on the role of one or more charioteers in a wild race through the Circus.

The first to cross the finish line wins your faction’s title, and to do so, you’ll have to pull out all the stops!

How do you play the game?

Each round, the player must:

Choose their pace, so as to adjust their speed according to their strategy.
Move around the track according to their speed, to beat their opponents or help their faction.
Brake!
But be careful not to capsize!

And why not go on a collision course? Crash into a competitor’s chariot, at the risk of seeing your own chariot break up...

and !
To go even further!

Advanced rules allow you to :

Select a chariot type: light, classic, or heavy! Piloting and strategy won’t be the same.
Added realism for negotiating turns. Watch out for skids!
More detailed boarding.
Add event cards.

—description from the publisher

Comme dans l’Antiquité, faites partie d’une faction et incarnez un ou des auriges et participez à une course endiablée dans le Cirque.

Le premier à passer la ligne d’arrivée fait gagner sa faction, et pour y parvenir tous les moyens sont bons !

Comment jouer ?

Le joueur doit à chaque tour :

Choisir son allure, de manière à ajuster sa vitesse en fonction de sa stratégie.
Se déplacer sur la piste en fonction de son allure, afin de battre ses adversaires ou aider sa faction.
Freiner !
Mais attention à ne pas chavirer !

Et pourquoi ne pas passer à l’abordage ? En percutant le char d’un concurrent, cependant au risque de voir son propre char se disloquer...

et !
Pour aller plus loin !

Des règles avancées permettent :

De sélectionner un type de char : léger, classique ou lourd ! Le pilotage et la stratégie ne seront pas les mêmes. 
D’ajouter plus de réalisme dans la négociation des virages. Attention aux glissades !
De détailler les abordages.
D’ajouter des cartes Événements.

You and your rivals have managed to put aside your differences for the greater good. You’ve trapped two newly created Death Knights in separate magical containment zones that sap most of their considerable power and link their fates together. Previously only available at conventions.

A familiar but totally new game for those who seek the thrill and challenge of naval conflict in all theaters of World War II.
For those who have played Axis & Allies War & Sea, these rules may feel familiar, but there are many improvements and distinctions from the classic game including:


     D10 based resolution
     Over the horizon rules for hidden movement & reconnaissance
     Fun cards to play on vessels for situational bonuses
     Use models from any manufacturer … or print your own!
     Critical hit cards can knock out weapon systems, engines, armor, etc.
     Rules for weather, terrain placement, refueling and more.


How To Resolve Hits
Roll a number of d10’s as shown for the specified range on the Armament Array. Total all results of a 3 or less and
compare that to the defender’s Armor. If the total number of hits exceeds Armor, the defender suffers a Damage. If the total number hits exceeds Integrity then the defender is sunk.

Each Damage reduces the defender’s Integrity and also increases the chance for a critical hit. Critical hit cards add excitement and thrills.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Epipolae: The Athenian Expedition in Sicily, 415-413 BCE
description:
Sicily, 415 BCE. The Athenian expedition to Sicily was one of the great campaigns of antiquity: a grand adventure to remote shores, featuring a massive fleet and army, that was heavily financed, and benefited from the complete commitment of its citizenry. The goal was the subjugation of all Sicily in order to secure another source of grain. It was simultaneously bold and foolish, and it ended in disaster. But it nearly succeeded.

For the first time, Epipolae brings Volko Ruhnke’s Levy & Campaign Series to the Ancient World, portraying 5th-century BCE Greek military operations with a focus on logistics and maneuver. Players will raise and equip their armies and navies, build alliances through diplomacy, and ravage, siege, and battle their way to victory.

Historically, the Athenians were initially led by three leaders: Nicias, Alcibiades, and Lamachus. Nicias planned to circumnavigate around Sicily, settling a dispute on behalf of an ally, then parading the majestic and renowned Athenian fleet in a show of strength on the return voyage home to Greece. Alcibiades aimed to travel around Sicily conducting diplomacy, building a coalition of city-states, bringing the entire island into Athen’s imperial alliance known as the Delian League. Lamachus was inclined to immediately besiege and capture Syracuse, the strongest city in Sicily, before they could react and adequately prepare.

Blood of Ancients: Expanded Edition enhances the already solid foundation of its predecessor, marrying strategic tabletop gameplay with the rich tapestry of ancient history. This edition provides greater clarity, intricately designed scenarios, and vivid illustrations, transporting players back to the grand battles that determined the fate of empires.

As the clash of steel resonates and war cries reverberate, pivotal confrontations that carved the course of history come alive on your tabletop. This isn't a mere game—it's an odyssey into the art of warfare and the strategies that shaped civilizations.

The flexibility of the ruleset allows players to employ any collection of figures across various scales, offering the freedom to recreate some of history's most iconic encounters. With the Blood of Ancients'  nuanced charge and counter-charge mechanics, each decision, each tactic, holds the potential to change the course of battle.

For those who love strategy, crave a challenge, or simply appreciate the grandeur of ancient warfare, Blood of Ancients: Expanded Edition is an invitation to command and conquer. Secure your rulebook, and let the battles of old ignite your modern gaming nights.

—description from the publisher

Glory be to the Immortal Khan, who soon will ascend from the Wasteland to the Next World. With no heir to inherit the Blood Throne, He appoints four chieftains from the ruling hordes--each more thirsty than the next for a chance to rule the ruins.

Blood Sworn, is the second expansion for Ruination, bringing even more to the post-post-apocalypse. Each horde now has two new additions: a chieftain and a horde deck. Each chieftain provides unique tactics during battles, as well as the possibility to be upgraded. Horde cards are asymmetrical one-use abilities with extremely powerful effects.

This expansion also includes:

- Updated rules for nomads, including all new nomad tokens and event cards that change every game
- New rules for the glory track, including a glory die
- Defense: a new stat to mitigate casualties
- Alternate exile cards
- New action decks with completely unique cards, including the aforementioned chieftain upgrades
- And even more powerful advancements

Rules and scenarios for gaming tabletop battles in antiquity. Alexander & His Adversaries includes

     Interesting information to learn more about the arms and armor of armies in antiquity

A fast and simple game for the younger kids. Especially apt for 3 to 5 year-olds.

boardgame: Fourteen Days in June: the Waterloo Campaign
description:
Fourteen Days in June: the Waterloo Campaign is a two-player wargame played on a hex-grid superimposed upon a map depicting north east France and (what is now) southern Belgium. The game focuses upon the struggle in June 1815 between the French army under Napoleon Bonaparte and the Allied armies led by the Duke of Wellington and von Blücher. The game portrays entire campaign. By doing so, and by means of innovations such as the “initial order system”, the game reflects the range of strategic options available to each side. For example, the French can decide where and when to strike; whilst the Allies can chose to gamble by second-guessing the French line(s) of attack and marshalling their forces to meet this or, as Blücher and Wellington did historically, adopt a more cautious approach and allocate sufficient force to each likely invasion route along the Franco-Belgium border to delay Napoleon for long enough for reinforcements to arrive. 
This uncertainty or “fog-of-war” which played such a crucial role in campaigns of the period is simulated further by a mechanic allowing players to conceal the strength of their various forces unless and until combat takes place. Even then, it is possible that not all the units in a force will be disclosed. This doubt as to the enemy’s  intentions and strength chimes with historical records of discussions about strategy at the headquarters of each side during the campaign. Many of these discussions were concerned with the more mundane – but, no less crucial - aspects of warfare such as planning routes of march. Such issues, too, are reflected in the game. For instance, Route Blocked Markers are used to reflect the fact that a full corps marching along a minor road would be spread out over several miles. Other friendly units are unable to move through not just the hex occupied by the force counter; but, also “preceding” hexes through which its rearmost elements are still marching. So, when issuing orders for units to concentrate in a specific area, or planning a combined attack, players need to ensure that two (or more) forces do not trip over each other by attempting to occupy the same road-space at the same time. Other realities of warfare before the advent of aeroplanes and radio are also simulated. For instance, the game incorporates simple mechanisms to reflect the risk of forces losing their way if they leave the principal road network and attempt to march across country; and, the delay between a supreme commander issuing an order and it being received and implemented by a junior officer in command of a force maybe 10 miles distant at a time when such communications were carried by horse.

So, will the Emperor rewrite History by defeating Wellington and Blucher before hoisting the French tricolour over Brussels; or, will the Allies prevail once again, leaving Napoleon to rue what might have been? The decision is yours.

—description from the publisher

Files exist for both standard 2.5 x 3.5 inch US Poker-sized cards, and square cards.
