Spongebob Squarepants version of UNO.

Players race to empty their hands and catch opposing players with cards left in theirs, which score points. In turns, players attempt to play a card by matching its color or number to the topmost card on the discard pile. If unable to play, players draw a card from the draw pile, and if still unable to play, they pass their turn.

Wild and special cards spice things up a bit by changing the turn directo, skipping the next players or making him take extra cards.

Th.B.A.Th - The Battle Against Th.E.Y. is a Victorian Quantum-Technological Race Against Th.E.Y (The Entangled.You), where you play as a Victorian scientist who inadvertently caused a quantum version of yourself in a parallel world to become entangled with your own self. That version of you is Th.E.Y. - The Entangled.You - and Th.E.Y. will try and become the only You in the universe, which you will, obviously, need to fight against and ensure you will be the only You in the universe.

You have only 24 hours (rounds) to create a technology to solve this problem - BUT there is a catch, EVERYTHING you do will cause Th.E.Y. to do the same thing in their parallel world and you will need to plan your steps carefully to ensure you will get the best outcome out of each action you take.

Also, now that we are talking about it, would destroying Th.E.Y. mean that you get destroyed also? Better think about it further and, maybe, play a game of ‘tie.’

A 40´-60´ Euro resource gathering, action selection, engine/ tableau building, and set Collection game for ages 12 and up, with a VERY simple AI to manage (the AI´s actions are directly related to yours, which means there is no downtime for you, and everything is under your control during the whole game), where the objective of the game is to control the game actions to ensure you will score either:

Th.B.A.Th. was created as an entrant to the 2023 Solitaire Print and Play Contest here in BoardGameGeek.

Welcome to the Thibion System, master. I am your host and servant P.E.O.R.S the Proto Earth Overseeing Robotic System, hopefully your travel from Earth has been pleasant and the cryogenic sleep has not been too harsh on you - after all, you will be going into cryogenic sleep a lot during your millenia shift.

My first task is explaining to you [what you already know] that you are the next Master Builder in our non stop efforts to create the new, and well balanced, Earth here in Thibion.
The infrastructure for your work has been already built here in Thibian Serenus Plains. As you can see, all the pipelines for the basic resources have been laid out and are fully operational.

For the next 2,000 years – or more - You will work through me in stints of 200 years to build Earthly features here in Thibion by using the pre-fabricated modules that have since arrived from Earth. I will take your instructions of where to place and develop the new modules and any technological adjustments that you may believe are necessary and execute them while you are back to cryogenic sleep.

The modules arriving from Earth will be of five different types: Plants, Animals, Water, Land and Technology, each in varying sizes. Once your shift is completed, the Galactic Eye will assess your performance.

Live 2 G(a/e)ther in Terra Nova is a light, easy to learn solitaire tile-laying / spatial-puzzle game in which you are trying to create a new world ensuring the right balance between each of the 5 elements for each planetary zone.
Make sure you spread the animals, plants, water and land evenly, and that your technology advances but doesn´t get in the way.

The game was created as an entry to the 2023 Solitaire Print and Play Contest here in BoardGameGeek.

Strategy and Strike TCG is a military trading card game that you can collect and battle with friends.

The five categories of cards, allows the player to build a deck to fight against other people. Each category of card does different things but the unit and support cards are the most important as they populate the battlefield.

     Unit cards for example are split into six types, that represent all military and government units around the world. They are what you use in the game to attack your opponent to win.
     Support cards are similar to unit cards in the fact that they can also be used to deal damage to other cards. There are many types of vehicles in the military so there is many types of ground, air, and naval support cards.
     Logistic cards allow you to equip your unit and support cards with all that they need to attack your opponent. They also boost a card's abilities and stats.
     Order cards are useful cards that provide the player with more abilities to use against their opponents. They may create either an instant or permanent effect on the game.
     Environment.


Gameplay
The game is a turn based game and each player takes turns. There are five phases you must go through each turn in the game.

    Drawing Phase: The first phase you draw a card from your deck and place this card in your hand.
    Deployment Phase: The second phase you can deploy your cards to the battlefield ensuring you have enough command points.
    Attacking Phase: The third phase you can initiate attacks on your opponent's cards with your unit and support cards.
    Counter Response Phase: The fourth phase your opponent can respond to any attacks or actions you deal against them.
    Opponent's Turn: Lastly you pass the turn over to your opponent or the next player. Once the turn comes back around to you, you restart from the Drawing Phase.


How to Win
Your objective is to eliminate all enemy unit and support cards that your opposition has.

—description from the publisher

Even though the new game is based on the bible story, we tried to design the new game feeling like the movie "Ben Hur" which is a masterpiece for ordinary people.

However, the game has some lucky elements. Therefore, players will be able to enjoy the game well because the game's feeling isn't just super heavy and serious!

description:
All Hoomanz Are Dead is a cooperative dungeon-crawling adventure board game for 1-5 players ages 12+, where each person plays as a cybernetically enhanced animal against a common enemy called S.I.M.O.N., the ultimate Artificial Intelligence that turned against its creators and decimated the entire human scientists from a high-tech lab with a genetically engineered VIRUS.

The game objective is to stop S.I.M.O.N. before time runs out, which requires players to cooperate, gain skills and equip the best weapons found throughout the game. Fight monsters, gather treasures, survive random events, but at all costs avoid allowing S.I.M.O.N. to take over the world!

description:
18Turkey is an 18xx game about the railway development of the Anatolian Peninsula. It depicts 19th century British, French, and German involvement as well as the Turkish nationalization of foreign-owned railways in the wake of the Great War.

—description from the designer

Broken heroes captured by a cruel enemy must battle for the chance of freedom. Once allies, they must now fight to the death. Will you overcome the odds and claim your victory?

In Shackled, 2 or 4 players take the role of these heroes, which are split into teams of two. Each round, players will secretly play one of several unique action cards tied to that hero, which will then be revealed simultaneously and played from fastest to slowest. Each action is tied to specific directions relative to how the character faces, meaning positioning and rotation are both very important. When a hero reaches zero health, the opposing team gets 10 points, and the hero is out until the end of the next round. There are also relics in the center of the map that offer points towards victory, as well as powerful abilities for the hero that captures them. The first team to reach 20 points is victorious!

—description from the designer

Dragon Bowl is a fantasy-themed board game made for bowling. Players take the game to the bowling center, set it up on the back table, and bowl to drive the game mechanics. Players, heroes, travel across a perilous landscape battling creatures and overcoming challenges on their way to defeat the marauding dragon and save the Kingdom.

Dragon Bowl takes the focus off of the bowling score and emphasizes shot-making. With six different bowling skill levels, beginner to pro, any level can play at the same time and anyone can win. A beginning bowler can defeat a pro bowler in Dragon Bowl.

Players select a hero and the skill level (based on bowling average or typical score bowled) at which they will compete. Heroes travel across a kingdom overrun with fierce creatures. When a player encounters a creature, she notes the creature's strength (denoted on an encounter card ranging from 6,8,10, or 12). Based on that strength, she roles the x-sided die to determine how to defeat the creature.

Ex: A beginning bowler encounters a creature with strength 8. The beginning bowler roles an 8-sided die. The result is 5. To defeat the creature, the beginning bowler must knock over at least 5 pins with two balls in the bowling frame. An intermediate bowler would also roll the 8-sided die and have to knock over the resulting number of pins on their first ball to defeat the creature. Each subsequent skill level takes the difficulty to another level.

There are six creature encounters, three defined bowling challenges, and the final battle against the dragon, which consists of overcoming a unique Victory Combo.

In addition to the bowling component, each hero has a unique special skill they can use once or twice during the game. One hero can skip an encounter once during the game, while others can retake a shot or add +1 to their pin count or even steal another hero's die roll. 
   
The game is designed to be played in the bowling center, but we do have people playing at home using some other bowling game like wii bowling or even a phone app.

—description from the designer

Players play as rulers, each having a piece on a looping trail. At the beginning of the game they each receive 70 coins, and roll in order to play. There are three types of cards next to the board: property cards (that show the property name, its value and its tax amount), soldier cards (that show the name of the unit, its value and its power rating), and chance cards (that need to be shuffled and put faced down next to the board).
On the board there are properties, soldier barracks and churches. When a player lands on a property, they can purchase it. When a property is purchased, if any other player lands on it, they have to pay the landlord a tax (written on the property card); also if the owner themselves lands on the property, they get that same amount from the treasury. The church tiles are chance tiles, and if a player lands on them they will have to draw from the chance cards. They can gain or lose coins from the chance cards.
The goal of the game is to get soldiers and gather an army. When a player lands on a soldier tile on the trail, if they have the required coin, they can add that soldier to their army.
The game ends when all the properties are sold, and the winner is the person with the most powerful army. The property value and the remaining coins are inconsequential. If the player with the most powerful army owes coins to the treasury or another player, they cannot be the winner and the second most powerful player will be the winner.
The visuals and the art style, as well as soldier units and properties, are themed around the Firefly Studios' 2002 real time strategy game "Stronghold Crusader".

There's been an accident in the Gear-Gum factory and all of the bots have been caught inside bubbles!

In Bubblebot, 2 players will compete in trying to keep their most valuable bots from floating away, while arranging the floated bots into sets and runs to keep their assembly line moving and maximum efficiency.

First to secure 20 Bubblebot orders wins!

—description from the publisher

Introduction:Vinver (from Old English: twigrap in andweard, meaning "diagonal duel") is a drawless game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the intersections (points) of an initially empty square board. The recommended board size is 19 points per side. The top and bottom edges of the board are colored black; the left and right edges are colored white. Each player has access to a sufficient supply of stones of their own color.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A chain is a set of orthogonally connected stones of the same color.
     To flip is to replace a stone with a stone of the opposite color.
     A crosscut is a 2×2 area formed by two stones of one color on one diagonal and two stones of the other color on the opposite diagonal.


				
				
					Turns:Black plays first and then alternates. On your turn, do one of the following actions:


     Place a stone of your color on an empty point.
     Flip an enemy stone for each crosscut it appears in.


				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when a player has connected the opposite edges of their color with a complete chain of their color, in which case they are the winner.

For the game to be fair, before the game begins, the first player places a black stone on an empty point and the second player chooses the sides. This balancing method is called the pie rule.

—description from the designer
Catapult tiny bean bags at mini cornhole boards with finger catapults in this tabletop version of a tailgate favorite.

"A NEW TAKE ON AN OLD CLASSIC: No "Old Maids" here! This thoroughly modern twist on the classic card game has players racing to end up as the awesome Cat Lady.
EASY TO PLAY: Match a cute collection of feline friends, like Fluffer Nutter, Aaron Purr, and Chip. The player with the Cat Lady card at the end wins!
PERFECT GIFT FOR CAT LOVERS: Fun to play and packed with gorgeous illustrations by Megan Lynn Kott, this game makes an excellent gift for feline lovers.
TAKE IT ANYWHERE: The petite, portable tins are the perfect size to throw in your purse or luggage for a vacation.
CARD GAME FUN FOR KIDS AND FAMILIES: This game is great for groups ranging from 2-6 players of all ages."

Thirty-One is a trick-taking game with six-numbered cards where players engage in l different play feeling for 31 days. The game contains 31 cards with 31 ranks that work as suits and where each card features six numbers in a similar to Dobble, where only one set of any two cards overlaps.
Letter Snake is a roll-and-write word game. Roll the dice and slither around the grid by inking in words. Complete the objectives listed on the game sheet to win

Tørnquists Quizrace is a trivia game where your knowledge plays a significant role, but where you also influence the race to the finish line through smart choices. Everyone gets the same questions, and everyone has access to the same opportunities along the way. The question is, what do you do with these opportunities? Do you sprint ahead and hope to create a gap between you and those behind, or do you stay further back and save opportunities in the hope of making a move towards the end? Tørnquists Quizrace is the game that maintains the excitement until the very end.

Rapid Dungeon is a simultaneous turn-based game.
Each player reveals one card simultaneously.
If the card you desire is among them, try to acquire it faster than anyone else.
The score varies depending on how you collect the cards.
The cards can be white or black, or red traps.
Player tiles have two sides, one white and one black.
To acquire a card, you need to place a player tile on it.
When placing a tile, you must choose either side to match the color of the card.
If there is at least one red trap card in the mix, the rules will be reversed.
This is a tabletop game that combines elements of speed gaming and set collection, testing your quick decision-making skills.

In Bonne Presse, each player takes turns playing the role of Editor-in-Chief, then the player with the most points wins the game.

The Editor-in-Chief draws 5 Word cards at random and chooses a Magazine for which the players will write this round. Trainees try to write a good headline, not off-topic, with as many of the words as possible.

The Editor-in-Chief then validates the headlines that fit in with the theme of the magazine: they score one point for each word drawn and used. Then he awards prizes for the funniest headline, and quite simply for the best headline!

Mice try to collect certain items in the forest, but on their way they have to move past tree stumps where the forst owl sleeps, and wake her up in the process.

If the owl is in good mood, the mouse may continue to move, if not, it must stop and it is the next player's turn.

The most notable feature of the game is a huge owl figure. Players can press its head, after which it will spread its wings and reveal either a friendly or an annoyed face.

Introduction:Fonver (from Old English: fōn in andweard, meaning "diagonal capture") is a drawless game for two players: White and Black. It is played on the intersections (points) of an initially empty square board. The recommended board size is 19 points per side. The top and bottom edges of the board are colored black; the left and right edges are colored white. Each player has access to a sufficient supply of stones of their own color.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A chain is a set of orthogonally connected stones of the same color.
     A crosscut is a 2×2 area formed by two stones of one color on one diagonal and two stones of the other color on the opposite diagonal.


				
				
					Turns:Black plays first, and then turns alternate. On your turn, place a stone of your color on an empty point, at least one stone per turn.

Having formed a crosscut, you must use one of the stones in the chains that include either of your own crosscut stones, to capture one of the enemy crosscut stones by replacing them with it. Continue this procedure as long as there are crosscuts on the board. If there are multiple crosscuts present they may be handled in any order, but the capturing piece must be from a chain related to the same crosscut where it is capturing.

				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when a player has connected the opposite edges of their color with a complete chain of their color, in which case they are the winner.

For the game to be fair, before the game begins, the first player places a black stone on an empty point and the second player chooses the sides. This balancing method is called the pie rule.

—description from the designer

Gladiator: Road to Rome is a fast-paced, solo or group card and dice game where players must battle each other or fight preset characters in the arena to survive.

Players choose one of 4 weapons and must strategically select cards to attempt to attack and defend themselves.

In solo campaign mode, your character is a captured Iceni warrior who must battle their way through 4 arenas against 16 increasingly difficult opponents to gain their freedom.

In Battle Royale mode, you fight other opponents to see who masters the arena.

boardgame: Frisian Landscapes (fan expansion to Fields of Arle)
description:
Frisian Landscapes Web App

New specific needs emerged, and the Arle community has great expectations on you. Will you carry out an intensive farming activity? Will you be the best builder? Will you be a good horse breeder? Craft your strategy following three common goals and find your way to success!

Fields of Arle: Frisian Landscapes is a Fields of Arle print-and-play expansion fully compatible with any other existing expansion of the base game.

The development of this expansion has taken place with the permission of the original publisher Feuerland Spiele.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Dixit: Disney Edition – Alice in Wonderland Promo
description: Promo includes a dial, a white rabbit figure meeple and a new card for the game.

boardgame: Wyvern Moor: The Dungeon Crawl Card Game
description:
The next campaign set in the addictive Dungeon Crawl Card Game system. A stand-alone sequel to Kreel Manor: The Dungeon Crawl Card Game, Wyvern Moor sees bold heroes plunge into the perilous gloom of Wyvern Moor, and descend into the dark depths of the dungeons beneath.

In Wyvern Moor, players embark on daring quests and terrifying adventures as legendary champions of the stunningly beautiful World of Kilforth. Wyvern Moor is fully compatible with Kreel Manor, and includes another set of heroes and adventures featuring the same addictive Dungeon Crawl Card Game system.

—description from the publisher

Each player writes on their Answer Card, in secret, the appropriate answer to the difficult question that was drawn, dictated by the Leader. The Leader writes the Correct Answer to this question on his card, as he is aware of it. Players respond in a way that makes their answer pass as the Leader's Correct Answer, as their score depends on it.

The Leader collects all the Answer Cards, shuffles them together with his own, and reads the answers to everyone in arbitrary order, announcing the order number of each card (1, 2, 3,…).

All players (except the Leader) bet their chips on the Game Board, on the order number corresponding to the answer they believe to be the Right Answer.

Once the players' answers are shown, each player receives 1 chip if they get the Correct Answer right, and 1 chip for each player they convince to bet on their answer. Whoever has the most chips after 12 moves wins.

—description from the designer

The heavens have split open, and new evils have come rushing out. Pluto's deadly games with the realm of mankind have just changed forever.

Pandora's Ruin brings a wealth of new content to the world of Hoplomachus: Victorum, adding to the vast options offered by the core game as well as introducing added variety in several places. Featuring new scions, new arena rules, new primus events, additional events to add scion-specificity to your campaigns, and an expedited play mode, Pandora's Ruin adds hours of playability and enhanced theme to your Victorum games.

Carve your way through the forest to smash chains in this true-to-sport disc golf board game.

Choose your teepad; line up your throw; and follow your disc as it travels up the flight path! There are over 250 different throw types at your disposal to navigates trees, bushes, high grass, rocks, water, and more! Create custom maps; play as few or as many holes as you'd like for flexible gameplay time; and enjoy the standard rules with your family, or beverage rules and skins matches with your friends.

This game features a robust disc flight physics engine that accounts for height, obstacle interaction, wind adjustments, ricochets, and more.

You'll have to account for Course Wind and changing course conditions as you compete for the lowest score across. May the best player win!

Once, long ago, a young girl was given a box and told not to open it. For the "crime" of curiosity, she was locked away, but now she's broken free and has no intention of ever going back.

Pandora's Wake is an expansion for Hoplomachus: Remastered featuring two brand-new arenas (including a remaster of the classic Hoplomachus Fa Mel Arena), each with their own unique game mode rules and units for solo and PvP play. This expansion also rounds out the combatants of Remastered by adding the Vesuvius faction, bringing it into cross-compatible parity with Hoplomachus: Victorum!

Not quite a part of Victorum, and not quite a part of Remastered, the Pandora's Might expansion bridges the gap between the two and brings a bevy of new heroes to the table in the fight against Pluto.

Usable in Victorum, this pack furnishes all your favorite heroes from Remastered with their own prowess deck, making them fully-functional now as both primus and hero.

Additonally, Pandora herself leads this intrepid band, but you may discover her powers to be a but more... unique. Use her at your own peril and try to balance the ability to earn practically any prowess in the game with the punishment of a flood of evil unleashed in the form of unrelenting scion influence. Pandora can also be recruited or drafted in some modes of Hoplomachus Remastered.

A few new chips and Opportunity Cards.


     Opportunity Card: Foul Nemesis
     Opportunity Card: Forge of the Lion
     Opportunity Chip: Farouk the Smith
     Opportunity Chip: Damascus Steel (Tactic)
     Bane Chip: Fishback

In Bacon, the goal is simple: collect bacon! But how do you achieve that? By fattening up adorable piglets and then, well, you know... It's easy to learn, quick to play, and full of laughter-inducing moments that'll have you squealing with delight.

Unleash strategic chaos with 71 action cards that keep your opponents on their toes. Fatten up 20 unique piglet cards, each with its own adorable design, to create your bacon empire. Keep track of your progress with 29 bacon score cards, and dive into the game with confidence using the comprehensive rulebook.

—description from the designer

VOID is a tactically-satisfying cooperative card game. In it you play a member of the crew of the ISS Shoemaker on the first crewed interstellar voyage in human history. Something has gone terribly wrong, and your jump engine has thrown the ship wildly off-course. Now you and your crew must work together to survive the many dangers of space and find your way back to Earth, our home.

In VOID, you are the crew of a starship with six systems. Each system has a resource you must manage; replenish the resource to keep the system functional or deplete it to use the system's ability. If ever a system is depleted with no resources left, it goes into failure and causes lasting problems until repaired (on top of losing access to it's system ability). Perhaps the most important system is the Engines, whose system ability allows the ship to jump one step closer to home.

Complicating the crew's mission are Event cards, one of which is drawn every turn. Sometimes, Events are neutral or even positive, but oftentimes they present the players with new challenges, as they deplete systems, cause damage to crew or even risk chain reactions. A Special kind of Event called a Mission Card is drawn each time the crew Jumps. There are nine Mission Cards in all in a given mission (chosen randomly from fifteen for variety) and once the crew has resolved the last of the nine, the Mission is successful. If the crew runs out of event cards or all systems go into failure, then the Mission has failed.

All the art and rules text of VOID are released under a creative commons license (CC By SA 4.0 to be exact).

—description from the publisher

Each player gets 22 figures: 1 king, 1 field marshal, 1 flag, 2 knights, 2 canons and 15 soldiers. The canons are placed in the castles. The different kinds of figures move in different ways about the crossing points of the grid. The objective of the game is to capture the enemy king. Capturing is done by moving over an enemy figure to a free space behind it.

In September 1939, Eire (the recently-renamed Irish Free State) declared that she would remain neutral in the new war erupting in Europe. This angered the British government, but Ireland had not participated in the Commonwealth of Nations since 1932.

Ireland was not invaded during the Second World War, but both Britain and Germany made plans to do so. Ireland eventually mobilized two divisions and two brigades, deployed along the border with Northern Ireland to resist a British invasion and along the island’s southern coast to fight off an attack by Britain or Germany. The Irish Army - Na Forsai Cosanta - eventually reached a strength of 38,000 and saw no action during World War II, though tens of thousands of Irish men and women volunteered to fight the Nazis in the British armed forces.

Fighting Irish is an expansion for Panzer Grenadier: Invasion that lets you send the Irish into battle anyway, in a small set of scenarios depicting a German airborne assault on the Emerald Isle. 
The Irish are tough, but they’re not very well-armed. Fighting Irish includes 24 new pieces depicting Irish troops and leaders in their traditional kelly green. 
It’s a good-looking set. 
Plus you get scenarios so you can play with them.

-Description from the publisher's website

An add-on of 33 promo cards available during the Creature Caravan Gamefound campaign.

Includes these promos:

Islebound: Emerald Edition Promos (10 cards)
Underground Buildings

Above and Below (17 cards)
Essen 2016
Groves
Megaland
Mystic's Hut
Watch it Played

Near and Far (6 cards)
Handy Headwear
Pandoran Box

Includes components for a 5th and 6th player, extra dice, and 45 creature cards.

The expansion includes a new type of card: items. Items can be "equipped" to creature cards, as long as the creature has the required type. Many of the item abilities activate when a die is placed on the creature that they're equipped to.

Backers of Creature Caravan will receive this expansion for free but Wanderers expansion will be available for purchase separately at a later date.

Put your Discman down, turn off Cheers... and get ready to play the game that is all that and a bag of chips. Yes Everybody Loves Raymond and they'll all love this 90's Trivia card game too. Aaaawl righty then, lets do this Ace!

This trivia game is conveniently packed in travel sized packaging making it great for long road trips! Be the first player to answer two questions in all five categories. Movies, music, TV, sports, & culture.

Where Many Were is a fast-playing set of big battle rules for the American Civil War that fits on just two pages.

Each base/element represents one battalion or regiment, and while they’re centred in the combat rules, command and control relies on divisional, corps, and army generals successfully manoeuvring their brigades. This lets brave & lucky units shine – as they did historically – while players grapple with the difficulty of commanding armies far larger & better-armed than their inherited staff systems were prepared for – as generals did historically!

The game features:


     Central “poker dice” mechanic keeps a Western flavour to all resolution.
     Command initiative system that introduces battlefield friction and even makes solo play fun and challenging.
     Simple but layered unit characteristics that interact flavourfully on the table, but can quickly be used to translate historical orders of battle into playable rosters.
     Integrated casualty and morale mechanics to keep the game flowing.
     A roster sheet to easily track the details of your force.


-Description from the publisher's website

MGs & Bayonets is a fast-playing set of WWII infantry battle rules that fits on just two pages. In addition to its featured period, it also works well for Interwar and early Cold War skirmishes.

Players control a 1:1 platoon or company of soldiers following a historical ORBAT, and play focuses on holding and denying terrain features on the table. With simple gradation between raw and veteran units, commanders can stack the odds in their favour before launching their final assault, just as captain and lieutenants did historically!

The game features:

     A simple bidding system of command initiative that lets the hasty move before the coordinated.
     Terrain-centric movement and shooting rules that organically incorporate reconnaissance by fire and area suppression.
     Integrated casualty and morale mechanics to keep the game flowing.
     An optional points system for pick-up or club play.


-Description from the publisher's website

That Bleak Armada is a generic set of rules for everyone who just wants to get their spacefleets on the table! 
The author’s group have played battles with up to 60 ships a side in 2-3 hours.

The game’s simple activation system pushes decisions on players every phase, providing believable results on an ordinary-size table (assumed to be between 4’x4’ and 6’x4’). It also makes the game suitable for solo play.

All measurements are from the ship’s “stem” or centre, so any scale of ship can be used. Similarly, the flexible (somewhat abstract) ship design system lets you easily create & price a profile for any ship you like!

No matter the galaxy you’re fighting in, whether you’re firing lasers or solid shot, That Bleak Armada lets you indulge your interstellar megalomania with a fun, fast game that’s easy to learn but hard to master.

-Description from the publisher's website

BOHICA: Vietnam Skirmish Rules  Bend Over, Here It Comes Again is designed for platoon size actions for figures of any scale.

Includes rules for the nasty surprises you'd expect, and practice scenarios

description:
"Why so serious?" With Echt grenzwertig you create the funniest, most absurd, and most violent statements from different card-combinations. Is "dissing old white men" a new torture method in North Korea? Or will "Bake hash brownies with your parents" be the first action of the German chancellor to solve the country's most urgent problems? There are (almost) no limits of possible combinations!

—description from the publisher

When added to the basic game of Tali, enough components for 10 Players.

Includes:
4 Unique Historically Based Character Cards
4 Character Tokens
40 Historical Punch out coins (3 Denominations, 8 gold, 12 silver, 20 copper)
By asking a series of yes-or-no questions, try to identify which character your opponent is holding before they can identify yours!

Campaign live on Gamefound!

https://gamefound.com/en/projects/pedro-andre/golen-bay?ref=search

In Golen Bay you and up to three friends become the leader of Captain Cannon and his crew, lost on a very rich island.

You must work together to seek hidden treasures. Using your characters´ abilities to steal bombs and blow up treasure chests is the only way you can return home, in this fast paced and cooperative thrilling adventure of strategy and teamwork.

—description from the publisher

GOLEN BAY is a pirate-themed board game where players use their characters' abilities to steal bombs and blow up treasure chests. Players compete to be the first to blow up all the treasure chests, making use of each character's unique skills and abilities.
"This is a game proudly created in Portugal and has been in development over the last two years so that all board game fans can finally have GOLEN BAY in their collection!", says the Golen Games team responsible for design, mechanics, and game production

GOLEN BAY is a turn-based pirates-themed tabletop game designed to provide instant gaming fun for 2 or 4 players. Inside the box are 50 cards with impeccably designed actions and characters, the game board, ten tokens, and a dice.

In a scenario with pirates assaulting GOLEN BAY and local inhabitants defending what is theirs, the objective is to make good use of the capabilities of the several cards to advance across the sand, throwing bombs and trying to blow up the chests that hold the opponent's treasures.
Whoever plays well and first manages to get their hands on five treasures is the big winner!

GOLEN BAY is a game for all ages, for the whole family, and stands out for being a fast-paced game, very easy to learn, and which still challenges us to create a strategy to achieve victory. Each session takes between 15 and 30 minutes, on average.

The 7 Seas is a 1-5 players worker placement adventure game combining elements of exploration, trade, and combat. Set in an alternative world, you play as navigators discovering the different seas, accumulating fame and fortune.

A game of The 7 seas lasts 5 rounds in Discovery mode, and 7 rounds in Advanced mode or Solo mode. Each round consists of 2 cycles of player turns going in clockwise order.

On your turn, choose 2 actions to perform in any order, by placing your captain or your officer on the action board. The round ends after each player has taken their 2 turns, and the Maintenance phase begins to prepare the next round.

At the End of the game, the player with the most glory points wins!

Gather resources and treasures, collect gems and cards, steal each other parrots, and much more! Even if luck isn't on your side, accumulate fortune coins to greatly increase your chances of success for your next turn!

Whether you play the Discovery mode for a quick and enjoyable game with family and friends or the Advanced mode for a richer and more strategic experience, get ready for an immersive adventure with multiple surprises, decisions to make and things to discover!

The game feels rich yet simple. On your turn, simply place your captain or your officer on the action board and perform your selected actions:

     Sail at sea by moving your ship on the different seas and revealing new cards. Discover wild islands and bustling ports. Encounter fierce pirates, wealthy merchants, strong weather phenomena, mighty sea giants and other surprises!
     Land on an island to organize an expedition on an island, collect valuable resources and even find buried treasures
     Dock at a port to exchange your resources and become a true merchant, accumulating wealth and fortune
     Renovate your ship by upgrading your sails to increase your movement, or your weapons to enhance your fighting power


Without further ado, let’s set sail for The 7 Seas, Captain!

—description from the publisher

Go down in the dungeon. Kill everything you meet. Backstab your friends and steal their stuff. Grab the treasure and RUN! That is what this legendary card game has been all about for many years.

Munchkin captures the experience of exploring a dungeon...without the annoying role-playing aspect. All you must do is kill the monsters and grab magical items! As a quick and silly game, Munchkin can drive any role-playing group into a fit of hysterical laughter. And while they are laughing, you steal their stuff.

In Munchkin Super-Mega-Set, players will receive all playing cards from the original Munchkin fantasy sets 1 to 10 that were illustrated by John Kovalic, as well as a selection of the most popular Munchkin fantasy boosters. Most of these have been out of print for years, and two of them appear as a bonus exclusive in this box.

Munchkin Super-Mega-Set was created by Pegasus Spiele for the company's 30th anniversary.

—description from the publisher

Spirit War is a luck-based card game where players engage in battle, based on the system of tri-elements, to be the last one standing. Don't be fooled by its initial simplicity, as this game possesses the potential to evolve into a captivating and strategic mind game.

—description from the publisher

Roll and Write (chose&color) puzzle adventure race game. Players try to find 7 pieces of the lost ark by traveling around the map. Whoever finds the ark first is the winner. Players have a limited number of rounds per game. When traveling, they use a map that is divided into hexagonal tiles representing 5 different types of terrain with different color. Infinity number of players with no delays. Turn-based gameplay but players are active also in other player's turn.Solo variant included.Very short set up time.“Coloring” part for relax time.
Competetive and/or cooperative play.Only one d6 for solo play needed.Variable Player Powers.

—description from the designer

A dispute between rock bands to gain more and more fame! To achieve this, bands must form the best repertoires according to rising and falling trends, in a demanding and constantly changing market. This is ROCKOR: a fast, abstract, thematic game with a lot of interaction between players who can gain or lose points almost all the time, even outside of their turns. At the end of the game, the band with the most fame will be the true rock legend!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Final Girl: The Killer from Tomorrow
description:
In The Killer from Tomorrow, a cyborg from the future has come back in time on a mission to destroy some kid they call "The Savior". Unfortunately, he's kind of a brat who doesn't want to listen to you. The Mark V Hunter will be your first challenge. You'll have to destroy it before the Mark X Hunter arrives to have any chance of getting through the ordeal alive. Will you rebuild the Mark V to assist you against the Mark X? Or go it alone and try to survive against long odds?

The newly built Sunny Daze Mall is THE place to be! An all-new two-story shopping mall (one of the first of its kind!) with the most popular stores and a food court like none other. It would be a real shame if people got attacked and thrown through the walls of stores or were tossed from the 2nd floor balcony by some uncontrollable killer.

Requires Final Girl Core Box to play.

- description by the publisher

description:
The game is based on an escape room principle where the players need to resolve different types of puzzles in order to proceed to the next level.

It is a mixture of an interactive computer game and a detective story with real objects pieces of evidence that survived over time.

What do you receive?

- Game materials
- Game instructions
- Mobile App

How it works?

- The game works on digital devices. The digital game is mainly used to get instructions and to enter the solutions to the questions
- The rest of the game takes place with real objects (it's not a card game!)
- Game duration 90-120 min
- Similar to the escape rooms concept the game can be played once
- Can be played again by other players

Game options

It is possible to play the game in several modes:
- As one team (Standard mode)
- Against the time
- Multiple team competition mode: additional game sets required which you can print with no additional cost. You will not receive any spoilers or indication for solutions so you can take part in the game

What is required?

- Preparation time: ~15 min
- Internet access
- Registration on our portal www.idventure.de, App and game download
- Curiosity, fun, "out of the box" thinking

We wish you an unforgettable experience with the game!

—description from the publisher (translated

Beame dich und deine Gäste auf dem Schiff ans Ende der Welt zu fahren und fühle dich wie Jack Sparrow!

Mit dem iDventure Spiel "Insel der 5 Kapitäne" begeben sich die Partygäste auf hohe See und in die Untiefen der Rätsel und Geheimnisse, die sich um die Entdeckung Amerikas ranken. Um den großen Inka-Schatz zu finden, müssen die Spieler die Rätsel des alten Piratentagebuchs lösen.

Das Spiel basiert auf wahren Gegebenheiten und auf Legenden von bekannten historischen Persönlichkeiten. Es enthält Lehrinhalte aus Geschichte, Mathematik, Geographie, Sprache und Logik und spannende Abenteuergeschichten.

Das iDventure Spiel funktioniert auf PCs, Tablets, Smartphones oder anderen digitalen Geräten. Das digitale Spiel dient hauptsächlich dazu, Videos mit Anweisungen zu sehen und die Lösungen auf die Fragen einzugeben.
Der Rest der Schnitzeljagd findet mit realen Gegenständen und Spielobjekten statt.

—description from the publisher

In Hell to Pay, you're not perfect. You've made your share of mistakes. In fact, maybe you deserve to be in this shifting Hellscape... but then again, maybe you don't. Razorface is a killer that will force you to reckon with yourself and deal with your "afflictions." He draws power from them and horrible mutilation and suffering await you if you find yourself unable to do so.

The Hellscape is a maze that is ever-changing. The sights you'll see may draw you in with ecstasy one moment and then horrify you completely the next. Will you stay in hell for eternity? Or will you be able to escape alive and get back to your normal life?

Requires Final Girl Core Box to play.

-- description by the publisher

description:
Standalone playable expansion of Para bellum (formerly Scharz).
The game provides optional battle expansions for the Para bellum games, but also provides separately playable historical battles:
The first of them - the Battle of Zama in 202 BC.
The system works with different types of units (different attack, lives, movement points), their experience (dice rolls, bonus lives), but mainly from the energy of the units. The first battle of the game - Zama, 202 BC, is a prime example of a dynamic asymmetrical battle with lots of tactical decisions and twists. All this without hidden information.

Peculiar things are happening in the small town of Falconwood. Government experiments and supernatural occurrences are happening with regularity. boardgame: Operation Rolling Thunder: Air War Over North Vietnam, 1965-1968
description:
Operation Rolling Thunder is an Operational  level board-wargame that recreates the historical bombing campaign conducted by the US Air Force and the US Navy over North Vietnam during the period spanning from 1965 to 1968. The game accommodates both solitaire and cooperative play (in which one player assumes command of the US Air Force, while the other oversees operations of the Navy (with an option to play in semi-competitive mode)).

The core objective for the player(s) is to inflict enough infrastructural damage to compel North Vietnam to engage in peace negotiations while disrupting the flow of troops and resources that sustain the insurgency in South Vietnam via the logistics network known as the Ho Chi Minh Trail.

The overarching campaign is divided into seasonal phases, wherein the player or players determine which objectives have been identified as potential targets for the current phase. They then calibrate the scope and composition of air raids, aligning missions with their chosen strategic imperatives. Decisions entail prioritizing missions, including tasks like safeguarding raids against enemy fighter interception through combat air patrols, neutralizing enemy ground-based air defenses, deploying strike capabilities to maximize damage on specified targets, and the hunting down of opportunity targets. Through the use of cards, players resort to a repertoire of historical tactics, specialized missions, selective target assignments, and technological assets to optimize raid efficiency and protection. The augmentation of US forces occurs incrementally, encompassing both numerical reinforcements and advancements in equipment. This includes the introduction of advanced-technology missiles, reflecting an evolving military landscape. Additionally, pilots progressively gain experience, achieving exceptional proficiency in specific mission types.
Targets of interest encompass oil and supply depots, military installations, industrial facilities, the railroad network, crucial bridges, and pivotal airfields, whose destruction bears varying consequences for North Vietnam's ability to generate supplies, mobilize forces, transport equipment, and defend its airspace. Consequently, players may experiment with different approaches, all the while remaining mindful of the alignment of their decisions with the underlying strategy implied by the Joint Chiefs of Staff target list. Higher-priority targets are invariably more robustly defended, necessitating a close monitoring of incurred losses; unacceptable levels can severely compromise or even prematurely shut down the campaign.
After recording the damage (on the game map) inflicted by US air raids on North Vietnam's war-making capacity, the player or players retain the option to dispatch armed reconnaissance missions, scouting for opportunity targets en route to the South, and may call upon the formidable B-52s from the Strategic Air Command to administer targeted strikes on the mountain passes leading to the Ho Chi Minh Trail and on the trail itself.

In tandem with the military dimension, the game incorporates a 'political layer.' Herein, players contend with the constraints imposed by fluctuating directives emanating from the White House, driven by apprehensions of potential escalation involving China and the Soviet Union. These directives can manifest as stricter rules of engagement, prohibited targets, or specific mandates. In response, players challenge the political echelons for operational freedom, judiciously selecting the campaign aspects where enhanced flexibility is warranted.

The player or players face a difficult challenge reminiscent of the historical endeavor, which fell short of achieving its primary objectives. Despite the severe humanitarian toll inflicted by the campaign, it failed to fracture the resolve of the North Vietnamese people and its leadership, who exhibited exceptional resilience in the face of sustained aerial bombardment. The Vietnam War persisted for several more years, culminating in the well-documented denouement.

—description from the publisher

description:
Will the young knights be able to defend their castle and attack their opponent?
All players move at the same time. Goal of the game: destroy one central or 2 side towers of the opponent, while maintaining your castle.
The game progress is divided into two steps: “Preparation” and “Battle”.

     "Preparation"


Players decide which actions they want to implement this turn and place their action tokens on the appropriate space on their castle board. On his turn, the player can attack the opponent's tower, defend his own towers, or extract gold from the mines.

     "Battle"


All players simultaneously raise their screens, compare the number of tokens on the strike, defense and mine cells, applying their effects. The game ends as soon as one of the players destroys 2 side or central towers. The winner is the player who destroyed the opponent's castle without having his towers destroyed as much as possible.

—description from the publisher

description:
This game was created so everyone can experience in a fun way how the ancients felt about their world.


     You will understand how important the cycles of the day and night skies and sky events were to the ancients, and how it effected everything they did.
     It will also test your knowledge of the moon, the planets, myths and world archaeoastronomy architecture, artifacts and alignments.
     You are learning the cycles of the sun by actually moving the sun as you play. The Sun moves very closely to how we experience the sun moving in our world.
     You will understand how the sun cycles signal the seasons of the year to the ancients. They had approximate ways of telling time like candles, water clocks and dividing daylight into 12 hours no matter what time of year it was. The Sun and Stars were extremely important to them.
     You will get to play around with being a good leader, a bad leader and a complicated leader


How the game is played:

Earth Goal (board 1):


     The player chooses a Star Map (leader or ruler) that will decide which building, star totem and constellation they'll be. This decides what geographical location their Sol Cards (trivia questions about archaeoastronomy) will have in their deck.
     The goal is to get your construction site, your building, your totem, and the sun (flashlight) to all line up during your turn. Once that's done, you can now go to the Heaven Board and become a 'God on Earth.'


Heaven Goal (board 2) and to Win:


     To have one or 2 jewels, depending on if you choose to just have fame for one generation or a constellation forever named after you and you are known for a thousand years. It is up to the player to decide what kind of win they want.  


Actual Mechanics, Earth Board:

The Sun is moved every month (all players take their turn) and the Festival Card the Sun is pointing at is played. After the player plays what the cards says,

if the player gets a correct question card, you can roll the die and move. If it's incorrect, it goes to the next player.

If it's a Star & Fortune card, then the player does what it says.

Actual Mechanics, Heaven Board:

The time is the same as the earth board and you move with the sun. You take a Heavens card every turn and do what it says. There is messing with the Earth Board cards, messing with other players and earning and losing jewels.

—description from the designer

The diseased skaven of Skabbik’s Plaguepack plan to usher in the birth of one of the Great Plagues – an apocalyptic disease prophecised amongst the fanatical verminkin of the Clans Pestilens. Their foulsome quest has taken the Plaguepack deep into the heart of Wyrdhollow, to seek one of the arcane ingredients in this foul brew. Should Skabbik's noisome flock succeed in their task, great swathes of the Mortal Realms may be reduced to rancid rotting mulch. Let their enemies beware – a sickening death awaits any who stand against the children of the Great Corruptor.

Skabbik’s Plaguepack employ special rules to Corrupt areas of the map – this foul process is vital to the warband's success, and the Plaguepack's fighters can Inspire, gain new abilities, and score Glory by doing so, inexorably creeping their way to victory. This set includes a full Rivals deck to use straight out of the box, and the models are all push-fit – so you can get playing in no time.

A limited edition release, Big Bash Smash was the official tabletop game of the Big Bash Cricket Series and claims to be the "best of cricket games smashed up and delivered in a quick, sharp and explosive tabletop game for the whole family".

Big Bash Smash offers three different game mode. Play a traditional match in Twenty20, be the first to 50 runs in the 50 Run Challenge, or see who can score the most runs in one over in a Super Over game!

Fans can also download a free scoreboard companion app, keep score with a digital scoreboard and capture the action with a slow-motion replay, just like the real thing.

This game borrows very heavily from the layout and style of the traditional and original TEST Match game, but with a few major differences. The mat is round and very colourful, with sponsors names printed on it and the 'fences' are simple freestanding cardboard, which do not surround the field. The mechanics of the bowler are also quite different in that the ball is ejected from the front of the stand by a spring lever, rather than being 'bowled' by the figure itself.

In the high stakes world of art thievery it takes cunning to make a name for yourself.  As you work to amass the greatest collection of pilfered valuables you must outrun the law, while watching your back from your fellow thieves.  As your collection grows so too will the temptation of your fellow thieves to take what you rightfully stole.  As the saying goes... There is no honor among thieves.

On a player's turn, they will have the opportunity to draw a card either from the deck or another player's hand.  The card drawn may be an event that has impact on the current player or the table itself.  Once a card is drawn, all players will have the opportunity to play cards from their hands  which may help or disrupt the current player.  Finally, the current player can "stash" a loot card from their hand or from another player's stash to collect victory point.  However, keep an eye out for forgeries which might have consequences.

The game continues until the last card is drawn.

Manage your warband, explore your surroundings and face your opponents in this fast paced skirmish wargame!

Skill & Fate is a skirmish wargame system that allows you to focus on interesting decisions instead of dealing with minutia of rules and tedious bookkeeping.

What makes Skill & Fate different is its focus on free form movement and fluid turn structure. Whenever you move your figures, you move them in a straight line until terrain stops you or you decide to stop. Whenever you're moving inside line of sight of an opponent, they may try to interrupt you and take an action. Combat is resolved by drawing cards with defense icons, and damage is marked by figures themselves (by making them prone or removing them from the board).

All you need to start playing is 12 cards per player, 12 beads or tokens (to mark command points), handful of miniatures and some terrain, all of which can be downloaded and printed out before shelling out money.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Peacemakers: Horrors of War – Shared Nightmares

Hyenas are sneaky predators that will use every moment of inattention to attack the participants of the Summer Parade. Hyena cards can be added to the game to make it even more unpredictable.

The game follows the normal rules with one exception: The Hyena card must be played on any previously placed animal card. Hyenas cannot be played on an "empty" column. A played Hyena card reduces the value of the card underneath it by 1. If, at the end of the round, the Hyena is the last card played in the column, the value of the tokens of this animal is 1 lower than on the last card played of this animal. You cannot play one Hyena onto another.
