You are a member of a political party and you are starting your electoral campaign to become President. The dispute over the vote in the provinces of the national territory should be your main priority to win the elections, although your actions will not be the only thing you should take into account. Public opinion has often weighed on people's decisions and actions; some have even called it the fourth estate. You will have to find a way to make friends with opinion leaders who may not respect your ideology, but do serve to improve your approval in society, for example. Or maybe you decide to join a very prestigious media that shares your ideals, which will be your ally throughout the campaign to provide you with its services.

Nowadays, unpredictability can reign in the country, so chance can play tricks on you or be beneficial for your campaign. But be careful because the economy here is fragile, and your budget funds will be limited. The key to managing them can be decisive in reaching the last week of the campaign with a chance, and not losing the possibility of increasing your cultural capital by multiplying your chances of victory. Sometimes it is not the one who has the most money who wins, but the one who best represents the culture of his country, or who has the political skills to influence his adversaries; But above all, the most important thing is that they don't put you in prison. At least until now we do not know of any ruler who has run while behind bars.

Enjoy a fun game of strategy, planning and financial intelligence to enjoy for hours with friends and/or family. And let whoever is the best politician win.

Clarification: The game will not be responsible for fights and/or discussions that may cause or possible deepening of the crack. Or yes... we talked about it in Segurola and Havana.

In the print-and-play, flip-and-fill game West Texas Railways, players lay track, purchase shares, and build junctions, all while balancing the money they have and everyone else's actions.

West Texas Railways is a shared incentives game, meaning that company values are added up (across all players playing) and divided amongst the purchased shares of all playing as well, so whether there are two of you or ten, it's a wild ride.

Once someone empties their bank, the game end is triggered. The player with the most money wins!

To play, players need a partial deck of cards and sheet printouts.

The Werewolves Big Box is a limited edition, that contains the famous Werewolves New Edition and the popular Night Of The Vampires sequel, plus 2 promotional cards. It also comes with a notepad and pencil for the moderator to keep track of all the roles in play. The Werewolves Big Box is a special release for Pegasus Spiele's 30th company anniversary.

In this edition all cards, as well as the box come with a mystic, metallic foil effect, and the box has a reversible cover. The extra-large character cards include a description of the character's ability, which makes the game more accessible for new players.

More about the two games contained in this Big Box: Werewolves is easily one of the most popular party games of all time and the ultimate game for larger groups of all ages. In 2018, Pegasus published a redesigned version of this game, featuring beautiful, atmospheric artwork on large cards that not only tell the players which role they are playing, but also how their role works, so people who have never played the game before don't have to give their secret identity away by asking what they have to do.

Also included in this Big Box is the stand-alone sequel "Werewolves – Night of the Vampires" that Pegasus released in 2022. It can be played on its own with 6 to 24 players or it can be combined with Werewolves to play with up to 48 players.

Due to the 2 promotional cards in this box - the Stray Cat and the Watchdog - you can even play it with up to 50 players.

The proven game principle remains the same: every night Werewolves and/or Vampires secretly devour a human Villager. The following day, all players discuss who among them could be a Vampire or Werewolf – and can lynch the most suspicious person after voting. Slip into the role of one the Villagers and unmask the Werewolves. Or play a Werewolf and convince the Villagers of your innocence while you go hunting at night. And wherever werewolves are up to mischief, vampires are not far away!

—description from the publisher

Tech billionaire Alan Tusk wants to celebrate his latest success with caviar and champagne: In a week, he plans to embark on the first manned flight to Mars. Among the guests on his luxury yacht are not only movie stars, celebrated sportsmen and -women or business magnates, but also several of Alan's old school friends. When Alan's body is found in his suite that evening, the celebration ends as a fiasco. Who could have done such a gruesome deed?

Deadly Dinner – Kaviar Kills is a classical murder mystery game: Enjoy a dinner (or just some snacks) with your family and/or friends, while all of you take on the role of a murder suspect. One of you committed the murder, but you all have secrets you would rather keep. Try to find out the secrets of the other guests without making yourself suspicious. Your goal is to solve the murder case … or get away with the bloody crime. Apart from the murderer trying to cover their tracks, every player also has a secret task they have to complete during the game.

—description from the publisher

You can compete alone or in pairs to take on the challenge of making the most impressive bouquets. Decide the leitmotif of your composition, and then pluck the finest flowers from under your opponents' noses, so you won't be left behind on this petal-strewn road. You earn points for the number of flowers in a bouquet (row of flowers) - simply put flower cards in the row on your team's side of the playing area. You have to match starting card with one of three attributes - a type of flower, its color, or ornament. As soon as there are at least 4 flowers in a row you can score, but more flowers means more points. On the other hand, waiting too long may cause losing all points for that row.


Możecie samodzielnie lub w parze stanąć do konkursu i podjąć wyzwanie wykonania najbardziej okazałych bukietów. Na początek zdecydujcie, jaka będzie cecha przewodnia Waszej kompozycji, a potem zadbajcie, by sprzątnąć sprzed nosa najlepsze okazy przeciwnikom i nie dać się wyprzedzić na tej usłanej płatkami drodze. Punkty zdobywacie za wykładanie kart kwiatów w rzędach po Waszej stronie obszaru gry. Musicie dołożyć kartę pasującą do karty startowej jedną z trzech cech - rodzajem kwiatu, jego kolorem albo dodatkiem. Za rząd, w którym znajdują się co najmniej 4 kwiaty możecie już zdobyć punkty, ale im więcej kwiatów w bukiecie tym więcej punktów. Z drugiej strony, czekanie zbyt długo może kosztować Was utratę wszystkich punktów za dany rząd.

The Battle of Britain is a mini-game about the World War II air campaign over Britain in 1940.  The game took part in "Postcards from the Front Wargame Design Jam" on itch.io and was a free download for backers of the "Halls of Hegra" Kickstarter campaign.  It is for 1-2 players, and does include a solitaire variant.  The game can be played in 5-10 minutes.  Components include a 5" x 7" postcard with the game board on the front and the rules on the back.  There are six counters attached to the postcard that require cutting or copying and mounting.  A deck of regular playing cards are also needed for game play.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: History: World War II (Battle of Britain)

description: Finally supplies for all pub quiz fans! The 250 brand new questions again require general knowledge and have something for everyone: We don't just look at the past, new and latest events, inventions and developments are also discussed here. The supplementary set can be used as a supplementary set of questions and, as always, can be combined with Kneipenquiz – Das Original, Kneipenquiz – Fußball-Spezial, as well as with all other supplementary sets. Alternatively, it can also be played separately, which makes it a good compact travel version.

Numba Card Game is an educational family game that brings fun to maths!

Numba Card Game teaches counting, addition and subtraction through matching numbers of fruits and being the first to shout Numba when there’s a match.

Numba Card Game is easy to pick up and play with anyone, anywhere making it perfect for children, families, adults and games nights.

Choose a number game card and place it face up in the middle. Each player gets a stack of fruit game cards and places them face down in front of them.

Take turns flipping over your top fruit game cards and everyone races to shout Numba! when they spot the number of fruits that match the number game card in the middle.

Each fruit game card has between 1 to 6 fruits of apples, bananas, oranges and strawberries.

Keep your eyes peeled as the fruit game cards get flipped over and be the first to shout Numba! to collect all the fruit game cards and win the number card game.

If you love to laugh out loud and enjoy testing your skills, Numba Card Game is for you!

—description from the publisher

Foxy Brawlers is a game of wits, conflict, deception, and adventure. A player must traverse the entire map and get to the finish, from where the Devil commands his forces and put an end to the little evil. 
There can only be 1 king and there is only 1 crown, don't let others stand in your way. You may team up and scheme against other players, but beware of the knife in the back as only one of you can reach the finish.

All players traverse the map by rolling the die and moving the rolled amount. After everyone has made their turn all players engage in a fight with a boss. Boss fight ends when a sole survivor remains, who will get 2 turns on the map after the fighting is done, so make sure other players lose.

In the northern regions known as Scandinavia live the indigenous Sami people. They play a running fight game called Sahkku. It’s popularity there is shown by the annual championship games and prestigious trophies they give to the winners. The game may be as old as 1,000 years or more, and simulates a battle between two groups of warriors. These warriors start the game asleep, and before they can enter the battle they must be awakened by a specific role of the dice. Thereafter, they move and capture opposing pieces by ending their turn on an occupied space. The path can be blocked by opposing pieces, sending them back to their starting row. There’s also a fickle King with special powers in the game, who joins up with either side when captured. A very different kind of game - one of only a few known to history

—description from the publisher

Tobit is a 2-player strategy game from the Republic of Khakassia in central Asia. The age of the game is unknown, except that it’s been a tradition in the region for many generations. The game board is interesting in the lack of corner spaces, and the odd number of rows in most examples. The pieces start the game as expected, on the near rows of spaces. It’s basically played like orthogonal Checkers, like other Middle Eastern jump-and-capture games. The difference appears when a piece reaches the far row and is promoted to a Tobit. Tobits can move like chess rooks and make long jumps to capture. Once they leave that back row, however, they lose their Tobit status. It’s a very fun game for all ages, right alongside Checkers.

—description from the publisher

The game of Rhombus begins with a large empty table space surrounded by two to four players, and each player holds a hand of three Rhombus cards. These cards are printed with geometric shapes in several colors, and the challenge is to play these cards to the table to form diamond shapes (rhombuses). Forming such shapes in different sizes and color combinations scores points, and it's possible to form multiple rhombuses with a single card placement. The colorful array of cards grows to cover the table until the last card is placed and final score tallied. Fast fun for all ages!

—description from the publisher

Heralds of Defeat is a Free PnP Tactical  level board-wargame that recreates the Civil War inside a Civil War that took place in Madrid during the first week of the last month of the Spanish Civil War.

As Madrid's Republican garrison split in two factions: Socialists and Anarchists seeking a peace agreement with Franco and the Comunists trying to extend the war, this two player game pits them into a bitter battle downtwon Madrid and its close environments.

Propaganda and negotiation are as important as combat itself, as both players need to knit a viable command structure in the middle of chaos; combatants shift back and forth between neutrality and beligerancy and Franco's fifth columnists pop up to wreck further chaos.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Each card in the 72-card deck is emblazoned with four color bars, each bar in two sections, and those sections are either red, green, yellow, or blue. The objective is for each of the 2 to 6 players to create a row of connected bars at least five cards wide, scoring points based on the length of their single-colored bars. But there’s more! Each card has a value that allows the players to purchase cards from an array on the table, or even bargain with each other. The more valuable cards also have special actions that can be used during play. Try for lots of shorter colorbars or go for the longer high-scoring colorbar. Your strategy may shift, depending on how the cards interconnect!

—description from the publisher

Vimanam is often described as "traditional" but I haven't been able to track down any specifics about its history. It certainly looks like it could be many generations old.

As a 2-player race game, however, it does work. All the usual aspects are here: sending opposing pieces back, specific rolls to start your pawns, safe spaces, and bearing off at the end. One pawn per space is a bit unusual for a game with more than one piece per player, but I like it. The shape of the board seems quite random, too, but the five-space sections are consistent and make counting moves easy. I haven't tried the "team play" idea - not sure how that would work actually - but have seen it referred to in a few sources.

It's fast and easy, and making a board is not a huge challenge if you want to tackle it. I haven't seen any for sale on line, but there are plenty of patterns.

—user summary

Strategic stacking and hand management with unique DNA pieces

Draw and play cards to rearrange the sequence of DNA base pairs to score points.

Start by stacking 6 base pairs on the stand. Each player gets dealt 4 cards face down. On your turn you may play up to 3 cards to rearrange the stack to meet one of the scoring criteria on a card from your hand.

Winner is the first to score 10 points.

boardgame: Poland: A Valiant Defense. The Soviet attack of September 17th, 1939
description: This is a solitare, print and play system, that currently focuses on the Soviet invasion of Poland in 1939. It is a hex and counter game that places you in command of a few squads of Polish troops as they try to slow the tide of Soviet invaders. Play time for each scenario shoud be about 45 min.

In a world where heroes are celebrated, you are not the hero – you are the unsung hero of the realm. Choose your role as a townsfolk character and work with your fellow players to survive and lead the heroes to beat the Big Bad Elder Dragon.

Gameplay: In "Not A Hero," players harness the power of their decks each turn, strategically collecting valuable information and gold. These precious resources empower their townsfolk to wield influence over the heroes, guiding them to embark on quests and enhancing their abilities and skills. But beware! As heroes conquer monsters, unearth treasures, and accomplish quests, the looming Elder Dragon threat intensifies. Mismanagement of the heroes can trigger devastating strikes, leading to the town's destruction and ultimate defeat. The townspeople's path to victory lies in nurturing their heroes to the point where they become mighty enough to vanquish the Elder Dragon.

Welcome to Waifu Arena: Kawaii Battles! A card game in which the prettiest waifus face each other in intense and exciting duels. If you're a fan of fast-paced, strategy-driven card games with spectacular art, this project is for you!

The most beautiful girls with unique powers, are the very essence of this world. However, an evil has arisen that threatens to unbalance: The Noctas, dark creatures that seek the destruction of the world. Now, it is time for our Waifus to fight and prove who is the ultimate waifu.

—description from the publisher

Enjoy exploring the ocean floor with your dive team collecting colorful shells to trade with Shelly the octopus in Shelly's Pearl! This early learning Life & Thinking Skills game is intended for ages 4 and up and helps young players achieve goals and practice strategy while building fine motor skills. Play Shelly’s Pearl with 2-4 players in as little as 10 minutes.

Shelly the octopus uses “they/them” pronouns. Shelly’s personal pronouns are shared only in the instruction booklet. The game does not utilize pronouns in the gameplay but does provide the opportunity to discuss personal pronouns if desired.

—description from the publisher

Shore Seekers utilizes the theme of turtle migration to help engage kids across numerous math levels, from addition to early multiplication.
 Shore Seekers is good for developing addition skills as players roll up to three dice, one at a time, adding the revealed numbers with the goal of reaching a sum equal to or less than seven.

Shore Seekers also helps with early multiplication skills when a player has rolled two or three dice, they multiply the sum of their dice by the number of dice rolled.

Players take turns rolling the dice, trying to reach a sum of seven or less. Based on the roll, each player then moves their turtle around the island. Whenever a turtle reaches or passes the entrance to the nesting site (space 21), it lays as many eggs as shown on the top clutch card. At the end of the game, the player whose turtle has laid the most eggs wins.

—description from the publisher

Step into the world of Moguls & Dominion: The Real Estate Rivalry, where up to six players are invited to engage in an epic showdown of wit, strategy, and ruthless ambition. In this immersive board game, you'll navigate the complex landscape of real estate and business, all in the pursuit of becoming the ultimate tycoon.

Your objective is clear: build an empire of properties and businesses using cunning strategies, shrewd deals, and the occasional betrayal. As you traverse the competitive terrain, alliances will be formed, deals will be brokered, and rivalries will ignite in a battle for supremacy.

'Be the Mogul,' strategically acquiring properties and amassing wealth through astute real estate decisions. Alternatively, choose the path of 'The Dominion,' focusing on lucrative business ventures that can secure your financial dominance. The game offers diverse avenues to success, but be prepared to face the consequences of your choices.

—description from the designer

Join Hazel and her siblings as they practice their beaver lodge-building skills. Players take turns rolling the die to determine their actions; connecting logs to create their lodge; moving cattails, or rolling the rock toward another player's lodge. The first player to use all 10 of their logs to build their lodge wins the game.

Fine motor skills are practiced as players connect individual log pieces to build unique structures and roll a rock, trying to avoid the cattail blockers on another player’s board.

Decision-making skills are practiced as well. Considering potential benefits or risks, players make decisions such as how to connect their log pieces, which duck they choose, and which player’s board they will roll the rock toward.

—description from the publisher

In each Q case, you try to solve a mystery case with 32 clues, with players revealing one clue at a time until all cards have been revealed or discarded. During your turn, each player must perform one of the following actions:

A) Reveal information:
Choose a card from your hand and place it on the table, so all players can read or see the entire information.
We recomend you read out loud all shared info when you place it on the table. If you play a clue that happens to be irrelevant
to the case, you lose points at the end of the game, but be careful! Some clues are vital to resolve the case.

You can share and expose your theories at any moment and talk about the cards you have in your hand but you cannot show them to the other players and you may only read out loud the words written in bold or the text framed inside an image:

B) Discard a card:
Discar a card face down without being able to comment anything about that card until the end of the game, at which point you will have to speak based on what you remember of the cards you discarded.

At the end of the game, when all clue cards have been revealed or discarded, you must check carefully all the available information and prepare a theory of what happened, working all together. Then, open the questionnaire and answer all questions. During this phase of the game, you can speak freely about your discarded cards, or the information you remember of them. Each right answer will add two points.

Alba debería haber vuelto a casa después de clase, pero son las ocho de la tarde y no hay rastro de ella.

¿Qué ha ocurrido? ¿Dónde está Alba? ¿Alguien la ha visto?

Vuestro equipo de detectives deberá analizar las pistas para responder a éstas y otras muchas preguntas.

¿Conseguiréis resolver el misterio de su desaparición?

—description from the back of the box

Players take on the role of mountaineers seeking to be the first to reach the top of a mountain.

The game is played in two phases. In the first, players gather equipment by advancing along a track using a dice. Part of the equipment is mandatory for phase 2, while the other part is optional but will provide bonuses for phase 2.

Each player decides individually when he has enough equipment to attempt the climb. He chooses the most appropriate route and advances according to a roll of a dice. Different dices are used depending on the weather. The first player to reach the summit wins the game.

It's largely a game of luck, with sort of a stop-and-go mechanism for the transition from the 1st to the 2nd phase (either start climbing early but with minimal equipment, or spend more time on the 1st phase to get better equipment and climb faster). There's also an element of interaction, which consists of swapping equipment with another player, either in phase 1 (where only duplicate cards can be picked), or in phase 2 (where even another player's single cards can be picked).

—user summary

Pau se ha llevado una desagradable sorpresa al pisar una caca de perro cuando volvía a casa.

¿Quién es el responsable? ¿Por qué nadie la ha recogido?

Vuestro equipo de detectives deberá analizar las pistas para responder a éstas y otras muchas preguntas.

¿Ayudaréis a resolver el misterio?

—description from the back of the box

Blazed is a one-of-a-kind, interactive and unpredictable cannabis smoking game combining risk strategy and luck.

Players take turns rolling dice to earn points and collect cards for toppling opponents in a race to 10,000. Once this milestone is reached, all players get one final turn to score as many points as possible before final scores are tallied. While a victor is eventually crowned, everyone wins when you get Blazed!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Emerge: Promo 01 – One Perfect Island Goal Card
description:
Available as part of the Game Boy Geek Season 12 (2024) Kickstarter.

Contents: One Goal Card.
Requires Emerge base game to play.
One Perfect Island: Place a Seal, Bird, and 4 Plants on a large island.

A whimsical twist on the traditional word game, Scrabble: Dr. Seuss puts your knowledge of the classic children’s books to the test!

Like the original Scrabble game, you can play regular Scrabble words, or you can lay down your favorite whimsical Seussian words to score the most points.

Customize each game with Dr. Seuss cards and Better Letter Battles to flip over any tile as a blank and maximize your moves!

Oh What a Rhyme Time you can have! It's time to rhyme in this Seussian spin on the classic crossword game! Scrabble: Dr. Seuss welcomes fans of these timeless books to play the traditional way, or play with added Seuss'd up bonus features! Relive your favorite stories and discover new ways to play in this whimsical edition of Scrabble!

—description from the publisher

Educational card game based on geographical facts of Chilean cities.

Can be played in two ways:


     Fact comparison. The highest number of the selected attribute (i.e. population) wins a card. The goal is to collect as many cards as possible. 
     Set collection. The goal is to collect as many sets (of 4 cards) as possible.


Contains 33 cards of Chilean cities.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
For the first time ever, there isn’t a Killer (nor minions) to contend with in Terror From Destiny! At least, not one that you can see or attack. Instead, the Killer is death or fate or Destiny (pick your poison), and it will be slowly taking the lives of the Fated Victims, five special victims that come with the Vignette and are always in play, regardless of which location you’re at. You must save as many of these Fated Victims as possible, and for each Victim you fail to save Destiny will exact a heavy price at the end of the game. However, there’s a catch (isn’t there always?): the Fated Victims must be saved in a VERY specific order, an order that you are completely in the dark about at the beginning of the game! If you make a mistake and save the Jock before you save the Cheerleader, Destiny is not going to take kindly to your meddling.

Requires Final Girl Core Box and any one feature location to play.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Turn up the music to find out who’s got the best moves. Combine the movements assigned to your hands, feet and head, preferably with your own signature dance moves, into a dazzling performance to the beat of a song chosen by others.

The dancer who scores the most points from the judges wins the title of Ultimate Dance Master. Remember that everyone can dance, including you!

Game includes 100 dance cards and a Pointualizer 3000 point slip pad.

Long ago, the world’s origins shrouded in mystery. According to the temple and sage, the ancient god named this fabulous world "Taiyi". A million years ago, a world-destroying catastrophe split the world into two closely related continents: the Dark and the Glory, following ancient gods' principles.

After billions of years of internal friction, the resources of "Taiyi" have become scarce. The continuous infiltration of chaos power from outside the continent has created extremely harsh living conditions for creatures. Due to the restraint of the truth power, the chaos power cannot directly invade "Taiyi". However, it uses the desires of sentient beings in "Taiyi" and manipulates puppets to greedily devour the world of "Taiyi"……

Win or Die stands as a fast-paced and highly competitive multiplayer strategic board game, focusing on 1V1 game mode. Its core mechanism akin to the classic game of Stratego. Game elements includes legion formation, fog of war, terrain occupation, unit countering, collection and allocation of resources, etc.
Players engage in battles without prior knowledge of their opponent's legion, launching attacks or using skills to unveil their deployment and lay them to ruin.

There are five selectable factions for players. Each faction has one leader and 6 different units. Each leader has two skill combinations, allowing players to form different legions.

Win or Die released in China in 2020 and received a score of 7.9 on Gstone (the largest Chinese board game database and social app). After three years of optimization, the game has improved its components and artworks. Ensuring the readability of unit information and making the game operations more convenient and intuitive for players.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Firefight Tactical is an innovative dice-driven tactical WW2 wargame at the squad level. Quick to play and learn, this compact game follows American forces through 12 scenarios in the weeks after the D-Day landings. The scenarios gradually introduce game features as you progress. Starting with small infantry engagements on tight maps, players will work their way to larger scenarios with multiple phases, artillery, and tank-on-tank action.
 The scenarios follow American forces from early skirmishes of the Normandy invasion at St. Mere Eglise and Carentan through to St. Lo and Operation Cobra and conclude with Operation Market Garden. The battlefields will vary from desperate battles over a “no man’s land” devoid of cover like Bloody Gulch to claustrophobic urban battles like Cherbourg. Each scenario can be played 2-player against a human opponent or against a customizable solitaire automa.

The Firefight Tactical system was designed to be quick to play and easy to learn, whilst being packed with tactical decision-making. Its compact size also enables play on relatively small playing surfaces, making it an ideal game to travel with. The core infantry rules are straightforward and can be learned from ~10 pages, including many examples and a glossary of actions.
 As a result of the dice and random battle maps, the game has intensely high replayability, even within a single scenario, so players will get tons of enjoyment from the "basic" game. Once you are ready for more, though, you can start adding more rules, covering support weapons, weather, armor, and so forth in the heavier scenarios. Firefight Tactical is the perfect game for those looking for a lot of tactical depth and replayability with a small footprint.

With the Challenger Expansion you can face off alone or with other players against 1-2 automated opponents (Challengers). They'll compete against you for the same objectives as a normal player would.

Consists of:
1 Rules booklet
2 Challenger boards (2-sided)
1 Mystcaller board (2-sided)

Dive into the exciting world of paleontology with this addictive and fun card game! Collect and combine dinosaur DNA, creating a unique Jurassic Park. Competition is fierce, and the goal is to revive as many dinosaur species as possible. Jurassic Park awaits you!

Digger is a competitive card game for 2-4 players and ages 8 and up. Each game lasts approximately 20-30 minutes.

OBJECTIVE
Obtain all the 5 different dinosaur tokens.

GAMEPLAY
ROUNDS: the game lasts 3 rounds.

At the beginning of their turn, the starting player must decide which rule (or trick) they will follow during said turn. A trick is the combination of cards that the players will use to win the turn. Each player’s trick must consist of a minimum of 2 cards and a maximum of 5, and they can be of various dinosaur species. There are 4 possible trick types: EVEN VALUES (2, 4, 6...), ODD VALUES (1, 3, 5...)., CONSECUTIVE VALUES (1, 2, 3…)., MATCHING VALUES (5, 5, 5…).

Once the starting player’s trick has been placed face up on the table, all the other players, in counter-clockwise order, will have to play their cards (a minimum of 2 and a maximum of 5), following the starting trick’s rule. Players may use any dinosaur species, even those that have not been used in the starting player’s trick.

The player who has played the card with the highest numerical value of each species will keep that card (having beaten the other players in that species) and place it on the table next to them. If they have played the highest card in more than one species, they will win those high cards and place them accordingly, one next to the other to differentiate the suits. The next player to their right must then play their trick, and so on until all players have played their tricks.

When all players have been the starting player the corresponding number of times, a round is completed. At the end of each round, the player who has won the most cards of each species will receive a token of that species.

Once the tokens have been dealt, all the cards (player cards, won cards, and play and discard decks) are put together in a new deck, which must be shuffled before the deal for the next round can begin.

END OF THE GAME AND VICTORY
There are three ways to end the game:


     A player obtains all the 5 different dinosaur species tokens.
     The 3 rounds are completed and the player with the most tokens of different species wins.
     One of the 5 species token piles runs out of tokens. If such a case is met, follow the rules from above.


—description from the publisher

Gingerbread Towers is a quick card-stacking game for 2-4 players.

The aim of the game is to contribute the most to the gingerbread tower. Each card features one, two, or three sweets. There are six different types of sweet in the game, and each player will be scoring on three of those types. Every turn you place a card on the tower, scoring for any types of sweet on your score cards. But cards on the same layer mustn’t touch, and any cards you knock off are deducted from your score.

As well as doing your best not to knock over the tower, you are also trying to pick up the highest-scoring cards and prevent your opponents from getting cards which benefit them.

Each game contains 54 cards including rules etc and comes in a printed tuck box.

-description from publisher
